夜谭随录 - Occasional Records of Night Talks

Translation created with model: together-qwen3-14b-3step-fewshot / step-by-step-3step-fewshot

    Home
    1

    夜谭随录　（清）闲斋氏　著

    　　自序子不语怪，此则非怪不录，悖矣，然而意不悖也。夫天地至广大也，万物至纷赜也，有其事必有其理，理之所在，怪何有焉？圣人穷尽天地万物之理，人见以为怪者，视之若寻常也。不然，凤鸟河图，商羊萍实，又保以称焉？世人于目所未见，耳所未闻，一旦见之闻之，鲜不为怪者，所谓少所见而多所怪也。

    Explanation

    1

    Night Talks and Casual Records by Mr. Xianzai

    Preface

    Confucius does not speak of strange things, but this text records nothing but strange things, which seems contradictory, but the intention is not contradictory. The heavens and earth are extremely vast, and all things are extremely complex. There must be a reason for every event, and where there is a reason, there is no strangeness. Sages exhaustively understand the principles of heaven, earth, and all things. What people see as strange, sages regard as ordinary. Otherwise, phoenixes, river maps, Shangyang, and floating fruit would not be considered auspicious signs. When people see or hear something they have never seen or heard before, they are rarely not surprised. This is what is meant by 'seeing little leads to being surprised by much.'

    2

    苟不以理穷，则人生世间，无论天地万物广大纷赜也，即一身之耳目口鼻，言笑动止，死生梦幻，何者非怪？不求其理，而以见闻所不及者为怪，悖也；既求其理，而犹以见闻所不及者为怪，悖之甚者也。予今年四十有四矣，未尝遇怪，而每喜与二三酒朋，于酒觞茶榻间，灭烛谈鬼，坐月说狐，稍涉匪夷，辄为记载，日久成帙，聊以自娱。昔坡公强人说鬼，岂曰用广见闻，抑曰谈虚无胜于言时事也。故人不妨妄言，己亦不妨妄听。夫可妄言也，可妄听也，而独不可妄录哉？虽然妄言妄听而即妄录之，是亦怪也。即《夜谭随录》，所谓为志怪之书也可。

    Explanation

    2

    If not by reason or logic, then in life, regardless of the vastness and complexity of the universe and all things, even the eyes, ears, nose, mouth, speech, laughter, movement, stillness, life, death, dreams, and illusions of one's own body—what is not strange? Not seeking the reason, but regarding what is beyond one's knowledge as strange, is absurd; even after seeking the reason, still regarding what is beyond one's knowledge as strange, is extremely absurd. I am now forty-four years old, and I have never encountered anything strange. I often enjoy being with a few drinking companions, among wine cups, tea tables, and couches, talking about ghosts with candles extinguished, and discussing foxes under the moon. Slightly involving the extraordinary, I immediately record it, and over time, it has become a collection, just to amuse myself. Formerly, Su Dongpo forced people to talk about ghosts. Is it to broaden one's knowledge? Or is it to say that discussing the void is better than talking about current affairs? Therefore, one should not hesitate to speak foolishly, and one should also not hesitate to listen foolishly. One can speak foolishly, one can listen foolishly, but why can one not record foolishly? Even so, to record foolishly what one speaks and listens to foolishly, this is also strange. It is like Yetai Suilu, which is also called a book of strange tales.

    3

    乾隆辛亥夏六月霁园主人书于蛾术斋之南窗卷一崔　秀　才奉天先达刘公，未遇时，故世家子。少倜傥好客，挥霍不吝，车马辐辏，门庭如市，行路者健羡。虽齐之孟尝，赵之平原不是过也。忽有崔元素者，投一刺，刘接见，询其邦族，曰：“山东临朐秀才也，游都门二十年矣。闻公喜接纳，来作食客耳。”刘大悦，与之往来，亦时济其薪水。崔率十余日一至，至必有所借贷，家人悉厌贱之，刘独不以为琐，每如其愿，未尝拂逆。如是者二年余。

    Explanation

    3

    In the sixth month of the year Xinhai during the Qianlong era (1791), the owner of Ji Garden wrote at the south window of the E Shu Study.

    The respected scholar Liu, who hailed from Fengtian, was not yet successful but came from a noble family. In his youth, he was carefree and fond of entertaining guests, spending lavishly without hesitation. His entrance was always bustling with carriages and horses, as if it were a marketplace, and passersby admired him. Even the famous Mengchang Jun of Qi and Pingyuan Jun of Zhao, known for their hospitality and patronage of scholars, would not have surpassed him.

    Suddenly, there was a person named Cui Yuansu who submitted a business card. Liu received him and asked about his family and origin. Cui replied, "I am a scholar from Linqu County, Shandong Province, who has been traveling in the capital for twenty years. I heard that you enjoy welcoming guests, so I came to become a guest."

    Liu was very pleased and interacted with him frequently, sometimes helping with his expenses. Cui usually came every ten or more days, and whenever he came, he would always borrow something. The family members all disliked and looked down on him, but Liu alone did not consider it trivial. He always fulfilled his wishes and never refused him. Like this, it continued for over two years.

    4

    刘迭遭大故，资产荡尽。又三年，一贫如洗。更屡试不第，亲故白眼相向，动辄得咎，传为口实，渐至不相闻问。婢仆逃散，并有心作罪以求去者接踵，仅存一老仆。内则一妻一女一子，鼎足而三焉。会腊尽，牛衣尘甑，无以卒岁。女能诗，戏吟曰：“闷杀连朝雨雪天，教人何处觅黄棉。岁除不比逢寒时，底事厨中也禁烟。”刘见之，笑曰：“此际玉搂起粟，若可煮食，足够一饱。今得汝诗，能不令人羞也？”因和之曰：“今年犹戴昔年天，昔日轻裘今破棉。寄语东风休报信，春来无力出厨烟。”

    Explanation

    4

    D Liu encountered a great calamity, and all his assets were wiped out. After another three years, he was utterly destitute. He failed the imperial examinations repeatedly, and relatives and friends gave him disdainful looks, blaming him for anything he did, and gossiping about his misfortune. Gradually, he came to be ignored. His maids and servants fled, and those who sought to leave by fabricating offenses followed one after another, leaving only one old servant. Inside, there was one wife, one daughter, and one son, making three in total. When the year was nearly over, they had nothing but coarse clothes and a dusty pot, unable to make it through the year. The daughter could write poetry and playfully recited, "The continuous rain and snow days are so depressing, how can one find yellow cotton? New Year's Eve is not the same as a cold day, why is there no smoke in the kitchen?" Liu saw this and laughed, saying, "At this moment, the jade bed rises with grains, if it could be cooked, it would be enough for a meal. Now that I have your poem, how can I not feel ashamed?" He then responded, "This year still wears the sky of last year, the light fur of the past is now patched with cotton. Send a message to the east wind not to report, when spring comes, there is no strength to produce smoke in the kitchen."

    5

    妻怒之以目，曰：“往日良朋密友，有求必应，啜汁者岂止一人。今年近岁逼，吃着俱无，犹不少思筹策，乃和儿女子作推敲丑态，想亦拼得饿死，故预作韭露挽歌耶？”刘曰：“然则欲我做贼去耶？”妻曰：“做贼亦得！第恐君无其才耳！顺城门外朱知县，方其落拓时，与汝为莫逆交，一日不见，亦不能耐。今闻其丁艰在家，宦囊颇厚，讵不能走一简，聊济燃眉耶？”刘曰：“微汝言，吾几忘之矣。”亟作书，遣老仆往投之。日暮赤手回，入门即骂曰：“丧心人不必复与相识矣！始而阍人辞以他出，我则不信；既而送客在门，相见。两眼棱棱，持书而入。再四促之，始传语言事忙，不暇修复。但借口致意，主人现在凡百需费，囊无一文，正愁无处措置，断难如命云云。似此丧心人，若复与相识，名节扫地尽矣！”刘企刻一日，满拟必获如意，骤闻此变，不禁索然。

    Explanation

    5

    The wife glared at him and said, "In the past, with our close friends and trusted companions, any request was met with a response, and those who have tasted the juice are not just one person. Now, as the year approaches and the season presses, we have nothing to eat, yet you still do not think or plan, but instead act like a child, hesitating and indecisive. It seems you are willing to starve to death, so you have already prepared a lament like the fleeting life of chives and their dew?" Liu replied, "Then do you want me to become a thief?" The wife said, "Becoming a thief is also an option! But I fear you lack the talent for it! There is Magistrate Zhu outside the Gate of the Smooth City. When he was in his impoverished days, he was your closest friend, and even a single day apart was unbearable. Now I hear that he is in mourning at home, with a substantial official salary. How could he not write a letter to temporarily relieve your urgent need?" Liu said, "Without your words, I would have almost forgotten." He quickly wrote a letter and sent an old servant to deliver it. At dusk, the servant returned empty-handed. Upon entering, the wife immediately scolded, "A heartless person like you does not deserve to be acquainted with anymore! At first, the gatekeeper claimed he was elsewhere, but I did not believe him; then, when he was sending off guests at the gate, we met. His eyes were sharp, and he held the letter as he entered. After urging him repeatedly, he finally said that he was too busy to write a response. He merely conveyed his greetings, saying that the master currently has no funds for any expenses, and is worried about where to find the money, so he cannot fulfill your request. A person like this, if we were to become acquainted again, our reputation and integrity would be completely ruined!" Liu had been eagerly expecting to achieve his desires in one day, but upon suddenly hearing this change, he could not help feeling disheartened.

    6

    妻哂曰：“莫逆交不足恃矣。然总角之交，应非泛泛也。城北杨君，非与君为总角交乎？”刘以为然，复走柬以干之。杨辞以生意淡泊，本利损亏，无囊可解。刘抚髀叹曰：“面朋口友，固不足怪。欲明通财之义，非道义之交不可。”乃挑灯作札，罄吐肝膈，翌日付老仆持送南城靳公子。靳世胄阀阅，田园遍畿辅。

    Explanation

    6

    The wife smiled and said, "The closest and most intimate friendships are not to be relied upon. However, childhood friendships should not be superficial. Is not Yang Jun from the north of the city your childhood friend?" Liu agreed and wrote another letter to seek him out. Yang declined, citing his business as unprofitable and his capital depleted. Liu sighed and said, "Superficial friends are not surprising. To understand the principle of sharing wealth, one must have a friendship based on moral principles." He then lit a lamp and wrote a letter, pouring out his deepest thoughts, and the next day entrusted an old servant to deliver it to the nobleman Jin Gongzi in the south of the city. Jin was of noble lineage with a long history of high social status and owned vast estates throughout the surrounding areas of the capital.

    7

    公子与刘为世交，又属至戚，每当晤对，夜以继日，所讲论非忠义大节，即出世大道，互相诱掖，不啻同胞，所谓立脚不随流俗，留心学做古人者。阅札即刻复答，谓：“叨在知己，亟当如命，奈心与力违，束手无策。君但勉为尚志之士，无自暴弃，又何忧贫贱哉！且天生刘君，必非碌碌者，君姑待之，保有大富贵日也。第好义如弟者，值此危急之秋，竟坐视良朋之困，不能一援手救，殊堪自愧，唯知己者谅之耳！”刘忿，掷书于地曰：“嗬嗬！平日披肝胆，谈道德，何啻羊、左、任、黎！每举一子一女，犹以百金为寿。今急切相需，乃不破一文，反以肤词迂说相敦勉。所谓道义之交，固如是乎？”

    Explanation

    7

    The two individuals, being both long-standing friends and close relatives, would meet and converse day and night. Their discussions were either about loyalty, righteousness, and great principles, or about the path to transcendence and enlightenment. They encouraged and supported each other, not unlike brothers, embodying the ideal of standing firm and not following the crowd, and paying attention to learning from the ancients. Upon receiving a letter, they would respond immediately, saying, "I am fortunate to have a true friend, and I should urgently follow your command. However, my heart and strength are at odds, and I am at a loss and unable to do anything. You should only strive to be a person of high ideals, do not give up on yourself, and why worry about poverty and low status! Moreover, God has created Mr. Liu, and he will certainly not be an ordinary person. You should wait for it, and there will surely be a day of great wealth and honor. Yet, a person who values righteousness like a brother, at this critical time, to sit idly by and watch a good friend in trouble, unable to lend a helping hand, is truly shameful. Only true friends can understand!" D Liu, in anger, threw the letter on the ground and said, "Huh! Usually, we share our hearts and discuss morality, not unlike Yang, Zuo, Ren, and Li! Every time we have a child, we still offer a hundred gold coins as a gift. Now, in urgent need, yet not spending a single coin, instead, using superficial and roundabout words to encourage. Is this what is called a friendship based on righteousness?"

    8

    老仆慰之曰：“主之朋友，大概未曾交得一人。亲戚中不乏富贵者，盍拚一失色，与之通融。”刘叹曰：“朋友列五伦之一，尚三呼不应，琐琐姻娅，又何望乎？”言次，闻门有剥啄声，报崔秀才来矣。妻曰：“呸！人家潦倒至此，彼尚欲来刲瘦胫耶？焉知并胫也无，即欲来刲，正恐无下刀处！”刘曰：“不然。此空谷足音也。”延之入。

    Explanation

    8

    The old servant comforted him, saying, "Master, you have probably not made many friends. Among your relatives, there are many who are wealthy and powerful. Why not try to change your appearance and accommodate them?" Liu sighed, saying, "Friendship is one of the five relationships in Confucian ethics, yet despite repeated attempts, there is no response. These relatives by marriage are trivial and insignificant. What can one expect from them?" As he spoke, they heard the sound of someone knocking on the door, and someone reported that the scholar Cui had arrived. His wife said, "Pah! If someone is in such a state of poverty, would they still want to come and cut off thin legs? How do we know they even have legs? Even if they do, there is probably no place to cut!" Liu said, "Not so. This is a rare and valuable visitor." He invited him in.

    9

    崔曰：“刘君纵理不入于口，而乃一寒如此哉？昔日之繁华，真耶幻耶？今日之索寞，幻耶真耶？鼯技易穷，青松落色，槿心朝在，夕不存矣。尚有一人肯杖策踵门如崔元素者否？”刘曰：“昔日自谓盟车笠，订金兰，得一二耐久朋，为终身胶漆，不意翻覆若此，不敢复言交游矣。”崔曰：“不然。廉将军免官客去，翟廷尉复职客来。人情自昔然也。君自不达，夫何怨尤！智者当务之为急。为今之计，当奈何？”刘曰：“束手待毙耳！”崔笑曰：“出此言，当罚锾矣。吾闻负重涉远，不择地而休；累重家贫，不择禄而仕。

    Explanation

    9

    Cui said, "Mr. Liu, though you may not speak eloquently, why are you in such a dire situation? Was the past glory real or illusory? Is today's desolation illusory or real? The skills of a weasel are easily exhausted, the color of a pine tree fades, the heart of a hibiscus is present in the morning but gone by evening. Is there still someone who would come to visit, like Cui Yuansu?" Liu replied, "In the past, I thought I had formed deep friendships, bound by the metal and wood of a sword and scabbard, expecting a few enduring friends who would be inseparable for life. I did not expect such a reversal, and I dare not speak of friendship anymore." Cui said, "That is not the case. When Liang Ji lost his position and his guests left, when Zhai Tingwei regained his position and new guests arrived. Human feelings have always been this way. You yourself do not understand, what is there to complain about! Wise people prioritize urgent matters. What should we do now?" Liu said, "To surrender and wait to die!" Cui laughed and said, "To say such a thing, I should fine you. I have heard that when carrying a heavy load and traveling a long distance, one does not choose a place to rest; when burdened with heavy responsibilities and living in poverty, one does not choose a salary and goes to work."

    10

    盍投笔从戎，聊博升斗，不犹愈于托钵向人，受守钱虏之轻薄乎？”刘曰：“峣峣者易缺，皎皎者易污，非所以自完也。”崔曰：“外以笔耕，内以针耨，亦可免冻馁。”刘曰：“局促效辕下驹，夙所羞也。”崔曰：“奇货可居，垄断可登，鸟兽之羽毛可织而衣。其遗粒足食也。贪贾三之，廉贾五之，盍为贾？”刘曰：“觊觎分毫，镏铢必较，素所鄙夷，而弗屑者也。”崔曰：“然则度君之心，量君之志，欲更扬眉吐气，非官不能矣。欲为官，须登第；欲登第，须理旧业读书；欲读书，须膏火之费。吾视君皆未易办也。吾有钱八十千，可辇至。”刘曰：“君方同病，讵忍波累？”崔曰：“人弃我取，人取我予，夫何辞焉？”遂言别。移时，以车辇八十千至，刘大感谢，欲备一餐相款。崔不坐而去。

    Explanation

    10

    Why not put down the brush and join the army, to barely make a living, would it not be better than begging for alms from others, and suffering the contempt of those who guard their wealth?" Liu replied, "The lofty are easily broken, and the pure are easily defiled; it is not a way to preserve oneself." Cui said, "Outside, one can till the land with the pen; inside, one can weave with the needle. This can also prevent freezing and hunger." Liu said, "Being confined like a horse tied to a post has always been a source of shame." Cui said, "Valuable goods can be hoarded, and the market can be controlled. Birds' feathers can be woven into clothes, and their leftovers can be eaten. Greedy merchants triple their profit, while honest merchants quintuple it. Why not become a merchant?" Liu said, "To covet every small amount and to be overly concerned with minor details has always been something I despise and would not stoop to." Cui said, "Therefore, considering your heart and aspirations, if you wish to rise above, it can only be through becoming an official. To become an official, one must pass the imperial examinations; to pass the examinations, one must study the classics; to study, one must have the expenses for oil and candles. I see that all these things are not easy for you. I have eighty thousand coins, which I can bring to you." Liu said, "You are also in the same situation; how could I add to your troubles?" Cui said, "When others discard, I take; when others take, I give; why should I hesitate?" So they parted ways. After some time, the money was brought in a cart; Liu greatly thanked him and wanted to prepare a meal as a gesture of gratitude. Cui did not sit down but left.

    11

    迟数日，复提一囊至，曰：“君曾肄业否？”刘曰：“新正伊迩，未免匆忙。”崔曰：“予思八十千，岂敷樽节之用，更蓄得一囊金，为君谋小康。”亟置之炕头，便出门，挽之不及。试启囊，灿然尽赤金也。一室俱惊，权之三百两。崔从此不复至，更不识其居处，徒铭感而已。出资购第宅，赎旧产，又于新居掘得窖金二瓮，遂成富室。僮仆去者，次第复来，百计夤缘，以求收录。亲友亦稍稍通庆吊。一年之间，繁华如故。刘不复好客，唯闭户下帷，日夜占毕。是年及第，官清要，贺客日盛。

    Explanation

    11

    After several days, Cui returned carrying a bag and said, "Have you ever studied?" Liu replied, "The New Year is approaching, and I cannot avoid being busy." Cui said, "I thought eighty thousand would not be enough for the expenses of a feast, so I also saved a bag of gold to help you achieve a modest comfort." He hurriedly placed it on the kang and then left, and Liu could not catch up with him. Trying to open the bag, Liu found it was all bright red gold. The whole room was shocked, and they weighed it at three hundred taels. Cui never came again, and Liu no longer knew his residence, only left with gratitude and remembrance. Liu spent money to buy a mansion, redeemed old property, and also found two jars of buried gold in the new house, thus becoming a wealthy household. Servants who had left gradually returned, and Liu tried every means to seek acceptance. Friends and relatives also gradually sent congratulations and condolences. Within a year, prosperity returned as before. Liu no longer enjoyed entertaining guests, only closed the door and hung up the curtain, reading day and night. That year, he passed the examination and obtained a high-ranking official position, and congratulatory guests increased daily.

    12

    值初度，预使人四出，凡亲故中贫窭落魄及不能举火者，尽招致之。及期，亲友毕集，竞出金玉锦绣，罗列满堂，为刘祝嘏。刘乃张筵高会，酒再巡，罢乐，出席，举觞属客，悉出所得，分赠诸贫贱之前，使各收贮。众愕然，不测何故。佥曰：“凡兹不腆，其所以奉祝长年者，纵不足贵，亦诸亲友之芹献也。曷为散之？”刘叹曰：“今日何幸，群公臻至，赐我百朋，所恨座中唯少崔秀才一人耳！崔若在，必能知我之为此举也！”因袖出一笺，则五言古诗一章也。命其子朗诵以示众，曰：主人好施与，挥霍无踌躇。

    　　客有谏之者，主人笑曰毋。

    　　君谓财可聚，我意财宜疏。

    　　不暇为君详，聊以言其粗。

    　　财为人所宝，人为财之奴。

    　　富者以其有，贫者以其无。

    　　有则气逾扬，无则气不舒。

    　　逾扬人愈亲，不舒人不知。

    Explanation

    12

    On the occasion of his birthday, Liu sent people out in advance to invite all relatives and acquaintances who were poor, destitute, and unable to make ends meet. When the time came, all the guests gathered, bringing out gold, jade, brocade, and other valuables, filling the hall and offering blessings to Liu. Liu then held a grand feast, and after the second round of drinks, the music stopped. He stepped out from the main table, raised a cup, and addressed the guests, taking out everything he had received and distributing it among the poor and lowly, instructing them to each store it. The guests were startled and unable to guess the reason. They said, "All these modest offerings, what is used to wish for a long life, even if not valuable, it is also the humble offering of relatives and friends. Why distribute it?" Liu sighed and said, "How fortunate we are today, all the esteemed guests have arrived, bestowing upon me a hundred friends. The only regret is that Cui the scholar is missing! If Cui were here, he would surely understand my reason for this action!" He then took out a note from his sleeve, which was a five-character ancient poem. He ordered his son to recite it to the guests, saying:

    The master enjoys giving, and spends without hesitation.
    A guest advised him, but the master smiled and said, "No need."

    You think wealth can be hoarded, but I believe it should be dispersed.
    I won’t take the time to explain in detail, but I’ll speak briefly.

    Wealth is valued by people, but people become slaves to wealth.
    The rich boast of their abundance, while the poor lament their lack.
    Those with wealth grow more arrogant, while those without feel unfulfilled.
    The more arrogant, the closer they seem, but the unfulfilled remain unnoticed.

    13

    昔我贫贱时，颠踣无人扶。

    　　有身不能衣，有口不能糊。

    　　贵戚与高朋，相逢皆避途。

    　　居然一厌物，俨若非丈夫。

    　　今日奋功名，食禄复衣襦。

    　　门庭闹如市，势利日以殊。

    　　一寿千黄金，一箸万青蚨。

    　　奢穷欲亦极，无劳用力图。

    　　当时何其啬，今日何其都？

    　　顾兹亲串惠，岂我所愿乎！

    　　昔贫今且富，昔我即今吾。

    　　清夜维其故，反侧心踟蹰。

    　　其故良有以，今昔人情符。

    　　周急不继富，圣言不可诬。

    　　忆昔齐晏子，举火蟾葭莩。

    　　又闻范文正，义田置东吴。

    　　设使天下人，能聚复能输。

    　　在在无和峤，处处有陶朱。

    　　流过阿堵物，何来庚癸呼。

    　　堪叹近富者，唯利之是趋。

    　　满盈神鬼恶，往往寄祸沽。

    　　用是常自惕，羞为守虏徒。

    　　况今得之如泥沙，当日求之无锱铢。

    Explanation

    13

    When I was poor and lowly, I stumbled and fell, with no one to help me.

    Having a body, I could not clothe it; having a mouth, I could not feed it.

    Noble relatives and high-ranking friends all avoided me when they met.

    I became an object of disgust, seeming not to be a real man.

    Now I have achieved success and fame, enjoying stipends and wearing fine clothes.

    The courtyard is as bustling as a market, with power and interests growing more distinct.

    A single banquet costs a thousand gold, a single chopstick costs ten thousand qīngfú (a unit of currency).

    Luxury reaches its extreme, with no need to strive hard.

    How stingy I was then, how generous I am now?

    Considering the favor shown to relatives and friends, is this what I wished for?

    From poverty to wealth, from past me to present me.

    In a clear night, pondering the past, tossing and turning with hesitation.

    There is a good reason for this, the sentiments of the past and present align.

    Help those in urgent need, not those who are already wealthy; the sage's words cannot be false.

    Recalling Yanzi of Qi, raising a fire in the midst of poverty.

    Hearing of Fan Zhongyan, establishing benevolent fields in the east of Wu.

    If all people could gather and give, there would be no more Ho Ch'iao, everywhere would be Tao Zhu.

    The flow of these things, where does the call for geng and gui come from?

    It is pitiable to see those near wealth, who only chase profit.

    When full, gods and ghosts become angry, often bringing disaster in exchange.

    Therefore, I am always cautious, ashamed to be a mere guard of the poor.

    Considering that I now obtain things like mud and sand, back then I sought even the smallest amount.

    14

    君不见栖栖穷巷孤寒儒，此时此际如苦荼！

    　　众闻之无不赧然，如芒在背，多有逃席而去者，亦不追挽。俄报崔先生至矣，刘倒屣左辟鞠之。崔握手而笑曰：“君可谓国狗之瘈，无所不噬矣！奈何效杜子春口舌为？且繁华索莫，其衍几何？苟不齐之，魔障釶起矣。彼接舆髡首，桑扈裸行，倏来忽逝，岂屑屑于菀枯隆杀哉？会尽人情，点头亦属多事耳！”刘再拜曰：“至味之言，敢不佩为弦韦？”

    　　是夕客散，独留崔宿，妻子亦出拜之。刘曰：“近日徙居何所？胡久不一至？致缺酬报。”崔曰：“昔者悉索君，君时亦望报否？”刘曰：“实无是心。”崔曰：“然则予独有是心哉？何不恕也！”刘大笑，因问家中更有何人。崔曰：“颇不孤孑，子女孙曾数十矣。”刘欣然曰：“小女未字，以归君家，何如？”崔曰：“此大不可也。”刘力诘问之，崔吱唔良久，始吐实曰：“君长者，言亦无害。所不敢与君结姻者，自愧非人，实艾山一老狐也。

    Explanation

    14

    You do not see the lonely scholar, wandering in a poor alley, who at this moment is as bitter as tea!

    When the audience heard this, they all blushed, feeling uneasy, and many left the gathering, without anyone trying to stop them. Shortly after, it was reported that Mr. Cui had arrived, and Liu hurriedly greeted him with great respect. Mr. Cui shook his hand and smiled, saying, "You can be called a rabid national dog, attacking everything without discrimination! Why imitate Du Zichun's way of speaking? Moreover, the contrast between prosperity and poverty, how long will it last? If it is not harmonized, demons and obstacles will arise. Those who cut off their hair like Jieyu, walk naked like Sanghu, come and go quickly, why bother with the ups and downs of life? Understanding human nature, even nodding is a lot of trouble!" Liu bowed twice and said, "Such profound words, how dare I not take them as a guide!"

    That night, the guests dispersed, but Liu kept Mr. Cui for the night, and his wife and children also came out to greet him. Liu asked, "Where have you moved recently? Why haven't you come here for a long time? I have been neglecting to visit you." Mr. Cui replied, "In the past, I sought you out, but did you ever expect a return visit?" Liu said, "I truly had no such thought." Mr. Cui said, "Then is it just me who has this feeling? Why not be more forgiving!" Liu laughed and asked about anyone else in his family. Mr. Cui said, "I am not alone, I have dozens of children and grandchildren." Liu happily said, "My daughter is unmarried, how about marrying her to your family?" Mr. Cui said, "This is very inappropriate." Liu pressed him for an explanation, and after hesitating for a long time, Mr. Cui finally revealed the truth, "You are a gentleman, my words will do no harm. The reason I dare not marry my daughter to you is that I am ashamed to call myself a human, I am actually an old fox from Mount Ai."

    15

    以君抱奇气，故不远千里来相结纳，致君贫而再富，亦定数，非吾之力。譬如作室，既镇其甍，又何如焉？吾特因人成事耳。今夙愿已了，即当长辞故人矣。”刘始大悟，不觉洒然曰：“君去固自得矣，将无使吾为忘筌忘蹄之人哉！”崔曰：“予非贪天功者，君何感焉？从此前程皆顺境矣。官不过三品，而富则十万，虽然，讵无一言为留别之赠？吾闻人心不同，有如其面，橡樟二木，七年乃知。知人之鉴，不易明也。甘以坏何如淡以成，毁方而瓦合，全交之至言，君其志之，勿为雉犬所笑。”言讫，辞出，永不复至。刘后官至臬司，以老告归。感崔之谊，朔望祀以香楮，终身不衰。

    Explanation

    15

    Because you possess extraordinary qualities, I traveled a thousand miles to meet and befriend you, transforming your poverty into wealth, which was also fated, not due to my efforts. It is like building a house; once the ridgepole is stabilized, what more can be done? I merely facilitated the outcome. Now that my long-held wish has been fulfilled, I shall bid farewell to you forever." Liu suddenly realized and said, "You depart, indeed, content in yourself, but will you not leave me as someone who forgets the means to reach the goal?" Cui replied, "I do not claim credit for heaven's work; why should you feel grateful? From now on, your path will be smooth. You may not rise higher than the third rank of officials, but you will be wealthy with ten thousand. Although, is there nothing I can say as a final parting gift? I have heard that people's hearts differ as much as their faces, and it takes seven years to truly know someone. The insight to discern people is not easily gained. Excessive sweetness leads to decay, while moderation leads to success. Compromising one's principles to fit in with others is the ultimate advice for preserving friendships; remember this, and do not be ridiculed by the likes of chickens and dogs." After speaking, Cui left, never to return. Later, Liu became a provincial governor and retired due to old age. Grateful for Cui's friendship, he regularly offered incense and paper money on the first and fifteenth day of the month, a practice that lasted his entire life.

    16

    闲斋曰：戋戋之俗，万变千更，交固不易言也。方其盛也，面朋口友，不招自来；及其衰也，迹合神违，百无一应。除毁方瓦合一道，诚无良法矣。胸中自有泾谓，皮里自具春秋。故穰穰而来，茕茕独往，交可以始终一也。不然，直欲尽化同人为异物，易济济为绥绥，有此理哉！

    　　兰岩曰：富贵则趋附之，贫贱则违避之，俗情概然，然曾无一人矫然独出，而仅让此狐。人而不如狐也，良可愧也。

    Explanation

    16

    闲斋曰：Humble customs are constantly changing and evolving, and maintaining friendships is not easy to express. When they are at their peak, people may seem to be friends in face and mouth, but they do not need to be invited to come; when they decline, they may be formally in agreement but spiritually at odds, and very few respond as expected. Apart from breaking rigid principles to fit in with others, there is no good method. One has clear distinctions in mind and hidden wisdom. Therefore, one may come crowded and leave lonely, and a friendship can remain consistent from beginning to end. Otherwise, one may want to completely transform ordinary people into strange beings, change a crowded scene into a peaceful one, is there such a thing?

    　　兰岩曰：When people are wealthy and powerful, they tend to approach them; when they are poor and lowly, they tend to avoid them. This is the common sentiment, but there is no one who stands out and acts differently, and only this fox is left. If people are not as clever as foxes, it is truly shameful.

    17

    碧碧周至诸生孙克复，流寓阶州。爱其地土腴永甘，卜筑山村，耕读自乐。屋左依山临壑，构一草阁，颇虚敞，可以眺远。阁下林深箐密，虽有一径，人迹罕经，仅过樵牧。

    　　一日，孙独凭阁上，远远见一人循径来，草笠布衫，仿佛甚美。既辨眉目，果然美甚，丹唇皓齿，华发素面，十七八一娈童也。孙骇曰：“世岂有男子而姣媚若此者乎？”急趋下阁，要遮而鞠之曰：“山深路僻，豺狼侁侁，小郎日暮孤行，进将安止？盍姑住此，明旦早行，庶不至旁观者代为忧虑。”少年曰：“夙非姻娅，生熟两不相谙，猎食或然，宿应不可。”孙素有断袖之癖，一旦值此璧人，欲情火炽，遽前拥之，少年大惊，曰：“奈何邂逅相遇，辄以横逆见加？”孙曰：“卿慧人也，何待解人！”少年惶遽，极力挤之，孙猝不及防，失足坠岩下。少年脱然去。

    Explanation

    17

    A Scholar Named Sun Kefu from Zhouzhi, who was living away from his hometown in Jiezhou, loved the fertile and sweet land there. He chose a mountain village for his residence, where he lived a life of farming and reading for personal enjoyment. To the left of his house, he built a grassy pavilion that was spacious and airy, perfect for looking far into the distance. Below the pavilion, the forest was dense and thick, with a single path rarely visited by people, only frequented by woodcutters and shepherds.

    One day, Sun Kefu stood alone on the pavilion, and from afar, he saw a man following the path. The man wore a straw hat and a simple robe, and he appeared very beautiful. As Sun Kefu got a closer look, he realized that the man was indeed exceptionally handsome, with red lips, white teeth, silver hair, and a pure face, looking to be around seventeen or eighteen years old. Sun Kefu was amazed and exclaimed, "How could there be a man so charming and graceful in the world?" He quickly descended from the pavilion and approached the man, saying, "The mountains are deep and the paths are remote, with many wild beasts lurking. How can a young man like you travel alone at dusk? Where will you go next? Why not stay here for the night? We can leave early tomorrow, so that others won't worry for you." The young man replied, "I am not related to you by marriage, and we are neither familiar nor unfamiliar with each other. I may hunt for food, but staying overnight is not possible." Sun Kefu, who had a homosexual inclination, was captivated by the young man's beauty and his desire flared up. He suddenly rushed forward to embrace the young man, who was greatly startled and said, "Why do you treat me so harshly after we meet by chance?" Sun Kefu said, "You are a wise person; why do you need someone to understand you?" The young man, frightened and anxious, pushed him away with great force. Sun Kefu, caught off guard, lost his footing and fell off a cliff. The young man then left abruptly.

    18

    孙为一树枝夹住，欲上不能，欲下不得，呼叫声嘶，无人知者，自拚必死。忽一女子，过而见之，讶曰：“如此阽危，何乐而为之？”孙曰：“为人所算耳！能救我否？”女曰：“救亦非难，第未识何以报德？”孙曰：“除却再夹树枝，余悉唯命。”女吃吃笑，解足缠抛于一端，援之而上。孙良久神定，整衣谢之。女徐徐束足，了不见答。孙方怪其倨，审谛之，则苗条婉妙，绝代美姝也。不觉缩颈吐舌，且惊且喜，阴念何今日奇遇之多也。

    　　时日已薄崦嵫，四山渐暝，乃再拜而请曰：“再生之德，未易仓猝图报，幸小住为佳。”女笑而睨之曰：“子大不良善，甫得生机，又造死业矣。”孙听其言谑，窥其意厚，大不似少年漠不关心者，遂携入阁，缱绻备至。约三更，女披衣起，曰：“今夕与人约，须践之，翌日重晤。”孙阻之以臂曰：“卜夜未卜昼。”复留与乱。因诘：“卿孱弱处子，虽乘以油壁，舁以笋舆，犹恐不胜劳瘁。

    18

    Sun was caught by a branch of a tree, unable to go up and unable to go down, shouting until his voice was hoarse, but no one knew about it, ready to die. Suddenly, a woman passed by and saw him, surprised and said, "What is the fun in being in such danger?" Sun replied, "I've been tricked by someone! Can you save me?" The woman said, "Saving is not difficult, but I don't know how to repay your kindness." Sun said, "Except for being caught in the tree again, everything else is at your command." The woman laughed softly, untying and throwing her foot bindings to one side, and pulled him up. After a long time, Sun's mind calmed down, straightening his clothes and thanking her. The woman slowly tied up her feet, without any further response. Sun found her attitude arrogant, looking closely, she was slender and graceful, a beauty of unparalleled beauty. He couldn't help but retract his neck and stick out his tongue, both surprised and happy, thinking to himself how many strange encounters he had today!

    At that time, the sun had set behind the mountain, and the mountains were gradually getting dark. He bowed twice and asked, "The virtue of saving a life is not easy to repay immediately. It would be better if you stayed a little longer." The woman laughed and looked sideways at him, saying, "You are very unkind. Just regained life, you are creating a new disaster." Sun listened to her joking words, saw that her intentions were kind, very unlike a young person who doesn't care, so he took her into the room, spending a lot of time together. Around midnight, the woman put on her clothes and got up, saying, "I have an appointment with someone tonight. I must keep it. We will meet again tomorrow." Sun blocked her with his arm, saying, "I don't know when I will see you again, but I hope it's not during the day." He held her back and they spent more time together. He then asked, "You are a weak young woman. Even if you ride in a luxurious carriage or are carried in a bamboo sedan chair, you might still be too weak to handle the fatigue."

    19

    底事单形只身，远陟空山，令人弥思弥惧，中心能无稍怖乎？”女自言：“宓氏，字碧碧，年十八，嫁前村方氏子，半年而寡。今日为母寿归家，来此捷径，不意遇子，不能自贞。诚夙份也，愿与子偕老。俾茕嫠有托，莫见弃否？”孙愀然曰：“得卿为之，小可何修哉！但碍有老母，赋性方严，出入小闲，尚须咨白。不告而娶，实不敢专。然而父母爱子，何必苛求。见卿可人，应无不纳。容徐图之。”女曰：“儿于子亦非无益者。子果肯降心相从，始终不二，则可以全性命，了死生。夜气之牿亡，旦夕可复。俾子蜕蜣丸而为蛨，化腐草而为夜光，必当同为人极之游，不复羁滞形骸，听阎摩罗什天尊为政矣。”孙大喜，相见恨晚。

    19

    What matter that I am alone, venturing far into an empty mountain, makes one think and fear more, how can one's heart not be slightly terrified?" The woman spoke to herself: "I am Mi, courtesy name Bi, aged eighteen, married to a man from the village named Fang, widowed after half a year. Today I am returning home to celebrate my mother's birthday, coming by this shortcut, unexpectedly meeting you, unable to remain chaste. Truly a predestined fate, I wish to grow old together with you. To give the lonely widow a place to rely on, will you not reject me?" Sun replied gloomily: "If you agree to it, what merit do I have! But having an elderly mother, with a strict and upright nature, for small matters of going out and coming in, still need to consult and inform. To marry without informing, I really dare not act alone. However, parents love their children, why be so demanding. Seeing you is pleasing, should not be rejected. Let me consider it carefully." The woman said: "I am not without benefit to you. If you truly consent to follow me, always faithful, then can preserve life, understand life and death. The loss of the night's energy, can be restored in a short time. Transform from a dung beetle into a cicada, transform from decaying grass into a night light, must travel together to the highest level of human existence, no longer bound by physical form, to be governed by the ruler of the underworld." Sun was greatly pleased, regretting meeting too late.

    20

    晨兴，即以告母。母呼女至前，反复详讯，乃谓孙曰：“儿勿草草，吾闻颜朱眸绿，尤物蛊人，倾万乘之国尚有余，祸匹夫之身庸有不足？老身七十矣，所见闺秀何啻千万，至若此之穷妖极艳，一见炫人心目者，实为乍睹，真祸水也。汝何德以堪之？且夭方氏之子，不祥孰甚？可急遣之，勿速死亡。”孙默然鹄立，面如死灰。女进曰：“姑之见亦左矣。儿非自媒才，诚以蘖苦不如荠甘，故腆颜自荐，儿不厌郎贫，姑奈何畏儿蛊乎？”母曰：“不然，小娘恋新欢，忘旧好，钟情者固不得不然。而老妇为豚犬作马牛，用心亦不得不尔。”女勃然怒曰：“何物老妪，酖毒若此！儿去此，岂便无啖饭处也！”且斥孙曰：“君木偶人，不足与语。不听好言，不久当死。穷薄相，即死亦为下鬼。彼时当袖手高坐于刀山剑树之旁，看汝挣扎耳！”遂愤愤出门，不知所之。

    20

    Early in the morning, he immediately told his mother. The mother called the daughter to the front and repeatedly questioned her in detail. She then said to Sun, "Do not be hasty. I have heard that those with red lips and green eyes are extraordinary beauties who can bewitch people, capable of overthrowing a state with a thousand chariots and still having more, causing harm to an ordinary person is certainly enough. I am seventy years old, and the number of women I have seen is no less than ten thousand. As for this kind of extreme beauty, one who dazzles the eyes upon first sight, it is truly a sight to behold, a water of disaster. What virtue do you have to bear it? And the son of the young widow, what could be more unlucky? You should quickly dismiss her, to avoid hastening your death." Sun stood silently like a heron, his face ashen. The daughter stepped forward and said, "Your opinion is also wrong. I did not come here to boast of my talents; it is true that the bitterness of brambles is not as sweet as the sweetness of shepherd's purse, so I boldly recommend myself. I do not mind your poverty, why should you fear that I will bewitch him?" The mother replied, "Not so, the young woman is infatuated with her new love and forgets her old affection, those who are deeply in love cannot help but act this way. But the old woman has to serve as a horse or ox for the pig and dog, my heart cannot help but do so." The daughter suddenly became angry and said, "What kind of old woman, poisonous like this! If I leave, will I have nowhere to eat!" She also scolded Sun, "You are like a wooden puppet, not worth talking to. If you do not listen to good advice, you will die soon. Your poor appearance, even if you die, will make you a lowly ghost. At that time, I will sit idly by the side of the mountain of knives and the tree of swords, watching you struggle!" She then left angrily, not knowing where she went.

    21

    孙涕泪纵横，颇形怨色。母慰之曰：“天下多美妇人，何必是？况深山穷谷，忽至丽人，非草木之妖，必狐鬼之怪，儿倘或迷惑不悟，冥想至邪，则老身将谁赖乎？”开喻再三，孙意少解。

    　　居无何，有翁媪二人，率男妇六七辈，直入草堂，汹汹叫骂。孙甫出讯，辄遭扭结。翁以杖叩孙之背曰：“跌落涧下，与死为邻，苟非吾女援手救，则山中鸦鹊饱汝肠胃久矣。今则弃捐吾女，抑何竟负恩而背本乎？”孙蓦然值此，色变气沮，不能发一言。家人咸集，莫能解纷。孙母乃策杖出，曰：“无哗，有事不妨好议。”媪曰：“亲母出矣。亲母之发，如此种种，底事出言无度，致小女归去，愤懑不餐。脱有不韪，亲母之肉，岂足食乎？”孙母始知即女之父母也。阴念来势凶猛，必将选事，不如姑却以婉词。方启齿，媪即止之，曰：“勿多言，可即垩壁除庭，明日即送鱼轩到门矣。”遽释孙，纷然而散。

    Explanation

    21

    The son's tears streamed down, and he showed a look of resentment. His mother consoled him, saying, "There are many beautiful women in the world; why must it be her? Moreover, in the deep mountains and remote valleys, if a beautiful woman suddenly appears, she is either a spirit of plants and trees or a fox or ghost. If you are deceived and fail to realize it, indulging in evil thoughts, then who will rely on me?" She repeatedly explained and advised him, and the son's resentment gradually eased.

    Not long after, two elderly men and women, accompanied by six or seven other men and women, stormed into the thatched cottage, shouting angrily. As soon as the son stepped out to inquire, they seized him. The old man struck the son on the back with a stick and said, "You nearly fell into the ravine and faced death. If it were not for my daughter's timely rescue, the crows and magpies in the mountains would have long since eaten your insides. Now you abandon my daughter, how can you betray her kindness and forget your roots?" The son suddenly found himself in this situation, his face changing and his spirit dampened; he could not utter a word. The family gathered, but none could resolve the dispute. The son's mother then stepped out with a cane and said, "Do not make a fuss; if there is something, we can discuss it calmly." The old woman said, "The mother has come out. Her hair is like this, and she speaks without measure, causing my daughter to return home, angry and not eating. If there is any wrongdoing, how can the flesh of my own mother be eaten?" The son's mother then realized that they were the parents of the woman. Secretly thinking, she considered their approach to be fierce, and they would likely take drastic action. It would be better to temporarily retreat using gentle words. As she was about to speak, the old woman stopped her and said, "Do not say more. You can whitewash the walls and clean the courtyard, and we will send a carriage to your door tomorrow." They immediately released the son, and they dispersed in disorder.

    22

    母谓孙曰：“视此行径，愈信为妖物矣。从来邪不犯正，尔心果守正，不难一麾而却也。”议已定，戒备以待。次日黎明，翁媪已送女至，鼓吹之谊，妆奁之盛，仆婢之多，内外填塞皆满。孙颇韵羡。母以扊扅撑宅门，隔阖大言曰：“吾家门庭，自来清肃，无故来挠，能不自愧？可速退，无自取辱。”翁媪怒发曰：“怜汝家中纷纭，无执干者，故不惜爱女送来伺奉。胡为强自高，其谓我缩领曲背，不能剚刃于老虔婆之腹中耶？”于是飞砖掷砾，攻击久之。母终置若罔闻。翁媪亦觉索然，但发恨声，曰：“且去休！且去休！自有设施在后。”因复散去。

    Explanation

    22

    The mother told her son, "Seeing this behavior, one becomes more convinced that it is a demon. Evil cannot harm the righteous. If your heart truly adheres to righteousness, it will not be difficult to repel them with a single gesture." Having made up her mind, she prepared for defense. The next morning at dawn, the old man and woman had already brought their daughter, accompanied by the ceremonial music and processions, the splendid bridal trousseau, and a large number of servants, filling the house both inside and out. The son was quite envious. The mother propped open the house door with a door bar and spoke loudly through the door, saying, "Our family's courtyard has always been clean and orderly. How could you come without cause to disturb us? You can quickly retreat and not bring shame upon yourselves." The old man and woman, filled with anger, said, "Pity your household is in chaos, with no one to wield the spear. That is why we did not spare the love for our daughter to send her to serve. Why do you pretend to be high and mighty? Do you think we are so weak that we cannot stab the old hag in the belly?" They then threw bricks and stones, attacking for a long time. The mother remained calm and ignored them completely. The old man and woman also felt disappointed and merely muttered in anger, saying, "Go away! Go away! We have plans for later." And then they dispersed.

    23

    村人知其事，传以为怪，二三齿德来说孙母曰：“吾村地僻人稀，守望之助不给。宅上孤儿寡妇，辄与异类为敌，执迂见以取寇仇，非所以计万全也。此间旧有狐仙村，人往往见之，然而未尝为患。兹来相挠者，为狐无疑。奉狐者，或与交游，或为姻戚，自古有之，无足为怪。令郎神气不凡，即娶狐妻，应不致祸。莫若姑听之，以解目前之害，不亦可乎？否则结怨既深，则为祟必亟，恐贤母子不能安枕而卧也。”孙亦几谏其母，母不得已，从之。是夕，翁媪复送女来，愉悦之色可鞠。若预知母有俯就之意者，成礼而返。

    Explanation

    23

    The villagers knew of the matter and spread it around as a strange occurrence. A few elders with wisdom came to speak to Grandmother Sun, saying: "Our village is remote and sparsely populated, and the help for mutual watchfulness is insufficient. An orphaned widow in our village often fights against beings of different kinds, holding onto outdated views to bring about enemies, which is not the way to ensure safety. There used to be a village of fox spirits here, and people often see them, but they have never caused harm. Those who are causing trouble now are undoubtedly fox spirits. Those who respect fox spirits may be friends or relatives with them, and this has existed since ancient times, so there is no need to find it strange. Your son has an extraordinary presence; if he marries a fox wife, it should not bring disaster. It would be better to let it go for now to resolve the current harm, wouldn't it? Otherwise, if the resentment deepens, the fox spirits will surely cause more harm, and I fear that you and your son will not be able to sleep peacefully." Grandson Sun also gently advised his mother, but she, having no choice, agreed. That evening, the old man and woman came again to bring their daughter, their happy expressions evident. As if they had already known that Grandmother Sun was willing to agree, they completed the ceremony and returned.

    24

    孙及女逑好甚敦，女事母亦极婉顺。日用所需，随念而至，一家大享坐食之福。

    　　女一日谓孙曰：“今日有君之内侄来，须自检束，勿贻后悔。”孙曰：“我之内侄，卿之犹子也，长幼自有各分，何检束之有？”既来，非他，正曩日挤身岩下之姣童也。孙大骇，回念前事，深自局促。而少年谈笑自若，毫不介意。孙始而安之，既而昵之，已而渐生狎亵，觑隙骤接其吻，少年惊怒曰：“狂奴故态，一毫未悛，岂有作人尊长而不自庄重如是者哉！”复力挤之，踣于案下，少年怫然而去。女至，见之，忿恨良久，徐乃叹曰：“徒费周张，酸子尚足与言性命事哉！”遂不辞而行。一切器物，不见人取携，一霎化为乌有。孙与少年接吻时，觉异香入脑，衣上亦有香气，数日不散，渐归两腋，遂患愠羝，终身不瘥。

    Explanation

    24

    The marriage between Sun and his wife was very harmonious. The daughter-in-law treated her mother-in-law with great grace and compliance. Daily necessities came as needed, and the whole family enjoyed the benefit of being well-fed without effort.

    One day, the wife told Sun, "Today, your nephew will come. You must restrain yourself and not cause regret." Sun replied, "My nephew is your nephew, and the roles of the older and younger generations are naturally distinct. What need is there to restrain myself?" When the youth arrived, he was none other than the handsome boy who had pushed him into the rock before. Sun was greatly startled and recalled the past incident, feeling deeply uneasy. However, the youth talked and laughed as if nothing had happened, showing no concern.

    Initially, Sun felt at ease, but soon he became close to the youth. Gradually, he began to act inappropriately, trying to kiss the youth. The youth was shocked and angry, saying, "You are still the same as before, not the slightest change. How can someone who is a respected elder behave so unrespectably?" He then pushed Sun, causing him to fall to the ground. The youth left in anger.

    When the wife returned, she saw the situation and was furious for a long time. She then sighed, "All this effort was wasted. This disagreeable youth is not worth talking to about matters of life and death." She left without saying goodbye. Everything in the room disappeared instantly, as if it had never been there. When Sun tried to kiss the youth, he felt an unusual fragrance in his head, and the scent lingered on his clothes for several days. It eventually spread to his armpits, and he developed a condition that never recovered.

    25

    闲斋曰：“狐性本淫，无足怪者。老狐何所图，而必欲以女嫁孙，以成其私奔之志，岂亦爱忘其丑，若知子恶之故欤？然女固不贞，而男又何洁也？是知世之好为龙阳、以巾帼自甘者，虽雄狐之不若矣！”

    　　兰岩曰：断袖之癖，人或有不免者，独怪孙生，始以轻薄致坠岩下，甫得救援，复生痴想，即有如此立志送女与人之老狐。得以大享坐食之福，亦至幸矣。乃于正宜庄重自持之时，忽尔故态复萌，顿忘愧悔，亦可谓不足有为者矣。卒为狐辱骂，而素所钟爱者亦弃之而去。身患恶疾，何以为人哉！丈夫也，而见鄙于妻子，已足羞矣，况异类耶？

    　　梨花京师时雍坊，有以十岁女来鬻者，孝廉舒树堂以钱三十千得之，命名梨花。既长，艳丽无匹，淡汝浓抹，靡不相宜。小草闲花，随意簪之，皆堪入画。诸女眷效之，百不一逮也。性且慧黠，一家怜爱之。

    Explanation

    25

    闲斋说：“狐狸的本性就是好色，这没什么可奇怪的。老狐狸有什么图谋，一定要把女儿嫁给孙某，以实现自己私奔的心愿，难道也是因为爱而忘记了丑陋，还是因为知道孙某厌恶的原因吗？然而女儿本来就不贞洁，而男子又有什么干净的呢？由此可知，世上那些喜欢成为龙阳、甘愿穿上女装的人，连雄狐狸都不如啊！”

    　　兰岩说：断袖之癖，人或许难以避免，只是奇怪孙某，最初因为轻浮导致自己掉入深渊，刚刚得救，又生出痴心妄想，即使有这样决心将女儿嫁给别人的狐狸。能够大享坐食之福，也是幸运得很了。然而在应该庄重自持的时候，突然旧习复萌，顿时忘记愧疚和悔恨，也可以说是没有什么作为的人了。最终被狐狸辱骂，而一直钟爱的人也抛弃了他。身患重病，还能算是人吗！作为丈夫，却被妻子和孩子鄙视，已经足够羞耻了，何况还是外人呢？

    　　梨花在京师时雍坊，有一个十岁女孩被卖来，孝廉舒树堂花了三十千钱买下她，给她取名梨花。长大后，她美丽无比，淡妆浓抹，无不适宜。随意插上小草闲花，都足以入画。其他女眷效仿她，却无法达到她的水平。她不仅聪明伶俐，全家人都十分喜爱她。

    26

    舒有女，幼字先达德公次子。及出阁，舒以二女奴为媵，梨花与焉。其一名春棠，亦可儿之殊色者。舒女则偏爱梨花，而公子待之尤厚。屡欲私之，奈梨花防维綦密，虽欲申以游语，亦不可得。会德公考满，擢粤西某郡守，携眷南行。予友恩茂先，与德舒二公，皆亲戚也，荐金华尚介夫入德公幕。阅三载，公迁粤东监司。冬十一月，介夫因事入都，委装茂先家，朝夕晤对，所在人情风土并德公家事，在所必谈。偶询及梨花，则曰：“司宅门久矣。”茂先曰：“言梨花耶？”介夫曰：“正所谓梨花也。”曰：“然则何云司宅门？”介夫曰：“梨花之事，新奇怪异，骇人听闻久矣。君为德府至戚，岂尚未知耶？”茂先愕然，急叩其详，介夫曰：“此下酒物也，不可浪言。”乃拨火煮酒，拥炉促膝，备述其事。茂先攸而惊，倏而笑，倏而咋舌，倏而拊髀，盖事既新奇，又介夫善为戏谑，故不能不为之色飞肉动也。

    Explanation

    26

    Shu had a daughter who was betrothed to the second son of a respected scholar named De Gong when she was young. When she married, Shu provided two female servants as concubines, and Li Hua was among them. One of the servants was named Chun Tang, who was also considered a beautiful woman. Shu's daughter favored Li Hua, and De Gong treated her especially well. He often tried to seduce her, but Li Hua was very cautious, and he could not succeed in his attempts to win her over.

    When De Gong completed his term of office and was promoted to the position of governor of a certain county in Guangxi Province, he took his family with him and traveled south. My friend En Maoxian, who was related to both De Gong and Shu Gong, recommended Shang Jiefu to join De Gong's entourage. After three years, De Gong was further promoted to a high-ranking official in Guangdong Province. In November of that year, Shang Jiefu had some business in the capital and entrusted his belongings to En Maoxian's home. They met frequently and had intimate conversations, during which they discussed local customs, geography, and family matters related to De Gong.

    One day, En Maoxian asked Shang Jiefu about Li Hua. Shang Jiefu replied, "She is now guarding the gate." En Maoxian was surprised and asked, "Are you talking about Li Hua?" Shang Jiefu confirmed, "Yes, I am talking about Li Hua." En Maoxian then asked, "What do you mean by 'guarding the gate'?" Shang Jiefu explained, "The story of Li Hua is strange, unusual, and has been widely discussed for a long time. You are a close relative of De Gong's family, so you must have heard about it."

    En Maoxian was startled and quickly asked for more details. Shang Jiefu said, "This is a topic worth discussing over drinks, but I won't speak lightly about it." He then lit the fire, heated the wine, and sat by the stove, ready to tell the story. As he began to recount the events, En Maoxian's emotions shifted rapidly: he was suddenly surprised, then laughed, then gasped, and finally patted his thigh. The story was so new and strange that it was hard for him to remain calm, especially since Shang Jiefu was skilled at telling jokes and making the narrative entertaining.

    27

    先是德公之任粤西也，目张家湾买四舟，公与夫人居一，介夫居一，仆从居一为庖厨，其一则公子夫妇及梨花、春棠也。行则鱼贯，泊则雁排。一日，暮宿吴城，月明如昼。介夫苦热，五更，复起纳凉，彼时群动昼息，忽闻第三船有款款启窗声。疑为暴客，潜起窥之，见一女子出船边，立而溺。虽隔两船，而月光朗映，阳具仿佛甚伟。审谛女子，则梨花也，心窃异之。第念梨花十岁至舒家，此时年十八，昔在茂先处，识之最熟，讵有假借？顾船是公子之船，人是梨花之人，而阳具则又居然阳具也。此疑团终难打破。

    Explanation

    27

    Previously, when Governor De took office in Guangxi, he set his sights on Zhangjiawan and bought four boats. One boat was for the governor and his wife, another for the intermediary, a third for the servants and kitchen staff, and the fourth for the young master, his wife, and Li Hua and Chun Tang. When traveling, they moved in a line like fish; when moored, they arranged themselves in a formation like geese. One day, as they moored for the night in Wu City, the moon was so bright it was like daytime. The intermediary, suffering from the heat, got up at the fifth watch to cool off. At that time, all was quiet, and suddenly he heard a gentle sound of a window opening from the third boat. Suspecting it was a bandit, he quietly got up to look and saw a woman step out of the boat and stand by the water's edge, where she urinated. Although separated by two boats, the moonlight clearly illuminated, and the penis appeared remarkably large. Upon careful observation, it was Li Hua, and he was secretly surprised. But considering that Li Hua had been in the Shu household since she was ten years old, and was now eighteen, and that he had known her best when she was in the Maodaxian household, could it be a deception? Considering the boat belonged to the young master, the person was Li Hua, and the penis was indeed a penis. This mystery remains unsolved.

    28

    次日晨餐罢，冥测于舱中。公有老仆张姓，独坐桅舱，喟然兴叹，自自讼曰：“行年六十，不为小矣，何见所未见之事，总无了休也！”介夫怪而诘之，张曰：“稚子康儿，年小而诡大；丫头梨花，人雌而声雄。此吾之所不解也。”介夫曰：“汝老成谙练人也，予有所疑，质之于子可乎？”张问是何疑事，试言之。介夫视无人，低语夜来所见，张闻之，惊曰：“吾固疑之矣。何不白诸吾主？”介夫曰：“意欲白之，但自念作客依人，不宜预人闺阃，故默默耳。”张曰：“噫！是何言也？先生不早言，异事出矣！”介夫曰：“予意先白公子，何如？”张曰：“然，吾即往告之。

    Explanation

    28

    The next morning, after breakfast, the translator pondered deeply in the cabin. There was an elderly servant named Zhang, who sat alone in the cabin near the mast, sighing and lamenting, speaking to himself: "Having reached sixty years of age, I am not young anymore. Why do I keep seeing things never seen before, and it never ends?" The translator, curious, asked Zhang for advice. Zhang asked what the doubt was and listened to the translator's explanation. The translator mentioned what was seen last night, and Zhang was shocked, saying, "I have suspected something. Why don't you inform my master?" The translator replied, "I intended to, but I thought being a guest and relying on others, it is not suitable to involve myself in private matters, so I remained silent." Zhang said, "Ah, what a foolish thing to say! You should have told me earlier. Strange events have occurred!" The translator said, "I thought I would inform the young noble first, what do you think?" Zhang replied, "Yes, I will go and inform him immediately."

    29

    ”是夕舟泊青山，张请间，谓公子曰：“二爷知家中有妖怪乎？”公子笑曰：“何作此语？”张曰：“妖怪不远，只在二爷船上。”且因耳语其故。公子大骇，入船隐叩细君。细君结舌瞠目，良久乃叹曰：“怪底守身如处子，且十八九岁，天癸未至，今若此，复何疑哉！”公子呼梨花诘之，赧然不应。公子闭门验之，梨花极力抵拒。公子乘隙探手胯间，则垂垂者已触指翘翘矣。公子大怒，缚而献诸公，公不胜错愕，作威以究其原，刑具排列左右。梨花大惧，始涕泣吐实，曰：“曩岁迫于饥寒，父母鬻子谋朝夕，是时女价十倍于男，故作此弊，以求多售。今既败露，罪当九死。第自反未为非法，祈全蝼蚁之命，当图衔结之报耳。”公怜其情，且辨其果系童身，竟曲宥之，并命剃发改妆，更名珠还，以志其异。举舟之人，莫不叹异。

    Explanation

    29

    On that night, the boat anchored at Qing Shan. Zhang asked for a private audience and said to the young man, "Do you know there is a demon in our family?" The young man laughed and said, "Why do you say such things?" Zhang replied, "The demon is not far; it is on your boat." He then whispered the reason in his ear. The young man was greatly alarmed and went into the boat to secretly question his wife. The wife was stunned and speechless, and after a long while, she sighed and said, "It's strange that she has kept herself like a maiden, and at eighteen or nineteen years old, her menstruation has not yet started, yet she is like this now. What else can be doubted?" The young man called for Li Hua and questioned her. She blushed and did not respond. The young man closed the door and examined her. Li Hua struggled to resist. The young man took the opportunity to probe her, and the hanging thing touched his finger and was already erect. The young man was greatly angry and tied her up, presenting her to the master. The master was greatly surprised and confused, using authority to investigate the cause, arranging torture instruments on both sides. Li Hua was greatly afraid and began to cry, confessing the truth. She said, "In the past, forced by hunger and cold, my parents sold me to make ends meet. At that time, the price of a girl was ten times that of a boy, so they did this to sell me for more money. Now that it has been exposed, I deserve to die nine deaths. But I reflect that I have not done anything illegal. I beg to save my life, and I will repay you with gratitude." The master pitied her situation and confirmed that she was indeed a virgin. Ultimately, he pardoned her, also ordering her to shave her head, change her clothes, and rename her Zhu Huan to commemorate this unusual event. All the people on the boat were amazed.

    30

    公复使送介夫验之，并折简晰之曰：“不意奇闻创见之事，出自本衙。所谓梨花，果桑茂之流亚也。幸童身如故，庶免株连。兹送其人至，请先生相之。所以必欲先生相之者，非谓魑魅魍魉，不能逃于秦鉴，盖欲先生解惑。倘异日举以告人，赖此解嘲，勿致东西南北之人，归德某以帏薄不修之罪也。”介夫笑而验之，戏语梨花曰：“勿怪南人多事，吾乡风俗，雄者可雌之，今子雌而化雄，正阳长阴消之候。予之有施于子，不可谓不厚矣。

    Explanation

    30

    The official again sent someone to send Jiefu to inspect it, and wrote a brief note explaining it, saying: "Unexpectingly, a strange and unprecedented event came from this office. What is called Li Hua is a kind of fruit tree similar to Sang Mao. Fortunately, his body remains unchanged, so as to avoid being implicated. Now I send the person here, please examine him, Master. The reason why I insist on having you examine him is not because ghosts and monsters cannot escape from the mirror of Qin, but to have you resolve my doubts. If one day I tell others about it, relying on this to relieve embarrassment, so as not to cause people from all directions to blame me for the fault of improper conduct." Jiefu smiled and inspected him, playfully telling Li Hua: "Do not be surprised that the people of the south are busy. In our hometown, those who are male can become female, and now you have transformed from female to male, which is the time when yang grows and yin declines. My influence on you cannot be said to be insignificant."

    31

    异日将何以报不彀乎？”梨花面　颈赤，羞涩莫容。介夫赠以双履及香扇，报公书曰：“儒生眼界不广，赖珠还以扩充之，亦南行之幸事也。童体的确，尤足感甚，非公至德，畴其能之？是知事不足怪，可怪者，见怪之不怪也。”公见书大笑。至任所，以其颖慧，命司宅门，颇能了当，公宠爱殊甚。张仆无子，公使认为假子，且以春棠妻之。公子固少年好事者，于花烛之夕，隐身窗外窥之，谓绰约灯下，绝妙一幅折枝图也。今已抱子矣。

    　　茂先神驰者一晌，又问：“龙阳君伎俩，介夫亦当识之否？”介夫笑曰：“其人方雄，君又欲雌之也。”相与拊掌而罢。茂先作《梨花开》四绝，寄示公子，有“一树梨花压海棠”之句，用成句恰妙。公子和韵报之，诗不具载。

    Explanation

    31

    In the future, how will you repay me?" The woman with the pear blossom-like face and a flushed neck was shy and could not bear it. Jiefu gave her a pair of shoes and a scented fan, and replied in a letter: "The scholar's vision is not broad, but relying on the return of the pearl to expand it, it is also a fortunate event to travel south. The child's body is indeed remarkable, especially touching, without your utmost virtue, who could achieve it? It is known that the matter is not strange, what is strange is seeing the strange as not strange." The master laughed upon seeing the letter. Upon arriving at his post, he appointed her to manage the gate due to her keen intelligence, and she was quite capable, greatly favored by the master. Zhang the servant had no children, so the master adopted her as a foster daughter and married her to Chun Tang. The young master, being a young man fond of such matters, hid behind the window on the wedding night to spy on her, saying she was graceful under the lamp, a truly exquisite depiction of a branch of pear blossoms. She is now holding a child.

    Maoxian was distracted for a moment, then asked, "Does Jiefu know the tricks of the Dragon Yang Jun?" Jiefu smiled and said, "That person is very strong, and you want to make him weak." They clapped hands together and stopped. Maoxian composed four quatrains titled "Pear Blossoms Bloom," sending them to the young master, which included the line "a tree of pear blossoms pressing down the peony," using the phrase perfectly. The young master responded with a poem in the same rhyme scheme, but the poem is not recorded here.

    32

    闲斋曰：梨花假女妆而守贞如处子，如其果女子，必非淫乱者，其得拥美妻，获厚利，去祸而就福也，固宜。

    　　兰岩曰：假女则艳丽无匹，还男则事事精当，梨花诚奇人也。尝见司宅门者，袖金入橐，茫然不解一事者多矣，几何不对梨花而愧死！

    　　香云零陵乔氏子，少孤贫，失业，依外舅为操舟，尝往来于襄汉间。会载数估客下荆门，过黄金峡。滩险，日暮不敢发，泊舟古戍前。舅命乔入山伐竹，迷不得出，傍徨殊甚，瞥见一媪，年约七旬，杖藜蹩躄，循山径而西。乔追上之，问何处可达江岸。媪笑曰：“江在东，郎向西，乖迂极矣。吾视郎嫩少年也，日暮途穷，虎狼将盛，欲归可乎？姑宿我家，明日晓发可矣。”乔心悸已久，闻言窃喜，佯以不便造次为辞。媪挽之行，曰：“言不由衷，令人倦听。”

    Explanation

    32

    Translation:

    闲斋曰：梨花假女妆而守贞如处子，如其果女子，必非淫乱者，其得拥美妻，获厚利，去祸而就福也，固宜。

    Translation: Xianzai said: Li Hua, posing as a woman and maintaining chastity like an unmarried girl, if she were indeed a woman, she would certainly not be promiscuous. She would be able to possess a beautiful wife, gain substantial benefits, and avoid misfortune while achieving happiness, which is indeed appropriate.

    　　兰岩曰：假女则艳丽无匹，还男则事事精当，梨花诚奇人也。尝见司宅门者，袖金入橐，茫然不解一事者多矣，几何不对梨花而愧死！

    Translation: Lanyan said: Posing as a woman, she is extremely beautiful; returning to being a man, she is precise and appropriate in all things. Li Hua is truly a remarkable person. I have seen those who manage the gates, with gold in their sleeves, many of whom are confused and do not understand anything. How many would not die of shame before Li Hua!

    　　香云零陵乔氏子，少孤贫，失业，依外舅为操舟，尝往来于襄汉间。会载数估客下荆门，过黄金峡。滩险，日暮不敢发，泊舟古戍前。舅命乔入山伐竹，迷不得出，傍徨殊甚，瞥见一媪，年约七旬，杖藜蹩躄，循山径而西。乔追上之，问何处可达江岸。媪笑曰：“江在东，郎向西，乖迂极矣。吾视郎嫩少年也，日暮途穷，虎狼将盛，欲归可乎？姑宿我家，明日晓发可矣。”乔心悸已久，闻言窃喜，佯以不便造次为辞。媪挽之行，曰：“言不由衷，令人倦听。”

    Translation: Xiangyun, a son of the Qiao family from Lingling, was young, orphaned, poor, and unemployed. Relying on his father-in-law to operate boats, he often traveled between Xiang and Han. Once, he happened to be transporting several merchants to Jingmen, passing through the Golden Gorge. The rapids were dangerous, and it was too late to depart, so he moored the boat in front of an ancient fort. His father-in-law ordered Qiao to go into the mountains to cut bamboo. Becoming lost and unable to find the way out, he wandered around very much, suddenly seeing an old woman about seventy years old, using a walking stick and limping, walking along the mountain path to the west. Qiao caught up with her and asked where one could reach the riverbank. The old woman laughed and said: "The river is to the east, you are going west, you are extremely foolish. I see you are a young man. It is late and the path is blocked, tigers and wolves will soon be out. Can you return? Stay at my house tonight, and you can leave early tomorrow." Qiao had been scared for a long time and secretly felt happy upon hearing it, pretending that it was inconvenient to act hastily. The old woman pulled him along and said: "Words not from the heart, making people tired of listening."

    33

    于是携入深山中，迤逦十余里。至其家，背高山，临巨涧，营窟而处。媪叩扉，呼香云，一女子出应，则二八佳丽人也。色茂开莲，香逾散麝，见客羞避。媪曰：“儿又作态耶？小郎失路至此，若无一盂胡麻饭以啖之，殊缺地主宜。且儿常常有嘱，既作承受人，讵可吝心力？今幸物色得此蕴藉郎，可息肩矣。”云益羞涩，避室中，不复出。媪笑向乔曰：“娇养惯，一见生客，辄作儿女态，幸郎无介意也。”乔谢不敢入室。室皆穴山为之，甚精洁。止三间，中一间为客坐，西一间垂墨花软帘，为云之闺闼，东一间起炉灶，具刀砧，庖厨也。纳乔坐，自入厨炊黍和羹，款洽臻至。问媪何姓，答以姓古，孀居十六年，止生一女，名香云，未字人。此居于此，今有缘与郎晤，奉屈暂就厨中宿矣。乔曰：“假一席地足矣，何敢望厨？”至夜分罢谈，乃宿焉。

    Explanation

    33

    He led her into the deep mountains, winding through more than ten miles. Upon reaching her home, it was situated back against a high mountain and facing a large ravine, with a cave or shelter as their dwelling. The old woman knocked on the door and called out to Xiang Yun, and a young woman answered. She was a beautiful maiden of sixteen or eighteen years old, her beauty like blooming lotus flowers, and her fragrance surpassing that of musk. She blushed and retreated upon seeing the guest. The old woman said, "Are you putting on an act again? The young gentleman has lost his way here; if we do not offer him a bowl of sesame porridge to eat, it would be lacking in the host's duty. And you often have instructions, having been entrusted with the responsibility, how can you be stingy with your efforts? Now, fortunately, we have found this refined young man, and we can rest our burdens." Xiang Yun became even more shy and retreated into the room, not coming out again. The old woman smiled at Qiao and said, "She is used to being pampered and acts like a child when she sees a stranger. Please do not take offense, young sir." Qiao thanked her and said he did not dare to enter the room. The rooms were all dug into the mountain, very clean and tidy. There were only three rooms: one in the middle for the guest, one on the west with a soft black flower curtain, which was Xiang Yun's boudoir, and one on the east with a stove, cutting board, and kitchen. She invited Qiao to sit, then went into the kitchen to cook millet porridge and soup, treating him with great hospitality. She asked Qiao what his surname was, and he replied that his surname was Gu. She told him she had been a widow for sixteen years and had only one daughter named Xiang Yun, who had not yet been married. She explained that this was their home, and now they had the good fortune to meet him, and she humbly invited him to stay in the kitchen for the night. Qiao said, "Offering me a place to stay is enough; how dare I expect anything more?" They talked until late at night and then went to bed.

    34

    翌日早起，请见古媪，将辞行。立帘外扬声致词，不应者良久。又言之，始闻香云应曰：“娘有事早出，想便回矣，请稍候。”其音清锐如雏莺之啭，听之生怜，乔诺诺默坐，神为之荡。

    　　居无何，忽见古与一媪一女，亦若母而女者，偕来，且扬言曰：“香云儿，汝杜姨同汝八妹来矣。”乔急避席拱立，不敢仰视。杜伫立审谛，向女郎曰：“果好一波俏郎！尔古姨真巨眼也。”女郎亦目之，含笑入室，谑云曰：“姊大无礼，娘为谁来，乃不出迓耶？”不闻云语，唯闻低笑声。杜寻亦入室，笑曰：“为甥女事，致我披星浥露来此，心急步迟，越山崖仄径，失足颠踬，几堕落上宅牛阹中，微汝妹顾扶，老身齑粉矣。汝将何以谢老身？”嗣闻云带笑小语，似候起居者。

    Explanation

    34

    The next morning, upon waking, Qiao requested an audience with the old woman, intending to take his leave. He stood outside the curtain, raising his voice to speak, but there was no response for a long time. He spoke again, and finally heard Xiang Yun respond, saying, "Mother has business and left early. She should be back soon. Please wait a moment." Her voice was clear and sharp, like the chirping of a young nightingale, and it sounded so pitiful. Qiao, in a state of uncertainty, sat silently, and his spirit was stirred.

    Not long after, suddenly saw Old Lady Gu with an old woman and a girl, who were also like a mother and daughter, come together. She said aloud, "Xiang Yun, your aunt Du and your eighth sister have come." Qiao hurriedly stood up, bowing respectfully, and dared not look up. Du stood still, examining carefully, and said to the young lady, "Indeed, a handsome young man! Your old aunt truly has good eyes." The young lady also looked at him, smiled, and entered the room, teasingly saying, "Sister, you are very rude. Mother came for whom, yet you didn't come out to greet her?" Did not hear what Xiang Yun said, only heard a low laugh. Du also entered the room, smiling, and said, "For the sake of my niece, caused me to come here in the starry night and dewy morning. Hurried heart, slow steps, crossed steep cliffs and narrow paths, lost footing and stumbled, almost fell into the upper courtyard's cow pen. If not for your sister's support, my old body would have been crushed. What will you do to thank me?" Later heard Xiang Yun speak in a low, smiling voice, as if greeting her mother.

    35

    杜旋出见乔，问曰：“郎尊姓？妙龄几何矣？”乔曰：“青年十九。”杜曰：“长二岁，正相当也。有父母兄弟否？”曰：“皆亡。”“娶乎？”曰：“未。”“业何事？”曰：“为舅操舟。”杜曰：“少年孤子，身可寄也。食力踝跣，业可弃也。主人古姥，老身之姊也，有女香云，老身之甥也，淑资丽质，郎已目睹，无更赘词。古姊唤老身作冰上人，欲赘郎为半子，能降格相从否？”乔骤聆之，阴喜过望，而口呐不能措一词。杜笑曰：“无可疑也。”亟请古媪上坐，令乔拜之曰：“即此是聘。山家无所忌，嫁衣完，便可成礼矣。”是夕欢饮而罢。

    Explanation

    35

    Du Xuan met Qiao outside and asked, "What is your honorable surname? How many years of youth do you have?" Qiao replied, "Nineteen." Du said, "Two years older, which is just right. Do you have parents or siblings?" Qiao said, "All have passed away." "Are you married?" "Not yet." "What is your occupation?" "I operate a boat for my uncle." Du said, "A young orphan, your body can be entrusted. Working hard with bare feet, your current occupation can be abandoned. Our host, the ancient old woman, is my aunt. She has a daughter named Xiang Yun, who is my niece. She has a beautiful and virtuous appearance, which you have already seen, so no need for further elaborate words. My aunt refers to me as the matchmaker, wanting to marry you as a stepson. Are you willing to lower your status to agree?" Qiao was secretly delighted beyond expectation but could not speak due to excitement. Du smiled and said, "There is no doubt." She quickly invited the old woman to sit and instructed Qiao to bow to her, saying, "This is the betrothal. In the mountain family, there are no taboos. Once the wedding clothes are ready, the ceremony can be performed." That evening, they drank joyfully and then parted.

    36

    次日杜归，留女伴香云，代制衣履。刀剪之声，终宵不绝，数日悉备。杜复至，张筵设宴，大会亲戚，来赴者接踵，尽属粉白黛绿，少妇老妪，而无一男子。欢笑哗然，竞为谐谑。更可异者，列筵十数，屋不更广，益不觉隘。既合卺，女郎把盏饮云曰：“杯儿双双，今夜作个新娘。”饮乔曰：“杯儿对对，今夜莫须死睡。”乔、云皆不禁失笑。杯未干，女郎曰：“此余酒将何以发付耶？”乃自饮之，笑而出。约三更，众客始散，女郎复启帘谓云曰：“姊好为之，三日来瞊时，再为我说项也。”言讫，吃吃笑而去。自是乔与云，鱼水其乐。胶漆其情，将谓终老是乡矣。

    Explanation

    36

    The next day, Du returned and left his female companion Xiangyun to make clothes and shoes. The sound of scissors and knives was unceasing throughout the night, and after several days, everything was ready. Du returned again, set up a feast, and gathered relatives. Those who came were so many that they followed one another like footprints, all young and old women, with no men present. They laughed and joked loudly, competing with each other in playful banter. More surprisingly, there were dozens of tables, but the house did not seem narrower; instead, it felt even less cramped. After the marriage ceremony, a young lady took a cup and said to Yun, "The cups are in pairs, tonight I will be a bride." Yun said to Qiao, "The cups are in pairs, tonight you don't need to stay awake." Qiao and Yun couldn't help but laugh. Before the cups were empty, the young lady said, "What should I do with the remaining wine?" She drank it herself, laughed, and left. Around the third watch, the guests began to disperse. The young lady opened the curtain and said to Yun, "Do well, sister. In three days, when we meet again, tell me more about it." After she finished speaking, she laughed softly and left. From then on, Qiao and Yun were as happy as fish and water, as close as glue and lacquer, and thought they would spend their old age together in this place.

    37

    逾月，古媪寝疾，杜携女郎来，候坐未安，忽有人传报上宅：“小娘子亲来问姆疾。”杜与女郎颇遑遽，急走出迓。云匿乔于厨，亦整衣趋。乔不知是何贵客，潜窥于窗。见朱茀绣　，驻一小车，女奴十余辈，拥一女子出自车中，素面画衣，非常艳丽，酷似画工所绘仙女，年可十五六。杜与女郎及云，咸跪路侧。女子曳杜起，曰：“姆亦在此耶？”杜曰：“知主姑眷念老乳妪，闻其疾，必劳玉趾，故率翠翠预候于此。”乔始知女郎名翠翠也。翠与云，亦再拜起居。

    Explanation

    37

    After a month, the elderly woman fell seriously ill. Resu brought the young lady to wait, but before they could settle, someone suddenly reported to the main residence that a young lady had come to inquire about the nurse's illness. Resu and the young lady, along with Yun, were quite startled and hurried out to meet her. Yun hid Qiao in the kitchen, and she quickly put on her clothes and hurried to join them. Qiao did not know who this distinguished guest was, so she secretly peeked through the window. She saw a red brocade embroidered robe, and a small carriage stopped. More than ten female servants surrounded a young woman who emerged from the carriage. She had a plain face and an embroidered robe, and she was exceptionally beautiful, resembling a fairy painted by an artist. She was about fifteen or sixteen years old. Resu, the young lady, and Yun all knelt on the side of the road. The young woman pulled Resu up and said, "Is the nurse here as well?" Resu replied, "Knowing that Madam is thinking of the old wet nurse, she heard of her illness and must have come herself. Therefore, I brought Cui Cui here to wait in advance." Qiao then realized that the young lady's name was Cui Cui. Cui Cui and Yun also bowed twice and stood up to greet her.

    38

    女子曰：“起。”云侧行左辟为导。女入室，握姑之手而问曰：“姆病户绮窗，广阔如大厦，几榻悉白石为之，器玩珍奇，位置精雅，名花异卉，罗置栏前，实天辟之洞天福地。侍女曳罗绮者，数十百人，莫不妖冶，顺承指顾，争先恐后。乔为禁锢，日供役使，且女子性严，稍不称意，辄施鞭扑。此间不乐，日思云而无由得面也。私询诸女，主姑与香云名分若何，皆笑而不答，愈滋疑惑。一日值女初度，乔见亲戚来拜祝者，咸执婢妾礼。杜、翠亦在，不敢复与乔语。

    Explanation

    38

    The woman said, "Please rise." She walked sideways to the left to lead the way. The woman entered the room and held the hand of the mother-in-law while asking, "The room is adorned with embroidered windows and doors, as spacious as a grand hall, with tables and beds all made of white stone. Rare and precious objects are arranged with exquisite elegance, and famous flowers and exotic plants are arranged in rows before the railing. This is truly a paradise on earth, a place of bliss. There are dozens or even hundreds of attendants wearing luxurious silk robes, all seductive and alluring, obediently following commands, and rushing forward eagerly. Qiao is confined here, daily subjected to labor, and the woman is strict; if anything does not meet her expectations, she immediately resorts to whipping or beating. I do not find joy here; I think of Yun every day but have no opportunity to meet her. I privately asked the women about the relationship between the mother-in-law and Xiangyun, but they all laughed and did not answer, which only deepened my doubts. On the occasion of the woman's birthday, Qiao saw relatives coming to pay their respects, all performing the duties of servants or concubines. Du and Cui were also present, and they were afraid to speak to Qiao again.

    39

    有顷，古媪与香云亦至，与乔相见，各泣数行下。女子出见之，怒曰：”淫婢逞媚，尚恋恋旧情耶？“令侍女褫其衣，缚之树上，既而曰：”今日有庆，不便刑人，俟明日当行死耳。“诸亲战栗，无敢出一语以求宽者。乔中心痛绝，前往觇之，云泣曰：”郎独不能舍身见救乎？“乔大痛，手缓其缚，窃取故衣衣之。适林外有将主姑命，呼乔者，云遂遁去。女侦知之，愈怒，鞭乔数十，血流被踵，古大哭曰：”主姑杀老身矣。

    Explanation

    39

    After a while, the elderly woman and Xiangyun also arrived, and they met with Qiao, each of them shedding tears in rows. The woman came out to see them and angrily said, "This lewd servant is showing off her charms, still clinging to old feelings?" She ordered the maid to strip off her clothes and tie her to a tree, then said, "Today there is a celebration, it is not convenient to punish her now, wait until tomorrow to carry out the execution." The relatives trembled with fear, none daring to speak a word to plead for mercy. Qiao was in excruciating pain in his heart, and he went to spy on her. Xiangyun wept and said, "Is there no way for you to sacrifice yourself to save me?" Qiao was greatly pained and loosened the bindings with his hands, secretly taking her old clothes to dress her. Just as someone outside the forest was summoning Qiao with the mistress's command, Xiangyun fled away. The woman found out about it and became even angrier, whipping Qiao dozens of times until his blood flowed down to his soles. The elderly woman wept bitterly, saying, "The mistress will kill me."

    40

    老身何负于主姑？乳哺之情纵不念，独不念扈十郎肆恶，老身横蔽主姑，以头撞十郎腹，夺取玉如意，免主姑于窘辱时乎？奈何不赦小过，致人骨肉生离！香云纤弱，即不饱狼虎，亦必为强暴所污矣，岂不痛哉！“女亦怒曰：”老魅尔何知！行且索尔死！“古哭叫，语侵女，亦不少让。女怒甚，复欲逐乔，乔折伏不起。女怜之，气稍平，问知过能改乎？”乔曰：“改矣。”“尚思香云否？”曰：“虽死九幽不忘也。”女不意其出此语，为之咋舌，移时乃叹曰：“痴儿郎知义者也。”向古媪慰谢再三，即使人分途求香云，得者赏一术。群女欢跃争往，古始止涕。

    Explanation

    40

    The old woman asked, "What have I done wrong to my master and mistress? Even if I do not remember the nurturing bond, do I not remember how Hu Shi Lang committed evil acts, and how I shielded the master and mistress by force, hitting him with my head and seizing the Jade如意 to save them from embarrassment? Why then should minor offenses not be forgiven, leading to family separation! Xiangyun is frail and delicate; if she is not devoured by wolves and tigers, she will surely be defiled by violence. Is this not a cause for great sorrow!" The daughter, enraged, said, "Old witch, what do you know! I will soon seek your death!" The old woman cried and called out, her words laced with insults, and she did not yield at all. The daughter, even more angry, tried again to expel the old woman, but the old woman refused to submit. Moved by pity, the daughter's anger subsided slightly, and she asked if the old woman could change. The old woman replied, "I have changed." The daughter then asked, "Do you still think of Xiangyun?" The old woman answered, "Even if I die in the underworld, I will never forget her." The daughter, surprised by these words, was taken aback, and after a moment, she sighed and said, "This foolish young man knows righteousness." She then comforted and apologized to the old woman repeatedly, sending people out in different directions to find Xiangyun, promising a reward for those who found her. The other women, eager, rushed off to search, and the old woman finally stopped crying.

    41

    翌日，一女走告曰：“香云走匿山谷中，为扈十郎所得，逼欲污之，不从，锢石室，不与饮食已一夜矣。”古媪闻之，泣曰：“吾儿贞烈，必不辱身，然而命蹇，何遭沙叱利之多也！”盖扈十郎者，女之表兄也。女使杜媪往索之，十郎曰：“欲释香云不难，主姑须自来易之去。”杜大怒，还述于女，女怒极，乃仗剑跨白鹿，诸女皆短衣持兵以从。命乔与翠翠，伏林内为疑兵，亲往索之。

    Explanation

    41

    The next day, a woman rushed to inform someone, saying, "Xiangyun ran and hid in the mountains, where she was captured by Hu Shilang. He forced and wanted to defile her, but she did not comply. He locked her in a stone chamber and did not provide her with food or drink for a whole night." The old woman heard this and wept, saying, "My daughter is chaste and loyal, she will not disgrace herself. However, her fate is unfortunate, how many times has she encountered such misfortune!" It was explained that Hu Shilang was the girl's cousin. The girl sent Madam Du to demand her back. Hu Shilang said, "To release Xiangyun is not difficult, you must come yourself to exchange for her." Madam Du became very angry and reported this to the girl. The girl was extremely angry and, wielding a sword and riding a white deer, she led all the other women, who were dressed in short clothes and holding weapons. She ordered Qiao and Cui Cui to hide in the forest as decoys, and she went personally to demand her back.

    42

    十郎腰弓矢，挺画戟，护卫甚众。兵刃既接，两军大开，十郎勇甚，诸女力不敌，各鸟兽散。女急退，鹿中流矢死。女被发徒奔，身被数创，失其双履。蹶不能兴，适乔奔至，负之以归。诸女亦渐集，无不心胆堕地。女大恸良久，感乔之德，呼之以兄，饮食器用，皆与己等。复聚众谋雪耻救云之举，众曰：“勍敌不可当也。”独翠翠进曰：“彼强我弱，非救助不可。欲求功，非太君来不可。”是夕，即使翠往。夜未央，翠返命曰：“太君来矣。”女率众跪迎，乔亦从众。太君亦曲背一妪耳。女泣诉致辱之由，太君曰：“有太婆在，儿勿气苦。”亟探袖，出一囊，呼翠至前命曰：“可将此往贮十郎。

    Explanation

    42

    Ten郎 wore his bow and arrows on his waist and held a painted halberd, with a large number of guards. When the weapons clashed, the two armies engaged. Ten郎 was very brave, and the women could not match his strength, so they scattered like birds and beasts. The woman retreated in a hurry, and a deer was hit by an arrow and died. The woman ran barefoot with her hair loose, covered in multiple wounds, and had lost her shoes. She fell down and could not get up, just as Qiao arrived and carried her back. The other women gradually gathered, and none of them were not terrified. The woman wept bitterly for a long time, grateful for Qiao's kindness, and called him her brother. The food, utensils, and daily necessities were all treated equally. They gathered again to plan how to avenge themselves and rescue Cloud, but the others said, "A strong enemy cannot be faced." Only Cui Cui said, "They are strong and we are weak; we cannot do it alone. To achieve anything, we need the help of Lady Tai." That evening, they sent Cui to go, and before midnight, Cui returned and said, "Lady Tai has arrived." The woman led the group to kneel and welcome Lady Tai, and Qiao followed them. Lady Tai was an old woman with a bent back. The woman cried and told Lady Tai about the humiliation, and Lady Tai said, "With Grandma here, don't be upset." She quickly reached into her sleeve and took out a bag, called Cui forward, and said, "Take this to store with Ten郎."

    43

    速与香云偕来。”翠诺而去，一饷时与云俱至，手提巨囊。开之，闯然一黑雄狐，觳觫而出，俯伏于太君之前，岳岳若乞哀状。太君呵之曰：“堕孽子！尚未克洗髓伐毛，辄尔堕落耶？不念尔祖，当亟殛之！”狐叩头谢。女子前，以鞭鞭之曰：“恣戾奴！平日赫耀之势，之态，今胡不肆耶？”太君止之曰：“儿休矣。老身必痛惩之。”又曰：“儿居此，终非了局，曷不举族从我？香云与乔郎，彼有夙世缘，未可摆脱，且听其去。伊母姑留我处，俟之三十年后，当大归也。”香云顿首奉教。太君赐乔名曰复。命驾先归。女赠乔、云甚厚，束缚辎重，令侍女护之先往，己乃与古杜二媪并翠翠送乔云出山，临歧泣别，然后归。

    43

    "Come quickly with Xiangyun." Cui nodded and left. Shortly after, Xiangyun arrived, carrying a large bag. When she opened it, a black male fox suddenly emerged, trembling and prostrated itself before the Lady. It stood proudly, as if begging for mercy. The Lady scolded it, saying, "Wicked offspring! You have not yet been purified of your bones and shaved your fur, and yet you have fallen! Do you not remember your ancestors? You should be punished immediately!" The fox kowtowed and thanked her. A woman stepped forward and whipped it, saying, "Arrogant and cruel servant! Why do you not act unrestrainedly now, given your glorious and radiant power and appearance in the past?" The Lady stopped her and said, "Enough, child. I will surely punish you severely." She then said, "Living here is not a final resolution. Why not bring your entire family to me? Xiangyun and Qiao Lang have a karmic connection from past lives and cannot be escaped. Let them go for now. Her mother can stay with me. Wait thirty years, and she will return to the great home." Xiangyun bowed deeply and followed her advice. The Lady bestowed the name Fu upon Qiao and ordered the carriage to return first. The woman gave Qiao and Xiangyun generous gifts, tied up their belongings, and sent a maid to accompany them first. She then left with the two elderly women Gu and Du and Cui Cui to see Qiao and Xiangyun off the mountain, crying as they parted before finally returning.

    44

    乔携云之襄阳，出资造舟，名“满江红”，专载游宦，以走江、黄、吴、楚。一日，载某太守公子并眷属之江南。住舟汉口。云偶出汲，为公子所见，迷惑失志，伺乔不在，密遣二女随侍，将吴绫越缟，往说云曰：“公子年少情多，富贵有权势，所谓炙手可热者。今艳子之貌，降心俯就，不惜珍宝之物，委贽于子。此真千载一时之机会，不可失也！子不从，则祸不可测；从之则珠翠环绕，锦绣纷披，饱粱肉而厌珍馐，一生吃着不尽。

    44

    Qiao took Xie Yun to Xiangyang, where he invested money in building a boat, naming it "满江红" (Red Across the River). The boat specialized in carrying traveling officials and traveled through the regions of Jiang, Huang, Wu, and Chu. One day, it carried the son of a certain prefect and his family to the south of the Yangtze River. The boat moored at Hankou. Xie Yun accidentally went out to draw water and was seen by the young master, who became bewitched and lost his resolve. Waiting for Qiao to be absent, the young master secretly sent two women to accompany and serve Xie Yun. He brought silk from Wu and fine silk from Yue and went to persuade Xie Yun, saying, "The young master is young and emotional, wealthy, noble, and powerful, a person who is very influential, as the saying goes, '炙手可热' (burning hand). Now, admiring your appearance, I lower my heart and lower myself, not sparing treasures, entrusting a gift to you. This is truly a once-in-a-thousand-years opportunity, which cannot be missed! If you do not follow, then disaster cannot be measured; if you follow, then jade and pearls will surround you, brocade scattered, eating enough rice and meat and being full of delicacies, a lifetime of eating without exhaustion."

    45

    讵若作舟子妇，衣粗食淡，埋首舱中，何啻明珠暗投哉！且子不闻乎，守经者立身之要也，通权者处世之方也。譬彼风马牛之不相及也，而络其口，穿其鼻，人得而左右之矣。今以势论，乔，马牛也，公子，人也。欲不为强驭，可得乎？惜子怜子，故陈利害于子，唯子图之！”云嫣然曰：“贤姊之言是也。公子风韵都美，儿亦慕悦久矣，幸即借二姊为羔雁。今夜人定后，请扣舷为号，可谋一会矣。”二婢大喜，归炫其能于公子。公子喜欲狂，重赏二婢。

    Explanation

    45

    How could it be like being a boatman's wife, wearing coarse clothes and eating plain food, burying oneself in the cabin, how could it be like throwing a pearl into the dark! Have you not heard that adhering to principles is essential for establishing oneself, and flexibility in dealing with circumstances is the way to conduct oneself in the world? Like wind and cattle, they do not relate to each other, yet one can control and manipulate it by using a bit and reins. Now, considering the social status, "乔" is like a horse, and the young master is a person. How could one avoid being forced to serve? I regard you with pity and affection, so I present the benefits and harms to you, it is up to you to consider this!” Yun smiled and said, “What my elder sister says is right. The young master is handsome and charming, I have admired and been delighted by him for a long time, I hope you will use your two sisters as matchmakers.
