After midnight tonight, please tap the side of the boat as a signal, we can arrange a meeting. The two maids were very happy and returned to boast their abilities to the young master. The young master was so delighted that he was almost beside himself, and generously rewarded the two maids.

    46

    至三更，举舟鼾寝。公子起坐不定，如鹿撞心。侧耳静听，移时果闻扣舷声，止而复作。急启窗纳之，果云也，不衣而至。公子此际，如在梦境中。不暇一言，即与狎匿。云忽惊，叱问何人，公子兴方阑，俯身若罔闻者。云又惊叫，家人惊起，疑有盗贼，执烛入窗，见二人赤身卧地上，烛之则公子与其妻媾耳。咸避去，夫妇赧然者久之。问妻何故赤身自窗外来，妻曰：“我在后舱睡熟，实不解何由到此也。”公子羞且怒，执乔送太守，谓其以妖术惑人。太守不明，锻炼成狱。

    Explanation

    46

    By the third watch, they had boarded the boat and fallen into a deep sleep. The young nobleman sat up restlessly, feeling as if a deer were colliding with his heart. He leaned in to listen quietly, and after a while, he indeed heard the sound of someone tapping the side of the boat, which stopped and then started again. Quickly opening the window, he admitted the person who had knocked, and it was indeed the woman, who came naked. At this moment, the young nobleman felt as if he were in a dream. Without a word, he immediately engaged in intimate relations with her. The woman suddenly screamed and shouted, "Who are you?" The young nobleman, whose passion was just beginning to wane, ignored her and continued. The woman screamed again, and the family woke up, suspecting a thief. They took a candle and entered through the window, seeing the two of them lying naked on the ground. Upon closer inspection, they realized it was the young nobleman and his wife engaged in intimate relations. Everyone fled in embarrassment, and the couple remained blushing for a long time. The young nobleman asked his wife why she had come out of the window naked, and she replied, "I was sleeping soundly in the rear cabin and truly do not know how I ended up here." The young nobleman felt both ashamed and angry, and he took the woman to the prefect, accusing her of using sorcery to deceive him. The prefect did not understand the situation and fabricated charges against her.

    47

    乔居犴狴，方痛覆盆，而夜半云忽至，手拂械锁，械锁自脱。携之出狱，人无见者。遂流寓南昌，仍为富室。二年间，有巨舟二十余艘。江楚操舟人莫不健羡焉。云从乔三十年，常如十七八岁人。生二子一女，女美丽有母风。乔乘间问云出处，云曰：“初不遽以诚告君者，恐君以异物见弃。亦既抱子，似亦无害。”因自言是狐，所谓主姑之女子，亦狐而为一山之主者。杜与翠与诸女子，皆狐也。唯庆君则天狐矣。乔始恍然，后渐泄于人，有求见者，云有见有不见。而见者辄自颠倒，云恶其聒，再迁于夔州。

    Explanation

    47

    Qiao lived in a prison cell, suffering under injustice, when suddenly a cloud arrived at midnight. With a wave of her hand, the shackles fell off by themselves. She carried him out of the prison, and no one saw him. Then he settled in Nanchang, still becoming a wealthy household. Within two years, he had more than twenty large ships, and no one who operated boats on the Yangtze and Chu rivers did not envy him. Cloud had been with Qiao for thirty years, always looking like a seventeen or eighteen-year-old. He had two sons and one daughter, the daughter was beautiful and resembled her mother. Qiao took the opportunity to ask Cloud about her origins. Cloud said, "I did not immediately tell you the truth because I feared you would abandon me as an unusual creature. Now that I have children, it seems there is no harm." She then said she was a fox, the daughter of the Master's concubine, also a fox who is the leader of a mountain. Du and Cui and the other women are all foxes. Only Qingsheng is a celestial fox. Qiao finally understood, and later gradually revealed it to others. Those who asked to see her, she sometimes appeared and sometimes did not. Those who saw her would be overwhelmed, Cloud disliked the disturbance, so she moved again to Kuizhou.

    48

    一夕，方坐话，翠忽至。乔云惊喜，降席而拜曰：“翠姨别来无恙？”翠答拜曰：“离别几何，乔郎须髯似戟，且就斑白矣！旧时风采可复再耶？人生如白驹过隙，转瞬痴猿觑镜，不能自识，譬夫以水和土，见日则燥，重为垩焉，非故物矣：何如金石其质，历劫不变者乎！人而无人道，是谓之陈人。人道者何？性命之原，不汩不没之谓也。夫泰山之□穿石，单极之□断干，渐靡使之然也。形骸情识，人之□、□也。此生不卒万死，非终也。子不见夫墦间之瘗者乎？路人过而伤之，伤之者，非徒伤也，伤其终不免于是也。

    Explanation

    48

    One evening, as we sat and chatted, Cui suddenly appeared. Qiao was overjoyed and stood up to bow, saying, "Is everything well with you, Aunt Cui?" Cui returned the bow, saying, "How long has it been since we parted? Your beard, like a halberd, is now gray and white! Can your former风采 be regained? Life is like a white horse passing through a crack in the wall; in a moment, a foolish monkey looks in the mirror and cannot recognize itself. It's like mixing water with earth; when exposed to the sun, it dries up and becomes lime, no longer the same thing. How about being as unchanging as metal and stone, enduring through countless calamities? If a person loses their humanity, they are called an old person. What is humanity? It is the origin of life and death, the purity that is not muddied or submerged. Mount Tai can pierce through stone, and a single pole can break a trunk, all due to gradual erosion. The body and emotions, these are the aspects of human existence. This life does not complete a thousand deaths; it is not the end. Have you not seen those buried between graves? Passersby feel sorrow for them, not just out of pity, but because they cannot escape this fate."

    49

    虽然，沧桑之变，彼恶知之？是累累者，数十百年后旋夷为都邑，旋坎为洿池，旋祀为坛灶，及为井墓。其循环往复，鸟有穷期。而其间之穷期，已无穷矣。凡此宜各自努力，人不能越俎而代之庖也。闻子在山中时，泊焉而无求，又能于屏风上行，质美若此，胡自弃之！”向云曰：“姊从乔郎数十年矣，宁吝所得，不一唤醒乎？”云曰：“奈其五内俱浊何！”翠曰：“不然。金注瓦注，固有不同，而其为注则一也。”云太息曰：“庄则不亲，狎则相简，虽有巧匠如工倕，但缩手袖间而已。”翠惨然而为之下泪，乔亦郁郁。是夜云伴翠宿于内寝，翌日向午不起。乔呼之不应，大疑，排闼入视，已失二人所在。举家惊扰，乔大哭，靡日不思。

    Explanation

    49

    Although, how could they know of the changes of time? These heaps will, after dozens or hundreds of years, be flattened into cities, dug into ponds, consecrated into altars, and become wells and tombs. Their cycles of change are endless, like birds without a final destination. And within these cycles, there is no end. All of this should be done by each person's own effort; no one can step in and take over another's role. I heard that when you were in the mountains, you were content and unambitious, and you could walk on a screen, showing your natural beauty; why do you abandon yourself?!" Xiang Yun said, "My sister has followed your master for decades; would she be stingy with what she has gained and not wake him up once?" Yun replied, "What can be done about his five viscera being all impure?" Cui said, "Not so. Whether it's gold or tiles, they are different, but their essence as objects of play is the same." Yun sighed, "If he is serious, he is not close; if he is familiar, he is distant; even with a skillful craftsman like Gong Chui, he can only tuck his hands into his sleeves." Cui was saddened and wept for him, and Qiao was also melancholy. That night, Yun stayed with Cui in the inner chamber and did not get up the next day at noon. Qiao called out to them without a response, greatly puzzled, pushed open the door to look, and found the two had disappeared. The whole family was alarmed, Qiao wept bitterly, and he missed them every day.

    50

    乔年八十余尚健，二子生孙，孙又生子。女适诸生某，亦弄孙矣。每隔五六年，云必来一探。又三四年不绝，容色终不少减。亲戚初面者，往往母其女，而女其母焉。予于乾隆庚午岁，从先祖父从三秦入七闽，路经武昌，月夜沽酒，聚舟人而饮食之，俾各述见闻，离奇怪诞，舟人共举此事，争说纷纭，且指江上一湘船见告：“此即乔家物也。”

    Explanation

    50

    In his eighty-plus years, Qiao remained vigorous, with his two sons having grandchildren, and those grandchildren having children of their own. His daughter married a certain scholar, and she too played with her grandchildren. Every five or six years, they said they would come to visit once. For another three or four years, they continued to do so without fail, and her appearance never diminished. People who met her for the first time often called her the mother of a daughter, and her the daughter of a mother. In the year Gengwu of the Qianlong era, I followed my late grandfather from San Qin to Qi Min, passing through Wuchang on the way. On a moonlit night, I bought wine and gathered the boatmen to drink with them, asking them to recount their observations, which were strange and bizarre. The boatmen all mentioned this matter, arguing and discussing it, and they pointed to a ship from Xiang on the river and told me, "This is the property of the Qiao family."

    51

    闲斋曰：世间尤物，得一可以倾城。乔以匹夫落魄，寝处诸尤物之间，卒至富豪名，以寿考终。其操持必有大过人者。翠必欲引而登之长生之域，亦婆心太挚矣。

    　　兰岩曰：乔业操舟，已属微贱，且无闻其有出类之才，其五内俱浊不待言矣。云何钟情至此？而主姑与翠翠，亦大有不能忘情者，岂果乔为情种耶？抑云喜其诚笃，可托终身乎？我辈不获有此奇遇者，殆择术之未精欤？五内之未尽浊欤？吾观香云事，而慨然矣。红丝系定，何啻千里之牵；破镜重圆，终作百年之合。偶参色相，致醋海淹断蓝桥；忽起干弋，令妖气生于内境。以德报怨，乔与女翻成附体之缘；祛死复生，翠与云永享飞仙之乐。斯狐中之不可多睹者耳！

    Explanation

    51

    Idle Studio says: Among the exceptional things of the world, possessing even one can captivate an entire city. Qiao, as an ordinary man who was destitute, found himself surrounded by various exceptional things, and eventually became wealthy and famous, living a long and full life. His conduct or management must have been greatly superior to others. Cui must have wanted to lead him to the realm of longevity, and her compassionate heart was too intense.

    Blue Cliff says: Qiao’s profession was rowing boats, which is already a humble occupation, and there is no mention of him having extraordinary talent. His inner self is clearly corrupt. How devoted is he to this? And the hostess and Cui Cui also have a great deal that cannot be forgotten. Is Qiao truly a person of deep feeling or passion? Or is it because she likes his sincerity and trustworthiness, making him someone she can entrust her life to? We who do not have this rare opportunity, is it perhaps because our methods of choice are not refined? Or is it perhaps because our inner selves are not entirely corrupt? I observe the matter of Xiang Yun and am moved. A predestined connection, how much more than a thousand miles of connection; a reconciliation, ultimately becoming a union for a hundred years. The accidental involvement of physical appearance causes jealousy to sever the bond, and suddenly starting conflict causes demonic energy to arise within the inner realm. To repay resentment with virtue, Qiao and the woman became entangled in a mutual relationship; to rid oneself of death and return to life, Cui and Yun will forever enjoy the joy of flying immortals. This is something rare to see among foxes.

    52

    龙化李高鱼枕碧山房，壁挂古剑。一日大雨雷，瞥见一黑物，长尺余，细如线，后一红线逐之，自窗凌空而入，绕室飞行，俄延壁上，穿入剑鞘中。即闻戛戛作声，旋出旋入，无所阻碍。良久，忽又飞出，蜿蜒空际，甫及檐，霹雳一声，屋宇震动，红光烛天，不及察二物所至，唯见窗下落鳞数片，酷似穿山甲。取剑视之，锋刃尽穿小孔，密如虫蛀，鞘亦如之。或曰：“此龙之变化。”想当然耳。

    　　李　翘　之石商李翘之，名林魁，五台人。其微时为石工以食力，尝与同行者十余辈，往村中观剧，二更始归。际晦日，夜黑如漆，正苦迍踬，忽山川大地放大光明，迎面十余里外现一菩萨宝相，高可数十丈，衣纹璎珞，灿若云霞，月面星毫，靡不华采，映彻世界，尽如琉璃。李且瞻且拜，口诵佛号不绝。顷之始隐，询之同人，悉蔑之睹也。

    Explanation

    52

    Dragon-transformed Li Gao, in his Bishanfang room, had an ancient sword hanging on the wall. One day, during a heavy rainstorm and thunder, he caught a brief glimpse of a black object, about a foot long and thin as a thread, followed by a red line chasing it. The object entered through the window, flying around the room, lingered on the wall, and then entered the sword sheath. Immediately, a clattering sound was heard, and the object came out and went back into the sheath repeatedly without any obstruction. After a while, it suddenly flew out again, twisting and turning in the air. Just as it reached the eaves, a thunderclap occurred, the house shook, and red light illuminated the sky. He only saw a few scales falling from the window, very much like those of a pangolin. When he examined the sword, the blade was riddled with small holes, as dense as if infested by insects, and the sheath was similarly affected. Some said, "This was a transformation of a dragon." It's just a guess.

    Stone Merchant Li Qiao

    Li Qiao, whose real name was Lin Kui, was from Wutai. In his humble days, he worked as a stone mason to earn a living. Once, he and over a dozen fellow workers went to a village to watch a play and returned home at midnight. On a dark night, when it was pitch black, they were struggling to move forward when suddenly the mountains, rivers, and earth were bathed in bright light. About ten miles ahead, a Bodhisattva's radiant image appeared, towering dozens of feet tall. The patterns on its robe and the jewels on its neck shone like clouds and rainbows, its moon-like face and star-like eyebrows exuding a splendid aura that illuminated the entire world, making everything look like crystal. Li Qiao gazed and bowed, reciting Buddhist chants without pause. After a while, the vision disappeared. When he asked his companions, none of them had seen anything.

    Please let me know if you need any further assistance or if there are any additional texts you would like me to translate.

    53

    李今年已望七矣，性正直，无私曲，重义气，好施与。初入都，即受知于大司农涂勤恪公，得为大工石商，致富数十万。公薨，李感恩不忘，岁修墓道。李以德报，为今人中之古人。二子亦岐嶷。天报善人，理自不爽。宜其于稠人之中，独瞻法相，非福德兼厚者，又乌得有此？自言有德必报，非沽名，行其所安耳。

    　　兰岩曰：此李心地自放光明耳。菩萨何来，独示之以宝相哉！人能洗心涤虑，自去其污，何处非菩萨宝相，琉璃世界耶？

    　　洪　由　义洪由义者，靖远协汛一洚子也。性慈善，喜放生。暇时坐黄河畔，见渔人起网，凡所弃小鱼细虾暨螺蚌之属，悉拾之投于水中。积数年不倦。

    Explanation

    53

    Li is now nearly seventy years old, with a straightforward and upright nature, without favoritism or bias. He values righteousness and friendship, and enjoys giving. When he first entered the capital, he was immediately recognized by Grand Agricultural Commissioner Tu Qinke Gong, and he became a major stone merchant, accumulating wealth in the hundreds of thousands. After the commissioner's death, Li did not forget his gratitude and annually maintained the path to the tomb. He repaid with virtue, becoming an ancient person in modern times. His two sons are also outstanding and talented. Heaven rewards good people, and the principle is never wrong. It is fitting that among the crowd, he alone could see the Dharma form. If not someone with great virtue and fortune, how could they have this? He himself says that virtue will surely be rewarded, not for fame, but to do what is right.

    Lan Yan said: This is simply Li's heart naturally emitting light. Why did the Bodhisattva appear and show him the precious form alone! If a person can cleanse their mind and thoughts, removing their own impurities, where is there not the precious form of the Bodhisattva, the world of crystal?

    Hong Youyi, a descendant of the Jingyuan auxiliary patrol, has a kind and compassionate nature and enjoys releasing animals from captivity. In his spare time, he sits by the Yellow River, seeing fishermen pulling up their nets. He picks up all the small fish, shrimp, and mollusks that are discarded and throws them back into the water. He does this for many years without tiring.

    54

    一日渡河，失足落水，随波逐浪者十余里，昏迷间，觉有人捉其臂，拖至一处。视之，则身在一大门下，四面黄水如壁立，门前二石赑屃，大约数亩。洪大骇异，方怀惑间，门忽启，见紫衣纱帽者二人，出谓洪曰：“可亟入，勿惧失仪也。”洪从之，至一广殿。殿上有贵人，年可四十许，衣冠奇古，左右侍从甚都。洪蒲伏阶下，贵人劳之曰：“汝大有恩于我部下，不但脱汝难，且当少为润泽。”因命取一珠，大如豌豆，赐之曰：“此如意珠也。握之凡有所需，无不如意。三年后可见还也。”洪唯唯拜赐，贵人仍命二紫衣吏送出。二吏嘱闭目。但闻波涛汹涌之声，顷刻而息，徐开其目，则已脚踏实地，而二吏失所在矣。珠犹在手，遂秘之以归。归则家人已成服，相见各惊疑。洪绐以得抱枯木，故不致死。家人喜而信之，乃释服。

    Explanation

    54

    One day, while crossing a river, Hong lost his footing and fell into the water. Drifting with the waves for over ten li, he was in a state of unconsciousness when he felt someone grabbing his arm and dragging him to a place. Looking around, he found himself under a large gate, with yellow water surrounding him like walls standing upright. At the gate stood two stone beasts (赑屃), about several mu in size. Hong was greatly frightened and surprised, still in a state of confusion. Suddenly, the gate opened, and two men dressed in purple robes and wearing gauze hats came out and said to Hong, "You can quickly enter; do not be afraid of losing your manners." Hong followed them to a large hall. On the dais was a noble person, about forty years old, wearing strange and ancient clothing, with very handsome attendants on both sides. Hong knelt on the steps, and the noble person comforted him, saying, "You have great merit for my subordinates, not only saving you from danger but also slightly rewarding you." The noble person then ordered someone to fetch a pearl, as big as a pea, and gave it to Hong, saying, "This is a wish-fulfilling pearl. If you hold it, whatever you need, it will be granted. You will see it returned to me in three years." Hong bowed and accepted the gift. The noble person then ordered the two purple-robed officials to escort him out. The two officials instructed him to close his eyes. He only heard the sound of surging waves, which stopped in an instant. Slowly opening his eyes, he found himself standing firmly on the ground, and the two officials had disappeared. The pearl was still in his hand, so he kept it secret and returned home. Upon returning, he found his family in mourning attire. They were surprised and puzzled to see each other. Hong lied, saying he had held onto a dead tree, so he did not die. The family was happy and believed him, and thus removed their mourning clothes.

    55

    洪素喜樗蒱，得珠后，重与其徒博。分明枭色，呼之，皆成庐雉，于是有博必胜。家业渐丰。适奉官之西安。西安为省会之处，汉唐故都，俗尚豪华，人情奢侈。王孙公子，肥马轻裘，一食万钱，一掷百万。洪侧身而入，掉臂而前，自午至晡，腰金百镒。旁观者但挢其舌，当局者徒热于心。满载而归，遂成巨富。为长子捐官，次子纳监。始以得珠之事，告其妻孥。愈以放生为务，由此河上人，称为洪善人。五原称富室者，推洪为巨擘。三年后，秋夜方寝，梦见前二紫衣吏至，曰：“瓜期届矣，珠当见还也。”洪跪而奉之，既寤而珠已失矣。后洪寿至期颐，无疾而殁。予在靖远时，洪之孙已五十余，犹为富家翁也。

    Explanation

    55

    Hong had always enjoyed the game of dice. After winning the pearl, he became obsessed with gambling with his disciples. When he clearly saw the dice roll an "枭" (a type of bird), he called out, and they all transformed into "庐雉" (another type of bird), indicating a sudden change in his luck. From then on, he always won at gambling. His family business gradually became abundant. He happened to serve as an official in Xi'an, a city that was once the capital of the Han and Tang dynasties, known for its luxurious and extravagant lifestyle. The nobles and aristocrats there rode fat horses and wore fine robes, spending ten thousand coins on a single meal and throwing away a million coins in a single bet. Hong entered humbly but walked ahead confidently, from noon until dusk, carrying a hundred measures of gold. Onlookers could only stretch their tongues in astonishment, while those directly involved felt a burning passion in their hearts. He returned home with a full load, thus becoming a wealthy man. He donated an official position to his eldest son and enrolled his second son in the imperial academy. Only then did he tell his wife and children about the pearl. He became even more committed to releasing animals, and as a result, people in the river region called him Hong the Benevolent. Among the wealthy families in Wuyuan, Hong was considered the most prominent. Three years later, on an autumn night as he was about to sleep, he dreamed of two officials dressed in purple robes approaching him. They said, "The period has arrived, and the pearl should be returned now." Hong knelt and presented it to them. When he woke up, the pearl was already gone. Later, Hong lived to the age of 100 and died without illness. When I was in Jingyuan, Hong's grandson was already over fifty years old and still a wealthy man.

    56

    兰岩曰：凡人意之所在，无不如愿以偿，不必功名富贵也。斯如意之最为难耳，乃得珠后，徒事樗蒱，以毕三年之愿，志亦小矣。虽然，人苟巨富，凡所欲得欲为者，无不能。洪可谓握要以图哉！

    　　某僧铭镜石三为予言：佑圣寺无凡上人，有弟子某者，少年韶秀，有人诱之为龙阳，某亦不拒。上人闻而责之，某曰：“然则不可乎？”上人曰：“如之何其可也！此间不可复居矣。”曰：“去之可乎？”曰：“可。”曰：“承师命。某日当行耳。”至日，房中寂然。视之，已化去矣。

    Explanation

    56

    兰岩曰：凡人意之所在，无不如愿以偿，不必功名富贵也。斯如意之最为难耳，乃得珠后，徒事樗蒱，以毕三年之愿，志亦小矣。虽然，人苟巨富，凡所欲得欲为者，无不能。洪可谓握要以图哉！

    　　某僧铭镜石三为予言：佑圣寺无凡上人，有弟子某者，少年韶秀，有人诱之为龙阳，某亦不拒。上人闻而责之，某曰：“然则不可乎？”上人曰：“如之何其可也！此间不可复居矣。”曰：“去之可乎？”曰：“可。”曰：“承师命。某日当行耳。”至日，房中寂然。视之，已化去矣。

    Translation:

    Lan Yan said: Whatever one's heart desires, it will be fulfilled, not necessarily through fame, wealth, or honor. The most difficult thing to achieve is this very fulfillment. After obtaining a pearl, one merely engages in gambling to fulfill a three-year wish, which is a small aspiration. However, if one is extremely wealthy, one can achieve whatever one desires to obtain or do. How can one say that one is grasping the essentials to achieve one's goals?

    A monk named Ming Jingshi San told me: At Yousheng Temple, there was a monk named Wufan, who had a disciple who was young and handsome. Someone tempted him into engaging in homosexual relationships, and he did not refuse. The master heard about it and rebuked him. The disciple said, "Then it is not permissible?" The master replied, "How can it be permissible! This place is no longer suitable for you to stay." The disciple asked, "Can I leave?" The master said, "Yes." The disciple said, "I will follow the master's command. I will leave on a certain day." On that day, the room was silent. When they looked inside, he had already transformed and vanished.

    57

    兰岩曰：浑然天真，洞然大道，此僧来去自如，人己无间，何毫无窒碍耶？

    　　邵　廷　铨江右峡江县，濒江有周瑜庙。颜曰：“巴丘古迹。”庙中旧有厝棺，尘封已久。天台邵□为临江府经历，三年考绩，授峡江令。在县两月，政声大作，其少子廷铨，妙龄韶秀，性恬淡，所至则多流览。爱郭外江山，白诸□，筑瓦屋数椽于周郎庙西，编竹为墙，辟畦莳花，为肄业之所。与邑庠边、魏二生相莫逆，暇则相寻往来，不间晨夕。

    　　会边生秋闱获隽，廷铨往贺之。殢酒而返，日已曛暮。柴门外遇一女郎，恣态妖娆，纤秾合度，衣裳缟素，绰有余妍。廷铨心为之荡，趋而鞠之。女娭光眇视，羞涩不支。廷铨指门内曰：“此即僻居，可以少息。睘睘日暮，窃为卿危之。”女作色曰：“少男处女，踪迹悬殊，何物书生强来饶舌！苟非缞绖在室，凡百隐忍，亟当白诸家人，股拆鸡肋矣！”言讫，怫然而去。

    Explanation

    57

    Lan Yan said: "Wholly natural and innocent, clearly understanding the great way, this monk comes and goes freely, without distinction between self and others, how can there be any hindrance at all?"

    Shao Tingquan, from Xiajiang County in Jiangxi, near the river, had a temple dedicated to Zhou Yu. The temple was inscribed with "Baqiu Ancient Site." There were old coffins in the temple, long sealed by dust. Tian Tai Shao served as the magistrate of Linjiang Prefecture, and after three years of evaluation, he was appointed as the magistrate of Xiajiang County. In just two months, his reputation for good governance was widely praised. His young son, Shao Tingquan, was handsome and calm in nature, often traveling and exploring. He loved the scenic views outside the city walls and informed his superiors, building a small thatched house with woven bamboo walls on the west side of Zhou Yu's temple, clearing land to plant flowers, as a place for study and learning. He became very close friends with two students from the local school, Bian and Wei, often visiting each other in their free time, regardless of day or night.

    One day, when Bian student passed the provincial examination, Shao Tingquan went to congratulate him. After drinking heavily, he returned as the day turned dusk. Outside the thatched house, he met a young woman with a graceful and charming appearance, moderate in both delicacy and fullness, wearing white clothes, with an extra touch of beauty. Shao Tingquan's heart was stirred, and he approached and asked her. The woman looked shyly and demurely, unable to respond. Shao Tingquan pointed to the door and said, "This is my secluded residence, you can rest for a while. As the day is coming to an end, I am concerned for your safety." The woman replied angrily, "A young man and a young woman, our backgrounds are different. What kind of scholar is trying to talk to me? If it weren't for the mourning attire in the house, I would bear with everything, but you should immediately inform your family. It's like a chicken rib, something useless and insignificant!" She finished speaking and left in anger.

    58

    廷铨大惭，入坐草堂，嗒若丧偶。馆童已入黑甜，方冥想间，忽闻扣扉声，止而复作。廷铨骇愕，蹑下下阶，潜从篱落下窥之。仿佛日暮间所值者，不觉喜出非望。即启扉，女款款入，辄嘱阖扉，相携入室。廷铨揖之曰：“卿弃我如遗，以为去如黄鹤矣。何故却回玉趾，重辱草堂，得勿与家人密计，问罪小生耶？”女嫣然曰：“儿纵忍人，何遽出此？适间唐突，聊以相戏耳。固将入城，途远莫及矣。向荷关切，虑及孱弱，故万不得已，欲托一宿。未稔果肯假一席地，度此一宵否？”廷铨大悦，曰：“万一不至，尚欲追而访之，况飞琼自降耶！”遂相与绸缪，如胶投漆中，鸡再鸣，乃揽衣而起，临去谓廷铨曰：“儿故近村曹氏女也，父母远宦黔中，儿因病独留，家中更无人，止一乳媪执爨，聋且聩，不足约束儿。君苟不弃儿，请自今暮来朝去，当徐与君计长久。”廷铨敬诺。送之门外，叮咛数四，唯恐爽约。女设誓而去。自是靡夕不至。

    Explanation

    58

    Tingquan was deeply ashamed, entering the thatched cottage and sitting down, feeling as if he had lost his beloved. The attendant had already fallen into a deep sleep, and Tingquan was in a state of deep contemplation when he suddenly heard a knock on the door, which stopped and started again. Tingquan was startled, tiptoed down the steps, and peered through the fence. It seemed to be the same woman he had met at dusk, and he could not help but be overjoyed beyond expectation. He immediately opened the door, and the woman entered gracefully, instructing him to close the door. They walked together into the room. Tingquan bowed and said, "You have abandoned me as if I were worthless, thinking you would leave like a crane. Why have you returned and disgraced the humble abode again? Could it be that you have secretly plotted with your family to blame the young scholar?" The woman smiled and said, "I may be cruel, but why would I say such a thing so suddenly? Earlier, I was impolite, just to play a joke. I was going to go into town, but the distance is too far to reach. I have been concerned about your well-being, considering your weakness. So, in desperation, I wanted to stay for a night. Not yet knowing if you would be willing to lend a place to sleep for the night?" Tingquan was greatly pleased and said, "If you did not come, I still wanted to go and find you, let alone that a fairy has descended!" They then spent the night together, as if glued to each other. When the rooster crowed again, Tingquan got up, and before leaving, the woman said to Tingquan, "I am a daughter of the Cao family from a nearby village. My parents are far away on official business in Guizhou Province. I stayed behind because of illness, and there is no one else at home. Only one wet nurse cooks, and she is deaf and mute, unable to discipline me. If you do not abandon me, please come tonight and leave tomorrow morning. We will plan for the long term gradually." Tingquan solemnly agreed. He sent her to the door, repeatedly admonishing her, only afraid that the appointment would be broken. The woman took an oath and left. From then on, she came every night without fail.

    59

    廷铨既被蛊惑，形神改常。边、魏二友疑之，私询馆童，童曰：“即不见问，亦将告曰。公子半月以来，饮食消减，日近尪瘠。诵读皆辍。日方晡，即闭门作休息计。每思密禀主人，未遑入城耳。”边曰：“汝但留意侦之，稍有见闻，亟来见报。是宜秘密勿泄！”童受计，是夕即于树下故作鼾睡，俄闻笑语声间作于房内，潜起密觇之，则见廷铨于床上拥一红衣骷髅，戏谑灯下。骷髅亦拥廷铨，忸怩作态。童大怖，缩颈而退。次日，告二生，二生惊曰：“讵有与枯骨缠绵而不置祸害者乎？谊系朋友，知而不谏，非义也。汝姑勿泄，吾等自有处置。”

    Explanation

    59

    Tingquan, having been bewitched, underwent a transformation in both his appearance and spirit. His friends, Bian and Wei, grew suspicious and privately questioned the household servant. The servant replied, "Even if you do not ask, I would have reported it anyway. For the past half month, the young master has been eating less, becoming increasingly emaciated. He has stopped his studies altogether. Around noon, he closes the door and makes plans for rest. He often thinks of secretly reporting to the master but has not had the time to enter the city." Bian said, "You should just pay attention and spy on him. If you hear anything, come and report it immediately. This should be kept a secret and do not reveal it!" The servant took his instructions and that evening, he pretended to be asleep under a tree. Soon, he heard laughter and chatter coming from inside the room. He stealthily rose to secretly observe and saw Tingquan on the bed, embracing a red-robed skeleton, jesting by the lamp. The skeleton also embraced Tingquan, acting coyly and pretentiously. The servant was greatly terrified, shrank back in fear, and retreated. The next day, he informed the two scholars, who were shocked and exclaimed, "How can there be anyone who engages in intimacy with a dried-up skeleton without causing harm? As friends, knowing and not advising is not right. You should keep it a secret for now, we will handle it ourselves."

    60

    适同社刘生，客粤还，边、魏约廷铨为作软脚局，羞鳖焉。魏下箸细咀其骨，而熟玩之，曰：“异哉！鳖骨非禽非兽，又不同他水族，具肉与裙，尚不美观。况余此白骨，奚足恋恋！”边曰：“恋恋者，恋其美也，美去何恋？”廷铨曰：“不然。千金马骨，骏安在乎？正以见骏骨如见骏马耳。”廷铨无心酬答，机锋恰与二生相对。相视默然，谓其不可谏。

    Explanation

    60

    Just now, a fellow member of our literary group, Liu, returned from a visit to Guangdong. Bian and Wei invited Tingquan to a soft-foot banquet, where a turtle dish was served. Wei picked up his chopsticks and chewed the bone of the turtle carefully, examining it thoroughly. He said, "Strange! The turtle bone is neither bird nor animal, and it is also different from other aquatic creatures. It has both meat and shell, yet it is still not considered beautiful. How much more so this white bone, how worthy is it to be attached to?" Bian replied, "Those who are attached to it, attach themselves to its beauty. Why be attached if the beauty is gone?" Tingquan said, "Not so. The bone of a thousand gold horse, where is the excellence of the horse? Precisely because seeing the bone is like seeing the swift horse itself." Tingquan had no intention to respond, but his sharp wit matched that of the other two characters. They looked at each other in silence, believing that the situation could not be advised.

    61

    乃密白邵令。令大惊，曰：“吾儿年少，气血未定，郊坰荒僻，不可以久居，二兄速叱之归署，庶绝大患！”边曰：“促公子入城，计良得矣。第鬼即不克甘心于今日，必将肆志于将来，非所以除害久远也。莫若稍缓旦夕，某当与魏兄密查出处，得其踪迹而后除之，所谓公私皆利，一劳永逸之道也。”魏曰：“不可。公子此际利害，间不容发，不急为之救，乃又虑及未然，兄之计，无乃左矣。”边笑曰：“兄所谓梦醒索烛，畏黑不睡者也。公子被惑半月，未致委顿，岂争此一夕哉！”邵曰：“边兄独见其大，吾何忧哉！此事一以委兄，愿假兄白马金鞍，并干办十人，听兄指挥。魏兄率六人为副，以善其后。

    Explanation

    61

    The nobleman's son was secretly reported to the magistrate. The magistrate was greatly alarmed and said, "My son is young, his blood and qi are not yet stable, and the outskirts are remote and desolate, unsuitable for long-term residence. The two elder brothers should quickly send him back to the city hall, in hopes of eliminating a great calamity!" Edge said, "Urging the young master to return to the city is a good plan. However, if the ghost cannot be satisfied today, it will surely act on its will in the future, which is not a solution for the long term. It would be better to wait a little longer, and I will secretly investigate the matter with Brother Wei, find out its whereabouts, and then deal with it. This is the way to achieve a one-time effort for permanent results." Wei said, "No, it is not advisable. The young master is in a critical situation, with no room for error. If we do not act quickly to save him, and instead worry about what might happen in the future, your plan seems to be off the mark." Edge smiled and said, "Brother Wei, you are like someone who wakes up and looks for a candle, fearing the dark and unable to sleep. The young master has been bewitched for half a month without becoming seriously ill, how can we worry about losing a single night?" The magistrate said, "Brother Edge has a broad perspective, I have no worries! I entrust this matter entirely to you. I will lend you a white horse with a golden saddle and ten assistants, at your disposal. Brother Wei will lead six people as a deputy to support you."

    62

    边慨然自任，饭仆秣马，日晡而往，共伏林间。预约馆童，令其为侦，伺鬼至即报。漏既下，馆童坌息来告曰：“至矣！”边部署已定，各止其所，亲偕馆童至窗下，窥之，见廷铨与鬼方检点就寝。边却回，招众共伏门外，待至鸡鸣，隐隐见柴扉轻启，廷铨送一女子出，旋阖扉而入。边潜尾女子，径冉冉入周郎庙，边还告众人曰：“彼巢穴应在庙中矣。”即命燃炬持械而往，庙中空无所有，唯一黑漆棺，停庑下。

    Explanation

    62

    Bian took it upon himself with a resolute attitude, feeding the servants and tending to the horses, and they departed by the afternoon, hiding together in the woods. They arranged with the inn boy to act as a lookout, to report when the ghost arrived. By the time the hour had passed, the inn boy came breathlessly to report, "It has arrived!" Bian had already arranged everything, and everyone took their positions. He personally accompanied the inn boy to the window to look inside, and they saw Tingquan and the ghost were just settling down to sleep. Bian returned and called everyone to hide outside the door, waiting until the rooster crowed. They faintly saw the thatched door gently open, and Tingquan sent a woman out, then closed the door and went in. Bian stealthily followed the woman, who slowly entered the Zhou Lang Temple. He returned and told everyone, "The den must be in the temple." He immediately ordered the torches lit and weapons taken, and they went. Inside the temple, there was nothing; only a black lacquered coffin stood under the eaves.

    63

    发蒙视之，榜曰：“故曲江县丞曹公之女秋霞之柩。”访诸居人，佥曰：“厝此二十余年矣。无有主者来取，实不知其作祟也。”边使人驰报邵公，邵亲至，开棺验之，衣色正符所见，头面余白骨，独二目炯炯不变，凹处渐生新肉。枕畔有白玉尺，方识为廷铨珍物。邵惊叹曰：“若此殊异，哪得不妖。非边兄，吾儿死为鬼婿矣！”亟令积薪焚之。日高始尽，臭达数里，尸啾唧有声，自此怪绝。廷铨被促归署，心殊怅悒，及备闻其故，始生惧焉。不敢复作痴想，后得第，官至郡守。边亦历仕至方伯焉。

    Explanation

    63

    Opening their eyes and seeing it, they found a sign that read: "The coffin of Qiu Xia, the daughter of the former county magistrate Cao from Qujiang County." They asked the local residents, and all replied: "It has been placed here for more than twenty years. No one has come to claim it, and no one knew that it was causing trouble." The brother sent a messenger to report to Shaogong, who personally came to open the coffin and examine it. The color of the clothing matched what was seen, and the head and face were left with only white bones, while the eyes remained bright and unchanged, and new flesh began to grow in the hollow areas. There was a white jade ruler by the pillow, which was then recognized as a precious item belonging to Tingquan. Shaogong exclaimed: "Such a strange occurrence, how could it not be a sign of evil. Without my brother, my son would have died as a ghost's bride!" He quickly ordered to pile up wood and burn it. It took until late in the day to finish burning, and the smell spread for several miles. The body made faint sounds during the burning, and from then on, the strange occurrences ceased. Tingquan was hurriedly called back to his office and felt particularly sad and uneasy. Upon hearing the full details, he began to feel fear. He dared not have foolish thoughts anymore. Later, he passed the imperial examinations and became a prefect. His brother also served and eventually became a provincial governor.

    64

    兰岩曰：拥骷髅而为佳丽，世间宁少此人哉？但只觉其美而不知其恶耳。嗟乎！蛾眉皓齿，转盼成空；断陇荒郊，凝思莫释。天壤间痴情人能自解哉？一夕欢娱，酿成粉骨碎身之祸，此女亦不智矣！

    　　卖　饼　翁阁学某先达，龆龀时，出就外傅。每过市，辄就一卖饼翁，市胡饼数枚，怀之到塾，习以为常。一日，复往市饼，翁忽罢业，留公坐而谓之曰：“吾观子神气清明，非凡品也，会将有一事奉邀，能从我乎？”公曰：“何事？”翁曰：“请留此宿，至晚当自知耳。”公自分幼少，稍迟归，老母且倚阁望，讵容外宿，因辞焉。翁叹曰：“我固知子不能主也。然亦缘分使然，聊言之耳。”

    Explanation

    64

    Lan Yan said: To embrace bones and become beautiful, such people are not rare in the world; one only perceives their beauty and does not realize their evil. Alas! Beautiful eyebrows and white teeth, in a blink of an eye, it becomes nothing; desolate and barren landscapes, deeply pondering and unable to let go. Can a foolish person in heaven and earth understand themselves? One night of pleasure, brewing a disaster that leads to the destruction of one's body and soul, this woman is also unwise!

    A respected scholar, during childhood, went to a private tutor. Every time he passed by the market, he would go to an old man who sold bread, buy several flatbreads, carry them to the school, and become accustomed to it. One day, he went to the market again, and the old man suddenly stopped his business, invited him to sit and said, "I see that you have a clear and pure spirit, you are not an ordinary person, there will be an invitation for a certain matter, can you follow me?" The scholar said, "What is the matter?" The old man said, "Please stay here tonight, by evening you will know." The scholar knew that he was young, and returning a bit late, his mother would be waiting anxiously at home, how could he stay out overnight, so he declined. The old man sighed and said, "I knew you could not make a decision. But it is also a matter of fate, just saying it."

    65

    次日，公早过其肆，见多人环观如堵，不解何故，挨入视之，则卖饼翁死矣。不觉心为之恻，归告于母，并述畴昔之言。母叹异，未尝不以未赴其约之为深幸也。

    　　迨后十余年，公及第，入翰林，给假归祭，泊舟于江浒。公偶上岸闲步，不觉行远，蓦一人自林间来，呼曰：“太史公别来无恙？”急识之，则卖饼翁也。讶曰：“叟哪得在此？”翁把公臂坐树底，笑曰：“想君必谓我为鬼物矣。吾明告君，昔吾所以约君者，以君有仙骨故也。惜君俗缘未尽耳。彼日夜静寝未安，闻市头来往无停履，起窥窗隙，见鬼神其形者甚夥，除道相戒：”真人赴岳庙，不可怠慢‘，云云。予时无所顾虑，潜出后门，由僻弄迂路至庙，庙前虚阒无人，殿后亦无所见，唯一丐者，鹑衣鹄面，当阶鼾睡，呼之不醒，但闻嘘声啡啡，知其有异，长跪其旁以伺，良久始觉，问何为，予稽首称真人，丐大怒，辱詈百端，予敬谨如故。丐起身且骂且去，予随之。

    Explanation

    65

    The next day, the gentleman went early to his shop and saw a crowd gathered around like a wall, not understanding why. He pushed his way in to look and found that the old man selling cakes had died. Unconsciously, his heart was moved with pity, and he went back to tell his mother, recounting the words they had spoken in the past. His mother exclaimed in surprise and never failed to feel deeply fortunate for not having kept their appointment.

    After more than ten years, the gentleman passed the imperial examinations, entered the Hanlin Academy, was granted leave to return home for a memorial service, and moored his boat on the bank of the river. He happened to go ashore for a leisurely walk and, unconsciously, went far. Suddenly, a person came from the woods, calling out, "Mr. Historian, how have you been?" He quickly recognized him as the old man selling cakes. Surprised, he said, "How could you be here?" The old man took his arm and sat him under a tree, laughing and saying, "I imagine you must think I am a ghost or spirit. I will clearly tell you. In the past, I made an appointment with you because you have the bones of a sage. Unfortunately, your worldly ties are not yet completed. Day and night, while quietly sleeping, I was not at peace, hearing the constant flow of people in the marketplace. I got up and peeked through a crack in the window, seeing many ghosts and spirits in human form, warning each other on the road: 'The true person is going to the temple of the mountain god; do not neglect him,' and so on. At that time, I had no hesitation, secretly went out the back door, took a roundabout path through a secluded alley to the temple. In front of the temple, it was empty and quiet, with no one around. Behind the hall, I saw nothing either, only a beggar, wearing patched clothes and with a pale face, snoring while sleeping on the steps. I called to him, but he did not wake up. I only heard faint breathing sounds, knowing he was different. I knelt beside him to wait, and after a long time, he woke up. I asked him what was going on, and I bowed deeply, calling him a true person. The beggar became very angry, insulting me in many ways, but I remained respectful and cautious as before. The beggar got up, continuing to curse and walk away, and I followed him.

    66

    绕出庙后，骂愈厉，予终不少却，丐乃辍骂，纵步如飞，予亦急走相逐，不离跬步，力亦不少乏。指顾间，入一深山，丐攀附滕葛，步履如猿猱之捷。心无退悔，颇能及之。至极巅，路穷只一独木略彴直接对山，相对约数丈，下临绝壑。丐回顾曰：“子之诚，我深喻之，至此可以止也。’予应之，曰：”上天下地，悉请相从，岂肯止此？‘丐复怒骂，径履木而过，予力揽其裾，与之俱，丐极力携挤，不觉失足堕涧中，予大呼，腾掷一跃，而登对山之顶，回首俯视，见自身僵卧涧下，而亦失丐之所在。恍然大觉，一刻山川大地，千生万劫，尽皆莹照，瞬息都过，唯留此心在腔子里，非真非幻，是幻是真。天已向晨，志所入山，则黄山也。自此一身轻捷，任意飞行。今得相逢，亦异数也。“

    Explanation

    66

    Went around the back of the temple, cursing more fiercely. I did not back down at all. The beggar then stopped cursing and walked away like a flying bird. I also hurried to chase after him, never more than a step behind, and did not feel any lack of strength. In a blink, we entered a deep mountain. The beggar climbed, clinging to vines and creepers, moving as swiftly as a monkey. My heart had no intention of retreating or regretting, and I was able to keep up with him. We reached the very top, where the path ended at a single narrow plank bridge directly opposite the mountain. The beggar looked back and said, "Your sincerity, I deeply understand it, but here we can stop." I replied, "Up to heaven and down to earth, I request to follow you everywhere; how could I stop here?" The beggar cursed angrily again and walked straight across the plank. I tried to grab his robe, wanting to go with him. The beggar forcefully pushed and shoved, and I unconsciously lost my footing and fell into the ravine. I shouted, leaped, and landed on the top of the opposite mountain. Looking back and looking down, I saw myself lying stiffly at the bottom of the ravine, and I also lost sight of the beggar. Suddenly, I realized that in an instant, the mountains and rivers, the earth, and countless lifetimes had all been illuminated; in the blink of an eye, everything had passed, leaving only this heart in my chest. Neither real nor illusory, both illusory and real. The sky was turning towards dawn, and the place we entered was the Huangshan (Yellow Mountain). From then on, my body became light and agile, and I could fly freely. Now we meet, which is also a rare occurrence.

    67

    公知其已仙，泣拜求度，翁曰：“尚非其时也。君于名场中，官可二品，唯‘躁进’二字不可犯，‘勇退’二字不可忘，志之志之，请从此别。”言讫，跃入江中，履水如平地，转瞬而逝，唯剩江心月白，一望无涯。公徘徊怅悒，望洋则叹。仆从来觅，默然归舟，神往者屡日，讫今于酒樽茶灶边每举以告所亲云。

    　　兰岩曰：无修炼法，无丹鼎药，倏而成仙，何其易也！予意此翁亦老死耳，魂游天外，惚如有所遇，非真有仙人引之入山也。不然或先达午倦，思想成梦，与蕉鹿等耳。天下事当作如是观。

    Explanation

    67

    The man knew that the old man had become a celestial being and wept and knelt, seeking initiation. The old man said, "It is not yet the time. In the realm of fame, your position can reach the rank of Second Class, but you must not violate the two words 'hasty advancement,' and you must not forget the two words 'courageous retreat.' Commit them to memory, commit them to memory, please bid farewell." After speaking, he jumped into the river and walked on water as if it were solid ground, disappearing in an instant. Only the moon shining white in the middle of the river remained, endless in sight. The man wandered, melancholy, and sighed at the vast ocean. I came to seek, returned silently to the boat, longingly for several days, until now, often mentioned to close friends at wine cups and tea stoves.

    Lan Yan said: Without any cultivation methods, without any elixirs or alchemical tools, instantly becoming a celestial being, how easy it is! I think this old man also died of old age, his soul wandering to the heavens, as if he met something, it was not that a true celestial being led him into the mountains. Otherwise, perhaps he was tired after midday, his thoughts turned into dreams, it was like the dream of the monkey and the deer. All things in the world should be viewed in this way.

    68

    苏　仲　芬苏太学桂，字仲芬。肄业入都，为王给谏西席。王寓近梁家园，虽属外城，地极荒僻，王患门户逼侧，裏居近市，欲别觅数椽以居子弟。适坊间有空宅一区，扃键以求售者，相隔仅一街，王喜其密迩，乃以百金易券焉。辟荒除秽，垩壁糊窗，又费数十金，遂焕然以新，俾仲芬及一仆一僮移居其中。王子弟朝往暮还，从仲芬讲贯，宾主甚便。或有言宅素凶者，仲芬曰：“我不信怪，怪何由作？勿多言徒乱人意也！”

    　　居无何，娇异渐兴。一日薄暮，仆自市沽酒归，见一曲背媪，目赤而多泪，自厨下出，指顾间已泯形迹。又一日，瞥见一老翁，戴软檐白毡帽，独立庭中，负手看月，长不及三尺。仆大声叱之，则隐。僮间亦遇之。独仲芬无所睹，愈咎其谬妄。会乡试，仲芬率其仆诣国子监录科，约三四日方得出城，唯留僮守宅。

    Explanation

    68

    Su Zhongfen, a scholar from the Imperial College, with the courtesy name Zhongfen, entered the capital to study and served as the private tutor of Wang's censor. Wang's residence was near Liangyuan Garden, which, although part of the outer city, was extremely remote and desolate. Wang was troubled by the cramped surroundings and the proximity to the market, and he wished to find a separate place to house his children. Coincidentally, there was an empty house in the neighborhood, locked and sealed to be sold, separated by only one street. Wang was pleased with its closeness and bought it for a hundred gold coins. After clearing the wilderness and removing filth, he whitewashed the walls and pasted paper on the windows, spending another several dozen gold coins, and the house was completely renovated. He allowed Zhongfen, a servant, and a boy to move in. Wang's children would come and go daily, studying with Zhongfen, and both host and guest were very comfortable. Some said the house had been traditionally ominous, but Zhongfen replied, "I do not believe in ghosts, how could they appear? Do not talk too much, it only confuses people!"

    After some time, strange phenomena began to occur. One evening, the servant returned from the market with wine and saw an old woman with a hunched back, red eyes, and many tears, emerging from the kitchen. In the blink of an eye, she disappeared. Another day, the servant caught a glimpse of an old man wearing a soft-brimmed white felt hat, standing alone in the courtyard, looking at the moon with his hands behind his back, no more than three feet tall. The servant shouted at him, and the old man vanished. The boy also encountered him occasionally. But Zhongfen saw nothing, blaming it more for being absurd. When the provincial examination came, Zhongfen took his servant to the Imperial College for registration, staying for about three or four days before leaving the city, leaving only the boy to guard the house.

    69

    时当七月，炎暑未消，僮支扉作榻，当户高眠。夜半时，睡初觉，闻庭中有女人笑语声，不禁毛发如磔，蝟缩衾中，唯露一耳在外，以察动静。惜为板壁所隔，听之不甚了了，间闻数语，颇明晓者，云：“鬻酒熟矣，我不谋今夕为婢子服役，并致老子夤夜奔驰。适我与十一妹出溲时，渠哆口坌息，尻高于首，诘其故，始知为婢子，往市鸡子，为沙回子家狪犬所逐，坐此狼狈。十一妹不情太盛。”转憨笑不止。“我家阿连大不平，行当与婢子较论矣。”随闻群笑声。又闻一女子骂且笑曰：“淫婢勿太轻狂，明日二翰林来，若尚敢如此喋喋，我等当醵金奉谢！”旋复有应答者，声音清锐如燕语，模糊不复可辨。直至五更始寂。僮瑟缩畏耸，浃体汗流，一夜不寐。翌日逢人则述之。

    Explanation

    69

    During the month of July, the scorching heat had yet to dissipate. A servant propped open a door to create a bed and slept high against it. In the middle of the night, just as he was waking up, he heard the sound of women's laughter and conversation in the courtyard. His hair stood on end, and he curled up in the quilt, exposing only one ear to listen to the sounds outside. The sound was muffled by the wall, making it unclear, but occasionally, he could faintly understand some phrases. One woman said, "The wine is ready, and I didn't plan to serve the servant and make the old man run errands at night." Another woman described, "Just now, when I went to the bathroom with my sister, I saw someone with a big mouth breathing heavily and a high buttocks. When I asked why, I found out she was a servant girl who went to buy eggs and was chased by a dog from the Sand Turn house, causing her to be in a mess. My sister is too emotional." She then laughed uncontrollably. "My brother is very angry and will soon argue with the servant girl." They heard the sound of group laughter. Another woman scolded and laughed, "Don't be too bold, servant girl. Two scholars will come tomorrow. If you still dare to talk so much, we will pay you a fine!" There was a faint response with a clear and sharp voice like a swallow, but it was too faint to understand. It was quiet until dawn. The servant trembled with fear, sweating all over, and did not sleep all night. The next day, he told others about what had happened.

    70

    王之侄皆少年好事者，闻之，伪请于给谏曰：“苏先生入城，馆中只余一僮，曾嘱予弟兄暂就彼宿，以防不虞，用是请命。”给谏许之，二子喜跃，并襆被以往。饮至夜半，始就枕席，假寐达旦，毫无所闻。次夜亦然。苏已出城，之二子乃移去，遂亦以怪异为谬，共相非笑，再告，亦不信矣。

    　　越二日，仲芬夜间苦热，起坐榻上，恍惚隔窗纱见一人步履院内。疑是僮仆未寐，初不以为意，俄而缓步近阶，徘徊月下，仿佛戴髢，如蜂之就窗。潜谛辨，是一女郎，衣轻绡，蹑高履，丰姿袅娜，已足销魂。继而侧身回睇，倾绝人寰。仲芬目眩意迷，马腾猿逐，心知其异，而不克自制。女睨窗而笑曰：“何物书迂，蓿盘甫彻，乃便窥人家闺秀耶？”仲芬应声曰：“蜂蝶苟无花香勾引，狂浪何为哉？闻子挠我仆僮久矣，今既遂披睹，盍入斗室，一示玉容，则书生虽死应亦得好处也。

    Explanation

    70

    The nephews of the king, all young and curious, heard about it and feigned a request to the censor, saying, "Mr. Su has entered the city, and there is only one servant left in the inn. He once instructed us brothers to temporarily stay there to prevent any unexpected events, so we are seeking permission." The censor agreed, and the two young men were delighted. They packed their bedding and went there. They drank until midnight, then lay down to sleep, taking brief naps until dawn, and heard nothing unusual. The next night was the same. Su had already left the city, and the two young men then moved on. They also considered strange things as false, ridiculed the idea, and did not believe any further reports.

    Two days later, on a hot night, Zhongfen got up from his bed, feeling restless. He vaguely saw someone walking in the courtyard through the window screen. He thought it might be a servant who hadn't gone to sleep yet and didn't pay much attention. Soon, the person slowly approached the steps, lingering under the moonlight, wearing a hairpin, like a bee approaching the window. He secretly observed and identified her as a young lady, dressed in light silk, wearing high shoes, with a graceful and charming appearance that was already enough to captivate him. She then turned her head sideways and looked back, her beauty surpassing the ordinary. Zhongfen was dazed and entranced, like a horse galloping or a monkey chasing, knowing that she was extraordinary, but unable to control himself. The girl glanced at the window and smiled, saying, "What kind of bookworm are you? Haven't you finished your meal yet, and already you are peeping at a lady of the house?" Zhongfen replied, "If bees and butterflies don't have the fragrance of flowers to lure them, why would they be so reckless? I have heard that you have been bothering my servants for a long time. Now that I have finally seen you, why don't you enter this small room and show me your beautiful face? Then, even if the scholar dies, he should also have a good time."

    71

    ”女不答，但嗤嗤笑之以鼻，款步而入。秋波流慧，娇媚可怜，窃意西子南威，不是过也。仲芬揖坐榻上，调冰水，剖沈瓜以进。女著藕色罗衫，如薄雾笼花，玉肌依稀可见。碧纱裙下，见粉光馯馯.挑灯睹之，则跣足曳朱履。仲芬以游语入之，曰：“古有赤脚婢，卿岂其流亚欤？”女冁然曰：“履上足如霜，不著鸦头袜。古美人未约足时，畴不如我，汝第未之见耳。”仲芬戏捉一足，谛视之，　跗丰妍，底平指剑，长止六寸，扑鼻作异香。心大动，突前拥之，女亦不拒，遂相狎昵，尽夕绸缪，鸡鸣始起。

    Explanation

    71

    The woman did not answer, but she laughed at him with a sneer and entered gracefully. Her eyes, like autumn waves, were full of wisdom, and she was cute and endearing. I secretly thought that she was no less beautiful than Xi Shi and Nan Wei. Zhong Fen bowed and sat on the bed, adjusting the ice water and cutting the melon to offer it. She wore a lotus-colored silk robe, like a thin mist surrounding a flower, and her fair skin was barely visible. Under her green silk skirt, her skin glowed faintly. When he lit the lamp to see her, he noticed that she was barefoot but wearing red shoes. Zhong Fen teased her, saying, "In ancient times, there were barefoot maids. Are you one of them?" The woman smiled and replied, "When shod, my feet are like frost, and I don't wear black stockings. In ancient times, when beautiful women didn't wear shoes, who was more beautiful than me? You just haven't seen it yet." Zhong Fen playfully grabbed one of her feet and examined it closely. Her foot was plump and beautiful, with a flat sole and pointed toes, measuring only six inches in length. It emitted a strange, intoxicating fragrance. His heart was greatly moved, and he suddenly embraced her. The woman did not resist, and they then engaged in intimate affection, staying up all night, and only rose at the crow of the rooster.

    72

    自此无夜不至，自述姓花，世系陇西，徙来顺天两世矣。宅后梁家园，儿家故居也。与君有夙份，故相就耳。仲芬曰：“诚非偶然也。第圣人之道，胞与为怀，故万物并育而不相害。予洞此理久矣。卿狐耶鬼耶？幸勿见诳。”女笑曰：“儿仙子也。奈何疑为狐鬼？”仲芬曰：“不然。吾闻之《仙书》曰：”不死者不食而神往。‘见卿饮食如凡人，且不戒荤酒，仙子固如是乎？“女哂曰：”人谓执而不化者为书痴，今信然矣。君既以书致诘，即请以书解纷。君独不见《神仙》诸记之所载乎？龙肝麟脯，惟仙食之；玉醴金浆，惟仙饮之；他如千年之桃、万年之藕、百石之醪、凤凰之髓、以及交梨火枣、橘液霞觞，凡此之属，散见于诗书者，指不胜屈。

    Explanation

    72

    From this time on, no night passes without her coming. She introduced herself as the surname Flower, her ancestral lineage traces back to Longxi, and she has lived in Shuntian for two generations. Her home is behind Liang Garden, which was our family's former residence. I have had a predestined connection with you, hence I come to you. Zhongfen said, "It is indeed not by chance. But the way of the sage, embracing all as one, allows all things to grow together without harming each other. I have understood this principle for a long time. Are you a fox spirit or a ghost? Please do not deceive me." The woman smiled and said, "I am a fairy. Why do you doubt that I am a fox spirit or a ghost?" Zhongfen replied, "No, I have heard in the Book of Immortals that 'those who do not die do not eat and their spirits wander.' I see you eating and drinking like an ordinary person, and you do not abstain from meat and alcohol. Are immortals really like this?" The woman laughed and said, "People say that those who cling to the book without changing are bookworms. Now I believe it. Since you have questioned me using the book, I will use the book to resolve the confusion. Have you not seen what is recorded in the various accounts of immortals? Dragon liver and unicorn meat, only immortals eat them; jade liquor and golden wine, only immortals drink them; other things like thousand-year-old peaches, ten-thousand-year-old lotus roots, hundred-stone wine, phoenix marrow, and various other exotic fruits and wines. All these things are scattered throughout the poems and books, too numerous to count."

    73

    仙人安有不饮食者？且君言过矣。若不饮食，即可为神仙矣，何蚕食而不饮，春尽则僵；蝉饮而不食，秋残则枯；蜉蝣不饮不食，乃朝生而暮死，谓为神仙，可乎，不可？“仲芬语塞，但轻拍其肩曰：”卿妄口夺理，吾不复与尔置辩。然既有称仙子矣，吾闻仙子能知未来事，卿视我今科傍上有名否？“女曰：”君才疏而气高，每从轻薄朋友，务为谐谑，此大不利。夫隐恶扬善，现在功德，何惜齿牙余慧，而必以朴讷为耻，惟尖巧之是逞乎？恐滑稽之名一立，而祯祥亦从之而减，非君子永言配命之道也。今科复无望矣。君苟从此自新，功名中尚可小就，否则会当见君于饿莩中耳。“仲芬闻之，面灰心死，悚然再拜曰：”卿言深中膏肓，敢不佩为弦韦！“女去，数月不至。

    Explanation

    73

    How could there be immortals who do not eat or drink? Moreover, your words are wrong. If one does not eat or drink, they could become a god or immortal. But why do silkworms eat but not drink, and die at the end of spring; cicadas drink but not eat, and wither at the end of autumn; mayflies neither eat nor drink, and live only from morning to evening. Can they be called immortals? No, they cannot. "Zong Fen was speechless and gently patted her shoulder, saying, 'You are using false words to win an argument; I will no longer argue with you. However, since you have called yourself an immortal, I have heard that immortals can foresee the future. Do you see my name on the list for this examination?' The woman replied, 'Your talent is limited but your spirit is high. You often associate with shallow friends and indulge in witty banter, which is very disadvantageous. Concealing evil and promoting good, and performing present deeds, why should you spare idle talk and feel ashamed of being simple and honest, only showing off your cleverness? Once the reputation of being witty and amusing is established, even good omens will diminish. This is not the way of a gentleman to align with destiny. There is no hope for this examination this time. If you reform yourself, you may still achieve some success in your career; otherwise, you will see me among the starving.' Zong Fen, upon hearing this, turned pale and felt his heart sink. He bowed twice in fear and said, 'Your words have hit the mark; how dare I not take them as a guiding principle!' The woman left, and did not come back for several months.

    74

    场事毕，仲芬文章佳甚，同人决其不出五魁。及揭晓，竟落孙山。女至，仲芬荧眥欲泪，女慰戒再三。诸同乡有操眊矂者，约登陶然亭。因举酒政，仲芬醉后不检，杂以因果佛经。日暮归寓，女已在房，正色责之曰：“圣人之言，何故侮之？取罪大矣！君正如吹胀猎脬，毫无骨力，所谓粪土之墙，不可杇者。儿相从欲胡为乎？”言讫，忿忿出房。仲芬惭怍无地，跪而牵裾，女艴然而去。去时遗衣一袭，仲芬始而缄密，久而渐泄于门人，索观其衣，薄如蝉翼，约重六铢。后数年，王子侄同入馆阁，二翰林之说始验。仲芬连踬棘闱，不获一荐，更思女子容色，咄咄书空。又一年，竟以贫病卒于京邸，柩厝义冢，至今未正首邱。李高鱼与仲芬为总角交，习知其事，时向予缅述之。询及女衣所在，已归绐谏携去江南矣。

    Explanation

    74

    The examination was over, and Zongfen's essay was exceptionally good; his fellow competitors were convinced he would not rank among the top five. When the results were announced, he surprisingly failed to make the list. The woman arrived, and Zongfen's eyes welled up with tears, ready to burst. She comforted and warned him repeatedly. Among the fellow townspeople, there were those who were blind to the truth, and they invited Zongfen to visit Tao Ran Pavilion. During a drinking session, Zongfen, after getting drunk, lost control and mixed in Buddhist texts about karma and causality. As dusk fell, he returned to his lodging, and the woman was already in the room. She scolded him sternly, saying, "Why do you mock the words of the sages? You have committed a great sin! You are like a swollen bladder, lacking any strength, as the saying goes, 'a wall made of dung and earth, which cannot be whitewashed.' What do you intend to achieve by following me?" After speaking, she angrily left the room. Zongfen felt utterly ashamed and knelt down, trying to stop her, but she left with a frown. As she departed, she left behind a robe. Zongfen initially kept it secret but eventually revealed it to his disciples over time. When they requested to see the robe, it was as thin as a cicada's wing, weighing approximately six zhu. Several years later, Wang's nephews both entered the imperial library, and the prophecy of the two Hanlin scholars came true. Zongfen repeatedly failed in the imperial examinations and received no recommendation, and he began to think more about the woman's appearance, muttering to himself. Another year passed, and he died in the capital due to poverty and illness. His coffin was placed in a public burial ground, and he has not yet been properly buried in his ancestral tomb. Li Gaoyu, who was Zongfen's childhood friend, knew the details of the story and often recounted it to me. When I asked about the location of the woman's robe, it was said that it had been taken back by the censor and carried away to Jiangnan.

    75

    闲斋曰：观仲芬所遇或谓是鬼，予力辩其为狐。

    　　恩茂先曰：无论是狐是鬼，仲芬儒衣儒冠而为人师表者，较此女为何如？

    　　兰岩曰：轻薄之口，见弃于狐，况于人哉。乃当闻言再拜之后，复不自检，亵渎圣神，是自取罪戾也。读书者可不以此为戒欤？

    　　红　姑　娘京城敌楼，内外凡五十座，高大深遂，往往为狐鼠所栖。内城东北隅角楼内，有一狐，化而为女子，红衫翠裙，年可十六七，艳丽绝伦。守城兵往往见之，咸知其非人，而罔不狂惑失志。以其衣红，共以红姑娘称之。间有儇薄少年，或际良宵薄醉，一动色心，至楼下薄言往挑，即闻娇音曰：“尔勿妄为也。”归辄头痛难忍，否则唇忽肿起如桃，必哀恳悔过，适乃已。以此群畏之，无敢戏言者。

    Explanation

    75

    Xianzhai remarked: Observing what Zhongfen encountered, some say it was a ghost, but I strongly argue that it was a fox.

    Enmaoxian said: Whether it is a fox or a ghost, Zhongfen, who dresses in Confucian attire and serves as a role model, how does he compare to this woman?

    Lanyan remarked: Flippant speech is rejected by the fox, how much more so by humans! After hearing the words and bowing twice, they again fail to restrain themselves, desecrating the sacred, and this is self-inflicted guilt. Those who read can they not take this as a warning?

    Red Girl: In the capital city, there are fifty fortified towers inside and outside, tall, large, and deep, often inhabited by foxes and rats. Inside the northeast corner tower of the inner city, there is a fox that transforms into a woman, wearing a red shirt and green skirt, about sixteen or seventeen years old, extremely beautiful. The city guards often see her, all knowing she is not human, and none can resist being bewitched and losing their resolve. Because of her red clothes, they collectively call her Red Girl. Occasionally, flirtatious young men, or during a pleasant night when slightly intoxicated, once aroused by lust, go down to the foot of the tower and lightly speak to invite her. Immediately, they hear a soft and sweet voice saying, "Do not act rashly." Returning home, they suffer from unbearable headaches, otherwise, their lips suddenly swell like peaches. They must humbly beg for forgiveness, only then does it stop. Because of this, everyone fears her, and no one dares to make light of her.

    76

    步军校赫色，年六十余矣。一夕，上城值宿，独坐铺中，思酒不得。三更后，门外闻弹指声，亟问不答，启户视之，则二八佳丽人也，五色并驰，不可殚形。详而视之，夺人目睛，后随二双鬟婢，捧酒壶，立月下。校素有胆，惊定，即悟其为狐。询其那得深夜来此高城？答曰：“儿洪氏，行三，知翁思酒，谨以家酿相贻。”校大喜，延之入室。即以其携来之酒肴，借以款仓卒客。醉后兴高，问：“三姐有所求乎？”女曰：“以狐媚惑人者，皆有求于人者也。翁一身贫病，且老，儿何求于翁？所以亲近翁者，以翁有大恩于儿故也。”校茫然不解所谓。女曰：“翁乃忘松亭赎儿之事耶？”翁始大悟，叹惋者久之，遂认为义女。

    Explanation

    76

    The Infantry Officer Heshuo, now over sixty years old, one night took his night watch duty atop the city wall, sitting alone in his quarters, yearning for wine but unable to obtain any. After the third watch, he heard the sound of finger snapping outside the door. He hurriedly asked, but received no reply. He opened the door and saw a woman of exceptional beauty, her appearance so dazzling that it was beyond description. Upon closer inspection, she was breathtakingly beautiful, followed by two young maids with twin buns, carrying wine pots and standing under the moonlight. The officer, who had always been brave, calmed down and realized that she must be a fox spirit. He asked her how she could come to this high city so late at night. She replied, "I am the third daughter of the Hong family, and I knew you were craving wine, so I have brought you some home-brewed wine as a gift." The officer was greatly pleased and invited her into his room. He immediately served her the wine and food she had brought, treating her as a guest who had arrived unexpectedly. After they drank and became merry, he asked, "Do you have anything you wish to ask of me, Sister Three?" The woman said, "Those who use their fox charms to allure others always have something they want from them. You, with your poor health and advanced age, what could I possibly want from you? I am close to you because you have done me a great favor." The officer was puzzled and did not understand what she meant. She said, "Have you forgotten the matter of redeeming me at Songting?" The officer suddenly realized and sighed for a long time, then took her in as his adopted daughter.

    77

    自是必当值宿，校必多方散其侪伍，独扶筇至角楼下，告曰：“致语三姑娘，我今日上班矣。”至晚女果至，二婢随进酒馔，珍美错陈。校夜夜餍之。每心有所欲，未发，女已先知，无不咄嗟立办。校尝以玉环赠，女再拜以受，什袭藏之。校与语谈时，自念皤然一翁，将旦夕犯雾露，泣数行下。女曰：“勿伤，儿视爹尚可三十年活也。”乃授校以导引之术，行之颇效。

    　　女无他异处，惟喜　面，一夜恒四五次。校少子方娶，苦无杯盘，将赁诸市。女曰：“是无庸，儿当为爹假之。”至期，果有金银器物，杂然陈于房中，不测所自。家人怪之，校以实告，始各欣喜。事毕，已皆失去矣。校次子为护军，闻女美，潜上城至值所，从窗隙窃窥，竟无所见，但翁一人自言自笑自饮而已。校酒后，偶匿其玉斝，归家旋失。果有急需，女必周以巨金，则尽朱提也。如是者十余年。

    Explanation

    77

    From this point on, one must take turns on duty. The schoolmaster must scatter his companions in various ways, and he alone supports a walking stick to the corner of the building, saying, "Convey to Miss San, I am on duty today." By evening, the girl indeed arrives, and two maids follow, bringing in wine and delicacies, beautifully arranged. The schoolmaster enjoys it every night. Whenever he has a desire, before he expresses it, the girl already knows and can fulfill it instantly. The schoolmaster once gave her a jade ring, and she bowed twice to receive it, carefully storing it away. When talking with her, he thought of himself as an old man, about to face the morning dew and mist, and tears streamed down his face. The girl said, "Do not be sad, I believe Father can live for another thirty years." She then taught him the technique of guiding and leading, which proved effective.

    The girl had no other unusual behavior, only she enjoyed seeing him, doing so several times a night. The schoolmaster's younger son was about to get married, but they lacked tableware, planning to rent it from the market. The girl said, "There is no need, I will borrow it for you." By the time, there were indeed silver and gold utensils, variously displayed in the room, their origin unknown. The family was surprised, and the schoolmaster told them the truth, and they all became happy. After the event, they had all disappeared. The schoolmaster's second son, a guard, heard of the girl's beauty, secretly climbed the city wall to the duty post, peering through a window crack, but saw nothing, only the old man talking to himself, laughing, and drinking alone. After drinking, the schoolmaster hid his jade cup, and upon returning home, it was gone. Whenever there was an urgent need, the girl would provide a large sum of money, all of it being pure gold. This went on for more than ten years.

    78

    女一夕忽泫然惨泣曰：“缘已尽矣，从此永别。”校惊问之，不答。五更后，哽咽而去。校亦酸恻，然未知所云所以永别者。翌日，执金吾以校年老，请于朝，勒令休致，校乃叹悟。

    　　先是校当壮岁时，为骁骑校，从征葛尔丹，凯旋至松亭，同人捕得一黑狐，欲杀之以取其皮，狐向校哀鸣，校心动，以金二两赎而纵之。事三十年矣，不意至是乃获其报，后校年至九十余，无疾而终，狐亦徙去，不知所之。

    　　兰岩曰：狐以异类，犹知酬恩报德，贞静自守，不甘以媚惑人。奈何世间以七尺之躯，胁肩谄笑，干求于人，恬不为怪，而及以守正不阿者为庸人，因自居为识时务之俊杰，比比是也。吁，可慨也哉！

    　　陈　宝　祠蒲东杜阳，姿质美秀，年二十，未婚。雍正初，从其舅为贾于兴安。舅年老，常居布店，使阳贩货，恒往返秦晋间，一年率二次。

    Explanation

    78

    One night, the woman suddenly wept bitterly, saying, "The bond has ended, from now on forever parting." The official was shocked and asked her, but she did not respond. After the fifth watch, she left while sobbing. The official felt sad and pitiful, but he did not understand the reason for her saying they would forever part. The next day, the执金吾, seeing the old age of the protagonist, made a request to the court, and the official was ordered to retire. The protagonist then sighed and understood.

    Previously, when the protagonist was in his prime, he served as a cavalry officer and followed the army to campaign against Galdan. After the victorious return, he reached Songting, where his companions caught a black fox. They wanted to kill it to take its skin, but the fox pleaded with the protagonist. The protagonist's heart moved, and he paid two taels of gold to redeem and release it. It had been thirty years since then, and the protagonist did not expect to receive such a reward. Later, the protagonist lived to be over ninety years old, dying peacefully without illness, and the fox also moved away, its whereabouts unknown.

    Lan Yan said: The fox, being a different species, still knows how to repay kindness and maintain its integrity, unwilling to seduce or deceive people. How can it be that in the world, people with seven feet of body fawn and flatter, seek favors from others, and become so accustomed to it that they no longer find it strange, while those who adhere to principles without bending to pressure are called ordinary people, and those who understand the times and are capable are called discerning and talented? This is common, and it is truly regrettable!

    Translation:

    One night, the woman suddenly wept bitterly, saying, "The bond has ended, from now on forever parting." The official was shocked and asked her, but she did not respond. After the fifth watch, she left while sobbing. The official felt sad and pitiful, but he did not understand the reason for her saying they would forever part. The next day, the执金吾, seeing the old age of the protagonist, made a request to the court, and the official was ordered to retire. The protagonist then sighed and understood.

    Previously, when the protagonist was in his prime, he served as a cavalry officer and followed the army to campaign against Galdan. After the victorious return, he reached Songting, where his companions caught a black fox. They wanted to kill it to take its skin, but the fox pleaded with the protagonist. The protagonist's heart moved, and he paid two taels of gold to redeem and release it. It had been thirty years since then, and the protagonist did not expect to receive such a reward. Later, the protagonist lived to be over ninety years old, dying peacefully without illness, and the fox also moved away, its whereabouts unknown.

    Lan Yan said: The fox, being a different species, still knows how to repay kindness and maintain its integrity, unwilling to seduce or deceive people. How can it be that in the world, people with seven feet of body fawn and flatter, seek favors from others, and become so accustomed to it that they no longer find it strange, while those who adhere to principles without bending to pressure are called ordinary people, and those who understand the times and are capable are called discerning and talented? This is common, and it is truly regrettable!

    79

    一日，发自褒斜入栈道，正苦崎岖，一虎来，攫其仆去。阳惊惶失足堕深壑中。幸为落叶所籍，不致损伤。举首四山入云，无由得出。无何，日已暮，林深箐密，泉水乱鸣。据石自伤，傍徨无策。既而万峰皆瞑，群动尽息，隐隐见林际灯光。阳大喜，迍邅以就之。

    　　至则巨第一区，门容驷马，门旁别有小室，灯火荧然。叩之，一长鬣叟出，讶曰：“郎哪得来此？”告以故，恍然曰：“郎其杜阳乎？”阳诧曰：“然。翁何以知之？”叟曰：“主人待郎久矣。请暂歇于此，当为郎先容也。”呼媪出，叟自去。俄偕一僮，提绛纱烛笼，坌息而至，促之曰：“主人伫俟，请速往。”阳从之，入朱门，沤钉兽环，宛似王侯第宅，历院落数重，悉雕墙峻宇，刻桷丹楹，僮仆往来，络绎不绝。复有群聚窥客者，粉白黛绿，累足骈肩，窃窃笑语。阳自惭市井，颇益逡巡。先至一湢室，童子进澡豆。浴讫，更新衣，易冠履，始引之达广厅。

    Explanation

    79

    One day, Yang departed from Baoxie and entered the Zhan road, just then finding it difficult and rugged. Suddenly, a tiger came and seized his servant and took it away. Yang was startled and panicked, losing his footing and falling into a deep ravine. Fortunately, he was cushioned by fallen leaves and did not suffer serious injury. Raising his head, he saw the four mountains reaching up to the clouds, with no way to get out. Soon, the sun had set, and the forest was deep and dense, with waterfalls and streams making loud noises. He sat on a rock, mourning himself, wandering aimlessly without a plan. Soon after, all the peaks fell into darkness, and all creatures ceased their movements. Dimly, he saw lights at the edge of the forest. Yang was greatly pleased and, despite his difficulties, made his way to it.

    Upon arriving, he found a large area, with a gate that could accommodate four horses. Beside the gate, there was a small room with dimly lit lamps. He knocked on the door, and an elderly man with long hair came out, surprised and asked, "How did you come here, sir?" Yang told him the reason, and the old man suddenly said, "Are you Du Yang?" Yang was surprised and asked, "Yes. How do you know?" The old man replied, "The master has been waiting for you for a long time. Please rest here for a moment; I will introduce you." He called out to a woman to come out, and the old man left. Soon, he came with a young servant carrying a red silk candle lantern, breathing heavily, and urged him, "The master is waiting, please go quickly." Yang followed him, entering the red gate, with ornate decorations and animal rings, resembling the residence of a prince or nobleman. He passed through several courtyards, all with carved walls, high buildings, and red pillars, with servants constantly coming and going. There were also groups of people peering at the guest, some with white faces and green eyebrows, standing shoulder to shoulder, whispering and laughing. Yang felt ashamed of his common background and hesitated even more. First, he went to a bathroom, where a young servant brought him soap. After bathing, he changed into new clothes and shoes. Finally, he was led to a large hall.

    80

    主人揖之，升阶，分庭抗礼。觑主人年可四十许，赤面修髯，被服五采，非复本朝制度。阳惊疑殊甚。主人致敬曰：“郎与小女有夙契，今当了之，幸勿却也。”阳达心而懦，不能尽其辞，惟再拜诺诺而已。主人即命成礼。傧至，见侍女如云，笙箫聒耳，拥闺秀搭面而出。绣衣楚楚，玉佩珊珊。堂中设红氍毹。一交拜间，麝兰芬馥，入脑薰心。及入房合卺，注目凝睇，女容华绝代，面色如朝霞和雪，光艳射人。虽未睹姑射飞仙，即此窃悬拟之。定情后，和好无间，问青春几何，曰十六；何姓，曰姓陈；父为何官，曰未尝筮仕也。三朝，亲戚来瞊者数十家，则尽富贵也。阳独与主人之甥封生者，相与莫逆。女时戒之曰：“大人无嗣，方欲郎充半子。郎孱弱，封哥性暴戾，可亲不可近也。”阳颔之而不能绝。

    Explanation

    80

    The host bowed to him, and they ascended the steps, standing in the courtyard as equals. Observing the host, who appeared to be around forty years old, with a red face, long beard, and dressed in multicolored garments, which were not in line with the current fashion. Yang was greatly surprised and somewhat suspicious. The host paid his respects and said, "There is a predestined connection between you and my daughter. I will now fulfill it, so please do not refuse." Yang understood but felt hesitant, unable to express himself fully, and could only agree with two bows and a formal "yes." The host then immediately proceeded with the wedding ceremony. As the attendants arrived, they saw a cloud of bridesmaids, and the sound of sheng and xiao filled the air, as the bride emerged, dressed in beautiful, colorful garments and adorned with jade pendants that jingled softly. A red carpet was laid out in the hall. During the exchange of bows, the fragrance of musk and orchids was so strong that it seemed to enter one's brain and heart. When they entered the room for the wedding night, Yang gazed at the bride, whose beauty was unparalleled, with a complexion like the morning glow and snow, radiating such brilliance that it almost blinded him. Though he had not seen a fairy from Mount Guanyue, he could only imagine her beauty in comparison. After the engagement was set, their relationship was harmonious and free of conflict. When asked about her age, she replied, "Sixteen." When asked her surname, she said, "Chen." When asked about her father's occupation, she said, "He has never held an official position." For the three-day post-wedding celebrations, dozens of wealthy relatives attended. Yang had a deep and unbreakable friendship with the host's nephew, Feng. The bride warned him, "My father has no heir and wishes for you to be like a half-brother to me. You are weak, and Feng is violent; you can be close to him but should not get too near." Yang nodded in agreement but could not sever the connection.

    81

    际女满月，亲戚咸集，阳拉封饮于房中。时当暑，封醉后，裸裎浮白。阳让之，曰：“此晏私之地，令表妹虽不在侧，亦须稍避嫌，奈何疏狂至此？”封辄怒，裂眥相向，曰：“汝本锥刀小子，窥窬分毫，吾怜汝游泳似鳏，聊执柯斧，得蒹葭倚于玉树，何啻登仙。乃酒后载呶，折辱于我，其将以我为匏瓜耶？”阳亦怒，提座侧铜镜击之，复挖毁其□。封跳怒咆哮，声如錯虎，诸亲来救，排解纷纭，举室喧嚣，掖封慰去。阳犹追出户外，谩骂万端。

    Explanation

    81

    When the daughter reached her first month, all the relatives gathered, and Yang invited Feng to drink in the room. It was summer, and after drinking, Feng became drunk, undressed, and toasted. Yang reprimanded him, saying, "This is a private place. Even though your cousin is not here, you should still avoid suspicion. Why be so reckless?" Feng immediately became angry, glaring at Yang, and said, "You are originally a small-minded person, peering at every detail. I pity you, swimming like a widower, and tried to help you, like a reed leaning on a jade tree, which is no different from ascending to heaven. But now you are drunk and making noise, humiliating me. Are you going to treat me like a gourd?" Yang also became angry, picked up a copper mirror from the side of the seat and hit him, then dug out his eyes. Feng jumped up in anger, roared like a tiger, and the relatives came to rescue, trying to mediate the chaos. The entire house was in turmoil, and they supported Feng and consoled him to leave. Yang still chased out to the courtyard, cursing him in many ways.

    82

    主人色变如灰，亦当阶鹄立，呼女至前来抚之曰：“奔蜂不能化藿蠋，越鸡不能伏鹄卵，予以杜郎入赘，胜负螟蛉。不意开罪封甥，祸不旋踵。亟当遣之，勿缓也！”女俯首悲啼，不能成语。阳闻之，悲愤自投于地，曰：“大人何遽出此言，致谋离逖？谅封蠢然一物，行类灌夫。自持葭莩，绎骚中冓。阳虽不敏，愿与旗鼓相当，必不贻大人之戚也。”主人惨然曰：“封甥杂居此山，历有年数，杜郎什伯，未足与□。老夫与弱息，并阖宅老小，无可畏封者。独虑杜郎睘睘独立，锁尾空山，　□走羽飞，悉非长技，不若出于幽谷，归慰父兄。天实为之，勿复留恋！”阳恸甚，跪不能起。女亦失声。主人乃遣两婢，掖阳出门，顿觉两脚离地，渐入半空，瞬息间，已置身栈阁。二婢遂化双雉，　□鸣而作。阳爽然若失，徘徊四顾，适阁畔有陈宝祠，荒废殊甚。阳入待旦，仰瞻所祀神，则俨然所见。感触再拜，涕泗滂沱。

    Explanation

    82

    The master's face turned ashen, and he stood motionless on the threshold. He called his daughter to come forward and comfort her, saying, "A flying bee cannot transform into a ground-dwelling moth, and a chicken from the south cannot hatch an egg of a crane. By allowing Yang to marry into our family, we have adopted him like a wasp carrying away a caterpillar. I did not expect to offend the phoenix, and misfortune is already at our door. We must send him away immediately, without delay!" The daughter bowed her head and wept bitterly, unable to form words. Upon hearing this, Yang was filled with grief and anger, throwing himself to the ground and exclaiming, "Why have you spoken these words so suddenly, leading to such a separation? Surely the phoenix is no more than a beast, behaving like a brute. It clings to its own kind, stirring up turmoil within our home. Though I am not clever, I am willing to stand my ground and will not bring shame upon you." The master looked grim and said, "The phoenix has lived in this mountain with us for many years. Yang, though ten or a hundred times less, is not enough to match it. Myself, my daughter, and the entire household, old and young, are not afraid of the phoenix. But I worry that Yang, lonely and independent, is confined in this empty mountain. With his limited skills, he cannot compete with running and flying creatures. It would be better for him to leave this deep valley and return to comfort his father and brothers. It is truly the will of heaven; do not linger any longer!" Yang was overcome with grief, unable to rise from his knees. The daughter also cried out in despair. The master then sent two maids to escort Yang out of the house. Yang suddenly felt both feet leaving the ground, gradually ascending into the half-sky, and in an instant, he found himself on a wooden platform. The two maids transformed into two pheasants, singing and acting as they flew. Yang felt lost and disoriented, wandering and looking around. Just beside the platform, there was a temple of Chen's treasure, which was extremely dilapidated. Yang entered the temple and waited until dawn, looking up at the deity being worshipped, who was exactly as he had seen before. Moved, he bowed twice, and tears streamed down his face.

    83

    次日，乞食返兴安。舅大惊，致诘，阳告以故。舅素博雅，闻之喟然，乃为之解曰：“封生者，即虎而攫仆者也，《广异记》有封使君之事，故袭以为姓。汝亦记十五岁时，从予至凤县南，捕得一雌雉，拟至邸第欲烹之，汝怜其哀鸣，潜纵之去。是以云有夙契也。古人得之而霸，吾侪小人，无可希冀，唯当致富耳。”后舅死，阳经商数年，累资百万。他日过落涧处，引领怅望，两泪交颐，重修陈宝祠，并招仆之魂以从祀焉。

    　　兰岩曰：物犹不忘旧恩，何以人而不知雉乎？

    Explanation

    83

    The next day, after begging for food, I returned to Xing'an. My uncle was greatly surprised and asked earnestly why I had done so. I openly revealed the reason. My uncle, who was typically well-educated and cultured, sighed deeply upon hearing the story and then explained, "The person known as 'Feng Sheng' is the one who was seized by a tiger. There is a story about 'Feng Shizhong' in the Widely Recorded Strange Events. That is why I adopted the surname 'Feng'. You also remember when we were fifteen years old, you followed me to the south of Feng County, where we caught a female pheasant. We planned to cook it at the mansion, but you pitied its sad cry and secretly released it. That is why it is said there is a predestined connection. Ancient people gained power through such connections, but we common people have no such hopes; we can only aim to become wealthy." After the uncle's death, I pretended to be a businessman for several years and accumulated a fortune of a million. One day, passing by the place where the tiger fell, I looked back with a sad expression, tears streaming down both cheeks. I rebuilt the Temple of Chen Bao and invited the spirit of the servant to join the worship.

    Lan Yan commented, "Animals still remember old kindness; why should humans not remember the pheasant?"

    84

    张五知县某，病怔忡，日夜心悸。恒纠合家人数十辈，通宵列烛环守，而犹一夜数惊，越半月余矣。坊间有张五者，年四十余，夙鬻豆腐为业。常起五更，一夜违时，四更便起，嘱妻作腐。妻曰：“无乃太早？”张曰：“一日不力作，一日食不足。早作早卖，一大好事。汝起点灯，我暂出解手便转也。”

    　　乃启门至弄内，方欲登溷，忽有二人过其前，唤曰：“张五，此间来！”张以为素识，从之至街口，同立人家檐下。审视二人，竟大昧平生，各着青衣，垂绿头带，冠红帽，执朱票，酷似衙门中隶役。向张曰：“有一事相烦，不可推诿。”张问何事，二役曰：“不必穷究，姑同我等去。”言毕，向东走。张心大不愿，而两脚殊不自由，踉跄随行，绕出街市，至知县衙门杙桓前。见六人立大门下，躬擐甲胄，皆长八九尺。二役不敢进，乃转至衙后一水窦前，使张先入，张不肯，役推之，不觉已在墙内。二役亦相继入。历高垣数重，悉如此，竟达寝所。

    Explanation

    84

    Magistrate Zhang Wu was suffering from怔忡病, experiencing palpitations day and night. His family gathered around him all night, lighting candles to guard him, but he was still startled multiple times each night, lasting for over half a month. In the marketplace, there was another Zhang Wu, a man in his forties who had long been selling tofu for a living. He usually got up at five o'clock, but one night he got up at four o'clock, telling his wife to make the tofu. His wife said, "Isn't it too early?" Zhang replied, "If one day I don't work hard, I won't have enough food for that day. Working early and selling early is a great good thing. You light the lamp, and I'll go out to relieve myself and come back soon."

    He opened the door and went into the alley, just about to use the toilet, when suddenly two people passed in front of him, calling out, "Zhang Wu, come this way!" Zhang thought they were familiar to him and followed them to the street corner, where they stood under the eaves of a house. Upon closer inspection, he realized he had never seen them before. They were dressed in blue robes, wearing green headbands, red hats, and holding red tickets, very much like officials from the courthouse. They said to Zhang, "We have something we need your help with, and you cannot refuse." Zhang asked what it was, and the two officials replied, "There is no need to ask further, just come with us." After they spoke, they headed east. Zhang was very reluctant, but his legs felt completely unresponsive, and he stumbled along with them, eventually leaving the market and arriving at the magistrate's office in front of the gate. There were six men standing at the main gate, all wearing armor and helmets, each about eight or nine feet tall. The two officials dared not enter and instead turned to a water outlet at the back of the office, making Zhang go in first. Zhang refused, but the officials pushed him, and without realizing it, he was already inside the wall. The two officials also followed him in. They passed through several high walls, all the same, and finally reached the sleeping quarters.

    85

    窗上灯光甚明，命张窥之，见知县某呻吟于床，床角及脚后，坐妇女六七人，地上满罽毯，亦有男妇八九人，群坐其间。还告二役，二役亦来　。五更向尽，二役颇忧惶，相与频频窥伺。又移时，某稍安，诸男女倦惫殊甚，或鼾而膉，或寝而伸。二役喜跃，急取一铁链付张曰：“汝速入房，将此链系知县项上，勿恐勿怖，竟牵之以出！”张惊曰：“彼知县，官长也。我何人，敢相近乎？”二役曰：“彼虽为官长，而贪财好色，滥杀酷刑，今且为罪人，奚复可畏？”张趦趄，终不敢前。二役慌遽，复极力推挤之，慞惶间已在房内。不得已，即以链系知县颈上，反走而去。二役迎之，同循旧路。张回顾知县，已系颈同行矣，大骇。知县默无一语。

    Explanation

    85

    The light on the window was very bright. Zhang was ordered to look inside and saw County Magistrate Someone groaning on the bed. At the corner of the bed and behind his feet, sat six or seven women. On the ground were full of carpets, and also there were eight or nine men and women, all sitting among them. Zhang returned to report to the two officials, and the two officials also came. Early morning was nearly over, and the two officials were quite worried and anxious, together frequently peeping and watching.
