After some time, Someone became slightly better, and all the men and women were very tired, some snoring and sleeping, some sleeping and stretching. The two officials were happy and leaped, rushing to take a chain and gave it to Zhang, saying, "You quickly enter the room, and tie this chain around the County Magistrate's neck. Do not be afraid, and finally pull him out!" Zhang was surprised and said, "That County Magistrate is an official. Who am I, to dare to approach him?" The two officials said, "Although he is an official, he is greedy for wealth and fond of women, and he kills and tortures excessively. Now he is a criminal, what more is there to fear?" Zhang hesitated and finally dared not go forward. The two officials were in a hurry, again pushing and squeezing him hard. In a state of panic, he was already inside the room. Unwillingly, he tied the chain around the County Magistrate's neck and ran away. The two officials met him, and together they followed the old path. Zhang looked back at the County Magistrate, who was already tied around the neck and walking together, and he was greatly frightened. The County Magistrate remained silent and said nothing.

    86

    甫至宅后，见一男一女，作淫戏于墙阴，略不羞避。二役过之，张问曰：“此何人？奈何恣行淫事，腆不畏人也？”役指知县谓张曰：“彼女子即渠之爱姬翠华，彼男子即渠之娈童郑禄也。因渠病卧，故私约于此。彼方自谓隐密，岂暇见我辈，又岂意我辈见之明且晰哉！”张目知县而笑，知县亦俯首不语。至水窦前，复见二人，结束同二役，亦械一人，囚首面而立。二役问曰：“已拘得乎？”应曰：“拘得矣。”其人见知县欲哭，役急批其颊而止。张私诘此人为谁，役曰：“即渠之幕宾，主刑名者郭某也。与同案，故同拘耳。”话间，闻内宅哭声群起。役曰：“时至矣。”遂出至坊间，预有二人驻囚舆二辆相候于通衢。四役因纳知县与郭于舆中。嘱张曰：“汝自归，慎勿泄于人也。”言讫，超舆叱牛而去。

    Explanation

    86

    Upon arriving at the rear of the house, he saw a man and a woman engaged in a lewd act in the shadow of the wall, showing no shame or attempt to hide. Two servants passed by, and Zhang asked, "Who are they? How dare they engage in such lewd acts, shamelessly unconcerned with others?" The servant pointed to the magistrate and told Zhang, "That woman is his beloved concubine, Cui Hua, and that man is his male lover, Zheng Lu. Because he was ill and lying down, they privately agreed to meet here. They thought it was secret, how could they spare the time to see us, and how could they have expected us to see it clearly!" Zhang looked at the magistrate and laughed, and the magistrate also lowered his head and remained silent. When they reached the water drain, they saw two more people, dressed similarly to the two servants, and one person was bound, standing with a prisoner's head facing them. The two servants asked, "Have we captured him?" The bound person replied, "We have captured him." The person saw the magistrate and wanted to cry, but the servant quickly slapped him on the cheek and stopped him. Zhang privately asked the servant who this person was, and the servant said, "He is his counselor, Mr. Guo, who handles legal matters. He is in the same case, so he was detained together." As they spoke, they heard crying sounds rising from inside the house. The servant said, "The time has come." They then went out to the marketplace, where two people had already waited with two prisoner carts at the main road. The four servants therefore put the magistrate and Mr. Guo into the carts. They instructed Zhang, "Return on your own, be careful not to reveal this to others." After finishing speaking, they jumped onto the carts, whipped the oxen, and left.

    87

    张至家，鸡已鸣矣。见妻背灯而泣，邻妇三五人，从旁劝慰之曰：“死者不可复生矣，天数夙定也。况气未绝，俟天明延医治之，料无妨也。”张闻之大惊，失声一呼，豁然如梦寤，则身卧炕头，妻坐守于侧，邻妇抢攘满室。张咨嗟不已。妻见其复苏也，惊定而喜。张问胡为哭乎？妻曰：“汝解手良久不回，我出视，汝僵卧檐下。浼邻人扛入室。手足虽温，而呼之不醒，自四更至此时，已半夜矣。何幸得复生耶！”张悟前此之事，皆魂魄所为也。起身揖邻妇而谢之。各欣然辞去。张乃备以其故告妻，妻亦骇叹。比晓，举城军民挠乱，佥知县官于五更时死矣。密访郭幕，亦同时暴亡。

    Explanation

    87

    Zhang arrived home, and the rooster had already crowed. He saw his wife crying with her back to the lamp, and three or five of the neighbors' wives were there, consoling her and saying, "The dead cannot be brought back to life; it is fate that has long been determined. Besides, his breath has not yet left him. Wait until dawn to invite a doctor to treat him; I doubt it will do any harm." Upon hearing this, Zhang was greatly startled, let out a cry, and suddenly as if waking from a dream, he found himself lying on the kang with his wife sitting beside him, and the neighbors' wives bustling about the room. Zhang sighed repeatedly. His wife, seeing that he had come back to life, was shocked but then overjoyed. Zhang asked why she was crying. She replied, "You were gone for a long time and did not return. I went out to look for you and found you lying stiff on the eaves. I had the neighbors carry you into the house. Your hands and feet were still warm, but you did not respond when we called you. From the fourth watch until now, it has been half the night. How fortunate that you have come back to life!" Zhang realized that all that had happened earlier was due to his soul. He rose, bowed to the neighbors' wives, and thanked them. They all left happily. Zhang then told his wife the whole story, and she was also amazed and sighed. By dawn, the entire city's military and civilians were in turmoil, and it was known that the magistrate had died at the fifth watch. A secret investigation into the Dehao revealed that he too had died suddenly at the same time.

    88

    张不谨，渐泄于人，某之子闻之大恚，械送县，笞三十。鞫郑禄与翠华私通事，果不诬，杖郑禄于县，瘐死囹圄。缢翠华于园，以殉。事出雍凉间，秦人至今述之。恩茂先曰：“诚然，先大父亦尝言之也。”

    　　兰岩曰：罪恶贯盈，天夺其禄。鬼得而辱之，民得而欺之。回首皋比临民，其威权安在哉！鬼卒不能系其颈，而假手于张；非鬼卒不能也，张目击之，以暴其恶耳。

    Explanation

    88

    Zhang was not careful, and gradually revealed it to others. Some son heard it and was greatly angry. He was chained and sent to the county, where he was beaten thirty times. The affair of Zheng Lu and Cui Hua's adultery was investigated, and indeed, it was not false. Zheng Lu was beaten in the county and died in prison. Cui Hua was hanged in the garden to die as a sacrifice. The matter occurred in Yong and Liang, and the people of Qin still talk about it today. En Maoxian said, "Indeed, my great-grandfather also mentioned it." Lan Yan said, "With sins full, heaven has taken away his fortune. The ghost can humiliate him, and the people can deceive him. Looking back at the tiger skin he used to command the people, where is his authority now? The ghost soldier could not strangle his neck, but used Zhang as a proxy. It was not the ghost soldier who could not do it, but Zhang hit him with his eyes, exposing his evil."

    89

    阿襮某宗伯致仕家居，以数千金买巨宅一区，宅后楼九楹，空无人居，但贮什物，恒扃锁，往往见异物。宗伯四子三女，女皆嫁巨室，三子亦婚名门。唯第四子，甫十六，未娶。房中侍女海棠者，年及笄，颇慧丽。适宗伯偶山游未归，海棠寝至夜半，忽为人舁至楼上，见锦屏绣幕，画烛华筵，坐客十余辈，男女相半，履舄交错，酒炙并行。

    　　命海棠起，着衣侑觞。棠面□，以不习对。坐中稚齿女子，丰姿妖冶，鬓发如云，衣广袖之襦，把文犀之盏，含笑谓棠曰：“尔非尔家四郎房中婢耶？我与尔家四郎有夙缘，鱼轩不久入门，自是一家人，无事腼腆也。”棠倚柱垂头，不作一语酬答。一靓妆女子，齿尤稚，骂曰：“奴种不堪作养！噤口愠色，欲谁仰妆之眉睫耶？此等人只可侍盥栉，提箕帚，哪晓歌舞中事！纵使能歌舞，亦不过哞哞作牛鸣，得得效驴跳。

    Explanation

    89

    A certain scholar-official named Aobo, having retired and returned home, purchased a grand mansion with thousands of gold pieces. Behind the house stood a nine-bay upper floor, empty and unoccupied, but used to store household items, always locked away. Strange phenomena often occurred there. The official had four sons and three daughters; all the daughters were married into prominent families, and the three eldest sons were also wed to noble households. Only the fourth son, just sixteen years old, had not yet taken a wife. In the house, there was a servant named Taohuang, who, at the age of笄 (coming of age for girls), was quite intelligent and beautiful. One night, while the official was away on a mountain excursion, Taohuang fell asleep until midnight when she was suddenly carried upstairs by someone. There she saw embroidered screens and brocade curtains, painted candles, and a splendid banquet, with over a dozen guests, half male and half female, their shoes and sandals mixed together, and wine and roasted meat served side by side.

    She was ordered to rise, dress herself, and serve wine. Taohuang, with a blank expression, replied that she was not familiar with such tasks. A young woman with a charming and alluring appearance, her hair like clouds, wearing a long-sleeved robe and holding a cup made of carved ivory, smiled and said to Taohuang, "Are you not the maid in the room of your fourth master? I have a predestined connection with your fourth master, and soon his carriage will arrive, and we will be part of the same family. There is no need to be shy." Taohuang leaned against a pillar, head down, and did not respond at all. Another young woman, even more youthful and dressed up, scolded, "You're not worth raising! Keep silent and show no anger. Who are you trying to impress with your makeup? People like you can only serve in the kitchen and clean. How could you possibly understand the art of singing and dancing? Even if you could sing and dance, you would only make mooing sounds like a cow and hop like a donkey."

    90

    三姐耐烦与语！”又一少年男子曰：“我道莫教渠来，三妹执不听，今何如？转坏我一　新绫袜，污印十个腡文！”满座大笑，不觉哄堂。前女子有羞愧色，向少年曰：“四哥何太小家相，亦学九妹嘲笑于我耶！海棠虽贱，颜色姿态，且远胜四嫂。今当稠人广众，不肯作倡优伎俩，正见其尊重处，何必相强，且袜一　，值钱几文，亦流于齿颊乎？妹以其初睡，不便令作赤脚婢，故聊为假借，亟当奉偿耳，苟有污，妹当代偿八□。”少年语塞，避席以谢之曰：“三妹娇养惯，性情犹昔日耶。聊以相戏，何遽破颜。”使人送之下楼，置故处，棠汗下如雨，心大悸，捶同宿二婢醒，告以故，二婢亦惧。

    Explanation

    90

    "Patience to speak with her!" said another young man. "I thought you wouldn't let her come, but the third sister insisted, and now what? She ruined my new silk stockings, staining ten characters!" Everyone laughed heartily, and the room erupted in laughter. The woman had a look of shame and embarrassment, and she said to the young man, "Fourth brother, why do you act so petty? Are you learning from the ninth sister to mock me? Although I am humble, my appearance and demeanor far surpass the fourth sister-in-law. In a crowded and public setting, I refuse to perform the tricks of a performer or entertainer, which shows my respect. Why force me? Besides, my stockings are worth only a few coins, and they won't be remembered by anyone. I lent them to her because she was just waking up, and it was inconvenient to make her a barefoot maid. I will repay them soon, and if there is any stain, I will pay for eight of them." The young man was speechless and apologized by stepping aside, saying, "Third sister, you are used to being pampered, and your temperament is still as it was before. I was just playing a joke on you. Why did you lose your temper so quickly?" He had someone take her downstairs and place her back in her original spot. The woman was sweating profusely, her heart pounding, and she woke up the two maids who were sleeping with her, telling them the reason. The two maids were also afraid.

    91

    次日，白诸四郎。四郎白其母。母怖，曰：“此必狐鬼，戒勿至后院！”四郎私叩海棠，心艳女子之美，又闻与己有夙缘之说，频频窥伺后院。徘徊间，瞥然一物坠面前，拾视之，则镂金条脱一只也。怀之以归，出示海棠，棠曰：“此狐之物，不可取。”四郎不听，棠恐为己累，告夫人。夫人素严厉，怒曰：“不肖子！岂不闻不听老人言，凄惶在眼前耶？”呼四郎至，索条脱观之，柳枝一圈耳。痛诃之，且命行杖。兄嫂毕至，环跪求宽。正纷嚣间，闻有女子，厉声于北窗之下者，曰：“此汝家亢宗子弟，奈何挞辱至此！所谓慈母，固如是乎？”夫人知为狐，迁怒曰：“人家教诲儿子，何与尔狐狸事！”狐曰：“呸！果何与我事！特念四郎年少，故不忍其犯夏楚，不然即打死，又何妨耶？”大郎怒，欲出杀之，声言觅刀。二郎三郎阻之不令往。狐亦大至，众口沸腾，飞瓦入房，器物皆碎。夫人惧，不复敢出声。群狐逾时始寂。

    Explanation

    91

    The next day, the fourth son was informed. He told his mother. She was terrified and said, "This must be a fox spirit; do not go to the back courtyard!" The fourth son secretly went to see Haitang, admiring the beauty of the woman, and having heard that there was a predestined connection between them, he frequently peered into the back courtyard. While wandering, something suddenly fell in front of him. He picked it up and saw that it was a single gold-embossed bracelet. He brought it back and showed it to Haitang, who said, "This is a fox's item; you should not take it." The fourth son did not listen. Haitang, fearing that it would bring trouble, reported it to the lady. The lady, who was usually strict, became angry and said, "Disgraceful son! Haven't you heard that if you don't listen to the elders, you will face misfortune?" She called the fourth son to her, demanded to see the bracelet, and found it to be just a piece of willow branch. She scolded him severely and ordered him to be punished with a stick. All the brothers and sisters-in-law came, surrounded him, and pleaded for leniency. In the midst of the commotion, a woman's loud voice was heard from under the north window, saying, "This is your family's honorable descendant; how could you treat him so harshly? Is this what you call a loving mother?" The lady realized it was a fox and transferred her anger, saying, "What business do fox spirits have in teaching your son?" The fox retorted, "Pah! What business do you have with me? But since the fourth son is still young, I spared him from being beaten; otherwise, I would have killed him already, what's the difference?" The eldest son became furious and wanted to go out to kill the fox, declaring that he would find a knife. The second and third sons stopped him from going. The foxes also became very agitated, causing a great uproar, with tiles flying into the room and all objects breaking. The lady was afraid and dared not speak again. After some time, the group of foxes finally calmed down.

    92

    于是昼夜乖戾，妖异旋生。二郎乘马上衙，往往途中失去二镫。海棠如厕，猝遇紫衣少年，搂之接吻，力拒久之，旋失所在。他侍女所遭尤强暴。大郎新授中书舍人，同僚出资公贺。至日，门庭若市，庖　人喧。宾来，丝肉并陈，水陆咸备，乃举酒献酬，则酒皆马溺；下箸款友，则箸皆粪蛆。客大哗，以为秽弄。大郎悟为狐祟，力白其故。客甚无聊，踵接而散。大郎送客去，恨愤至楼下，跳骂逾时，二弟劝归。馁甚，妻曰：“厨下馐馔极多，盍取食乎？”乃命婢索点心，啖之颇美，及入喉，觉蠕蠕动，啯啅有声，即吐哺视之，则尽疥癞小蛙也。遂大呕，不敢复食。日暮，出饱于市，亲族相戒不饮食于其家。

    Explanation

    92

    Thus, day and night were out of harmony, and strange and ominous events began to occur. The eldest son, riding his horse to the government office, often lost two horse shoes on the road. While using the restroom, the maid named Hai Tang suddenly encountered a young man in purple clothes, who embraced and kissed her. She resisted fiercely for a long time, but he suddenly disappeared. Other maidservants also suffered similar severe treatment. The eldest son, newly appointed as a high-ranking official, had his colleagues contribute money to publicly congratulate him. On the day of the celebration, the courtyard was as busy as a market, and the cooks were noisy. When the guests arrived, fine silk and meat were served, with both land and sea delicacies. They raised their cups to toast each other, but the wine was all horse urine; they used chopsticks to eat with friends, but the chopsticks were all filled with fecal maggots. The guests were greatly upset, thinking it was a disgusting prank. The eldest son realized it was a fox spirit and tried to explain the reason. The guests were very bored and left one after another. The eldest son, seeing the guests leave, jumped and cursed for a long time, and the second brother advised him to return. He was very hungry, and his wife said, "There are many delicious dishes in the kitchen. Why not eat?" He then ordered a maid to bring some snacks. The snacks tasted quite good, but when he swallowed them, he felt something wriggling and heard a sound. He immediately spat out the food and looked at it, and it turned out to be small frogs covered in scabs. He vomited violently and dared not eat again. By evening, he went out to eat in the market, and relatives warned each other not to eat at his house.

    93

    大郎有内弟，为侍卫，少年好事者也。来省其姊，话及狐事，侍卫笑曰：“鸷鸟累百，不如一鹗。汝家无胆勇者，何以弹压妖魅，我今夜住此，必获宁贴。不然，亦当为彼勍敌。”大郎曰：“汝状如妇人女子，狐见之且恐有异图，夫何能镇靖之有？”侍卫忿然曰：“姑待之，今宵即见功效也！”会夫人归宁，大郎乃留之。及暮，欣然携襆被，独宿楼下。其姊及二郎、三郎谏止之，悉不听。入夜，初无声响，益坦率。久之体倦，即就枕。

    Explanation

    93

    The Elder Brother had a younger brother-in-law who was a bodyguard, a young man who liked to do things. He came to visit his sister, and they talked about foxes. The bodyguard laughed and said, "A hundred fierce birds are not as good as one eagle. Your family has no brave person, how can you suppress evil spirits? I will stay here tonight and will definitely get peace. Otherwise, I will also be a strong enemy to them." The Elder Brother said, "Your appearance is like that of a woman or a girl, the fox might see it and have strange intentions, how can you calm it down?" The bodyguard angrily said, "Just wait, tonight you will see the results!" Coincidentally, the lady returned to her parents' home, so the Elder Brother kept him. At dusk, he joyfully carried his bedding and slept alone below the house. His sister and the second and third brothers advised him to stop, but he did not listen to any of them. At night, initially there was no sound, and he became even more relaxed. After a long time, his body became tired, and he immediately went to bed.

    94

    至四更，大郎寤，拥衾起坐，敲火吸烟，闻床下似有鼾声，异之，撼醒其妻，共起烛之，见一人裸卧床下，身无寸缕，大惊叫有贼，婢媪毕集，禽而挞之，其人惊寤，则侍卫也。众大骇，侍卫惭愧无地。大郎以衣衣之，叩其故，不解何以于此。昧爽，驱马而归，衣服履袜，得诸圊中，污秽不可复着。三娘昼寝，为火烧其衣，扑之愈烈，仓皇脱去，衣固依然无恙也。怒骂不已。自此为患益盛。闺中秽物，悬诸大门，或下体亵衣抛之当路。衣未制而先毁，镜甫淬而旋昏。

    Explanation

    94

    At the fourth watch, the eldest son awoke, wrapped himself in a quilt and sat up, struck a fire to light his pipe, and heard what sounded like snoring coming from under the bed. Finding this strange, he shook awake his wife, and together they lit a candle to investigate. They saw a man nakedly lying under the bed, with not a stitch of clothing on. Greatly alarmed, they cried out that there was a thief. The maidservants and servants gathered, caught the man, and beat him. The man woke up in shock and was revealed to be a guard. Everyone was greatly startled, and the guard was filled with shame and had nowhere to hide. The eldest son clothed him, asked him why he was there, and could not understand why he had come. At dawn, the guard rode away on horseback, and his clothes and socks were found in the latrine, soiled and unusable. The third wife took a daytime nap, and her clothes were burned by fire. When she tried to extinguish the fire, it burned more fiercely. In a panic, she hurriedly removed her clothes, which remained unharmed. She continued to curse angrily. From this point on, the troubles grew worse. Dirty items from the bedroom were hung on the main gate, and sometimes underwear was thrown in the middle of the road. Clothes were destroyed before they were even made, and a mirror that had just been polished became dim again.

    95

    浃数旬，宗伯游山回，夫人备述家中事，议迁居以避之，宗伯曰：“妇人信邪，偏多疑惧，勿复扰攘，自获宁谧矣！”越半月，上下果相安，咸以为主人福估。宗伯亦颇自诩，曰：“何如？可见见怪不怪，其怪自败也。”又数日，忽阍人坌息入，曰：“方大人来拜矣。”益少宗伯方公，文名籍甚，且为宗伯乡试座师，一朝枉驾辱临，举家欣感，急索衣冠出迓。拱之升堂，再拜起居。云坐则坐。方公久坐不去，言语葛藤，又深怪宗伯疏慵，不常存问。宗伯汗流浃背，谢罪不遑。方公未刻入门，酉时进馔，自漏下以迄午夜，语犹刺刺不休，宗伯精耗神昏，百骸俱倦，支持鼓励，强作笑言。久之不复闻方公声息，若哑若聋，宗伯罔测其故。颐使大郎侍侧潜窥，但见面上茸茸，不辨何物。耳语宗伯，宗伯大疑，即前审谛，乌得为方公，但一刍灵踞右席耳。父子不胜骇异，既而知为狐所弄。乃大笑曰：“骗得好！骗得好！”当时上下无不捧。

    Explanation

    95

    After several weeks, the high-ranking official returned from a mountain trip, and his wife recounted strange occurrences at home. They discussed moving to a new place to avoid these disturbances. The official remarked, "Women are prone to superstition and excessive fear; let us not be further disturbed, and we will find peace on our own!" After half a month, everything settled down, and everyone believed that the master's good fortune had brought about this tranquility. The official boasted, "How about that? It shows that when you no longer find something strange, it loses its power."

    A few days later, the gatekeeper rushed in breathlessly and said, "Master Fang has come to pay respects!" Master Fang was a respected official, well-known for his literary talents, and had been the official's mentor during the provincial exam. His unexpected visit brought great joy and gratitude to the household, and they quickly dressed and went out to greet him. They bowed deeply as he ascended the hall, and when he sat, they did so as well. Master Fang sat for a long time without leaving, speaking incessantly and criticizing the official for being lazy and neglectful. The official was drenched in sweat, unable to apologize properly. Master Fang arrived at noon and stayed until the evening meal, and from then until midnight, he continued to talk nonstop. The official was mentally and physically exhausted, but he forced himself to maintain a cheerful demeanor. After a long time, the official no longer heard Master Fang's voice, as if he had become mute or deaf. Confused, the official had his eldest son secretly observe and saw something indistinct on Master Fang's face. He whispered to the official, who was greatly puzzled and went forward to examine closely. To his astonishment, it was not Master Fang at all, but a straw figure sitting in his place. The father and son were shocked and amazed. Later, they realized that this was a trick played by a fox spirit. They burst out laughing, saying, "Well played! Well played!" At that time, everyone in the household applauded in admiration.

    96

    迟明，宗伯扶筇至楼下，曰：“主人寄声阿紫：吾闻社鼠不灌，屋鼠不薰，以所凭者，得其地也。况狐之为物，岁久能仙，既能于兽有灵，何必与人为祟？如为炫其幻术，则幻术岂足服人；倘用以惊愚，则惊愚何堪利己？胥出下策，终非上乘。吾今与汝约请画粉墙为界，楼九楹任汝所为，墙以南主人居之，两不相侵，言归于好。如复相扰，则背城借一矣！”楼上无有应答者。遂鸠工垩粉墙，横亘东西，长逾十丈。一夕，深宵独坐，见一翁一妪，貌殊奇古，率男女五七辈同拜于地，谢曰：“公真豁达大度人也！昔者之言，敢不受命。特四公子，将有大厄，愿以三女阿　□者充公子妾媵，至旦夕呵护，聊以报德，幸公勿弃也。”宗伯问阿□安在，翁指示之。宗伯谛视，秾不短，纤不长，国色无双，平生所未睹，喜而诺之。问何日亲迎，翁媪曰：“旗俗不亲迎，且既承慨许，当即令其趋事舅姑，敢议礼乎？”寻辞去，不复为祟。

    Explanation

    96

    At dawn, the Zongbo, supporting himself with a cane, came downstairs and said, "The master sends his greetings to Ah Zi: I have heard that rats in the community do not get flooded, and house rats do not get fumigated, because they rely on their environment. Even more so, foxes, after many years, can become spirits. If they have a spirit among beasts, why must they cause trouble for humans? If they want to show off their magical tricks, then magic is not enough to convince people. If they use it to frighten the ignorant, then frightening the ignorant is not worth benefiting oneself. All these are low strategies and not high tactics. I now make a pact with you: we will draw a chalk line on the wall as a boundary. The nine rooms of the building are yours to do with as you please. The area south of the wall will be the master's domain, and neither side will encroach on the other. We will reconcile and return to harmony. If you continue to cause trouble, then we will fight a final battle!" There was no response from upstairs. They then hired workers to draw a chalk line on the wall, which stretched across the east and west, over ten zhang long. One night, while sitting alone in the deep night, the Zongbo saw an old man and an old woman, both with extraordinary ancient appearances, leading five or six men and women who knelt on the ground together and thanked him, saying, "You are truly a broad-minded and generous person! We dare not refuse your previous words. However, the four young masters are about to face a great calamity. We wish to offer our three daughters, Ah □, as concubines to the young masters, to take care of them day and night, as a way to repay your kindness. Please do not reject us." The Zongbo asked where Ah □ was, and the old man pointed her out. The Zongbo carefully observed her: she was neither too short nor too tall, with a beauty unparalleled in the country, a sight he had never seen before. He was delighted and agreed. He asked when the wedding would take place, and the old man and woman replied, "According to our customs, there is no need for the groom to come to fetch the bride. Since we have already gratefully accepted your offer, we will immediately have her serve her in-laws. How dare we discuss etiquette?" Soon after, they left, and the foxes no longer caused trouble.

    97

    越三日，宗伯与夫人方坐谈，蓦见一女子褰帘入，画衣素面而拜，自称阿□，奉父母命，来侍四郎。夫人见其慧丽，亦喜而安焉。女事舅姑极婉顺，妯娌之间亦甚和好，夫妇异常缱绻，操作甚勤，女红精妙无匹，与海棠尤相得。会夏日，大雨大雷，女惊惶失措，抱四郎卧帐中，现形为一黑牝狐。四郎无计摆脱，不胜忐忑，霹雳绕屋，奔腾逾时。始定，狐复化为女，跪谢四郎，欣喜之色可掬。夜半遂失所在，后不复来。四郎思之不置。后四郎早贵，官至阁学。是盖狐欲避劫，故托庇于四郎前。老狐言公子有厄者，妄也。观其逃劫而喜，去不复来，始有意，终无情，概可知矣。

    　　兰岩曰：为避劫而自来，甫逃劫而竟去，窃为狐所不取。

    Explanation

    97

    Three days later, the High Official was sitting in conversation with his wife when suddenly a woman entered by lifting the curtain. She wore painted clothes and had a plain face, and she bowed, introducing herself as Ah □, having come by the command of her parents to serve Master Si. The lady found her intelligent and beautiful, and she too was pleased and accepted her. The woman served her in-laws with utmost gentleness and compliance, and she got along very well with her sisters-in-law. The couple was unusually affectionate, and she worked very diligently, her needlework being exquisite and unmatched, especially compatible with Taihong. On a summer day, there was a heavy rainstorm accompanied by thunder. The woman panicked and at a loss, embracing Master Si in the bed, and transformed into a black female fox. Master Si was unable to find a way to escape, extremely anxious, as lightning struck around the house, roaring for over an hour. When it finally calmed, the fox returned to its human form, kneeling to thank Master Si, her joyful expression evident. She disappeared at midnight and did not return. Master Si could not stop thinking about her. Later, Master Si achieved early prominence and reached the rank of a scholar-official in the government. This was because the fox wanted to avoid disaster and sought protection under Master Si. The old fox had said that Master Si would encounter misfortune, but that was false. Observing that the fox escaped disaster with joy and then left and never returned, initially showing affection but ultimately being indifferent, one can generally understand her nature.

    Lan Yan commented: To come to avoid disaster and then, having escaped disaster, to depart—this I find not to be the way of the fox.

    98

    娄　芳　华娄芳华籍辅氏，二十未婚。从其舅氏杨尉于蓝田。邑有董孝廉者，辋川人，富于学，杨使娄从之游。往返颇遐，中途有古刹，至则信宿焉。率一月一归省舅。居无何，缁衣寥落，一老僧仅存，目且双瞽。娄至，惟独宿西院，无可与谈。

    　　值仲夏，复经其处，日将暮，枯坐无聊赖，散步寺门前，觉有异香。有顷香渐浓，倏见一女郎，从一婢，遵山径自东而西。年十六七，姿容美丽，目所未睹，掩袂而过，数回首盼娄，若甚注意者。婢年亦相等，明眸皓齿，颇妩媚。娄心荡，绕出捷径，要遮而揖之曰：“山深日暮，小娘欲安之乎？”女却步羞涩，仓皇裣衽。婢极坦率，直前以身蔽女，而应答曰：“何处小郎，强来与人家闺秀语！我家小娘子出身矜贵，门楣王榭，甲第金张，虽至亲如弟兄，稍涉疏远者，未尝轻交一言，况于葭莩，更何论行路！郎君冒昧乃尔，其欺我双鬟雏稚，不能握拳透爪，徒为嚼齿穿龈耶？”言讫掩口，视女而笑。女亦粲然。

    Explanation

    98

    Liu Fanghua, a maiden from the Fu family, was twenty years old and unmarried. She followed her uncle, Yang Wei, to Lan Tian. In the county, there was a man named Dong Xiaolian, a native of Wangchuan, who was well-versed in learning. Yang sent Liu to study with him. Their travels were quite distant, and along the way, there was an ancient temple where they would stay for two nights whenever they arrived. Liu would return home to visit her uncle approximately once a month. Not long after, the number of monks had dwindled, leaving only one elderly monk who was blind in both eyes. When Liu arrived, she stayed alone in the western courtyard, with no one to converse with.

    One midsummer, she passed by the temple again. As the day was drawing to a close, she sat idly, feeling bored. She went for a walk in front of the temple and noticed a strange fragrance. Soon, the scent grew stronger, and suddenly, she saw a young lady accompanied by a maid, walking along a mountain path from east to west. The lady was around sixteen or seventeen years old, with a beauty that had never been seen before. She covered her sleeves and walked past, glancing back at Liu several times, as if paying special attention to her. The maid was of similar age, with bright eyes and white teeth, and was very charming. Liu's heart was stirred, and she took a shortcut to intercept them, bowing politely and saying, "It's getting dark in the deep mountains. Where are you headed, young lady?" The lady stepped back shyly and hurriedly curtsied. The maid was very straightforward, stepping forward to shield the lady and replied, "Where is this young man coming from? Why are you trying to talk to a lady like this? My young lady comes from a noble family, with a prestigious lineage. Even close relatives like brothers have never spoken to someone so distant, let alone distant relatives or strangers! Sir, you are being quite bold. Are you trying to take advantage of these young girls who can't defend themselves, just to show off your strength? After saying this, the maid covered her mouth, looked at the lady, and laughed. The lady also smiled brightly.

    99

    娄察其色不愠，乃伪为跼蹐，再拜而谢曰：“小子无状，见子从小娘，日暮偶行，未免有情虑及豺虎。意者蜗居伊迩，草榻空悬，私愿孔奢，欲留一宿。小子万幸，得滥厕居停。小娘或不肯，犹望子善为缓颊，胡为翻来诮让？所谓可儿者，固若是耶？”婢格格笑曰：“书痴愚而诈，几令儿无以应，当怂恿小娘子，与汝角口矣！”于是耳语女子者久之。女掩口笑曰：“男女不亲受授，可同宿乎？”娄闻之喜，鞠躬而前曰：“兰若虽隘，足以偃息，否则同榻亦权宜之道也。”女不言而笑。婢因一手把娄袖，一手揽女腕，搴之使相就，曰：“好，好！千里姻缘似线牵也。今日郎有言，操蛇之神，无不闻之；泉水松风，悉为羔雁。行矣！无辜负普救佳会也！”乃与娄同掖女子入寺。

    Explanation

    99

    Liu observed that the woman's expression was not angry, so he pretended to be fearful and bowed twice to apologize, saying, "I am unworthy, I saw you with the young lady, and at dusk we happened to be walking together, and I could not help but be concerned about the wolves and tigers. Perhaps my humble dwelling is nearby, with an empty and hanging simple bed, my overly ambitious wish is to stay for one night. I am extremely fortunate to be able to intrude and stay. The young lady may not agree, but I still hope you will skillfully plead on my behalf. Why did you come back to scold me? The so-called adorable one, is it really like this?" The maid laughed in a high-pitched, girlish way, saying, "The book痴 is foolish and deceitful, almost making it impossible for me to respond. I will urge the young lady to argue with you!" She then whispered to the woman for a long time. The woman covered her mouth and laughed, saying, "Men and women should not be taught by each other, can they stay together overnight?" Liu heard this and was pleased, bowing and saying, "Although the temple is small, it is enough to rest, otherwise sharing a bed is also a temporary solution." The woman laughed without speaking. The maid then held Liu's sleeve with one hand and the woman's wrist with the other, pulling them together, saying, "Good, good! A thousand-mile marriage is like thread-pulling. Today the young man has spoken, all gods have heard it; the spring water and pine breeze are all for the young goats and geese. Go! Do not miss this good opportunity!" She then supported the woman into the temple with Liu.

    100

    娄以寒俭，恐贻笑丽人，颇形惶遽。女子笑语婢，婢笑曰：“主人仓猝如此，何苦谆谆款客耶？”因命娄于佛殿前，设长梯，婢旖旎而升，巡檐探取雀騦数十枚。袖中出银铫一具，复出一漆盒子，取油少许，色如酥，炙騦盈铫，又出酒一樽，色碧而香烈，味极醇。娄与女对食，香美无区。是夕同寝，娄几死于温柔乡。诘朝握别，共订后期。女曰：“此间虽僻，犹结庐在人境也。儿家去值西仅十余里，有屋数椽，可以避嚣。白板扉外，有古杏五株，甘棠一树，可志。暮当遣侍儿来导，郎君一见过。”娄诺之，女与婢出门而去。娄怅望良久，遂不复之辋川。出入寺门，引领以待。

    Explanation

    100

    Liu, being poor and frugal, feared that he would be laughed at by a beautiful woman, and thus appeared very anxious. The woman smiled and said to her maid, "The master is so hurried; why go through the trouble of treating guests so earnestly?" She then ordered Liu to set up a long ladder, and the maid climbed it gracefully. She followed the eaves and caught several dozen sparrows. She took out a silver pot from her sleeve, and then a lacquer box, and took a little oil that was yellowish-white, and roasted the sparrows in the pot. She also took out a jar of wine that was green and fragrant, with a very rich taste. Liu and the woman ate together, and the food was fragrant and delicious beyond compare. That night, they slept together, and Liu almost died of pleasure in the luxurious setting. The next morning, they shook hands and parted, agreeing to meet again. The woman said, "This place is somewhat remote, but it is still a home in the world. My house is only about ten miles west of here, with several small rooms where we can avoid the noise. Outside the white wooden door, there are five ancient apricot trees and one loquat tree, which can serve as landmarks. In the evening, I will send a maid to guide you, so please come by." Liu agreed, and the woman and her maid left. Liu stood there looking wistfully for a long time, and then he no longer went to Wangchuan. He often went in and out of the temple gate, waiting eagerly.

    101

    抵暮，婢始至，见娄辄笑曰：“郎君玉立林下，缥若神仙，无怪娘子殷殷注念半日，数十次促奴来也。”娄见之，惊喜欲狂，问小娘所在。婢曰：“但踵奴行，无多问。”乃相与越涧循壑，迍踬于峻赠荦确间。历数嵚崎，娄履袜尽穿，不堪其惫，而婢子践流蹑石，其行如飞。约十余里，于山谷中入一橡林。时日已西没，风声如吼，但觉浓荫染袂，空翠爽肌，渐觉异香扑鼻。

    Explanation

    101

    As evening approached, the servant finally arrived and saw娄, immediately smiling and saying, "The young master stands tall and elegant in the woods, as ethereal as a fairy. No wonder the young lady was deeply focused and concerned for half a day, calling for me dozens of times."娄, upon seeing her, was overjoyed and overwhelmed with emotion, asking where the young lady was. The servant replied, "Just follow me, don't ask too many questions." They then crossed a stream and followed a ravine, stumbling through steep and rugged terrain. After traversing several rugged paths,娄's shoes and socks were completely worn out, and he could no longer bear the fatigue, while the servant treading water and stepping on stones moved like the wind. After about ten miles, they entered an oak forest in a valley. By then, the sun had set in the west, and the wind roared, but all they felt was the thick shade staining their sleeves, the fresh greenness refreshing their skin, and gradually, a strange fragrance filled their noses.

    102

    宛转间，抵一精舍，花木繁盛，泉石清幽。婢曰：“至矣。郎君非仓猝客，可即入也。”娄入，见女倚栏待，相见欢然。婢治具作供，罗列山珍，而以雀騦为上品，意女羊枣之嗜也。房中位置，悉与世异。女喜作古妆。小婢外，更有垂髫女六七人，尽苗条婉媚。女驭下极严，诸婢无不仰其眉睫。而侍前婢独宽，常呼其名曰：“收香。”八人中，收香慧黠尤甚。又有老婢，年约七旬，独司庖□，亦来窥娄，转身即笑谓诸婢曰：“阿堵贫儿，乃老身百十年前药宠中物耳。娘子少见多怪，辄一交若醴，窃恐非耐久交也！”娄闻之，颇恚赧。收香为之御侮曰：“彼自惠好，无尤于汝，可干涉百十年前事！污人耳久矣！奈何复举以告人，肴善之外，缝纫补缀为汝事，其他不必干预！且郎君处此，于汝亦大有波润，独不念碗中余沈，柈中剩胾，又谁曾与汝争一匕一箸耶！”七婢笑以和之。娄与女亦各拊掌。老婢惭而去。

    Explanation

    102

    In a winding path, they arrived at a refined hermitage, where the flowers and trees were lush and the water and stones were serene. A maid said, "We have arrived. Sir, you are not a hurried guest, so you may enter immediately." Lou entered and saw a woman leaning on the railing, waiting for him. They greeted each other joyfully. The maid prepared the meal and set out a lavish spread of delicacies, with the pheasant being the most prized dish, indicating the woman's fondness for a particular taste. The arrangement of the rooms was entirely different from the world outside. The woman enjoyed ancient makeup. Outside the small maids, there were six or seven young girls with braided hair, all slender and charming. The woman managed her subordinates very strictly, and all the maids revered her. However, the maid who served her was lenient, and she often called her by name, "Shouxiang." Among the eight maids, Shouxiang was particularly intelligent and clever. There was also an elderly maid, about seventy years old, who alone managed the kitchen and came to peek at Lou. She turned and laughed, saying to the other maids, "This poor fellow is just an old favorite from a hundred years ago. Lady, you are too surprised by what you see. Just one encounter like wine, I fear it might not be a lasting relationship!" Lou heard this and felt quite embarrassed. Shouxiang defended him, saying, "He is kind and friendly, and you have no reason to blame him. Why bring up matters from a hundred years ago? This has been annoying for a long time! Why repeat it? Besides cooking well, sewing and mending are your duties. Do not interfere with other matters! And Sir is here, and it greatly benefits you. Do you not remember the leftovers in the bowl and plate? Who would argue with you over a spoon or chopstick?" The seven maids laughed to calm the situation. Lou and the woman also clapped their hands. The elderly maid felt ashamed and left.

    103

    居月余，娄欲归省其舅，女难之，收香悻悻，以两手抚娄背，推之出门，曰：“郎君心坚确，即强留在此，亦寡欢情，请速去，勿稍淹也！”娄未及应，而双扉已合，乃悒悒觅路而返。甫至僧舍，已遇其舅，率数仆抢攘而至。见娄大哗曰：“汝一人何之耶？”娄不得已，以实告，冀舅唤冰人为娶女也。而舅大惊曰：“深山之中，何所不有，据所遭必妖魅也。”亟纠合乡勇数十人，促娄导入谷中，至橡林，娄颇俄延。

    Explanation

    103

    Lou stayed for more than a month, and he wanted to return home to visit his uncle. The woman found this difficult and, with a resentful expression, placed both hands on Lou's back and pushed him out the door. She said, "Young master, your heart is firm and resolute. Even if I force you to stay here, there will be little joy. Please leave quickly, do not linger!" Before Lou could respond, the double doors had already closed, and he sullenly searched for the way back. Just as he arrived at the temple, he met his uncle, who had rushed to arrive with several servants. Seeing Lou, his uncle shouted loudly, "Why are you alone?" Lou, having no choice, told the truth, hoping his uncle would call a matchmaker to marry the girl. His uncle was greatly shocked and said, "In the deep mountains, what doesn't exist? According to what happened, it must be a demon or ghost." He quickly gathered dozens of local militia and urged Lou to lead them into the valley. They reached the oak forest, and Lou hesitated for a bit.

    104

    舅怒，以马箠挝之，娄终托言迷失，不复能记忆。舅束手曰：“然则奈何？”方欲谋归去，忽林间有异香袭人，众异之，复返入林，循香气至一山洞，藤萝附石，乔木千章。洞口香气倍浓。舅曰：“此必妖物窟宅。未可擅入，以火薰之可也。”于是代枯积朽，爝火烧之。烟入洞中，为风所吸，声艻艻然，俄有兽突出，乡勇以锄奋击，尽毙岩下。一食顷，得香獐二头，狐七头，苍狼一头，以驴载归县中，食肉寝皮。娄由是痛恨，眠食俱废。一月后，病遂不起。

    Explanation

    104

    The uncle was angry and beat him with a horsewhip. Liu eventually claimed to have gotten lost and could no longer remember. The uncle, at a loss, said, "What then shall we do?" Just as they were planning to return home, there was a strange fragrance in the forest that overwhelmed the senses. The group found it strange and returned to the forest, following the scent to a mountain cave. Vines and creepers clung to the rocks, and towering trees stretched for miles. The fragrance at the cave's entrance was even stronger. The uncle said, "This must be the lair of a monster. It is not advisable to enter擅自 (enter without permission), but burning it with fire is acceptable." They gathered dry and decaying wood and burned it with a torch. The smoke entered the cave and was sucked in by the wind, making a roaring sound. Suddenly, a beast leaped out. The local brave men attacked with their shovels and killed all of them at the base of the rock. After a meal's time, they caught two musk deer, seven foxes, and one gray wolf. They loaded them onto a donkey and brought them back to the county, eating the meat and sleeping on the skins. Liu became extremely angry and stopped eating and sleeping. After a month, he fell ill and never recovered.

    105

    闲斋曰：“麝之见猎，以脐之有香也。象有齿，犀有角，鹖有尾，雕有翎，鲏有皮，　□有膏，　□有甲，螰有珠，貂有毛，蚺有胆，皆麝类也。彼方自以为天之笃之，而不知天之毒之也。惟人亦然，女有色，士有才。

    　　兰岩曰：二獐以情死，以香败。倘能自守一时之欲，则古洞幽深，谁复得而扰之哉？甚矣！情欲一动即死机也，香气所闻即败兆也。惜哉！

    　　噶雄噶，少小也。雄，俊美也。抱罕人称“噶雄，”狱中土人之称“少俊”也。噶雄者何？人名也。人而名噶雄，以其人少且俊也。雄，杨姓，本粤东人，其祖为河州副将，卒于官，路远，柩不能归，葬河州。遂家焉。父锟为守备，四十而死。雄幼孤，长养叔婶。叔　为千总。是时大同周公文锦，为河州副将，怜其宦裔，落拓，乃以雄为余丁，令掌书记。

    Explanation

    105

    Idle Studio said: "The musk deer reacts to hunting because of the fragrance of the musk gland. Elephants have tusks, rhinoceroses have horns, pheasants have tails, eagles have feathers, pike have skin, [animal] has fat, [animal] has scales, abalone has pearls, sables have fur, pythons have bile—these are all like the musk deer. They believe they are favored by heaven, but they do not realize that this favor is actually a form of poison or curse. Humans are also like the animals, with women having beauty and men having talent.

    The Blue Rock said: Two deer die because of their emotions and are defeated by their fragrance. If they could control their desires for a moment, the ancient cave would remain undisturbed. How great! Emotional desire is a death trap, and the fragrance is a sign of defeat. What a pity!

    Gaxiong, a young boy. Xiong, handsome and beautiful. The people of Baohan call him 'Gaxiong,' and the locals in prison call him 'Young Handsome.' What is Gaxiong? It is a person's name. The person is named Gaxiong because he is young and handsome. Xiong, surnamed Yang, originally from Guangdong, his grandfather was a deputy general of Hezhou, died in office, the journey was too far, the coffin could not be returned, so he was buried in Hezhou. And settled there. His father, Kun, was a guard commander, died at the age of forty. Xiong was orphaned at a young age and raised by his uncle and aunt. His uncle was a hundred-man commander. At that time, Zhou Gong Wenjin of Datong was the deputy general of Hezhou, pitied his official lineage, and was destitute, so he appointed Xiong as a surplus soldier and ordered him to manage the records."

    106

    雄年甫十七，慧黠得人心。周有少女，尤眷爱雄，时与饮食什物，虽无他事，而两心相慕悦，非一朝一夕之故矣。有务子者，年与雄埒，为人亦狡狯颖秀。日与雄同供书房役使，夜则值宿斋中。际夏月，务子宿廊下，雄宿轩内，因苦热，户牖不闭，一梦初觉，映着月光，见一女人立榻前，大惊，蓄缩不敢动。女以手抚之，小语曰：“莫怕，我来矣。”声似周女，审谛不讹，化惊为喜，急起问曰：“深夜间何事到此？”女笑曰：“怜子鳏寂，来相伴耳。”言讫，急解衣升榻，启衾而入。肌理腻洁，拊不留手，香气馥馥，夺魄消魂，欲为柳下惠，不能黾勉矣。是夜绸缪，至五更始去。雄冥思其乐，如醉如梦，恍惚之况，犹云雨之锁阳台也。

    Explanation

    106

    Xiong was just seventeen years old, clever and charming, winning the hearts of others. Zhou had a young girl who cherished Xiong deeply, often sharing food and daily necessities. Though there were no other matters, their hearts were mutually admiring and delighted with each other, not due to a single day or evening. There was a person named Wuzi, who was the same age as Xiong and was also cunning and clever, with a sharp and refined mind. They served together in the study during the day and took turns staying in the斋 at night. During the summer month, Wuzi stayed in the corridor, while Xiong stayed in the轩. Due to the intense heat, the doors and windows were not closed. Just waking from a dream, lit by the moonlight, Xiong saw a woman standing at the foot of the bed, greatly startled, shrinking back and unable to move. The woman gently touched him and whispered, "Don't be afraid, I am here." Her voice sounded like that of Zhou's daughter, and upon careful examination, there was no mistake. Xiong transformed from fear to joy and quickly got up, asking, "Why did you come here so late at night?" The woman smiled and said, "I pitied your loneliness, to keep you company." After finishing speaking, she quickly undressed and climbed onto the bed, uncovering the quilt and entering. Her skin texture was smooth and clean, touching left no trace, and her fragrant aroma stole the soul and dissipated the spirit. Xiong wanted to be like Liu Xiaohui, but unable to muster the effort. That night, they were intimate until the fifth watch of the night when she left. Xiong deeply thought about the pleasure, as if drunk or in a dream, in a state of dizziness, like the clouds and rain locking the terrace.

    107

    次日入内，周女方晓妆，雄目之微笑，女亦笑迎之。雄终虑泄于务子，假周命，令务子宿于箭亭。务子谓箭亭自有老军值宿，何事需我？雄曰：“主人命，谁敢致诰？”务子唯唯。虽移襆被去，而心疑之，夜半逾垣，观其动静。甫至阶下，即闻房中笑语。由暗处窃窥窗隙，月射四壁，纤毫毕照，见雄主与女狎，辨为周女。心大动，精泄而返。老军方反侧于床，问焉往。务子以登溷对。

    Explanation

    107

    The next day, entering the inner chambers, Lady Zhou was just finishing her makeup. The hero smiled at her, and she also smiled in return. The hero ultimately worried about revealing himself to Wu Zi, so he pretended to have Lady Zhou's command and ordered Wu Zi to stay at the Arrow Pavilion. Wu Zi said that the Arrow Pavilion already had old soldiers on duty, and questioned the need for his presence. The hero replied, "The master's command, who dares to question it?" Wu Zi nodded in agreement. Although he moved his bedding away, he was suspicious. At midnight, he climbed over the wall to observe his movements. As soon as he reached the steps, he immediately heard laughter and conversation inside the room. From a dark place, he secretly peered through the window crack. The moonlight illuminated the four walls, and every detail was clearly visible. He saw the hero and the woman in intimate conversation and recognized her as Lady Zhou. His heart was greatly stirred, and he ejaculated and returned. The old soldier was tossing and turning in bed, and asked where he was going. Wu Zi replied that he was going to the toilet.

    108

    老军怒曰：“吾通宵常不寐，何事不能觉察！汝二更去，四更始回，必有非为。不吐实，亟当扭禀辕门官矣！”务子惧，因以实告。老军本冬烘，闻之骇曰：“以下蒸上，丧无日矣。汝知而不举，罪亦同坐。听我教，首之可也。”务子因嫉雄之宠，承老军教，密白于周。周大怒，入宅让其夫人。夫人曰：“女日夜在我侧，不离跬步，何所见闻，辄来唧聒，其为选事乎？即好选事，亦不应自衅乃尔。正所谓自将马桶向头上戴者！尚堪作朝廷堂堂二品官耶？”周忿极愧极，反目大哄，女涕泣不食，周杖雄二十，逐之出境。

    Explanation

    108

    The old soldier angrily said, "I have been awake all night, how could you not notice anything! You left at the second watch and returned at the fourth watch; there must be something suspicious. If you don't tell the truth, I'll immediately report you to the gate officer!" The young man was frightened and confessed the truth. The old soldier, who was rather dull-witted, heard this and said in horror, "If subordinates rise against superiors, the downfall will come soon. If you know but do not report, you will also be held responsible. Listen to my advice, and you should confess." The young man, jealous of the favor given to Xiong, followed the old soldier's advice and secretly reported to Zhou. Zhou became very angry and went into the house to scold his wife. The wife said, "The woman has been by my side day and night, never leaving my side. What have you seen or heard, yet you come to nag? Is this about selecting someone? Even if you like to select people, you shouldn't cause trouble like this. This is exactly like putting a chamber pot on one's head! How can you still be a respectable second-rank official in the court?" Zhou, extremely angry and ashamed, turned against her and had a big quarrel. The woman wept and refused to eat. Zhou beat Xiong twenty times and expelled him from the territory.

    109

    雄无依，栖身洮州一古庙中。一日乞食已，方清夜自伤，忽见女致前谓曰：“子勿忧，以天地之大，何处不可托足。请与子偕隐，何如？”雄见女，悲喜交至，泣且拜曰：“一身之外，别无长物。子虽钟情之笃，我宁忍见子为乞人妇乎？”女曰：“何至于是。子姑携我向湟中，有我在，保子一生吃著不尽也。”乃相与之西宁。女出资置房产、器用、仆婢，俨然富室。

    Explanation

    109

    Xiong, without any support, took shelter in an ancient temple in Taozhou. One day, after finishing his begging for food, he was in the quiet night, lamenting his situation, when suddenly a woman appeared before him and said, "Do not worry, for with the vastness of heaven and earth, there are many places where you can find support. How about we live in seclusion together?" Upon seeing the woman, Xiong's sorrow and joy came together, and he wept and bowed, saying, "Besides myself, I have nothing of value. Although your affection for me is deep, how could I bear to see you become a beggar's wife?" The woman replied, "Why go to such lengths. Take me to Xining, and with me, I will ensure you have more than enough to eat and wear for the rest of your life." So they traveled together to Xining. The woman used her resources to purchase property, household items, and hired servants and maids, making their household appear as a wealthy and prosperous one.

    110

    而雄窃察之，初不见女有一囊一箧，良不解取给何所，殊为怀惑，居无何，会其叔　因公至湟中，遭雄于阛阓间，乘肥衣轻，不敢遽认。询诸市人，佥曰：“河州杨公孙也，新寓于此，才半年耳。”　怏怏归逆旅，使老仆密侦之，果雄也。仆私指其家，传语曰：“郎君何以发迹？老奴从二爷来此数日矣，郎君独不一念其鞠育情，一往起居耶？”雄入白于女，女曰：“大恩不可忘于路人，况从父耶”且子为富家翁，而使叔寄身传舍，可乎？“雄乃往谒　，再拜敦请。　许之，甫登堂，侄妇出拜。视之，周女也。大惊。密询其故，雄俱言之。　叹异，默思于来时，不闻署中有失女事，岂其本官讳此，恐招物议耶？

    Explanation

    110

    While Xiong secretly observed, he did not initially see the girl with a bag or a chest, and he truly did not understand where she got her provisions, which greatly puzzled him. After some time, his uncle happened to come to Huangzhong on official business and met Xiong in the market, dressed in fine clothes and riding a fat horse. Xiong dared not recognize him immediately. He asked the people in the market, and all said, "He is Yang Gong's grandson from Hexi Prefecture, who has recently moved here, only for half a year." Displeased, he returned to the inn and sent an old servant to secretly investigate. The servant confirmed that it was indeed Xiong. The servant privately pointed out his home and conveyed a message saying, "How did you rise to prominence? The old servant has been here with your father for several days. Do you not even think of the kindness of your upbringing, and do you not visit him once in a while?" Xiong went inside and reported to the girl, who said, "Great kindness should not be forgotten even by a stranger, how much more so by a paternal uncle? Moreover, you are a wealthy man, yet you make your uncle stay in an inn, is that right?" Xiong then went to pay respects, bowed twice, and earnestly invited him. The uncle agreed, and as soon as he entered the hall, his niece-in-law came out to bow. Upon seeing her, it was Zhou's daughter, and he was greatly surprised. He secretly inquired about the reason, and Xiong told him everything. The uncle sighed in wonder, silently reflecting on the journey, and did not hear of any missing daughter incident in the office. Could it be that the official was concealing this, fearing public criticism?

    111

    居二日，便归河州。启周屏左右，备述所见。周大骇曰：“吾女宛然在室，顷且同饭，哪得有此？然不可不究竟也。”亟使人往擒雄至，严鞠之，得其端委。忿曰：“奈何使妖物，久假吾女之名而不归，玷吾帷薄乎？”商榷于夫人曰：“雄之祖，生为此处副总戎，与吾家门户正相当也。女十七，与雄同庚，年岁适相匹也。即以女妻之，可乎？”夫人曰：“不敢请耳。固所愿也。”

    　　花烛之夕，忽见西宁之女先已在室，雄张皇不知所出，女笑而止之曰：“何事迴避？儿虽是狐，今实为报德来。子年少固不能晰。昔令祖官此地时，尝猎于土门关，儿贯矢被获，令祖悯之，纵之使窜。屡图报复，不得其间，兹得乘此为冰上人，夙愿偿矣。然苟非子与周女有夙缘，儿亦无能为力也。”言讫，出户，旋失所在。众始悟此因果。狐实曲成之也，谓之狐媒。

    Explanation

    111

    After two days, he returned to Hezhou. Speaking privately with Zhou, he recounted what he had seen. Zhou was greatly alarmed and said, "My daughter is still in the room, and just now she had a meal together. How could this be? But it cannot be left unresolved." He immediately sent someone to capture Xiong, questioned him severely, and got to the bottom of it. Anger filled his voice as he said, "How could an evil spirit have been using my daughter's name for so long without returning, defiling my family's honor?" Discussing with his wife, he said, "Xiong's ancestor was born as the deputy commander here, matching our family's status exactly. The daughter is seventeen, the same age as Xiong, and their ages are perfectly matched. Then marry the daughter to him, is it acceptable?" The wife replied, "I dare not ask, it is indeed what I wish."

    On the wedding night, suddenly they saw the girl from Xining already in the room. Xiong was flustered and didn't know what to do. The girl smiled and stopped him, saying, "Why avoid? I am a fox, but I am here to repay a debt of gratitude. You are young and cannot understand. When your ancestor was in office here, once hunted at Tumen Pass, I was shot and captured. Your ancestor pitied me and let me escape. I tried to seek revenge many times but never found the opportunity. Now I can take advantage of this to become a matchmaker, my long-held wish has been fulfilled. But if not for your predestined connection with Zhou's daughter, I would have been powerless." After speaking, she went out the door and immediately disappeared. Everyone then realized this cause and effect. The fox actually orchestrated it, called it a fox matchmaker.

    112

    闲斋曰：予从先王父镇河湟时，雄甫二十余，已在材官之列，女亦无恙。虽一至署中，上下目睹其婉媚，迥异侪俗，洵佳人也。雄后官至参戎。周女诰封淑人。四十即致仕，居河州，犹富甲一郡云。

    　　兰岩曰：一狐耳，数十年之恩，犹切于心，而身报之。乃人有昨日之恩，今日忘之者，抑独何欤！

    　　刘　锻　工锻工刘姓，汀州连城人，乾隆丙子入都。道经汶上，宿逆旅。适有番禺许生，公车北上，与刘同舍。有少年，甫弱冠，眉目如画，云是江右人，预委装于室之东北隅。比许至，已无隙地。主人不欲留，许殊窘迫。少年曰：“四海之内，皆兄弟也。店中果无容膝地耶？与小弟同榻可也。”主人乃留之。许目少年大喜，市酒肉飨焉。

    Explanation

    112

    Xianzhai said: When I followed my grandfather in his governance of the Hexi region, Xiongfu was in his twenties and already held the rank of a military officer. The woman was also unharmed. Although he visited the office once, all who saw her were struck by her grace and charm, which set her apart from the common crowd. She was indeed a beautiful woman. Later, Xiong reached the rank of a high-ranking general. His wife, Zhou, was posthumously honored with the title of Shuren. At the age of forty, he retired from office and settled in Hedao, where he remained the wealthiest in the entire prefecture.

    Lanyan said: He was just a fox, yet the debt of gratitude for his kindness over the past few decades still weighed heavily on his heart, and he repaid it personally. Yet there are people who forget the kindness of yesterday today—what is the reason for that?

    Liu, the blacksmith, was from Liancheng, Tingzhou. In the third year of Qianlong's reign, he entered the capital. On his journey, he passed through Wenshang and stayed at an inn. Coincidentally, there was Xu, a scholar from Panyu, traveling north to take the imperial examination, and he shared the same lodging with Liu. There was a young man, just turned twenty, with features as beautiful as a painting. He claimed to be from Jiangxi and had left his luggage in the northeast corner of the room. By the time Xu arrived, there was no empty space. The innkeeper did not want to keep them, and Xu was in a very difficult situation. The young man said, "All under heaven are brothers. Is there really no room for a knee in the inn? Let me share a bed with you." The innkeeper then kept them. Xu looked at the young man with great joy and bought wine and meat to entertain him.

    113

    刘亦得醉饱，既就枕席，睡未安，忽闻少年厉声曰：“奈何无礼至此！汝视我为何如人耶？”许悄然不发一语。既而少年作怒，哂声曰：“此亦错怪汝，汝未知我之伎俩耳。姑一试使汝知之。”言未绝，随闻砉然一声，白光如匹练，出自帐中，绕室如飞电，寒侵肌骨。刘汗下如雨，屏息不敢少动。一食顷，少年喝言“住！”白光旋敛。少年下床结束曰：“苟非刘丈在室，蛮崽尚得活耶？”更至刘前谢曰：“年少性躁，适间惊扰，方才不安，少有馈遗，聊赎愆罪。前途尚有锐务，须早发。幸左顾，忽见拒也。”亟委一柿黄布囊于枕畔，启扉径去。

    Explanation

    113

    D Liu also drank his fill, having settled into bed, he was not yet at ease when he suddenly heard a stern voice from a young man saying, "How could it be so disrespectful! What do you think of me as a person?" Xu remained silent, not uttering a single word. Soon after, the youth became angry and laughed, saying, "This is also a misunderstanding of you; you don't know my tricks. Let me try once to show you." Before the words were finished, he heard a sharp sound, a white light like a ribbon emerged from the tent, circling the room like lightning, the cold penetrating his skin and bones. Liu's sweat poured like rain, holding his breath and daring not to move a bit. After a mealtime, the youth shouted, "Stop!" The white light quickly gathered. The youth got out of bed and prepared himself, saying, "If not for Uncle Liu being in the room, would the savage child still be alive?" He then went to Liu and apologized, saying, "The youth is impulsive, just now causing a disturbance, just now feeling uneasy, a little gift, to slightly atone for the offense. There are urgent matters ahead, must leave early. Please look to the left, suddenly see a refusal." He quickly placed a yellow cloth bag beside the pillow and opened the door, leaving directly.

    114

    良久，刘心始定。呼许询之，许大愧恧，力叩之，乃吐实曰：“初见少年姣好，深慕之。既抵足，肌肤滑腻如脂。试握其足，不动，拊其髀，又不动。不禁心大荡，欲以龙阳君待之，亦酒醉所致耳。讵意其大有神术耶？”早起，刘怪许无眉，许亦讶刘短须。大惊。及相与束装，毛发适在衾中。方悟夜间白光迅飞时，尽为所削而不觉也。刘私启所委布囊，是白金二笏，至京营运，遂成巨商。许下第，肄业成均，寻病卒。刘常出入文公子士玉之门，故公子能详之。

    　　兰岩曰：飞仙剑客，世所恒有，奈何梦梦，欲以龙阳君待之，哉！其不为所诛也，亦幸免耳。白面许生，功名念切。甫获一席之安，淫心辄炽，几蹈不测，固亦宜然。独不解少年慷慨之抵足，炫以姣容，亲以柔体，不已冶容诲淫哉？或藉此一示其神术耶？

    Explanation

    114

    After a long time, Liu finally calmed down. He called out to Xu to ask him, and Xu, deeply ashamed, persistently inquired, and then confessed, saying, "At first sight of the handsome young man, I deeply admired him. After we lay down together, his skin was smooth and slippery like fat. I tried to hold his foot, but it didn't move; I tried to touch his thigh, but it didn't move. I couldn't help feeling extremely aroused and wanted to treat him as a lover, which was also due to being drunk. Who would have thought he had such supernatural powers?" Early in the morning, Liu found it strange that Xu had no eyebrows, and Xu was surprised that Liu had short mustaches. They were greatly shocked. When they packed their belongings together, their hair and fur were just in the quilt. Only then did they realize that during the night when the white light flew swiftly, they had all been shaved without noticing. Liu secretly opened the cloth bag they had left behind and found two pieces of silver. Upon reaching the capital, they started a business and became wealthy. Xu failed the imperial examinations, studied at the Imperial Academy, and soon died of illness. Liu frequently visited the house of Wen Gongzi Shiyu, so the young master could tell the story in detail.

    Lan Yan commented: Flying swordsmen and immortal guests are commonly found in the world, but how could one be so oblivious, wanting to treat him as a lover? To not be killed by him was also a fortunate escape. Xu, with his fair complexion, was eager for fame and fortune. As soon as he gained a moment of peace, his lust flared up, nearly leading to disaster, which is indeed understandable. However, I do not understand how the young man, with his generous spirit, could flaunt his good looks and use his gentle body to seduce, is this not encouraging licentiousness? Or was this a way to display his supernatural powers?

    115

    蝟精昌邑胡辉岩为予言：其乡禾稼将登，有列芦棚于田间，令子弟夜宿其中，以防窃刈者，连棚十余。一童子，余姓，年稍长于其侪，独卧一棚。居无何，日渐瘠羸，父兄怪而诘之，不吐实。乃阴嘱诸童子，密觇其所为。

    　　是日薄暮，诸童戏于塍上，瞥见一丑女人径入余棚，诸童恐怖，奔告其家。其家人纠合同井，执锄铺往，观女人已出棚回西去，面色如瓦兽，巨口大目，蹀躞而行。逐之二里许，仓卒入乱草中，不复出。迹之，得一穴，大如屋，黝然而黑，不知胡底。因群聚定策，积枯穴口，烧烟薰之。有顷，一物突出，冒烟而奔。众哗四走。物勉行数十步，即不复动。众渐集验之，则一猬死田间耳。剥其皮而张之，大半亩，厚数寸，刺长二尺余，作殷红色，割而分其肉，怪乃绝。余氏子独啜泣，以为磔其丽人也。胡至今尚藏皮一片，每出以示人焉。

    　　兰岩曰：苟遇情人，虽与以南威西子，不易也，人亦自美其所美耳。余氏子安得不泣！

    Explanation

    115

    A Hedgehog Spirit

    Changyi's Hu Huiyan told me: In his village, the crops were about to be harvested, and several thatched huts were set up in the fields for the children to sleep in at night to prevent thieves from stealing the crops. There were more than ten such huts. One boy named Yu, slightly older than his peers, slept alone in one of the huts. After some time, he gradually became thin and weak. His father and brothers found this strange and questioned him, but he did not reveal the truth. They then secretly instructed the other children to spy on what he was doing.

    On that day, as evening approached, the children were playing by the field edges. Suddenly, they saw an ugly woman enter Yu's hut. The children were terrified and ran to tell their families. The family gathered the neighbors, took shovels, and went to investigate. They saw the woman had already left the hut and headed west. Her face was like a clay animal, with a large mouth and big eyes. She walked with a slow, dragging gait. They chased her for about two miles, and she suddenly entered the tall grass and did not come out again. They followed the trail and found a hole as big as a house, dark and deep. They gathered to plan and piled dry grass at the entrance, then set it on fire to smoke her out. After a short time, something emerged, covered in smoke, and ran away. The crowd scattered in all directions. The creature managed to walk a few dozen steps before collapsing. The crowd gradually gathered to examine it and found it was just a dead hedgehog. They peeled the skin and stretched it, which was as large as half an acre, several inches thick, with spines over two feet long, colored a deep red. They cut the meat and divided it among themselves, and the mystery was solved. Yu alone wept, believing that they had killed his beautiful woman. The narrator still keeps a piece of the skin and shows it to people from time to time.

    Lan Yan commented: If one meets someone they love, even if given the beauty of Nan Wei and Xi Zi, it is not enough, for people always find their own beloved beautiful. How could Yu not weep!

    116

    小手舅氏海公为骁骑校，好道，自号捉心主人。居东直门外楼子庄，去城三四里许，常奉祀一狐，亲友求见者，主人先白狐。狐自壁窦中出一小手，与客把握，肥白软腻，如六七岁小儿，其谈论必因人而施，声如燕子。力求一见，终不许。或潜窥之，辄隐身壁角，让曰：“何故来此相嬲！欺我不敢打耶？”随有石如卵大，飞落窥者面旁，相去颧颊，间不容发。咸惊张失色，亟揖而谢之。

    　　一日，主人将往城湾习骑射，狐曰：“三日内勿往，往必有灾。”主人乃止。会军政在即，本参领先期较阅，主人不得已就之。驰骤间，马忽蹶堕，伤左腕，遂为废人，罢职家居。每至薪水不供，未免室人交谪。狐独慰之曰：“莫非数也，是正可以优游，何事怨怼？”室人迁怒曰：“无柴米，一日不得过，乌得不郁郁郁？”狐哂曰：“发福发财，会各有时，不能少待耶？我本欲报汝家数千金，以时未致，故不无少俟。今既不能耐，不得不躁为之。”

    116

    Uncle Hai, the Righteous Gentleman, was a cavalry commander who loved Daoism and self-named himself the Capturer of the Heart Master. He lived outside the Dongzhimen Gate in Louzi Village, about three to four miles from the city. He often served a fox as a deity. When relatives or friends asked to see him, the master first informed the fox. The fox would extend a small hand from a wall crevice, grasp the guest's hand, which was fat, white, soft, and smooth, like that of a six or seven-year-old child. Its conversation was tailored to the person, and its voice was like that of a swallow. They tried to see it, but it never allowed. Sometimes they secretly peeked at it, and it immediately hid in the corner of the wall, reproachfully saying, "Why come here to tease! Do you think I won't hit you?" A stone as large as an egg would follow, fly, and land near the face of the peeper, so close to the cheekbones that not even a hair could fit between them. All were shocked and pale, quickly bowing and apologizing to it.

    One day, the master was going to the city bay to practice riding and shooting. The fox said, "Do not go within three days, or you will surely have disaster." The master then stopped. Just as military affairs were imminent, the commanding officer led an early review. The master, having no choice, participated. In the midst of galloping, the horse suddenly stumbled and fell, injuring his left wrist and becoming a disabled person. He resigned from his position and stayed at home. Whenever provisions ran out, the family members inevitably blamed each other. The fox alone comforted him, saying, "Is it not fate? This is exactly the time to enjoy leisure, why be resentful?" The family members shifted their anger, saying, "Without firewood and rice, we cannot survive for a day, how can we not be depressed?" The fox laughed, saying, "Good fortune and wealth will come in their own time, can't you wait a little longer? I originally intended to reward your family with thousands of gold, but the time has not yet come, so there is no choice but to wait a little. Now that you can't wait, you must act hastily."

    117

    乃教主人购南铅数百金，纳入窦中。戒勿窥伺。由是每夜三更后，即闻房中风匣者，五更后始辍。七七日，呼主人至窦前，以白镪授之，翘边细丝，悉成纹宝。主人惊喜，男女六七人，往来取藏，竟夕始竭，权之得五千金。问狐此从何来，可以驻世否？狐曰：“我与君夙有缘，故用一施仙术，烧炼相赠，非齐奴物也，是非赝物，何不可驻世之有？君第用之无疑虑，我亦从此去矣。”主人切切挽留，不闻应答，久愈寂然，盖已逝矣。主人感其德，为主虔祀之。以金营运数年，财雄一乡，今渐衰矣。

    Explanation

    117

    Then the fox instructed the owner to buy several hundred taels of southern lead and insert it into a hidden passage, warning him not to spy. As a result, every night after three o'clock, he would immediately hear a sound of a bellows or wind instrument in the room, which would finally stop after five o'clock. After forty-nine days, the fox called the owner to the hidden passage and gave him silver. The fine threads or strands had all turned into patterned treasures. The owner was amazed and grateful, and he worshiped the fox devoutly. Six or seven people came and went to retrieve and store the treasures, and it took the whole night to finish. He weighed them and found they amounted to five thousand taels. He asked the fox where these things came from and if they could be preserved in the world. The fox said, "I have a special connection with you, so I used a bit of magic to smelt and transform them for you. They are not fake, so why can't they be preserved? You may use them without any doubt, and I will also leave from here." The owner earnestly tried to persuade him to stay, but he did not hear any response. After a while, it grew increasingly silent, and it seemed he had already passed away. The owner was grateful for his virtue and worshiped him devoutly. He used the money to run his business for several years, becoming wealthy in the entire village, but now he is gradually declining.

    118

    兰岩曰：此狐不可多得，非以其以财赠也。嘉其以义交耳。

    　　蜃气平遥陶贾，贩货至巴里坤，过西海。雨初霁，海中笼重雾，山色皆失。陶爱其空濛，暂憩一树下。俄而雾散，隐隐见海中，有两山并峙，中间一抹云气，横如白练。云渐阔，忽现一浮屠顶，金光四射，瞬息高出云表，数之得五级，俄九级。一饷时，得十三级。色如虹，绕塔尽现楼阁，千层万叠，悉如五色玻璃。出没隐现，须臾变化。

    118

    Lan Yan said: This fox is rare and valuable, not because of the gift of money. Praise him for his friendship based on righteousness.

    A merchant named Tao from Pingyao traveled through the misty regions, trading goods to Barkhali and passing through the Western Sea. After the rain had just cleared, the sea was enveloped in heavy fog, and the mountain colors were all lost. Tao loved its hazy beauty and temporarily rested under a tree. Soon the fog dispersed, and faintly, he saw two mountains standing side by side in the sea, with a streak of cloud in the middle, horizontal like a white silk ribbon. The cloud gradually spread wider, and suddenly revealed the top of a pagoda, radiating golden light. Instantly, it rose above the clouds, counting it as five stories, soon nine stories. In a short while, it reached thirteen stories. The color was like a rainbow, surrounding the pagoda, all the buildings appeared, layered thousands upon thousands, all like colored glass. Appearing and disappearing, changing in an instant.

    119

    陶，市井人，初不知有蜃气变幻事，惊怪而已。少焉，楼阁半泯，浮屠亦渐敛缩，只余八九级。大风忽起，波浪拍天，楼阁浮屠，片片吹如碎锦，顷刻都灭。陶冒风而行，至营中，质诸土人，始知为海市云。

    　　清　河　民清和民某甲，夜还自城。跨一驴，独行郊野，误入墦间，乖迂殊甚。忽有人在后呼其名，甲策蹇不顾。其人追呼甚急，指顾间亦在驴背，以两手环抱甲腰。手如冰，且牢不可脱。甲故有胆，阳作不知，而阴解腰缠。蓦然出不意，反缚之，并系己胸。其人窘迫，絮絮求释不绝。甲置若罔闻，急驰而返。至门大呼“捉得一鬼来矣！”家人燃火出应，甲已弃鞍解缚，所缚化朽槥一片，不复有人矣。

    Explanation

    119

    Tao, a commoner from the marketplace, initially did not know about the phenomenon of mirages and was only surprised and amazed by it. Soon after, the pavilions and towers began to fade away, and the pagoda gradually shrank, leaving only eight or nine levels. Suddenly, a strong wind arose, and waves crashed against the sky, blowing the pavilions and pagoda apart like pieces of broken brocade, all disappearing in an instant. Tao braved the wind and continued walking, eventually reaching the camp where he asked the local people, and only then did he realize it was a sea mirage.

    During the Qing period, in the He region, there was a commoner named Ming Qing who returned to the city at night. Riding a donkey, he traveled alone in the countryside and mistakenly entered a grave field, finding himself in an extremely awkward and mistaken situation. Suddenly, someone called out his name from behind. Ming Qing urged the donkey forward without looking back. The person chased and called out very urgently, and in the blink of an eye, the person was also on the donkey's back, wrapping his arms around Ming Qing's waist. His hands were as cold as ice and tightly held, making it impossible to escape. Ming Qing was naturally brave and pretended not to know, secretly untied the belt around his waist. Suddenly, unexpectedly, he bound the person in reverse and tied him to his own chest. The person was in a difficult situation and continuously begged for release, but Ming Qing ignored him as if he hadn't heard. He rushed back quickly and upon reaching the gate, shouted, "I've caught a ghost!" His family lit a fire and came out to respond. By then, Ming Qing had already abandoned the saddle and untied the bindings, and the bound person turned into a piece of rotten coffin, with no longer being a person there.

    120

    王京王京者，宜君炮手。参戎出署，例放三炮于辕门。次第燃之，其二皆匉訇而鸣矣，其一久之久之不鸣也。参戎出且归矣，京惧责，跂足于炮口，试窥之。炮忽大震，京昏绝仆地。同事负之归家，皮肤如墨，而两目独炯炯。缨帽直飞去十五里外，三道墩塘汛兵得之，竟完好不殷。半年后始愈，面色如猪肝，满布斑点如靛青者数百余，大似莲子。虽妻子亦不复识，无论亲故。七情俱昧，不言不笑，亦不行立，但能坐卧。每见人来探，或独居一室，辄举手向天，张口作炮声云：“轰！”

    120

    Wang Jing, a cannon operator from Yijun, was ordered to fire three cannons at the main gate of the military camp when the colonel left and returned. The cannons were lit in sequence, and two of them roared loudly, but the third one remained silent for a long time. The colonel had already left and was on his way back, and Wang Jing, fearing punishment, stood on tiptoe at the muzzle of the cannon, trying to look inside. Suddenly, the cannon exploded, and Wang Jing fainted and fell to the ground. His colleagues carried him home, where his skin was blackened, but his eyes remained bright. His headdress flew straight away fifteen li and was found intact by patrol soldiers at San Dao Dun Tang. After half a year, he finally recovered, but his face was as dark as liver, covered with hundreds of blue spots as large as lotus seeds. Even his wife and children no longer recognized him, let alone relatives and acquaintances. He lost all his emotions, neither speaking nor laughing, nor walking nor standing, only able to sit and lie down. Whenever someone came to visit him, or when he was alone in a room, he would always raise his hand to the sky, open his mouth to make a cannon sound, saying, "Boom!"

    121

    兰岩曰：七情俱昧，形如木鸡，王京可谓悟道矣。

    　　诡黄诡黄者，不详其里居名字。以所为诡秘有邪术，往往以术致良家妇女于幽僻之处而淫之，不翅什伯，故人皆称之如此。性疑，一妻一妾，防闲独密。妻固郡中大家女，少艾而美。妾亦不恶。

    　　有玳官，年十七八，貌姣好。夙以龙阳之技，毛遂于黄。虽日觊觎于其妻妾，终碍黄，无侧足处也。巨商某，有子妇艳绝，见者常拟为神仙中人。黄偶遇之于佛会，神为之往，乃伪为星士，得其生身甲子，夤夜作法，致之于书斋，恣意淫媾。兴阑，仍以法遣之去。玳于窗隙中窥见之，心大动，乘间盗得其书。复睻知黄妻妾年甲，隐城外一废寺中，夜半如法拘之。初无动静。一饷时，闻檐外簌簌有声，启户视之，则黄之妻妾，白身而至，形如中酒。玳惊喜相半；徐徐扶之入殿，次第污之。

    Explanation

    121

    Lan Yan said: When all seven emotions are completely obscured, one's form resembles a wooden chicken; Wang Jing can be said to have attained enlightenment.

    As for the person known as "Gui Huang Gui Huang," his background and name are not detailed. He was known for his deceitful and mysterious ways, often using his occult powers to lure respectable women into secluded places where he would defile them, not just dozens or hundreds. Because of this, people called him by this name. He was suspicious by nature, having one wife and one concubine, whom he guarded strictly. His wife was a young and beautiful woman from a prominent family in the county, and his concubine was also not unattractive.

    There was a man named Dai Gong, who was seventeen or eighteen years old and handsome. He had long offered himself to Huang with the skills of the Dragon-Yang. Although he daily coveted Huang's wife and concubine, he could not get close to them due to Huang's suspicions. A wealthy merchant had a wife who was extremely beautiful, and those who saw her often likened her to a celestial being. Huang happened to meet her at a Buddhist gathering, and his spirit was drawn to her. He then pretended to be an astrologer, learned her birth year and zodiac sign, and at midnight performed a ritual, bringing her to his study, where he indulged in unrestrained sexual intercourse. When his mood passed, he still used his magic to send her away. Dai Gong saw this through a crack in the window, and his heart was greatly stirred. He took the opportunity to steal Huang's book. Using divination, he discovered the ages of Huang's wife and concubine, and at night, he secretly went to an abandoned temple outside the city, where he performed a ritual to bring them there. At first, there was no response. After a short while, he heard rustling sounds outside the eaves. He opened the door to see that Huang's wife and concubine had arrived naked, their appearance resembling that of someone who had drunk too much. Dai Gong was half surprised and half delighted; he gently led them into the hall, and one after another, he defiled them.

    122

    会有少年五六辈，夜猎归，道经寺前，下马少息，闻殿上有笑谑声，群执炬排闼而入。玳大惊，不知所出。众以火烛之，咸讶曰：“美人难再得也。”遂各解衣，更番奸嬲。玳亦不免后庭。鸡再鸣，始哄然舍去。二妇创甚，四体不能举，玳大窘，欲作法遣归，而颠倒持咒，法不复验。窘甚，遂逋逃。二妇裸卧至日中，为游人所见，鸣诸太守。郡人有识者曰：“此非诡黄之妻妾耶？天何报此恶人之速也！”太守鞫二妇，尽得黄平日所为，拘黄至，严刑榜掠，黄历历招供。太守大怒，立毙杖下。二妇官鬻。后有见玳于邵舟次者，已变服为黄冠矣。

    Explanation

    122

    At night, a group of five or six young men returned from a hunting expedition, passing by the temple gate. They dismounted to rest briefly and heard laughter and jesting sounds coming from the hall. The group, holding torches, pushed open the door and entered. Dai was greatly alarmed and did not know what to do. The group lit it up with fire, and all were surprised, saying, "Such a beautiful woman is hard to find again." They then undressed one by one and took turns to molest. Dai could not escape being involved in the back part of the temple. The rooster crowed again, and they finally left in a commotion. The two women were severely injured and could not move their limbs. Dai was in great distress and wanted to use magic to send them back. But when reciting the spell backward, the magic did not work. In great distress, Dai fled. The two women lay naked until noon and were seen by passersby, who reported it to the prefect. A local who recognized them said, "Aren't these the wives and concubines of the deceitful Huang? How quickly has heaven punished this wicked man!" The prefect questioned the two women and learned all about Huang's past deeds. He arrested Huang and subjected him to severe beating. Huang confessed to everything. The prefect became very angry and immediately killed Huang with a stick. The two women were sold as official slaves. Later, someone saw Dai at the dock in Shao, already dressed as a Taoist priest.

    123

    恩茂先曰：因果之说，人多不信，观于此，尚有疑义哉？近闻京师有某生，短视而善谑，每与其同学游行，见妇女必指点，论其妍丑，佐以秽言。值上元夜，复从其类，踏月看灯。天街士女如云。暮逢一少妇坐车中，　足于辕，众共赞此妇人大妙。生亦神狂，谑浪不已，咸随车行数十武。生曰：“彼足于辕，能有捎得其鞋者，当共聚金钱沽美酒，以谢之。”一少年友挺身自任曰：“作此事，舍我其谁哉！”急走至前，顺势捎之。车速力猛，并脱一袜，妇惊仆车中，白足毕露。众悉鼓掌。舆夫知势不敌，急驱而去。

    Explanation

    123

    Enmaoxian said: The concept of cause and effect is often disbelieved by people; considering this, is there still any doubt? Recently, it was heard that in the capital city there was a certain student who was shortsighted and fond of joking. He would often wander around with his classmates, and when he saw women, he would always point at them, commenting on their beauty or ugliness, accompanied by vulgar remarks. On the Lantern Festival night, he again went out with his group, strolling under the moonlight to admire the lanterns. The main streets of the capital were crowded with men and women like clouds. In the evening, they encountered a young woman sitting in a carriage, with her foot on the crossbar. Everyone praised this woman as extremely beautiful. The man was also in a state of ecstasy, continued to joke and tease without stopping, and all followed the carriage for several steps. He said, "If anyone can grab her shoe while it's on the crossbar, we will all pool money to buy good wine to thank him." A young friend stepped forward and said, "To do this, who else but me?" He quickly ran up and grabbed it with a swift motion. The carriage was moving fast and with great force, and as a result, one sock came off. The woman was startled and fell into the carriage, revealing her bare feet. Everyone clapped their hands. The carriage driver knew he could not compete and quickly drove away.

    124

    少年以手提鞋，以鼻嗅袜，而诩于众曰：“手段莫高强否？”众佩服，聚饮而散。生归见其妻哭于房，惊问所苦，妻不顾而唾曰：“汝尚得为人耶？予今晚自母家还，过四牌楼，见汝辈十余恶少，喧呶街上，指我戏谑，神情已大不堪。既又或前或后，随车不去，我正不测汝辈欲何为，乃蓦于狐群狗党中，走出一少年，径至车前，来捎我鞋，惊惶间已失鞋，且并脱去一袜，万目共睹，出丑尽矣！汝犹从旁大笑。汝尚得为人耶？”生始悟夜来所弄者，即其妻也。亟索只履单袜而审视之，果与所捎者分毫不爽。虽悔恨亦无及矣。由此观之，所得者小，则所失者亦小；所得者大，则所失者尤大。因果之报，如影随形，谁谓天高远而鬼神杳渺哉？

    Explanation

    124

    A young man, holding up his shoes and sniffing his socks, boasted to the crowd: "Isn't my skill impressive?" The crowd admired him, gathered to drink together, and then dispersed. The young man returned home to find his wife crying in the room. Shocked, he asked what was wrong. She ignored him and spat out, saying, "How can you still be a human being? I came back from my mother's house tonight, and as I passed by the Four Pylons, I saw you and about ten other bad young men making a loud commotion on the street, pointing at me and mocking me. The situation was already very uncomfortable. And then some of them either went in front of or behind the carriage, following it without leaving. I didn't know what you all wanted to do. Suddenly among a group of scoundrels, a young man stepped out and walked straight to the front of the carriage, and took my shoes away. In my panic, I lost my shoes and even had one sock taken off. Everyone saw it. I was completely humiliated. You still laughed from the side." The young man finally realized that the girl he had been playing with was his wife. He quickly searched for his single shoe and sock and examined them carefully. They were indeed exactly the ones that had been taken. Even though he regretted it, it was too late. From this we can see that, if what you gain is small, then what you lose is also small; if what you gain is great, then what you lose is even greater. The retribution of cause and effect follows like a shadow. Who would say that heaven is far away and ghosts and deities are distant?

    125

    兰岩曰：以术浮人，自遭显报，乃并不假之外人，而即以自用之人，反而施之，不亦快哉！

    　　梁生汴州梁生，少失怙恃，家极贫，聘妻未婚而妻死，无力复聘。知交谑之，号为梁无告。然为人温雅，能饮，善弈，故为侪类所喜，尤与同学汪、刘二生相莫逆。刘父为刺史，汪家资巨万，皆称豪富，生以寒士周旋于其间，人或非笑，咸以为贫伴富，身无裤，胡不自量乃尔。生闻之，笑曰：“我两肩荷一口，彼虽朱顿之富，其奈我何哉！”人愈嗤其无品，更号之为梁希谢，盖取《金瓶梅》中谢希大以嘲之也。

    Explanation

    125

    Lan Yan said: Using skill to deceive others, one inevitably faces clear retribution, yet instead of turning to outsiders for help, one uses those close to oneself and applies the same methods in reverse—surely, this is delightful!

    Liang, a man from Bianzhou, lost his parents at a young age and was extremely poor. He had been engaged to a wife, but she died before their marriage, leaving him unable to remarry. His acquaintances mocked him, calling him "Liang the Unassisted." However, Liang was gentle and refined, enjoyed drinking, and was skilled at playing chess, which made him popular among his peers, especially with two fellow students, Wang and Liu. Liu's father was a cishi (military governor), and Wang was wealthy beyond measure; both were considered extremely rich and powerful. Despite being a poor scholar, Liang moved among them, and some people ridiculed him, saying that a poor man should not associate with the rich, that he had no sense of self-worth. When Liang heard this, he laughed and said, "I carry only one mouth on my two shoulders. Even if they are as wealthy as Zhu Dun, what can they do to me?" People mocked him even more for his lack of refinement and began to call him "Liang Xi Xie," taking the name "Xie Xi Da" from the novel Jin Ping Mei to mock him.

    126

    刘一妻五妾，汪一妻四妾，又各有美婢娈童。每当宴会，必出以侑觞，争相炫耀。一日，汪以千金从江南复致二丽人，苗条婉媚，诸妾莫匹，以为天下尤物，尽于此矣。乃折简张筵，召客高会。酒再巡，丽人出见，屏开幔卷，冉冉而至。异香满室，坐客皆惊，一拜辄入，不发一言。客饮龁俱停，目炫神夺。汪志得意满，浮白数觥，谓：“诸君何福，得遇仙子！”众舌卷莫答。梁独含笑末坐，品酒味肴，浑如未睹。刘生痴坐良久，始爽然谓梁曰：“众人皆醉，而子独醒，非无目，即无情者！”生徐曰：“已一目了然矣。虽然，入我目，不能动我情也。

    Explanation

    126

    Duan had five concubines, while Wang had four, and each had beautiful maidservants and handsome young male servants. Whenever they held a banquet, the concubines and attendants would be brought out to serve wine, vying with each other in showing off their beauty. One day, Wang spent a thousand gold pieces to bring two more beautiful women from the south. They were slender and graceful, surpassing all the other concubines, and were considered the most beautiful women in the world. He then invited guests by folding a note and setting up a grand feast. After the wine had been served twice, the new beauties appeared, with screens and curtains drawn open, and they glided in gracefully. The room was filled with a strange fragrance, and all the guests were surprised. They bowed once and then entered without saying a word. The guests stopped drinking and eating, dazzled by their appearance. Wang was full of confidence and satisfaction, drinking several cups of wine, and said, "What good fortune you have to meet a fairy!" The guests were speechless and could not respond. Only Liang smiled from the corner seat, tasting the wine and dishes, acting as if he had not seen anything. Duan sat there in a daze for a long time, then said to Liang with a sense of relief, "Everyone is drunk, but you are the only one who is sober. Are you blind, or are you emotionless?" Liang calmly replied, "I have already seen it clearly. Even though they enter my eyes, they cannot move my heart."

    127

    ”汪不悦，曰：“然则何如？”梁曰：“较二兄素所宠眷者，诚有天渊之隔，若即以此为西子，为夷光，尚未也。二兄偏僻，必以我言为河汉，请晰言之。可乎？”众曰：“可。”梁曰：“夫夫也，发为妆掩，足为裙遮，置二者姑不具论。就其共见者指摘一二，妍媸立判矣。”汪曰：“愿闻。”梁曰：“眉修矣，烟煤之所画也；眼媚矣，黑白不甚分也；唇樱矣，胭脂之所点也；肩削腰细矣，而拔颈戾肘，俨然用力，抹胸束肚，宛然有痕，皆戕贼而为之也。吾闻古之美人，面色如朝霞和雪，光艳照人，而四体五官，皆若粉饰。若使乱头粗服，粉黛不施，窃恐国固城坚，虽笑绽两腮，欲倾之而不可得也。”座上客闻此刻论，正合忌心，咸哄堂而和之以笑。汪面□，猝难应答。

    Explanation

    127

    Wang was displeased and said, "Then what should be done?" Liang replied, "Comparing the two brothers' usual favorites, there is indeed a vast difference. Even if we compare it to Xi Shi and Yi Guang, it is still not enough. The two brothers are narrow-minded and will surely consider my words as far-fetched. Please explain it clearly. Can you do that?" The crowd said, "Yes." Liang said, "As for the subject, her hair is covered by makeup, and her feet are hidden by skirts. Let us set these two points aside for now. Let us point out a few things that are commonly seen, and beauty and ugliness will be immediately distinguishable." Wang said, "I wish to hear."
