Liang said, "Her eyebrows are painted with soot; her eyes are attractive, but the black and white are not well defined; her lips are red, painted with rouge; her shoulders and waist are slender, but her neck is stiff, and her elbows are awkward, as if she is forcing herself, with visible marks from tight clothing around her chest and stomach. All these features are the result of artificial alteration. I have heard that ancient beautiful women had faces radiant like a blend of morning glow and snow, shining brightly, and their bodies and facial features were as if they were powdered or decorated. If they let their hair be messy and wear plain clothes without makeup, I fear that even if they smiled widely, their beauty would still be unattainable." The audience listening to this discussion found it aligned with their biases and laughed in agreement. Wang was speechless and unable to respond immediately.

    128

    刘独以为不然，曰：“梁兄眼大如豆，乃亦摇唇鼓舌，吹毛求疵，那足为月旦评！请问西子夷光，是何形象？光艳照人，莫照坏人眼睛否？温柔乡中事，必得身处富贵之实境者，方能确识珠围翠绕之趣。若穷措大看得几行书，辄谓书中有女，据为己有；及见真美在前，一时把捉不定，明知此生，断无此乐，转不得不目空一世，谬论解嘲。独不自念一糟糖妇尚不能消受，至今游泳似鳏，更求一赤脚婢亦不可得，只苦煞贵手，不知一夜几番作肉虎子也！”诸客闻语言儇薄，不复大笑，唯汪生大噱，忿恚都消。

    Explanation

    128

    Liu alone thought otherwise, saying, "Brother Liang, with eyes as small as beans, wags his lips and blows his cheeks, picking out flaws as if searching for them under a magnifying glass. How can such actions be considered a high standard of criticism? Let me ask, what is the image of Xi Shi and Yi Guang? Their radiant beauty, does it not blind people's eyes? The pleasures of a romantic or sexual nature, only those who actually experience the luxury and comfort of wealth and status can truly appreciate the allure of being surrounded by pearls and jade. If a poor scholar reads a few lines of a book and immediately imagines a woman in the book, claiming her as his own; when he encounters true beauty, he is momentarily unable to grasp it, knowing full well that such pleasures are unattainable in this life, he is forced to look down on the whole world and make absurd claims to mock himself. Does he not realize that he cannot even handle a simple, unadorned woman, swimming like a widower, and even seeking a barefoot maid is beyond his reach, tormenting his precious hand, not knowing how many times he has become a phallic symbol in one night!" The guests, hearing his light and frivolous words, no longer laughed heartily, but only Wang Sheng laughed loudly, and his anger and resentment were completely dissipated.

    129

    生知空言无补，不终席而去。从此与汪、刘不甚亲密，交情潜替，同学传其事，共联句以戏之曰：“年少生成老面皮，那知谢大甚难希。而今一发穷无告，不久西山唱采薇。”梁得诗，懊恼殊甚，冥想彼以富贵骄人，喜谀恶直，我何独不能以贫贱骄人，黾勉争气，其觅一妾，聊以自娱乎？第苦囊中羞涩，妄心徒炽，世间又无红拂、红绡之侠烈者，虽有佳人，乌能自至？不胜郁闷。入市闲游，偶见老人，摊卖废书于通衢。梁检视，忽得一帙，纸色甚旧，而装饰极雅，展卷披阅，盖手录陶诗全集，小楷妩媚，不识为谁写，觅款于卷尾，始知为赵文敏真迹。私心狂喜，如掘藏金，问索钱几何，老人曰：“非百文断不售也。”生恐其停留长短，即解衣典而偿之。怀归，待价。适郡中有巨绅，素癖书画，购求颇亟。梁浼人转视之。绅一见，如获拱璧，往返议价，卒得千金。

    Explanation

    129

    The protagonist knew that empty words were of no use, so he left before the meal was finished. From then on, his friendship with Wang and Liu became less intimate, and their relationship gradually faded. Fellow students spread the story and composed a joint verse to mock him: "A young man with an old, unyielding demeanor, how could he know that the great Xie is hard to emulate. Now completely impoverished and without anyone to turn to, soon he will be singing 'Cai Wei' on the western hills." Upon hearing the poem, the protagonist was extremely懊恼, deeply reflecting that those who are proud of their wealth and status enjoy flattery and hate honesty, but why can't he also be proud of his poverty and strive hard? Could he not find a concubine to amuse himself? However, his purse was empty, and his desire burned in vain. There were no heroic figures like Hong Fu or Hong Xiao in the world, and even if there were beautiful women, how could they come to him? Overwhelmed with sadness, he wandered through the market. By chance, he saw an old man selling old books on a busy street. The protagonist inspected the books and suddenly found a volume with very old paper but extremely elegant decoration. He opened the book and read it, discovering it was a handwritten copy of the complete works of Tao Yuanming, with elegant and graceful small script. He did not know who had written it, so he looked for the signature at the end of the book and found out it was a genuine work of Zhao Mengfu. Secretly overjoyed, as if he had discovered buried treasure, he asked how much the old man wanted. The old man said, "I will not sell it for less than a hundred coins." Fearing that the old man might change his mind, the protagonist immediately pawned his clothes to pay for it. He took the book home and waited for a good price. Just then, a wealthy and influential person in the region, who had a long-standing love for calligraphy and painting, sought with great urgency. The protagonist asked someone to show him the book. The wealthy person saw it and regarded it as a precious jade, negotiating back and forth, and finally obtained a thousand gold pieces.

    130

    梁秘而不宣，阴嘱媒妁，旁求佳丽。凡相数十人，无当意者。既而有曲背媪携一女子至，年约十六七，鬒发皓齿，腻理靡颜，天然艳丽，洵平生所未睹，神为之夺。延之坐，问“此即媪所出耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“有女如此，何忧不匹王侯？”媪曰：“侯门似海，一入岂可复见乎？猥以贫老，不得以俾归读书子，但取衣食充口体，不至冻饿以死，又可以作亲戚往返，是为至愿，不敢作非望也。”梁曰：“若然，足见高明。但寒士聘仪简陋，勉奉百金为寿，肯见许否？”媪曰：“的是书痴语。以君长厚，故尔相托。此非老身钱树子，讵忍居为奇货？休休！但提起一文钱，便携之他适矣！”梁不复强，仅具酒相款。媪则醉饱，嘱女善侍夫子，勿念老身，迟日当来饭也，出门径去。女亦晏然，不甚怀想，梁出资为具衣饰，靡不华好。女国色天成，不假纤毫粉饰，淡妆浓抹，罔不相宜，真天人也。梁不破一文，蓦然得此，实梦想所不到。绸缪缱绻，异乎寻常。

    Explanation

    130

    Liang kept his search secret, privately instructing matchmakers to find a beautiful woman for him. He had seen dozens of candidates, but none met his expectations. Soon after, an elderly woman with a hunched back brought a girl to him. She was about sixteen or seventeen years old, with thick black hair, white teeth, smooth skin, and a delicate face, naturally beautiful and truly a sight he had never seen before, so captivating that it took his breath away. He invited her to sit and asked, "Is this the girl you brought?" She replied, "Yes." He said, "With a daughter like this, what need have you to worry about not matching her with a nobleman?" The old woman responded, "The gates of a noble household are as vast as the sea; once inside, how could one ever see the outside again? With my poverty and age, I cannot marry her off to a scholar. I only want her to have enough food and clothing to sustain herself, so she does not die of cold or hunger, and also to maintain family ties, which is my greatest wish. I dare not hope for more." Liang said, "If that is the case, it shows your wisdom. But as a poor scholar, my marriage gifts are modest. I offer you a hundred gold coins as a token of respect. Would you consider this offer?" The old woman said, "This is the talk of a bookish fool. I trusted you because of your integrity. This girl is not a tree of wealth for me; how could I treat her as a rare commodity? Hmph! Just mention a single coin, and I will take her elsewhere!" Liang did not press further, and they merely drank wine together. The old woman, satisfied and drunk, instructed the girl to serve her husband well and not to think of her, promising to visit again later, and then left. The girl remained calm and did not seem to miss her. Liang provided her with fine clothing and ornaments, all of which were splendid. The girl was naturally beautiful, needing no makeup, and looked equally lovely whether dressed simply or elaborately. She was truly a heavenly being. Liang had not spent a single coin, yet he suddenly obtained such a beauty, which was truly beyond his wildest dreams. Their relationship was close and intimate, far beyond the ordinary.

    131

    居无何，同学悉知，相传以为奇事。汪生往见刘生曰：“兄闻之乎？梁无告亦纳姬矣！”刘笑曰：“汴城之大如海，岂乏见弃之女为齐人之妾者？纵有一二分姿色，业操作其家者月余，朝粃糠，晚齑粥，不卜已是鹄面鸠形，见之必呕！”汪曰：“予意亦然，但曩昔曾受其辱，至今不甘。今日借辞往贺，薄而观之，觌面揶揄，以杜其口，亦大快事。”刘笑诺。遂各具分金五星，标曰：“贺仪”，华服高车以往。梁闻报，笑谓女曰：“今此二人，或敢侮予。”为述前事。女微笑曰：“郎无虑，任其所为，儿当为郎小祟之，以泄积忿。”梁嘱设馔。

    Explanation

    131

    After a short while, all the students became aware of the situation and passed it around as a strange and remarkable event. Wang went to see Liu and said, "Have you heard? Liang, who has no family, has also taken in a concubine!" Liu laughed and said, "Kaifeng is as vast as the sea; surely there are abandoned women who can serve as concubines for men from Qi! Even if she has a slight beauty, if she has been working in her family for more than a month, eating bran and chaff for breakfast and a thin soup for dinner, she is bound to be gaunt and emaciated, and seeing her would make one vomit!" Wang replied, "I agree, but I was once insulted by her in the past and have not yet forgiven her. Today, I will go to congratulate her, take a brief look at her, mock her face to face, and shut her mouth, which will be a great satisfaction." Liu laughed and agreed. They each prepared five gold coins, marked them as "congratulations," and went in their splendid attire and high carriages. Liang heard the news and laughed, telling the woman, "Now these two might dare to insult me." He recounted the previous events. The woman smiled and said, "Don't worry, let them do as they please; I will give them a little trouble to vent my long-standing resentment." Liang instructed her to prepare a meal.

    132

    二生至，各叙契阔，并申贺意。梁硍谦不已。酒数巡，二生请见如夫人，梁辞以粗使小婢，不过用执庖厨，以分己力，何敢污贵客之目？二生固请，梁始诺而呼女，甫出户。二生即迷惑失志，嗒然若丧。女款步而前，敛衽而拜。二生不自觉其腰之折也。梁曰：“二公皆通家昆弟，无事回避，今降尊至此，当奉一觞。”女唯唯，捧爵以进。手指纤纤如削玉，二生颠倒，如提傀儡。梁大笑。尽醉而散。二生归途相议，不信人间有此仙人，从此粉黛无颜色矣。焉得一亲玉体，死亦无憾。刘忽曰：“是不难，岂不知梁无告以酒为命者乎？后日是其初度，何难设一席，就其家为寿，暗置乌头酒中，听其鼾睡，彼时为所欲为，将奈我何？无告相狎有年，谅无他说。即使兴讼，各拼数百金，何事不了！”汪大喜。

    Explanation

    132

    The two scholars arrived, each recounting the long parting and reunion, and expressing congratulations. Liang Heng continued to be modest. After several rounds of wine, the two scholars requested to see his concubines. Liang Heng declined, saying he only had unrefined maidservants, who were only used for cooking and to share the household chores, how dare he offend the eyes of noble guests? The two scholars insisted, and Liang Heng finally agreed and called for the woman. As soon as she stepped out the door, the two scholars were immediately confused and disheartened, as if they had lost something. The woman walked gracefully forward and bowed respectfully. The two scholars did not realize their waists had bent. Liang Heng said, "Both of you are close relatives, there is no need to avoid each other. Since you have come to such a humble place, I will offer you a cup of wine." The woman nodded and held the cup to serve. Her fingers were slender and delicate like carved jade, and the two scholars were so enchanted, as if they were being manipulated like puppets. Liang Heng laughed heartily. They drank until they were drunk and then dispersed. On their way back, the two scholars discussed, and they could not believe there were such divine beings in the world. From now on, no makeup or cosmetics could compare. How could they not touch her body, even if it meant dying without regret? Liu suddenly said, "It's not difficult. Don't you know that Liang relies on wine for his livelihood? Tomorrow is his birthday. How difficult is it to set up a banquet, go to his home to celebrate, and secretly put aconite in the wine? Let him fall asleep from drinking. At that time, we can do whatever we want, what can he do? We have been intimate with him for years, I'm sure there are no other secrets. Even if he sues us, we will each spend hundreds of gold pieces, what can't be resolved?" Wang was very pleased.

    133

    至日，果担肴携酒而往，女谓梁曰：“今日二子，来意不善。郎但坐视，儿自有术播弄之。”梁固酒徒，见杯忘死，又素信女之慧黠，知无足虑。日未晡，瞢腾大醉，俨如僵尸，仰卧床上。二生乃阖扉秉烛迫女。女嫣然曰：“二君富贵而韶艾，心非木石，能不两袒？第此非行乐地也，舍后有小楼，幽僻精洁，盍往彼一叙谈乎？”二生闻之，喜跃欲狂，左右各一，掖之而往。绕出屋后，果有楼，且甚高耸。汪曰：“过汝家屡矣，那得有此？”女曰：“新建未匝月也。”接踵而登。楼分内外两楹，外间三面有窗，可以眺远，已预设一席，酒肴俱备，银烛双辉。

    Explanation

    133

    On the day, he indeed carried food and wine to visit, and the daughter said to Liang, "Today these two men have ill intentions. Just sit and watch; I have my own way of handling them." Liang, being a habitual drinker, forgot his death when he saw a cup of wine. He also always trusted the daughter's intelligence and cunning, knowing there was nothing to worry about. Before sunset, he became drowsily drunk, as if a zombie, lying face up on the bed. The two men then closed the door and held a candle to pressure the daughter. The daughter smiled charmingly and said, "The two of you are wealthy and noble, handsome and vigorous, but your hearts are not like wood or stone; how can you not be swayed? But this is not a place for pleasure. Behind the house there is a small pavilion, secluded, remote, and clean. Why not go there to converse?" The two men heard this and were overjoyed, almost leaping with excitement. They each took one of her arms and led her to the pavilion. They went around to the back of the house, and indeed there was a pavilion, quite high and majestic. Wang said, "I have passed by your house many times, how could there be such a thing?" The daughter replied, "It was newly built less than a month ago." They climbed up one after another. The pavilion was divided into two rooms, inside and outside. The outer room had windows on three sides, from which one could look far. A table was already set up, with wine and food all prepared, and the silver candles shone brightly.

    134

    刘拍女肩曰：“卿真可人也。”女但微笑不言。时际盛夏，二生解衣脱帽挂柱上，然后纵饮。女忽曰：“几忘之，儿有些少下酒物，会须取来佐酒。”乃入内间，久之不出。刘起觇之，汪亦踵入。往来搜索，毫无踪迹。汪至衴子前，闻衴内簌簌作声，迫视之，见女仓皇起伏。汪惊喜曰：“何事匿此？”急挨身入衴.女夺门而走，汪追之下楼。女匿身花下，汪直前拥抱，女极力抵拒，汪持之愈坚，方抢攘间，忽数人击柝而至，闻有人声，并力擒捉，批颊骂贼。汪释女，分辩曰：“我秀才也，奈何以贼见目，且肆挞辱？”众就月光审视，亦惊曰：“确是汪三爷，何为在此？祈恕罪！”汪不能答。

    Explanation

    134

    Dong patted the girl's shoulder and said, "You are truly a delightful person." The girl merely smiled without speaking. It was the height of summer, and the two young men removed their outer garments and hats, hanging them on the pillars, and then began to drink freely. Suddenly, the girl said, "I almost forgot, I have some snacks to go with the drink; I will fetch them for you." She then went inside and did not come out for a long time. Dong got up to peek, and Wang followed him in. They searched back and forth but found no trace of her. Wang approached the curtain, hearing rustling sounds from within. He pressed his eyes against it and saw the girl frantically moving about. Surprised, Wang exclaimed, "Why are you hiding here?" He quickly squeezed through the curtain. The girl rushed out the door, and Wang chased her downstairs. She hid behind the flowers, and Wang moved forward to embrace her. The girl resisted fiercely, but Wang held her more tightly. Just as they were struggling, several people struck a wooden block, and hearing voices, they caught them and began slapping and cursing them as thieves. Wang released the girl and defended himself, saying, "I am a scholar, why treat me like a thief and abuse me so?" The others examined him by the moonlight and were surprised to say, "It is indeed Master Wang, what are you doing here? Please forgive us!" Wang could not answer.

    135

    众视地上人，则刘公子也。群扶起，谢孟浪之罪。盖逻卒夜巡，误以为贼耳。二生夙以豪富知名，故汴人强半识之。刘让汪曰：“兄酒狂太盛，窘我出何心？”汪此时方知是刘，不胜骇愕。逻卒曰：“夜深矣，不便归府，请留二人相伴，坐以待旦，可乎？”二生许之。坐稍定，彼此相看，止各着一汗衫，殊不雅观。因思衣服尚在楼柱，浼二卒代索之。卒曰：“此处荒僻，何得有楼？”二生四顾，并不见楼，惟断垣内，大树一株，高数十尺而已。愈骇，怀惑不释。问卒：“梁相公宅在何处？”卒曰：“素不相识其人，焉知其家？且此为孙布政家废园，人迹罕到。

    Explanation

    135

    The crowd looked at the person on the ground, and it was D Liu, the nobleman. The crowd helped him up and apologized for their mistake. It turned out that the night guards had mistakenly identified him as a thief. The two young men were well-known for their wealth and prosperity, so most of the people from Bian recognized them. D Liu asked Wang, "Brother, your drunkenness was too excessive; what was your intention in making me feel embarrassed?" Wang realized that the person he was speaking to was D Liu and was greatly surprised and astonished. The guards said, "It is late now, and it is inconvenient to return to the mansion. Please let us stay with the two of you and sit here until dawn. Is that acceptable?" The two young men agreed. After sitting for a while, they looked at each other and noticed that they were only wearing single shirts, which was not very elegant. They remembered that their clothes were still on the pillar of the building and asked the guards to retrieve them. The guards replied, "This place is desolate; how could there be a building here?" The two young men looked around and did not see the building. Instead, they saw a large tree in a ruined wall, about several dozen feet tall. They became even more surprised and puzzled. They asked the guards, "Where is the residence of Mr. Liang?" The guards said, "We have never met the person before, so how would we know his home? And this is the abandoned garden of the former governor Sun, rarely visited by people."

    136

    虽有人家，亦甚隔绝寥落，只火药局相近耳。抑素不闻乎？孙家园，狐鬼繁。则人家谁有肯近此。”二生大惊，不敢少动。俄而向曙，斜月在西。忽见地上树影中，一块独浓，因风摇摆，不似粗枝密叶，亦不似栖鸟鹊巢，莫测何物。仰视树上，隐隐似人，咸惊异，起身奔走，同止一矢地外，远望相猜，终不可决。天大明，其人附枝不动，众洊集审谛之，非人也，正二生之衣帽，悬挂其上。始各大笑。一人缘而取之，俾二生认着，遂各散归。一时传说，以为口实。二生不甘其侮，以梁生假幻术戏人，乃纠集恶仆，重至其家，欲大兴问罪之举。比至，则门庭俱寂，空无一人，已不知逋逃何处矣。

    Explanation

    136

    Although there are houses, they are also very isolated and sparse, with only the powder depot nearby. Have you not heard? Sun's garden is filled with foxes and ghosts. Then who would dare to live near here?" The two students were greatly startled and dared not move a step. Soon it was nearly dawn, with a slanting moon in the west. Suddenly, they saw a patch of dense shadow in the tree shadow on the ground, swaying in the wind, not resembling thick branches and dense leaves, nor a bird's nest. It was impossible to determine what it was. Looking up at the tree, it vaguely resembled a person. All were surprised and got up to run away, stopping at a place a bowshot away, looking far off and guessing, but finally could not decide. As the day grew bright, the figure attached to the branch did not move; the crowd gathered and examined it carefully, finding it was not a person but the clothes and hat of the two students hanging on the branch. They began to laugh loudly. One person climbed up and took it down, making the two students recognize it, and then everyone dispersed and returned home. At the time, it became a topic of conversation and was taken as a reason to talk about. The two students were not satisfied with this insult, believing that Liang pretended to use magic to play tricks on people. So they gathered some bad servants and went back to his house, intending to make a big fuss and demand an explanation. When they arrived, the courtyard was silent and empty, with no one around, and Liang had already fled to an unknown place.

    137

    数年后，同学友有公车入都者，于磁州道上遇梁生，轻裘肥马，侍从甚都。相见各述契阔，邀还其家，由僻径行约数里，于小山下密林中，入一巨宅，富贵如神仙。友问：“兄何时发迹至此？”梁笑曰：“兄当日附和汪、刘，以贫友为谈柄。今视梁某，仍是希谢面孔否？”友大惭。翌日登堂拜嫂，诚不世姝也。友退谓梁曰：“嫂夫人，果何妙术，能恶剧之。”梁曰：“士无行，不当如是耶？”居三日，乃促装辞行。梁以百金为赠，并送之以诗，中有“阿紫相依千载期”之句，始知梁为狐婿矣。他日归告汪、刘，复生欣慕，于是脂车秣马，强其友同往迹之。至则青山如故，绿水依然，而第宅与人，化为乌有。相与惆怅而返。

    　　茂先曰：此狐大为贫友见侮于富豪者吐气。

    　　兰岩曰：人贵存本来面目耳，岂独巾帼然哉！

    Explanation

    137

    Several years later, a fellow student who had traveled to the capital in an official carriage met Liang on the road through Cizhou. Liang was dressed in fine furs and rode a fat horse, accompanied by a splendid retinue. Upon meeting, they recounted their long separation and invited each other back to their homes. They took a secluded path for about several miles, entering a large mansion hidden in a dense forest at the foot of a small hill. The mansion was as opulent as that of a celestial being. The friend asked, "When did you achieve such wealth and status?" Liang smiled and replied, "When you followed Wang and Liu, you used my poor friend as a topic of ridicule. Now, do you still see Liang as a beggar's face?" The friend felt greatly ashamed. The next day, he ascended to pay respects to Liang's wife, who was indeed a beautiful woman. Afterward, he told Liang, "Your wife, what special skill does she have to be so adept at such mischief?" Liang said, "A man of no moral character should not act like this, should he?" After staying for three days, Liang hurriedly prepared to leave. He gave the friend a hundred gold pieces as a gift and also sent him a poem, which included the line "Azhi, we shall be together for a thousand years." This revealed that Liang was actually a fox spirit who had married into a human family. Later, the friend returned to tell Wang and Liu, who were envious and decided to follow the friend's footsteps. When they arrived, the green mountains and clear waters remained unchanged, but the mansion and its inhabitants had vanished without a trace. They returned, filled with regret.

    Maoxian commented: This fox greatly relieved the poor friend who was mocked by the rich.

    Lan Yan remarked: It is precious to maintain one's original face, not just for women.

    138

    某耯某倅之任羊城，路出广州，遇风，暮泊道士洑之僻港焉。苦舟中欣播，登岸闲步。时际三秋，黄花引眸，不觉行远。过一林，于数矢外，见灯光荧荧。即之，则茅屋数椽，绕之笆篱，篱内有老树一株，下有六人，席地饮，见客惊起，逊坐，意殊款洽。倅固好此杯中物者，就座不辞。座中有一老翁，一少年而广颡。又有三女子，一衣藕色，一衣绿，一衣浅红，年皆及笄。又一书生，年可五十余，甚娴雅。云是土著主人也。问客何来，倅以之官告，并述邦族，咸致敬曰：“贵人也，小酌殊亵。”倅曰：“萍踪乍合，实关夙分。王前于士不以为降，况区区一倅哉！翌日，亦当奉屈舟中，草酌表意耳。”书生曰：“诚如所教，诸君勿为形迹拘矣。诸君事，非贵人不足与谋也。”众初有惨色，既闻是言，莫不色喜，乃相与欢饮。倅亦各询里居姓氏。书生代白，谓老人余姓，少年骆姓，三女方姓，为堂姊妹，皆广州人，自身姓庄，为庠生。“倅各以谀词酬之。

    Explanation

    138

    A certain person and his deputy were appointed to Guangzhou, passing through Guangzhou, encountering a storm, and docking at a remote harbor in the evening. Discomforted by the boat's swaying, he stepped ashore for a leisurely walk. It was the season of three autumns, and the yellow flowers caught his eye, leading him to walk further without realizing it. Passing through a grove, he saw a flickering light several paces away. Approaching it, he found a simple thatched cottage surrounded by bamboo fences. Inside, there was an old tree, and under it, six people were sitting on the ground drinking. They were surprised to see a guest and quickly stood up, inviting him to sit, and the atmosphere was unusually warm and friendly. The deputy, who was naturally fond of alcohol, sat down without hesitation. Among them was an elderly man, a young man with a broad forehead, and three young women dressed in light pink, green, and pale red, all of marriageable age. There was also a scholar in his fifties, who was very refined and elegant. He said he was the local host. The hosts asked where the guest came from, and the deputy replied that he was going on an official mission and mentioned his family background. They all showed great respect, saying, "You are a noble person, and this small drink is a slight honor." The deputy said, "Like floating water plants that meet by chance, it is truly a matter of predestined fate. A king would not consider it a demotion to be in the company of scholars, let alone a humble deputy like me! The next day, I will invite you to my boat for a simple toast to express my feelings." The scholar said, "If you follow the advice, do not be bound by formalities. Your matters are too important to be discussed with ordinary people." Initially, they had a sad expression, but after hearing these words, they all smiled and began to drink joyfully. The deputy also asked about their hometowns and surnames. The scholar spoke on behalf of them, saying that the old man was surnamed Yu, the young man was surnamed Luo, the three women were surnamed Fang and were cousins, and all were from Guangzhou. He himself was surnamed Zhuang and was a student in a local school. The deputy responded with flattering words.

    139

    纵饮之顷，老翁忽愀然曰：“老朽幼在学堂时，最喜读《瘗旅文》，人皆以所好不祥。今孤行数千里外，漂泊无依，彼吏目尚有一子一仆相追随，较老朽真天渊矣！”少年及三女子闻之，皆唏嘘流涕。书生抛一觥，曰：“佳客在前，不理觞政。但呴呴呕呕，徒乱人意，独不虑寡佳客欢耶？况已言事有可谋，何复作楚囚对泣！”五人颇愧赧，唯唯受罚。三女子次第奉倅酒，请歌以侑之。

    Explanation

    139

    During the unrestrained drinking, the old man suddenly became solemn and said, "When I was young and studying in the school, I most enjoyed reading Yi Li Wen. Everyone thought my favorite thing was unlucky. Now, traveling alone thousands of miles away, wandering without support, that official still has a son and a servant following him, compared to me, it’s like heaven and earth!" The young man and the three women heard this and all sighed and shed tears. The scholar threw down his cup and said, "With a good guest present, we shouldn’t follow the drinking rules. But babbling and murmuring only confuses people. Don’t you worry about making the good guest unhappy? Moreover, we’ve already said that there’s something to be planned. Why should we cry like Chu prisoners?" The five people felt somewhat ashamed and embarrassed, nodding and accepting punishment. The three women took turns serving the deputy official wine, requesting a song to accompany the drink.

    140

    倅将避席，书生捺之坐，且曰：“伊行悉出至诚，贵人奈何辜负？”倅不得已，为之引满，书生鼓掌当拍，少年嘬口作箫笛声，清越逼肖。红衣女再咳而歌曰：“夜深枫露凉，蟋蟀吟秋草。空江孤月明，魂迷故园道。”音轻锐凄恻，听者莫不酸鼻。书生颦蹙曰：“一人向隅，满座不乐；况满座向隅，将何以愉快一人耶？幸玉姑莫更发此声，致主客索寞！”少年曰：“玉姑愁绪纷如，那复有欢声向客？余不揣为代之。”乃飞一觞，歌以送之曰：“滚滚江上涛，溶溶沙际月。渺渺雁惊秋，迢迢乡梦绝。”其声烈烈如枭鸣。一座都笑。倅独赏其音节。

    Explanation

    140

    The deputy was about to leave his seat, but the scholar pressed him to sit down and said, "All his actions come from utmost sincerity; how can a person of high status betray him?" The deputy, having no choice, filled a cup for him. The scholar clapped his hands in rhythm, and the young man puckered his lips, making the sound of a flute or bamboo pipe, which was clear and lifelike. The girl in red robes coughed again and sang:

    "Night deep, the maple dew is cool,
    Cricket sings in the autumn grass.
    Empty river, lonely moon shines bright,
    Soul lost on the path to the homeland."

    Her voice was light, sharp, and heartrending, and all who heard it could not help but feel a pang in their nose. The scholar frowned and said, "If one person is in the corner, the whole room is not happy; how much more so, if everyone is in the corner, how can one person be made happy? Please, Miss Jade, do not sing this again, for it makes the host and guests feel gloomy!" The young man said, "Miss Jade's worries are like a tangle; how can there be joyful sounds for the guests? I will take her place." He then lifted a cup and sang to send it off:

    "Rolling waves on the river,
    Moonlight over the sandbank.
    Distant geese startled by autumn,
    Faraway dreams of the homeland broken."

    His voice was loud and intense, like the cry of an owl. The entire audience laughed. Only the deputy appreciated the rhythm and melody of the music.

    141

    老翁曰：“无以嬉戏，转妨正事。适庄先生言，唯贵人可以了大家事，何隐忍不急商榷？”书生笑曰：“终是老人，虽日暮穷途，犹刻刻不忘切己事。然诚为要务，请为贵人陈述。敢冀鼎力，以副奢望，莫推诿乎否？”倅已半酣，攘臂曰：“人固有具热肠侠骨如某者乎？天涯邂逅，良朋盍簪，气味已投，金兰分定。又何事嗫嚅其辞，令人郁闷耶？”众闻之皆喜，即席展拜。书生再拜曰：“一言慨诺，众所心感。众所求事，此际未可尽言，贵人且志之，请于明日，循江岸向西，行里余，有老人矮而髯，操渔舟为业者，就而告以今日之事，并吾等情状，则彼自有说，必能使贵人豁然不疑也。”倅曰：“谨奉教。”于是四座欢甚，无复愁苦故态。

    Explanation

    141

    The old man said, "Do not engage in idle play, as it will only hinder important matters. Recently, Mr. Zhuang said that only a noble person can resolve important matters. Why hesitate or delay in discussing it urgently?" The scholar smiled and said, "You are still an old man, even though you are in a difficult situation, you still constantly remember your urgent matters. I dare to hope for your full support to fulfill my high expectations. Will you not shirk or avoid it?" The second person, already half-drunk, waved his arm and said, "Is there anyone who truly possesses the warm heart and chivalrous spirit like me? Meeting by chance in distant places, like-minded friends gather, our interests and personalities are aligned, and our friendship is firmly established. Why hesitate or stutter in your words, making others feel depressed?" Upon hearing this, everyone was pleased and immediately bowed in respect. The scholar bowed twice and said, "Your gracious agreement has deeply moved us all. The matter we seek is not yet fully explained, but please remember it. Tomorrow, follow the riverbank to the west, walk a little further, and there will be an old man who is short and bearded, making a living by fishing. Go to him and tell him about today's matter, and our situation. He will have his own explanation and will certainly make the noble person understand and have no doubts." The second person said, "I will follow your instructions." Thus, the four seats were filled with great joy, and the previous sadness and distress were completely gone.

    142

    已而斗移漏转，约略四更，老翁曰：“贵人去舟已远，纪纲复不来接引，应下榻此间矣。”少年曰：“此自无庸议，但庄先生所居不广，大家留此，未免抵颈交趾，非所以待贵人。吾二人且去休。玉姑姊妹，不妨留此，侍贵人枕席，预报抚存之德。”三女闻之，垂首赧然。倅辞谢曰：“某虽失学，尝闻三女为粲。粲，美物也，而何德之堪之！”老翁曰：“不然，贵人热肠，为天人所钦瞩，何言不德？彼玉姑姊妹，虽云贱品，岂无环草私愿，聊酹涓埃于一夕乎？矫情震物，贵人曷取焉？”倅阳为拗阻，而阴实愉悦，乃以目视书生。书生曰：“未知雅抱何如耳。”倅曰：“某生平未尝拂人之情，粲不我弃，反敢弃粲乎？”众皆怂恿之。书生独正色曰：“玉姑姊妹，猥以沦落，孱困至极。

    Explanation

    142

    After a while, the stars had shifted and the water clock had turned, approximately the fourth watch of the night. The old man said, "The person of high status has already left the boat far behind, and the guide has not come to pick us up. We should stay here." The young man replied, "This need not be discussed. However, Mr. Zhuang's residence is not very spacious. If everyone stays here, we will be so close as to touch necks and toes, which is not suitable for a person of high status. We two will go rest. The three ladies, Yu Gu, can stay here to serve the person of high status, showing our gratitude and care." The three women heard this and lowered their heads, blushing. The deputy declined politely, saying, "Although I may have lost my studies, I have heard that the three ladies are beautiful. Beauty is a precious thing, but what virtue do I possess to deserve it?" The old man said, "Not so. The person of high status has a warm heart and is admired by heaven and people. Why would he be considered unworthy? The three ladies, though of humble status, surely have modest wishes and would be willing to make a small offering for the night. To pretend to be different to impress others, the person of high status would not do such a thing." The deputy outwardly resisted but inwardly pleased, then glanced at the scholar. The scholar said, "I do not know what noble intentions you have." The deputy replied, "Throughout my life, I have never disappointed others. If the ladies do not abandon me, how could I abandon them?" Everyone encouraged him. The scholar alone spoke seriously, "The three ladies, though humble and fallen, are in a very difficult situation.

    143

    得贵人发恻隐心，调饥甫慰，虽欲不听众人之所迫，及贵人之所为，不特不能，且亦不敢，正以蛹之以茧自缚，无力解脱，缄口制心，讵无隐憾。所赖仁人君子，奋拯溺扶危之志，遏偎红倚翠之心，是所望也。苟闻孟浪之谈，辄行苟且之事，背明德而逞私欲，是以义始而以利终也，岂鲰生翘首跂足之所望于贵人者哉？理痼于中，言激于外。幸宥其冒昧，取共憨愚。

    Explanation

    143

    "Having gained the compassionate heart of a noble person, just now alleviating a pressing need, though one might wish not to heed the pressures of the many or the actions of the noble, not only is it impossible, but it is also forbidden. To bind oneself like a silkworm in its cocoon, powerless to break free, to seal one's mouth and control one's heart, is there no hidden regret? The hope lies in the virtuous and the noble, who have the resolve to rescue the drowning and support the endangered, to curb the heart that clings to red and leans on green. It is this that is hoped for. If one hears reckless talk and acts carelessly, abandoning bright virtue to indulge in private desires, it is to start with righteousness but end with profit. Is this what the small man, looking up and stretching out his toes, hopes for from the noble? Principles are rooted inside, words are provoked from outside. May one be pardoned for its rashness and take its foolishness."

    144

    ”倅惭汗无地，下席揖谢曰：“余翁所言，诚惛耄之乱命；骆君之意，尤□蘖之狂情。小子素愚，能不为其簧鼓！得先生诃而止之，不致禽处。古人所以尚诤友也。敢不拜药石之赐！”书生答拜而赞美之，曰：“贵人见善即迁，闻过辄改，多福未可量也！余、骆二君，归心太挚，遂行不恕。闻贵人悔过，亦当改之。”二人跼蹐不安，顿首引咎。三女子欣然色喜，再三叩谢，相继辞去。书生导倅入室，室甚卑隘，萧然环堵，惟正中设一竹榻，壁挂一篝灯，余无所有。书生安置已，反曳双扉，郑重而去，倅亦就枕。

    Explanation

    144

    The deputy, feeling so ashamed that he had no place to hide his sweat, stepped down from his seat to bow and thank, saying: "What my elder said was indeed a confused command from an old and foolish person; Luo Jun's intent was even more the reckless passion of a young and inexperienced person. A simpleton like me, how could I not be swayed by such flattery? Thanks to the master's rebuke, I was stopped from going too far. This is why ancient people valued诤友 (friends who offer honest criticism). How dare I not accept this medicine as a gift?" The scholar bowed in return and praised him, saying: "A noble person changes when they see good and corrects themselves when they hear of their faults. Their future blessings are incalculable! The two gentlemen, Yu and Luo, were so eager to return home that they acted without restraint. Hearing that the noble person has repented, they should also correct themselves." The two men felt uneasy and bowed repeatedly to take responsibility. The three women were delighted and thanked repeatedly, then left one after another. The scholar led the deputy into the room, which was very small and sparsely furnished, with only a bamboo mat in the center and a hanging lamp on the wall, nothing else. After the scholar had settled him, he pulled the doors closed and left solemnly, and the deputy also lay down to rest.

    145

    既觉，则独卧一古冢旁古树之下。但见紫英黄萼，秋草纵横。重露砭肌，江天向晓，不胜眙愕。亟起着衣，僮仆已踪迹而至，悉哆口坌息，绕倅大哗曰：“何苦露宿于此！仆辈奔走一夜，到处觅寻，几曾停履！”倅曰：“唉！即予亦岂得已而不已哉！事极尴尬，正须与汝辈证明。”乃率众循江西行，约里许，果见一矮老人白发绕颊如毡，方解缆于芦汀，势将他徙。倅呼而止之，密告所遇，老人瞠目良久，始惙然曰：“君洵从庄秀才墓道中来矣。行年七十，不谓今日乃见异事。”倅问：“庄秀才何如人也？”老人叹曰：“此亦奇缘，非偶然也，可不明告乎？”因道：“此间道士洑之下流分港也。

    Explanation

    145

    After waking up, I found myself alone, lying by an ancient tomb under an old tree. All I could see were purple flowers and yellow calyxes, with autumn grasses sprawling everywhere. The heavy dew pierced my skin, and the river and sky were turning toward dawn. I couldn't bear the shock and surprise. I quickly got up and dressed, and the servants had already tracked me down and arrived. They were all gaping and panting, circling around me and making a lot of noise. "Why suffer from sleeping out here!" they exclaimed. "We have been running all night, searching everywhere, hardly stopping!" I sighed, "Even I had no choice but to do so! The situation is very awkward, and it needs to be proven to you." Then I led the group along the west bank of the river, about a li away, and indeed saw an old man who was short and had white hair around his cheeks like a felt. He was just untieing the rope on the reed bank, about to move elsewhere. I called out to stop him and confidentially told him what happened. The old man stared for a long time and then weakly said, "You truly came from the tomb path of Scholar Zhuang. At the age of seventy, I did not expect to see such unusual things today." I asked, "What kind of person was Scholar Zhuang?" The old man sighed, "This is also a strange coincidence, not accidental, can it not be clearly told?" He then explained, "This is the downstream branch of the Daoist Fú."

    146

    向西北茂林中，依山结庐以居者，有庄叟焉，年望七旬。予为比邻，交谊最深。叟木讷无他长，惟事念佛。其子为秀才，五十而死，死且二载矣。适闻君所饮宿处，即其殡宫也。秀才生时，质直好义，每值风雨大作，必亲至江干以拯溺为务。廿余年来，不下数百人。即有死者，亦必敛以棺衾，付其同行者载之去。唯有一老翁、一少年并三女子，名姓里居，俱无可考，故致今犹厝秀才墓侧，自客岁秋间，叟每嘱予，命留心于广南仕宦者。今据君夜来所遇，皆云家广州，且正符五人形状，又有姓可访，意叟必有所见闻矣。君如有意，何不同往一叩庄叟乎？”倅曰：“能为导否？”曰：“义在所在，岂有让君独劳？”乃舍棹扶浆，蹒跚导倅以行。

    Explanation

    146

    In the dense forest to the northwest, there lived an elderly man named Zhuang who had built a thatched hut against the mountain. He was about seventy years old. I lived nearby and had a deep friendship with him. Zhuang was straightforward and not particularly skilled in any other area, but he was devoted to reciting Buddhist scriptures. His son was a scholar who had passed the first level of the imperial examination. He died at the age of fifty, and it had been two years since his death. I just heard that the place where you stayed and drank was actually their funeral hall. The scholar was honest and righteous, and whenever there was a heavy rainstorm, he would personally go to the riverbank to rescue drowning people. Over twenty years, he saved hundreds of people. Even if someone died, he would bury them with a coffin and shroud and have them carried away by those who were with him. There were five people—a elderly man, a young man, and three women—whose names, places of origin, and residences were all unknown, so they were still temporarily placed beside the scholar's grave. Since last autumn, the elderly man had often instructed me to pay attention to officials from the south. Now, according to what you encountered last night, they all said they were from Guangzhou, and their appearances matched those of the five people. There were also surnames that could be traced. It seems that the elderly man must have some knowledge of this. If you are interested, why not go together to inquire about the elderly man?" The deputy asked, "Can you guide me?" I replied, "Where there is righteousness, how can I let you work alone?" So I left the oar and held the paddle, slowly guiding the deputy to walk.

    147

    去门尚远，已见叟策黎杖，捻念珠，立树下持经咒矣。相见各有所述，叟乃叹曰：“老夫一心净土，无暇旁求。不意畴昔梦见亡儿，谓‘所厝五棺，二男三女，皆珠江人也。苟有仕宦其地者，携回葬之。虽无亲故，亦正首丘，不强于念佛万声耶？”老夫志之，二年于兹矣。昨宵复见梦云：“今日心愿可了。’故立俟于此。讵意若是之验，虽以托老友，而老友能尽心力，又强似我念佛功德矣！尊官诚能为是义举，不妨火化之，携骼南行，但摒挡一月俸钱，买半亩地，葬之，亦仁人之事也，不又强似老友之尽心力乎？”倅感其言，亟往取五棺，聚薪化之，分贮罐中，载之以去。

    Explanation

    147

    The door was still far away, but I had already seen the old man holding a walking stick, twirling prayer beads, and standing under a tree reciting scriptures and incantations. When we met, we each shared our stories, and the old man sighed and said, "I am devoted to the Pure Land and have no time to seek other things. Unintentionally, I dreamed of my deceased son in the past, who said, 'There are five coffins buried, two boys and three girls, all from the Pearl River region. If there is someone in office in that place, bring them back and bury them. Even without relatives or friends, it is still the proper place for a grave, is it not stronger than reciting ten thousand mantras?' I have remembered this for two years now. Last night, I saw a dream again saying, 'Today, the wish can be fulfilled.' Therefore, I stood waiting here. Who would have thought it would be so effective, even if entrusted to an old friend, the old friend can put in all his effort, and it is even stronger than my practice of reciting mantras! If your honor can undertake this righteous deed, it is not wrong to cremate them and carry the bones south, just set aside a month's salary, buy half an acre of land, and bury them. It is also a righteous act, is it not even stronger than the old friend's efforts?" The deputy was moved by his words and hastily went to fetch the five coffins, collected firewood and burned them, stored them separately in jars, and carried them away.

    148

    闲斋曰：若庄秀才，可谓锐于行仁者矣。生时未了事，死必了之。若倅者，可谓勇于行义者矣。不能利而行，必勉强而行之。然非庄不能成倅之义，亦非倅不能成庄之仁。兹二人者，所谓相需济美者也，而庄尚矣。至于庄叟之好善，渔叟之酬知，士夫所未逮者，彼则行所无事焉。岂唯齿之当尊，亦且德之宜表。世儒眼大如豆，又乌知村翁野老，固多不失其赤子之心也，可不勉哉！

    　　兰岩曰：庄生生前好义，拯敛多人，死后复能规友以义，嘱父留心于无主之魂，致能各归乡土，诚义人义举也！五十而亡，终于诸生，天何报施之薄哉！

    Explanation

    148

    Xian Zhai said: Zhuang the scholar can be called one who is keen on practicing benevolence. If there are matters left undone in life, they must be completed in death. As for the deputy, he can be called one who is brave in practicing righteousness. If it is not for personal gain, one must still act with effort. However, without Zhuang, the deputy's righteousness could not be fulfilled, and without the deputy, Zhuang's benevolence could not be achieved. These two individuals are said to complement each other to achieve excellence, and Zhuang is even more so. As for Zhuang's father's love of goodness and the fisherman's repayment of kindness, these are things that gentlemen have not yet accomplished, yet they are carried out effortlessly by them. Not only are they worthy of respect in terms of age or status, but they are also worthy of being exemplified in terms of virtue. Worldly scholars have eyes as small as beans; how can they know that village elders and country folk often retain their innocent, pure hearts? How can one not strive!

    Lan Yan said: Zhuang was diligent in performing good deeds before his death, rescuing and helping many people. After his death, he was able to advise his friend with righteousness, instructing his father to care for the spirits of those without a resting place, ensuring they could return to their hometowns. Truly, he was a righteous person and a righteous deed! He died at the age of fifty, ending his life as a candidate for the imperial examinations. How thin is the reward from heaven?

    149

    倩霞汀镇右营游击李锦，为予言：耿精忠封闽时，骄奢淫佚。有林青者，年二十，为耿府护卫。独承眷爱，不啻子侄，以故得出入藩邸不禁，虽耿之爱妃宠妾，皆得见之。合府呼为小林。

    　　值七夕，耿与诸妃夜宴，见林侍侧，戏问曰：“汝娶室乎？”对曰：“尚未。”耿笑曰：“吾贵为藩王，日与诸姬极床第之乐，视双星之一年一会，代为寡欢。今汝少年稚齿，正当行乐及时，乃游泳似鳏，其何能耐？吾侍女如云，容汝自择一人，以为佳偶。”林跪曰：“承恩命，但得倩霞为妻，平生愿足矣。”耿笑顾诸姬曰：“谁谓小蛮子选色不精哉！倩霞方龆龀，即从吾于沈阳，学作内家妆。迄今又十年，年十九矣。吾非不欲纳之，特以吾子欲之故也。今吾子殁矣，诸子过稚，吾又将老，诚不可老夫女妻，蹈枯肠之咎。若以归此子，洵属佳偶。虽然，谈何易也！吾思得一法，翌日当令窥窗自选，视其福厚薄耳。”遂尽欢而罢。

    Explanation

    149

    Right Guard Li Jin of the Right Camp in Qianxia Town told me: When Geng Jingzhong was enfeoffed as the Prince of Fujian, he was extravagant and indulgent in pleasure. There was a man named Lin Qing, who was twenty years old and served as a guard in Geng's mansion. He was favored above all others, almost like a son or nephew, and thus had unrestricted access to the governor's residence. Even Geng's beloved concubines and favored wives could see him. The entire household called him Xiao Lin.

    On the Qixi Festival, Geng held a night banquet with his concubines. Seeing Lin Qing standing by his side, Geng playfully asked, "Have you married yet?" Lin Qing replied, "Not yet." Geng laughed and said, "I, as a prince, enjoy the utmost pleasures with my concubines every day. Comparing this to the annual meeting of the Cowherd and Weaver Girl stars, I feel deprived. Now that you are young and should enjoy life, why do you behave like a widower? How can you bear it? I have many concubines; allow you to choose one for yourself as a good match." Lin Qing knelt and said, "I accept your kind command. If I could marry Qian Xia, it would fulfill my lifelong wish." Geng laughed and looked at his concubines, saying, "Who would say that the young man is not discerning in choosing a partner? Qian Xia was a young girl when she accompanied me to Shenyang and learned to be a lady-in-waiting. It has been ten years since then, and she is now nineteen. I was not unwilling to take her, but my son wanted her. Now that my son has passed away, my other sons are too young, and I am growing old. It would indeed be inappropriate for an old man to marry a young woman, risking the consequences of a 'dry gut.' If I were to give her to this young man, it would truly be a good match. However, it is not easy to do so! I have thought of a way: tomorrow, I will let him look through a window and choose her based on his fortune." And so, they all enjoyed themselves before parting.

    150

    次日，耿命以红锦为步幛，长数寻，周布于广厅，每相去尺余，穿一穴如碗大。共选艳女三十人，各出一掌于穴外，而全身悉隐幛中。使内监导林入，嘱曰：“此三十人中，有倩霞在，汝自识之，择定即书名于其裳，吾将亲验焉。”林受命，往复审视，莫不纤纤如玉，实难分辨。方踟蹰间，猛忆倩霞左手无名指有爪长二寸许，盍执以为证？于是还阅至十六掌，果符所见，亟取笔书名，回白于耿。耿验之，果倩霞也。愕然曰：“岂有是哉！”呼倩霞出其手，反复视之，见指爪，乃大笑曰：“弊窦在此矣！汝姑退，明日更有良法，必使尽善无弊而后可。”林怏怏而出。归寓祷诸大士。是夜梦一女奴，持白绢一片，赠林，上有花纹，作川字形，林拜受而寤。不解何意，辗转不能复寐，披衣待晓。

    Explanation

    150

    The next day, Geng ordered a red brocade screen to be made, several zhen long in length, which surrounded the large hall. Each screen was separated by more than a foot, with a hole the size of a bowl. Thirty beautiful women were selected, each extending a hand through the hole while their entire bodies remained hidden behind the screen. An eunuch was sent to guide Lin in and instructed him, saying, "Among these thirty, there is Qian Xia; you will recognize her. Once chosen, write her name on her garment, and I will personally verify it." Lin accepted the command and repeatedly examined them. None were not slender like jade; indeed, it was difficult to distinguish. Just as he was hesitating, he suddenly remembered that Qian Xia had a claw on her left ring finger, about two inches long. Why not take it as proof? So he returned to the sixteenth hand, and indeed, it matched what he saw. Quickly taking a brush, he wrote her name and returned to report to Geng. Geng verified it and exclaimed in surprise, "Could there be such a thing?" He called Qian Xia to show her hand, examined it repeatedly, saw the claw, then laughed loudly, saying, "The flaw lies here! You may leave for now. Tomorrow, there will be a better method. Only after ensuring everything is perfect and free of flaws will it be acceptable." Lin left with a sullen expression. Returning to his lodging, he prayed to the Bodhisattvas. That night, he dreamed of a female servant holding a piece of white silk, giving it to him. The silk had patterns shaped like the character "川." Lin bowed and accepted it, then woke up. Not understanding its meaning, he turned restlessly and could not fall asleep again. Dressed, he waited for dawn.

    151

    晨起，方盥漱，即有传王命召林者。急衣冠趋府，耿已坐斋中。谕曰：“步幛复设，汝可复去接天婚矣。”一监导而入。及厅内，锦帐布置如故，但每一穴出一白足。林骇然欲避，监挽之曰：“王以手有弊，故示以脚耳。依旧五指一掌，特无二寸爪甲。汝其细认之。”林不得已，乃依次阅视，但见　踦春妍，趾拇玉润者，不一而足。卒见一足，洁白细腻，异于他足，且隐隐有川字纹在趾间，宛然梦中所见于缯上者。恍然悟，即书名焉。白耿验之，倩霞也。大惊叹曰：“天缘也。”遂以倩霞妻之，更赐千金为妆奁之费。

    Explanation

    151

    In the morning, just as Lin was washing and rinsing, he received a royal summons. Hurriedly dressing and heading to the government office, he found Geng already seated in the study. The king announced, "The step screen is set up again; you can go once more to meet the heavenly marriage." A supervisor guided him in. Upon entering the hall, the brocade tent was arranged as before, but from each hole emerged a white foot. Startled, Lin wanted to avoid it, but the supervisor pulled him and said, "The king has a defect in his hand, so he shows his feet instead. They still have five fingers and one palm, but no two-inch claws. You should carefully recognize them." Reluctantly, Lin examined them in order, seeing the spring beauty of the toes and the smoothness of the toes and thumbs, endless in variety. Finally, he saw one foot, white and delicate, different from the others, with faint river character patterns between the toes, as if it were what he had seen in a dream on a silk fabric. Suddenly realizing, he immediately wrote the name. Geng verified it, and it was Qianxia. Greatly exclaiming, he said, "It is a heavenly connection." He then married Qianxia to Lin and further granted a thousand gold pieces as a dowry.

    152

    林青得倩霞，出于意表。深感耿恩德，欲图厚报，每形诸颜色，徵诸话言。倩霞说之曰：“王之有恩于君，固矣。然王之行事，类此者甚多，未可谓以国士遇君也。且君以弱冠补黑衣，一年之间，得至护卫。诚以王为冰山之靠也。而王淫虐已极，及身必致奇祸。皮之不存，毛将安附？不如去此他适，庶几为全身远害之道。”林曰：“一官萦绊，去将安之？”倩霞曰：“君意未决耳，意果决，莫虑无栖止处。儿有姨在京师，盍往投之？”林亦知耿将为逆，无计遐举，闻倩霞言殊喜，急打叠细软，市两骏马，与霞乘夜北遁。依托姨家，入籍宛平，出资贩茶，遂为富室。

    Explanation

    152

    Lin Qing unexpectedly received Qianxia, deeply grateful for Geng's kindness, wanting to repay with great favor. Often showing in facial expressions and expressing in words. Qianxia said to him: "Geng's kindness to you is indeed significant. However, Geng's actions like this are many, and it cannot be said that he treats you as a national hero. Moreover, you were appointed as a black guard at the age of twenty, within a year, reaching the position of a guard. Truly, you consider Geng as a support like an iceberg. Yet, Geng is extremely indulgent and cruel, and will surely bring extraordinary misfortune upon himself. If the skin is gone, where will the hair attach? It would be better to leave and find another place, to be a way to preserve oneself and avoid harm." Lin said: "A minor official position that binds one, where to go when leaving?" Qianxia said: "You have not yet made up your mind, if you do, there is no need to worry about finding a place to stay. My aunt lives in the capital city, why not go and seek her out?" Lin also knew that Geng would rebel, with no way to escape far away, and was very pleased to hear what Qianxia said. Quickly packed his valuables, bought two fine horses, and fled north at night. Relied on his aunt's family, registered in Wuan, invested in tea trading, and became a wealthy family.

    153

    霞固开元人。耿为总兵时，尝统兵过宁远，路见霞牧豕于田畔，一老妪坐户下缉苎麻。霞时才九岁，虽乱头粗服，脂粉不施，而眉目如画。耿问老妪，云是孙女。耿出白金十两欲取之，妪不从。耿大怒，掠之以归。及长，修短得衷，纤秾合度，玉肌花貌，艳丽殊常。耿屡欲纳之，而袁姬不容，故迟至十九，忽归林。倩霞在耿府十年，府中事无巨细，悉能言之。其姨及诸女眷，逐日于绣窗茶榻间听其追术，以广新闻。略记数则，比诸媚猪艾豭之条，为逆藩秽史。

    　　倩霞言：耿内宠甚多，自妻以下，曳罗绮如夫人者二十余人。唯袁姬齿稚色妖，宠冠诸妾，而淫妒性成，耿爱而惮之。袁冶容诲淫，闽中夏热，袁晚浴后，着蝉纱雾毂，肌体隐约可见。耿少子，别姬所出，最佻达，为见惯之司空，遂蒸焉。每交接，不避婢媵，丑声外扬，不知者，惟耿与其妻耳。

    Explanation

    153

    Xia was indeed a person from the Kaiyuan period. When Geng was the commander-in-chief, he once led troops through Ningyuan, where he saw Xia tending pigs by the fields, and an old woman sitting by the door weaving ramie. At that time, Xia was only nine years old, though her hair was unkempt and her clothes were coarse, and she did not apply makeup, yet her eyes and eyebrows were like a painting. Geng asked the old woman, and she said she was Xia's grandmother. Geng offered ten taels of white gold to take her, but the old woman refused. Geng became furious and seized her, taking her away. By the time she grew up, her proportions were well-balanced, her figure was fine and full in the right measure, her skin like jade and her face like flowers, and her beauty was unusually striking. Geng repeatedly wanted to take her as a concubine, but Lady Yuan did not allow it, so he delayed until she was nineteen, when she suddenly returned to the woods. Qian Xia stayed in Geng's household for ten years, and no matter how big or small, all matters in the household, she could speak about everything. Her aunts and other female relatives listened to her recount events every day in the embroidered window and tea table, to broaden their news. I will briefly record a few instances, comparing them to the records of seduction and debauchery, as part of the history of rebellion and corruption.

    Qian Xia said: Geng had many concubines, with over twenty women under him, all dressed in silk and satin like ladies. Lady Yuan, however, was young and beautiful, and her favor surpassed all the other concubines, though her nature was given to lust and jealousy. Geng loved her but feared her. Lady Yuan, with her alluring appearance, encouraged licentious behavior. In Fujian, the summer was hot, and after bathing late at night, she wore thin silk and misty gauze, her body barely visible. Geng's youngest son, born to Lady Yuan, was the most debauched, and he was accustomed to such behavior, so he eventually engaged in adultery. Every time they met, he did not avoid the maidservants, and the scandal spread widely, though the only ones unaware were Geng and his wife.

    154

    藩下有卢大眼者，质直而能事，耿倚之为左右手。一日，侍耿闲话，适少子趋过于前，衣服华异，腰间杂佩甚多。耿顾而乐之，谓卢曰：“诚翩翩一美少年也！使宰河阳，当为万花主人。此间风俗不美，当防闲其出，勿近娈童。”卢曰：“佩玉蕊兮，王无所系之。”耿曰：“何谓也？”卢对曰：“昔日臣猎于野，鞲鹰嗾犬，不遗狡兔，而一矢外地，有介鹿而不之顾也，岂臣见其小而不见其大哉？亦以神之有注有不注耳。王见世子不服饰，而不叹其妖，是犹臣之见兔而不见鹿也，所失不亦多乎？夫冠者所以壮其首，服者所以章其身。故冠　以触邪也，冠蝉以洁操也；衣豹示服猛也，袭貂昭美德也；志道则佩环也，修德则佩琨也；玦以决疑，　以解纷也。所以见其佩而知其能也。今世子衣服炫异，是谓不衷；修饰容仪，是谓阶厉。臣恐秽德之彰，在萧墙之内，不在寝门之外也。”耿大怒，选事杖杀之。

    Explanation

    154

    Under the fiefdom of Fan, there was a man named Lu Dayan, who was honest and capable in his duties. Geng relied on him as his right and left hand. One day, while serving Geng during a casual conversation, Lu Dayan saw the younger son pass by in front, dressed in splendidly different clothes and wearing many ornaments on his waist. Geng looked at him and was pleased, saying to Lu Dayan, "Truly, he is a handsome young man! If he were to govern Heyang, he would be the master of ten thousand flowers. The customs here are not good. You should prevent him from going out and not let him get close to young men." Lu Dayan replied, "The jade flower is worn, but the king has nothing to bind it to." Geng asked, "What do you mean?" Lu Dayan responded, "In the past, when I hunted in the wild, I used a hawk and a dog, not missing any cunning rabbits, but I ignored a deer that was far away. Is it because I see the small and not the big? It is also because the spirit focuses on some things and not others. The king sees the prince without proper attire but does not lament his beauty, just as I see the rabbit and not the deer. Is there not much to lose? The hat strengthens the head, and the clothes distinguish the body. The hat is to ward off evil, and the cicada hat is to purify one's conduct. Wearing a leopard skin shows bravery, and wearing a sable fur coat shows virtue. If one is devoted to the path, they wear a jade ring; if one cultivates virtue, they wear a jade disc. A jade ring is for resolving doubts, and a jade disc is for untangling confusion. Seeing what one wears, one can know their abilities. Now the prince's clothes are flashy and different, which is called not being in harmony. Adorning his appearance is called ascending to danger. I fear that the corruption will be revealed inside the palace, not outside the palace gates." Geng became very angry and ordered the execution of Lu Dayan by beating him to death.

    155

    藩府多梨园子弟，皆极一时之选。有贴旦名珍儿者，尤姣媚。耿少子与结断袖之契。耿入觐，辄出宿其家。袁姬廉知其事，大恨曰：“儇薄子！敢如此妄作耶！”亟率侍女十数人，联灯列炬，潜出府后门，掩其不备。王子大惊，肘行以逆之，叩头求免，珍儿伏地战栗，不敢仰视。袁叱令举首，烛之美甚，遽慰之曰：“汝无恐，吾非噬人者。”竟与偕归，亦留其乱。是夜袁即脱阴而死。死后府中有鬼怪为厉，往往形现，俨然一白猴。耿闻之，泣曰：“吾固知其为巴山老猿所化也。”以珍儿殉之，怪乃绝。

    　　又耿每盛怒时，往往剥人皮，岁以十数。侍女玉笙者误碎玉斝，耿怒，命剥其皮。甫缚之，已惊仆而死。舁出，将瘗郊外，中路复苏。舁者匿为义女，嫁于庠生李某。李及第，授山东一县令，玉笙今为孺人矣。

    Explanation

    155

    The prince's mansion was filled with talented performers from the imperial music and dance troupe, all of whom were the finest of their time. Among them was a beautiful and charming actress named Zhen'er, who was particularly graceful. The young man named Geng formed a close relationship with her. Whenever Geng paid a formal visit to the emperor, he would secretly stay at her home. Lady Yuan, upon learning of the affair, was greatly angered and said, "You frivolous and fickle person! How dare you act so recklessly?" She quickly gathered a dozen or so maids, linked lamps and torches, and secretly exited through the back gate of the mansion, catching them unawares. The prince was greatly alarmed and crawled on his elbows to meet her, kneeling and begging for forgiveness. Zhen'er crouched on the ground trembling, too afraid to look up. Lady Yuan commanded her to lift her head, and the light revealed her beauty greatly. She quickly reassured her, saying, "Do not be afraid; I am not a harmful person." She eventually took her back with her and continued to keep her company. That night, Lady Yuan died due to a miscarriage. After her death, the mansion was haunted by ghosts and apparitions, which often appeared in the form of a white monkey. Geng, upon hearing this, wept and said, "I knew it was transformed by the old monkey of Ba Mountain." He sacrificed Zhen'er, and the ghostly disturbances ceased.

    Moreover, Geng often acted violently in his fits of rage, peeling off human skin several times a year. One day, a servant girl named Yusheng accidentally broke a jade wine vessel. Geng, enraged, ordered her to be bound. Just as she was being bound, she fell dead from shock. They carried her out to bury her in the outskirts, but she recovered on the way. The person carrying her hid her as a foster daughter and married her to a scholar named Li. Li passed the imperial examination and was appointed as the magistrate of a county in Shandong. Yusheng is now a lady-in-waiting.

    156

    又，王子喜为夜游。时有刘参将者，新任城守营，立法严肃。代鼛击柝，终宵戒严。适夜巡，王子微服过所欢，为刘所执，问何事夜行，叱令通名。王子不以实告，刘怒，即街头褫衣笞二十，血肉狼藉，卧月余始瘥。此事无知者，惟我等侍婢知之最审耳。

    　　耿平居喜食鸡翠，每下箸，非数百不餍。袁姬犹嗜榛栗及熊白，耿为百方致之。庖人胹之失饪，往往获死。侍女灵芝，忽被狐祟，喜近男子。耿怒，亟选藩下少年二十人，命次第裸呈以淫之。阅人已遍，而灵芝不惫。耿笑曰：“丘壑可盈，是不可厌也。”旋释之。

    　　又自言在府时，独耿妻钟爱至，共寝床。耿妻好佛，罕与耿相见，故得始终自保，不然，亦难免于服役诸婢，同罹祸患污辱矣。第于众目之前，白足听选，终觉抱惭于一生耳。女伴闻之，遂相传说。耿死，林携倩霞仍归福清，子孙繁盛，至今不绝。

    Explanation

    156

    The Prince enjoyed night wanderings. At that time, there was a military commander named Liu who had recently taken up his post at the city garrison and enforced strict laws. He would replace the large drum with a wooden block to signal the time, maintaining a state of vigilance throughout the night. One evening, while on patrol, the Prince, dressed in plain clothes, passed by a place of pleasure and was apprehended by Liu. Liu questioned him about his nighttime activities and demanded his name. The Prince did not reveal his true identity, which angered Liu. Liu then stripped him naked and beat him twenty times with a cane, leaving him in a state of disarray. The Prince lay ill for over a month before he recovered. This incident was known only to us, the serving maids, who understood it best.

    Geng, in his daily life, loved to eat chicken and peacock meat. He would not be satisfied until he had consumed hundreds of dishes. Lady Yuan also enjoyed hazelnuts and chestnuts, as well as bear fat. Geng went to great lengths to obtain these delicacies for her. The cook often failed to prepare them properly, leading to frequent mishaps. One of the serving girls, Lingzhi, suddenly became possessed by a fox spirit and developed an attraction to men. Geng became furious and quickly selected twenty young men from his entourage, ordering them to present themselves naked in turn for his pleasure. After exhausting all the young men, he found that Lingzhi still showed no signs of weariness. Geng laughed and said, "The hills and valleys can be filled, and this cannot be tiresome." He then released her.

    He also mentioned that while he was in the palace, only his wife favored him, and they shared the same bed. Geng's wife was devoted to Buddhism and rarely saw him, which allowed him to maintain his status and avoid the same fate as the other serving maids, who suffered the same misfortune and humiliation. However, standing barefoot and being chosen for such tasks always left him feeling ashamed throughout his life. The other women heard about this and spread the story. After Geng's death, Lin took Qianxia back to Fuqing, and their descendants thrived, continuing to this day.

    157

    兰岩曰：热闹场中，抽身远避，士君子之所难也。倩霞以一女，见逆藩凶暴，遂知祸不旋踵，劝林勇退，何其识之精，行之决哉！吁！巾帼也，胜大丈夫矣！

    　　落漈海水至彭湖，势渐低，近琉球，则谓之落漈.落漈者，水趋下而不回也。洋船至澎湖以下，遇飓风作，漂流漈中，回者百一。盖海水之中，又有急流以海水为崖岸焉，斯亦奇矣。予在鄞江时，闻闽人过台船，漂入落漈者，其迅如飞，瞬息不知行几千里，舟中数十人，咸以为断无生理，但相顾傍徨，任其漂泊颠沛。久之，忽闻大震一声，人人颠倒，船遂不动，众莫测其故，徐出视之，方知抵一荒一岛。船为漈水所推，直上沙岸，故搁不行。众告语欢呼。

    Explanation

    157

    Lan Yan said: In the midst of a bustling scene, to withdraw and avoid, is a challenge for a gentleman. Qian Xia, as a woman, witnessed the rebellion of a feudal lord's brutality and thus realized that disaster would not be far off. She advised Lin to retreat bravely, how precise her insight and resolute her action! Alas! She, a woman, surpasses great men!

    The sea water falls to Penghu, its momentum gradually decreases. Approaching Ryukyu, it is called the waterfall of the sea. The waterfall of the sea is when water rushes down and does not return. When foreign ships reach Penghu and below, they encounter a typhoon, drifting in the waterfall, only one in a hundred returns. In the sea water, there are rapid currents that act like cliffs, this is also strange. When I was in Yinzhou, I heard about Fujian people passing through Taiwan, those who drifted into the sea waterfall, it is swift like flying, in an instant, it is unknown how many thousand miles it has traveled. There are dozens of people on the boat, all thought there was no hope for survival, only looked at each other in bewilderment, letting themselves drift and suffer. After a long time, suddenly heard a loud tremor, everyone was thrown off balance, the ship stopped moving, everyone could not understand the reason, slowly went out to see, only then realized they had reached a deserted island. The ship was pushed by the waterfall water, straight onto the sandy shore, therefore, it stopped. The people told each other and cheered.

    158

    岸上砂石悉赤金，怪鸟颇伙，不一其形，见人亦不惊飞。饥则捕食之，有如鹅者，味独美。夜间绕船尽鬼，啾啾不绝，至晓乃殁。夜则复然。居半年，渐与鬼习，可通言语，鬼因言：“此间去中国数千里，往日陷于落漈，流尸至此，去家窎远，通梦无由。然久栖于此，颇谙海洋潮汐之理，大概阅三十年，落漈一年，今屈指计之，一两月后当平满矣。君等亟修补船只，可望生还也。”众感谢，或问：“所食似鹅之鸟，何鸟耶？”鬼曰：“此非鸟，亦鬼也，历年既久，精气耗散，故幻此形耳。”众为叹息，因各运斤操斧，连夜修葺废舟，工甫竣，落漈早平满，与海水无所分别。众欢声雷动，推船下水，治帆将发。鬼群哭而送之，竞取岸上金沙相赠，且嘱曰：“归去勿相忘！幸致声乡里，好作佛事，为我等荐拔。”众争许之。扬帆破浪，行一日夜，达闽之重门。众感鬼之情，伤其堕落，共出资建水陆，并访其家，赈恤之，分其所赠余金。诸客拥巨万，多为富商。

    Explanation

    158

    The shore was covered with red gold, and there were many strange birds, each with a different shape, who did not fly away when they saw people. When hungry, they would capture and eat them, and there were some that looked like geese, whose taste was uniquely delicious. At night, all around the ship were ghosts, chirping incessantly, until dawn when they would die. This happened again every night. After living there for half a year, they gradually became familiar with the ghosts and could communicate with them. The ghosts then said, "This place is thousands of miles away from China. In the past, we were trapped in a waterfall, and our bodies floated here, far from home, with no way to communicate through dreams. However, having lived here for a long time, we have become very familiar with the tides of the ocean. Generally, it takes about thirty years, and the waterfall occurs once a year. Now, counting on my fingers, it will be level and full in one or two months. You should quickly repair your boats, and you can hope to return." The group thanked them, and someone asked, "What kind of bird is the one that looks like a goose?" The ghost replied, "This is not a bird, it is also a ghost. After many years, its spirit and energy have dissipated, so it has taken this form." The group sighed and then each person took an axe and began to repair the damaged boat at night. As soon as the work was completed, the waterfall had already become level and full, indistinguishable from the sea. The group cheered loudly, pushed the boat into the water, and prepared the sails to set sail. The ghosts cried and bid farewell, competing to give them sand from the shore as a gift, and they said, "Do not forget us when you return! Please let your hometown know, and do good Buddhist deeds to help us be reborn." The group eagerly agreed. They sailed through the waves, traveling for a day and night, reaching the heavy gates of Fujian. The group was moved by the ghosts' feelings, felt sorry for their downfall, and together contributed money to build a water and land temple, visited their families, provided relief, and distributed the remaining gold they had been given. The guests each possessed millions, and many were wealthy merchants.

    159

    兰岩曰：赤金人所争爱，至戚良朋，为此结怨构讼者多矣。乃有地焉，金杂砂砾，在在所取，斯诚乐国，未有肯舍而之他者。乃群鬼痛哭求拔，直有不可一朝居之势。鬼何不恋此多金哉？亦以死可悲耳。世之拥多金而心死者，恬不为怪，然亦无甚趣味矣。不思避而恋之，佛氏有灵，恐不能为此种人荐拔也。

    Explanation

    159

    Lanyan said: Gold is fiercely contested and loved, even causing close relatives and good friends to harbor grudges and litigate over it. Yet, there is a place where gold is mixed with sand and gravel, and can be taken everywhere, truly a land of joy. There is no one willing to leave such a place. Then the ghosts cry out in pain and seek salvation, there is a situation that cannot be endured even for a day. Why don't the ghosts love this much gold? But because death is tragic. Those in the world who possess much gold but die in their hearts do not find it strange, but it is also not very interesting. They do not think of avoiding it but instead cling to it, if the Buddha has power, it is feared that they cannot save these people.

    160

    伊五兵丁伊五，身□□而貌么襏，贫不能自活。独走出城，将自缢林中，为一老人所见，问为何所苦，而轻生若此，伊以情告，老人嗤曰：“葛藟犹能庇其本根，况人耶？观子神气完兄，城府不密，载道之器也。予有书一册，授子习而精之，足够一生吃着。”言次出诸袖中，尽符录耳。抄写亦甚潦草，伊展阅，即反之曰：“此犹石田，无所用之。”老人曰：“何也？”伊曰：“予僦屋以居，卑庳近市，此符纵验，亦何从而习之乎？”老人曰：“此亦当虑，但子能从我，则无患矣。”伊曰：“求死之人，何所不可。”乃偕循一僻径，迤左行，有止水一湫，蒹葭聚翠，广袤数里，深邃处得一矮屋，虽茅茨不剪，颇虚敞精洁，遂止宿其中，从老人受学。一日两餐，必餍酒肉，七日而术成。老人与屋皆不见。伊知遇异人，欣然而返。

    Explanation

    160

    A Man Named Yi Wu

    Yi Wu, a soldier, was disheveled and unkempt, too poor to support himself. Alone, he walked out of the city, intending to hang himself in the forest. He was seen by an old man who asked what he was suffering from and why he was so willing to die. Yi Wu explained his situation, and the old man laughed and said, "Even a wild grapevine can protect its roots, let alone a person? I see that your spirit and appearance are complete and whole, and you have a broad mind, capable of carrying great knowledge. I have a book, which I will teach you to study and master. It will be enough for you to live on for life." As he spoke, the old man took the book out of his sleeve, and it was full of talismans and incantations. The writing was very sloppy. Yi Wu looked at it and immediately returned it, saying, "This is like barren land, useless." The old man asked, "Why?" Yi Wu replied, "I rent a house to live in, which is low and close to the market. Even if this talisman works, where would I practice it?" The old man said, "This is also something to consider, but if you can follow me, there will be no problem." Yi Wu said, "A person seeking death, what cannot be done?" So they followed a secluded path, moving leftward. There was a small pond with reeds growing around it, extending for several miles. In the deep part, there was a small cottage, though made of thatched roof, it was quite spacious, clean, and tidy. They stayed there and learned from the old man. They had two meals a day, always satisfying themselves with wine and meat. After seven days, the technique was mastered. The old man and the house disappeared. Yi Wu realized he had met a strange person and returned happily.

    161

    平日面朋酒友，怪其小康，群思咀嚼之，往往讽以谀辞。伊慨然敬诺，乃相与赴富春楼。同七八人，恣情饮啖，计所费八千四百文。众坐视其何以偿，蓦一黑面汉至席前，拱立曰：“主人知伊五爷在此款客，敬奉酒资，祈检致。”随解腰缠，置几上而去。数之，适八千四百文。众大骇，伊独不之怪，已而各醉饱，同步市中。见一人乘大白马，急驰而过。伊纵步追及之，捉衔大叱曰：“可即与我！”其人下马求免，形色仓皇。

    Explanation

    161

    In daily face-to-face gatherings with drinking buddies, they found it strange that he was in good health and often thought about savoring it, often flattering him with flattering words. He solemnly agreed and then went to the restaurant together. With seven or eight people, they indulged in drinking and eating, calculating the cost at eight thousand four hundred coins. Everyone was greatly shocked, but he alone was not surprised. Afterward, everyone was drunk and full, and they walked together in the market. They saw a man riding a white horse, galloping past quickly. He took a step and caught up with him, grabbed the reins and shouted loudly, "Give it to me immediately!" The man dismounted and begged for mercy, looking terrified.

    162

    伊怒曰：“不与我，我即用武矣！”其人不得已，探怀出一物奉伊，伊受而释之，其人怏怏仍驰去。众环问其故，并索观所得物，伊出示，但一小皮囊，淡藕色，形如半胀猪腹，不测何物。伊曰：“所谓储气囊，其中所贮，小鬼魂魄也。彼驰马者，系过往游神，往往偷攫人家小儿，倘不遇我，又死一小儿矣！会须与诸君往活之。”众固未信，莫不翕然从行。俄入一僻巷，向西一人家，寂阒闭门，中有哭者。伊取小囊，就门隙张之，出浓烟一缕，蛇游而入，随闻其家有人曰：“孩子苏矣！”旋止哭，欢声彻户外。伊急挥众而返。人由是神之。

    Explanation

    162

    伊怒曰：“不与我，我即用武矣！”其人不得已，探怀出一物奉伊，伊受而释之，其人怏怏仍驰去。众环问其故，并索观所得物，伊出示，但一小皮囊，淡藕色，形如半胀猪腹，不测何物。伊曰：“所谓储气囊，其中所贮，小鬼魂魄也。彼驰马者，系过往游神，往往偷攫人家小儿，倘不遇我，又死一小儿矣！会须与诸君往活之。”众固未信，莫不翕然从行。俄入一僻巷，向西一人家，寂阒闭门，中有哭者。伊取小囊，就门隙张之，出浓烟一缕，蛇游而入，随闻其家有人曰：“孩子苏矣！”旋止哭，欢声彻户外。伊急挥众而返。人由是神之。

    Translation:
    He became angry and said, "If you do not give it to me, I will use force!" The man, having no choice, reached into his bosom and took out an object to present to him. He accepted it and released it, and the man, discontented, rode away. The crowd surrounded him and asked for the reason, demanding to see the object he had obtained. He showed it to them, which was just a small leather bag, light lotus-colored, shaped like a half-inflated pig's belly, with an unknown substance inside. He said, "This is called an air storage bag, and what is stored inside are the spirits of small ghosts. That rider on horseback is a passing spirit, often stealing children from households. If I had not met him, another child would have died! We will need to go with you all to revive him." The crowd still did not believe him, but they all readily agreed to follow along. Soon they entered a secluded alley, leading to a house to the west, where the doors were quietly closed, and someone was crying inside. He took the small bag, opened it at the door crack, releasing a thick wisp of smoke that snaked its way inside. Then they heard someone in the house say, "The child is revived!" The crying stopped immediately, and joyful cries echoed outside. He quickly dismissed the crowd and returned. People thus began to revere him.

    163

    南城某贵公，有女为邪物所凭，闻伊有神术，厚礼招致。女在室，已知伊来，形色惨沮，望流而仿佯。伊入室，女屏息屋隅，提熨斗自卫。伊周视动止，出谓贵公曰：“小姐之病，器物之妖也，今夕当为公诛锄之。”贵公喜，凡有所需，莫不如命。夜漏下，伊启囊取一小铜剑，其锋畟畟，吐光如彗，仗之入室，贵公率家人院外伺之。寻闻室中叱咤扑击之声，与物之腾掷声，女之诟詈声，喧哗庞杂。良久寂然。但闻女叩头有声，切切哀恳，语悲苦哽咽，不甚了了。寻闻伊呼烛甚急，婢妪争相执炬，一涌而入。伊已收剑入囊，女伏床下不动。伊指地一物示贵公曰：“此即为祟者，今见擒矣。”视之，则一藤夹脉也，聚薪焚之，精血流溢，气味如烧肉，逾时始尽。伊复书符，令女吞之，病遂若失。贵公甚德伊，赠赉极厚，伊以其资购室娶妇，俨然素封矣。

    Explanation

    163

    A nobleman from Nancheng had a daughter who was possessed by a malevolent spirit. Hearing that the man had supernatural powers, he invited him with great respect and gifts. The girl, already aware of his arrival, appeared sad and depressed, looking aimlessly around the room. When the man entered, the girl held her breath and hid in a corner, holding an iron as a weapon to protect herself. The man observed every movement and then told the nobleman, "Your daughter's illness is caused by a spirit attached to an object, and tonight I will eliminate it for you." The nobleman was pleased and granted him whatever he needed. As the night deepened, the man opened his bag and took out a small copper sword, its sharp edge emitting light like a comet. He carried it into the room, and the nobleman, along with his family, waited outside. Soon, they heard sounds of shouting, striking, and the object being thrown, mixed with the girl's curses, creating a noisy and chaotic scene. After a long while, everything became silent. They could only hear the sound of the girl kneeling and bowing her head, making a heartfelt plea in a voice choked with sobs, though her words were not very clear. Soon, the man called for a candle with urgency, and the maids and servants rushed in with torches. The man had already put the sword back in its sheath, and the girl lay motionless under the bed. He pointed to an object on the ground and said to the nobleman, "This is the spirit that was causing the trouble, and it has now been captured." Looking at it, they saw it was a piece of vine with a pulse-like pattern. They gathered firewood and burned it, and as it burned, blood flowed out, smelling like burning meat. It took some time to finish. The man then wrote a talisman and made the girl swallow it, and her illness was completely gone. The nobleman greatly appreciated the man and gave him very generous gifts. With the money he received, the man bought a house and married a wife, becoming a wealthy person.

    164

    兰岩曰：求死幸免，反得异术，伊诚有夙契耳。不然，彼老人日游天壤，一遇困穷，辄为援引，吾恐老人不能周遍也。

    　　段　公　子平阳，陶唐氏之故都也。其俗勤俭，多窑居，富室尤盛。新安赵给谏吉士《竹枝词》云：“三月山田长麦苗，村庄生计日萧条。羡他豪富城中客，住得砖窑胜土窑。”盖纪实也。

    　　镇署三堂后，有窑五圈，窑上覆楼五楹，绕以女墙，旧为狐所凭据。乾隆初，总戎段公出巡所汛未归。公子方弱冠，夏月偕一童，宿花厅之西轩。二更后，月明如昼，砌虫唧唧，夜气清凉。闻院内履声藉藉，公子白身起，穴窗外窥。隐隐见一少男一幼女，对坐花台畔，丰姿都美，同看明月。少间，女子曰：“讵意今宵，月色清皎乃尔。

    Explanation

    164

    Lanyan said: Seeking death but being fortunate to escape, instead gaining a strange skill, he truly had a predestined connection. Otherwise, that old man travels daily across the universe, and whenever he encounters someone in poverty, he always helps them, I fear the old man cannot reach everyone.

    Duan Gongzi Pingyang, the ancient capital of the Taotang clan. The local customs are frugal and thrifty, with many people living in kilns, and wealthy families are especially numerous. Xin'an Zhao Jiguan Jishi wrote in his Zhu Zhi Ci (Bamboo Branch Poem): "In March, the mountain fields grow wheat sprouts, and the village economy grows increasingly bleak. Envy those wealthy city dwellers, who live in brick kilns better than earthen ones." This is a factual account.

    Behind the town hall, there are five circles of kilns, with five buildings covering the kilns, surrounded by a parapet wall. It was once inhabited by foxes. In the early years of Qianlong, General Duan went out on patrol and did not return. The young master was just coming of age, and in the summer, he stayed in the west pavilion of the flower hall with a servant. After the second watch, the moon was as bright as daylight, and the insects on the steps chirped, the night air was cool. Hearing footsteps inside the courtyard, the young master got up and peeped through a hole in the window. He vaguely saw a young man and a young girl sitting together on a flower terrace, both of them beautiful in appearance and demeanor, watching the moon together. A little later, the girl said, "Who would have thought that tonight, the moon is so clear and bright."

    165

    三哥尚忆去岁中元，在姑射山石室中，与无一师，饮般若汤，食穿篱菜，唱和《柳梢青》，言笑晏晏时乎？”男子曰：“瞬息事，那得更忘！第彼时，我甚不欢畅，颇厌髡奴醉后，斥　笑鹏，而妹亦饮酒过多，可南可北，我在旁大有为妹悲歧路之意。昨过李氏新阡，墓已宿草，我尚涕泗，而妹竟处之淡漠然焉。今夕又将别有所图，是歧路之中，又有歧焉。究不足为宗族效法。”女曰：“少年不努力，老大徒伤悲。人生世间，如轻尘栖　草耳。妹虽形秽，宁不自爱？岂因李生之亡，遽甘心茕独乎？且妹之所以报李生者，亦至矣。初至其家，家无儋石之储。

    Explanation

    165

    Brother Three still remembers last year's Midsummer Festival, when he was in the stone room on Mount Cangyi, drinking banruo tea, eating vegetables that grew through the fence, singing and composing Liushaoqing poems, and speaking and laughing pleasantly. The man said, "Such a short time, how could it be forgotten! But at that time, I was not very happy. I was quite annoyed by the drunken monk's behavior, scornfully laughing at Peng, and my sister also drank too much, able to go north or south, and I had a great deal of sorrow for my sister's crossroads. Yesterday, I passed by the new grave of the Li family, and the tomb was already overgrown with grass, and I was still shedding tears, but my sister treated it indifferently. Tonight, she will again have other plans, which is another crossroad in her life. Ultimately, it is not worthy of being an example for the clan." The woman said, "If a young man does not work hard, he will only regret it when he is old. Life in this world is like a light dust settling on grass. Although I am unattractive, do I not take care of myself? Would I suddenly become content with loneliness because of Li's death? Moreover, the way I repay Li is also sufficient. When I first arrived at his home, there was not even enough food to last a day."

    166

    釜无水，焉得生鱼？并无甑，何得生尘？李生方卧，牛衣中，呼癸呼庚，褴褛不让行乞，妹即为新庐舍，给饔飧，制衣履，二年之内，百废俱兴。人谓茑萝不能独生，必托乔木。李则乔木而附茑萝矣。设当时妹即两袒，亦何负于李生；况今冢骨已枯乎？再李生才如袜线，百不逮人。面朋面友，萋菲时作。轻鸡爱雉，每每唐突西施。始犹娟秀，半年后貌渐寝，将就木，面目愈支离。妹不自解，曩日何故煞有痴情。伊思啜鱼婢羹，犹汲汲为之烹饪。

    166

    Without water in the pot, how can live fish be obtained? Without food in the steamer, how can dust be produced? Li lay in tattered clothing, calling out numbers in a game, his condition no better than that of a beggar. His sister provided him with a new home, food, and clothing, and within two years, all neglected things were revived. People say that morning glory plants cannot grow independently and must rely on tall trees. Li, however, is like a tall tree supporting such plants. If his sister had been indifferent then, what would it have mattered to Li; especially now that his bones are already cold? Moreover, Li's talent is as thin as a thread, far inferior to others. He has superficial friendships that are often insincere. He undervalues ordinary things but overvalues rare ones, often acting inappropriately towards someone beautiful. At first, he was still handsome, but after half a year, his appearance began to deteriorate, and as he neared death, his face became more disfigured. His sister couldn't understand, why had she been so deeply in love with him in the past? She thought of preparing a simple meal for him, yet eagerly continued to cook for him.

    167

    三哥岂不知之？”男子曰：“我亦聊言之耳，乌能使妹必听？但虑夙冤累积，获罪于天。城门失火，殃及池鱼也。兄妹肺腑，岂忍漠置，不一规诫乎？劝妹亟归，勿复干犯恶客。纵使见爱，亦不足为宗族光也。”女子不悦，曰：“见爱虽不足光五宗，见恶谅不致夷三族。三哥幸勿预妹事，即有祸，必不相嫁也！”男亦艴然拂袖而起，行至院门，复回首向女曰：“望尊重，颠踬勿悔！”女他顾不答。男子去。女自哂还自诵曰：“何时作如许态！岂遗却兔园册子耶？自且有欲炙之色，乃欲人见熊蹯而勿　。然则前日鹿腊，何伏案自决，不以一胾让人耶？”寻亦不复言，缓缓入花荫，绕过亭后，寂不复见。

    Explanation

    167

    "Doesn't Brother Three know this?" the man said. "I am just saying this casually; how can I make sure your sister will definitely listen? I am only concerned about the accumulated long-standing grudges that might bring divine punishment. If the city gate catches fire, the fish in the pond will suffer too. As siblings, how can we ignore each other's well-being and not give her some guidance? I advise your sister to return home quickly and avoid further contact with bad company. Even if she is loved, it is not enough to bring glory to our five clans." The woman was displeased and said, "Even if she is loved, it is not enough to bring glory to our five clans, and if she is hated, it probably won't lead to the destruction of three clans. Brother Three, please do not interfere with your sister's affairs. If there is any trouble, I will not marry her!" The man also rose angrily, brushed his sleeves, and left, walking to the courtyard gate. He turned back to speak to the woman, saying, "Respect yourself, and do not regret your mistakes!" The woman looked away without answering. The man left. The woman smiled to herself and recited internally, "When did I become like this? Could I have forgotten the Tuyuan Cezhi (a book of moral teachings)? I already have a look of intense desire, yet I want others to not indulge in excessive pleasures. Then again, in the past, when I faced the temptation of deer meat, why did I make a decision by myself without sharing a large piece of meat with others?" She then remained silent, slowly entering the shade of the flowers, circling around the pavilion, and disappearing into the quietness behind it.

    168

    公子心知为狐，而心艳其美，又怜其慧黠，就枕不能成寐。良久，忽闻叩户声，诘之，曰：“开门自分晓，底用多问。”音呖呖如莺簧，知为女至，大喜，即启户纳之。异香满室。谛视之，美丽绝伦，真天人也。相与把握甚昵。公子虑僮觉寤，女至榻前，以袖拂僮面者三，却回曰：“无妨矣。”公子叩其所自来，女自言萧姓，与公子夙有缘，故来相就。公子神已迷，意已夺，不暇致详，遂与绸缪，相得无间。黎明始去。自此无夕不至。女好饮善谈，称神语怪，言多不经。而枕席之间，狂荡无节。半月后，公子精神恍惚，食减骨柴。夫人颇怪之，而密询，不得其实。严究书僮，僮曰：“未见他异，惟半月前，睡即梦魔，手足尽痿，不能转侧。至今无夜不然，鸡鸣方醒。”夫人大疑，不复使公子宿轩中，命从己宿。是夜三更，夫人与诸婢，亦皆梦魇，大惧，而无如之何，惟与诸婢媪轮环斗叶子，坐守达旦。

    Explanation

    168

    The Young Nobleman knew in his heart that the visitor was a fox spirit, yet he admired its beauty and pitied its cleverness. Lying down to sleep, he could not fall asleep. After a long time, he suddenly heard knocking on the door. He questioned the visitor, who replied, "I opened the door myself; why ask so many questions?" Her voice was clear and melodious, like the sound of a skylark's flute. Knowing it was a woman, he was greatly pleased and immediately opened the door to welcome her in. The room was filled with a strange fragrance. He looked at her closely and found her beautiful beyond compare, truly a celestial being. They held hands very closely. Worried that the servant would wake up, the woman went to the bed, wiping the servant's face three times with her sleeve, then turned back and said, "It's fine." The young nobleman asked where she came from, and she introduced herself as surnamed Xiao, having had a long-standing connection with him, and therefore came to meet him. His spirit was already bewitched, his will already taken, and he had no time to inquire further. They became intimate, getting along perfectly. She left at dawn. From then on, she came every night. She loved to drink and talk, often talking about gods and strange things, and her words were often unbelievable. In bed, they were wild and unrestrained. After half a month, the young nobleman's spirit was confused, and he ate less, becoming emaciated. The lady-in-waiting found it very strange and secretly questioned him, but could not find out the truth. She strictly questioned the servant, who said, "No other strange things, only half a month ago, he went straight into nightmares, his hands and feet became paralyzed, and he could not turn over. Since then every night the same, woke up only when the rooster crowed."
