The lady-in-waiting was greatly suspicious and no longer allowed the young master to sleep in the pavilion, ordering him to sleep with her. That night at midnight, the lady-in-waiting and the maids also had nightmares, were greatly afraid, but could do nothing, only played leaf card games with the maids and nurses in shifts, sitting up until dawn.

    169

    无何段公归，夫人告之以故。公曰：“无哗，今夜令儿从我宿。”因与宿斋中。公劳顿，着枕辄酣寝。公子对榻卧，瞬息万虑不安，俄闻院中人语，曰：“妹莫孟浪，今夕断不可往。”又闻女应曰：“前已有言，勿复尔尔！”公子辩其为女子声音，急起拥衾坐。女弹指窗棂曰：“何不开门？”公子潜伏窗下，低嘱曰：“今夜家大人宿此，且迴避，他日再谋会。”女笑曰：“今夜携得妙药来，何反自参商？且尊大人焉得预儿媳事？”公子嬖惑已久，无复踟蹰，亟启扉。段公已寤，隔帷视之，知为狐媚，乃伪寐以俟。随闻女子曰：“大人果在此宿乎？”公子令噤声，女子嗤嗤笑，徐至榻前，徐搴帷向公，将以袖拂公面，公骤起捉之。女大惊，摆扑欲遁。公于枕畔抽剑，急刺之，迎刃而解，化一黑狐，死床下。衣在公手如蜕。然移烛看剑，血不濡缕，诚宝剑也。

    Explanation

    169

    Shortly after Duan Gong returned home, his wife informed him of the matter. Duan Gong said, "Be quiet. Tonight, I will have my son stay with me." So they stayed in the study. Duan Gong was very tired and fell asleep as soon as he lay down. The young master lay on the opposite bed, but every thought was restless in an instant. Soon, he heard voices in the courtyard. One woman said, "Sister, do not act rashly, tonight it is absolutely not possible to go." Another woman replied, "I have already said so, do not repeat yourself." The young master recognized the voice as that of a woman and quickly got up and sat up in bed. The woman tapped on the window and said, "Why don't you open the door?" The young master crouched under the window and whispered, "Tonight, my father is staying here, please avoid it, we can meet again another day." Duan Gong had already woken up, looked through the curtain, and knew it was a fox's trick, so he pretended to sleep and waited. She slowly approached the bed and slowly pulled the curtain towards Duan Gong, intending to brush against his face with her sleeve. Duan Gong suddenly got up and caught her. The woman was greatly surprised, struggled, and tried to escape. Duan Gong drew a sword from beside his pillow, quickly stabbed her, and she was cut in two, transforming into a black fox that died under the bed. The clothes were in Duan Gong's hand, as if they had shed off. But when he moved the candle to look at the sword, there was no blood on it, truly a precious sword.

    170

    公子啜泣跪床下，请其尸瘗之。公笑曰：“痴孩儿！见其异物犹恋恋耶？”怜其情切，即以尸与之，公子为其具棺衾，葬于后圃。次夜，闻园中哭者甚众，移时始寂，旋失尸之所在。署中狐祟遂绝。公子后出仕为司马，为他事正法，段公亦恚忿而死，人多以为杀狐之报云。

    Explanation

    170

    The young man wept and knelt at the foot of the bed, asking to bury its body. The father laughed and said, "Foolish child! Still clinging to an odd thing?" Moved by his deep affection, he gave the body to him. The young man prepared a coffin and shroud, and buried it in the rear garden. The next night, he heard many weeping in the garden, which ceased after some time, but soon the location of the body was lost. The fox spirits in the office ceased. Later, the young man served as a Military Governor and was executed for other matters. The official named Duan also died in anger and resentment. Many people believed it was retribution for killing the fox.

    171

    兰岩曰：谏而不听，致罹败亡，狐亦愚矣。情之所钟，死不足惜，狐又足嘉矣。然观其于李氏子，淡焉漠焉，则狐非情种，直淫物也，死不足惜矣。

    　　戆子谢梅庄济世在翰林时，佣三仆，一黠，一朴，一戆。会同馆诸公，就谢为茱萸会，把菊持螯，主宾尽乐，酒酣，一客曰：“吾辈兴阑矣，安得歌者侑一觞乎？”黠者应声曰：“有。”既又虑戆者作梗，乃白主人有他事，遣之以出，令朴者司阍，而自往召之。未至，戆者已归，见二人抱琵琶，率四五姣童在门。诧曰：“胡为乎来？”黠者曰：“奉主命。”戆者瞋目厉声曰：“自我门下十余年，未尝见此辈出入，必醉命也！”挥拳逐去。客哄然散，谢深衔之。一夕，燃烛酌酒校书，天寒，瓶已罄，颜未酡，黠者　朴者再沽，遭戆者于道，夺瓶还。谏曰：“今日二瓶，明日三瓶，有益无损也。多沽伤费，多饮伤身，有损无益也。”谢强颔之。

    Explanation

    171

    Lan Yan said: Advising without being heeded, leading to disaster and ruin, the fox is also foolish. Where passion is deeply felt, death is not to be regretted; the fox is also worthy of praise. However, observing its attitude toward Li's son, it is indifferent and cold, so the fox is not a creature of passion, but merely a lustful beast, and its death is not to be regretted.

    The simple-minded Xie Meizhuang Jishi was in the Hanlin Academy when he employed three servants: one cunning, one honest, and one simple-minded. The officials of the Tonghui Pavilion gathered at Xie's place for a chrysanthemum feast, holding chrysanthemums and crabs, and the host and guests were all enjoying themselves. When the wine was flowing freely, one guest said, "Our spirits are waning, how can we get a singer to entertain us with a cup of wine?" The cunning one responded immediately, "There is one." But then he worried that the simple-minded one might interfere, so he told the master that he had other matters, and sent him out. He ordered the honest one to guard the door, and went himself to fetch the singer. Before he arrived, the simple-minded one had already returned. He saw two people holding a pipa, with several beautiful children at the door. He asked, "Why have you come?" The cunning one replied, "I am following the master's command." The simple-minded one glared and shouted, "I have been in your service for over a decade, and never seen such people come in and out, it must be because you are drunk!" He hit them and drove them away. The guests dispersed in a commotion, and Xie deeply resented him. One evening, he lit candles, poured wine, and proofread books. It was cold, the bottles were empty, but his face was not flushed. The cunning one and the honest one went out again to buy more wine. On the way, they met the simple-minded one, who took the bottles back. He advised, "Today two bottles, tomorrow three bottles, there is benefit but no harm. Buying too much is wasteful, drinking too much is harmful, there is harm but no benefit." Xie nodded reluctantly.

    172

    既而改御史，早朝，书童掌灯，倾油污朝衣。黠者顿足曰：“不吉。”谢因而怒，命朴者行杖。戆者止之，复谏曰：“仆尝闻主言，古人有羹污衣，烛燃须，而不动声色者，主第能言而不能行乎？”谢迁怒曰：“尔欲沽直耶？市恩耶？”曰：“皆非敢然也。恩出自主，仆何有焉？仆效愚忠，而主曰沽直。主今居言路，异日跪御榻与天子争是非，坐朝班与大臣争献替，弃印绶其若屣，甘迁谪以如归，主亦沽直而为之乎？人亦谓主沽直而为之乎？”谢语塞，谢之，而阴愈衔之。由是黠者乘隙，日夜伺其短，谤之。朴者共媒蘖，劝主人逐之。会谢有罪下狱，不果。

    Explanation

    172

    Afterward, Xie was appointed as an imperial censor. On the morning of an early court session, the servant boy holding the lamp spilled oil, soiling the court robes. The clever one stomped his foot and said, "It's inauspicious." Xie became angry because of this and ordered the straightforward one to carry out the punishment. The blunt one stopped him and then advised, saying, "I have often heard the master say that ancient people, despite spilling soup on their clothes or burning their beards with candles, remained calm. Is it that the master can only talk but not act?" Xie, angered by the question, retorted, "Are you trying to buy righteousness? Or are you trying to sell favor?" The advisor replied, "I am not daring to do either of those things. The favor comes from the master; what do I have to do with it? I am trying to show my simple loyalty, but the master calls it buying righteousness. Now that the master is in a position to speak freely, in the future you will kneel before the emperor and argue right and wrong, sit in the court and argue with ministers, throw away your seals as if they were sandals, and willingly accept demotion as if returning home. Is the master also buying righteousness to do this? Will people also say the master is buying righteousness to do this?" Xie was speechless, thanked him, but secretly harbored resentment. As a result, the clever one took advantage of the opportunity, day and night waiting for his faults, and slandered him. The straightforward ones joined in spreading rumors and advised the master to expel him. Coincidentally, Xie was imprisoned for a crime, and the plan did not succeed.

    173

    未几，奉命戍边。出狱治装，黠者逋矣。朴者亦力求他去。戆者攘臂而前曰：“此吾主报国之时，即吾侪报主之时也。仆愿往。”市马造车，制穹庐，备粮糗以从。谢乃喟然叹曰：“吾向以为黠者有用，朴者可用也。今而知黠者有用而不可用，而戆者可用也；朴者可用而实无用，而戆者有用也。”遂养以为子，名戆子焉。

    　　至军营，居未久，而资斧告匮，鬻及裘、马。久之，渐不可支。戆子日荷火枪，出十余里外，猎取麋鹿獾兔，以谢谋餐。一日，逐一鹿于乱草中，蹶而仆，足陷入地中尺余，出足视，沙中白金灿然，数之，得二十巨铤，适千金。取之以归，谢以咨白将军。将军闻而异之，询其故，得知戆子所为，拊髀曰：“沙漠乌得有藏金，盖天所以旌义仆也！”仍以金归谢，召戆子，奖以衣、裘、羊、马、金十两。自是塞外王侯，皆加殊礼。及赦归，谢官湖湘。戆子劝其勇退，谢致仕颐养林泉。戆子寿至九十，无疾而终，感以为忠义之报云。

    Explanation

    173

    Soon after, he was ordered to garrison the frontier. Upon leaving prison and preparing for departure, the cunning ones fled, and the simple ones also tried hard to leave. The straightforward one stepped forward and said, "This is the time for my master to serve the country, and it is also the time for us to serve our master. I am willing to go." He bought horses, made carriages, built a tent, and prepared food and provisions to follow. The master sighed deeply and said, "I previously thought that the cunning ones were useful and the simple ones were usable. Now I know that the cunning ones are useful but not usable, while the straightforward ones are usable. The simple ones are usable but actually useless, while the straightforward ones are useful." He then adopted him as a son and named him Gāngzi.

    Upon reaching the military camp, they did not stay long before their funds ran out, and they even sold their furs and horses. After some time, they could barely sustain themselves. Gāngzi carried a fire gun every day, went out more than ten miles, and hunted deer, badgers, and rabbits to provide meals for the master. One day, while chasing a deer through the grass, he stumbled and fell, and his foot sank into the ground by more than a foot. When he pulled out his foot and looked, he saw shining white gold in the sand. Counting it, he found twenty large bars, which totaled a thousand gold coins. Taking it back, the master consulted General Bái. General Bái was surprised to hear about it, asked about the reason, and learned that it was Gāngzi's doing. P

    174

    兰岩曰：直言不避，始终如一，此其所以卒享寿考也。彼奔走逢迎，不顾名义，一旦失势，即引避而唯恐祸及，诚小人作用耳。宁独仆人也乎哉？李伯瑟曰：“古今来，此三种人尽之，却被一枝笔描写无遗，朴者犹可恕，黠者直可诛，而戆者不朽矣。

    　　某　马　甲马甲某乙，居安定门外营房中，贫甚，差役多误。其佐领遣领催某甲往传语：“亟出应役，不则必斥革矣。”甲素与乙相善，即往见之，入门，马矢满地，破壁通邻。屋三间，稭隔一间为卧室，妻避其中。时际秋寒，乙着白布单衫，白足趿决踵鞋，甲一见，恻然曰：“弟一寒如此哉！”因致佐领语，且曰：“料弟贫苦，我归见牛录章京（即佐领），当为缓颊。但日云暮矣，不克入城，舍此无信宿处。”解衣付之曰：“弟应久不举火，讵可以口腹相累？此衣可质钱四五千，姑将去，市肉沽酒，来消此寒夜，余者留为数日薪水费，幸勿外也！”乙赧然抱衣去。

    Explanation

    174

    Lan Yan said: "Speaking frankly without avoiding, being consistent throughout, this is why he ultimately enjoys a long life. Those who rush around to curry favor, disregarding principles and reputation, once they lose power, immediately retreat in fear of being implicated in disaster—this is truly the behavior of petty people. Is this limited to servants alone? Li Boce said: 'From ancient times to the present, these three types of people encompass all, yet they are all depicted without omission by a single brush. The simple can still be forgiven, the cunning are deserving of punishment, and the stubborn will endure forever.'

    A certain man named Jia, living in the military barracks outside the An Ding Gate, was very poor and often made mistakes in his duties. His supervisor sent a lower-ranking official named Jia to deliver a message: 'Hurry up and come to serve, or else you will surely be dismissed.' Jia had always been friendly with Yi, so he went to see him. Upon entering, he saw horse droppings all over the ground, with broken walls connecting to the neighbor's house. There were three rooms, with straw dividing one room into a bedroom, and Yi's wife hiding inside. It was already autumn, and Yi was wearing a white cotton shirt, barefoot in shoes with loose soles. Jia, seeing this, felt moved and said, "Brother, you are so cold!" He then relayed the supervisor's message and added, "I know you are poor. I will go back and speak on your behalf to the supervisor. But it is already evening, and I cannot enter the city. Besides, there is no place to stay for two nights." He took off his clothes and gave them to Yi, saying, "You must have been without food for a long time. How could I burden you with my own needs? This coat can be pawned for four or five thousand coins. Please take it for now, buy meat and wine, and spend this cold night. The rest can be kept for several days' expenses. Please do not waste it!" Yi, blushing, took the clothes and left.

    175

    营房去市远，曛暮未归。甲独坐炕头，寂无聊赖，检得鼓词一本，就灯下观之。有顷，闻房中哀泣声，知为乙妻苦贫。窃为感叹间，蓦见一屈背妇人，蹒跚入室，至佛案前，塞一物于香炉脚下，仍出户出，面目丑恶，酷类僵尸。甲觉其异，起视炉脚下，所塞物，则纸钱十余枚。深怪之，不禁毛戴，付诸丙丁。

    Explanation

    175

    The military barracks were far from the market, and as dusk fell, he had not yet returned. Sitting alone on the kang, person A felt lonely and aimless, picking up a book of drum poetry to read by the light of the lamp. After a while, he heard weeping sounds from the room, realizing that it was the wife of person B, suffering from poverty. As he privately sighed, he suddenly saw a hunched-back woman limping into the room. She reached the Buddhist altar, stuffed something under the foot of the incense burner, and then went out through the door. Her face was ugly, resembling a zombie. Person A found this strange, got up to look at the foot of the incense burner, and saw that the object she had stuffed was more than ten pieces of paper money. Deeply finding it strange, he could not help but have his hair stand on end and burned it.

    176

    房中泣声渐粗，倍觉惨切。潜于帘隙窥之，乙妻已作缳于梁间，将自缢。甲大惊，不复避嫌，急入救之，慰解再四。乙妻含悲致谢。出坐明间，如芒在背，前所见妇人又来，觅炉下纸钱不得，惶遽之状可厌。甲叱之，惊走暗处，遂不复见。索之不得，骇问乙妻见否，乙妻曰：“彼靡夜不来，来则我辄心伤，不克自禁，转念不如一死为快。初不识其为何如人也。”甲颔之曰：“冥念致邪，苟能安命无他想，则此物奚其致哉！此后尚须慎之。”

    　　既而乙归，甲备述其事，因劝曰：“时衰鬼弄人，此处不可复居。予城中有屋楼椽，携弟妇姑就居之，否则恐致殃也。”夫妻感其谊，乃移入城，后得无事。甲白诸官，闻而异之，因亦怜而宥之矣。

    Explanation

    176

    In the room, the sound of weeping grew louder, evoking even more acute sorrow. Peeping through the curtain gap, Jia saw Yi's wife had already set up a noose on the beam, intending to hang herself. Jia was greatly alarmed, no longer concerned with propriety, and rushed in to save her, comforting and reassuring her repeatedly. Yi's wife expressed her gratitude with tears. Upon exiting and sitting in the main room, Jia felt as if thorns were on his back. The woman he had seen before came again, unable to find the paper money under the stove, displaying an anxious and panicked state that was repulsive. Jia scolded her, and she fled to a dark place in fright, disappearing thereafter. Unable to find her, Jia was startled and asked Yi's wife if she had seen her. Yi's wife said, "She comes every night. When she comes, I always feel heartbroken, unable to control myself, and thought that it would be better to die. Initially, I did not know who she was." Jia nodded and said, "Evil thoughts lead to evil. If one can accept one's fate and have no other thoughts, how could this thing come about? You must still be cautious afterward."

    Afterwards, Yi returned, and Jia fully recounted the events, then advised, "When times are bad, ghosts play with people. This place cannot be lived in anymore. I have a house with a tower and rafters in the city. Take your sister-in-law and mother-in-law to live there, otherwise, there might be disaster." The couple felt his kindness and moved into the city. Later, there were no problems. Jia reported to the officials, who heard it and found it strange, thus also pitied and forgave him.

    177

    兰岩曰：贫苦致此，殊为可怜，乃鬼复乘此而谋替代，宁冥冥中一任鬼魅作祟耶？救其死而居以安宅，所谓良朋者，甲岂少愧哉！

    　　米　芗　老康熙间，总兵王辅臣叛乱，所过掳掠，得妇女，不问其年之老少、貌之妍丑，悉贮布囊中，四金一囊，听人收买。三原民米芗老，年二十，未娶，独以银五两诣营，以一两赂主者，冀获佳丽。主者导入营，令其自择。米逐囊揣摩，检得腰细足纤者一囊，负之以行。至逆旅起视，则闯然一老妪也，满面瘢耆，年近七旬。米悔恨无及，默坐床上，面如死灰。无何，一斑白叟，控黑卫载一好女子来投宿，扶女下，系卫于槽，即米之西室委装焉。相与拱揖，各叩里居姓字。叟自述：“刘姓，蛤蟆洼人，年六十七，昨以银四两，自营中买得一囊人，不意齿太稚，幸好颜色，归而著以纸阁芦帘，亦足以娱老矣。”米闻之，心热如火，惋惜良深。刘意得甚，拉米过市饮酒，米念借他人酒杯，浇自己块垒，计亦得，乃从之去。

    Explanation

    177

    Lan Yan said: To be reduced to such poverty is truly pitiable. Yet, ghosts take advantage of this situation to plot a replacement. Is it right to allow ghosts and evil spirits to wreak havoc in the darkness? To save him from death and provide him with a safe home, what a good friend is supposed to do, how could A not feel ashamed?

    During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, General Wang Fuchen rebelled, and wherever he went, he plundered and captured women. He did not care about their age, whether young or old, or their appearance, whether beautiful or ugly. He stored them all in cloth bags, charging four taels of silver per bag, allowing people to buy them. Mi Xianglao, a twenty-year-old unmarried man from San Yuan, went to the camp alone with five taels of silver, offering one tael as a bribe to the overseer, hoping to acquire a beautiful woman. The overseer led him into the camp and allowed him to choose for himself. Mi examined each bag, feeling for the one with a slim waist and slender feet, and chose one. He carried it away. When he arrived at an inn and opened the bag, he was met with a sudden old woman, her face covered in scars, nearly seventy years old. Mi was filled with regret and sat silently on the bed, his face ashen. Soon after, an elderly man with white hair arrived, riding a black horse and bringing a beautiful woman to stay the night. He helped the woman down, tied the horse to a manger, and left her luggage in the west room. They greeted each other formally, exchanging their hometowns and names. The elderly man introduced himself: "I am Liu, from Hama Valley, sixty-seven years old. Yesterday, I bought a woman from the camp for four taels of silver. I did not expect her to be so young, but she has a good appearance. I will set up a simple shelter for her, which should be enough to entertain me in my old age." Mi heard this and his heart burned like fire, deeply regretting his own misfortune. Liu was very pleased and invited Mi to the market for a drink. Mi thought, "Using someone else's wine cup to pour out my own troubles," and found the plan good, so he went with him.

    178

    妪俟其去远，蹀躞至西舍，启帘入，女子方掩面泣，见妪乃起裣衽，秋波凝泪，态如雨浸桃花。妪诘其由，女曰：“奴平凉人，姓葛氏，年十七矣。父母兄弟，皆被贼杀，奴独被掠，逼欲淫污，奴哭骂，群贼怒，故以奴鬻之老翁，细思不如死休，是以悲耳。”妪叹曰：“是真造化小儿，颠倒众生，不可思议矣。老身老而不死，遭此乱离，且无端窘一少年，心亦何忍。适见尔家老翁，龙钟之态，正与老身年相当。况老夫女妻，未必便利。彼二人一喜一闷，不醉无归。我二人盍李代桃僵，易地而寝，待明日五更，尔与我家少年郎早起速行，拼我老骨头，与老翁同就于木，勿悲也。”女踟蹰不遽从，妪正色曰：“此所谓交易而退，各得其所，一举两得之策也，可速去，迟则事谐矣！”解衣相易。女拜谢，妪导入米房，以被覆之，嘱勿言，乃自归西室，蒙首而卧。二更后，叟与米皆醉归，奔走劳苦，亦各就枕。

    178

    The old woman waited until he was far away, then paced to the western house, opened the curtain, and entered. The young woman was covering her face and weeping; upon seeing the old woman, she rose and performed a respectful gesture, her eyes filled with tears, her appearance as beautiful and sorrowful as a peach blossom drenched in rain. The old woman asked her the reason, and the young woman replied, "I am from Pingliang, surnamed Ge, and am seventeen years old. My parents and brothers were all killed by bandits, and I alone was captured, forced into immoral acts. I wept and cursed, which angered the bandits, so they sold me to an old man. Upon reflection, I would rather die than endure this, hence my sorrow." The old woman sighed, "This is indeed the whims of fate, confusing and bewildering people, beyond comprehension. I, though old but still alive, have suffered through this turmoil and separation, and have unjustly put a young person in a difficult situation. How can my heart bear it? Just now, I saw your old man, frail and worn, his state matching my own age. Moreover, he is not well-suited to his young wife. The two of them, one happy and one sad, will not return home until they are drunk. Why don’t we swap places and sleep, so that tomorrow at the fifth watch, you and my young man can rise early and leave quickly? I am willing to sacrifice my old bones and die with the old man, so do not grieve." The young woman hesitated and did not immediately follow. The old woman spoke seriously, "This is precisely the plan where we trade and then leave, each getting what they want, a strategy that achieves two goals with one action. You should go quickly, for if you delay, the matter will be settled!" They exchanged clothes. The young woman thanked her, and the old woman led her to the rice room, covering her with a blanket and instructing her not to speak. She then returned to the western room, covered her head, and lay down. After the second watch, the old man and the boy returned drunk, having run around and worked hard, and each went to bed.

    179

    三更后，米梦中闻叩户声，披衣起视，则老妪也。米讶曰：“汝何往？”妪止之，令禁声，旋入室闭户，以情告之。米且惊且喜曰：“虽承周折，奈损人利己何？”妪哂曰：“不听老人言，则郎君弃掷一小娘，断送一老翁矣，于人何益，于己得无损乎？”米首肯，妪启衾促女起，嘱之再四。米与女泣拜，妪止之，嘱：“早行！恐叟寤，老身从此别矣。”即出户去。米亟束装，女以青纱幛面，米扶之出店，店主人曰：“无乃太早发？”米漫应之曰：“早行避炎暑也。”遂遁去。翌日，叟见妪大惊，诘知其故，怒极，挥以老拳，妪亦老健，搒掠不少让。合店人环观如堵。叟忿诉其冤，欲策蹇追之，闻者无不粲然。居停主人曰：“彼得少艾而遁，岂肯复遵大路以俟汝追耶？况四更已行，此时走数十里矣。人苦不自知耳，人苟自知而安分者，竟载此妪以归，老夫妻正好过日，勿生妄念也！”叟痴立移时，气渐平，味主人言，大有理，遂载妪去。迄今秦陇人皆能悉之。

    179

    After the third watch, Mi was awakened in his sleep by the sound of someone knocking on the door. He got up, put on his clothes, and looked outside to see an old woman. Mi was surprised and asked, "Where are you going?" The old woman stopped him and told him to keep quiet. She quickly entered the room, closed the door, and told him the situation. Mi, both surprised and delighted, said, "Even though it has been complicated, what about harming others for one's own benefit?" The old woman smiled and said, "If you don't listen to the old person's advice, the young man would abandon a young girl, destroying an old man. What benefit is there to others, and what harm is there to oneself?" Mi nodded in agreement. The old woman opened the quilt and urged the girl to get up, telling her repeatedly. Mi and the girl wept and bowed, but the old woman stopped them and urged, "Hurry up and leave! Fear that the old man will wake up, I will part ways from you forever." She then left the room. Mi hurriedly packed his things, and the girl covered her face with a green veil. Mi helped her out of the inn, and the innkeeper said, "Isn't it too early to leave?" Mi answered carelessly, "We are leaving early to avoid the heat." They then fled. The next day, the old man saw the old woman and was greatly shocked, asking to find out the reason. He became extremely angry and hit her with a fist. The old woman, though old and strong, did not hold back in beating her. People from the inn gathered around like a wall. The old man complained bitterly of his injustice and wanted to ride a donkey to chase after them. Everyone who heard laughed. The innkeeper said, "He escaped with a young and beautiful woman. How could they follow the main road and wait for you to catch up? Especially since they have already left by the fourth watch, they have already traveled dozens of miles by now. People often don't know their own limitations. If people know themselves and are content with their lot, they would finally take this old woman back home, an old couple can live happily together. Don't have wild thoughts!" The old man stood there dazed for a while, and his anger gradually subsided. He considered the innkeeper's words, which made a lot of sense, and finally took the old woman away. To this day, people in Qin and Long regions all know about it.

    180

    兰岩曰：妪为米谋，亦云忠矣。然亦天假之缘，故尔易易。世之极尽心力而卒不能有成者，岂少也哉？安得此妪，遍天下而调停之？

    　　韩生宜君诸生韩某，年二十，资质韶秀。读书于玉皇庙之后阁，服役者，一小童而已。一日，童送食上阁，见生瞑目兀坐，寂然不动，以两指夹书一页，似欲翻阅者。亟唤不应，童心悸，呼道士入阁，视之，皮肉已寒，气已绝矣。道士大骇，告其家。家惟孀母并一姊，闻之，惊惶失措，急至阁，抚尸大骇。鸣诸官，邑宰刘公（士夫）往相之，一无伤损，唯阴囊肿起如猪脬，阳具青黑，坚硬如铁，自脐下中分一线，直至肛门，红似胭脂。老于仵作行者皆不能辨。讯道士及童，实不知情，大索阁上，亦无可迹，遂成疑案，事遂寝。后廉知生小有才，而渔色无厌，故有是报。

    Explanation

    180

    Final Translation

    Lan Yan said: The old woman was loyal in her efforts to secure rice, which is indeed commendable. However, she also benefited from divine assistance, making her task seem effortless. There are many who exhaust all their efforts yet ultimately fail to achieve success; are they not few? How can one find such an old woman to mediate throughout the world?

    Han Sheng, a student from Yijun, was twenty years old, with a graceful and elegant appearance. He studied in the rear hall of the Temple of the Jade Emperor, served by only a young boy. One day, the boy brought food up to the hall and saw Han Sheng with closed eyes, sitting motionless, not moving at all, holding a page of a book with two fingers as if he wanted to turn it. The boy called out urgently but received no response. Frightened, he summoned a Taoist priest to enter the hall. Upon examination, the priest found that Han Sheng's body was already cold, and his breath had ceased. The priest was greatly alarmed and informed his family. His family consisted only of a widowed mother and an elder sister. Upon hearing the news, they were terrified and rushed to the hall, where they found the body and were horrified. They reported the incident to the authorities. The county magistrate, Liu Gong (Shifu), investigated the matter. There were no signs of injury; only the scrotum was swollen like a pig bladder, the penis was blue-black and hard as iron, and a red line ran from below the navel to the anus, resembling cinnabar. Even experienced forensic examiners could not determine the cause. The Taoist priest and the boy were questioned, and they truly did not know what had happened. A thorough search of the hall revealed no traces, so the case became a mystery and was eventually closed. Later, it was discovered that Han Sheng had been slightly talented but had been insatiable in his pursuit of sexual pleasure, which led to his demise.

    181

    兰岩曰：渔色者，宜警是哉！

    　　卷二修鳞山左梅和鼎，客潮阳，粜籴致富。娶妻纳妾，买童仆，蓄婢媪，置田宅于白云坛，遂家焉。然富而不骄不吝，性倜傥，好施予，异乡客有落拓不能归者，苟一告急，无不应之如响。以故人钦其高义，而弗忌其多财。梅暮年能甘寂寞，居恒无所事事，辟宅后隙地数亩，结构一轩，左园右圃。岭南地暖土肥，插竹数日成林，植树弥月垂荫。复叠石为山，穿沼蓄鱼，奇花异卉，足以自娱。

    Explanation

    181

    Lan Yan said: Those who pursue sexual pleasures should be warned about this!

    In the second volume, Mei He Ding, originally from the left of Xiulin Mountain, traveled to Chaozhou, where he became wealthy through the buying and selling of grain. He married a wife and took concubines, bought child servants, and kept female servants. He established land and property at Baiyun Tan and thus settled down. However, despite his wealth, he was neither arrogant nor stingy. He had a bold and unrestrained character and enjoyed giving. If there were any strangers from distant places who were destitute and unable to return home, and if they appealed to him in any way, he would always respond promptly. Therefore, people admired his noble character and did not envy his wealth. In his later years, Mei was content with solitude and often had nothing to do. He cleared several acres of unused land behind his house and built a small pavilion. To the left was a garden, and to the right was a nursery. The warm soil and fertile land of the southern region allowed bamboo to form a forest in a few days, and trees to provide shade after a month. He piled up stones to form a mountain, dug ponds to keep fish, and collected rare and exotic flowers, which were sufficient for his own entertainment.

    182

    东邻有修鳞者，为郡诸生。少失怙恃，三十未婚，炊烟屡绝，而处之泰然。梅重其品行，常通庆吊，而修生从不向之摇尾。梅或偶供廪给，必力辞不受，即强而后可，亦必宛转报复，适敌其数而止。梅叹曰：“古人一介不轻取予，吾于修君信之矣！”自是益敬之。

    　　会夏日，梅瀹茗纳凉，暴雨骤至，承霤如倾。逾时雨霁，□□在东。忽报修先生至。梅惊喜出意外，谓高士屐齿来破苔纹，殊足为交游光宠。倒屣迎之，握手甚欢，修匆匆作寒暄语，即目视假山，咄咄称怪事，曰：“此其定都山乎？”山东北十数武，有巨石横卧，修度其方向，曰：“所谓大石国也。”于是循假山而南，得鱼沼。沼西岸见蚁封高二三寸，指示梅曰：“此东海，此蚍蜉国也。”复蹲身沼畔，拂花拔草，若有所觅。

    Explanation

    182

    A man named Xiulin lived to the east of the house, and he was a student of the prefecture. He lost his parents when young and remained unmarried at thirty. His cooking fires often went out, yet he dealt with it calmly. Mei valued his character and often visited to offer congratulations or condolences, but Xiulin never fawned over him. Mei sometimes provided him with provisions, but he always refused to accept, even if forced, he would accept, and he would find a way to repay to the exact amount given. Mei sighed and said, "Ancients did not lightly take or give even a small thing; I believe in Master Xiulin!" From then on, he respected him even more.

    One summer day, Mei brewed tea to cool off, and a sudden heavy rain came, pouring down. After some time, the rain stopped, and something was to the east. Suddenly someone reported that Master Xiulin had arrived. Mei was surprised and delighted, beyond expectation, thinking that a high-minded gentleman had come, breaking through the mossy path, it was very honorable for the social circle. He hurriedly put on his shoes to welcome him, and they shook hands very happily. Xiulin quickly made polite conversation, immediately looking at the artificial hill, exclaiming in surprise about a strange thing, saying, "Is this the capital hill?" To the northeast of the hill, about ten or so steps, there was a large stone lying horizontally. Xiulin measured its direction, saying, "It is said to be the Stone Kingdom." Then he followed the artificial hill to the south, reaching a fish pond. On the west bank of the pond, he saw ant hills two or three inches high, pointing to Mei and saying, "This is the East Sea, and this is the Land of Ants." He crouched by the pond, brushed away flowers and pulled out grass, as if searching for something.

    183

    梅从旁，不测所为，但接踵追随，行云则行，止云则止，方匿笑书痴作用，迥不犹人。既而修觅得一物，愕然曰：“果有是哉！”梅就视之，则枯鲋一尾，长三寸余，蛀已过半矣。修却步，把梅手，还至假山下，绕石搜索，见群蚁营穴，衔土出入不休，瞿然若有所失，伫立咨嗟，潸然泣下。梅致诘其故，修叹曰：“事极奇异，请入轩为丈人详告之。”梅怀惑殊甚，及闻修备述甚异，梅废然良久，顿悟身世，相与谈禅论道，为方外交。共入罗浮山采药，不返。梅仲子蟠根，从鄞江上官周学画，故其先人与修生事，上官知之极详，予闻之，喟然曰：“此南柯之续也，请志之。”

    Explanation

    183

    Mei followed closely, unpredictable in his actions, always stepping in line and following the natural flow of events. He secretly mocked the bookish tendencies of Xu, completely different from others. Later, Xu found an object and exclaimed in surprise, "Is it really so?" Mei looked at it, and it was a dried fish, about three inches long, half-eaten by insects. Xu stepped back, took Mei's hand, and returned to the artificial hill, searching around the stones. He saw a group of ants building their nests, constantly carrying soil in and out, looking startled as if something were missing. He stood still and sighed, tears welling up and falling. Mei asked for the reason, and Xu sighed, "The matter is extremely strange, please come to the pavilion and explain it in detail to me." Mei was very puzzled, and upon hearing Xu's detailed account of the strange occurrence, he remained silent for a long time, suddenly understanding his life. They discussed Zen and Daoist philosophy together, becoming spiritual friends. Together, they entered Luofu Mountain to gather herbs and did not return. Mei Zhongzi, a descendant of Mei, studied painting under Shangguan Zhou from Yinjiang, because his ancestors and Xu had a relationship. Shangguan knew it in great detail. I heard this and sighed, "This is a continuation of the South柯 dream, please record it."

    184

    先是修生独居一室，旦夕攻苦，盖锐志于功名者。一日，倦读，当北窗午睡，朦胧间见一黑衣人，排闼入，长寸许，促修：“速起，使臣持节至矣。”修方欲致询，其人已出，修逡巡下榻，自觉身已暴缩。错愕间，彩帐已列满阶下，持节者衣冠皆非时制，侍卫甚都，传呼曰：“蚍蜉国王召修鳞赴阙，可即就道！”修再拜辞谢曰：“草莽微臣，无往见之义，讵敢应旌旂之召，致乖羔雁之仪？”使臣曰：“王以先生贤者，礼当就见，恐致逾垣，故使近卢玄蚼恭御蒲轮，代将白璧，望先生幡然应聘，惠辱海邦。萃野渭滨之事，唯先生之所熟闻；而箕山颖水之风，非寡君之所敢望。修再三谦让，然后受命。左右进冠服，掖之升车，人马纷纭，循阶而行。约数十里，甫抵西墙下，阴念墙西即梅氏园矣，行来何觉太远。

    Explanation

    184

    Previously, the scholar lived alone in a room, devoting himself to arduous study day and night, presumably with a keen ambition for fame and success. One day, tired from reading, he was taking a nap at noon by the north window. In a drowsy state, he saw a black-clothed figure burst through the door, about an inch tall, urging him: "Hurry up and get up, the imperial envoy with the ceremonial staff has arrived!" As the scholar was about to inquire, the figure had already left. He hesitated, got out of bed, and suddenly felt himself shrink drastically. Startled, he saw colorful tents arranged all along the steps, with the envoy and attendants dressed in attire that was not of the current era, and the guards were exceptionally handsome. They shouted: "The king of the ants summons the scholar to the palace, you may depart immediately!" The scholar bowed twice and politely declined, saying: "A lowly official from the wilderness, I have no obligation to visit, how dare I respond to the call of the banners, which would disrupt the proper etiquette of a visit?" The envoy replied: "The king regards you as a distinguished scholar, it is proper to meet. Fearing that you might leap over the wall, I have sent the envoy Lu Xuan Fu to respectfully present a carriage and a white jade, hoping you will suddenly accept the invitation and grace our distant land with your presence. The story of the meeting at the Wei River, which you are surely familiar with, and the reclusive spirit of Mount Ji and the River Yi, are not something our humble king dares to aspire to." The scholar repeatedly humbly declined, then accepted the command. The attendants brought robes and hats, helped him onto the carriage, and they set off with a flurry of people and horses, walking along the steps. After traveling for about several miles, they finally reached the west wall. He secretly thought that beyond the west wall was the Mei family garden, but how did it feel so far away?

    185

    疑心满腹，第无如之何，姑听之。俄墙下辟一城门，楼堞具备，榜曰：”东关，“石磴百余级，前驱旂鼓按部而入，有数人跪道左曰：”关吏祗候矣。“入关，息驾馆驿，铺陈极华美，水陆馈饷无算，唯与玄蚼对享。翌日出关，关吏请扈从。玄蚼颐指曰：”免！“气象尊大，修知其为国之贵臣也。日中抵国门，王郊迎三十里，冠紫金冠，衣赤锦袍，披素罗鹤氅，貌甚奇伟，执礼甚恭，修下车趋拜，王答拜曰：”祖宗灵爽，得先生辱临敝邑。惟是敝邑之社稷实有赖焉。先生不远千里，必有以教寡人，寡人虽不敏，悉举封内，惟先生之命是听。“对曰：”臣学识短浅，性癖山林，既乏夷吾富国之才，讵有景略匡时之志？不意礼隆三聘，滥叨丝马之加，敢不力赞一筹，勉效涓埃之报。“王大喜，载以后车，告诸太庙，爵为上卿，军国大事，悉取决焉。

    185

    He was full of doubts, but there was no way to deal with them, so he let it be for now. Suddenly, a city gate appeared under the wall, with towers and battlements complete, and the sign read: "East Pass." Hundreds of stone steps led up, and the vanguard with flags and drums entered in formation. Several people knelt on the left side of the road and said, "The customs officials are waiting respectfully." After entering the pass, he rested the carriage at the guesthouse, where the furnishings were extremely luxurious, and food and supplies from land and water were countless. He shared a meal only with Xuan Xu. The next day, as he was leaving the pass, the customs officials requested to accompany him. Xuan Xu waved his hand and said, "No!" His presence was majestic and grand, and Xiu knew he was a high-ranking official of the country. At noon, they arrived at the city gate, and the king greeted him thirty miles outside the city, wearing a purple gold crown, a red brocade robe, and a white silk crane cloak. His appearance was very extraordinary and majestic, and he performed the rituals with great respect. Xiu did not get out of the carriage to bow, and the king returned the bow and said, "The spirits of our ancestors are benevolent, and we are honored by your visit to our humble city. Our city truly relies on your help. You have come from afar, and you must have something to teach me. I, though not very intelligent, will offer everything within my domain and follow your commands." Xiu replied, "My knowledge and learning are limited, and I have a preference for the mountains and forests. I lack the talent of Yi Wu to enrich the country, and I have no intention of saving the times like Jing Lue. I did not expect such grand courtesy with three invitations, and I have unworthily received the honor of silk and horses. How dare I not offer a suggestion, and I will strive to make a small contribution." The king was greatly pleased, and he had Xiu ride in the rear carriage. He informed the ancestral temples and bestowed the title of Grand Minister. All military and state affairs were decided by Xiu.

    186

    修素谈经济，一旦置身通显，鞠躬尽瘁，夙夜酬知。乃请命出巡，遍历郡县，沿海四十余城，各审其山川形势，亲绘地图，其国西负连山，东滨巨海，长林大泽，广斥无边。四境分为大镇，置使相，各辖六七城，皆宗臣领之。其人好勇多力，其俗尚意气，喜游猎。向南八百里有都会，名大石国，其风慓悍，乐于战斗。国人畏之。修周巡半载，悉了然于胸中。归国复命，退而上疏曰：“臣奉命巡视封疆，往返数千里，经历四十城，郡县无秦之酷吏，边关皆汉之嫖姚，三老兴歌，万民安堵，太平有象，如此可徵。第古之圣君贤相，安不忘危，治必防乱。

    Explanation

    186

    He who usually engaged in economic matters, once placed in a position of prominence, devoted himself wholeheartedly, working tirelessly to fulfill his duties. He then requested permission for an inspection tour, traveling through prefectures and counties, inspecting more than forty coastal cities, examining their terrain and landscapes, and personally drawing maps. The country was backed by continuous mountains to the west and bordered by a vast sea to the east, with long forests, large marshes, and vast, boundless plains. The four borders were divided into major towns, with envoys and ministers each governing six or seven cities, all led by noble ministers. The people valued courage and had great strength, and their customs emphasized spirit and temperament, enjoying hunting and traveling. To the south, eight hundred li away, there was a major city named Great Stone Country, whose customs were bold and fierce, and its people enjoyed fighting. The people of the country feared it. After touring the region for half a year, he fully understood the situation in his heart. Upon returning to the country and reporting back, he submitted a memorial saying: "I received orders to inspect the frontiers, traveling a round trip of several thousand li, passing through forty cities. The prefectures and counties have no harsh officials like those of Qin, and all border passes have Han's fierce warriors. The three elders sing songs, and all the people live in peace. There are signs of peace, which can be verified like this. However, ancient sage kings and wise ministers, even in times of peace, did not forget danger, and in governance, they must prevent disorder."

    187

    强邻孔迩，常如虎视之耽耽；鄙俗相沿，不啻狼贪之逐逐。伏乞防微杜渐，易俗移风，黾勉群工，以臻上理。”疏入，王优诏答之。居无何，大石果入寇，镇南都护司空玄蚼告急。王加修太宰，赐上方剑，命监玄□、玄□军，督西南二镇兵万八千以御之。修出奇兵绕出敌后，夹攻，大破之。俘馘数千，擒其枭帅。大石惧，上表请藩也：“修帅，神人也，南人不复反矣。”修谕以利害，尽归其俘。及凯旋，王犒师于紫菌之宫，工歌《采芑》以飨将士。以修为右仆射兼侍中尚书令，平章军国重事，尚拖花公主，赐甲第一区。金玉锦绣，赏赉无算。修安享四十年，五子三婿，皆为显官，势力煊赫。

    187

    A powerful neighbor is very close, always watching with the intent of a tiger; lowly customs have been passed down, no less than a wolf's relentless pursuit. I humbly request that we nip things in the bud, change customs and transform the风气, encourage the workers diligently, to reach the highest level of governance." The memorial was submitted, and the king responded with a favorable edict. Not long after, the Great Stone indeed invaded, and the Governor of the Southern Garrison, Sima Xuan, reported an emergency. The king promoted Xu to the position of Taizai, bestowed the imperial sword, and ordered Xu to oversee the armies of Xuan and Xuan, supervising 18,000 troops from the two southwestern garrisons to resist them. Xu sent out a surprise force to circle around the enemy's rear, conducted a pincer attack, and soundly defeated them. Captured thousands of prisoners and heads, and captured their fierce leaders. The Great Stone feared and submitted a memorial requesting vassalage: "Xu is a god-like leader, the people of the south will not rebel again." Xu explained the pros and cons, returned all the captives. Upon returning victorious, the king feasted the troops at the Purple Mushroom Palace, and the musicians sang 'Cai Qi' to entertain the soldiers. Xu was appointed Right Censor-in-Chief, concurrently serving as Chief of the Imperial Secretariat and Minister of Works, in charge of major military and state affairs, and married Princess Tuo Hua. He was bestowed a mansion, and received countless rewards of gold, jade, silk, and brocade. Xu enjoyed a peaceful life for forty years, with five sons and three sons-in-law, all prominent officials, and his power and influence were very prominent.

    188

    会海滨有巨鱼，潮落失水，王命贵臣玄蚼率全国之民，往取厥鱼。鱼长百丈，头尾如山，国人抢攘弥月，仅移百里。修上疏谏止，以为竭民力以纵口腹之欲，非盛德事，请罢其役。王召修面谕之曰：“夫学贵流通而忌拘泥，若夫戾人情而乖时务，王安石所以非救时宰相也。我国人情风土，相国莅治数十年，岂尚未之深悉耶？高原硗瘠，下隰斥卤，民不耕而食，赖游猎以为生计。巨鱼出水，是天赐丰年。群臣皆贺，而相国独有异议，勿得人情时务犹有未娴乎？”修见所言不纳，怏怏而退。一日，太史玄□奏山蒸土润，主有大水。王大惊，议迁都以避之。镇北都护宁朔侯侍中玄□上言：“积石山高远深邃，堪可经营新邑。”王大喜，命修往相宅。修奉命致山，相其阴阳，度其原稤，见山童水远，深以为不可。

    Explanation

    188

    In the coastal area, there was a giant fish. When the tide receded, the fish was stranded. The king ordered the high-ranking official Xuan Fu to mobilize the entire population to capture the fish. The fish was a hundred zhang long, with its head and tail like mountains. The people of the country were in a state of panic for a month, managing to move the fish only a hundred li. Xiu submitted a memorial to advise against the king's order, arguing that using people's labor to satisfy the king's personal desires was not virtuous and requested that the work be stopped. The king summoned Xiu to explain his decision directly. "Learning should be flexible and open, but rigid and narrow-minded. If actions go against human nature and current affairs, then Wang Anshi was not a good prime minister for his time. The customs and local conditions of the country, the prime minister has governed for many years, surely he should know them well. The land is barren and salty, the people do not farm but rely on hunting and fishing for their livelihood. The appearance of the fish is a sign of a bountiful year. All ministers except the prime minister were in favor of the king's plan. The historian reported that the mountains were steaming and the soil was moist, indicating an impending flood. The king considered moving the capital to avoid the flood. The general and member of the court suggested a new capital. The new location was high, distant, and deep, suitable for building a new capital. The king was pleased and ordered Xiu to inspect the site. Xiu was sent to the mountain to examine its orientation and terrain. Xiu found the mountain barren and the water far away, deeming it unsuitable for the new capital.

    189

    封章驰奏曰：“臣奉勅相宅，周视名山，禹迹无存，河源殊远。乃疆乃理，非同亶父之岐，常邑常宁，讵等盘庚之亳？窃思旧都巩固，负山海以称雄，数代承平，通鱼盐以为利，不如绥爰有众，奠厥攸居。”黄门以事关重大，即时转奏。王览奏叹曰：“书生之见，胡不远大如此？”即批答曰：“伻来以图及献卜，知相国思深虑远，足见忠爱。惟是都城滨海，水患堪虞。如迁都之议一梗，则通国之民，皆鱼鳖矣。兹更使中书令玄蚼为相国辅，速定新都。寡人率宫嫔臣民，行当就迁矣。”修得诏默然。玄蚼至，乃相与于山之南麓，筑城垣，建宫室，开阛阓，葺民居。连夜兴作，工粗竣。王已徙都中十余万户，陆续而至。修大惊，遮道而谏曰：“王奈何轻弃根本之地，独不虑敌国外患，乘间窃发耶？”王降舆慰劳，改积石山曰定都山，以修为故都留守，晋爵定都公。

    Explanation

    189

    The Chancellor presented a formal memorial and said, "I have been ordered to survey the land. I examined famous mountains, but found no traces of Yu, and discovered the river's source was very distant. The boundaries and territories are not the same as the fertile land of Da Pu, nor are ordinary towns and peaceful regions comparable to Pan Geng's Bo. I humbly think that the old capital is firmly established, relying on mountains and seas to claim dominance, having enjoyed several generations of peace and stability, trading fish and salt as a source of profit, but it is better to settle the people and establish a place for them to live." The eunuch official thought the matter was of great importance and immediately forwarded the memorial. The king read the memorial and sighed, "The perspective of a scholar, why so narrow-minded?" He then replied, "Messengers came with plans and divination results, knowing that the Chancellor thinks deeply and far-sightedly, clearly demonstrating loyalty and love. Only that the capital is by the sea, there is a risk of water disasters. If the proposal to move the capital is blocked, then the entire population would be like fish and turtles. Now, the imperial command is to appoint the Secretary of the Central Secretariat, Xuan Ke, as the Chancellor's assistant, quickly determine the new capital. I will lead the palace ladies, officials, and people to move soon." The Chancellor received the imperial decree silently. Xuan Ke arrived, and they then worked together at the southern foot of the mountain, built city walls, constructed palaces and buildings, opened markets, and renovated residential areas. They worked day and night, and the construction was roughly completed. The king had already moved over ten thousand households to the new capital, arriving in succession. The Chancellor was greatly surprised and blocked the way to advise, saying, "Why did you lightly abandon the fundamental land, did you not consider the threats from external enemies, seizing an opportunity to launch an attack?" The king descended from his carriage to comfort and encourage, changed the name of Mount Jishui to Dingdu Mountain, appointed the Chancellor as the governor of the old capital, and elevated his title to Duke of Dingdu.

    190

    修拜命即行，公主及诸子皆不预闻。从人半路多亡去，殊深愤恚。及抵旧都，见万井一空，益忧愤，仰天叹曰：“不意竭忠尽力，反为逐臣，王阳厚阴疏，可谓智足以拒谏矣！孤臣恋恋，复欲何为？不如解组归田，遂我初服。名利场中非复我侧足地也！”因悬绶国门，单骑出关。关吏叩马问相国将何往，修具告之。吏曰：“相国忠则忠矣，然而趋吉避凶之理，未之深晰也。拘拘于不迁之议，而使数百万生灵，尽葬鱼腹。相国讵忍见之乎？”修曰：“流言飞语，何足凭信？”吏曰：“请相国少留八日，当见灾异，王之心可明。相国之怨，谅亦可少息矣。”修本不舍王，乃停骖焉。

    Explanation

    190

    Received the order and immediately set out, the princess and her children were all unaware of the situation. Many of his followers deserted halfway, causing him deep resentment and anger. Upon reaching the old capital, he saw a thousand wells empty, which further increased his worry and frustration. He looked up to heaven and sighed, saying, "I did not expect that despite my utmost loyalty and effort, I would be exiled. The king's favor is fickle, and he is wise enough to reject advice! A loyal minister who is deeply attached, what more can I do? It is better to resign and return to the countryside, resuming my original attire. The field of fame and profit is no longer a place where I can stand!" He then hung his official seal at the city gate and rode out alone. The gatekeeper stopped him and asked where the chancellor was going, to which he fully explained. The gatekeeper said, "The chancellor is indeed loyal, but he has not thoroughly understood the principles of seeking good and avoiding evil. Being stuck in the argument against relocation, he has caused millions of lives to be buried in the fish's belly. How could the chancellor bear to see such a thing?" The chancellor replied, "Rumors and slander, what reason is there to believe them?" The gatekeeper said, "Please, your highness, stay for eight more days, and you will see disasters, which will clarify the king's heart. The chancellor's grievances, I believe, will be somewhat alleviated." The chancellor, who originally did not want to leave the king, stopped his carriage.

    191

    居三日，天地阴晦，大雨如倾，浃旬不止。平地水深数丈，树头牵夫藻荇，舵尾压于楼台。骇目惊心，声如万马。关城地高，犹浸三版。修始信迁都之计非左，乃西向再拜而泣曰：“臣负王心矣！纵王不见谴，臣何面目复见臣民乎？”言讫，掷仆头于地，跃身赴水，渹然一声，午梦忽觉，依然身卧桃笙。

    　　夏雨初歇，檐溜犹滴。蹶然而起，自讼奇奇。蹑履出户，缘阶而行，冥想梦中去路，依稀可认，寻踪至西墙下，花砖缺处，有小穴，大如钱，恍似东关形势，对穴窥之，则梅之渔沼假山历历可辨，穴口有游蚁出入，方悟四十年功名富贵，皆梦中蚁国幻化而为之也。

    　　闲斋曰：梅慷慨，修耿介，皆仙品也，故不学仙而入仙籍。习见世人于忠孝根本之地及一切福田，皆荒芜不治；而功名富贵，声色货利之场，中热如烧。及稍不如愿，辄妄欲学仙，其实七情六欲，触绪纷来，虽有彭咸在侧，前推而后挽之，恐一步行，万不敌其一步却也。

    Explanation

    191

    After three days, the sky darkened and the weather turned gloomy, with heavy rain pouring down like a bucket being emptied, continuing without pause for ten days. The water on flat ground deepened to several zhang, with trees at the head entangled with algae and water plants, and the stern of the ship pressing against the pavilions. The scene was startling to the eyes and shocking to the heart, with the sound like that of ten thousand horses. The city wall, though high, was still submerged to three boards. Xiu began to believe that the plan to relocate the capital was not wrong, and he then bowed twice to the west and wept, saying, "I have failed the king's trust! Even if the king does not blame me, how can I face my subjects again?" After speaking, he threw his head to the ground, leaped into the water, and there was a loud splash. Suddenly awakening from a noonday dream, he still lay on a peach-wood mat.

    The summer rain had just stopped, with water dripping from the eaves. He suddenly rose, condemning himself for being so strange, stepped into shoes and went out the door, walking along the steps, meditating on the path taken in the dream, which was vaguely recognizable. Following the trail to the western wall, he noticed a missing tile in the flower bricks, with a small hole as big as a coin, which vaguely resembled the layout of the eastern gate. Peering into the hole, he saw the fish pond, fake mountains, and other features of Mei's garden clearly visible, with ants entering and exiting the hole. Only then did he realize that forty years of fame, wealth, and power were all illusions in a dream.

    Xianzhai said: Mei is generous, and Xiu is upright, both like immortals, therefore they enter the ranks of immortals without learning the ways of immortals. Frequently seeing people neglecting the foundations of loyalty and filial piety and all fields of merit, all are neglected and uncultivated; but in the fields of fame, wealth, and sensual pleasures, they are burning with desire. When they do not get their way, they rashly wish to become immortals. In reality, their seven emotions and six desires arise in confusion, even if there were Peng Xian beside them, pushing them forward and pulling them back, they would fear that one step forward is not worth a thousand steps back.

    192

    兰岩曰：四十年功名显赫，转成一梦耳。拘迂见斥，总不免书生之见。修生不达时，修生乃悟道矣。

    　　来存予家老仆来存者，李姓，德名，沈阳人。善国语，性情直，有膂力。少壮时，曾负贩于蒙古诸部落，其人情风土，知之甚悉。而所遭怪异，数见不鲜。予从家君扶祖榇自闽入都，于仲家浅泊舟三日，候放闸。夏夜苦热，披襟坐船头，对月当风，向李询塞外风景及所见闻。李因言其客喀尔喀时，其人骑兽，似鹿而非，有语言，无文字，亦无机械，如游循蜚因提之世。其俗无主客，客至张幕，辄走乞烟食，坐而眙脾脯醢齑，与之，乃去。客至其幕，径入啜且啖，夜宿毡炕前，主代牧，不偿。予顾之曰：“视争畔构讼，析产阋墙，行百里者必腰缠，惠一餐者有德色，大悬殊也。

    Explanation

    192

    Lan Yan said: Forty years of fame and glory, which turned out to be just a dream. Being criticized for being rigid and narrow-minded, one cannot escape the views of a scholar. When a scholar does not achieve success, the scholar realizes the way.

    Lai Cun, an old servant of my family, is named Li De, from Shenyang. He is proficient in the national language, with a straightforward disposition and strong physique. In his youth, he once traded with various Mongolian tribes. He knows their customs and local conditions very well. The strange things he encountered were not uncommon. I accompanied my father to carry my grandfather's coffin from Fujian to the capital, and we docked our boat at Zhongjiaqian for three days, waiting for the sluice to be opened. The summer night was extremely hot. Unbuttoned his robe and sat at the front of the boat, facing the moon and the wind. I asked Li about the scenery and what he had seen and heard in the frontier regions. Li then spoke of his visit to the Khalkha region, where the people ride animals that look like deer but are not. They have language but no writing, and no machinery, like the age of Yu Xun Fei Yin Ti. Their custom is without hosts and guests. When guests arrive, they set up a tent and go to beg for tobacco and food. They sit and look at the meat, pickles, and other foods, and when given, they leave. When guests come to their tent, they directly enter and drink and eat. At night, they sleep in front of the felt kang, the host takes care of the animals, and does not charge. I looked at them and said: "Compared to those who quarrel over boundaries and lawsuits, divide property, and fight within the family, those who travel a hundred miles must carry money, and those who receive a meal show a look of gratitude. There is a great difference."

    193

    ”杭蔼山之西北，地名陀罗海，即振武军驻防处。近黑道，故寒。七月雨雪，五月始释；山之巅，六月不释。筑土为屋，屋内纸糊数寸。毡帷暖炕，早起，被池堆霜。出门数步，凌封髭须。手僵不得呵，耳鼻窸窣有声，或烂且脱。幸风自东南来，夏风始反，不尔，冻且死。然南人至此地，亦罕有冻而死者。予笑曰：“世无不可炼之人，人无不可历之境也。”陀罗海苦塞矣，而不苦饥。茶一斤易一羊，十斤易一牛。中国人至彼，恣烹炙，餍熏□，头蹄满衢，血骨遍地，回思羹黎藿，饭粝粱，兹诚乐郊矣。所惜冬苦寒，否则诚乐。予嗤之曰：“得陇望蜀，人之心无止期也。角缺齿丰，天与人无全境也。”

    Explanation

    193

    To the northwest of Hangai Mountain, there is a place called Toluo Hai, where the Zhenwu Army is stationed. It is near the Black Path, hence it is cold. Snow falls in July and melts in May; on the mountain peaks, it does not melt until June. Houses are built from earth, and the interiors are lined with paper several inches thick. Felt curtains and warm kang stoves are used; when waking up early, the bed is covered with frost. After stepping out a few steps, ice freezes the beard. Hands freeze so tightly that one cannot warm them, and ears and nose make crackling sounds, sometimes becoming raw and peeling off. Fortunately, the wind comes from the southeast, reversing the usual summer wind direction, otherwise, they would freeze to death. However, people from the south rarely freeze to death here. I smiled and said, "There is no person who cannot be tempered, and no situation that cannot be endured." Toluo Hai is harshly isolated, but not hungry. One pound of tea can be exchanged for one sheep, and ten pounds for one cow. When Chinese people arrive, they indulge in roasting and smoking food, and the streets are filled with heads and hooves, and the ground is covered with blood and bones. Reflecting on simpler, more humble meals, this is truly a happy place. What is regrettable is the bitter cold in winter, otherwise, it would be truly enjoyable. I scoffed and said, "Having gained one thing, one desires another; human desire knows no bounds. Just as some people have missing teeth but others have full ones, heaven and man have no perfect state."

    194

    似麋而大者，曰堪达尔汗，疑其即□也。前昂后低，多力。毛粗而长，为裘暖，角扁而厚，为决良。人以其皮可裘而角可决也。驰马弯弓，逐而歼之，获厚利。予蹙然曰：“夫皮所以蔽，角所以卫也。今乃知庇身者适以庇人，自卫者，反以自毙。是可悲也！”李曰：“其唇方大而厚，多膏，味极美。八珍中有猩唇，即此物也。以角试水，毒则角绿色。又有扫雪者，大于貂，绒白毫长，光逊之；人制为冠，以其似貂也。”予尝考汉制：珥貂，侍中，左右常侍。我朝制：大臣衣貂，近臣亦不禁。美观云乎哉。诚以貂夏□而冬毨，其操似松；内劲而外温，其德似玉。然则苟非其人，人将赋鹈梁焉，真弗贵也，况赝乎？有木，曰查克，产推河，似丝柳而不垂，耐霜雪，坚而且材，灼为炭，置径寸于炉中，数日始尽。治产难，亦治心痛，然大者拱，高者寻，风斯拔之，盖地沙且碱，根难聚而易朽也。予叹曰：“岂无美材，生非其地也，惜哉！”

    Explanation

    194

    A creature resembling a deer but larger, known as the Kan达尔汗, might be a specific species, though the exact identity is uncertain. It has a raised front and lowered back, and is strong. Its fur is coarse and long, making it warm as a fur coat, and its horns are flat and thick, good for making tools or weapons. People use its fur for coats and its horns for tools or weapons. Riding horses and drawing bows, they pursue and kill it, reaping substantial profits. I sighed and said, "The skin is used for covering or protection, and the horns are used for defense. Now it is known that what protects oneself actually protects others, and what is used for self-defense, instead, leads to one's own demise. This is indeed pitiful!" Li said, "Its lips are square, large, and thick, rich in fat, and taste extremely delicious. Among the eight delicacies in Chinese cuisine, there is 'ape lips,' which is this creature. Using the horn to test water, if the water is toxic, the horn turns green. There is also a creature called the 'snow cleaner,' larger than a marten, with white and long-haired fur, though less lustrous than the Kan达尔汗; people make it into hats because it resembles a marten." I once studied the Han dynasty system, where wearing sable fur on the ears was a privilege of the Imperial Secretariat and the Imperial Attendants. In our current dynasty, high officials wear sable fur, and close attendants are not prohibited from doing so. Is it for beauty? Indeed, the marten sheds its fur in summer and molts in winter, its nature is like that of a pine tree; firm inside and warm outside, its virtue is like that of jade. Thus, if it is not the right person, people will criticize the misuse of resources, it is truly not valuable, let alone a fake. There is a tree called Chak, which grows along the Push River, resembling willow but not drooping, resistant to frost and snow, strong and useful as timber, burned into charcoal, placed in the furnace, a diameter of one inch, it takes several days to burn completely. It is difficult to cultivate and also treats heart pain, however, the large ones are as thick as an arm, and the tall ones are as high as a man, the wind easily uproots them, because the land is sandy and alkaline, the roots are hard to form and easily rot. I sighed and said, "Are there no beautiful materials? They grow in the wrong place. What a pity!"

    195

    戈壁即瀚海也，内多奇石，石之色大者如马肝，小者如珠、如玉、如玛瑙、珊瑚、蜜蜡。金中虚而外朗，起脶纹，皆马肝石所孕也。初剖之，癞，日炙雨濯，风扫霜雪浸，剥落尽，则光璀璨矣。予矍然曰：“异哉！天之剥落之者，乃所以璀璨之也。”

    Explanation

    195

    The Gobi Desert, known as the Sea of Sand, is rich in unusual stones. The larger stones have a liver-like hue, while the smaller ones resemble pearls, jade, agate, coral, and amber. Some stones are hollow within but radiant on the outside, adorned with ridge patterns that resemble fingerprints, all formed by the "liver stones." When first cut open, these stones have a rough, bumpy surface. Over time, exposed to the sun and rain, swept by wind and frosted by snow, the rough layers wear away, revealing a shiny and brilliant appearance. I was astonished and remarked, "Strange! It is the natural process of weathering that brings out their brilliance!"

    196

    亦有木焉，一木而万木之叶皆具，名曰□，以其冒全材而实不成一材也。予曰：“独木也乎哉？”问：“其地有酒乎？”曰：“有。□□挏醷而蒸之，曰阿拉气，薄甚，唐人所谓十钟不醉人者。阿拉气酿取斗曰阿拉旃斗，取升曰科尔旃升，取合曰波罗搭拉苏，一名哈唎，以次厚合。又取龠曰赊尔旃，则龠敌斛矣。”予莞尔曰：“是其酿与冶同矣。冶炼形，酿炼气，炼至多为至少，故能以至少胜至多。虽然，此难为哺糟啜醨者道也。”闻其地亦产良马，汗不血。中国人以地非大宛，貌非汗血也，未有过而问者。昔日夫子称骥以德，后人称祒□、称腰諦以力。今舍德与力，而以地与貌，是紫燕白兔伏枥而嘶寒风，九方歅执靶而笑者也。

    Explanation

    196

    There is also a tree, one tree that has all the leaves of myriad trees, named □, because it encompasses all materials but does not form a single material. I asked, "Is it just a single tree?" I inquired, "Does the land have wine?" They replied, "Yes. It is prepared by boiling and fermenting, called Ala Qi, very weak, as the Tang people said, requiring ten measures to get someone drunk. Ala Qi, when brewed into a measure, is called Ala Zhan Dou; when brewed into a sheng, it is called Ker Zhan Sheng; when brewed into a he, it is called Bolo Dalasu, also known as Ha Li, gradually increasing in strength. When brewed into a yue, it is called She Er Zhan, and the smallest measure is as strong as the largest." I smiled and said, "Its brewing is the same as smelting. Smelting shapes the form, brewing refines the essence, refining many into few, thus it can overcome much with little. Nevertheless, this is hard for those who feed on dregs and drink thin wine to understand." I heard that the land also produces good horses, whose sweat does not turn to blood. Chinese people judge horses by their place and appearance, not by their virtue or strength, and no one has asked about it. In the past, Confucius praised horses for their virtue, and later people praised them for their strength. Now we abandon virtue and strength, and judge by place and appearance, it is like purple swallows and white rabbits crouching in the stable, bellowing in the cold wind, while Jiǔfāng Yīn holds the reins and laughs.

    197

    李又言其于康熙五十二年，由喀尔喀至巴里坤。其地有兽，似猿非猿，似猴非猴，中国呼为人同，甘凉人呼为野人，番人呼为噶里。往往窥视穹庐，见人饮食，辄乞其余。或窃取烟具、小刀之属，为人所见，即弃掷而奔。杀之不忍，逐之复来，胥无如之何。尝狎一人同，每莝豆樵汲等事，唤之悉能任使。至其寝食，虽不能言，颇能察色。居一年，治任将归，啾唧马前，捉衔捩镫，泪下如沈。李亦为之酸鼻。相从十余里，挥之不去，乃嘱之曰：“汝之不能从我至中国，犹我之不能留居此土也。可止矣，若再行数里，将迷途不得返矣。”人同始悲鸣而去。予闻之，愕然而讶，喟然而叹曰：“天地之间，人为贵，非五官之谓也。先官而具曰五常，后官而合曰五典，傅官而行曰五事。官而不事，又无典常，则人将兽之，若之何兽也，而人之同之？虽然，三五偕而后为人，人也，可不慎欤？

    Explanation

    197

    In the 52nd year of the Kangxi era, Li spoke of his journey from Khalkha to Balikun. In that land, there was an animal that resembled a monkey but was not quite a monkey. It was known as "Rén Tóng" in China, "Yě Rén" in Gansu and Lanzhou, and "Gā Lǐ" among the local people. This creature often peeped into yurts, watching people eat and begging for leftovers. Sometimes, it would steal items like tobacco pipes and small knives. When caught, it would drop them and flee. Killing it was unbearable, but chasing it away only made it return, and everyone was at a loss as to what to do. Li once befriended one such creature. Whenever tasks like cutting fodder, fetching water, or gathering firewood were needed, he would call it, and it would dutifully perform them. In terms of sleeping and eating, though it could not speak, it was quite adept at reading human expressions. After a year, Li prepared to leave and return home. The creature made a whining sound in front of his horse, holding the reins and pressing the stirrups, with tears falling like rain. Li felt a pang of sadness. The creature followed him for more than ten miles, unable to be driven away. Li then told it, "You cannot accompany me to China, just as I cannot stay here. You should stop now; if you go a few more miles, you will get lost and not be able to return." The creature began to mourn and finally left. Upon hearing this, I was startled and surprised, and sighed deeply, saying, "Among all things in heaven and earth, humans are the most precious, not referring to the five senses. Before the senses, there are the five constants; after the senses, there are the five classics; and through the senses, there are the five affairs. If one has senses but does not act, and lacks the classics and constants, then one will become like an animal. How can an animal be considered human? Although, only by combining the three and the five can one be a true human. One should be cautious, for one is a human."

    198

    兰岩曰：逐段具有至理，奈人之不能参透者多，何哉？

    　　杂记五则吾闻狐之类不一，有草狐、沙狐、元狐、火狐、白狐、灰狐、雪狐之别。或曰：是□□者年老则妖作，冠枯颅，衣槲带，幻人形。此物为害百出。焚山搜穴，挟矢嗾卢，赤其族，庶几妖绝矣乎。而不知是能为妖，非必为妖也；偶为妖，非尽物皆为妖也。且夫狐之妖有数，而物之妖无穷，裸虫、鳞介、花木、庙中偶、窑中金，是物皆能妖也。物之妖以夜，而人之妖以昼。胁肩谄笑，假虎凭城，翠眉红裙，朱衣白面，斯人无非妖也，奈何独欲赤狐之族乎？传曰：“妖由人兴。”人事尽，则妖端绝矣，于狐何尤？或曰：老而妖者名紺狐，又名灵狐，似猫而黑，北地多有之，盖别一种云。予与诸同学偶谈及狐怪，择尤者五则，记之。

    198

    Lan Yan said: Each section contains profound truths, but why do so many people fail to understand it?

    Miscellaneous Notes on Five Cases

    I have heard that there are different types of foxes, including grass foxes, sand foxes, Yuan foxes, fire foxes, white foxes, gray foxes, and snow foxes. Some say: those who become demons in old age are called indigo foxes, also known as spirit foxes, resembling cats but black, and they are common in the north, which is a different kind. This creature causes many harms. Burning mountains and searching dens, carrying arrows and provoking dogs, exterminating the entire clan, might almost eliminate the root of demons. But one does not know that they can become demons, not necessarily becoming demons; sometimes becoming demons, not all things becoming demons. Moreover, the number of fox demons is limited, while the number of demonized things is infinite, including insects, fish, plants, temple statues, and kiln-produced items, all of which can become demons. Demons of things appear at night, while demons of people appear during the day. Sycophants, false heroes, hypocrites, and hypocrites, these people are all demons. Why single out the extermination of foxes? It is said in the classics: "Demons arise from human actions." When human affairs are exhausted, the root of demons is cut off. What blame is there for the foxes? Some say: those who become demons in old age are called indigo foxes, also known as spirit foxes, resembling cats but black, and they are common in the north, which is a different kind. I and my fellow students occasionally discussed fox spirits, and I selected the five best cases to record them.

    199

    贵筑刘紫来昱东，肄业满，主于昌邑胡辉岩之山东会馆。中秋夜，聚饮于南楼下，在座者海阳鞠慕周庄行、胡岱峰子翼、贵阳邬敬斋维肃、薛鲁园廷楷，并予与主人相与说狐。予举红姑娘事，咸以为异。紫来因述其客山右时，闻一富室家多狐，往往幻形为祟，惊怖家口。或作佝偻老人，独步厅上；或作老妪，持栲栳出入仓厨；或作靓妆少女，倚门阅市，颠倒行人；又于壁上，忽现楼台，及郛郭雉堞之类，愈出愈奇。虽不害人，而其家颇厌苦之。

    Explanation

    199

    Dongyang Liu Zilai from Guiyang, having completed his studies, presided over the Shandong Guild Hall in Changyi, hosted by Hu Huayan. On the Mid-Autumn Festival night, they gathered for a drinking session under the southern pavilion. Present were Juyu Zhuang Xing from Haiyang, Ziyi Hu Ziyi, Weisù Wu Jingzai from Guiyang, Tingkai Xue Luyuan, and myself along with the host, all discussing tales of foxes. I recounted the story of the Red Girl, which everyone found strange. Zilai then narrated his experiences when he was a guest in Shanxi, where he heard of a wealthy household plagued by many foxes. These foxes often transformed into various forms to cause disturbances, frightening the household. Sometimes they appeared as stooped old men, walking alone in the hall; sometimes as old women, carrying baskets in and out of the kitchen; sometimes as beautifully dressed young girls, leaning against the door and watching the market, confusing passersby; and on the walls, they suddenly appeared as towers and city walls, becoming more and more bizarre. Although they did not harm anyone, the household found their presence very troublesome.

    200

    主人有女，所居邻佛堂，堂中有坛数十，蓄酒甚多，户常扃键，女日暮归寝，与侍女过佛堂下，闻堂中漉酒声。窥之，见二曲背媪，就坛盗酒饮，且饮且争。少焉，一媪大醉，酩酊之态，殊觉可笑，女不禁嗤然失声。媪闻之，愠曰：“何与尔丫头事！吃数怀酒耳，问笑之有？”侍女应声曰：“见人偷酒，吃得如此醉，焉得不笑？”媪怒，大声詈曰：“遮莫来撩拨尔祖姥！我将咬尔爹黑鸟！”女闻其言秽，亟避去。侍女不堪其骂，独立窗下痛诋之。飞瓦忽至，伤唇击落二齿，大痛而奔。随闻堂中大噱。主人闻之，戒家人勿多言，一夜无事。

    Explanation

    200

    The Master had a daughter who lived near a temple. Inside the temple hall, there were dozens of altars, and a lot of wine was stored, with the door often locked. At dusk, the girl returned home to sleep, passing by the temple with her maid. They heard the sound of wine being filtered inside the hall. Peering inside, they saw two old women with bent backs approaching the altars to steal and drink wine, drinking and arguing as they went. A short while later, one of the old women became heavily drunk, her state of drunkenness seeming very funny, and the girl couldn't help but laugh out loud. The old woman heard this and angrily said, "What business is it of yours, little girl! I've just drunk a few cups of wine, so what's there to laugh about?" The maid immediately responded, "Seeing someone stealing wine and drinking to such a drunken state, how could you not laugh?" The old woman got angry and loudly cursed, "Don't come and provoke my grandmother! I'll bite your father's black bird!" The girl heard the filthy words and hurriedly fled. The maid couldn't bear the cursing and stood alone by the window, scolding them fiercely. Suddenly, a flying tile arrived, injuring her lip and knocking out two teeth, causing her great pain as she ran away. Then, they heard a big laugh from inside the hall. The master heard this and warned the family not to talk much, and nothing happened that night.

    201

    次日，主人早起，见枕畔一物黟然，审视之，一男子势也，血色尚新，大骇，恐闺人见之，潜以火箸夹取置溷中。聚童仆察之，悉无恙。时侍女之父，从一县令在河南，方狎一妓。一夜，妓忽来就，相与共寝，鼾睡间，私处痛如刀割，大呼晕绝，同人惊起来探，已失势之所在。妓已不在侧，咸以为异，白于官，拘妓讯鞫，妓言昨夜与诸女作伴作叶子戏，通宵不寐，实不知情。竟成疑案。使人送之还乡，虽不致死，然已阉废。主人无如之何，亟徙居以避之，始获宁宇。盖侍女父失势时，即主人枕畔得势时也。侍女张姓，其父色黑，号黑张，故狐有咬黑鸟之说云。

    Explanation

    201

    The next day, the host got up early and saw a dark object by his pillow. Upon closer inspection, it was a male member, still fresh with blood. He was greatly frightened and feared that his wife or mistress might see it, so he secretly took it with tongs and placed it in the latrine. He gathered the servants to inspect the matter, and everything was found to be fine. At that time, the father of the servant girl was accompanying a county magistrate in Henan, where he was intimate with a courtesan. One night, the courtesan suddenly came to him, and they slept together. In the midst of their deep sleep, he felt severe pain in his private parts, as if cut by a knife, and he shouted and fainted. His companions woke up in alarm and found that his member had disappeared. The courtesan was no longer by his side, and everyone was astonished. They reported the matter to the authorities, who arrested and interrogated the courtesan. She claimed that the previous night she had been playing cards with other women and had stayed awake all night, truly unaware of what had happened. The case remained unresolved. They sent him back to his hometown, where although he did not die, he was castrated and rendered infertile. The host had no choice but to quickly move to a new residence to avoid the situation, and only then did he achieve peace. It seems that the time when the servant girl's father lost power was the same time when the host gained power. The servant girl was surnamed Zhang, and her father was dark-skinned, known as Black Zhang, hence the saying of the fox biting a black bird.

    202

    闲斋曰：吾闻狐性极淫，故名曰淫狐。乃其报冤，亦出于淫。可谓好名之甚者矣。夫名者，实之宾。狐之淫，发于其性，是先有其实而后名附之。狐岂为淫乎？然则世之名过其实者，曾淫狐之不若也。

    　　兰岩曰：自盗酒，而反殃及人父，此狐非但不仁，抑且无趣，殆所谓老羞成怒者耶？

    　　胡辉岸谓：贵筑蔡孝廉，博雅士也。尝向辉岩述及其乡人褚十二，少从其外祖顾明经游巴蜀，假馆于临邛罗氏。罗固巨族，累代为显宦，后世虽渐凌替，而第宅闳深，园庭幽胜，犹甲于一乡。罗二子一侄二甥，并受业于顾，褚亦附学其间。褚与罗之甥秦生者，相交莫逆，同设榻于园之西轩，居半岁余矣。

    Explanation

    202

    Idle Studio says: I have heard that the nature of foxes is extremely lewd, hence they are called lewd foxes. Yet in seeking revenge, they also act out of lewdness. One could say they are excessively fond of reputation. A name is but a guest to reality. The lewdness of the fox arises from its nature; it first has the reality and then the name attaches to it. Is the fox truly lewd? Then, those in the world whose reputation exceeds their reality are even less virtuous than these lewd foxes.

    Lan Rock says: A fox that steals wine and yet causes harm to a man's father is not only unkind but also boring, almost as if it is old shame turning into anger.

    Hu Huian says: Mr. Cai, the respected scholar from Guizhou, is a man of broad learning. He once recounted to Huian about his friend Zhu Shier, who, when young, traveled to Bashu with his maternal grandfather, Mr. Gu, the Imperial Scholar, and stayed temporarily at the mansion of the Luo family in Linqiong. The Luo family was a prominent clan, having been influential officials for generations. Although their status has gradually declined in later times, their mansion remains grand and spacious, and their garden is secluded and beautiful, still the best in the neighborhood. The Luo family had two sons, one nephew, and two nieces, all of whom studied under Mr. Gu. Zhu Shier also joined in their studies. Zhu Shier and Qin Sheng, the Luo family's nephew, were inseparable friends and shared a room in the west pavilion of the garden, staying there for more than half a year.

    203

    时当秋月，值罗次子毕婚，顾连日困于酒食，秦亦理事甚忙。褚独步轩中，深苦岑寂，抽书破闷。漏二下，秦生携酒盒来与褚小酌，曰：“逐日碌碌，未遑晤对，今宵稍暇，聊具杯酒，与子谈心。”于是屏童仆，扃园门，挑灯细酌，颇极欢畅。褚浮白曰：“人生行乐耳，须富贵何时？”秦笑曰：“偃鼠饮河，不过满腹，徒饮岂足以为乐？予有一妙人，兄如见之，当思老于是乡矣！”褚问为何如人，秦支吾不以实告。力叩之，秦始小语曰：“予下榻此园，二年于兹矣。尝交一丽人，年甫十七，兄到后，踪迹稍疏，然每际花月之夕，或值兄醉梦之时，未尝不把握也。以兄待我厚，故敢泄肺腑事，幸兄勿复泄于人！”褚曰：“虑弟相戏耳。如果然，则非狐即鬼，乌可亲昵？”秦曰：“诚为狐，非鬼也。狐而色比宓妃，才同谢女，何不可亲昵之有！”

    Explanation

    203

    During the autumn moon, Luo's second son was getting married, and Zhu had been exhausted by continuous feasting and drinking. Qin was also very busy with his affairs. Zhu walked alone in the hall, deeply feeling the loneliness and emptiness, and pulled out a book to relieve boredom. It was two hours after midnight when Qin brought a wine box to share a small drink with Zhu, saying, "Every day we are busy with trivial matters, and we have no time to meet and talk. Tonight we have a little free time, let's have a few cups of wine and talk to you heart-to-heart." They dismissed the servants, locked the garden gate, lit the lamp, and drank slowly, feeling very happy and relaxed. Zhu toasting said, "Life is about enjoying oneself, when will one become wealthy and powerful?" Qin smiled and said, "A mouse drinking from the Yellow River, it's only enough to fill its belly. Just drinking is not enough for enjoyment. I have a wonderful person, if you see her, you will think I am old in this place!" Zhu asked what kind of person she was, but Qin hesitated and did not tell the truth. Zhu insisted on asking, and Qin finally whispered, "I have been staying in this garden for two years now. I have met a beautiful woman who is just seventeen years old. Since you arrived, our encounters have become less frequent, but every time during the moonlit nights or when you are drunk and dreaming, I have never failed to hold her hand. Because you treat me well, I dare to reveal my innermost thoughts, please do not tell anyone else!" Zhu said, "I am just joking. If it's true, then she is either a fox or a ghost, how could one be close to them?" Qin said, "If she is indeed a fox, not a ghost. A fox with a beauty comparable to that of the goddess of the moon, and talent equal to that of Xie Daoyun, how could there be any reason not to be close to her!"

    204

    褚终属少年，血气未定，且被酒兴高，力求一见之。秦有难色，褚款语相央，至于屈膝。秦莞尔曰：“见之亦无不可，弟未卜丽人许否，姑试之，以观兄之缘。”乃起身绕出迴廊湖山下，轻声唤“怜姐”者三，于是女子分花步月，冉冉而至，丰姿绰约，美丽非常，目所未睹。著碧罗画衣，曳练裙，秋波流慧，莲靥生潮，含羞睨褚而责秦曰：“小酸子！谓我不敢见此书痴耶？”褚面□口讷，勉强揖之。秦曰：“褚十二兄面嫩，怜姐勿笑之。”女曰：“此非面嫩，乃良心现也。

    Explanation

    204

    Chu, still a young man, had not yet stabilized his blood and qi, and being drunk with high spirits, he insisted on seeing her. Qin showed reluctance, but Chu spoke earnestly and pleaded, even bowing down. Qin smiled slightly and said, "Seeing her is also not impossible, but I cannot predict whether the beautiful lady will agree. Let's try it, to see my elder brother's fortune." He then rose and walked around to the lakeside under the winding corridor, calling out "Lian Sister" three times softly. The woman, walking among flowers and under the moonlight, gradually approached. She had a graceful and elegant appearance, extremely beautiful, never seen before. She wore a green silk embroidered robe and dragged a white silk skirt. Her eyes were like autumn waves, full of wisdom, and her cheeks like lotus blossoms, blushing. She looked at Chu shyly and scolded Qin, saying, "Little pedant! Do you think I am too shy to see this bookworm?" Chu's face turned red and he was speechless, bowing awkwardly. Qin said, "Chu's face is still young, Lian Sister, don't laugh at him." The woman replied, "This is not just a young face, but a manifestation of good conscience."

    205

    岂似尔天良尽丧，毫不知羞耻哉！”乃相与入轩，见酒具，笑曰：“二酸子，人家儿子娶妇，此际正好扪結，尔等乃收取　余，滋润馋吻，恭喜今夜得两枚饱嗉矣！”秦曰：“既不能作东道主，奈何相嘲？”女曰：“尔诚旅店蜰虫，欲谋食客矣，适从六姊处食羊桃，留得数枚，出以奉人可乎？”秦曰：“甚妙！”女因手袖中出一金镶椰瓢，盛羊桃五枚，鲜如初摘。蜀中固无是物，不测所自。二生分食，甚甘，既而珍馔芳醪，悉于瓢中出之，罗列满案。酣饮间，忽目褚曰：“观子芳姿淑质，自足撞破迷楼，第千叶桃花，早荣早落，华而不实，理有固然。会须行乐及时，何可株待？”于是笑言款洽，游语渐浸。褚神迷不能自主，秦从旁颇形妒色。女睨之而笑曰：“小酸子！真是醋葫芦也。凡人萍水相遭，逢场作戏，何足介意！若少时新妇之事，罗家郎又将何以为情乎？”秦问少时新妇有何事，女曰：“行当自见耳。”

    Explanation

    205

    "How can you have lost all sense of right and wrong, and not know shame at all?" They then entered the pavilion together and saw the wine utensils, laughing and saying, "Two little snots, when someone's son marries a wife, this is the perfect time to grab the leftovers, you two are taking what's left over, satisfying your greedy mouths, congratulations, you will get two full stomachs tonight!" Qin said, "Since we can't be the host, why are you mocking us?" The woman said, "You are truly parasites in a hotel, seeking food for guests, I just ate some sheep peaches from my sixth sister's place and have a few left, can I offer them to you?" Qin said, "That's great!" The woman then took a gold-embellished coconut ladle from her sleeve, filled it with five fresh sheep peaches, as if they had just been picked. There are no such things in Shu, and it's unclear where they came from. The two young men shared the peaches, finding them very delicious. Soon after, fine food and fragrant wine were all taken out from the ladle, arranged on the table. While drinking heartily, she suddenly looked at Chu and said, "Your beautiful appearance and graceful demeanor are enough to break through a maze, but like a thousand-petaled peach blossom that blooms and falls early, beautiful but lacking substance, this is a natural law. It's important to enjoy pleasure in due time, why wait passively?" Thus, they laughed and talked amicably, their conversation gradually deepening. Chu was dazed and could not control himself, Qin showed signs of jealousy from the side. The woman glanced at him and laughed, "Little snot! You're truly a sour gourd. When people meet by chance, they act on the spur of the moment, why worry about it! If there are matters related to a newly married wife, what will Luo's husband do about it?" Qin asked what matters there were for a newly married wife, the woman said, "You will see for yourself."

    206

    有顷，蓦闻人声鼎沸，园树皆红，人出视之，则庖人失火，延及洞房，坊正官军，咸来扑救，亲邻渐集，蚁聚蜂屯。家口数十人，幸皆未寝，独新妇与罗氏子，身无寸缕，股栗庭前，映着火光，纤毫毕见，二生不能正视。女乃至前解罗衣裹之，掖归别室。诸姑伯姊，接踵来慰，抢攘间已失女之所在。众以为邻女闺中去矣。唯秦褚二生知之，但缄密不言耳。自此，褚无夕不与晤对，相得甚欢，然终未及乱。盖褚既腼腆，而女亦有贞操，一似韦崟之与任氏也。及秦生从其父归成都，女泣别，不复再至。又二年，顾死，褚扶枢归乡，未及获隽。次岁成进士，工部观政，未娶而卒，年甫二十有四云。

    Explanation

    206

    After a while, suddenly a loud commotion was heard, and the garden trees were ablaze with red flames. People rushed out to see what was happening, and it turned out that a cook had started a fire, which quickly spread to the bridal chamber. Local officials and soldiers came to extinguish the fire, and family and neighbors gradually gathered, like ants and bees swarming together. There were dozens of family members, fortunately none of whom had gone to bed. Only the newlywed wife and the young man from the Luo family were completely naked, shivering in fear in front of the courtyard, their every detail visible in the firelight. The two young men could not bear to look at them. The girl then went forward, took off her robe, and wrapped them in it, helping them back to a separate room. Aunts and elder sisters came one after another to comfort them, but in the chaos, the girl was lost. Everyone thought she had gone back to her room. Only the two young men from Qin and Chu knew what had happened, but they kept it a secret. From then on, the young man from Chu met with her every night, and they got along very well and were very happy together, but they never went too far. It was because the young man was bashful, and the girl also had virtue, just like Wei Yin and Ren Shi. When the young man from Qin followed his father back to Chengdu, the girl parted with tears and did not return. Two years later, she died suddenly, and the young man from Chu carried her coffin back to his hometown, but he did not have time to achieve success. The next year, he passed the imperial examination and served as an official in the Ministry of Works, but he died unmarried at the age of twenty-four.

    207

    闲斋曰：酸子所以多妒者，穷其故无他，闭户守妻子若将终身，本无远大志耳。贞狐与褚，不过话言形色之间涉于狎亵，书痴而形诸色，何所见之小也！

    　　兰岩曰：守身贞，见理明，出词雅，比狐不多得。

    　　鞠慕周最善说狐，不能悉记。其有奇者，足发一大噱。言其客关中时，因事之扶风。所识有丁孝廉者，年近四十，断三弦，子女皆幼，号啼绕膝。不耐鳏居，仍谋胶续，屡乖所愿。丁素究心神仙之事，精于导引，每澄心枯坐，吐故纳新，则见一黑狐对面蹲踞，瞠目相向。丁叱之，即刻奔逸，如此者屡矣。亦习不为怪。一夜方坐，觉有人登榻，与己并肩坐，衣香袭人。丁自念此皆妄虑之招，心不动则魔何由生，任之可也。于是垂目息心，凝然不动。

    Explanation

    207

    Xianzai said: The reason acid people are often jealous is simply that they close the door and guard their wives and children as if they will stay forever, originally without any grand ambitions. The chaste fox and Chu are nothing more than words and expressions that involve familiarity or intimacy; a bookworm who shows it in their appearance, how small is their vision!

    Lanyan said: To maintain one's chastity, to understand principles clearly, to speak or write in an elegant manner, such a fox is not easily found.

    Ju Mu Zhou is the best at telling stories about foxes, but he cannot fully remember. Those that are particularly interesting, enough to cause a big laugh. Speaking of when he was a guest in Guanzhong, because of some matter in Fufeng, there was a scholar named Ding who was known to him, nearly forty years old, skilled in playing a three-stringed lute, with young children who cried around his knees. Unable to bear being a widower, still planning to remarry, repeatedly failing to achieve his wishes. Ding had always been interested in immortality and spiritual matters, skilled in guiding and leading practices. Often meditating in a state of emptiness, expelling old and inhaling new, then he saw a black fox sitting opposite him, staring at each other. Ding scolded it, and it immediately fled away; this happened many times. He also got used to it and no longer found it strange. One night, he was sitting, and he felt someone climb onto the bed, sitting next to him. The fragrance of her clothes was overwhelming. Ding thought to himself that all this was the result of his own delusions; if the mind does not move, how can demons arise? It can be left to its own devices. So he closed his eyes and calmed his mind, remaining motionless.

    208

    既而其人以颊偎腮，寻以口亲吻，粉香脂腻，肌滑如脂。丁不能复耐，张目瞩之，则二八丽人，光采耀目，睨丁而笑。丁曰：“吾固知汝为向日狐，奈何扰人功课，可速去！否则惹老拳，非善知识矣！”女犹掩口嗤嗤，俄延不去。丁躁甚，以足蹴之，颠堕床下。旋即起立，忿忿整衣，曰：“鲁莽如此，岂复读书人行径耶？儿去不复再来，汝其勿悔！”丁鞠拱而谢曰：“深感厚谊，敢云悔乎？”女曰：“从此虽焚香叩头，祈我再至，恐亦不能矣！”丁哂曰：“永不敢启动矣。”女不顾而去。

    Explanation

    208

    Soon after, the woman leaned her cheek against his, and soon after, she kissed him. Her skin was fragrant and smooth, like butter. Ding could not bear it any longer, and he opened his eyes to look at her. There, he saw a beautiful woman of sixteen, whose radiance dazzled his eyes. She looked at him sideways and smiled.
