Ding said, "I knew you were the fox from before. Why are you disturbing my studies? You can leave quickly! Otherwise, you'll get hit by an old man, and that's not a good friend!" The woman still covered her mouth and giggled, delaying her departure. Ding became very impatient and kicked her with his foot, causing her to fall off the bed. She immediately stood up, angrily adjusting her clothes, and said, "So reckless, is this the behavior of a scholar? I will leave and never come back, you had better not regret it!" Ding bowed and apologized, saying, "I deeply appreciate your kindness. How could I regret it?" The woman replied, "Even if you burn incense and bow, praying for me to come again, I doubt I can." Ding laughed and said, "I will never do it again." The woman left without looking back.

    209

    越数日，丁晚浴于房，又见女搴湘帘入，笑曰：“我又来观汝裸浴矣。”丁不应，女蹲身其旁，以手抚之，曰：“背上垢厚二寸矣，我为汝擦之，可乎？”丁心大动，胯间物翘然而举，女格格笑不止，戏批其颊作小响曰：“何物书迂，轻薄乃尔！不怕污却人家女儿眼目耶？”丁阴计：学道人岂可逞欲，况明知是狐，何故动心？因瞋目大怒，奋拳挥之，中鼻，女负痛滚地，唧唧哀鸣，冲帘而遁，继此不复再至。

    Explanation

    209

    After several days, Ding was taking a bath in his room when he saw the girl lift the Xiang curtain and enter, laughing and saying, "I've come again to watch you bathe naked." Ding did not respond, and the girl knelt beside him, touching his back and saying, "The dirt on your back is as thick as two inches. Let me clean it for you, shall I?" Ding's heart raced, and his loins stirred. The girl laughed uncontrollably, playfully slapping his cheek and saying, "What kind of bookish person are you, so frivolous! Aren't you afraid of tainting the eyes of other girls?" Ding thought to himself: "A scholar cannot indulge in desires, especially knowing it is a fox. Why did I get aroused?" He glared angrily and punched her, hitting her nose. The girl rolled on the ground in pain, whimpering softly, rushed through the curtain, and fled. After this, she did not come again.

    210

    丁家业素封，儿女虽各有阿保，而衣食会计，终苦内助无人，更嘱冰人，遍觅佳遇。一日，有媒媪来，言有卞大户者，家资百万，一女十八矣，慧美贤淑，世罕其匹。君读书人，多疑少信，固多以媒妁为妄，但唤一女眷往相之，便足证吾言不谬。丁以为然，央姑母及寡嫂同诣卞宅，周视动止，真仙中人也。欣喜而归，盛夸其色。咸谓阅人多矣，未见有如此女之艳者，宁独吾乡，虽天下独步可也。丁大悦，即日纳聘。及奠雁，亲故满堂，希冀一面。入房合卺，乍睹艳绝，审谛之，非他，即向之狐女也。

    Explanation

    210

    The family of Ding was rich without official rank. Although their children had their own guardians, they still struggled with household management due to the lack of a capable female helper. They entrusted the matchmaker to search extensively for a good match. One day, an old woman came and said, "There is a wealthy family named Bian with a fortune of a million. They have a daughter who is eighteen years old, intelligent, beautiful, virtuous, and kind, rarely matched in the world. You are an educated person, skeptical and not easily trusting, but if you call a female relative to see her, it will prove my words to be true." Ding agreed and asked his aunt and his widow sister-in-law to visit the Bian family. They observed her movements and behavior, and she was truly a person of extraordinary beauty and virtue. They returned happily, greatly praising her appearance. They said, "Not just in our village, even in the whole world, she is unparalleled." Ding was very pleased and immediately accepted the dowry. When the wedding ceremony began, with relatives and friends filling the room, everyone hoped for a glimpse of her. On the wedding night, as they entered the room and shared the wine cup, at first glance, she was stunningly beautiful. Upon closer examination, she was not someone else, but the fox girl from before.

    211

    丁大骇，叩之，女笑曰：“儿非无益于君者，君道念已坚，成功可冀，然尚有要诀，不无梦梦，儿来当循循善诱，同登仙籍，不亦可乎？”媒媪从旁挽说曰：“姻缘自有天定，新郎无更拘泥。”丁大怒，提扊扅击之，媪与女破窗而走，丁出户逐之，已失所在。亟命燃炬大索，得诸厕中。咸大哗，并力奋击，厕中人提裤惊呼，颠扑于地，烛之，非狐，盖丁之侄妇与寡嫂也，污秽满身，伤痕遍体。举室索然，舁之以归。次日，同往卞家，无复第宅，但见楸梧数本，古墓数坯而已。自此狐祟遂绝。鞠在秦与丁交厚，闻其自述如此。

    　　兰岩曰：人谓儒者多迂，而丁卒以迂而卫道，诚非真迂也。

    Explanation

    211

    Ding was greatly alarmed and asked it. The woman smiled and said, "I am not without benefit to you. Your resolve has become firm, and success can be hoped for. However, there are still key principles that are not entirely clear. I will guide you gently and patiently, and together we can ascend to the ranks of immortals, which is not a bad outcome, is it?" The matchmaker, standing by, persuaded, saying, "Marriage is determined by heaven; the groom should not be too rigid." Ding became furious and lifted the door bolt to strike them. The matchmaker and the woman broke through the window and fled. Ding rushed out of the house to chase them but lost sight of them. He immediately ordered a torch lit and searched thoroughly. They were found in the toilet. Everyone was greatly alarmed and struck them together. The person in the toilet, holding up her pants and crying out in shock, fell to the ground. When they examined her, she was not a fox but Ding's niece-in-law and widowed sister-in-law, filthy and bruised all over. The entire household was silent and desolate, and they carried her back home. The next day, they went together to Bian's house, but there was no longer a mansion; they saw only a few ginkgo trees and ancient tombstones. From then on, the fox's mischief ceased. Ju in Qin was close friends with Ding and heard his account of these events.

    Lan Yan said: People say that Confucian scholars are often rigid, but Ding, in the end, protected the way through his rigidity, which is truly not truly stubborn.

    212

    薛鲁园谓：此皆不奇，奇莫奇于宛邱之狐矣。宛邱牧李公，有女及笄，风致焉然，为狐所据，夫人深以为忧。时郡有女巫，颇能制邪。适李公入省，夫人延巫至署，告以所苦，使驱除之。巫大言曰：“此何难，不过致夫人破数十贯钱耳。请今夜即为夫人除之，务使小妖狐吃个大苦。”夫人喜，厚款而去。晡时，偕其徒负鼓囊而致，设坛于园。夫人率婢妇隐屏后观之。方禹步间，大风骤起，飞尘迷目，而烛不灭。俄见四五少年，提木杵逼近案侧，仆师徒三人于地，褫其裙裈，各以木杵塞阴中。咸附掌曰：“请先吃个大苦！”夫人大惧，急命人往救厥巫。巫已自拔木杵，蹶然而兴。夫人慰之曰：“贤师徒吃苦甚矣。”巫萎顿劻勷而前，犹勉强作笑颜曰：“此亦大快乐事，夫人奈何道苦？”二徒尤惫，猩红满衣，数婢扶掖而至。巫回顾嘱之曰：“此血衣最难得，归去须珍藏之。”夫人问藏之何为，巫曰：“藏之可辟妖魅。”夫人大笑，谴之。

    Explanation

    212

    Xue Lu Yuan said: All these are not strange, nothing is more extraordinary than the fox of Wanyou. The magistrate of Wanyou, Li Gong, had a daughter who had come of age, and she was graceful and charming. She was possessed by a fox, and the lady deeply worried about it. At that time, there was a female witch in the county who could control evil spirits. When Li Gong went to the capital, the lady invited the witch to the magistrate's office and told her about her troubles, asking her to drive out the spirit. The witch loudly declared: "This is not difficult, it will only cost the lady a few tens of strings of cash. I will drive out the little fox spirit tonight, and make it suffer greatly." The lady was pleased and treated her generously before she departed. In the afternoon, the witch arrived with her disciples, carrying drums and bags, and set up a ritual platform in the garden. The lady and her maids hid behind a screen to watch. Just as the witch was performing the ritual, a strong wind suddenly arose, and the flying dust blinded their eyes, but the candle did not go out. Soon, a few young men carrying wooden mallets approached the table, knocked the three disciples to the ground, stripped off their skirts and undergarments, and each used a wooden mallet to plug their private parts. They all clapped their hands and said: "Please let us suffer greatly first!" The lady was greatly afraid and quickly ordered someone to rescue the witch. The witch had already pulled out the wooden mallets and stood up abruptly. The lady consoled her, saying: "Your disciples suffered greatly." The witch, limping and staggering forward, still managed a weak smile and said: "This is also a great pleasure, why do you say it is painful?" The two disciples were especially exhausted, their clothes stained with bright red blood, and several maids helped them to the platform. The witch turned back and told them: "This bloody clothes are very rare, you must treasure them when you return home." The lady asked why they should keep them, and the witch replied: "Keeping them can ward off evil spirits." The lady laughed and dismissed them.

    213

    兰岩曰：或曰女巫大言不惭，致招此报，不知愚夫愚妇，不足深责。所不可解者，文人学士，亦往往不免，恨无木杵以塞之也。

    　　慕周拊髀曰：“是诚奇文也。然余所闻某教授之事，亦罕遘哉。友人某为某县教授，学宫素多狐。莅任方数日，即有投刺者，署‘治下胡万龄顿首拜’。及接见，则皤然一翁，长三尺余，神气清爽，飘然若仙，对之起敬。自言本晋人，流寓于此，近百年矣。今有事将楚游，以公长者，敢以家口寄托。某知其为狐，竟诺之，翁拜谢而去。晡时，举族皆至，约二十余人。某延入内室，款洽甚至。翁深感其谊，举酒相嘱曰：”老少数十指，悉仰矞云之庇，他日归，当图厚报。‘某素豪迈，掀髯笑曰：“翁第行勿忧，宝眷必不致失所。’翁感荷之色可掬。次日，束装就道。某官闲俸薄，及有此义举，薪水不继于庖，而毫不介意。

    Explanation

    213

    Lanyan said: Some say that a female witch boasts without shame, inviting this retribution, but I do not believe that foolish men and women deserve severe blame. What is not understandable is that scholars and literati also often cannot escape this, and I regret that there is no wooden pestle to block it.

    Muzhou clapped his thigh and said: "This is indeed a remarkable piece. However, what I have heard about a certain professor's story is also rare indeed. A friend of mine is the professor of a certain county, and the school has always had many foxes. Just a few days after taking office, there was someone who presented a visiting card, signed 'under my jurisdiction, Hu Wanling, respectfully bows.' Upon meeting, there was an elderly man with a white beard, over three feet tall, clear and refreshing spirit, floating as if a fairy, one feels respect for him. He said he was originally from the Jin state, living here as a guest for nearly a hundred years. Now he has business to go to Chu, since you are a respected person, dare to entrust my family to you. I knew he was a fox spirit, so I agreed to it. The old man bowed and thanked me before leaving. In the afternoon, the whole family came, about twenty people. I invited them into my inner room, treating them with great hospitality. The old man deeply appreciated our kindness, raised a cup and said: 'My dozens of fingers all rely on your protection. When I return, I will repay you handsomely.' I am usually bold and open, laughed and said: 'Just go, don't worry. Your family will certainly not be destitute.' The old man's gratitude was visible on his face. The next day, he packed and set off. I have a low salary and a leisurely position, and with this act of kindness, food and drink were scarce in the kitchen, but I did not care at all."

    214

    翁二子三女，皆妖艳绝伦，时来某内寝，亲昵如父子。某眷属悉不在署中，唯次子随行，方弱冠，资质过于中人，每见三女辄避去，不接一谈。女向某间入以游语，某遽正色责之，女惭谢而去，数日不敢复至。公子偶过内宅，遇少女小遗阶下，笑而挑之，公子俯首引避，佯若不见不闻。如此者岁余，始终如一日。翁既归，再拜而谢曰：“贤乔梓真异人也，无可为酬，谨奉画一轴为公寿。‘某欣然受之。迟数日，翁率其族辞去，遂不复晤。阅其画，画极平平，唯画一翁一妪，正面并坐，酷似人家影像，不足赏鉴，置之而已。会三年考绩，学使者以某年老勒休致。某宦囊羞涩，羁滞不能归。

    214

    The old man had two sons and three daughters, all of whom were extraordinarily beautiful and charming. They would often visit the official's inner quarters, treating him with the closeness of father and son. The official's family members were all absent from the office, except for his second son, who was just reaching adulthood and was more talented than the average person. Whenever he saw the three daughters, he would avoid them and not engage in conversation. One day, when the daughters entered the inner quarters and spoke playfully, the official sternly reprimanded them. The daughters felt ashamed, apologized, and left, daring not to return for several days. One day, the young man happened to pass by the inner quarters and saw a young girl accidentally urinate on the steps. He laughed and teased her, but she lowered her head and turned away, pretending not to see or hear him. This went on for over a year, and it remained the same every day.

    After the old man returned, he bowed twice and said with thanks, "You and your son are truly extraordinary people. There is nothing I can offer as a reward, so I humbly present this painting as a birthday gift for you." The official gladly accepted it. After several days, the old man led his family away, and they never met again. When the official examined the painting, he found it quite ordinary, depicting only an old man and an old woman sitting face to face, closely resembling a family portrait. It was not worth appreciating, so he put it aside and left it there. When the three-year performance review came up, the provincial examiner forced him to retire due to his old age. The official was financially embarrassed and stranded, unable to return home.

    215

    无意坐香肆中闲话，瞥见一人，停舆入肆，胖体重颐，衣冠济楚，仆从如云。肆主接待甚恭。某欲避之，其人挽留再四，乃叙坐，各道姓名。其人鞠躬曰：”弟张太学也，世为鹾商。豚儿某在庠，公识之否？“某曰：”是即公郎耶？名下士也。’张大喜，延至其家。登堂拜父，某谛视之，其貌如所得画中翁，逼肖。虽异之，犹不甚为意。越旬余，张父死，求画师写真，数易人，无能有仿佛于万一者。某因出画示张，张展轴大骇，且拜且哭，告某曰：“不特亡父传神酷肖，先慈弃世二十年，何对此亦宛若生前也？敢请其故。‘某备述得画之由，张叹曰：”此狐借我，欲厚赠公，以报德也。狐有施于吾甚重，可不体其意以报长者乎？’乃取画，赠以千金，某始得携子归里。迄今犹素封也。“

    Explanation

    215

    Sitting idly in a tea house, I was chatting when I glanced at someone who stopped his carriage and entered the shop. The man was plump with a heavy jaw, dressed neatly, and accompanied by a retinue as numerous as clouds. The shop owner received him with great respect. I wanted to avoid him, but he insisted on keeping me, so we sat down and introduced ourselves. The man bowed and said, "I am Zhang, a scholar, and my family has been in the salt trade for generations. My son is in school, do you know him?" I replied, "Is that your son? He is indeed a worthy scholar." Zhang was greatly pleased and invited me to his home. We visited his father in the hall, and I examined him closely. His appearance was exactly like the old man in the painting I had, a close resemblance. Although I was surprised, I did not pay much attention. After about ten days, Zhang's father died. He sought a painter to create a portrait, but several painters failed to capture even a semblance of his appearance. I then showed him the painting, and Zhang was greatly startled. He bowed and wept, telling me, "Not only does the painting capture my father's spirit so accurately, but my late mother, who passed away twenty years ago, looks as if she is still alive in this image. Please tell me how you obtained this painting." I fully explained the origin of the painting. Zhang sighed and said, "This fox borrowed the painting from me, wanting to give you a generous gift in return for your kindness. The fox has done much for me; how could I not follow its intention to repay you?" He then took the painting and gave me a thousand gold coins. I was finally able to take my son back to my hometown. Until now, I am still a wealthy person.

    216

    闲斋曰：一画也，致三人各了心愿，狐之术亦巧且幻矣。然奇不害正，宜其安处学宫，不遭驱逐也。

    　　兰岩曰：薪水不继，毫不介怀；妖丽相挑，终不及乱。教授父子其享厚报也，固宜。

    Explanation

    216

    Master Xianzai said: With a single stroke, the fox fulfilled the wishes of three people, its trickery being both clever and magical. Yet, the strange does not harm the proper, so it is appropriate that it remains safe in the school and is not expelled.

    Master Lan Yan said: Even if the firewood and water supply is cut off, it is not at all concerned; even if the beautiful and seductive are tempting, it does not go so far as to cause chaos. The professor and his son will surely receive great rewards, which is certainly fitting.

    217

    韩　樾　子令狐韩樾子，虽世为贾人，而丰姿娇媚，善赋，能诗词，尤工弦管。弱冠，游京师，独乘骏骡，出市陉道上，值雨，见一少年妇，色绝艳，跨蹇驴，或前或后，与韩同路行。晡时雨愈洪，道旁适有坏屋数椽，空无人居，韩暂入避之，妇亦踵至。韩颇不自安，然无如之何，姑听之。既而骏驴见骡，厥势昂举，耸鼻而鸣。妇视韩掩袖而笑，韩心动不可遏，阴念日暮人稀，郊鲁男子若何为？固挑之曰：“驴犹如此，人何以堪？小娘笑厥物之不雅观也，抑之更有甚于此者？”妇怒之以目曰：“我自笑之可笑耳，不谋与汝扳谈！”韩跪而拥之曰：“念此邂逅，实天假之缘，途中倾慕之私，卿喻之否？”妇粲然，曳之令起，曰：“儿苟无意于子，何为履子之迹。入此颓圮之地乎？儿家即在直北乔木处，去此仅十余里，然不欲与子偕归者，猥以舅姑性严，良人及伯叔亦皆正直。母家匪远，盍枉驾见辱，虽险不足虑也。”

    Explanation

    217

    Han Yuezi, though born into a family of merchants, possessed a graceful and charming appearance, excelled in composing poetry and prose, and was particularly skilled in playing musical instruments. At the age of twenty, he traveled to the capital city, riding a swift mule along the road leading to the market area. It began to rain, and he encountered a young woman with exceptionally beautiful features, who was riding a slow and clumsy donkey, moving either ahead or behind him. They were traveling on the same road. By the late afternoon, the rain had become heavier, and there happened to be a dilapidated house by the roadside, empty and unoccupied. Han temporarily entered the house to take shelter, and the woman followed shortly after. Han felt quite uneasy but had no choice but to accept the situation. Soon, the donkey, seeing the mule, raised its posture and snorted. The woman looked at Han, laughed while covering her mouth with a sleeve, and Han's heart was stirred, unable to suppress his feelings. He secretly thought that it was late in the evening and there were few people around, and wondered what a man in a secluded area would do. He deliberately teased her, saying, "Even the donkey is reacting this way, how could a person handle a situation worse than this? Are you laughing at the donkey's unattractive appearance, or is there something even worse?" The woman glared at him and said, "I am just laughing at something funny, not planning to talk to you!" Han knelt down and embraced her, saying, "Think of this as a chance encounter ordained by fate. My private admiration during the journey, do you understand?" The woman smiled brightly, pulled him up, and said, "If I had no interest in you, why would I follow your footsteps and enter this dilapidated place? My home is located in a place with tall trees to the north, only about ten miles away. However, I don't want to go back with you because my in-laws are strict, and my husband and uncles are also upright. My mother's home is not far away. Why don't you come and visit me? Even though it is dangerous, it is not a concern."

    218

    韩为摇惑已久，不复三思，遂控骡，随之以往。行入万山中，跋履迍邅，约数十里，始达其处。千峰环抱，万木森罗，靠涧依山，得一巨宅，四面别无人居。韩疑之而未发，妇已知之。笑曰：“子疑儿家无邻比乎？盖祖父辟世者也，居此近百年矣，凡人罕得至此。正可与子盘桓，勿忖度也。”亟弃鞍以马箠挝门，有二婢出应，双髻垂髫，色丽齿稚，妇以“小红”“小绿”呼之。登堂，轮奂之美如王侯。

    Explanation

    218

    Han had been swayed by confusion for a long time and had ceased to think carefully. He then took control of the donkey and followed along. They entered into the midst of countless mountains, with a difficult and obstructed journey, covering about several dozen miles before finally reaching the place. Thousand peaks encircled, ten thousand trees densely arranged, leaning on a ravine and relying on a mountain, they found a large mansion, with no one else living around it. Han doubted it but did not say anything, while the woman already knew it. She smiled and said, "Do you doubt that my family has no neighbors? It is because my ancestors withdrew from the world, and they have lived here for nearly a hundred years. Few people can come here. It is exactly suitable for you to stay with me, do not hesitate." Quickly dismounting, she hit the door with the whip, and two maids came out to answer, with twin buns and hanging hair, beautiful and young. The woman called them "Little Red" and "Little Green." Entering the hall, the grandeur and beauty were like those of a king or noble.

    219

    妇易衣而出，锦裙绣袴　，绰约如仙子，与前策蹇冒雨时，什佰增也。又为韩易衣履，亦极鲜华。韩西向拱立，请尊人拜见。妇曰：“儿幼孤，失怙已十年矣，更无兄弟，唯一姐一妹，各适所天，此间为儿独居，无可见客者，子勿复以礼自拘。”乃相携入闺闼，闺中位置，精奇雅洁，又为改观，几案皆檀楠，炉瓶悉金玉，北设钿榻，南列蜃窗，东壁悬古画，西壁悬合欢图也，联为董思白书。厅上置金猊，□异香，地平如镜，不染纤毫尘翳。妇捺之使坐，小婢沏茗，茗尤香美，一旗一盏，不识何名。

    Explanation

    219

    The woman changed her clothes and emerged, wearing brocade skirts and embroidered trousers, graceful and ethereal like a fairy, a hundred times more beautiful than when she had ridden a slow horse in the rain before. She also changed Han's clothes and shoes, which were also extremely fresh and splendid. Han stood with hands clasped and facing west, asking the respected person to pay respects. The woman said, "My child was orphaned at a young age and has lost her father for ten years; she has no brothers, only an older sister and a younger sister, each married to her own fate. Here she lives alone, with no one to entertain guests. Do not bind yourself with formalities." She then led them together into the inner chamber. The arrangement inside the room was exquisite, strange, elegant, and clean, offering a new perspective. The tables and desks were all made of sandalwood and camphorwood, and the incense burners and vases were all made of gold and jade. A lacquered couch was set up in the north, and a shell window was arranged in the south. An ancient painting hung on the east wall, and a picture of a pair of mandarin ducks hung on the west wall, with couplets written by Dong Sibai. On the hall, there was a gold lion incense burner, emitting a strange fragrance. The floor was as smooth as a mirror, free of the slightest dust. The woman gently pressed him to sit, and a young maid brewed tea, which was especially fragrant and delicious, served in a flag and a cup, but the name of the tea was unknown.

    220

    韩问何姓，适何人，青春几何，妇笑曰：“琐琐根究，得勿志之以告所欢也？”韩笑曰：“予虽为客，而年甫二九花柳之事，实所未谙，且赋性孤清，守如处子，今与卿眷恋，亦发轫之始，所以琐琐渎询者，欲心铭弗谖也。何事见疑。”妇曰：“勿面急，聊以相戏耳。”因言：“姓韦氏，字阿娟，行二，年二十。初适阜平元氏子，三年前，元不谨于行，阖门为仇家所歼，儿从间道逃归，仅以身免，孀居于此。同胞一姊，字阿妍，嫁上党。

    Explanation

    220

    Han asked what her surname was, to whom she was married, and how old she was. The woman laughed and said, "Why are you asking such trivial questions? Are you not going to remember it and tell your beloved?" Han smiled and replied, "Although I am a guest, I am only nineteen and have no experience with romantic matters. Moreover, I am naturally solitary and pure, like a maiden. Now that I am with you, it is also the beginning of a new journey. The reason I am asking so many questions is that I want to engrave it in my heart and never forget. Why do you doubt me?" The woman said, "Don't get angry; I was just teasing you." She then said, "My surname is Wei, my name is A Juan, I am the second daughter, and I am twenty years old. I was first married to a son of the Yuan family from Fu Ping three years ago. The Yuan family was not careful in their conduct, and their entire family was killed by their enemies. I escaped through a secret path and returned alone, managing to survive. I am now a widow here. I have an elder sister named A Yan, who is married to someone from Shangdang."

    221

    妹字阿秀，嫁灵邱，与子同庚，今将往探之，不意遇子，非夙缘乌能如是？”韩曰：“然则舅姑性严，诸昆正直之说，胡为而云然也？”娟笑曰：“亦饰说也。”韩亦笑曰：“卿尚有一毫诚实哉？相聚才半日，诳言已足够一车矣。”二婢亦笑。有顷，珍异毕陈，觥筹交错。娟则娇痴宛转，软倚轻偎；韩则熨贴殷勤，凝注倾倒。俄而三星在户，移烛登床，至于衾裯枕席间，其事不可竟究矣。娟善吴歈，每发声，音响靡靡，韩发吭和之，两心如醉。居匝月，不离跬步，日惟劈笺斗酒，坐月茵花，温柔乡景味，备细领略。

    Explanation

    221

    Sister Axiu, married to Lingqiu, is the same age as your son. I am now going to visit her, but unexpectedly met you. Without a predestined connection, how could it be like this?" Han replied, "Then why do they say that the mother-in-law and father-in-law are strict, and the brothers are upright?" Juan laughed and said, "It's also just a fabricated story." Han also laughed and said, "Do you still have a bit of honesty? We've been together for only half a day, and the false stories are already enough to fill a cart." The two maids also laughed. Soon, all the delicacies and rarities were laid out, and cups and chopsticks were intermingled. Juan was cute and coy, gently leaning and lightly hugging; Han was warm and attentive, focusing intently and being captivated. Soon, three stars were in the window, and the candle was moved to the bed. In the bed and bedding, the matter cannot be fully described. Juan was good at singing Wu songs, and every time she sang, the sound was soft and gentle. Han joined in with a deep voice, and both hearts were drunk. They lived for a month, never more than a step apart, only splitting the paper and drinking wine, sitting under the moon and flowers, fully experiencing the flavor of the tender and warm scene.

    222

    一日，娟复往探其姐，韩独倚栏观鱼，适小红送茶至，韩戏捻其腕，红微笑睨之曰：“小娘甫出门，郎君便尔放浪耶？”韩抱持之，曰：“古人谓秀色可餐，若卿者可以疗饥矣。”随探手于怀，肌肤腻不留手，胸乳椒发，情不可禁，遂与绸缪。兴未阑，小绿猝至，不及掩饰。绿却步含笑，佯作采花，韩知其可狎，以手招之，绿齿尤稚，反走欲逃。韩置红迫之，将及，忽闻院外笑语声，呖呖如莺燕。绿且走且回首笑曰：“郎勿嬲，小娘归矣！”韩亦敛步。寻闻叩环声，绿徐徐理鬓纳履，启扉视之，笑而扬声曰：“秀姨何久不临贶，玉体得勿少郗耶？”小红继至，问小绿与谁语，绿曰：“红姐不亟来起居耶？灵邱秀姨来矣！”红两颊红晕，再拜曰：“小娘往上党，未克言旋，秀姨可居此以候之。”韩隐玉兰花下偷窥，则一倩妆少艾，扶一女奴，冉冉而入，冶容丽色，不可正视。韩目炫心摇，知为阿秀，无计迴避，不得已径前揖之。

    Explanation

    222

    One day, Juan went again to visit her sister. Han alone leaned on the railing, watching the fish. Just as Xiao Hong brought tea, Han playfully pinched her wrist. Xiao Hong smiled and glanced at him, saying, "Little mistress just left home, and already you are so unrestrained?" Han embraced her, saying, "Ancient people said that beautiful colors are edible; if you were like this, you could cure hunger." He then reached into his sleeve, feeling her smooth skin that left no trace, her plump breasts like red peppers. Emotion could not be restrained, so they engaged in intimate activities. Their passion was not yet spent when Xiao Lu suddenly arrived, unable to hide. Xiao Lu took a step back, smiling, pretending to pick flowers. Han knew she could be teased and waved his hand to call her. Xiao Lu, still very young, ran away trying to escape. Han ignored Xiao Hong and chased after her. As he was about to catch her, he suddenly heard laughter outside the courtyard, clear and melodious like the chirping of orioles and swallows. Xiao Lu, while running, turned back and laughed, saying, "Don't tease me, the little mistress is returning!" Han also stopped walking. Soon he heard the sound of the doorbell. Xiao Lu slowly combed her hair and put on her shoes, then opened the door and saw her, smiling and raising her voice, saying, "Aunt Xiu, why have you not visited for so long? Is your body unwell?" Xiao Hong came next and asked Xiao Lu who she was talking to. Xiao Lu said, "Isn't it time for you to come and take care of me? Aunt Xiu from Lingqiu has come!" Xiao Hong's cheeks turned red. She bowed twice and said, "Little mistress went to Wuxiang, unable to return. Aunt Xiu can stay here to wait for her." Han hid behind the magnolia tree and peeped. Then a beautifully dressed young woman, accompanied by a maid, entered gracefully. Her charming appearance and beautiful face were too captivating to look at directly. Han's eyes were dazzled, his heart swayed, knowing it was Aunt Xiu, he had no way to avoid her. Unwillingly, he stepped forward and bowed to her.

    223

    秀惊却羞涩，引袖遮面，细语问小红，郎君系阿谁，红无以对，韩辄应以曰：“猗氏韩樾子也。”秀曰：“那得在此？”曰：“令姐之所招致。”秀作色曰：“姊孀居三年矣，院门以内，虽五尺之童，未尝侧足，汝异乡他姓，稚齿韶年，既非周亲，又非故旧，贸焉戾止，意欲何为？”韩惶遽自投于地曰：“小人罪当死，乞秀姨宽宥之。”秀曰：“果谁为汝姨？会须缚而鸣诸官，尝试桎梏，第汝云二姐招致，故舍之，待其归而面证。”韩顿首谢。

    Explanation

    223

    Xiu was startled and blushed, pulling her sleeve to cover her face. She whispered softly to Xiao Hong, asking, "Who is this young man?" Xiao Hong had no answer. Han immediately replied, "I am the son of Han Yue from Yi Shi." Xiu said, "How can you be here?" Han responded, "Your sister invited me." Xiu changed her expression and said sternly, "My sister has been widowed for three years. Inside the courtyard gate, not even a five-foot-tall child has ever set foot. You are from a different place with a different surname, young and inexperienced. You are neither a close relative nor an old acquaintance. How did you arrive here uninvited, and what do you intend to do?" Han, frightened and hurried, threw himself to the ground and said, "I deserve to die, I beg Aunt Xiu to forgive me." Xiu said, "Who is your aunt? I will certainly bind you and report you to the authorities, trying you in shackles. But since you say my second sister invited you, I will spare you. I will wait for her to return and confront her face-to-face." Han knelt and bowed in gratitude.

    224

    秀立迴廊下，把茗盏，召韩问曰：“居此几时矣？”韩曰：“月余矣。”曰：“终日何所事？”曰：“无所事事。”秀哂曰：“无所事事，岂以汝作木偶人看哉”我观汝精满气足神旺，苟非为入幕之宾，焉能若是？汝之事，我知之矣。“韩俯首不言，小绿嗤嗤笑，秀目视小红，小红颇有赧色。秀移步入室，呼小红耳语良久，红颔之，掩笑出户，点首招韩，曰：”来，与郎君语。“韩随之西轩下，红握手密告曰：”适秀姨慕郎君温文韶秀，今夕欲留此与郎君一叙，嘱儿致意，异日小娘回，切勿泄！“韩聆之，惊喜欲狂，曰：”敢不如命！“红反命，旋闻房中嘻笑。

    Explanation

    224

    Xiu stood under the corridor, holding a tea cup, and called Han, asking, "How long have you been here?" Han replied, "More than a month." Xiu asked, "What do you do all day?" Han answered, "I do nothing." Xiu smiled slightly and said, "Doing nothing? Am I treating you like a puppet?" I see that you are full of vitality, strong in energy, and high in spirit. If not for a close relationship, how could it be so? Your affairs, I know them well. Han bowed his head and remained silent. Xiao Lü giggled softly. Xiu looked at Xiao Hong, who blushed slightly. Xiu moved step by step into the room, called Xiao Hong, and whispered to her for a long time. Xiao Hong nodded in agreement, covered her mouth with a laugh, and left the room. She nodded and beckoned Han, saying, "Come, speak with the young master." Han followed her to the west pavilion. Xiao Hong took his hand and whispered, "Just now, Aunt Xiu admired the young master's gentle, refined, and handsome demeanor. Tonight, she wants to keep you here to have a conversation. Instruct the child to convey this message. When the young mistress returns someday, strictly do not reveal it!" Han listened to it, and was extremely surprised and delighted, saying, "I will obey your command!" Xiao Hong returned the message, and suddenly, laughter was heard in the room.

    225

    日才落，便见小绿秉烛，女奴捧盘盛酒肴，往反数回。小红即出邀曰：“可以入矣。”韩汲水洗面澡颈，易新衣。及阶，小红启帘，秀迎笑曰：“适间戏作恐吓语，亦有惧心否？”韩应曰：“初亦甚惧，既察卿之色不恶，且自念亦未尝获罪，遂不复惧。”女笑而睨之曰：“子真佞口，乱人闺阃，尚不伏罪。”韩请以酒自罚，小绿从旁谑之曰：“郎恶醉，犹强酒耶？”小红低语曰：“想试用时，但拈得酒杯耳。”小绿曰：“宁独酒杯，更撚得小红睡鞋矣。”红面赤，不复置啄。秀与韩皆笑，各赐以酒。是夜并宿娟榻，秀肌肤滑腻似娟，而柔媚淫荡远过之。韩不胜其惫，日上八砖，犹拥衾卧。秀先起临镜晓妆，忽女奴迅走入报曰：“娟姨归矣。”

    Explanation

    225

    As the sun set, Xiao Lu lit the candles, and the female servants carried trays laden with wine and delicacies, going back and forth several times. Xiao Hong immediately stepped out to invite, saying, "You may enter now." Han drew water to wash his face and neck, then changed into new clothes. Upon reaching the threshold, Xiao Hong opened the curtain, and Xiu greeted with a warm smile, asking, "Did you also feel afraid just now when I played a prank with threatening words?" Han replied, "At first, I was very afraid, but after observing your pleasant demeanor and realizing I had never done anything wrong, I no longer felt afraid." The girl laughed and glanced at him, saying, "You truly have a flattering tongue, causing trouble in the private chambers, and still refuse to admit guilt." Han requested to punish himself with wine, and Xiao Lu teased from the side, asking, "Do you hate being drunk, yet still force yourself to drink?" Xiao Hong whispered, "If you want to try it, just pick up the wine cup." Xiao Lu said, "How about just the wine cup? What about picking up Xiao Hong's slippers?" Xiao Hong blushed and fell silent. Xiu and Han both laughed, and each was given wine. That night, they shared a bed in Juan's room. Xiu's skin was smooth and lustrous, resembling Juan's, but her soft and alluring charm far surpassed it. Han could not bear his exhaustion, still wrapped in a quilt and lying down as the sun rose in the morning. Xiu got up first, looking in the mirror to apply her morning makeup, when a female servant suddenly rushed in to report, "Aunt Juan has returned."

    226

    韩枕上闻之，手足失措，白身下床，仓皇不知所避，仍匿帐中。秀颜色不少变，调脂弄粉如故。俄而娟入室，径坐椅上，轩眉瞋目，凭几支颐，怒不可犯。红、绿屏足帘外，股战臂摇。韩屏息鸳帏，莈指听察。一饷时，秀妆罢，盥手理裳，缓步至前，抚娟背，含笑问曰：“姊归乎？闻往省妍姐，彼近况奚似？妹与姊契阔久，故来一望，胡相见不作一寒暄语，岂其有所开罪，而姊介蒂于心乎？”娟哂曰：“自作事宁不自知，乃故意问人耶？”秀曰：“然则姊所介蒂者，妹知之矣。

    Explanation

    226

    Han, lying in bed, heard the news and was at a loss, unable to act properly. He quickly got out of bed, in a panic, not knowing where to hide, and still hid in the bed canopy. Xiu's expression did not change at all; she applied makeup as usual. Soon, Juan entered the room, sat down on the chair, raised her eyebrows and glared, leaned on the table and supported her chin, her anger was palpable and intimidating. The red and green screens were outside the curtains, her legs trembled and her arms shook. Han held his breath in the bridal chamber, listening carefully with fingers poised. After a short while, Xiu finished applying her makeup, washed her hands, adjusted her clothes, and slowly walked forward, patting Juan's back and asking with a smile, "Did your sister return? I heard she went to visit Yan, how is she doing? I haven't seen her in a long time, so I came to see her. Why didn't you greet each other with a casual word? Could there be something that offended you, and has your sister taken it to heart?" Juan sneered and said, "How could you not know what you did, are you deliberately asking me?" Xiu replied, "Then, what your sister is holding a grudge about, I know."

    227

    得勿为帐中人乎？幕中人，妹何由识？实姊所罗而致之者，妹不幸与之相值。帷薄之丑，事往心伤，悔且无及。姊之墙茨，滋蔓及妹，姊之罪也，方谓慰藉之不暇，乃反以忿气见加乎？”言讫，掩面而泣，娟气平，亟起，为拭泪以安之曰：“妹若大尚不识耍，娇痴如在母侧时耶？姊与妹如一人，又何间焉？姑试之，日久便知矣。”秀始破涕成笑。娟出韩于帷，犹白身未裤，涕痕满面。娟、秀相顾而笑，久之，始整衣盥漱，载笑载言。韩一旦获两美姝，韩暮腾欢，诚荒淫无度，留连半载，不减朱威武以宣府为家里也。

    Explanation

    227

    Are you not to become one of the people in the tent? How could you know the people in the tent? In fact, they are the ones my sister arranged to bring in, and unfortunately, you encountered them. The shame of the inner curtain, once past, leaves the heart wounded, and regret is already too late. The thorns on my sister's wall spread to you, which is my sister's fault; she should have been comforting you, but instead, she showed anger towards you. After finishing her words, she covered her face and wept; when the anger subsided, she quickly got up, wiped away the tears, and comforted her. "If you don't understand playfulness yet, are you still as naive and playful as when you were by your mother's side? Sister and sister are as one; what difference does it make? Let's try it; you'll know after some time." Show began to smile through her tears. Jüan brought Han out from behind the curtain, still naked except for a skirt, with tears still on her face. Jüan and Show looked at each other and laughed, and after a while, they began to dress and wash, laughing and talking. Han suddenly gained two beautiful women; Han's evening was filled with joy, truly indulging in pleasure without limit, lingering for half a year, no less than Zhu Weiwu treating the Xuanfu as his home.

    228

    会春雨初霁，月色满庭，偕娟、秀饮于木香亭。酣畅间，秀不避侍婢，噙酒哺韩，韩即以哺娟，曰：“挹彼注兹其乐何如？”娟曰：“乐则乐矣，无乃太亵。古士女雅会，未必如此。子亦雅人深致者，盍举殇政，或逞吟怀，即婢子辈效而尤之，亦可继康成佳话。”秀和之曰：“姊每于极乐忘形处，辄下勉功，以为节制，此妹素所心仪者。请分韵为小诗，以承姊命。”于是女奴拭砚，小绿裁笺，小红左右其间，吮毫濡墨。娟、秀之诗同就，韩一见则啧啧曰：“即此两幅《洛神赋》小楷，已足珍如拱璧矣。”娟诗曰：“红梅正馥白梅芳，无赖东风趁蝶狂。只说清芬堪殢汝，谁知韩寿惯偷香。”秀诗曰：“月光加幕草如茵，无事红螺点绛唇。未死会须行乐事，忍看入室有他人。”

    Explanation

    228

    When the spring rain had just cleared, the moonlight filled the courtyard. Together with Juan and Xiu, we drank at the Wood Fragrance Pavilion. In the midst of enjoyment, Xiu did not avoid the maidservant, holding wine to feed Han, who then fed Juan. Han said, "To take from one and give to another, how delightful is it?" Juan replied, "It is enjoyable, but is it not too intimate? In ancient refined gatherings of men and women, it was not necessarily like this. You too are a person of refined taste and deep insight; why not propose a toast, or express your poetic sentiments? Even if the maidservants imitate and surpass us, we can continue the fine tradition of Kang Cheng." Xiu agreed, saying, "Whenever you are in the utmost joy and forget your form, you always remind us of our duties to maintain restraint. This is what I have always admired. Let us divide the rhyme and compose a small poem to fulfill your command." Thus, the maid wiped the inkstone, Xiao Lu prepared the paper, and Xiao Hong assisted with the ink. The poems by Juan and Xiu were completed, and Han, upon seeing them, exclaimed, "These two pieces of small calligraphy in the style of 'The Ode to the Goddess of the Luo River' are already precious as if they were treasures." Juan's poem read: "The red plum blossoms are fragrant, and the white plums are sweet. The mischievous spring wind chases butterflies recklessly. One might say the clear fragrance can intoxicate you, but who knows that Han Shou was known for stealing fragrances?" Xiu's poem read: "The moonlight illuminates the grass like a carpet. Without any reason, the lipstick paints the lips. While we are still alive, we must enjoy life; how can we bear to see others entering our rooms?"

    229

    韩持两诗，三复而赞美之。娟愠曰：“子太无分晓，彼作诗骂人，尚两可其间，毫无详定，使子入场衡文，必致人文颠倒。”秀笑曰：“勿听姊语，姊诗亦寓讽刺，何独怪人？”韩两解之曰：“诗人多诬，亲姊妹无介意也。若谓讽刺之意，二卿自思，亦难回护，我亦将搁笔费平章矣。”娟、秀乃各解颜，韩诗既成，娟、秀争欲先观，花笺纷纷捽碎，合之不复成文。韩笑曰：“适足为魏公藏拙。”遂焚之。夜阑始罢。

    Explanation

    229

    Han held two poems, read them repeatedly, and praised them. Juan was displeased and said, "You are too indecisive. The poems are criticizing someone, yet you remain neutral, making no detailed determination. If you were to judge texts, it would surely lead to confusion." Xiu laughed and said, "Do not listen to your sister's words. Her poem also contains satire. Why single out others for blame?" Han explained, "Poets often make false accusations. Sisters should not take it personally. If we consider the satirical meaning, it is difficult to defend. I will also put down my pen and ask for a fair evaluation." The sisters then relaxed their expressions. Han's poem was completed, and the sisters competed to read it first, tearing the paper notes into pieces, which could no longer be reassembled. Han laughed and said, "This is enough to hide my lack of skill." He then burned the poem. The activity continued late into the night.

    230

    次日，复宴于亭。韩偶见燕子将雏，陡忆萱闱，不禁废然思返，以语娟、秀，娟、秀色如失左右手，良久，秀掩袂而泣，娟独凄然叹曰：“此子之孝思也，即不欲行，尚当劝驾，况敢阻挠？特再面无时，离别之悲，谁能遣此？”因相对唏嘘，终宵不寐。三人目尽肿，红、绿亦泣涕不自禁。戒途之日，娟、秀各有所赠，韩亦各有所贻，且慰之曰：“暂归省母，约三两月可复相聚，无太自苦。”娟曰：“前期未可定也，行矣，幸自爱！”秀哽咽不能出一语，但极力握手而已。

    Explanation

    230

    The next day, they held another banquet at the pavilion. Han happened to see a swallow carrying its young, which suddenly reminded him of his mother's room. He could not help but think of returning and told Juan and Xiu about it. Juan and Xiu looked as if they had lost their left and right hands. For a long time, Xiu covered her sleeves and wept, while Juan sighed sadly and said, "This is the son's filial thoughts. Even if he does not want to go, he should still be encouraged; how dare we obstruct him? But since there will be no more meetings, the sorrow of parting, who can bear it?" They sighed together and stayed awake all night. The three people's eyes were swollen, and Hong and Lv also could not stop crying. On the day of departure, Juan and Xiu each gave something as a gift, and Han also gave something as a gift. He comforted them by saying, "I am temporarily returning to visit my mother. We can meet again in three or two months. Do not be too hard on yourselves." Juan said, "The exact time is not yet determined. Go, take care of yourself!" Xiu choked up and could not say a word, but she held his hand tightly.

    231

    韩饮泣而别，仍跨故骡，星夜还家，至则母死已数七日矣。韩自恸不克送母终，又思娟、秀不置，郁郁成疾，半载始瘥。及服阕，复治装出井陉，循路入山，重至其处。风景如故，第宅无存，但见顽石寒泉，乱云红树，空山寂历，幽鸟啼鸣，四顾茫茫，杳无人迹。徘徊向夕，大恸而归。韩表兄王姓者，为布客于都中，傅属国与之相善，每闻其述之如此，为狐为鬼，为鸟兽草木之妖，无有能辨之者。

    　　兰岩曰：亦不必辨其为何物之妖，第观其秀娟天成，吟咏清丽，多情可儿，世无其匹。于狎亵时，微言以节制之。闻思亲语，劝驾以成就之，绝无陷井之心，真不多有者耳。美人不见，零涕空山。当者伤心，读者酸鼻。

    Explanation

    231

    Hàn wept and bid farewell, still mounting his old donkey, he traveled home under the stars. Upon arrival, he found that his mother had died several days ago. Hàn was deeply grieved that he could not accompany her to her death, and he also could not stop thinking about Juān and Xiù. His melancholy turned into illness, and he did not recover until half a year later. After completing the mourning period, he prepared to leave again and followed the path out of Jǐngxíng, entering the mountains once more to return to the place. The scenery remained the same, but the mansion was no longer there. All he saw were jagged rocks and cold springs, tangled clouds and red trees, an empty and desolate mountain, mysterious birds singing, and looking around, everything was vast and empty, with no signs of human presence. He wandered until evening, cried bitterly, and returned home. Hàn's cousin, named Wáng, was a merchant in the capital, and Fú Shǔguó was friendly with him. Every time Wáng heard him recount it like this, he described the beings as either foxes or ghosts, or spirits of birds, beasts, or plants, and no one could distinguish them.

    Lányán said: It is not necessary to determine what kind of spirit they were. Just observe how naturally beautiful they were, how their poetry was clear and elegant, how they were full of emotion and lovable, and how there was no one in the world like them. During intimate moments, they gently advised to keep things in check. When they heard words of longing for their parents, they encouraged and helped them achieve their goals, showing no signs of deceit, truly rare qualities. The beautiful woman is no longer seen, tears flow down the empty mountain. Those who experience this will be saddened, and those who read about it will feel a pang in their hearts.

    232

    永　护　军阜城门内某胡同，有空宅一区，甚凶，税而居者，往往惊狂致死。护军永某，素以胆勇自诩，同人欲以凶宅试之，谓有人敢宿其中者，当醵金具酒食相款。永曰：“舍我其谁？”挺身请往，众许之。既暮，独携酒肉襆被以往。二更后，饮至半酣，拔剑击柱，大言曰：“果有鬼物，何不现形一闹！却躲何处去耶？”久之，寂然，永大笑，寻亦就枕。甫交睫，似有步履声，张目视之，见内室灯光莹莹，急起捉刃，潜于门隙窥之，则灯下坐一无妇人，一手按头膝上，一手持栉，梳其发，二目炯炯，直视门隙。永骇甚，不能移步。既而梳已，以两手捉耳置腔上，矍然而兴，将启户，欲出。永失声却走，邻家闻之，明炬操兵来探，永已訇匍阶下，肘膝皆伤。述其所见，闻者胥惊。永归，病数日方起，同人见则嘲笑之，永不复置辩焉。

    　　兰岩曰：大言不惭者，辄以天下事，无不可为。甫尝一试，便尔狼狈不堪。奈何不于欲言时，预为计虑哉？

    Explanation

    232

    In a certain lane inside the Fu Cheng Gate, there is an abandoned house that is extremely ominous. Those who rent it and live there often go mad and die. A certain officer named Yong, who always boasted about his courage, was challenged by his peers to spend a night in the haunted house. They promised that anyone who dared to stay there would be rewarded with money, food, and drinks. Yong said, "If not me, then who?" He volunteered to go, and his friends agreed. As evening fell, he went alone with food, wine, and bedding. After the second watch of the night, when he was half-drunk, he drew his sword and struck a pillar, shouting loudly, "If there are any ghosts, why don't you show yourselves and cause some trouble! Where are you hiding?" After a long time, everything was silent. Yong laughed loudly and soon went to bed. As soon as he closed his eyes, he heard footsteps. He opened his eyes and saw a faint light shining in the inner room. He quickly grabbed his weapon and peeked through the door crack. There, he saw a woman sitting under the light, one hand on her head resting on her knees, the other holding a comb, combing her hair. Her eyes were bright, staring directly at the door crack. Yong was extremely frightened and could not move. After she finished combing her hair, she held her ears above her head, suddenly stood up, and was about to open the door to leave. Yong screamed and ran away. The neighbors, hearing the commotion, came with torches and weapons to investigate. Yong had already fallen down the stairs, injuring his elbows and knees. When he described what he saw, everyone was shocked. Yong returned home and was sick for several days before recovering. His friends laughed at him, but Yong did not argue back anymore.

    Lanyan said: "Those who speak grandly without shame always believe that everything can be done. As soon as they try, they become utterly disheveled. Why don't they think ahead when they want to speak?"

    233

    朱　外　委永平朱外委，以公务独乘一骑，腰弓矢，夜行，路卑岸高，迍邅一狭路中。时际十月望后，风严霜重，粉月在林，忽闻有哭声甚哀，翘首四望，见直西数十步外一妇。深夜那得有此，得非妖物耶？于是驻马把弓抽矢，向空施一觢头响箭，声如唳鹤，直出林表。其哭顿止。又施之，妇人忽起立，高与林齐，举步来追。朱大惊，策马而奔，得入一古庙中，弃马闭门，屏息神座下，潜于破壁窥之。俄尔妇人至，往来寻索。庙外土墙，仅及其腰，披发白面，怒色怖人。既而见马，知在庙中，探身攫扑，阶石皆碎。朱惊仆昏绝，良久寂然，惟闻庙侧啯咂之声，顷之亦止。不觉惫极而睡，次日辰刻方醒，路上行人渐伙，始大呼救人。众怀惑入视，惊问所苦，朱以夜间所遇告，闻者靡不缩颈，或以为魃魉，或以为丧门之神，终莫能测。共出视马，但见皮骨狼藉满地，鞍鞯亦成齑粉。众以为非常怪异，相禁夜行。朱徒步归营，病月余始复。

    Explanation

    233

    Zhu, an official from Yongping, was riding alone on a horse for official business, wearing a bow and arrows on his waist, traveling at night. The road was low, and the embankment was high, and he was stuck in a narrow path. It was the days after the Mid-Autumn Festival, with cold winds and heavy frost, and the moon was shining through the trees. Suddenly, he heard a mournful cry. He raised his head and looked around, and saw a woman to the west, about several dozen steps away. How could there be a woman at night? Could it be an evil spirit? Zhu stopped his horse, drew an arrow, and shot a whistling arrow into the air. The sound was like the cry of a crane, flying straight out of the forest. The woman's cry stopped immediately. Zhu shot another arrow, and the woman suddenly stood up, as tall as the trees, and began to chase him. Zhu was greatly shocked, spurred his horse, and fled, entering an old temple. He abandoned his horse, closed the door, held his breath under the altar, and hid behind a broken wall to peek at her. Soon, the woman arrived, searching back and forth. The earthen wall outside the temple was only as high as her waist, and she had long hair and a white face, with a frightening angry expression. She saw the horse and knew it was in the temple, so she leaned forward to grab and pounce on it, shattering the steps. Zhu was shocked, fell, and fainted. After a long time, there was silence, but he could only hear a low rumbling sound near the temple, which soon stopped. He slept due to extreme tiredness and woke up in the morning, around the third watch. The number of passersby gradually increased, and he began to shout for help. The crowd, full of suspicion, entered to see, and was shocked to ask what had happened. Zhu told them about his encounter at night. Everyone who heard it shrank back in fear, some thinking it was a ghost, others thinking it was a god of death, but none could understand it. They all went out to look at the horse, and saw only the skin and bones scattered all over the ground, and the saddle and saddlecloth turned to powder. Everyone thought it was a very strange and unusual event, and agreed to avoid traveling at night. Zhu returned to camp on foot and was ill for more than a month before recovering.

    234

    兰岩曰：无制服之能，则贸然触其怒，几至粉身碎骨，何其愚哉！言愿世之待恶人者，当以此为戒也。

    　　锔人护军某，夏日荷鸟枪于城外打生。值大雨，趋避于教场之演武厅。良久雨愈洪，飞电迅雷，绕厅不去。某惊惧，环视厅中，俄见一巨蝎伏梁上，大如琵琶。骇极，因悟雷之不去，必为此物。我盍为阿香助一臂，以分天功？亟取枪下药与锭，向蝎然之。枪甫发，忽大震一声，不禁昏仆于地。虽不能转侧，而心殊了了，恍惚有数人入厅，汹汹曰：“误殛一人，奈何？”一人曰：“速视之，尚可救否。”一人曰：“筋骨皆脱，似不可活。”俄一人近身，以手扪結曰：“无害，可以锔之。”遂有来锔者，返侧播弄，移时而后散去。某徐徐自苏，扶枪强步，殊不觉痛楚。但见遍身骨节，及节皮当联络处，有肉锔子，长二寸许，阔五分，大小无少差谬，甚以为异。巨蝎死地上，凶恶可怖，即缚之枪上，荷以归。至今其家，犹存蝎羓云。

    Explanation

    234

    Lan Yan said: Without the ability to subdue or control, one rashly provokes his anger, almost to the point of being crushed into dust and broken bones, how foolish! Speaking of how the world should treat evil people, one should take this as a warning.

    A certain military official named Jujun Hu Jun, in the summer, carried a bird gun outside the city to hunt. Encountering heavy rain, he sought shelter in the drill hall of the military training ground. After a long time, the rain became even heavier, with lightning and thunder surrounding the hall and not leaving. The person was frightened, looked around the hall, and soon saw a giant scorpion on the beam, as large as a pipa. Extremely terrified, realizing that the thunder must be due to this creature, he thought, "Why not lend a hand to Axiang to share in the heavenly work?" Quickly took the gunpowder and fuse from the gun, aimed at the scorpion, and lit it. As soon as the gun was fired, there was a sudden loud explosion, and the person fainted to the ground. Although unable to move, the mind was still very clear. Vaguely, several people entered the hall, angrily saying, "We mistakenly killed someone, what shall we do?" One person said, "Quickly look at him, can he still be saved?" Another person said, "His bones are all dislocated, it seems he cannot survive." Soon, one person came close, touched the body, and said, "No harm, we can fix it with锔." Then锔 came, manipulated the body, and after some time, left. The person slowly woke up, supported himself with the gun, and did not feel any pain. But saw that all over the body, at the joints and where the skin should connect, there were flesh锔, about two inches long and five fen wide, with no slight difference. Considered it very strange. The giant scorpion died on the ground, looking fierce and terrifying. Immediately tied it to the gun and carried it back. To this day, his family still keeps the dried scorpion.

    235

    兰岩曰：尝观《聊斋志异》，有易首者矣，有做心者矣，与此可称三奇。彼放荡形骸者，安得如此肉锔子以锔之哉！

    　　某　掌　班黎园掌班某，押班赴苏州演剧，半月始归。路经某村，村中某乙夙与有交，便道就之，甚见款洽。乙园亭幽绝，皓然纸糊如雪，夜饮尽欢。乙辞去。诸伶兴未阑，结伴掷色，呼叫正哗，忽骰盆中有血一点，疑是鼻破，群相眴视。既而随骰而落，腥血淋漓，相顾错愕，举目环睇，瞥见当头顶隔，渍一血痕，大如案，咸大骇，各结舌无言，仰首注目。俄而血迹四浸，隔纸脱落，见一物下垂，谛之，则妇人纤足一双也，血流被踵。众惊悸了狂，夺门奔走，自相蹂躏。比人来救，而众已神痴矣。久之心始定，同述所见，闻者皆惧，乃相于爝火操兵，大索厅上，毫无所见，顶隔迹完好，无少破损，并无血痕。甚以为怪，遂移于别室息焉。次日，白诸主人，乙闻之，色变如灰。某叩之，语极支吾，但厚赠某暨诸伶，嘱秘而勿宣。

    Explanation

    235

    Lanyan said: I once read Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio, where there are stories of heads being replaced and hearts being transplanted. This case can be called the third wonder. How could such a person who lives a carefree and unrestrained life be controlled with a meaty clamp?

    The manager of the Liluan troupe, Mr. Liyuan, led the troupe to Suzhou for a performance and returned after half a month. On the way, he passed through a certain village where he had a long-standing friendship with a man named Mr. Yi. He took a shortcut to visit him and was treated very hospitably. Mr. Yi's garden was extremely secluded and beautiful, with white paper windows like snow. They drank and feasted joyfully at night. When Mr. Yi left, the actors were still full of enthusiasm and gathered to play dice, shouting and making a lot of noise. Suddenly, there was a drop of blood in the dice bowl, and they suspected it was a broken nose. They all looked at each other in surprise. Then it followed the dice and fell, and the blood was gushing out. They looked at each other in shock, looked around, and suddenly saw a bloodstain on the ceiling, as big as a table. They were all greatly frightened and were all speechless. They looked up and stared. Soon the bloodstain spread in all directions, and the paper ceiling fell off. They saw something hanging down. Upon closer inspection, it was a pair of a woman's delicate feet, with the blood flowing down to the heels. The crowd was terrified and ran around in panic, rushing out through the door and trampling over each other. When people came to help, the crowd had already gone mad. After a while, their hearts calmed down, and they all described what they had seen. Those who heard were all afraid. They lit torches and armed themselves, searching the hall thoroughly. They saw nothing. The ceiling was intact, with no damage at all, and there was no bloodstain. They found it very strange and moved to another room to rest. The next day, they told the owner. Mr. Yi heard and his face turned ashen. The manager asked him, and he spoke in a very evasive manner. But he gave generous gifts to the manager and the actors and instructed them to keep it secret.

    236

    某至京，心颇耿耿，适乙之表弟某秀才，赴科入都，馆于其家。某私以曩日所见质之，秀才故与乙有隙，因尽发其隐，某始破惑。先是，乙挟势自恣，同村数百家，强半是其佃户，无有不为其威慑者。村西有程姓农人，其子妇出瞔于田，乙见而艳之，以索积逋，勒为针线人。居无何，乙父子递欲淫污，妇悉不从。一夕，乙长子复诱之空室逼之。妇窘迫，爪伤其面，乙子大怒，缚而褫其衣，淫讫，挞之数百，血肉狼藉，至夜而毙，悬尸于梁，蔽以顶隔，所以灭其迹也。因大兴讼，半年未决，会岁凶，程父子流徙，甫殷妇尸，迄今越十年矣，无敢过而问者。某与诸伶所见，意即妇之冤鬼，而所宿之厅，盖悬尸处也。

    236

    When I arrived in the capital, my heart was filled with unease. Coincidentally, the cousin of person B, a certain scholar, came to the capital for the imperial examinations and stayed at his home. I privately consulted him about what I had seen before, and since the scholar had a conflict with person B, he revealed all the hidden details, and I finally dispelled my confusion. Previously, person B wielded power arbitrarily, and more than half of the hundreds of families in the same village were his tenants, none of whom were not intimidated by him. In the west of the village, there was a farmer named Cheng, and his wife went to work in the fields. Person B saw her and found her attractive, demanding unpaid debts and forcing her into being a seamstress. Shortly after, person B and his son repeatedly tried to defile her, but the woman refused. One night, person B's eldest son lured her into an empty room and forced her. The woman was in distress and scratched his face. Person B's son greatly angered, bound her and stripped her clothes, and after having intercourse, beat her hundreds of times, leaving her bloody and disheveled. She died at night, and her body was hanged from the beam, covered with a partition to erase the traces. A major lawsuit ensued, but it was not resolved in half a year. Just when there was a famine, the Cheng family was exiled, and the body of the woman was just discovered. Until now, it has been ten years, and no one dared to pass by and ask. What the narrator and the musicians saw, meaning the ghost of the wronged woman, and the hall where they stayed, was where the body was hanged.

    237

    尸异有老人乘车入崇文门者，未及门，暴死于车中。守军执御夫，鸣诸官。会日暮，因验不及，置诸铺中。半夜忽失老人所在。守军失措，相与计议，或言某处有新厝一棺，未葬，盍乘夜窃取其尸，聊以塞责。众可之。遂取尸置车中。翌日官来相尸，于发辫内得一铁钉，入脑三寸余，以为车夫谋杀，竟坐死刑。迟数日，老人忽来自首，细述彼日因一时中恶，昏绝车上，既苏，夜已二更，遂下车步归，今闻冤及车夫，心实不忍，故来告白。官使车夫辨之，不诬。穷究致尸之由，守军不能隐，悉以情告。复拘厝棺者，讯之，则一少妇也，初不认尸，吓以严刑，始吐实。盖钉死者，即其夫也，为与恶少私通，故于夤夜钉杀之，以为断断不致败露，初不意如此发觉，诚为天网不漏矣。乃释车夫，而坐奸夫淫妇之罪焉，守军责而赏之。

    Explanation

    237

    An Unusual Death of an Elderly Man

    There was an unusual death of an elderly man who was traveling in a carriage and entered the Chongwen Gate. He died suddenly in the carriage before reaching the gate. The guards arrested the coachman and reported the incident to the officials. As dusk approached, the investigation could not be completed, so the body was left in the barracks. In the middle of the night, the body of the elderly man suddenly disappeared. The guards were at a loss and began to discuss what to do. One of them suggested that there was a newly prepared coffin nearby that had not yet been buried, and they should steal the body at night to cover up their responsibility. The guards agreed, and they took the body and placed it in the carriage. The next day, the officials came to examine the body and found an iron nail embedded in the victim's hair, three inches deep. They concluded that the coachman had committed murder and sentenced him to death. Several days later, the elderly man suddenly returned and confessed to his own death, explaining that he had fainted in the carriage due to a sudden illness and regained consciousness after two hours of the night. He had then gotten out of the carriage and walked home. Upon hearing that the coachman was being wrongfully accused, he felt deeply distressed and came forward to clear the matter. The officials verified the elderly man's story, and it was found to be true. They then investigated the source of the body and the guards could not hide the truth, so they revealed everything. They also arrested the woman who owned the coffin and interrogated her. She was a young woman who initially denied any connection to the body but, under severe punishment, finally confessed the truth. The person who was nailed to death was her husband, who had committed adultery with a young man. She had killed him with an iron nail at night, believing that the crime would never be discovered. However, she did not expect that the truth would be uncovered in this way, truly showing that heaven's net is wide but not without its limits. The officials released the coachman and punished the adulterous couple, while also punishing and rewarding the guards.

    238

    兰岩曰：借此事以雪彼冤，天诚巧也。

    　　红衣妇人西十库在西安门内，例有披甲人值宿其中。某甲与同值十余人，沽酒夜饮，皆半酣。二更后，甲起解手，至库旁永巷中，于月光下，隐隐见一红衣妇人，蹲身墙边，如小遗状。甲醉后心动，潜就搂之，妇人回其首，别无眉目口鼻，但见白面模糊，如豆腐然。甲惊仆地上。同人迟其来，往觇之，气已绝矣，舁至铺中救之，逾时始苏，自述所遭如此。

    　　兰岩曰：三杯入腹，便尔胆大如天，不顾理法。一骇气绝，不知酒醒否？

    　　阿稚沟某村，有兄弟樵苏于山者，季入山之深，仲求之弗得，归告其翁。翁惊且怒曰：“不为雁序而作鹡鸰，明知弟幼弱，不加防护，任其独行，不饱豺虎，必遭颠坠。汝虑我死后，数亩山田，不能独受，故幸灾乐祸，曳曳独归耶？”仲无以自明，但涕泣自誓，而随父同至山中，遍觅不获，寻亦置之。

    Explanation

    238

    Final Translation

    Lanyan said: "Using this matter to clear that person's wrong, Heaven is indeed wondrous."

    A red-clad woman was seen in the Western Ten Warehouses, located inside the Xi'an Gate, where it was customary for armored guards to be on night duty. One guard, along with ten others, bought wine for a night drink and were all slightly drunk. After the second watch, the guard got up to relieve himself and went to a long alley beside the warehouse. Under the moonlight, he faintly saw a red-clad woman crouching by the wall, in a posture resembling urination. Drunk, he felt a sudden desire and secretly approached to embrace her. The woman turned her head, revealing no eyes, eyebrows, or nose, but only a pale and indistinct face, like tofu. The guard fell to the ground in shock. The others waited for him to return, went to look at her, and found her breath had stopped. They carried her to the barracks for rescue, and after a while, she revived. She recounted what had happened to her.

    Lanyan said: "After three cups of wine, one becomes as bold as the sky, without regard for reason or law. One shock and her breath stops; one doesn't know if she is sober or not?"

    In a village in the Azi Gou area, there were brothers who went mountain cutting and collecting firewood. The younger brother went deep into the mountains, and the elder brother searched for him but could not find him. He returned to tell their father, who was surprised and angry. "Not following the order of geese but acting like a sparrow, knowing that your younger brother is young and weak, you did not protect him. Letting him go alone, he would either be eaten by wild animals or fall and get injured. You worry that after my death, the few acres of mountain land cannot be inherited by one person, so you hope for disaster and take joy in it, dragging and returning alone?" The elder brother could not prove himself, but tearfully swore to himself and followed his father to the mountains, searching everywhere but not finding him. Soon after, he gave up looking for him.

    239

    二年余，因值秋成，翁来往田间，负手观获，有猎者过之，左提雉免，右牵一生黑狐，毛光润如漆可鉴，两目炯炯，向翁躇躇不前。翁心动，以青蚨二千，赎而欲纵之。猎者曰：“不可。此紺狐也，能为妖。”翁曰：“倘为妖，必报吾德，汝亦有施焉。”卒纵之。其狐奉头而窜，瞬息不知所逝。翁目送而笑曰：“蠢然如此，伎俩尽矣，能妖之狐，恐不如是。”猎者亦笑而去。

    　　一日，翁有事入都。途中值雪，山路迍踬，颇不易行，蹒跚间，忽一媪自仄径来，白翁曰：“翁老苦甚矣。如此大雪，日且暮，前去人居正遥，我怜翁老，盍姑就蜗居一息乎？”翁感而许之。媪反步为导，逾一壑，即抵其家。媪剥啄，一婢出应，色殊佳丽，修饰亦极华美，以太太呼媪。媪曰：“客至矣，速备酒饭，且唤三姐来。”婢诺而去。媪延翁入庭，分宾主坐。翁环顾内外，屋宇闳敞，垣墉高峻，陈设珍怪，悉不知名。居然巨室，不类山家。

    Explanation

    239

    Two years later, just as the autumn harvest was completed, the old man was walking through the fields with his hands behind his back, watching the harvest. A hunter passed by, holding a pheasant and a hare on the left, and a black fox on the right. The fox's fur was as smooth and lustrous as lacquer, shiny enough to be used as a mirror, and its eyes were bright and it hesitated to move forward toward the old man. The old man was moved, and offered two thousand coins to redeem it, intending to release it. The hunter said, "No. This is a purple fox, capable of causing mischief." The old man replied, "If it causes mischief, it will surely repay my kindness; you too will have done something good." Finally, he released it. The fox bowed its head and fled, disappearing in an instant without a trace. The old man watched it go and laughed, saying, "So foolishly, its tricks are exhausted; a fox capable of causing mischief would likely not be so." The hunter laughed and left.

    One day, the old man had business in the capital. On the way, it snowed heavily, and the mountain path was difficult to traverse, making it hard to proceed. As he stumbled along, an old woman suddenly appeared from a side path and said to him, "You are an old man and must be very tired. With such heavy snow and the day nearing its end, the nearest human habitation is still far away. I pity your old age. Why don't you rest for a while in my humble abode?" The old man was touched and agreed. The old woman led the way backward, crossing a ravine, and soon they arrived at her home. She knocked on the door, and a maid answered. The maid was exceptionally beautiful and elegantly dressed, and she addressed the old woman as "Madam." The old woman said, "A guest has arrived. Quickly prepare some wine and food, and call for Miss San." The maid nodded and left. The old woman invited the old man into the courtyard, and they sat as host and guest. The old man looked around inside and outside, and saw that the house was spacious, the walls high and sturdy, and the furnishings were filled with rare and strange objects, all of which he did not recognize. It was clearly a grand mansion, unlike any he had seen in the mountains.

    240

    自愧山野不文，颇形蹐跼.俄闻屏后笑语声，美婢四五人，拥一女郎出，年约十七八，姱容修态，光彩照人，绣衣画裙，俨似画中仙子。翁逡巡不知措身。处女一见愕然，色甚惊喜，就媪耳语良久。媪拊掌格格笑，曰：“真大奇事，既属恩人，可即申谢。”女乃下阶展拜，如礼神明。翁将答拜，奈为两婢所持，欲下一揖而不可得也。拜讫，媪复拜之曰：“天假之缘，得邂逅相遇，大恩大德，非一拜可以称报，容缓图之。”翁不解所为，唯曰：“老朽何修，得毋谬误。”媪曰：“翁年高健忘，不复记忆矣。俟徐言之。”

    Explanation

    240

    Ashamed of my lack of culture and refinement, I felt quite uneasy. Soon, I heard laughter and conversation from behind the screen. Four or five beautiful maids escorted a young lady out. She was around seventeen or eighteen years old, with a beautiful appearance and graceful posture, radiant and attractive, dressed in embroidered clothes and painted skirts, seemingly a fairy from a painting. The old man hesitated and did not know how to act. The maiden was surprised upon seeing him, her face showing great joy and surprise, and she whispered to the old woman for a long time. The old woman clapped her hands and laughed happily, saying, "Truly a great wonder, since he is our benefactor, we can immediately express our gratitude." The young lady then descended the steps and bowed, as if paying homage to a deity. The old man was about to return the bow, but was held back by the two maids, unable to perform a single bow. After the bow, the old woman bowed back to him, saying, "By the grace of heaven, we have met by chance. Your great kindness and virtue cannot be repaid by a single bow; we will think of it later." The old man did not understand what was happening, and only said, "What have I done to deserve this? Could I have made a mistake?" The old woman replied, "The old man is old and forgetful, no longer remembers. We will explain it slowly."

    241

    既而设筵，翁居上，独据一席，媪与女共一席，居下。酒炙并陈，水陆咸备，翁逐品茫然，但知适口，咀嚼饮啜，细玩其形状，辨其滋味而已。酒再巡，女亲起浣爵，跪进一觞。翁退位座后，连称不敢，媪曰：“聊以抒忱，幸勿却也。”翁尽三爵，复请入席。媪询及里居姓氏，翁对以某村某氏，媪顾谓女曰：“与汝表妹夫同乡，且同姓也，毋乃其族之叔伯行乎？”又问尊阃年几何矣，子女几人？翁曰：“无女，老妻尚存，年五十有二，长子二十，务农；幼子如在，今年当十七，二年前，入山采药，不知所往，想已为异物矣。

    Explanation

    241

    Shortly after, they set up a banquet. The old man sat at the head of the table, occupying a single seat alone, while the old woman and her daughter shared a seat below. Various dishes and drinks were laid out, both land and water delicacies were available. The old man enjoyed the food without much discrimination, focusing on what tasted good, chewing and sipping as he carefully examined the appearance of the dishes and savored their flavors. During the second round of drinks, the daughter personally washed the wine cups and knelt to present a cup of wine to her father. The old man moved back to a position behind the seat and repeatedly said he was not worthy, but the old woman said, "Just to express my feelings, please don't refuse." The old man finished three cups of wine and then asked to return to his seat. The old woman inquired about his place of residence and surname, and the old man replied that he was from a certain village and surname. The old woman then turned to her daughter and said, "He is from the same village and shares the same surname as your sister-in-law's husband, isn't he a cousin of our family?" She then asked about the age of the old man's wife and the number of children they had. The old man replied, "We have no daughters, but my old wife is still alive, aged fifty-two. Our eldest son is twenty and works in agriculture. Our youngest son, if he is still alive, would be seventeen this year. Two years ago, he went into the mountains to gather herbs, and no one knows where he went. One might assume he has become a ghost or spirit."

    242

    ”媪闻之矍然，曰：“噫！二令郎非清瘦长眉，而眉间有针清者乎？”翁矍然曰：“然，诚如尊说，何以知之？”媪向女曰：“怪底说来与阿癯符合，强半合恩人是楂梨。”女曰：“阿癯言时，期期艾艾，且喜啖未熟山桃，娘盍问果有是否？若然，则诚然矣。”翁闻之，辄潸然曰：“豚儿果有是疾是癖，无可复疑矣。”媪喜曰：“正愁无以报德，今当使父子团聚，何快如之！”亟呼前婢，密语数四。婢欣然去，移时入报曰：“来矣！来矣！”

    　　随见一鲜衣少年，同一靓妆女子自外而至，媪指翁谓少年曰：“识得否？”少年一见大恸，趋拜膝下。翁以目视媪，媪曰：“恩人勿惊疑，且看二年前所失之令郎，较此奚如？”翁帏烛审神，的是其子，不禁泪涔涔随声零落。媪与女从旁慰藉之，始各止悲。女子展拜，翁问为谁，媪曰：“甥女阿雏也，久为恩人之子妇矣。

    Explanation

    242

    The old woman heard this and was startled, saying, "Ah! Are the two young masters not slender with long eyebrows, and do they have a small mark between their eyebrows like a needle point?" The old man was also startled and said, "Yes, indeed as you say, how do you know this?" The old woman turned to the girl and said, "It's strange that what you said matches what Ah Qu says, and most of it fits the benefactor, like a peach and a plum." The girl said, "When Ah Qu speaks, he stutters and hesitates, and he likes to eat unripe mountain peaches. Shouldn't we ask if this is true? If so, then it's certainly true." The old man heard this and was moved to tears, saying, "My son indeed has this condition, there is nothing left to doubt." The old woman was pleased and said, "I was just worried about how to repay the kindness, now I will make the father and son reunite, what could be more joyful!" She quickly called the maid, spoke to her in confidence several times. The maid went away happily and returned after a while to report, "He has come! He has come!"

    Soon, a young man dressed in fine clothes and a young woman dressed elegantly came from outside. The old woman pointed to the old man and said to the young man, "Do you recognize him?" The young man saw him and was overcome with emotion, rushing forward to kneel and bow. The old man looked at the old woman, and she said, "Do not be surprised, benefactor. Look at the son you lost two years ago, how does he compare to this one?" The old man examined him closely with the light of the curtain and confirmed that it was indeed his son, unable to hold back his tears as they fell. The old woman and the girl comforted them, and they finally stopped crying. The young woman bowed, and the old man asked who she was. The old woman said, "She is your granddaughter-in-law, Ah Chu, who has been the wife of your son for a long time."

    243

    昔者令郎樵柴，误坠岩下，适遇甥女救之，彼时以甥女冉弱未字人，僭为主张，即以令郎入赘，不意即恩人之子，苟知之，送归久矣。今于此会合，洵非偶然，行当使甥女归事舅姑耳。”翁谢曰：“感大德，毕生之幸，特家贫不堪屈令甥女。再尚有事入京，容徐议之。”媪曰：“恩人无须辞费，甥女既归公郎，荆钗布裙，分所宜尔。若为入京，亦不过为阿堵物耳。不腆妆奁，虽不丰亦不甚薄，保恩人下半世不复求人。”翁喜惬过望，是夕欢饮而散。季伴翁宿于厅西，翁于枕上细询由来，语刺刺不能休，至鸡鸣方寐。次日，媪令阿雏束装从翁去。

    Explanation

    243

    In the past, your son was cutting firewood and accidentally fell down a cliff, where he was coincidentally rescued by his niece. At that time, the old man, presumptuously taking it upon himself to decide, arranged for his son to become a son-in-law to the niece, as she was young and unmarried. However, it was not known that she was the son of the benefactor, and if it had been known, he would have been sent back long ago. Now, with their meeting, it is indeed not accidental, and it will be necessary to make the niece serve her in-laws. The old man thanked, saying, "I am deeply grateful for your great kindness, which is the greatest happiness in my life. But my family is poor and cannot burden your niece. Moreover, there are still matters to attend to in the capital, allowing me to discuss it later." The old woman said, "The benefactor need not be hesitant or reluctant. The niece, having become the wife of the public official, will wear humble attire, which is as it should be. If it is to go to the capital, it is no more than for material things. A modest dowry, though not abundant, is not very meager, ensuring the benefactor does not need to rely on others for the rest of his life." The old man was extremely pleased, exceeding his expectations. That night, they drank joyfully and parted. The younger one accompanied the old man to stay in the west hall, where the old man, on the pillow, carefully inquired about the background. They talked incessantly without stopping until the rooster crowed before falling asleep. The next day, the old woman instructed Ah Chui to pack and follow the old man.

    244

    将行之前一日，媪置酒为饯。酒再巡，媪避席谓翁曰：“相处数日，恩人亦知老身为何如人乎？”翁恍然自愧，还自詈曰：“老悖但知舔犊，诸事不顾耶。敢问邦族。”媪曰：“老身姚氏，本秦人。甥女葛氏，同乡井。老身孀居有年，又无子，只此女，行三，名阿稚，虽荷恩人再生恩，早夕思报未果。今闻家中大郎，亦未婚，愿以女萝附托松柏，莫见弃否？”翁逊谢，曰：“诚援令甥女，已为非分，讵敢复苦令爱。”媪曰：“老身不文，但知言脱于口不可复收。请先归，少有嫁资，俟粗备，当亲送鱼轩至宅，无事亲迎也。”翁不能却，即向季索得镂玉香球一枚，聊以为信。媪亲结之阿稚胸前罗带上，稚垂颈颇形羞涩。

    Explanation

    244

    The day before their departure, the old woman prepared a farewell feast. After the wine had been circulated twice, the old woman left her seat and spoke to the man: "Having spent several days together, do you know what kind of person I am?" The man suddenly realized his own shortcomings and scolded himself: "Old fool, I only know to nurse my calf, neglecting everything else. What about my family?" The old woman said: "I am surnamed Yao and originally from Qin. My niece is also from the same place. I have been a widow for many years and have no children, only this daughter, the third one, named Ah Zhi. Although you have given her a second life, she has not yet had the chance to repay you. Now I hear that your eldest son has not yet married. I wish to entrust my daughter's future to you. Will you not reject her?" The man humbly thanked her, saying: "To accept your niece is already beyond my share, how dare I ask for your daughter as well?" The old woman said: "I am not well-versed in literature, but once I speak, I cannot take it back. Please go back first. I will prepare a small dowry, and when it is ready, I will personally send the wedding carriage to your home. There is no need for a formal wedding procession." The man could not refuse, so he asked his younger brother for a carved jade perfume ball as a token of engagement. The old woman personally tied it to the chest of Ah Zhi's silk belt. Ah Zhi lowered her head, showing some shyness.

    245

    翌日就道，相与嘱别，各有泣　。门前驾三犊车，翁父子乘一辆，阿雏暨二婢乘一辆，其一辆为辎重，辘辘而发。山路崎岖，望之似不能通轨，而车到处，绰然有余地，亦不觉轩轾。翁朴实而不知究理者，唯深赞车制之巧、黄犊之健而已。日未晡，车停不进，视之，已至家门矣，尤讶其速。仲出，见之惊，问归何急，装何厚，既而见其弟，又载三艳女来，遂结舌不能致诘。翁未遑悉述，先令季导妇入见其姑。

    Explanation

    245

    The next day, they set out. They parted with mutual admonitions, each with tears. Before the gate, three ox-drawn carts were stationed. The old man and his son rode one cart, the young daughter and two maids rode another, and the third cart carried the load, and they set off with a rumbling sound. The mountain road was rugged, and it seemed impossible for the carts to pass, but wherever they went, there was ample space, and the ride was smooth. The old man, who was simple and did not understand the intricacies, only deeply praised the clever design of the carts and the strength of the yellow oxen. Before sunset, the carts stopped and did not move forward. When they looked, they had already reached the family gate, and they were especially surprised by the speed. The second son came out, saw them, and was surprised, asking why they returned in such a hurry and why they were carrying so much. Then he saw his younger brother, who also brought three beautiful women, and was so surprised that he could not ask further questions. The old man did not have time to explain everything, and first ordered the younger brother to guide the women to see the grandmother.

    246

    视卸装已，止御者宿，厚赏而重犒之。御夫拜赐，即欲辞去。翁以日暮途远力止之，而车已驰去。翁方顿足，怪其何苦夜行，忽见数十步外，一车为树根所绊，翻入田间，侧不能起。翁急前救之，非复故物，但草人刍牛，并秸车一辆耳。大惊，奔告其子，阿雏曰：“妗固有此戏术，时一为之，不足诧异。”亟令季收而贮诸箱中。翁入见老妻，备告得妇之由，并述聘妇之事，妻亦惊喜。邻里相传，咸来致贺。凡见阿雏者，男则颠倒，女则欣慕。猜疑默拟，议论纷纭。

    Explanation

    246

    After the unloading was done, the old man stopped the driver from leaving, generously rewarding and lavishly entertaining him. The driver bowed in gratitude but immediately wanted to leave. Seeing the night falling and the journey long, the old man strongly persuaded him to stay, but the carriage had already driven away. The old man was stamping his feet in frustration, wondering why they would travel at night. Suddenly, he saw several steps away, a carriage caught by a tree root, tipped over into a field, lying on its side, unable to get up. The old man hurried forward to rescue it, only to find it was no longer the same thing, but a straw man, a straw cow, and a cart made of straw. Greatly shocked, he rushed to tell his son, Achui said, "My sister-in-law has this trick, occasionally does it, not worth being surprised." Quickly ordered Ji to collect and store them in boxes. The old man went in to see his old wife, fully explained the reason for getting a wife, and described the process of hiring a wife, his wife was also surprised and happy. The news spread among the neighbors, and they all came to congratulate. Anyone who saw Achui was amazed by men and delighted and admired by women. People speculated silently, and discussions were mixed and varied.

    247

    居无何，阿雏谓季曰：“致语阿翁，速办筵席，妗子送三姐至矣。”季告翁，翁曰：“嗤，媳偶作梦，汝奈何附和之。”季惭而退，一食顷，闻门外人声鼎沸，挝门者若甚众，翁急出视，媪已降舆，侍女六七人扶阿稚，红巾覆面，锦衣绣裳，一涌而入。妆奁随之以进，光采耀目，填塞草堂。媪一挥，从人车马一霎尽散，谓翁曰：“亲翁勿慞惶。凡有所需，谅甥女已皆预备矣，不必蠲吉，今日便佳，即可唤婿来拜堂也。”仲逡巡趋出，参差不复成礼，众婢皆笑。入房，合卺讫，阿雏指使布筵，则丰盛十数席，水陆俱备，不测何时何人所置办。翁夫妇大骇，乃叙坐而饮。饮次，翁见妆奁堆积，深以所居狭隘，不能容纳为忧。媪曰：“无虑，再多数倍，亦能相容也。

    Explanation

    247

    Not long after, Little Sister told Second Brother, "Convey to Father-in-law that he should quickly prepare a banquet; Auntie has brought Sister Three here." Second Brother informed his father-in-law, who replied, "Hmph, the daughter-in-law had a dream; how can you go along with it?" Second Brother felt ashamed and withdrew. In a moment, he heard a loud commotion outside, as if many people were pounding on the door. His father-in-law hurriedly went out to see, and the old lady had already descended from the carriage. Six or seven maidservants supported Little Sister, who had a red scarf covering her face and was dressed in finely embroidered robes. They all rushed in at once. The bridal trousseau followed in, shining brightly and filling the thatched cottage. The old lady waved her hand, and in an instant, the servants, carriage, and horses dispersed. She said to the old man, "Dear Father-in-law, do not be alarmed. Surely Niece has already prepared everything you might need. There is no need to choose a good day; today is just fine. You can then call the groom to come for the wedding ceremony." Second Brother hesitantly rushed out, and the ceremony was somewhat disordered and not fully carried out. All the maidservants laughed. They entered the room, and after the wine cups were joined and the ceremony concluded, Little Sister gave orders to set up the banquet. A dozen or so richly set tables were prepared, with both aquatic and terrestrial dishes available. It was unclear when or by whom these were prepared. The old man and his wife were greatly shocked and sat down in order to begin drinking. During the drinking, the old man saw the bridal trousseau piled up and was deeply concerned about the narrowness of their house, worrying that it could not accommodate everything. The old lady said, "Do not worry; even if it were several times more, it could still be accommodated."

    248

    ”因令诸婢往来移运，盈阶满堂之物，悉入洞房，房不加广，而位置罗列，饶有隙地。翁私叹富贵家，诸事得法，随地巧设，较我贫拙家多收数斛麦，乍添一瓮蔬，则填塞无坐卧处，视此真心思才力，百不逮一也。三朝后，媪辞去，留二婢为媵。将发，翁私嘱其妻曰：“亲母初见时，谓我与其女有再生恩，故以女嫁二郎，彼时未便研究，汝其密询之，勿作葫芦提，致人闷闷。”妻如所教，询诸媪，媪曰：“人在汝家，徐叩之可知也。”亟升车去。翁又嘱仲乘间问阿稚，稚曰：“翁所作事，翁自知之，何问我为？”翁终茫然不悟，第安之而已。二新妇入门后，顺事舅姑，调和琴瑟，咸无闲言。且从此衣食丰裕，凡百需用，取诸笥中，无所不给。望似农家，实同朱、顿。村人艳妇之美，羡翁之富，无不耽耽。颇有宵小，夜间潜来为盗，幸二妇觉察，往往戏弄之，而翁殊为厌苦。

    Explanation

    248

    The old man thus commanded the maids to move things back and forth, all the things filling the steps and hall were all moved into the inner room. The room did not become wider, but the arrangement and placement were orderly, with plenty of space left. The old man privately sighed that wealthy families managed everything well, setting things up skillfully wherever they went, unlike my poor and clumsy family where even a few extra bushels of wheat or a jar of vegetables would fill up the space, leaving no place to sit or lie down. Seeing this, one truly understands the difference in thought and effort, a hundredfold less than the wealthy. Three days later, the old woman left, leaving two maids as attendants. As she was about to leave, the old man privately instructed his wife to say, "The mother-in-law said at first meeting that we had a life-saving debt, so she gave her daughter to the second son. At that time, it was not convenient to investigate further, so you should secretly ask her, do not be vague, causing people to feel uneasy." The wife followed his instructions and asked the old woman. The old woman replied, "She is in your home, if you ask her slowly, you will know." She quickly got into the carriage and left. The old man also instructed the second son to ask the young lady when he had the chance. The young lady said, "The old man's actions, the old man knows himself, why ask me?" The old man remained puzzled and did not understand, just accepting it as it was. After the two new wives entered the home, they served their in-laws well, harmonized the relationship between husband and wife, and there were no idle words. From then on, food and clothing were abundant, all needs were met, taking whatever was needed from the chests, nothing was lacking. Looking like a farming family, but in reality, it was like a wealthy family. Villagers admired the beauty of the wives and envied the old man's wealth, all of them were envious. Some ruffians came secretly at night to steal, fortunately, the two wives noticed and often played tricks on them, but the old man found it very troublesome.

    249

    偶出田间行食，见前猎者坐村内，方调一犬，翁薄观之，垂毛绿眼，状极猛恶。翁啧啧曰：“此其所谓狮子狗乎？”猎者曰：“否、否，此名为□　，能咋虎，家畜一头，无论窃盗，即有昆仑神技者，亦且畏之。予以钱八千，得之于贩羊回民者。齐卢秦猃，不是过也。”翁阴念八千钱，易与耳，得此狞犬，何复忧盗贼乎？遂以钱十千，欲买之。猎者曰：“不可，此犬咋人立死。”翁曰：“正欲其能咋死人也。”遂牵归。甫纵于庭，适二新妇自庭后来，笑语方哗，忽举目见犬，息声失色，瞥然却走。犬大吠直前，逐而攫之。翁惊呼奔救，稚已被噬断喉，踣地不动。

    Explanation

    249

    A man, while casually walking in the fields and eating, saw a hunter sitting in the village, training a dog. An old villager glanced at the dog, which had long, hanging hair and green eyes, and appeared extremely fierce and menacing. The villager remarked in admiration, "Is this the so-called lion dog?" The hunter replied, "No, no, this is called [name missing], and it can attack tigers. It is a domesticated animal, and it can handle thieves, no matter how skilled they are. I paid eight thousand coins to obtain it from a Muslim sheep trader. It is not inferior to dogs from Qi, Lu, Qin, or Xian." The villager secretly thought that eight thousand coins was a reasonable price and wondered why he would need to worry about thieves anymore if he could get this ferocious dog. He then offered ten thousand coins to buy the dog. The hunter refused, saying, "No, this dog can kill a person instantly." The villager replied, "That's exactly what I want!" and led the dog home. As soon as he released the dog in the courtyard, two new brides who were coming from the courtyard, laughing and talking loudly, suddenly saw the dog. They stopped talking and turned pale, quickly running away. The dog barked loudly and ran forward, chasing and grabbing one of them. The villager shouted in surprise and ran to save her, but the young girl was already bitten and had her throat cut, falling to the ground motionless.

    250

    犬又舍稚追雏，咋其踵，仆倒地十余步。二子亦惊出，偕翁极力挞犬救之，已死。但见二黑狐卧地上，衣服履袜，宛如蝉蜕。二子嚎咷大恸。翁错愕良久，猛悟当日赎狐事，所以云有再生恩也。且悲且悔，怜其义，议治棺衾，厚葬之。方商酌间，忽自外有哭而入者，盖媪也。席地抱二尸而哭之，曰：“讵意儿辈，罹此闵凶，学术短浅，安能御此惨暴乎？呜呼哀哉，大恩不报之说，良有以也！”翁合家亦环绕而哭，声彻邻比。媪以手扪尸胸曰：“幸尚可救，归以药之，可也。”翁率二子，执挺缚犬，打杀之。媪谢之曰：“亲翁是举，足明素心矣！”寻于腰间，解一白布囊，盛二尸，负之出门，翁等追送之，已远矣。

    Explanation

    250

    The dog, having abandoned the younger one, chased the even younger one, biting its heels and dragging it down the ground for more than ten steps. The two sons came out in shock, and together with their father, they hit the dog hard to save the child, but it was already dead. They saw two black foxes lying on the ground, their clothes, shoes, and socks as if they were cicada shells. The two sons wailed and cried bitterly. The father was stunned for a long time, then suddenly realized the matter of redeeming the foxes that day, which is why it is said to have the virtue of rebirth.
