One of the women suddenly exclaimed in surprise, "The young man by the roadside, could it be Wang Yue?" Upon hearing this, he startedle

    294

    视汝头如蓬葆，辛苦至矣。可便升车往见汝父。“越大喜，执绥而升，与姊弟把握，语刺刺不休，因问老乳媪亦来乎？母曰：”家中只此媪，以其能甘贫，至死无二心，故得偕来。“媪叹曰：”耄矣，何能为，主庖失饪，补缀遗针，日前所为，旋踵则忘，前者为小姑拆洗白绫衫，乃失手误浸粥盆中，惹小姑笑得打嗳，郎尚忆在家时乎？无巨细，何事不能办？即如操量执概，切菜杀鸡，诸本分贱役，亦孺人代为分任。至今犹不舍，携以升天。昔者首途，见勾牒上大书‘义媪’而不名，实不自解，何修而造福至此。“小姑笑曰：”一心纯厖，当获此报，迨抵任后一切仓厨出纳，恣汝掊克，谁复敢与汝较一语？“媪咋舌曰：”果有此，犬彘且不食其余，那复有今日。“言次，有飞骑至，报曰：”至矣。“

    Explanation

    294

    Seeing your head as a tuft of grass, your hardships have reached their limit. You may now ascend the carriage to see your father. "Yue was overjoyed, held the reins and ascended, and with his sisters and brothers, they chattered incessantly. Yue then asked if the old wet nurse had also come? His mother replied: 'There is only this old woman in the household, because she can endure poverty and remain loyal until death, so she was allowed to come along.' The old woman sighed: 'I am old, what can I do? Managing the kitchen and failing in cooking, mending and sewing, what I did earlier, I forget immediately. Earlier, when I was helping the younger sister wash a white silk shirt, I accidentally soaked it in a porridge bowl, which made the younger sister laugh and burp. Do you still remember when we were at home? No matter how big or small, what task cannot be accomplished? Even tasks like measuring and handling, chopping vegetables and killing chickens, various humble duties, were also taken over by the lady. Even now, she still cannot let go, carrying her to heaven. When we first set out, I saw an official document that prominently wrote 'Virtuous Old Woman' without giving her a specific name. I truly do not understand, what merit have I done to be rewarded like this?' The younger sister laughed: 'With a pure and devoted heart, you will receive this reward. After we arrive at our post, everything related to the kitchen and storage will be left to you to manage and exploit, who would dare to argue with you?' The old woman gasped: 'If that is true, even dogs and pigs would not eat the leftovers, how could there be such a day today?' While they were speaking, a rider arrived, reporting: 'They have arrived.'

    295

    俄入一山，来迓者接踵。有黄发鲐背老人者，有服橐鞬若将军者，有贝带冠虎而人者，有夜叉形而操蛇者，纷纷道左，不可殚形。母子初甚惧怯，而渐亦安之。既而至一府第前，阍人十数辈，争相叩拜。母子甫降辇，即闻呵殿声，随闻弦管嗷曹，女子数行，皆宫装夹甬道而立。一人自内出，冠纶巾，披鹤氅，越视之，即其所遇瘦而髯之老人也。怀惑间，其母已与老人相持而泣，姊泣谓越曰：“弟弗识耶？此即父也。”越哭拜，父抚之曰：“儿能孝，虽魂梦中神仙亦不愿为，唯念二亲，故父得灵显示现，以慰汝心。

    Explanation

    295

    Soon they entered a mountain, and those who came to greet them were one after another. There were elderly people with white hair and backs like a turtle, there were men dressed in quivers and bows like generals, there were beings wearing shell belts and tiger crowns, half-human and half-tiger, and there were nightmare-like creatures holding snakes. They crowded on the left side of the road, too many to describe. The mother and son were initially very frightened, but gradually became accustomed to it. Soon they arrived at the front of a mansion, and dozens of gatekeepers bowed and knelt in competition. As soon as they got off the carriage, they heard the sound of a procession, and then heard the sounds of music and singing. Several rows of women, all dressed in imperial attire, stood in the corridor. One man came out from inside, wearing a silk cap and a crane robe. Looking at him, it was the thin, bearded old man he had met before. In a state of confusion, his mother had already been holding the old man and crying. His sister cried and said to him, "Don't you recognize me? This is your father." He cried and bowed, and the father comforted him, saying, "You can be filial, even if I were a god in a dream, I wouldn't want to be. Only thinking of your parents, so the father appeared in a spiritual form to comfort your heart."

    296

    汝孝思已尽，可以归矣。汝母及汝姊弟，以阳数终，同归疫劫。惟汝前程尚远，此处不可久羁。俟四十年后，自当迎汝至此聚首也。”越闻之，牵衣弗释，母抚其背曰：“四十年别耳，儿勿自创。”姊弟亦从旁劝勉，越终不舍去。父怒叱之曰：“忤逆子！不速去，斧锧立加矣！”嗾左右曳之出。越以手攀阈，仰首顾母而哭曰：“儿辛苦万端，始得依依膝下，更复奚之！”父突前以靴尖踢之，越大恸，蹶然而兴，恍如梦觉，则身故在土窟中藉茅坐也。汗出如浆。久之，神始定。逡巡出穴，茅屋化为乌有，但见晨光布野。

    Explanation

    296

    Your filial piety is complete; you may return now. Your mother and your sister and brother, because of the yang number ending, will all return to the calamity of the epidemic. Only you have a long journey ahead, and you cannot stay here for long. Wait until forty years later, and I will surely welcome you here to reunite. Yue heard this and held onto his clothes, unwilling to let go. His mother patted his back and said, "It's only a separation of forty years; don't worry, child." His sister and brother also encouraged him from the side, but Yue still could not bring himself to leave. His father angrily scolded him, "Rebellious son! If you don't leave quickly, the executioner will be here immediately!" He ordered the attendants to drag Yue out. Yue held onto the threshold with his hand, looked up at his mother, and cried, saying, "I have endured countless hardships to finally be able to stay by your side; where else should I go?" His father suddenly stepped forward and kicked him with his boot, and Yue was overwhelmed with grief. He suddenly stood up, as if waking from a dream, and realized that he was still sitting on the straw in the earth cave. Sweat poured out like juice. After a while, his spirit began to calm down. He hesitated and then left the cave; the thatched hut had vanished. He only saw the morning light spreading across the fields.

    297

    徘徊逾时，心伤如割。信步行数里路，见一土地祠，拟入祠谋一餐，入则人聚如蚁，神前牲醴错陈，史巫纷若。越不测何事，觅庙主将询之，入其室，堂中坐立多人，庙主已死，僵卧床上，臭且　。越惊而走出，忽一巫见而抱持之，弃鼓投地，崩角稽首，大言曰：“公子自至耶？”呼众至，告曰：“此即新任神圣之长公子也，寻亲至此，纯孝动天。”众乃环拜。

    Explanation

    297

    Wandering for a long time, my heartache was like a wound. Walking several miles at ease, I saw a land shrine and intended to enter it to get a meal. Upon entering, I found a large crowd gathered like ants, with sacrificial offerings and wine arranged before the deity, and historians and shamans bustling about. I wondered what mysterious or unexplainable event was happening, so I sought out the temple master to ask him. Entering his room, I found many people sitting and standing in the hall, and the temple master had died, lying stiff on the bed, with a foul smell. I was startled and walked out, when suddenly a shaman saw me and held me, throwing down his drum and bowing with his head touching the ground. He shouted loudly, "Has the young master come here by himself?" He called the crowd over and announced, "This is the eldest son of the newly appointed divine being, who has come here in search of his family, and his pure filial piety has moved heaven." The crowd then surrounded and bowed to me.

    298

    越诘其故，巫曰：“前日半夜间，此庙庙主梦云南汪太学，升作此庙土地，庙主拥篲迎。神怒其多方诈公子资财，无仁心，杖遣之。醒而臀肉青肿，逢人则自暴其恶，卧三日竟死。村人感神之灵，醵金为赛，嘱史巫通辞，愿四时肸蚃，公举不懈。神降言‘公子名越，年十七，极孝，方与神会，不日即回生，现在山之西土窟中卧。’群议公迓，讵意独行至此。”越闻之，不胜骇愕，众因相竞奉越，浴以香汤，衣而食之者，骈肩累迹。关尹知之，恐其惑众，迎入署敬礼之，劝其归滇。越亦思母，遂夤夜避众出关。

    Explanation

    298

    Yue questioned the reason, and the shaman said: "The previous day at midnight, the master of this temple had a dream in which Master Wang of Yunnan ascended to become the earth god of this temple. The temple master welcomed him with a broom. The god was angry that the young nobleman deceived his wealth in various ways, lacking benevolence, and struck him away with a staff. Upon waking, he found his buttocks bruised and swollen, and whenever he met people, he would reveal his evil deeds. He lay down for three days and finally died. The villagers were moved by the god's spirit and collected money to hold a ritual, entrusting the historian and shaman to convey the message, wishing for divine communication throughout the four seasons, offering without tiring. The god descended and said, 'The young nobleman's name is Yue, aged seventeen, extremely filial, and just met with the god. He will revive soon and is now lying in an earth cave west of the mountain.' The crowd discussed publicly welcoming him, but unexpectedly, he walked alone to this place." Upon hearing this, Yue was overwhelmed with astonishment. The people then competed to serve Yue, bathing him in fragrant water, dressing and feeding him, with people shoulder to shoulder and one after another. The customs official knew of this and feared that it would confuse the people, so he welcomed Yue into his office with respect and advised him to return to Yunnan. Yue also thought of his mother and thus left the border at midnight, avoiding the crowd.

    299

    行月余抵家，则见宅舍倾圯，葵燕麦，荒废怆心，麋疃鹿场，凄凉满目。惊谘邻里，始知母与姊弟及老乳媪果皆于两月前病疫死矣。四柩悉为司有瘗丛葬处。越一恸几绝，邻人哀之，共相慰藉。越乃罄其资产，扶四柩复至溆浦，与其父合葬焉。叙浦人敬越如神明，群襄窀穸之事，又为植树，顷刻成林，即墓侧结庐，奉越居之。邑富人某以二女妻越，遂录籍于溆浦，力田不仕，生三子，皆业儒，越享素封四十余年。一夕，见其弟，将父母命来迎，乃处置家事，无疾而终，人皆叹为纯孝之报云。

    Explanation

    299

    After traveling for more than a month, Yue arrived home and saw that his house was in ruins, overgrown with weeds, and filled with heart-wrenching desolation. The land was wild and untamed, and the scene was desolate all around. He was startled and asked his neighbors, who told him that his mother, sisters, brothers, and the old wet nurse had all died from an epidemic two months ago. The four coffins were buried in a mass burial site managed by a specific authority. Yue was overwhelmed with grief and nearly collapsed. The neighbors pitied him and comforted him. Yue then used up all his assets, carried the four coffins back to Xupu, and buried them with his father. The people of Xupu revered Yue as if he were a deity, and they all contributed to the burial affairs. They also planted trees, which quickly formed a forest. At the side of the grave, they built a hut and served Yue, who lived there. A wealthy person from the town married two daughters to Yue, who then registered in Xupu, worked hard in the fields, and did not seek official positions. He had three sons, all of whom became scholars, and Yue enjoyed a simple and modest life for more than forty years. One night, Yue saw his younger brother, who came to fetch him with the parents' command. Yue then arranged the family affairs, died without illness, and people all praised it as a reward for pure filial piety.

    300

    兰岩曰：纯孝性成，不避险阻，其获厚报也，固宜。

    　　春　秋　楼某巨公，失其姓名里居，为人刚正不阿。未达时，客游塞上，入归化城某将军幕府，相得甚欢。每论史，至古忠臣烈士，则慷慨激烈。同人窃听，莫不掩口胡卢，笑其迂绝。惟将军敬重不衰，凡百请益。会陀罗海营中，建关圣庙工竣，求将军作碑记，将军曰：“我满洲之不读书者也，君其为我捉刀。”公曰：“关圣威灵，弥纶宇宙。某所见古今碑记，无非颂扬忠义，千百如出一口，求一另成机轴，以阐发所以为圣为神之道者，未之一睹，今请假一精舍，休十日粮，为公竭力为之。”将军大悦，曰：“君文成不加点，不涂乙，素具逸才者也。此间庙祀，正需君文，以传不朽耳。”乃于营中，葺净室爽垲者三楹，日用之物，无不备具，祗奉以二童子，不呼不入也。公居其中，闭目凝神，至忘寝食。将军使人密侦之，但见枯坐耳。居数日，思虑茫然，机神转涩。

    Explanation

    300

    Translation:

    Lan Yan said: Innate filial piety, not avoiding dangers and obstacles, it is indeed appropriate that they receive a great reward.

    During the Spring and Autumn period, there was a prominent figure named "Spring and Autumn" whose name and place of origin are unknown. He was a man of integrity and did not bow to others. When he was not yet successful, he traveled as a guest to the frontier and entered the camp of a general in Guhua City, where they got along very well. Whenever they discussed history, especially when it came to ancient loyal ministers and righteous heroes, he would speak passionately and intensely. His fellow scholars would listen secretly, and none of them would not cover their mouths and laugh silently, mocking him for being impractical. Only the general continued to respect him and asked for guidance on all matters. One day, in the camp at Toluohai, the construction of the Guan Yu temple was completed, and the general asked the general to write an inscription. The general said, "I am a Manchu who does not read books. You should write it for me." The scholar replied, "The divine power of Guan Yu is omnipresent. What I have seen in ancient and modern inscriptions all praise loyalty and righteousness, and they are all alike in their praise. I have never seen one that has a different structure or framework to elucidate the way of being a saint or a god. Now, if I could borrow a quiet room and take a ten-day leave, I will do my best to write it for you." The general was very pleased and said, "Your writing is flawless and does not require any corrections. You are a man of extraordinary talent. The temple here needs your writing to ensure it is immortalized." So, in the camp, they prepared a clean and well-ventilated room, and all daily necessities were provided. Two boys attended to him, entering only when called. The scholar stayed in the room, closing his eyes and concentrating, to the point of forgetting to eat and sleep. The general secretly observed him and only saw him sitting motionless. After several days, his thoughts were confused, and his inspiration became dull.

    301

    一日方晚饭，二童子忽戏阶下，公见之怒发，辍食吐哺，骂曰：“奴才奈何乱我心曲！”亟起操杖欲挞之，二童巧避，一击不中，而中假山，杖折为二。即投杖大笑，急走入室，濡笔挥洒，奋腕直书。童子密报将军，比将军至，而文已成矣。见将军来，大呼曰：“奏刀祎然，幸不辱命。”将军三复读之，叹曰：“贯串流走，彼昌黎送孟东野序，殊为排砌矣。至诚感神，君其有神助耶？”公曰：“初构思，心中纷如乱麻，闻泉水松风，皆厌其聒，三日后此心死矣。今日将晡，犹无一字，方怒童儿嬉戏，将申挞伐，杖折而机忽开，操觚时，自觉如征鸟厉疾，一挥而就，戛戛乎岂难哉，汨汨然而来矣，诚不解何由得此。”将军拊髀曰：“非偶然也。”于是奉百金为润笔，即请公书之。公自作记后，名满塞外，后登第，历仕致清要。

    Explanation

    301

    One day, just as he was having dinner, two young boys suddenly played downstairs. The master saw them and his hair stood on end; he stopped eating and spat out his food, shouting, "You servants, how dare you disrupt my inner thoughts!" He immediately stood up, took a stick, and wanted to beat them. The two boys cleverly avoided, and one strike missed, but hit the artificial hill, breaking the stick in two. He immediately threw down the stick and laughed, then hurried into the room, dipped his brush, and wrote vigorously. The boys secretly reported to the general, and by the time the general arrived, the writing was already complete. Seeing the general coming, he shouted, "The knife is sharp, and I have not disgraced my mission." The general read it three times and sighed, "It flows seamlessly, unlike the Sending Meng Dongye Off by Han Yu, which is quite arranged. Sincerity can move the gods, did you have divine assistance?" The master replied, "At first, I was thinking, my heart was as tangled as a ball of yarn; hearing the sound of water and wind through pine trees, I found them all annoying. Three days later, this heart died. Today, as evening approached, I still had no words. Just as I was angry at the children playing, I was about to punish them, but the stick broke, and suddenly the idea opened up. When I held the brush, I felt like a swift bird, finishing it in one stroke. It was not difficult, it came naturally; I truly do not understand why I was able to do this." The general slapped his thigh and said, "It was not by chance." He then offered a hundred gold coins as a reward and immediately asked the master to write it. After the master wrote the memorial, his name became famous in the frontier regions. Later, he passed the imperial examination and held high official positions.

    302

    一夜梦至一处，见有呵殿而过者，仪卫甚盛。辇上贵人，则关圣也。公趋前，望尘而拜。关圣下车劳之曰：“君作记，良费苦心。时至矣，当待君于春秋楼，好为入幕之宾也。”言讫，拥去。公寤，阴异之，知不久于人世，即致仕归。归途值大雨，息驾一古刹中。刹左有危阁，题额则“春秋楼”也。恍然悟，沐浴具衣冠，屏去僮仆，端坐楼上而逝，空中隐隐有音乐声，逾时始歇，合刹莫不闻之。

    Explanation

    302

    In a night's dream, I arrived at a place and saw a grand procession passing by with shouts and commands, the ceremonial guards were very grand. The noble person on the sedan chair was Guan Yu. I hurried forward, bowing while looking at the dust. Guan Yu got off the sedan chair and greeted me, saying, "You have written your record, it has truly taken a lot of effort. The time has come, I will wait for you at the Spring and Autumn Pavilion, to be a valued guest." After he finished speaking, he carried me away. I awoke, secretly found it strange, and knew I would not live long. I then retired and returned home. On my way back, it rained heavily, and I stopped at an ancient temple. To the left of the temple, there was a perilous pavilion, inscribed with the name "Spring and Autumn Pavilion." Suddenly realizing, I took a bath and put on ceremonial clothes, dismissed the servants, and sat upright on the upper floor and passed away. There was a faint music sound in the air, which lasted for a while before stopping. All the temples did not fail to hear it.

    303

    棘闱志异八则果报之异，在在有之，而见于棘闱者尤著。或云：举子入场之前一夕，执事官公服致诚以召鬼神，请神以红旗，招家亲以蓝旗，引恩怨鬼以黑旗。召讫，插三色旗于明远楼四角，吏且招且呼曰：“有冤者报冤，有仇者报仇。”云云。故场中怪异叠见，愈出愈奇。予之亲戚，往往有监试者，予以招神招鬼之事质之，亦云不妄，因举所闻之尤异者记八则。

    　　陈扶青夫子言：雍正间，江南乡试，有常熟某生，年四十余，第三场入宿字号。前二场颇得意，兴致甚高。中秋夜，与相识玩月，分韵作诗，有“皓月今宵满，红颜往日残”之句，众索其解，生凄然对曰：“诸君皆同类，无妨实吐也。忆昔游吴门时，馆于某缙绅家。子弟四人，悉主人子侄。有柳生者，其内侄也，丰姿如玉，予挑之数四，佯若不知。适值令节，诸生皆给假展墓，唯与柳生相对，予复作诗以挑之曰：”绣被凭谁覆，相逢自有因。

    Explanation

    303

    Strange Phenomena in the Examination Hall: Eight Cases of Retribution

    Strange phenomena of retribution are common, but those seen in the examination hall are especially prominent. Some say that on the night before the candidates enter the examination hall, the officials in charge, dressed in official robes, sincerely summon ghosts and spirits. They use a red flag to invite deities, a blue flag to call upon family spirits, and a black flag to summon spirits of grievances and grudges. After summoning, they insert three-colored flags at the corners of the Mingyuan Tower. The officials call out while summoning, saying, "Those with grievances will seek retribution, those with grudges will seek revenge." Therefore, strange and unusual phenomena appear repeatedly, becoming increasingly bizarre. My relatives often have people who monitor the exams, and I questioned them about the matter of summoning spirits. They also said it was not false, so I recorded eight particularly strange cases that I have heard.

    Mr. Chen Fuqing said: During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, in the Jiangnan provincial examination, there was a scholar from Changshu who was in his forties. On the third day of the exam, he entered the dormitory assigned to him. He had been quite successful in the first two days and was in high spirits. On the Mid-Autumn Festival night, he met friends to admire the moon and composed poems by dividing the rhymes. One of the lines was, "The bright moon is full tonight, but the beauty of the past is now faded." His companions asked for an explanation, and he replied with sorrow, "We are all of the same kind, so it's not a problem to speak frankly. I remember when I was traveling in Wu Gate, I stayed at the home of a certain scholar-gentleman. He had four sons, all of whom were his nephews. There was a young man named Liu, who was his nephew, and he was as graceful as jade. I tried to court him several times, but he pretended not to know. On a festival day, all the students were given leave to visit their ancestors' graves, except for me and Liu. I wrote a poem to court him again, saying: 'Who will cover the embroidered quilt? Our meeting has its own cause.'"

    304

    亭亭临玉树，可许凤栖身。‘柳得诗，面色发赧，因而嚼之，予以为可动矣。会友人见饷，予蓄有媚药，入酒中饮之，易醉而狂，强柳生尽一巨觥，遂得一遂所欲。次日酒醒，知己被污，竟投环内寝。举家不知其故，予虽知之而不敢泄，饮泣而已。主人构讼，半年始解。今夜月色，不减当年，而未免有情，谁能遣此？故感慨系之耳。“言讫，泪涔涔下。闻者无不毛戴，陆续散去。五更后，忽闻人声鼎沸，往来不停履，相告曰：”有人缢死屎号中矣。“诘旦验之，则常熟生也。

    　　兰岩曰：酒为色媒，谓醉后每动淫心也。乃柳生以少年丰标，忽遭此无行之人，诗以戏之，酒以诱之，而卒为所污辱。斯又酒能诲淫，而非假酒淫人也。虽然，柳当阅诗后，纵不严以绝之，亦当托故以避之，不能见机而作，复与饮酒忘醉，亦不得谓无罪矣。羞而自缢，嗟何及哉？天下之甘言卑礼，无因至前者，皆祸机之所伏也。慎勿不加察而徒自诩予智哉！

    Explanation

    304

    A tall and graceful tree stands by a precious jade tree, suitable for a phoenix to perch on. When Li Sheng received the poem, his face turned red with embarrassment, and he chewed it, thinking it would be effective. A friend brought some wine, and I had a love potion stored. I put it in the wine and drank it, becoming easily drunk and crazy. I forced Li Sheng to finish a large cup, thus fulfilling my desires. The next day, after drinking, he realized his friend was defiled and threw himself into the inner chamber. The whole family did not know the reason, and I, though aware, dared not reveal it, only weeping silently. The owner filed a lawsuit, which took half a year to resolve. The moonlight tonight is no less than it was then, but it is inevitable to have feelings, who can dispel this? Thus, these feelings are expressed. After saying this, tears streamed down. Those who heard it were all shocked and left one by one. After the fifth watch, suddenly there was a commotion, and people kept coming and going. They told each other: someone has hanged themselves in the toilet. The next morning, when they checked, it was indeed the Changshu student.

    The author, Lan Yan, said: Wine is a matchmaker for lust, meaning that after drinking, one often has lustful thoughts. Li Sheng, with his youthful beauty, suddenly encountered this immoral person. I used poetry to tease him and wine to lure him, and he was ultimately defiled. This shows that wine can incite lust, but it is not wine that lures people into lust. However, Li Sheng, after reading the poem, even if he did not strictly cut off the relationship, he should have found an excuse to avoid it. He could not see the signs and acted, drinking again and getting drunk, so he cannot be said to be blameless. Ashamed, he hanged himself, alas, how could it be avoided? All sweet words and humble courtesies that lead to something that should not happen are the seeds of disaster. Be careful not to be careless and just boast of your wisdom.

    305

    先生又言：乾隆某年，结伴入南闱。同舍俞生，江阴诸生也。甫毕头场，即治任。众怪而问之，言语支吾，而颜色凄楚。愈力诘之，不得已始明告曰：“言之丑矣。先君子宦游半世，及解组归，遂病怔忡，数年不愈。捐馆时，呼予兄弟四人至榻前，泣嘱曰：”吾平生无昧心事，唯任某县令时，曾受贿二千金，冤杀二囚，为大罪恶，阴报当斩嗣，以祖上有拯溺功，仅留一子单传，五世不得温饱。吾今人非高于泰山，鬼责深于沧海，地狱之设，幸脱无由。子孙或不知命，妄想功名，适益吾罪，非孝道也。汝兄弟其各勉为善事，自图结果，‘言讫而瞑。

    Explanation

    305

    Mr. [Name] also said: In the year of Qianlong, he went to the southern examination hall with companions. Among them was Yu Sheng, a student from Jiangyin. Just after finishing the first session of the exams, Yu Sheng immediately packed his belongings. His companions were puzzled and asked him, to which he spoke evasively and appeared sad. When pressed further, Yu Sheng reluctantly admitted, "It is a shameful thing to say. My father spent half his life traveling for official duties, and upon retiring, he fell ill with palpitations, which persisted for several years without healing. When he passed away, he called me and my three brothers to his bedside, crying and entrusting us with his final words: 'I have no hidden sins in my life, except that when I served as the magistrate of a certain county, I accepted a bribe of two thousand taels of silver and wrongfully killed two prisoners. These are great sins, and according to the spiritual retribution, my descendants should be executed. However, because my ancestors had the merit of rescuing someone from drowning, only one son was left to continue the lineage, and for five generations, they will not enjoy comfort and abundance. Now, my status is not higher than Mount Tai, and the responsibility to the spirits is deeper than the sea; there is no way to escape hell. If my descendants do not understand their fate and foolishly pursue fame and success, it will only increase my sins, which is not filial piety. You brothers should each strive to do good deeds and seek your own salvation.' He finished speaking and passed away."

    306

    后兄弟相继死，唯我仅存，乡试二次，悉被墨渖污卷。昨在棘中，文思颇涌，三更即脱稿。倏一人披帷而入，立灯前，惊神之，乃先君也，颜色愁苦，怒责予曰：“奈何忘我遗嘱，屡为非分，致我奔走道途，辛苦备尝？若再不悛，祸不旋踵矣！’随以手械一击，烛灭砚翻，旋失所在。予惊走而恸，比栉胥来致询，见予油墨满卷，各嗟叹而散。予今年二十有五，登蓝榜，不足为恨，所痛先人负谴，拘击九幽。行当削发入山，披缁出世，学目连大士，救拔亡灵，忏悔之情，幸诸君垂鉴焉。”众闻之，靡不咋舌神惊，善念为之一炽，先生退而作《归山诗》以送之。

    Explanation

    306

    After my brothers died one after another, only I remained. I failed the provincial examination twice, and my scrolls were stained with ink each time. Yesterday, in the examination hall, my thoughts flowed freely, and by the third watch, I had finished my essay. Suddenly, a person entered through the curtain, standing before the lamp. I was shocked and saw it was my late father. His face was sad and angry, and he scolded me, saying, "Why do you forget my last words and repeatedly act beyond your station? This has caused me to run around and suffer greatly. If you do not repent, disaster will follow immediately!" He struck me with a hand tool, extinguishing the candle and overturning the inkstone, and then he disappeared. I fled in shock and wept bitterly. Neighbors and officials came to inquire, seeing my scroll covered in ink, they all sighed and left. I am now twenty-five and have passed the blue list, but it is not worth regretting. What pains me is that my ancestors are punished, imprisoned in the underworld. I will soon shave my head and enter the mountains, becoming a monk and leaving the world. I will learn from the Bodhisattva Maudgalyayana, rescuing the spirits of the dead. My feelings of repentance, I hope you all take note of. Everyone heard this and was amazed and shocked, their good intentions were greatly stirred. The master then composed a poem titled "Returning to the Mountains" to send him off.

    307

    兰岩曰：获罪于天，无所祷也，圣人言之谆切矣。嗟乎！升堂鼓响，阶下者如对阎罗；覆盆冤成，受刑者恍遭地狱。奈何以嗷嗷赤子，方延颈于父母之堂；而簇簇黄金，已私受于夤缘之吏。遂使沉冤莫雪于生前，宿孽旋生于死后。三战三黜，子孙五世贫寒；一代一丁，兄弟崇朝殂谢。可不慎欤？可不戒欤？

    　　某科会试，一江南举人，入头场，文战至二更，往末号解手，三更不返。相识数数来觅，不见。共异之。提灯往观，见一人横卧溷尿中，验之，举人也。呼之不应，大惊，急救之，良久始苏。自述在此，方欲解手，见一物大如牛，白如雪，倚墙根蠕动，霍霍有声。心殊恐怖，大声叱之，物忽起立，乃是一白人，面作青白色，两眼大如鸡子，碧而有光，不觉身如梦魇，呼叫不能出声，亦不自知僵仆之由也。众皆悚然，扶之归号，次日，曳白而出。

    Explanation

    307

    Translation

    Lan Yan said: If one offends Heaven, there is no prayer that can help. The sages have spoken of this with earnestness. Alas! When the drums sound in the hall, those below seem to be facing Yama, the king of hell; when a wrongful accusation is made, the one who suffers seems to be in hell. How can we allow innocent children, who are stretching out their necks to their parents' house, to be wrongfully accused? Yet, bribes and favors are already being accepted by corrupt officials. Thus, grievances remain unresolved before death, and past sins manifest immediately after death. Three times defeated, three times dismissed, their descendants live in poverty for five generations; one generation, one son, brothers die suddenly in the morning. Can we not be cautious? Can we not be vigilant?

    During a certain imperial examination, a scholar from Jiangnan entered the first round of the exam. He was writing until the second watch when he went to the end of the hall to relieve himself. He did not return by the third watch. His acquaintances came to look for him repeatedly but could not find him. They were all surprised. They took lamps to look for him and saw a person lying on his side in the toilet. Upon examination, it was the scholar. They called him, but he did not respond. They were greatly alarmed and rushed to help him. After a long time, he regained consciousness. He recounted that he had just gone to relieve himself and saw a creature as large as a cow, white as snow, leaning against the wall and moving slowly, making a rustling sound. He was extremely frightened, shouted loudly at it, and the creature suddenly stood up, revealing itself to be a white person with a face that was pale green, two eyes as large as eggs, and a bright green glow. He felt as if he were in a dream, unable to make a sound, and did not know why he had collapsed. Everyone was startled, helped him back to his seat, and the next day, he left the exam without completing it.

    308

    兰岩曰：神魂迷罔，乃见此异物以夺其魄，曳白而出，亦云幸矣。

    　　李伯瑟言：其表弟康生，夙以才貌擅名。年甫二十有二，即设帐于巨绅单氏家。单三世为官，富甲一郡，僮仆婢媪，数十百人。而单赋性残酷，家法极严，家人小有过犯，鞭仆立下，甚有炮烙等刑，往往毕命，恬不为怪。康工谀善媚，入馆后宾主颇相得。第少年喜事，每捕风捉影，见事生风。生徒五人，曰修，曰保，曰杰，曰偲，皆单之子；侄曰炳文，单之弱弟而异母者也。炳文年十七，聪颖异常，所为诗文，康多不能易，阳推许而阴忌之。唯保与康最契，故主人家事，若大若小，主人眷属，若男若女，无不悉知。有一事，保必侦以告康；见一人，康必指以问保。谊虽师弟，实类友朋也。

    308

    Lan Yan said: In a state of bewilderment and confusion, one encounters this strange entity that seizes one's spirit, dragging out the white, and it is also said to be fortunate.

    Li Boshuo said: His cousin, Kang Sheng, was long known for his talent and appearance. At the age of twenty-two, he began teaching in the household of the wealthy official Dan. The Dan family had been in office for three generations, being the wealthiest in the entire prefecture, with dozens of servants and maids. Dan was cruel by nature, and the family rules were extremely strict. For minor offenses, servants were whipped immediately, and there were even cruel punishments such as pao luo, often resulting in death, which was taken calmly without finding it strange. Kang was skilled in flattery and ingratiating behavior, and after joining the household, he and the master got along well. However, the young man enjoyed stirring up trouble, often making unfounded accusations and creating drama over trivial matters. He had five students: Xiu, Bao, Jie, Si, all of whom were Dan's sons; and his nephew, Bingwen, who was Dan's younger brother but from a different mother. Bingwen was seventeen years old, exceptionally intelligent, and his poems and essays often left Kang unable to change them, leading Kang to publicly praise him while secretly envying him. Only Bao and Kang were closest, so they knew all matters of the master's household, whether big or small, and all family members, whether male or female. If there was an event, Bao would always investigate and report to Kang; if they saw someone, Kang would always point and ask Bao. Although they were teacher and student, they were actually like friends.

    309

    会东家宴内亲，日暮散去，内眷送客回，笑语过书院门。康于门隙窥见一婢，翠衣素裙，冶容媚诚，风致嫣然，顿觉心神把握不定。正凝想间，适馆僮秉烛来，陈酒核，康曰：“诸郎在内作底事？”僮曰：“有内客留宿，诸郎正忙，少停，二郎即出陪先生饮酒矣。”康颔之。俄尔保至，师弟欢然对酌。因以所见翠衣婢质之，保曰：“先生所谘，得非白皙如雪，眸黑齿皓，多发如云，黝髹可鉴者乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“此三姑母房中使女小蕙也。

    Explanation

    309

    At a time when the eastern neighbor was hosting an internal family banquet, as evening fell and the guests departed, the women of the household saw them off, laughing and chatting as they passed the academy's gate. Kang, peering through a crack in the gate, saw a servant girl wearing a green dress and plain skirt, with a seductive appearance and charming demeanor. Her demeanor was charming and alluring, and he suddenly felt his heart and mind become unsettled. Just as he was deep in thought, just then, the academy's servant boy came with a candle and set out the remnants of wine. Kang asked, "What are the young men doing inside?" The servant boy replied, "There are guests staying over, and the young men are busy. In a moment, the second son will come out to accompany the master in drinking." Kang nodded. Soon after, Bao arrived, and the master and his disciples happily drank together. Kang then asked Bao about the servant girl he had seen. Bao said, "What the master is asking about, is it not a girl as white as snow, with black eyes and white teeth, hair like clouds, and dark and glossy like polished ebony?" Kang replied, "Yes." Bao continued, "This is Xiao Hui, the servant girl in my aunt's room."

    310

    ”丫头极慧黠，善针黹，一定皆偏爱之。年十九矣，犹未有婿也。“康擎杯戏问曰：”如此珍美，日日在前，汝弟兄亦各尝其滋味否？“保微笑曰：”畴不垂涎，第恨其有郤要之狡狯，往往交臂失之，独文炳夙与之交好而已。“康欣然曰：”荷荷，文炳自负高明，乃至汙人清白，岂非得已而不已耶？吾想小蕙端重，恐文炳未必能玷，汝所言亦想当然耳。“保曰：”不然。二人形迹，生及偲皆目击之矣。“康前席曰：”目击何如？“保曰：”偲潜窥于湢室中，生猝遇于花园门之内也。“康大笑而罢。

    Explanation

    310

    The maid is extremely clever and witty, skilled in needlework, and everyone favors her. She is nineteen years old, yet still has no husband. Kang holds a cup and playfully asks, "So precious and beautiful, always in front of you, have you brothers each tasted its flavor?" Bao smiles and says, "Who wouldn't drool over her, but regret her cunning and shrewdness, often missing out on her. Only Wenbing has long been friendly with her." Kang happily says, "Indeed, Wenbing is self-proclaimed as highly intelligent, even to the point of tarnishing someone's purity, isn't it that he has no choice but to do so? I think Xiao Hui is proper and dignified, I doubt Wenbing can defile her, what you say is just an assumption." Bao says, "Not so. The actions of the two, Sheng and Si have all seen with their own eyes." Kang leans forward and asks, "What did you see?" Bao says, "Si secretly peeks into the bathroom, Sheng suddenly encounters her inside the garden gate." Kang laughs heartily and ends the conversation.

    311

    一日，杰质蛮触故事于康，康不能详，文炳从旁述之，康大惭，转戒之曰：“学者当以十三经为根本，廿一史为学问，荒唐子书，知之何异秽墟？”文炳曰：“一事不知，儒者之耻，宰相须用读书人，以其能取多而用宏也。”康曰：“读书变化气质，汝气质如此，何敢称儒？吾虽少长于汝，然而师傅也，汝弟子也。以弟子而上凌师傅，读书何为？且汝自矜儒术者，而淫人婢女，乱人闺阃者乎？”文炳失色，不复敢言。修弟兄亦再三解纷，康始怒息，然终不与文炳接谈。单知之，笞文炳十数，且置酒谢康曰：“丈夫泄愤杯酒间，况师弟乎？弱弟无知，不足与校也。”康唯唯。于是卜夜痛饮。

    Explanation

    311

    One day, Jie asked Kang about the details of the "Mánchù" story, but Kang could not provide a detailed account. Wenbing recounted it from the side, and Kang was greatly ashamed. He then warned Jie, saying, "Scholars should take the Thirteen Classics as their foundation and the Twenty-One Histories as their learning. Knowing absurd and frivolous works is no different from knowing a filthy ruin." Wenbing replied, "Not knowing one thing is a disgrace for a Confucian scholar. A prime minister must be a scholar because they can draw on a wide range of knowledge and apply it broadly." Kang said, "Reading books transforms one's character. Your character is like this; how dare you call yourself a Confucian scholar? Although I am younger than you, I am your teacher, and you are my student. As a student, to overbear your teacher, what is the point of reading? And you, who boast of being a Confucian scholar, are you not the one who seduces a servant girl and disrupts someone's household?" Wenbing turned pale and no longer dared to speak. The brothers of Xiu also repeatedly tried to mediate, and Kang finally calmed down, but he still refused to converse with Wenbing. Dan knew this and lashed Wenbing several times. He then prepared a feast and thanked Kang, saying, "A man vents his anger in a cup of wine; how much more so between a teacher and a student? My weak younger brother is ignorant and not worth arguing with." Kang nodded in agreement. Thus, they drank until dawn.

    312

    单微醉，兴高自述平生得意语，刺刺不休。康乘间谀之曰：“老先生文章政事，皆堪不朽，唯家法稍弛，外人耳而目之，殊可惜耳。”单赩然曰：“老夫家政，自谓不愧石柳。先生今出此言，得毋有所见闻乎？”康曰：“承相爱，故知无不言，只是事涉隐私，不便渎陈也。”单大疑，屏去左右，密诘之，康乃举文炳私小蕙事，附会以告，且曰：“令公郎所亲见者。

    Explanation

    312

    Shan was slightly drunk and, in a state of high spirits, began to recount his life's achievements, talking incessantly. Kang took the opportunity to flatter him, saying, "Elder Scholar, your literary and political achievements are all worthy of lasting fame. However, your family's rules have slightly relaxed, and outsiders observe with both ears and eyes, which is truly a pity." Shan blushed and said, "I consider my family's governance to be without shame, even compared to Shi Liu. If you have seen something, you must have heard or seen it."

    Kang replied, "Because I love you, I will not hold back from telling you everything. But it involves private matters, so it's not easy to speak of them openly." Shan was greatly suspicious and, after removing his attendants, questioned him closely. Kang then fabricated and told him about Wen Bing's private affair with Xiao Hui, and said, "What your honorable young man has personally witnessed."

    313

    老先生为乡里仪型，奈何因小儿女一夕之欢，微瑕之玷，致失乡望？”单固以家法自诩，一旦被人面摘其疵，怒发如雷，掷杯而入，大声索小蕙，挞而鞫之。小蕙不胜棰楚，一一吐实。单怒极，令褫其衣，梆庭柱上，以巨砧杵塞阴中，呼文炳至前合观之。文炳掩面伏地，哭不能起，单叱而鞭之，声色极厉，夫人再四求宽，怒终不息，锁文炳厕中，方归寝所。夫人潜释小蕙，抬之入室，一息奄奄，血濡床席，家人无不泣而怜之。守至夜半，忽矍然而起，大声曰：“奴死必为厉鬼，以报竖儒矣！”言讫，长号数声而绝。上下靡不悲悼。康闻之，颇不自安，托故解馆归，每念及小蕙之事，则浃背汗下。

    Explanation

    313

    The elderly respected man, a model for the village, how could he lose the respect of the villagers because of a single night's pleasure with a young girl, a minor flaw or blemish? Single Guo prided himself on his family discipline, but once his flaws were exposed in public, he became extremely angry, threw the cup down, and entered the room, loudly demanding the presence of Xiao Hui, who he beat and interrogated. Xiao Hui, unable to endure the beating, revealed everything truthfully. Single Guo, in his rage, ordered her clothes to be stripped off and tied her to the pillar in the courtyard, inserting a large pestle into her private parts, and called Wen Bing to view the scene together. Wen Bing covered his face and fell to the ground, crying and unable to stand up, as Single Guo scolded and whipped him, his voice and manner extremely harsh. The lady repeatedly pleaded for leniency, but his anger did not subside, and he locked Wen Bing in the toilet before returning to the bedroom. The lady secretly released Xiao Hui, carried her into the room, where she was barely alive, blood staining the bed and mat, and the family members all wept and felt pity for her. They stayed until midnight, when Xiao Hui suddenly sat up with a start, saying loudly, "I will surely become a vengeful ghost after death to take revenge on the young scholar!" After speaking, she cried out several times and then died. All above and below were deeply saddened. Kang, upon hearing about it, felt quite uneasy, made an excuse to leave the school and return home, and every time he thought of the matter involving Xiao Hui, he sweated profusely all over his back.

    314

    适槐黄近，挑灯夜读，其母李氏，即伯瑟姑母也，亲调鱼羹，送入书室。于窗下见一女子，裸形浴血而立，惊号仆地，旋失女子所在。康即出救母归寝，问何故惊倒，母告以所见，康大惊失色。母曰：“此宅固凶，不可复居，且乡试在即，不如入省会，暂居舅家，倘博一第，另境居宅可也。”康以为然，亟买舟以往，寄居伯瑟家。时伯瑟亦以乡试故，就贡院侧僧舍肄业。康至乃同下帷。一日闲话间，伯瑟忽问：“贵邑有单文柄者，与相识否？”康曰：“弟之门人也。兄胡为问及之？”伯瑟曰：“久慕其才名，昨又从一友人处，得其《惨魂篇》，抉元珠于屈宋矣。味其辞，隐恨殊深。不意其为弟之高徒也。

    Explanation

    314

    As the locust tree turned yellow, indicating late autumn, I lit the lamp and read by night. My mother, Lady Li, who was also my aunt, prepared fish soup and brought it to my study. From the window, I saw a woman standing naked and covered in blood, screaming and then collapsing to the ground. Soon, the woman disappeared. I immediately went out to help my mother back to bed and asked her why she had fainted. She told me what she had seen, and I was deeply shocked. My mother said, "This house is inherently ominous, and we cannot stay here. The provincial examination is approaching. It would be better to go to the provincial capital and temporarily stay with my brother, until I pass the exam, we can find a new house elsewhere." I agreed, and quickly arranged to travel by boat. We stayed at my uncle's house. At the time, my uncle was also preparing for the provincial examination and was studying in a monk's quarters near the examination hall. When I arrived, we began our studies together. One day, during a casual conversation, my uncle suddenly asked, "Do you know someone named Dan Wenbing from your hometown?" I replied, "He is my student. Why do you ask?" My uncle said, "I have long admired his talent. Yesterday, I obtained his 'Tragic Soul' from a friend, and it is as brilliant as the pearls of Qu Yuan and Song Yu. The words are filled with deep hidden resentment. I did not expect him to be your high-disciple."

    315

    ”因出一纸示康，其辞曰：“夜迢修而转侧兮，心似焚以怦怦。缘幽兰之早折兮，悼芳蕙之先零。何恶莸之枝蔓兮，甚贼苗之稂莠。欲剪拔以粪除兮，足而棘刺手。告田父以假其锄锸兮，络冒头而钳制口。冀美人于一晤兮，倏神结而为梦。出衱阇以暇瞩兮，见蓬颗之蔽冢。声嘤嘤以启悲兮，先秋风而听之。魂冉冉其欲离乎窀穸兮，犹逡巡以鼠思。羌儃佪而夷犹兮，非畴昔之娇态。频拭目以端睨兮，徒神奔而鬼怪。讵绮罗之化蝶兮，体袒裼而裸裎。袁冰玉之销铄兮，怆以纵横。妾薄命以贻戚兮，职王孙之故也。君独生以曷欢兮，宁不怀兹楚也！谇曰：已矣！魂其归来兮，毋踯躅以流连。吾将与子同穴兮，心则石而力则锦。”

    Explanation

    315

    He then took out a piece of paper to show Kang, and the words he said were as follows: "The night is long and winding, and I toss and turn, my heart feels like it is burning and beating. Because the fragrant orchid was broken early, I lament the early withering of the fragrant orchid. Why does the evil weed spread its branches, the thief's seed is like a weed. I want to cut it out and remove it with fertilizer, but my feet are stung by thorns, and my hands are pricked. I ask the farmer to lend me his spade and mattock, but my head is covered, and my mouth is clamped. I hope to meet the beautiful lady once, but suddenly, the spirit ties itself into a dream. I step out of the temple to look around leisurely, and I see the grave covered by weeds. The sound of crying opens up sorrow, and I hear it before the autumn wind. My soul slowly wants to leave the tomb, still hesitating like a mouse. Indeed, hesitating and lingering, not the delicate demeanor of the past. I frequently wipe my eyes and look straight ahead, but only spirits run and ghosts appear. How could the silk and brocade transform into butterflies, the body is exposed and naked. The ice and jade of Yuan are melted, sorrow spreads in all directions. I have a thin fate and bring sorrow, it is due to the fault of Wangsun. You alone live, what joy do you have, how can you not feel this sorrow! He said sternly: It is over! Soul, return to me. Do not hesitate and linger. I will be with you in the same grave, my heart is as hard as stone, and my strength is as soft as silk."

    316

    康览之次，曰：“文炳文炳，汝其赋《角弓》！小蕙小蕙，汝其怨《终风》乎？予不任咎也！”伯瑟曰：“敢问何谓也？”康备陈其故，且曰：“弟闻释氏有忏悔之说，场后唤兄为我设一坛斋醮可乎？”伯瑟悚然汗下，痴坐良久，始叹曰：“弟不自尤，尚委咎他人，岂竟欲铁铸大错耶？”于是不欢而散。

    Explanation

    316

    Kang reviewed the matter and said, "Bright and shining, you should compose 'The Bow and the Arrow'! Delicate and subtle, you should lament 'The Final Wind'? I do not bear the blame!" Bese asked, "May I ask what you mean?" Kang fully explained the reason, and added, "I have heard that Buddhism has the doctrine of repentance. After the event, could you call me to set up a ritual offering for me?" Bese was startled and broke out in a cold sweat, sitting in silence for a long time before sighing, "I do not blame myself, yet I still shift the blame onto others. Am I truly trying to make an unalterable mistake?" Thus, they parted without achieving a satisfactory outcome.

    317

    无何，入棘，弟兄适同一号。是夜场内，咸闻女子哭声，深以为怪，唯康颜色沮丧，不饮不食。次夕三更，伯瑟文初就，方假寐，忽闻帘外人声往来，皆云大怪事。伯瑟即启帷出，视见康号前，人如堵墙，心知有异，挤身而入。见康裸坐房檐下，瞠目直视，大叫曰：“单廷献时辰未到，姑纵之。今且犁此贼之舌，再去质证！”言讫，引手自抠其舌，极力拔之，出口四五寸，血流唇外。伯瑟骇甚，力救之，手爪透入舌根，牢不可脱。比官来相验，已连根拔出，昏倒地上，斯须而毙。伯瑟不忍暴其恶，次日出场，领尸而归。是科伯瑟高捷，公车入都，与予交最善，每闻其说如此。文炳赋《惨魂篇》后，半年亦死，其将与蕙结未了缘欤？又闻有传其事于单者，单哂而置之，暴戾如故，迄今无恙。

    Explanation

    317

    Soon after, they entered the imperial examination hall, and the brothers happened to be in the same room. That night in the examination venue, everyone heard the sound of a woman weeping, and they all found it very strange, but Kang's expression was downcast, and he neither drank nor ate. The next night at midnight, Berse, who was just beginning his studies, was dozing, and suddenly heard people talking outside the curtain, all saying it was a very strange thing. Berse immediately opened the curtain and stepped out, seeing that Kang was surrounded by people like a wall, and he knew something was unusual, so he pushed his way in. He saw Kang sitting naked under the eaves, staring wide-eyed and fixedly, and shouting, "It is not yet the time for Dan Tingxian. Let him go for now. Now I will plow this thief's tongue and then go to prove it!" After he finished speaking, he reached out and pulled his own tongue, trying to pull it out, and it came out several inches, with blood flowing outside his lips. Berse was greatly frightened and tried to save him, but his hands and claws penetrated to the root of his tongue, and it could not be pulled out. When the official came to examine the situation, Kang had already pulled his tongue out by the root, fainted on the ground, and died shortly after. Berse could not expose his evil, so the next day he left the examination venue and took the body back with him. In this examination, Berse achieved a high rank, entered the capital by imperial carriage, and was my closest friend, and I often heard him say so. After Wen Bing wrote the "Tragic Soul" chapter, he also died half a year later. Is it that he will complete an unfinished bond with Hui? Someone reported this to Dan, and Dan smiled and dismissed it. He remains as violent and cruel as before and remains unharmed to this day.

    318

    兰岩曰：致二人死于非命，罪何能逭哉！康以小怨则生嫉妒，拔舌而毙，报亦惨矣。至于挟能招忌，文炳亦难免咎。独惜小蕙，名辱身死，为不幸耳。

    　　严十三言其秋试时，同闱一举子，系下江人。夜间偶出登厕，即归号，见所坐矮屋中，烛光映帘，帘上有人影黯然，心殊惧怯，徘徊不敢入。问老军：“何人坐我号中？”老军曰：“乌知其为谁，想亦君之相识也。”举子曰：“汝为我密观之，看是何形状，亟来告我。”老军潜从帘隙窥之，良久却报曰：“其背灯危坐，年可四十许，瘦黄面，短黑髯，无甚异人处，惟鬓旁毡帽下，斜插一红漆竹箸，为不可解耳。”举子闻之，骤惊，狂叫“有鬼”而走。老军追问之，但蹲身栅边，摆手摇头，不敢复返。老军白诸职事，问之，亦陨涕不言。重至其号视之，已失鬼之所在，恐生他变，令人守之。次日送出，终莫测红箸之由。

    Explanation

    318

    Lan Yan said: To have led to the death of two people in a violent manner, how can one escape punishment! Kang harbored resentment and thus generated jealousy, ultimately dying by having his tongue pulled out; the retribution was indeed cruel. As for excelling and attracting envy, Wen Bing also could not avoid blame. It is truly unfortunate that Xiao Hui suffered dishonor and died.

    Yan San recounted his experience during the imperial examination. A fellow candidate in the same examination hall was from the lower Yangtze region. At night, he happened to go out to the toilet and then returned to his examination room, where he saw a dim shadow on the curtain in the small room where he sat, illuminated by candlelight. He was very afraid and hesitated to enter. He asked the old soldier, "Who is sitting in my examination room?" The old soldier replied, "How would I know who it is? Perhaps you know him." The candidate said, "You should secretly observe it for me, look at what shape it has, and come and tell me quickly." The old soldier secretly peered through the gap in the curtain. After a long time, he reported back, "He is sitting upright with his back to the light, about forty years old, with a thin, yellow face and short black beard. There is nothing particularly unusual about him, only a red lacquered bamboo chopstick was slanted in his hat beside his temples, which is inexplicable." Upon hearing this, the candidate was suddenly terrified, shouted "There's a ghost!" and ran away. The old soldier asked him again, but he only crouched by the fence, shaking his head and hands, and dared not return. The old soldier reported to the officials, who asked him, but he shed tears and said nothing. They went back to his examination room to look, and the ghost was no longer there. Fearing other changes, they ordered someone to guard it. They sent him out the next day, and in the end, the reason for the red chopstick remained a mystery.

    319

    兰曰：隐微事，夫谁知其怨所结者乎？鬼既能寻至闱中，而但示其形，使其惊狂奔避，抑之何故？噫！异矣，乾隆丙子科，顺天乡试，有大书于卷面者，曰：“黄四姑娘开拆，见蓝榜。”

    　　监生润玉，弱冠有文誉，丰姿韶秀，如玉山照人，同学以翰苑期之，玉亦自命不凡，视赋《鹿鸣》，捷南宫，如拾地芥耳，所居偪邻尚书某公之宅。尚书有女，已字侯门，尚未遣嫁，而才慧容色，名动一时。玉偶见之于升车时，素面隔碧纱，如春烟之笼秋棠也。归而思慕，不能一刻置。一日闲步后圃，闻隔墙女子嗽声，急设梯于柳荫中，登而窥之，则尚书宅内之溷轩也。见一女丽甚，识为车中人，方登厕，兰烟出口，臀白如霜，玉目夺神摇，犹恨不能满意。

    Explanation

    319

    Ah! It is indeed strange, in the year 1776 during the Qianlong era, during the provincial examination in Shuntian, there was a message prominently written on the examination paper: "Yellow Four Miss opens and sees the blue list."

    Ruan Yu, a student, had literary fame at the age of twenty, with a handsome and graceful appearance, like a bright jade mountain. His classmates expected him to become a scholar-official, and Yu also considered himself extraordinary, viewing the composition of "The Deer's Cry" and success in the imperial examinations as easy as picking up a straw from the ground. He lived next to the residence of a certain minister. The minister's daughter had already been betrothed to a noble family but had not yet been married, and she was intelligent and beautiful, famous at the time. Yu saw her getting into her carriage, with a plain face behind green gauze, like spring mist enveloping autumn peonies. He returned home and longed for her, unable to stop thinking about her for a moment. One day, he walked idly in the garden behind the house, heard the coughing sound of a woman on the other side of the wall, quickly set up a ladder under the willow shade, climbed and peered through, and it was the latrine of the minister's residence. He saw a very beautiful woman, recognized her as the woman in the carriage, who was just entering the latrine. Violet smoke came out of her mouth, her buttocks were white as frost, Yu's eyes were captivated and his spirit shaken, still feeling unsatisfied.

    320

    日暮人静，乃暗于花荫密处，壁脚下凿去半砖，使洞彻不碍目，终日观之，于是女之稳私，无有不为玉所谛见者。积半年，女已出阁，玉无从再窥，颇怅恨。因冥想其私处，有朱色痣一点，为赋《长相思》词以咏之。为一友所见，举而投诸火，正色责之，并诫其无复举以告人，于德行有大累，玉笑其迂。后入闱，夜梦一人抉其目，痛甚而寤。恶之，而目痛不止，两瞳如针刺，不能启睫，遂缴白卷而出。归家三日，痛不绝，遂成双瞽。及晓揭，毁词友人已魁列矣。

    　　兰岩曰：一言规谏，遂获高魁；一意淫恶，便成双瞽。慎之，戒之！

    　　福斋堂曰：始而私之于己，既而篡之于人，致幽闺贞体，不啻裸游于五都市，诚所谓玩人丧德者矣。夫瞽于目者，必先瞽于心也；高其名者，必先高其品也。名教中自有乐地，一失足即蹈苦海，故君子必慎其独也。杨慎远窜夷僰，犹传《杂事秘辛》，宜其终身不齿，才人其鉴之哉！

    Explanation

    320

    As dusk fell and the people quieted, Yu hid in the dense shade of the flowers and chipped away half a brick from the base of the wall, creating a clear peephole. He observed her all day, and thus, her private matters were not hidden from Yu's eyes. After half a year, the woman was married, and Yu could no longer spy on her, feeling quite regretful and resentful. He then meditated on her private parts, noticing a red mole, and composed a poem "Chang Xiang Si" to praise it. A friend saw the poem, picked it up, and threw it into the fire, scolding Yu sternly and warning him not to repeat such actions to others, as it was a great burden on virtue and conduct. Yu laughed at his friend's foolishness. Later, when Yu entered the examination hall, he had a nightmare where a person pulled out his eyes, causing him great pain and awakening him. He hated the dream, and his eye pain did not stop; both pupils felt like they were being pricked by needles, and he could not open his eyelids. Consequently, he submitted a blank paper and left. Upon returning home for three days, the pain persisted, and he became completely blind. When the results were announced, the friend who destroyed the poem had already ranked first.

    Lan Yan said: "A single word of advice led to high honors; one intent on lust and evil resulted in blindness. Be cautious, and take heed!"

    Fuzhizhong said: "He first kept it for himself, then shared it with others, leading to the exposure of her private body, as if she were nakedly wandering through the five major cities. This is truly what is meant by 'playing with people and losing one's virtue.' Those who become blind in the eyes must first become blind in the heart; those who achieve fame must first cultivate high moral character. Within the teachings of names and conduct, there is a place of joy, but one misstep can lead to a sea of suffering. Therefore, gentlemen must be cautious in their solitude. Yang Shèn was exiled to the remote and barbarous land of Yibi, yet his 'Miscellaneous Secrets' still circulated, and he was forever disgraced. Talented individuals should take this as a warning!"

    321

    蔡生，江左名士也。公车入都，馆满洲某氏家。其家主人物故已久，唯主母孀居，抚一子一女，仆婢十数人，一老仆已历三世矣，诚悫忠靖，主母待之甚厚。仆亦素重蔡名，深喜幼主得师，敬礼不替。会主母将嫁女，乏妆奁之资。畿内有田数顷，使仆征其租，以岁歉，去月余，仅获八十金以归。计之不敷用，仍付之曰：“谚云：”饭到口，钱到手‘。零星费去，至时愈不足用。汝姑收贮，待取足总缴可也。“仆诺而退，自念身常出外，妻子头钝不解事，倘此银有失，大事去矣。乃携入馆中，以情告蔡，乞代为存贮。时左右无人，蔡即纳于箱中而锁之，曰：”汝第去办正事，寄此无妨也。“仆谢而去。

    Explanation

    321

    P, a renowned scholar from the Jiangnan region, traveled to the capital by public carriage and stayed at the home of a Manchu family. The family's male members had passed away long ago, leaving only the lady of the house as a widow, who raised one son and one daughter with the help of ten or more servants, including an old servant who had served the family for three generations. This old servant was known for his sincerity, honesty, loyalty, and respectfulness, and the lady of the house treated him with great kindness. The servant, who had always respected P's reputation, was deeply pleased that the young master had gained a teacher and continued to show him the same respect and courtesy. When the lady of the house was about to marry off her daughter and lacked the funds for the dowry, she sent the servant to collect the rent from their land in the vicinity. Due to a poor harvest, after more than a month, he only managed to collect eighty gold pieces and returned. Calculating the amount, it was not enough, so she gave it to him and said, "As the saying goes, 'Food reaches the mouth, money reaches the hand.' If spent in small amounts, it will be even less sufficient by then. You might as well keep it in storage, wait until you have enough, and then return it." The servant nodded and left, thinking to himself that he often goes out, and his wife and children are simple and don't understand things. If this money is lost, it will be a major disaster. He then took it to the inn where P was staying and told P about the situation, asking P to store it for him. At the time, there were no people around, so P immediately put it in a box and locked it, saying, "You just go ahead and handle the main business, leaving this here is fine." The servant thanked and left.

    322

    又半月，征得余金，复命，主母索前项，仆曰：“在蔡先生处。”即往取之。蔡不承，曰：“汝那得有银寄我处？”仆曰：“先生无戏言，可即见付。”蔡怒曰：“何物老奴，敢来诬我！我为汝家教子弟，岂为汝家作看财奴耶？不速退，当惹老拳矣！”仆大惊，争辩不已。蔡声色俱厉，即刻欲解馆。母闻之，立门外扬声慰蔡曰：“先生勿气，吾当为先生责此叛奴。”蔡始不言，主母呼仆入，痛责之曰：“先生读书人，且南方名士，希图我家数十两银耶？此必汝将去自救燃眉，卒乃诬罔好人。吾母子孤儿寡妇，出门跬步不能行，所赖者汝一人耳。今有若此，尚何望乎？”言讫，掩面而泣。仆无以自明，但自批其病，以自骂，是夜遂缢死。

    322

    After another half month, the remaining funds were gathered, and the servant reported back to the mistress, who demanded the previous amount. The servant replied, "It is at Mr. Cai's place." He immediately went to retrieve it. Mr. Cai refused to acknowledge it, saying, "How did you manage to have silver deposited with me?" The servant insisted, "Mr. Cai, you would not jest; please give it to me." Mr. Cai, furious, shouted, "What kind of old servant dares to accuse me! I am here to teach your children, not to be your family's keeper of money! Do not stay, or you will get a beating!" The servant was greatly surprised and continued to argue. Mr. Cai spoke with a stern voice and stern expression, immediately wanting to terminate the contract. The mother heard this, stood outside, and loudly comforted Mr. Cai, saying, "Mr. Cai, do not be angry; I will punish this rebellious servant for you." Mr. Cai finally fell silent. The mistress called the servant inside and severely reprimanded him, saying, "Mr. Cai is a scholar and a Southern gentleman; would you be plotting to steal dozens of taels of silver from our house? This must be because you were about to leave to save yourself from a crisis, and so you falsely accused an innocent person. My mother and I are an orphan and a widow; we cannot even take a step outside without your help. Now with this, what can we expect?" She covered her face and wept. The servant could not prove his innocence, so he beat himself for his illness and cursed himself. That night, he hanged himself.

    323

    次年，蔡入闱，精神恍惚，下帷秉烛，亲笔备录其事于纸，自述昧心蔑理，罪不可逭，解带自缢于黄茆白苇中。比人知觉，体已冰矣。尤可怪者，面壁端坐，带环去喉寸余，不解何由至死。其自供之词，众争录之。仁和叶省三先生，亦录得一纸，每出以示人，以为文人无行者戒。

    　　兰岩曰：予尝见世之所谓名士矣，曳长裾，舞大袖，以为名士风流；吟诗论文，弈棋饮酒，以为名士博雅。而究其中，则绝少躬行；核其实，则每多不肖。而第观其肤末者，未尝不群然信之，曰：“此名士也。焉有为此者乎？”唯其名士也，而能为此耳。亦唯称其名士也，而后知人之深信不疑，而敢为此耳。

    Explanation

    323

    The following year, Cai entered the examination hall, his mind confused and his spirit dazed. He lowered the curtain and held the candle, writing down the events in detail on paper. He confessed to acting against his conscience and despising reason, acknowledging that his guilt was inescapable. He then hanged himself by loosening his belt in the reeds and yellow grass of Huangmao Baiwei. When others discovered him, his body had already turned cold. What was particularly strange was that he was found sitting facing a wall, with the belt loop about an inch away from his throat, and no one could understand why it led to his death. His self-confession was recorded by everyone who vied to capture it. Mr. Ye Shengsan of Renhe also recorded it on a sheet of paper, often showing it to others as a warning to those who lack moral conduct among the literati.

    Lan Yan remarked: "I have often seen what is called a scholar-official in this world, dragging long robes and swaying wide sleeves, believing this to be the elegance of a scholar-official; reciting poetry and discussing literature, playing chess and drinking wine, believing this to be the erudition of a scholar-official. But when one examines their inner character, there is rarely any practice of virtue; when one scrutinizes their true nature, there are often many who are unworthy. Yet those who merely observe the superficial aspects of their behavior never fail to believe in them, saying, 'This is a scholar-official. How could such a person do such things?' It is precisely because they are scholar-officials that they are able to do such things. And it is only by calling them scholar-officials that people deeply believe in them and dare to do such things."

    324

    某科乡试，一生构文至半夜，瞥见一人，披帷而入，古衣古冠，面目甚怪，生口噤不能言。其伸一掌，向生曰：“我司文之神也。汝祖宗有阴德，今科当领荐，可书一字于吾掌，为异日填榜之验。”生大喜，即濡墨大书一“魁”字，其人遂灭，而字故在卷上，墨渍数重，因被贴出。

    　　恩茂先言：一士子临场祈梦于泡子河畔吕公祠，梦见一人，如画家所绘寿星，头状粘白纸条，自内而出。觉而异之，既入棘，以犯例被贴。或解之曰：“盖头场贴出也。”一笑。

    　　闲斋曰：棘闱之地，国家设以取士者也。堕行丧德之徒，冥报昭然，毫厘不爽。如是，何关节怀挟者，犹敢于光天化日中，行险以侥幸哉？

    Explanation

    324

    During a certain provincial examination, a scholar was writing his essay until midnight when he suddenly saw a figure enter through a curtain. The figure wore ancient clothing and a crown, with a very strange appearance. The scholar was so frightened that he could not speak. The figure extended a hand and said to the scholar, "I am the god in charge of the exam papers. Your ancestors have performed good deeds, and you will be selected this year. Write a character on my hand as a sign of your future success." The scholar was greatly pleased and immediately dipped his ink and wrote a large "guì" (meaning "first place") on the figure's hand. The figure then disappeared, but the character remained on the paper, with multiple layers of ink, and the scholar was indeed selected as a successful candidate.

    According to Mr. Enmaoxian, a scholar once prayed for a dream at the Temple of Mr. Lü by Baozi River before the exam. He dreamed of a person resembling a painter's depiction of the longevity star, with a white paper strip attached to the head. Upon waking, he found the dream strange. When he entered the exam hall, he was disqualified for violating a rule. Someone explained that it meant he was supposed to be selected in the first round. He laughed.

    Mr. Xianzhai said: The exam hall is a place set up by the state to select scholars. Those who act immorally will face divine retribution, with no room for error. How can those who try to cheat or manipulate the system still dare to risk everything in broad daylight, hoping for侥幸?

    325

    兰岩曰：秀人入闱，一存得失之心，则方寸中，顷刻百端丛生，而鬼神遂乘得其隙，而侮弄之，因而摈斥之。是二子者，焉知非妄想心炽，精神恍惚，而结成一幻境哉！

    　　回煞五则人死有回煞之说，都下犹信之。有举话出避者，虽贵家巨族，亦必空其室，以避他所，谓之躲殃。至期，例扫徐亡人所居之室，炕上地下，遍筛布芦布；凡有铜钱，悉以白纸封之，恐鬼畏之也。更于炕头设矮几，几上陈火酒一杯，煮鸡子数枚，燃灯一盏，反扃其户。次日，鸣铁器开门，验灰土有鸡距、虎爪，马蹄、蛇足等迹，种种不一。大抵亡人所属何相，即现何迹，以卜亡人罪孽之重轻，谓锁罪轻而绳罪重也。草木鸡犬，往往有遭之而枯毙者。习俗移人，贤者不免，所谓相率成风，牢不可破者也。第其理未可尽诬，或者死者有知，归省所恋欤？

    Explanation

    325

    Lan Yan said: When talented individuals enter the examination hall, if they harbor a mindset focused on success or failure, then in their hearts, a hundred worries arise in an instant. The spirits then take advantage of this vulnerability to mock and deceive them, and as a result, they are rejected. These two individuals, how can we know that their rejection was not due to their hearts being filled with delusional thoughts, leading to a state of mental confusion and the creation of an illusory world?

    The Five Rules of Returning Souls: There is a belief that the soul of the deceased returns to the house on the fifth day after death, and this belief is still held in the capital. Some people raise the matter and leave their homes to avoid it, even if they are from noble families or large clans. They must empty their rooms to avoid the return of the soul, which is called "dodging disaster." On the designated day, they sweep the room where the deceased lived according to custom, spreading straw mats evenly on the bed and the floor. All copper coins are sealed with white paper, because spirits will fear them. Furthermore, on the head of the bed, a low table is set up, with a cup of fire wine placed on it, several boiled eggs, and one lamp is lit. The door is locked. The next day, a metal instrument is rung to open the door, and the ashes are inspected for traces of chicken claws, tiger paws, horse hooves, and snake feet. Various and not the same. Generally, the deceased belongs to what aspect, will appear what traces, to predict the severity of the deceased's sins. Said that binding sins are lighter, while rope sins are heavier. Plants, chickens, and dogs often suffer and die. Customs influence people, even virtuous people cannot avoid it. So-called, follow each other and become a trend, firm and unbreakable. But the principle cannot be completely discredited. Perhaps the dead have knowledge, returning to visit what they love?

    326

    予友德书绅，不幸短命。方其弱冠时，季弟殁，出殃之夕，德不信，一更后，潜至窗下窥之。室中一灯莹莹，毫无所见，因笑流俗之妄。才思却回，忽见小旋风起灯下，有墨物如鱼网，罩几上，灯焰绿如莹火，光敛如钱，倏暗。德伏窗外，如醉又如梦，不能动履。但觉灯明则神思如寤，灯暗则毛发尽张。俄尔墨物不见，灯骤明，德气始舒畅，闻耳畔有声甚杂，盖家人寻觅至此，呼叫之也。德面色如土，数日失神。每向予述之，为不妄也。

    Explanation

    326

    My friend, De Shushen, was unfortunately short-lived. When he was in his early twenties, his youngest brother died. On the night of the mourning, De did not believe it. After one watch, he sneaked up to the window and peered inside. In the room, there was a bright, clear light, and he saw nothing, so he laughed at the foolishness of the common people. Suddenly, his thoughts turned back, and he saw a small whirlwind rise under the lamp. There was a dark object like a fishing net covering the table, and the lamp flame was green like a bright fire, gathering like a coin, and then suddenly went dark. De crouched outside the window, dazed and dreaming, unable to move. He only felt that when the light was on, his spirit was as if awake, and when the light was off, all his hairs stood on end. Suddenly, the dark object disappeared, and the light suddenly brightened. De's breath began to feel comfortable, and he heard many mixed sounds near his ear. It was because family members were searching here and calling out. De's face was as pale as soil, and he was in a daze for several days. He often told me about it, and it is not false.

    327

    兰岩曰：神气为鬼所夺，所以不寿耳。

    　　同学锡谷斋，尝言其一亲戚家，有塾师新死。际回煞之夜，主人矫俗弊，无所陈设。次日黎明，谷斋以事过之，主人未起，暂就书房中坐候之。馆僮入取茶，谷斋独坐炕头吸烟，忽见一黑物，如乱发一团，去地尺余，旋转不已，渐近衣袂。执祛审视，不辨是何物，初大如升，渐如碗，如杯，滚入炕洞中，一半在外，犹转不已，久之始没。窃异之。馆僮取茶至，问之，结舌不能对，愈增疑惑。及主人出，复质之，初亦茫然，继乃大悟，曰：“得毋回煞之说，未可尽诬乎？”因告以塾师之事，共相叹惋，疑团始释，既而谷斋笑曰：“幸我月令尚好，不然，其不得殃也几希矣！”

    327
    Lanyan remarked: "The spirit is taken by the ghost, that is why he does not live long."

A fellow student named Xigu Zhai once recounted a story about a relative's family where a private tutor had recently died. On the night of the "returning soul," the owner corrected the vulgar superstitions by not setting up any offerings. The next morning, at dawn, Xigu Zhai went to the house and waited in the study for the owner to wake up. A student servant went to fetch tea, and Xigu Zhai sat alone on the kang, smoking, when he suddenly saw a black object, like a tangled mass of hair, about a foot above the ground, rotating continuously, gradually moving closer to his sleeve. He held the sleeve and examined it closely but could not identify what it was. Initially, it was as large as a winnowing basket, then gradually became the size of a bowl, and finally a cup, rolling into the stove hole. Half of it remained outside, still turning without stopping, and after a while, it finally disappeared. Xigu Zhai found it strange. When the servant brought the tea, he asked him about it, but the servant remained speechless, increasing his confusion. When the owner came out, Xigu Zhai asked him again, and the owner was initially also confused but then suddenly understood, saying, "Is it not possible that the belief in 'returning the soul' is not entirely false?" He then told the story of the private tutor, and they all expressed regret, resolving the doubts. Afterwards, Xigu Zhai smiled and said, "Fortunately, my monthly fortune is still good; otherwise, the chance of not being affected would be very slim!"
    328

    兰岩曰：鬼者阴之灵，至虚且幻，兹何具有形象耶？又不解其入炕洞何为？

    　　城北徐公家，一老妪死，际回煞。徐二子皆少年好事，相约往观。初无怪异，将去之，灯忽骤暗，隐隐见一物，如象鼻，就器吸酒，咕咕有声，釶然坠地上，化为大猫，而人面白如粉，绕地旋转，若有所见。二子惊悸，发狂震骇。家人诘得其故，交责不已。次日，启户视之，鸡子酒浆，空无所有，灰上人迹，两两相并，仅如二三岁小儿。东壁书十一字，非篆非草，淡墨色，人不能识，向午忽自灭，洵为鬼笔。徐二子相继病死。

    Explanation

    328

    Lan Yan said: Ghosts are the spirits of the yin, extremely insubstantial and illusory. How can they have any form? And why would they enter the stove hole?

    At the Xu family home in the northern part of the city, an elderly woman died during the period of ji huishah (a time believed to be particularly dangerous or significant, often associated with funerals). Xu's two sons, both young and curious, agreed to go and observe. Initially, there were no strange occurrences, but as they were about to leave, the lamp suddenly grew dim. A vague shape appeared, resembling an elephant's trunk, and it approached a vessel, drinking wine and making a gurgling sound. It then fell to the ground with a thud, transforming into a large cat. The person's face was white like powder, and it spun around the ground as if seeing something. The two sons were startled and went mad with fear. The family questioned and found out the reason, blaming each other continuously.

    The next day, they opened the door to look and found that the eggs and wine were empty. There were footprints in the ashes, paired together, only about the size of a two or three-year-old child. On the eastern wall, there were eleven characters written, neither seal script nor cursive, in light ink. People could not recognize them, and they suddenly disappeared at noon. Truly, it was the writing of a ghost. Xu's two sons died one after another.

    329

    兰岩曰：鬼有饮食，大为奇怪。想人踪两两，即徐氏二子之魂魄耳。相继病死，其理可悟。

    　　延安折天桂为广文时，佣一老妪，服役炊爨。其子黄椿，年二十余，为郡驿卒。某年冬，从榆镇差官马进，驰羽书入都，道经某堡，大雨雪。堡固荒僻，无客舍，投宿民家。其家辟正室以居之，马以为敬己，深德之。与黄夜饭讫，同炕而寝。二更后，黄忽惊惶，闻屋后声甚异，心殊怛怖，连呼马老爷不应，急起敲火烛之，则马方袒跣向隅，蹲踞地上，耸肩用力，若有所捺。黄惊询何为，马但摇首，不暇应答。

    Explanation

    329

    Lan Yan said: Ghosts having food and drink is highly unusual. It seems that the footsteps were in pairs, indicating the spirits of the two sons of the Xu family. They died one after another, and the reason can be understood.

    During the time when Zhe Tianguī was serving as a scholar-official in Yan'an, he hired an elderly woman to serve and cook. His son, Huang Chun, who was in his twenties, was a postal officer for the county. In a certain year during winter, an official named Ma Jin from Yuzhen rushed urgent messages to the capital, passing through a certain fort where it rained heavily with snow. The fort was isolated and remote, with no guesthouses, so they sought lodging in a civilian home. The family opened the main room to accommodate him, and Ma considered it a sign of respect and deeply appreciated it. After dinner, Ma and Huang slept on the same kang. After the second watch, Huang suddenly became alarmed, hearing a very unusual sound behind the house, feeling extremely frightened, and continuously calling out to Master Ma without receiving a response. He quickly lit a fire and candle to see, and found that Ma was naked and barefoot facing the corner, squatting on the ground, shouldering up and exerting force, as if being pressed down. Huang asked what was happening, but Ma only shook his head, too preoccupied to answer.

    330

    黄大疑，急前审谛之，见壁角有物，形如蝟，被捺唧唧作声，渐捺渐缩，不禁大骇，欲前助力，物忽化为浓烟，滚滚四散，成数十团，或钻入壁隙，或飞上棚顶，须臾而尽。黄扶马坐息炕间，问所捉何物，马哆口瞠目，犹有余恐。良久稍定，始述曰：“吾方起溲，瞥见一婆娑老妪，徘徊炕下，两眼有光如莹，颇能自照，心知为鬼，以杖击之，仆地化为一蝟，走向屋角，故就而捺之，诚不识为何怪也。”黄闻之，栗生于肌，发竖于顶，不敢复寝。亟呼主人诘之，支离不以实告。马伪怒，欲鸣于官。主人惧，因言其故，盖其祖母新死，是夜正回煞之候也，叩其体貌，正符所见。马为之叹惋，遂不复少留，束装秣马，冒雪宵征。

    Explanation

    330

    A sudden doubt arose in Huang, and he hurried forward to examine closely. He saw an object in the corner of the wall, shaped like a hedgehog. When he pressed it down, it made a sound and gradually shrunk. He could not help but be greatly frightened. He wanted to go forward and help, but the object suddenly turned into dense smoke, rolling and scattering in all directions, forming dozens of clumps. Some squeezed into cracks in the wall, while others flew up to the roof, disappearing in a moment. Huang supported the horse and rested on the kang. He asked what the object caught was, and the horse had its mouth open and was staring, still having lingering fear. After a long time, it gradually calmed down and began to describe: "I just went to urinate and saw an old woman with a graceful appearance wandering under the kang. Her eyes shone like jewels and could self-illuminate. I knew in my heart that it was a ghost, so I hit it with a stick. It fell to the ground and turned into a hedgehog, then went to the corner of the house. Therefore, I approached and pressed it down. Truly, I did not know what kind of monster it was." Upon hearing this, chills arose in Huang's muscles, and his hair stood up on his head. He did not dare to sleep again. He hurriedly called the master and questioned him, but the master stammered and did not tell the truth. The horse pretended to be angry and wanted to report to the authorities. The master was afraid and therefore said the reason. It is said that his grandmother just died, and that night was exactly the time of the return of the soul. When he asked about its appearance, it exactly matched what was seen. The horse sighed and felt sorrow, so he did not stay any longer. He packed his belongings, fed the horse, and set out in the snow at night.

    331

    兰岩曰：倏而妪，倏而蝟，倏而烟，煞大作怪。

    　　秦人谓大为老。有张老嘴者，又号老胆，以口大胆大而得名也。其子妇死，值回煞，张出差在外，未之知也，是夜适归，叩门久无应者，怒发，排闼而入，重门亦如之。至厅前一间，无一人，唯西厢灯火耿耿，阴念：“此屋从无人居，那得灯光？”试觑之，倏见一妇人，长仅尺余，直扑窗隙，仓卒惊却数步。妇人甫出窗，旋化黑烟一团，随风而散。张知为鬼物，不复踪迹。亟叩宅门，家人闻之，大扰，良久始辨其音响，开门纳而告之故。张乃叹惋，盖子妇病笃，不便终于正寝，移之西厢，逾夕而殁。张所见，妇之鬼也，是适值出殃云。张竟无恙？

    　　兰岩曰：张其阳旺耶？不然，何竟无恙。

    　　夜星子二则京师某宦家，其祖留一妾，年九十余，甚老耄。居后房，上下呼为老姨。日坐炕头，不言不笑，不能动履，形似饥鹰。而健食无疾病。尝畜一猫，与相守不离，寝食共之。

    Explanation

    331

    Translation:

    Lan Yan said: Suddenly an old woman, suddenly a porcupine, suddenly smoke, truly a great disturbance.

    In the Qin region, the word for "big" is "old." There was a man named Zhang, who was also called "Old Bold" because of his bold mouth and large daring. His daughter-in-law died, and it happened to be the time of the return of the spirit (回煞). Zhang was out on official business and did not know about it. That night, he happened to return home, and after knocking on the door for a long time with no answer, he became angry and pushed open the door, even entering through multiple doors. He entered the hall and found no one in the room, only the lights in the west chamber still burning. He thought to himself, "This house has never been occupied, how could there be lights?" He tried to look, and suddenly saw an old woman, barely a foot tall, darting through the window gap. He was startled and took several steps back. The old woman just left the window, and then transformed into a black smoke that dispersed with the wind. Zhang realized it was a ghost and did not pursue it further. He quickly knocked on the main door, and upon hearing him, the family was greatly disturbed. After a long time, they finally recognized his voice and opened the door to tell him the reason. Zhang sighed, saying, "My daughter-in-law was seriously ill and could not die in the main bedroom, so she was moved to the west chamber, and she died after spending one night there." What Zhang saw was the ghost of the old woman, which coincided with the time when the spirit was supposed to return. Zhang was unharmed in the end.

    Lan Yan said: Could Zhang have strong yang energy? Otherwise, why was he unharmed?

    Two Stories of Night Stars: In the capital, there was a certain official's household. His ancestor left a concubine behind, who was over ninety years old and very old. She lived in the back room, and everyone called her "Old Aunt." Every day, she sat at the head of the kang, neither speaking nor laughing, unable to move or walk, looking like a hungry eagle. Yet, she ate well and had no illnesses. She once kept a cat, which she stayed with without separation, sleeping and eating together.

    332

    宦一幼子，尚在襁褓，夜夜啼号，至晓方辍，匝月不愈，患之。俗传小儿夜啼，谓之夜星子，即有能捉之者。于是延捉者至家，礼待甚厚。捉者一半老妇人耳。是夕就小儿旁，设桑弧桃矢，长大不过五寸，矢上系素丝数丈，理其端于无名之指，而拈之。至夜半，月色上窗，儿啼暂作，顷之，隐隐见窗纸有影，倏进倏却，仿佛一妇人，长六七寸，操戈骑马而行。捉者摆手低语曰：“夜星子来矣，来矣！”亟弯弓射之，中肩，唧唧有声，弃戈返骑，捉者越窗引线，率众逐之，拾其戈观之，一搓线小竹签也。迹至后房，其丝竟入门隙，群呼老姨不应，因排其闼，燃烛入室，遍觅无所见。搜索久之，忽一小婢，惊指曰：“老姨中箭矣！”众视之，果见小矢钉老姨肩上，呻吟不已，而所畜猫犹在跨下也。咸大错愕，亟为拔矢，血流不止。捉者命扑杀其猫，小儿因不复夜啼，老姨亦由此得病，数日亦死。

    Explanation

    332

    A Servant's Young Child

    A servant had a young child who was still in swaddling clothes. The child cried and wailed every night until dawn, and this condition persisted for a month without improvement, causing great concern. Folklore held that children who cried at night were afflicted by the "night star child," and there were those who claimed to be able to catch it. Thus, the parents invited such a catcher to their home and treated her with great courtesy. The catcher turned out to be an old woman. That night, she set up a willow bow and peach arrow, no more than five inches long, with several yards of white silk tied to the arrow, the end of which was fastened to the ring finger. She held it in place. By midnight, moonlight shone through the window, and the child cried briefly. After a while, a faint shadow appeared on the window paper, suddenly advancing and retreating, resembling a woman about six to seven inches tall, wielding a spear and riding a horse. The old woman waved her hand and whispered, "The night star child has come, has come!" She quickly drew the bow and shot at it, hitting the shoulder. The night star child made a squeaking sound, dropped the spear, and turned back to ride away. The old woman jumped through the window and pulled the string, leading the crowd in pursuit. They picked up the spear and examined it, finding it to be a small bamboo stick tied with thread. The trail led to the back room, and the thread finally entered the gap in the door. The crowd called out to the old aunt but got no response, so they forced open the door, lit a candle, and entered the room. They searched everywhere but found nothing. After searching for a long time, suddenly a little maid pointed and said in surprise, "The old aunt was hit by an arrow!" The crowd looked at it and indeed saw a small arrow stuck in the old aunt's shoulder, who was moaning continuously, with the cat she kept still under her. All were greatly startled, and they quickly pulled out the arrow, but blood flowed continuously. The old woman ordered the cat to be killed, and the child then stopped crying at night. The old aunt also fell ill because of this and died a few days later.

    333

    兰岩曰：怪出于老姨，诚不知其何为。想系猫之所为，老妇龙钟，为其所使耳。卒乃中箭而亡，不亦冤乎？

    　　子在咸安寓时，闻同学隆君兴言：其一亲戚家，有小儿夜啼，越两月不愈。有老妪识为夜星子，自云能捉之。问所需，无难办者：唯用木作方笼，四面糊白纸，罨灶上，灶窟内设油灯一盏，燃之，光射纸上。俟小儿啼作，即灶前覆一粗磁碗，碗上横置一菜刀，踞小凳面灶门而坐。家人悉令回避，童男稚女则弗禁。时隆君年甫十二三，立妪身后观焉。妪一手叩刀，哝哝不解作何语。食顷，灯骤暗，纸上隐隐见黑影，往来闪烁不定，或人、或马、或猫犬，悉仿佛其形。妪诅咒愈急，灯愈暗，黑影往来愈伙，最后一影，色黯黝，映纸独真，止而不动，形颇似槥。妪急举刀背，力碎覆碗，砉然一声，烛中灯忽大明，黑影印纸上不灭，如淡墨所染。妪举笼以火焚之，儿啼顿止。

    Explanation

    333

    Lan Yan said: The strange event came from the old aunt; truly, we don't know what it was. Probably it was caused by the cat, as the old woman, though feeble, was being made to do it. Finally, she was shot and died; isn't that a pity?

    When I was staying in Xianan, I heard my classmate Long Junxing say: In one of his relatives' homes, there was a child who cried at night, and it lasted for two months without healing. There was an old woman who identified it as a night star child and claimed she could catch it. When asked what she needed, there was nothing difficult to provide: only a wooden square cage was required, with white paper covering the four sides, placed over the stove, with an oil lamp set inside the stove cavity. When lit, the light shone on the paper. After waiting until the child started crying, a coarse porcelain bowl was covered in front of the stove, with a kitchen knife placed horizontally on top of it, and the old woman sat on a small stool facing the stove door. All family members were ordered to stay away, but young boys and girls were not restricted. At that time, Long Junxing was only twelve or thirteen years old and stood behind the old woman to watch. The old woman tapped the knife with one hand and muttered something incomprehensible. After a meal, the lamp suddenly went dark, and a dark shadow appeared faintly on the paper, moving and flickering uncertainly, sometimes human, sometimes horse, sometimes cat or dog, all resembling their shapes. The old woman cursed more urgently, and the lamp became darker, with the dark shadow moving more frequently. The last shadow was dark and black, clearly reflected on the paper, stopped and did not move, and its shape was quite like a coffin. The old woman quickly raised the back of the knife, with force broke the covered bowl, and a sharp sound was heard. The lamp in the candle suddenly became bright, and the dark shadow remained on the paper without fading, as if it had been lightly stained with ink. The old woman raised the cage and burned it with fire, and the child's crying stopped immediately.

    334

    兰岩曰：如此怪异，非老妪不能降，亦非老妪不能解。

    　　尸变二则陕西某村胡氏女，嫁为李家妇。一朝反目，女负气出门，不知所之，李以为归其母家，往探之，未尝归也。遍叩亲故，皆无有。遂成讼，有司严刑拷掠，不能成狱，缧绁者岁余。村后固接乱山，人踪罕到，唯刍荛雉兔者，间一过焉。一樵入林伐木，于万树中，见一人悬柏树上，目大如盏，举两手作扑人状，声吱吱若鸣蝙蝠，身摇摇如戏秋千。狂奔下山，述于村人。村人聚众制梃，鼓勇而往，四面击之，良久不动。李之邻里咸在，其服色虽旧犹，犹辨为胡氏女，白其父母令观之，真实不虚。抚尸大恸，因验而焚之，臭达数里，叽叽作声，疑案始结。

    Explanation

    334

    Translation:

    Lan Yan said: Such strange occurrences could only be subdued and resolved by an old woman.

    Two cases of corpse transformation:

    In a certain village in Shaanxi, a woman from the Hu family, married into the Li family, had a quarrel with her husband and left in a huff, going out in a fit of anger, and no one knew where she went. Her husband thought she had returned to her mother's house and went to look for her, but she had never gone there. They searched all relatives and friends, but found none. They filed a lawsuit, and the authorities subjected them to harsh interrogation, but could not establish a case, and she remained in chains for over a year.

    Behind the village lies a rugged mountain range, rarely visited by people, only occasionally passed by those who cut grass, gather firewood, hunt pheasants, and catch rabbits. A woodcutter entered the forest to cut wood and, among the thousands of trees, saw a person hanging from a pine tree, eyes as large as bowls, raising both hands as if to strike someone, making a squeaking sound like a bat, and body swaying back and forth like a swing. He ran madly down the mountain and told the villagers.

    The villagers gathered and armed themselves with sticks, rallied their courage, and went to attack the body from all sides, striking it for a long time without it moving. Li's neighbors were all present, and although her clothing was old, they could still recognize her as the Hu family woman. They informed her parents to come and see, and it was indeed true. They embraced the corpse and wept bitterly, then examined and burned it, the stench reaching several miles, making a chirping sound, and the mysterious case was finally resolved.

    335

    兰岩曰：匹夫匹妇，自经于沟渎，而莫之知也，因属常事。独不解年余悬万山中，僵而能鸣，索且不朽，是为不可信，而可怪耳。

    　　先达某公，未第时，公车入都，因避嚣，停装于广渠门外一古寺中。僧舍虽多，大率湫隘，不堪肆业。唯小阁两楹，殊精洁高敞，因税居焉。僧嘱曰：“居此亦不妨，第须防暴客。后窗可不必开也。”公诺之。

    　　居无何，甚苦炎热，自思夏日楼居，古人所忌，非北风不能消暑，纵有暴客，亦不足为寒士忧。遂拔销启窗，见绿野青畴，一望无际。楼下临丛葬处，古冢累累，不止什佰，更有未葬而甃厝于茂草间者，凡十余柩。公笑曰：“对此正不敢不乐。”夜月上，清风送凉，乃命酒独酌，凭窗远瞩，醉后洒酒奠楼下曰：“长眠人苟有荷锸者，见此杯中物，当来歆也。”

    Explanation

    335

    Translation

    Lanyan said: Ordinary men and women, who commit suicide by hanging themselves over ditches or streams, and no one knows about it, are therefore considered common occurrences. But I do not understand why, after more than a year hanging in the mountains, they are stiff but still able to make a sound, and the rope used to hang them is still intact. This is hard to believe, but it is strange.
