    A respected scholar, before passing the imperial examinations, traveled to the capital on official business, seeking a quiet place to avoid the noise and disturbance. He stopped at an old temple outside Guangqu Gate in the capital. Although there were many monk's quarters, they were mostly damp and narrow, unsuitable for study. Only a small pavilion with two rooms was especially clean, bright, and spacious, so he rented it. The monk warned him, saying, "Living here is not a problem, but you must beware of violent intruders. The back window does not have to be opened." The scholar agreed.

    After some time, he was very uncomfortable due to the heat. Thinking to himself that living in a tower during summer was something the ancients avoided, and only a northern wind could dispel the heat, even if there were violent intruders, it was not worth worrying about for a poor scholar. So he removed the lock and opened the window, seeing green fields and cultivated land stretching as far as the eye could see. The floor below overlooked a burial ground, with numerous ancient tombs, more than ten or a hundred, and there were also unburied bodies placed in the thick grass, altogether more than ten coffins. The scholar laughed and said, "In this situation, one cannot help but be happy." At night when the moon rises, a cool breeze brings relief. He then ordered wine and drank alone, leaning on the window and looking far off. After getting drunk, he poured wine on the floor below and said, "If the people who are sleeping forever have anyone with a spade, if they see this wine in the cup, they will come and enjoy it."

    336

    约二更余，闻一厝柩边，窸窣有声，公疑为草木之兵。俄而声渐厉，柩盖蓦然落地，一尸匍匐而出，遍身雪白，两眼绿色，映日如萤光。公大骇，然素有气敢任，且念彼虽鬼物，而楼高数寻，亦不足畏，第观其出欲何为。亟灭烛以观之。尸既出，向其柩拜之，复合，僵立瞪目，釶然西去，迅疾如风。公错愕不已，料其必反，乃闭半窗，屏息以待。三更向近，月色西沉，尸果还，瞬息至柩前，方下拜，公适喉痒，不禁大嗽。尸伥然起，直视楼窗，公急闭窗，用力猛，窗脱榫，顿然落楼下。尸即奔至，向窗一踊，不及者咫尺。公大窘，提烛檠击之，颠而复踊。公仓皇间，于手边得书一帙，极力扑之，中其脑，倨然仆地，遂不复动。公始下楼，挝僧房门以告，僧大惊曰：“戒公者，诚非无谓，奈何弗听，致此恐怖？请暂同老僧榻，明日当为计也。”公就枕，夜梦数惊。

    Explanation

    336

    About two hours after midnight, the official heard something placed beside the coffin, making a rustling sound. He suspected it was a phantom soldier. Soon the sound grew louder, and the coffin lid suddenly fell to the ground. A corpse crawled out, entirely white with green eyes, shining like fireflies in the sunlight. The official was greatly frightened, but he was usually brave and bold. He thought that although it was a ghost, the building was several xun high, and it was not to be feared. He just observed what it intended to do. Quickly extinguishing the candle to observe it, the corpse came out and bowed to its coffin, then reformed itself, stood stiffly, and stared with wide eyes, moving swiftly westward, as fast as the wind. The official was stunned and bewildered, anticipating that it would return. So he closed half the window and held his breath, waiting. Approaching the third watch of the night, the moonlight set to the west. The corpse indeed returned, in an instant arriving at the coffin, just as it was about to bow. The official just happened to have an itchy throat and could not help coughing. The corpse rose with a startled look, staring directly at the window of the building. The official hastily closed the window, using great force, the window frame came loose, and suddenly fell to the floor below. The corpse immediately rushed over, jumped at the window, failing by a foot or so. The official was greatly embarrassed, picked up the candlestick and struck it, fell and then jumped again. In his panic, he found a volume of books nearby, struck it with all his might, hit its head, and it fell to the ground in a hunched position, and did not move again. The official then went downstairs and knocked on the temple room door to report. The monk was greatly surprised and said, "The warning to you was indeed not without reason. Why didn't you listen, leading to this terror? Please stay temporarily with the old monk's bed, and we will plan for tomorrow." The official went to bed and had several frightening dreams at night.

    337

    翌日，僧纠合长工十余人，执兵而往，见尸无敢向前，久之始集，以物枨拨之，举体白发长寸许，巨口过腮，十指坚甲如鹰爪。僧曰：“怪底一夏无雨，此魃为虐也。”报官验讫，聚薪焚之，唧唧之声不绝，臭不可近。视所击书，则《周易》下卷也。僧笑曰：“措大兵器，亦大异人。”公旋移居入城，逢人则述之。后及第，官少宰。

    Explanation

    337

    The next day, the monk gathered over ten laborers and went armed. No one dared to approach the body, but after a long time, they gathered and used a stick to probe it. The entire body was covered with white hair about an inch long, a large mouth that extended over the cheeks, and ten fingers with hard nails like eagle claws. The monk said, "Strange that there was no rain all summer, this is the calamity of the drought demon." They reported to the authorities and verified the situation, then gathered firewood to burn it. A continuous chirping sound was heard, and the stench was unbearable. Looking at the book that was hit, it was the lower volume of the I Ching. The monk laughed and said, "The scholar's weapons, also very unusual." The official soon moved into the city and told everyone he met. Later, he passed the imperial examination and became the Minister of Works.

    338

    兰岩曰：荒郊断垄，赍恨终天；蔓草寒烟，含悲长卧。怅孤魂于万里，无日还家；叹骨朽于百年，谁人布奠。致成旱魃为虐，戾气成妖。鬼也，而不安于穴，聚薪而焚之，良可慨也。

    　　猫怪三则某公子为笔帖式，家颇饶裕，父母俱存，兄弟无故，得人生之一乐焉。上下食指甚繁，而犹喜畜猫，白老乌员，何止十数。每食则群集案前，嗷嗷聒耳，饭鲜眠毯，习以为恒。适饭后闲话，家人咸不在侧，夫人呼丫环，数四不应，忽闻窗外，有代唤者，声甚异。公子启帘视之，寂无人，唯一猫奴踞窗台上，回首向公子，面有笑容。公子大骇，入告夫人。诸昆弟闻之，同出视猫，戏问曰：“适间唤人者，其汝也耶？”猫曰：“然”，众大哗。其父以为不祥，亟命捉之，猫曰：“莫拏我，莫拏我！”言讫一跃，径上屋檐而逝，数日不复来。举室惶然，谈论不已。

    Explanation

    338

    Final Translation

    Lan Yan said: In the desolate and abandoned countryside, the souls bear their grievances to the heavens; overgrown grass and cold smoke, they lie in sorrow for a long time. The wandering spirits are far from home, with no day to return; bones rot after a hundred years, and who will offer sacrifices? This leads to the disaster of drought, and evil energy becomes an omen. Ghosts, not resting in their lairs, are gathered and burned, truly a cause for regret.

    Cat Ghost Stories

    A certain young master, a clerk in the Qing dynasty, came from a wealthy family. His parents were both alive, and there were no problems among the brothers, enjoying one of the joys of life. The family had many mouths to feed, yet he still enjoyed keeping cats. He had more than ten cats, white and black. Every time they ate, the cats gathered around the table and made a lot of noise, sleeping on mats and eating fresh food, used to it. After dinner, they were chatting idly, with no family members around. The wife called the maid several times, but she did not respond. Suddenly, someone called from outside the window, and the voice was very strange. The young master opened the curtain to look, but there was no one there; only a cat was sitting on the windowsill, turning its head to the young master with a smile on its face. The young master was greatly startled and went to tell the wife. The other brothers heard and came out to see the cat. They jokingly asked, "Was it you who called just now?" The cat said, "Yes," and everyone was shocked. The father thought it was an omen and ordered them to catch the cat. The cat said, "Don't catch me, don't catch me!" and then jumped up onto the roof and disappeared, not coming back for several days. The whole household was in a state of panic and continued to talk about it.

    339

    一日，小婢方饷猫，此猫复杂群中来就食。急走入房，潜告诸公子。诸公子复大扰，同出捉之，缚而鞭之数十。猫但嗷嗷，倔强之态可恶。欲杀之，其父止之曰：“彼能作妖，杀之恐不利，不如舍之。”公子阴命二仆，盛以米囊，负而投诸河。甫出城，囊骤穴，临河而返，猫已先归。直至寝室，启帘而入，公子兄弟方咸集父母侧论猫事，瞥见猫来，胥发怔。

    Explanation

    339

    On one day, a young maid was feeding the cat when this cat came from the crowd to eat. She quickly entered the room and secretly informed the young masters. The young masters became greatly agitated again and went out together to catch it, binding and whipping it dozens of times. The cat only meowed, its stubborn attitude was hateful. They wanted to kill it, but its father stopped them and said, "It can cause mischief; killing it might not be good. It would be better to let it go." The young master secretly ordered two servants to put it in a sack of rice, carried it, and threw it into the river. Just as they left the city, the sack suddenly made a hole, reached the river and returned, and the cat had already returned. They continued until they reached the bedroom, opened the curtain and entered, and found the young master and his brothers just gathering around their parents to discuss the cat's matter. They saw the cat coming and all of them were stunned.

    340

    猫登踞胡床，怒视其父，目眥欲裂，张须切齿，厉声而骂曰：“何物老奴！尸诸余气，乃欲谋溺杀我耶？在汝家，自当推汝为翁；若在我家，云乃辈犹可耳孙，汝奈何丧心至此？且汝家祸在萧墙，不旋踵而至，不自惊怕，而谋杀我，岂非大谬！汝盍亦自省平日之所为乎？生具螾蚁之材，夤缘得禄。初仕刑部，以　距得上官心。出知二州，愈事贪酷。桁杨斧锧，威福自诩。作官二十年，草菅人命者，不知凡几。尚思恬退林泉，正命牖下，妄想极矣！所谓兽心人面，汝实人中妖孽，乃反以我言为怪，真怪事也！”遂大骂不已，辱及所生。举室纷拏，莫不抢攘。或挥古剑，或掷铜瓶，茗碗香炉，尽作攻击之具。猫哂笑而起曰：“我去，我去，汝不久败坏之家，我不谋与汝辈争也。”亟出户，缘树而逝，至此不复再至。

    Explanation

    340

    The cat climbed onto a trundle bed, glared at its father with eyes nearly popping out, furrowed its whiskers, and bared its teeth. Shouting angrily, it said, "What kind of old slave! With the remnants of life, you dare to plot to drown me? In your home, you should naturally be the master; but in my home, you are just a great-grandson, how could you lose your mind to this extent? Moreover, your family's disaster is within the palace walls, it will come soon, you should be afraid, but you plot to kill me, is that not a great mistake! Why don't you also reflect on your daily actions? Born with the talents of a worm or ant, you gained your position through connections. Initially serving in the Ministry of Punishment, you gained the favor of your superiors. When you were governor of two states, you became even more greedy and cruel. With shackles and axes, you boasted of your power and favor. Having served as an official for twenty years, you have killed countless lives. Still thinking of retiring to the mountains and forests, living a peaceful life, what a wild dream! So-called beastly heart and human face, you are truly a monster among people, yet you find my words strange, what a strange thing!" The cat continued to curse endlessly, even insulting the mother. The entire household was in chaos, everyone was in a panic. Some swung ancient swords, others threw copper bottles, tea bowls and incense burners were all used as weapons. The cat laughed and stood up, saying, "I'm leaving, I'm leaving, your soon-to-be-ruined family, I don't intend to compete with you." Quickly left the house, climbed the tree and disappeared, never to return again.

    341

    半年后，其家大疫，死者日以三四。公子坐争地免官，父母忧郁相继死。二年之内，诸昆弟、姊妹、妯娌、子侄、奴仆死者，几无孑遗。唯公子夫妇及一老仆暨一婢仅存，一寒如范叔也。

    　　闲斋曰：妖由人作，见以为怪，斯怪作也。唐魏元忠谓：“见怪不怪，其怪自灭。”非见理明晰，不能作是语。虽然，内省多疚，亦不易作坦率汉。

    　　永野亭黄门为予言，其一亲戚家，喜畜猫。忽有作人言者，察之，猫也。大骇，缚而挞之，求其故，猫曰：“无有不能言者，但犯忌，故不敢耳。今偶脱于口，驷不及舌，悔亦何及！若牝猫则未有不能言者矣。”其家不之信，令再缚一牝者，挞而求其语，初但嗷嗷，以目视前猫，前猫曰：“我且不得不言，况汝耶？”于是亦作人言求免，其家始信而纵之，后亦多不祥。予闻其说，愈谓《太平广记》所载，猫言“莫如此，莫如此”之事，为不诳也。

    Explanation

    341

    Six months later, a major epidemic struck the family, with people dying daily in threes and fours. The young master was dismissed from his office for quarreling over land, and his parents died of depression one after another. Within two years, all his brothers, sisters, brothers-in-law, sisters-in-law, nephews, and servants died, leaving almost no survivors. Only the young master, his wife, one old servant, and one maid remained, living in poverty like Fan Shu.

    Xianzai said: Ghosts are caused by people; seeing them as strange, this strangeness arises. Tang Dynasty's Wei Yuanzhong said, "If you see ghosts without being surprised, the ghosts will disappear on their own." Without a clear understanding of the truth, one cannot make such a statement. However, if one has many regrets when reflecting inwardly, it is not easy to be an open and honest person.

    Yongyeting Huangmen told me about one of his relatives' families who loved keeping cats. Suddenly, one cat began to speak in human language. Investigating, they found it was indeed a cat. Greatly frightened, they tied it up and beat it, asking for the reason. The cat said, "There is none that cannot speak, but they are afraid of offending, so they dare not. Now it slipped out by chance, once the tongue has spoken, four horses cannot catch up, it is too late to regret! If it is a female cat, there is none that cannot speak." His family did not believe it, so they ordered to tie up another female cat and beat it, asking for its words. At first, it just made noise, looking at the previous cat with its eyes. The previous cat said, "I already had to speak, how much more so you?" Then it also spoke in human language to plead for mercy. The family finally believed and released it, but later, there were many misfortunes. I heard this story and found that the account in Taiping Guangji of a cat saying "Do not do this, do not do this" was not false.

    342

    兰岩曰：以言遭楚，猫应自悔，然犹以驷不及舌，痛自惩责；乃人也，每以多言取祸，反怨天尤人，不克自省，诚此猫之不若矣。

    　　护军参领舒某，喜咏歌，行立坐卧，罕不呜呜。一日，友人过访，欢饮于室，漏已二下，尚相与赓歌不辍，忽闻户外细声唱所谓“敬德打朝”者，谛聆之，字音清楚合拍，妙不可言。舒服役只一僮，素不解歌。兹忽闻此，深疑之，潜出窥何，则见一猫人立月中，既歌且舞。舒惊呼其友，猫已在墙，以石投之，一跃而逝，而余音犹在墙外也。

    Explanation

    342

    Lan Yan said: To suffer misfortune due to words, the cat should repent, yet even with the irreversibility of spoken words, one must deeply self-punish and blame oneself; as humans, we often bring disaster upon ourselves through excessive talk, instead blaming heaven and others, unable to self-reflect, truly, we are not as good as this cat.

    A military official named Shu, who held the rank of Huajun Canling, loved singing and would often hum while walking, standing, sitting, or lying down. One day, a friend visited him, and they drank merrily in his room. By the second watch, they were still singing together without ceasing. Suddenly, they heard a soft voice outside singing what was called "Jingde Dazhao." They listened closely, and the words were clear and in perfect rhythm, truly wonderful. Shu had only one servant who did not understand singing. Now, hearing this, he was deeply puzzled and secretly went out to see what it was. He saw a cat standing upright in the moonlight, singing and dancing. Shu called out to his friend in surprise, but the cat was already on the wall. He threw a stone at it, and the cat leaped away, while the lingering sound of its song could still be heard outside the wall.

    343

    兰岩曰：上有好者，下必有甚焉者矣。舒喜歌唱，而猫亦效尤，舒应乐其善继主人也，何以石投之哉？

    　　驴都中有以屠驴为业者。主人晚过其肆，漏已下，肆门已闭，室中灯火甚明，疑为作工者赌博，从板隙潜窥，见二佣共杀死一妇人，方断脰剖腹，血肉狼藉。大惊，亟反走，鸣诸官，官卒步军十余人，同往捕之。排闼涌入，则见死驴在地，主人独指以为人，步军曰：“死驴也，乌得为人？”与其争论，环观者咸笑其妄，其人犹忿争不已。官怒其哓哓，叱曰：“指驴为马，且不可，奈何指驴为人？”鞭之而去。其人愤极，重欲对众检视，乃亦是驴。始而愕然，既而废然，遂改业，誓不杀生。

    Explanation

    343

    Lanyan said: When those above have a preference, those below will inevitably go further in that direction. Shu Xi enjoys singing, and the cat imitates him as well. Shu Xi should be pleased that the cat continues the master's behavior so well, so why throw stones at it?

    In the city of Lüdu, there was a man who made a living by slaughtering donkeys. One evening, the owner passed by his shop. The water clock had already passed, and the shop door was closed. The room inside was brightly lit, leading him to suspect that the workers were gambling. He peered through a crack in the wall and saw two servants killing a woman, cutting her neck and opening her belly, with blood and flesh scattered everywhere. He was greatly shocked and quickly fled, reporting the incident to the authorities. A dozen officials and foot soldiers went to apprehend the culprits. They pushed open the door and rushed in, only to find a dead donkey on the ground. The owner alone pointed to it, claiming it was a person. The foot soldiers said, "It's a dead donkey; how can it be a person?" They argued with him, and the onlookers all laughed at his foolishness. The man continued to argue fiercely. The official, angered by his persistent shouting, shouted, "You can't even point a donkey as a horse, how can you point a donkey as a person?" He whipped the man and sent him away. The man, furious, wanted to show everyone that it was indeed a donkey. He checked again and found that it was indeed a donkey. At first, he was startled, then dejected, and finally changed his profession, vowing never to kill living beings again.

    344

    兰岩曰：驴，宠然一蠢物也，与人大异，何此人确见其为人哉？殆亦业此屠驴者乎？非昏于即也，岂誓不杀生？此驴也不复为人，此人也不复为驴矣。

    　　异犬某侯袭爵之前一岁，年甫十七，丰姿如玉，而癖好斗鸡走狗。尝豢一黄犬，甚爱之，至同寝食。至夏日，率犬出东门，游行郊坰.大雨骤至，避一墓门下。墓前有积潦，广袤数亩，芦荻满之。坐未安，复有三恶少鞲鹰负弩而至，见侯各耳语。侯故白皙，一恶少作韵语曰：“黑者黑如铁也，赤者赤如血也，白者白如雪也。”其二人和之以笑。侯虽微，固世家之裔，且懦弱孤立，闻之大惧，冒雨欲行，恶少挽留甚力，侯大窘曰：“汝等欲何为？”恶少皆笑而不答，但相与拘持之。

    Explanation

    344

    Lanyan said: The donkey, a pampered and stupid creature, is very different from humans. How can this person see the donkey as a human? Perhaps he is also a professional who slaughters donkeys? Is he not simply ignorant, or does he swear never to kill living beings? This donkey is no longer a human, and this person is no longer a donkey.

    Before the year when a certain nobleman (侯) inherited his title, he was just seventeen years old, graceful and beautiful like jade, and had a passion for cockfighting and dog racing. He once kept a yellow dog, which he loved very much, to the point of sharing sleep and meals with it. In the summer, he would take the dog out through the eastern gate and wander in the outskirts of the city. Suddenly, a heavy rainstorm came, and they sought shelter under the entrance of a tomb. In front of the tomb was a flooded area, several acres wide, filled with reeds and bulrushes. They had not settled comfortably when three ruffians, wearing gloves for eagles and carrying crossbows, arrived. Seeing the nobleman, they whispered to each other. The nobleman was naturally fair-skinned, and one of the ruffians recited a verse: "The black one is as black as iron, the red one is as red as blood, the white one is as white as snow." The other two laughed along with it. Although the nobleman was of low rank, he was a descendant of a prominent family, and he was timid and isolated. Hearing this, he was greatly frightened and braved the rain to leave. The ruffians held him back very strongly, and the nobleman was greatly embarrassed and asked, "What do you want to do?" The ruffians all laughed but did not answer, and instead, they held him tightly.

    345

    犬在旁大嗥来啮，恶少以巨石投之，中脑，犬闷绝。乃尽褫侯衣，不留寸缕，缚手足，俯而捺诸草中，欲淫之。侯哭叫声嘶，滚地不定。会有数骑自林间来，恶少仓皇逸去。骑者至，见而诧异，解其缚而询其故，侯蝟缩而泣，告之。骑者怜其遭，衣而送之家。犬亦踉跄随之归，数日不食，创溃而毙。侯大恸，瘗之园中，祝而祭之，如丧良朋也。是夜梦犬作人言，谓侯曰：“主人遇我厚矣，将有以图报。主从此出门，须慎。苟际危急，我受豢养恩，至时必相救。”觉以为异，谨志之。

    Explanation

    345

    A dog beside him howled loudly and came to bite. The ruffian threw a large stone at it, hitting its brain, and the dog fainted. Then he stripped off all of his clothes, leaving not a single thread, bound his hands and feet, and pushed him face down in the grass, intending to defile him. The young man cried out in a hoarse voice, rolling around on the ground uncontrollably. Just then, several riders came from the woods, and the ruffian hastily fled. The riders arrived, saw the scene, and were astonished. They untied him and asked what had happened. The young man, curled up like a hedgehog, wept and told them. The riders pitied his plight, dressed him, and sent him home. The dog also limped back with him, not eating for several days, and died from its wounds. The young man was greatly grieved, buried it in the garden, and offered prayers and a sacrifice, treating it like a good friend. That night, he dreamed that the dog spoke in human language, saying to him, "You have treated me well, and I will find a way to repay you. Whenever you go out from here, be cautious. If you encounter danger, I, who have received your kindness, will surely come to your aid." Upon waking, he found it strange and carefully recorded it.

    346

    一日，有事于通州，归而泛舟于大通河，仍见前三恶少，更同二少年，掉臂趁船，咸目侯而笑。侯惧甚，至闸上泊舟，同载者星散。侯入旗亭，潜视三恶少去远，始觅僻径急行。约里余，猝见三恶少，突起黍稷中，捉侯入幽僻处，掩其口，复褫其衣，一少年方欲奸之，忽一巨犬窜出垝垣，直前莈其阴，少年痛绝而踣。犬更追其二人，一落其腓，一伤其臀。侯得无恙，着衣蹑履，蹊田而奔。

    Explanation

    346

    One day, while on business in Tongzhou, he returned and sailed on the Datong River, where he once again encountered the three ruffians, accompanied by two more youths. They swaggered onto the boat and all looked at him and laughed. Filled with fear, he arrived at the lock and docked the boat, and his companions scattered like stars. He entered a pavilion, secretly watched the three ruffians depart, and then took a secluded path to hurry away. After running about a mile, he suddenly encountered the three ruffians again in the fields of millet and grain. They grabbed him, covered his mouth, stripped his clothes, and one youth was about to rape him. Suddenly, a large dog jumped out of a ruined wall, bit the youth's genitals, causing him to fall in pain. The dog then chased the other two youths, injuring one's calf and the other's buttocks. The protagonist remained unharmed, put on his clothes and shoes, and ran through the fields.

    347

    犬返走，侯尾而唤之，直至一茅舍前，犬踣于篱落下，就视之，则一病癞黄狗也，意颇怳然。有老妪扫麦于场，谛侯而笑曰：“此吾家老犬也，病癞半年，昨夜死矣。小哥薄而观之，独不嫌其秽乎？”侯漫应之，惓惓而返。夜复梦犬来告：“主人之恩已稍酬报，冥中怜我之忠，行当托生为人，于焉拜辞，再见无由矣。”言讫，涕泣叩头而去。侯深感其义，计犬死期，每七日必设祭于葬所，至今不衰。后闻之三恶少，二作废人，伤阴者，越宿即陨。恩茂先为侯内兄，茂先深知其事。侯今袭爵三年，予曾于茂先汤饼会中识之，真璧人也。

    　　兰岩曰：食其禄而报其主，犬诚忠矣。乃死后犹借手癞狗，以舒其猛烈之气。犬固不昧其灵，而能如是，可以人而不知犬乎？

    Explanation

    347

    The dog turned back and ran, and Lord followed and called it, until it reached a thatched cottage, where the dog fell at the fence. Approaching to see it, it was a sick, leprous yellow dog, and Lord's mind was quite bewildered. An old woman was sweeping wheat in the yard, and looking at Lord and smiling, she said, "This is my family's old dog, it was sick with leprosy for half a year, and it died last night. Young master, thin and observing it, only not afraid of its filth?" Lord casually replied, reluctantly returned. At night, the dog came again to tell, "The master's grace has been slightly repaid. In the afterlife, pity my loyalty, will be reborn as a person. Bidding farewell, no way to meet again." After speaking, tears and sobs, bowing and leaving. Lord deeply felt its righteousness, counting the dog's death date, every seven days must set up a sacrifice at the burial place, until now not diminished. Later heard of three evil young men, two became invalids, injured yin, the next day died. En Maoshan is Lord's brother-in-law, Maoshan deeply knows the matter. Lord now inherits the title for three years, I once met him at Maoshan's soup cake gathering, truly a precious person.

    Lanyan said: Eating his salary and reporting to his master, the dog is truly loyal. Even after death still using the hand of a leprous dog, to release its fierce spirit. The dog does not forget its spirit, and can do so, can humans not know dogs?

    348

    那　步　军步军那木契，司栅于某胡同。会冬夜，方拥裘击柝，三更向尽，见二青衣人，驱鸭数百，欲过栅南去。那叱曰：“此何时，尚欲过栅耶？”二人不应，辄驱鸭自栅下过。那大怒，方欲阻之，而人与群鸭纷然在栅南矣，驱鸭径去，初无阻碍。那大惊，毛戴，亟呼其伴告之，共相错愕。自是小儿多患痘疹，百无一生。那所见殆非无因也。

    　　兰岩曰：鸭为儿厉，诚不可解。

    　　施二京师某坊，有废寺一区，殿宇宏巨。僧房数十间，强半倾圯，佛像暴露，钟鱼阒寂。惟一老衲及两沙弥主之，生涯冷落，所恃膳葺闲房，招小经纪者僦居，月觅钱四五缗，为香火之资而已。有交城民施二者，秋夏耕稼，冬春入京，以卖糖为业，至则税居寺中东院中，历数年矣。施二一夕卖糖归，已二更矣。风雪甚寒，独酌未寝。忽闻东壁有人语言相问答者，施停杯倾听，了了可辨。

    Explanation

    348

    A Military Officer's Encounter with the Supernatural

    During the winter, a military officer named Na Muci, who was in charge of guarding a certain alley, was on duty one night. As the third watch was drawing to a close, he saw two men in blue robes driving hundreds of ducks, intending to pass south of the fence. Na shouted, "What time is this to still want to pass the fence?" The two men did not respond and immediately drove the ducks under the fence. Na became very angry and was just about to stop them, but the people and the ducks were already scattered in the south of the fence, driving the ducks away without any hindrance. Na was greatly shocked, his hair standing on end, and quickly called his companions to tell them, causing them to be equally startled. From then on, many children suffered from smallpox, and none survived. What Na saw was probably not without reason.

    Lan Yan commented: Ducks are harmful to children, which is truly inexplicable.

    In a certain district of the capital, there was a man named Shi Er from Jiaocheng. In autumn and summer, he worked in the fields, and in winter and spring, he went to the capital to make a living by selling sugar. When he arrived, he paid rent to live in the eastern courtyard of an abandoned temple. He had been living there for several years. One evening, after returning from selling sugar, it was already the second watch. The wind and snow were very cold, and he drank alone, not sleeping. Suddenly, he heard people talking to each other on the east wall. Shi stopped drinking and listened, and it was clearly distinguishable.

    349

    闻一人音响，似五六十岁人者，叹曰：“比日腰痛异常，又苦□戾，今闻朔风霍霍，思家迫切，未晓儿子辈，亦曾念及阿爹否？”言讫，慨叹再四。又一人曰：“我岂不倍加愁苦！百日以来，守株待免，地方拘束，跬步不敢出寺门，饥寒甚矣。昨见和尚斫驴脯，置砧板厨下，私心窃喜，谓可一润馋吻，不意转盼间，为什参领家恶犬所舔，其声□□。受此犬之啮。迄今股上有大疤痕。”老人曰：“明日徐四来，可以得代否？”其人曰：“地方已许我矣，有隙可乘，即得代也。”老人复叹谓再三。已而寂然。施知是鬼，为之毛戴，急出房就其同业者，具述所闻，闻者亦寒慄焉。

    　　翌日，果有以剃头为活者，来僦居东壁，人极粗蠢。因与施比邻，一来致谒。问里居，通姓氏，则霸州徐四也。施愕，乘间密以夜间所闻告之，劝其改居，徐谢曰：“老兄意良厚，但我自有命，彼何能为？掌梦上帝，必不容鬼物厉人也。”施唯唯，不复置喙而罢。

    Explanation

    349

    I heard a voice, like that of a man in his fifties or sixties, sigh and say, "Recently, I've been suffering from severe back pain and also from bitterness. Now hearing the north wind howling, I think of home with urgency. I wonder if my sons have also thought of their father?" After speaking, he sighed four times. Another person said, "Am I not suffering even more? For a hundred days, I've been waiting like a farmer for a rabbit to run into a tree stump, restrained by local authorities, not daring to step outside the temple gate, extremely hungry and cold. Yesterday, I saw the monk chopping up a donkey's flank and placing it on the chopping board in the kitchen, secretly delighted, thinking it could satisfy my hunger. Unfortunately, in the blink of an eye, it was licked by a vicious dog belonging to the military officer. The sound was pitiful/frightening. Suffered from being bitten by this dog. Still has a large scar on the thigh." The old man said, "Will Xu Si come tomorrow, and can I get a replacement?" The person replied, "The local authorities have agreed to let me go. If there is an opportunity, I will get a replacement." The old man sighed repeatedly. Then it became silent. Shi realized it was a ghost, his hair stood on end. Quickly left the room to join his colleagues, telling them what he had heard. Those who heard it also shivered with fear.

    The next day, indeed, a man who makes a living by shaving came to rent a room in the east wall, a very crude and dull person. Because he lived next to Shi, he came to pay his respects. When asked about his hometown and surname, it turned out to be Xu Si from Ba Prefecture. Shi was startled and took the opportunity to secretly tell him what he had heard at night, advising him to move. Xu thanked him, saying, "My elder brother's intentions are very kind, but I have my own fate. What can they do? The God who oversees dreams will not allow ghosts to harm people." Shi nodded, and did not speak again.

    350

    居无何，徐为人薙头，误落其须，其人怒詈，徐不少让，遂被殴击。既归寺，忿恨不已。施与同侪就其室，慰藉之曰：“吾等作小经纪，小手艺，凡百宜忍耐，讵可轻肆性气？独不见夫茶寮酒肆中，壁上之书乎？非‘和为贵’，即‘忍为高’也。”徐不忍，曰：“宁死异乡，不甘此辱！”众复醵钱，沽酒劝解，至四更各散去。施归房，尚闻徐怨恨声，既而有悲泣声，侧耳谤察之，声渐异，且作小语曰：“我虽一时气苦，岂竟走之条路耶？”顷之又曰：“果能如此，死亦无憾！”至鸡鸣，声始寂然。施大疑，即披衣出户，潜从窗外探之，房中昏暗，乍视无所睹，凝睇久之，方隐隐见一人，悬梁上，又一白衣，背立其前，双手捩其足。大惊，失声却走。寺僧方将上楼发晨钟，闻人声来探，相遭于门，各复惊倒，及同业者渐集，始辨是施也。诘得其故，同往观之，徐果自缢死矣。殴徐者，行路之人，无从追捕，枉捐命躯。据施所闻所见，事属前定，洵非偶然。

    Explanation

    350

    Not long after, Xu had his head shaved, but mistakenly cut off his beard. The person became furious and cursed him, and Xu did not yield even slightly, so he was beaten up. After returning to the temple, Xu was filled with anger and resentment. Shi and his peers went to Xu's room to comfort him, saying, "We engage in small trades and minor skills; in everything, we should endure and not act rashly. Haven't you seen the inscriptions in the tea houses and wine shops? They say 'Harmony is precious' or 'Patience is exalted.'" Xu could not bear it and said, "Rather die in a foreign land than endure this disgrace!" The group then pooled money to buy wine and try to console him, but they dispersed after the fourth watch. Shi returned to his room and still heard Xu's complaints. Soon after, there were sounds of sobbing. Shi listened carefully and heard the sound becoming different, and Xu whispered, "Though I am momentarily filled with anger, do I really want to take this path?" Shortly after, he said, "If I can truly do this, I have no regrets even if I die!" At dawn, the sound finally became silent. Shi was greatly puzzled and immediately put on his clothes and went out of the door, sneaking out of the window to look inside. The room was dimly lit, and at first glance, nothing could be seen. Staring intently for a long time, he faintly saw a person hanging from the beam, and another in white standing behind him, twisting his feet with both hands. Shi was greatly startled, shouting and retreating. The temple monk was just about to go up the stairs to ring the morning bell when he heard someone coming to investigate. They met at the door, and both were shocked and fell down. When other colleagues gradually gathered, they recognized it was Shi. After inquiring and finding out the reason, they went together to see, and indeed, Xu had hanged himself. The person who beat Xu was a passerby and could not be caught, leading to Xu's unnecessary death. According to what Shi heard and saw, the matter was predetermined and indeed not accidental.

    351

    兰岩曰：宿孽前定，卒不能逃，或亦有然。第冥冥中纵此鬼物杀人自代，而此死者复又求代，相报有已时乎？况亦只得地方一许，便可任所欲为。彼为冥官者，竟毫不加察耶？抑坐受其贿耶？是可慨也！

    　　盛　紫　川予友盛紫川，禄为秀才时，偶探一亲戚归，夜已半，路过海潮庵转弯处，忽见一妇人劈面来，著旧蓝布衫，曳破鞋，月下视之，约年四十许，面色灰败。紫川不禁毛戴，伫足让其行，而妇人亦止步相向，彼此相去仅隔车轨。妇人渐渐开眼，眼光绿色，紫川遂昏然如梦魇，默念此必鬼也，当焕发吾之精神，彼必畏避。因凝神定慧，瞠目视之，倏如梦醒。妇人两眼复合，绿光旋敛。既而紫川发一寒噤，妇人眼复开，紫川仍闭目如梦魇。如此者数次，相拒久之。赖有逻卒击柝而来，妇人始逡巡西去。紫川归，面如土色。越数日，始复其旧。每向人述之。

    Explanation

    351

    兰岩曰：Past sins are predestined, and one cannot ultimately escape them, though there may be exceptions. However, in the unseen realm, if a ghostly being kills a person to take their place, and then this deceased person seeks retribution, is there ever an end to mutual retribution? Moreover, in such a limited place, the ghostly being can act as it pleases. Are the officials of the underworld truly oblivious to this, or do they accept bribes while sitting idly? This is truly lamentable!

    　　盛　紫　川 My friend, Sheng Zichuan, was a scholar during his time. One night, after visiting a relative, he was halfway home when it was already midnight, and he passed by the turning point of Hai Chao Temple. Suddenly, a woman appeared face-to-face with him, wearing an old blue cloth shirt and dragging a worn-out shoe. Under the moonlight, she seemed to be around forty years old, with a pale and discolored face. Zichuan could not help but feel a chill run down his spine and hesitated, letting her pass. The woman also stopped and faced him, with only a cart track's distance between them. The woman gradually opened her eyes, and they glowed green. Zichuan then felt as if he was in a dreamlike state, silently thinking, "This must be a ghost. I should muster my spirit, and she will surely be afraid." He concentrated his mind and stared at her, and suddenly, he felt as if he had woken from a dream. The woman's eyes closed, and the green light faded. Soon after, Zichuan shivered, and the woman's eyes opened again. Zichuan closed his eyes again, as if in a dreamlike state. This cycle repeated several times, lasting for a long time. Fortunately, a night watchman came by, striking his wooden block, and the woman hesitantly moved westward. Zichuan returned home, his face as pale as chalk. After several days, he gradually returned to normal. He often recounted this experience to others.

    352

    卷三邱生连城赖冠千言：其乡有邱贡生者，忘其名，贵家子也。年甫二十，丰姿如玉。雍正间，自闽入都，将肄业于成均，以图进取。未考到，暂寓左安门外某寺中。寺近某贵公废园，地极荒僻。生少喜动，不耐岑寂。饭后，携小童散步行食。初不知有园，及遥见林树葱郁，楼阁参差，讶而询诸耕者，始得其详，亟往游焉。小童怯行路，或蹙额瞠目，或出言怨咨，生恶其聒，嗾使先归，小童喜跃而去。生且行且笑曰：“奴子别有肺肠，想天之生人，本无雅俗，彼受其父母精血之浊而生，故致此。譬夫犬之食秽，其种类然也。”

    Explanation

    352

    卷三邱生连城赖冠千言：其乡有邱贡生者，忘其名，贵家子也。年甫二十，丰姿如玉。雍正间，自闽入都，将肄业于成均，以图进取。未考到，暂寓左安门外某寺中。寺近某贵公废园，地极荒僻。生少喜动，不耐岑寂。饭后，携小童散步行食。初不知有园，及遥见林树葱郁，楼阁参差，讶而询诸耕者，始得其详，亟往游焉。小童怯行路，或蹙额瞠目，或出言怨咨，生恶其聒，嗾使先归，小童喜跃而去。生且行且笑曰：“奴子别有肺肠，想天之生人，本无雅俗，彼受其父母精血之浊而生，故致此。譬夫犬之食秽，其种类然也。”

    Translation:

    In the third volume, the story of Qiu Sheng Liancheng and Lai Guan Qian is told: There was a successful candidate in the imperial examination from his hometown, whose name is forgotten, but he was the son of a wealthy family. He was just twenty years old, with a graceful and refined appearance. During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, he traveled from Fujian to the capital to study at the Imperial Academy, aiming for advancement. Before taking the exam, he temporarily stayed at a temple near Zuo'an Gate. The temple was close to a ruined garden belonging to a noble family, a place that was extremely remote and desolate. Being young, he enjoyed movement and could not tolerate loneliness. After meals, he would take a small servant with him for a leisurely walk. Initially, he did not know about the garden, but when he saw the lush trees and scattered buildings from afar, he was amazed and asked the farmers, who finally told him about it. He then hurried to visit. The servant was afraid of the journey, sometimes frowning and staring, sometimes complaining, and the subject disliked the noise, urging the servant to return first. The servant happily left. As he walked, the subject laughed and said, "This servant has a different mindset. I believe that people are inherently neither refined nor vulgar. He was born from impure parents, so he turned out this way. It's like a dog eating filth, which is natural for its kind."

    353

    比至园，日已夕矣，荒榛绕径，丰草堆阶，门亦扃锁。搴衣拨莽，越垝垣以入。园中古桧高槐，浓荫夹径。纡回循径行，忽达一桥，朱栏摧折，红板朽残。桥下芦荻丛生，蛙鸣积漈.过桥抵一轩，蛛丝当户，纱绿在窗。生徙倚栏干，徘徊忘返，不觉古墙月上，苔砌中喧。晚风入树林间，如闻吟啸。本欲穷其幽邃，当此际心殊怛怖，乃折轩前凤仙花，却步欲归。

    　　忽闻回廊下，有清锐其音者，叱曰：“何处小蛮奴，擅入人家窥伺？贵人眷属居此，肯容汝折一茎草，踏一块砖耶？”生惊视之，则十六七二女嬛也。一绿衣，一碧衣，眉目如画，面无怒色，但作恶声耳。生自知冒昧，急弃花整衣，趋而揖之曰：“异乡年少，孤客无知，孟浪采花，罪不容赦，倘蒙宽宥，佩德不忘。”绿衣者曰：“或即宽宥，亦平常事，那便是德，那便不忘？书痴便绐，欲绐阿谁耶？”碧衣者曰：“今不痛加惩治，彼以为我辈孱弱，必源源而来矣。

    Explanation

    353

    By the time he arrived at the garden, the day had already turned to evening. The garden was overgrown with thorny bushes around the path, and thick grass piled up on the steps. The gate was also locked. Lifting his clothes and pushing through the overgrowth, he climbed over the broken wall to enter. In the garden, ancient cypress trees and tall locust trees provided dense shade along the path. Winding his way along the path, he suddenly reached a bridge. The red railings were broken and the red planks were rotten and decayed. Under the bridge, reeds and rushes grew thickly, and frogs croaked in the accumulated water. Crossing the bridge, he reached a pavilion. Cobwebs hung in the doorway, and green silk hung in the windows. The young man leaned against the railings, wandered back and forth, and forgot to return. Unaware that the moon had risen behind the ancient wall, the moss-covered steps were noisy. The evening wind entered the forest, and it seemed as if someone was singing and whistling. He originally intended to explore its deep and secluded areas, but at this moment, his heart was particularly filled with fear. He then picked a fuchsia flower in front of the pavilion, took a step back, and wanted to return.

    Suddenly, he heard a clear and sharp voice from under the corridor. She shouted, saying, "Where is this little barbarian,擅自闯入别人家窥探? The noble people's family lives here, would they allow you to pick a single blade of grass or step on a single brick?" The young man was startled and looked at them. Then he saw two young women, about sixteen or seventeen years old. One wore a green robe, the other a blue robe, their eyebrows and eyes were like paintings, their faces showed no anger, but they made a harsh sound. The young man knew he had acted rashly. He quickly threw away the flower and straightened his clothes. He hurried forward and bowed, saying, "A young stranger from a foreign land, a lonely guest who acted without knowledge, rashly picked a flower, a crime that cannot be forgiven. If you could grant me leniency, I will never forget your kindness." The woman in green said, "Even if you were to grant leniency, it would be a common thing, that would be kindness, that would be unforgettable. A bookworm would deceive, who would he deceive?" The woman in blue said, "If we do not severely punish him now, he will think we are weak and will come again and again."

    354

    ”言次，复有数女奴，自轩后出，问曰：“何事喋喋，娘子候回话矣。”二女同笑曰：“回底话？知他何处书生，南蛮　舌，令人一字不解。”众女环观相与曰：“蛮子殊不丑，盖捉去听娘子发付之。”众曰：“有理！”生大惧，投地求释，众置若罔闻，或揪耳轮，或拥发辫，后推而前挽之。生固无缚鸡力，遭此纷拏，不克自主。须臾至一广厅下，始各缓手。生喘息稍定，又闻传话曰：“命捉上楼去也。”众又拥生至楼下，前二女先登，众未登，共立檐下，屏气无敢息者。有顷，前二女各抱绣袱含笑出户曰：“几误大事，诸姊妹各散，无事聚此矣。”众皆默默索然散去。

    Explanation

    354

    While speaking, several female servants appeared from behind the screen, asking, "What are you talking about so much? Madam is waiting for a reply." The two girls laughed together and said, "The reply? Who knows where this scholar is from, with his southern barbarian tongue, it's incomprehensible to me." The other girls gathered around and said, "This barbarian isn't ugly at all; we should take him away to hear what Madam has to say." They all agreed, "That makes sense!" The speaker was greatly afraid, threw himself on the ground to beg for release, but the group ignored him, some grabbing his ears, others pulling his hair, pushing him from behind and pulling him from the front. He had no strength to tie up a chicken, faced this chaos, and could not control himself. After a moment, they arrived at a large hall, and then they relaxed their grip. The speaker's breathing slightly calmed, and then he heard a command, "Take him upstairs." The group again took him to the foot of the stairs, the two girls went up first, the others did not follow, standing under the eaves, holding their breath, too afraid to exhale. After a while, the two girls came out smiling, each holding a brocade cloth, saying, "We almost made a big mistake; let's all disperse, there's no need to gather here." The others all silently and dejectedly dispersed.

    355

    二女挽生入左室，一切甚精洁，中有池，香汤芬馥，知为湢浴之所。二女持巾执帨，伺生浴讫，彻体易新衣，长短合度，鲜华照人。二女啧啧叹美不绝口。俄有提灯来迓者，亦二八女奴也。导引入房，暂就客座，一女侍侧，前二女入内寝。房中位置器物精奇，目所未睹。生中心忐忑，不测吉凶。

    　　良久，忽觉异香扑鼻，笑语喁喁，虾须帘启，二女从一女郎亭亭出户，容辉艳丽，旷世无匹，年约十八九，衣藕色画衣，拖墨花裙，含羞向生侧身裣衽。生却步逡巡，不觉屈膝，女郎挽之入座，曰：“君非鄞江邱贡生耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“然则与儿有姻缘之契也。儿卫氏，字素娟，世系陇西，令尊公为秦州参戎时，与先君结耐久交，因有婚姻之约，彼时尔我尚在襁褓中，不能记忆，迄今计之，十有七年矣。一旦邂逅于此，红丝系足，岂偶然耶？昨夜梦神人见告，故能预知郎名姓里居，幸郎勿猜也。

    Explanation

    355

    Two women led the guest into the left room, where everything was very clean and tidy. There was a pool in the middle, filled with fragrant hot water, known to be a place for bathing. The two women held towels and waited for the guest to finish bathing. He then changed into new clothes, which were the right length and very beautiful, shining brightly. The two women kept praising him without stopping. Soon, someone came with a lantern to meet them, another young servant girl. They were led into the room, where they sat temporarily as guests. One woman stood by the side, while the previous two women went into the inner chamber. The room was filled with finely crafted and unique objects, none of which had been seen before. The guest's heart was filled with anxiety, unable to predict good or bad fortune.

    After a long while, suddenly, a strange fragrance filled the nose, and soft laughter could be heard. The curtain with shrimp-like fringes opened, and two women followed a graceful young lady out of the door. Her beauty was radiant and striking, unmatched in the world. She was about eighteen or nineteen years old, wearing a lotus-colored embroidered robe and a black floral skirt. She blushed and slightly bowed her head towards the guest. The guest hesitated and took a step back, not realizing he had knelt. The young lady pulled the guest to sit down and said, "Are you not the candidate from Yinzhou, Qiu?" He replied, "Yes." She continued, "Then, you and I have a marriage pact. I am Wei, surnamed Su Juan. My family is from Longxi, and my father was the military commander of Qin Prefecture. He formed a lasting friendship with your father, and therefore, there was a marriage agreement. At that time, both of us were still in our cradles and could not remember. Counting up to now, it has been seventeen years. Meeting here by chance, with red thread tying our feet, is it not by destiny? Last night, I dreamed of a divine person who told me, so I could foresee your name, surname, and hometown. Please do not guess."

    356

    ”生虽少孤，至于父为秦州参戎，则知之烂熟，兹闻女郎言有据，并不致疑。且对此丽人，神魂丧失，无暇致详，但再拜曰：“第恐濒海鲰生，有辱门第耳。不然，淮南王之鸡犬，未有不望上大罗天者。”娟笑顾二女曰：“汝道郎君言不可晓，何为字字了了？”二女笑曰：“方初见郎君时，但闻　碭如鸟鸣，虽悦耳，实笑人。今与娘子应答，又甚清楚，想前操土音，今说官话也。”娟嘤咛而笑，生亦笑曰：“其可儿也，敢问芳名？”娟曰：“绿衣者翘翘，碧衣者楚楚。”生曰：“谨志不忘。”二女曰：“于郎固有德，何可便忘？”生复笑。

    Explanation

    356

    Though I was orphaned at a young age, by the time my father held the rank of a military officer in Qin Prefecture, I had become thoroughly familiar with the ways of the world. Now, hearing the young lady speak with such authority, I have no reason to doubt her. Moreover, faced with such a beautiful woman, I am utterly entranced, with no time to delve into details. I can only bow twice and say, "I only fear that I, a humble scholar from the coastal region, might bring shame to your family. Otherwise, even the chickens and dogs of the King of Huainan, who were transformed into immortals, all looked forward to ascending to the highest heaven." The young lady named Jian smiled and turned to the two girls, saying, "You said that the young man's words were incomprehensible, yet every word is clear and precise?" The two girls laughed and replied, "When we first met the young man, we only heard him speak in a way that sounded like a bird's cry, though it was pleasant to the ear, it was truly laughable. Now, in conversation with you, his speech is quite clear, so we suspect that he spoke in a local dialect before, but now he speaks the official language." Jian giggled, and the young man also laughed, saying, "How lovely! I dare to ask for your name?" Jian responded, "The one in green is elegant, and the one in blue is graceful." The young man said, "I will remember them carefully." The two girls said, "The lady has indeed done us a favor; how could we forget so soon?" The young man laughed again.

    357

    随闻内城蒲牢声，如海鲸之鸣，知漏下矣。娟命酒，顷刻肴核排列，无非珍异，尤多不知名者，固非人间所有。生饮次，问娟有父母兄弟姊妹乎？娟曰：“皆下世矣。虽有姊妹行，亦各适所天，他日会有相见时也。”又问：“卿富贵极矣，而园庭荒庭若此，何也？”娟曰：“此宗室贵公之园，借以暂居，与郎毕姻后，仍返故宅耳。”生又问曰：“卿先世作何官？”娟笑曰：“二十岁人，底事呴呴呕呕，如老妇然。夜深矣，无事多问。”生颊为之赤，举觞自罚，三更始就寝。象床雕几，锦枕绣衾，红烛高烧，金炉香袅，恍游天上，如在梦中矣。娟虽齿稚，而帷薄之间，狎亵殊甚，每移灯近榻，令二婢更番侍侧，通宵嬉　。生力惫，则进酒一小卮，色似珊瑚，香逾艾纳，饮之，精神骤旺，兴发如狂。娟体虽软弱，颇能支也。自此好合无间，朝夕不离跬步。

    Explanation

    357

    Upon hearing the sound of the inner city's Pulu bell, like the roar of a whale in the sea, he knew that the water clock had fallen. Juan ordered wine, and in an instant, a variety of delicacies were arranged, none but rare and precious, especially many that were unknown, undoubtedly not of this world. Sheng drank, and asked Juan if she had parents, siblings, or sisters. Juan replied, "They have all passed away. Though I have sisters, they have each gone to their own fate, and there will be a time when we will meet again." Sheng then asked, "You are extremely wealthy, yet the garden is so desolate, why is that?" Juan said, "This is the garden of a nobleman from the royal family, where I am staying temporarily. After I marry the young man, I will return to my original home." Sheng asked again, "What positions did your ancestors hold?" Juan laughed and said, "A twenty-year-old, fussing and arguing like an old woman. It's late, don't ask too many questions." Sheng's cheeks turned red, and he took a cup and punished himself. It was three in the morning before he went to sleep. An ivory bed with carved tables, brocade pillows and embroidered quilts, red candles burning brightly, fragrant smoke rising from a golden incense burner—everything made him feel as if he were floating in heaven, like being in a dream. Though Juan was young, in the privacy of their chamber, their intimacy was intense. She would move the lamp closer to the bed, and have two maids take turns serving her side, keeping her company all night. When Sheng grew tired, she would offer him a small cup of wine, its color like coral and its fragrance surpassing that of musk. After drinking it, his spirit surged, and his excitement grew wild. Though Juan's body was weak, she could still manage. From then on, they were perfectly harmonious, never apart even for a step.

    358

    娟有异术，往往收取各种花子，祝之，化为异香；含之，齿舌俱馥。又能摄取诸物，从心所欲，顷刻至前，助荔枝杨梅之难至者，莫不应之如响。一日，谓生曰：“可检点作归计矣。”生曰：“以我车来，以尔贿迁。”娟曰：“无需于君，但劳玉趾一行耳。日间或不便，夜去可也。”是夕，男妇来者甚众，见娟与生，皆下拜，几榻箱笼，争相负荷，须臾而尽。娟携生率翘、楚，缓步从之。未一里，即至一巨室，雕甍画栋，榱桷连延，五步一轩，十步一阁，回廊曲栏，花木幽深，应接不暇。惟自忖度，非夙有仙缘乌能得此？虽南面百城，弗与易矣。既而入室，陈设尤华美。于是食餍甘肥，衣厌细软，息功名之念，绝乡国之思，转盼已逾两月。

    Explanation

    358

    Ju possessed extraordinary abilities, often gathering various seeds, praying over them, and transforming them into strange fragrances; holding them in her mouth, her teeth and tongue were filled with their scent. She could also attract and bring various objects, acting according to her wishes, arriving in an instant, and assisting in bringing things that were hard to obtain, such as lychees and wax apples, with responses as swift as echoes. One day, she told the young man, "It is time to prepare for departure." He replied, "Let my carriage come, and let your belongings be transported." Ju said, "No need for you; just walk over once. It might not be convenient during the day; it is better to go at night." That evening, many men and women came. When they saw Ju and the young man, they all bowed, and they helped with beds, tables, boxes, and trunks, completing everything in a short while. Ju led the young man, Qiao, and Chu, walking slowly behind them. Not even a mile later, they arrived at a large mansion, with carved eaves and painted beams, interconnected rafters and beams, a pavilion every five steps, and a tower every ten steps, winding corridors and curved railings, dense and secluded flower and tree groves, too many to take in. Reflecting on it, she thought, "Only those with a predestined connection with the immortals could achieve such a place; even governing a hundred cities facing south cannot be exchanged for it." After entering the mansion, the furnishings were especially luxurious. They then ate delicious and fatty food, wore fine and soft clothes, gave up thoughts of fame and fortune, eliminated thoughts of home and country, and in a blink of an eye, more than two months had passed.

    359

    娟往往他出，出必与楚楚俱。或数日始返，返必退处别室，越一宿，然后同生寝食，率以为常。生诘之，笑而不答，第颂梁武帝诗以应之曰：“满塘莲花开，红光照碧水，色同心复同，藕异心无异。”生莫解其意，亦不复穷究。

    Explanation

    359

    Juan often went out, and whenever she went out, she always went with Chu-chu. She might return after several days. Upon returning, she always retreated to a separate room. After spending one night, she then shared the same quarters and meals with the male protagonist, and this became a regular practice. The male protagonist questioned her about it, but she smiled and did not answer. Instead, she recited a poem by Emperor Wu of Liang to respond: "The lotus flowers in the pond bloom, red light shines on the green water, their colors and hearts are the same, yet the roots are different, but the hearts are not." The male protagonist did not understand the meaning of the poem and did not further investigate.

    360

    数日后，娟又有所诣，携翘翘去，留楚楚伴生。生因乘间询楚楚曰：“娘子每出数日，究竟何往？”楚楚曰：“诗中之意，郎犹未会耶？”生曰：“几经寻味，终不得解。”楚楚掩口笑曰：“措大心思若此，何啻著低棋者，虽穷思极算，又岂有高着出耶？”生语塞，聊以谑语解嘲曰：“予固未尝搜诗意，特见子狡猾，欲以发付之耳。”楚楚闻之，且哂曰：“听饰词，殊可笑，转欲请问如何发付矣？谅郎君口同百舌，胆如鼷鼠，讵敢作犯法事，亦不过一言半语，讨人便宜而已。正俗语所谓说大话燥脾者，真足以笑煞人也！”语既尖酸，态复妖媚，生不能复耐，猝捉其臂，捺之床上，开掌作欲打状，曰：“小婢子敢再嘲笑，受此一掌！”楚斜卧榻上，并不转侧，但瞑目作娇音应曰：“一掌便何如，欲打谁耶？”生随势接吻曰：“忍打卿耶？聊相戏耳。”言次，楚楚亵衣已被褪落，渐入佳境矣。由此二人绸缪臻至，惟恐娟归之速。

    Explanation

    360

    Several days later, Juan went somewhere again, taking Qiaoqiao with her and leaving Chuchu to keep company with the speaker. Taking advantage of the opportunity, the speaker asked Chuchu, "Where does Lady go every time she leaves for several days?" Chuchu replied, "The meaning in the poem, has the gentleman not understood it yet?" The speaker said, "After several attempts to understand, I still cannot grasp it." Chuchu covered her mouth and laughed, "The gentleman's thoughts are so limited, how can they compare to a low-level chess player? Even if he thinks deeply and calculates thoroughly, how could there be a good move?" The speaker was speechless and tried to lighten the mood with a joke, "I have never searched for the meaning of the poem, I just see you as cunning and want to deal with it." Chuchu heard this and laughed, "Listening to flattering words, it's very funny. Now I want to ask how to deal with it. I believe the gentleman talks a lot like a parrot, is as timid as a small mouse, how dare he do something illegal? He only takes advantage of people with a few words. It's just what the common saying means: talking big and irritating people, it's truly laughable!" Her words were sharp and acidic, and her demeanor was charming and alluring. The speaker could no longer bear it and suddenly grabbed her arm, pushing her onto the bed, opening his palm as if to strike, saying, "Little maid, dare to laugh again, and you'll get a slap!" Chuchu lay sideways on the bed, not turning over, but closed her eyes and responded in a coquettish voice, "What would a slap do, who do you want to hit?" The speaker followed up with a kiss, saying, "How can I hit you? Just for fun." As he spoke, Chuchu's undergarments were already removed, and they gradually entered a better state. From then on, the two were deeply entwined, only fearing that Juan would return quickly.

    361

    无何娟回，熟视楚楚，颜色顿异，生在旁殊怀愧悚，翘翘低语告娟曰：“娘子之螟　不去，终当泄其秘密。”娟但摇首令勿言。少间曰：“休，休！木有瘿，犀有通，石有晕，物以病而见责者多矣。何怪小女子乎？直突而不徙薪，无怪其然。且儿既不能雌伏，宁能禁人不雄飞哉？此间本非乐土，今又成秽墟，会须直还故居，以谋宁谧耳。”楚楚目生，生会意，遽下席长跽而谢曰：“承卿不弃寒微，宝窗自选，岂敢恋兹春色，逞其豕心！”娟曳之起曰：“有则改之，无则加勉。若夫不夷不惠，可否之间，固处世之所珍，非用情之所贵也。”生顿首受教，相睦如初。

    Explanation

    361

    Soon after, Ju returned, looking intently at Chu Chu, her expression suddenly changed. The man standing beside her felt deeply ashamed and fearful, whispering to Ju, "If the issue with your [problem] remains unresolved, it will eventually reveal its secrets." Ju merely shook her head, indicating that he should not speak. After a short pause, she said, "Enough, enough! Wood has knots, rhinoceros horns have flaws, and stones have veins. Many things are criticized or blamed for their imperfections. Why should I blame a young woman for hers? To face the fire without moving the firewood is not surprising. Moreover, if I cannot remain passive, how can I prevent others from taking action? This place is not a happy land, and now it has become a desolate ruin. We must return directly to our original home to seek peace and tranquility." Chu Chu looked at the man with a certain expression, and he understood her meaning. He quickly knelt down and apologized, saying, "I accept your kindness in not rejecting my humble status, and I chose you through the window. How dare I be attached to this spring scenery and act on my selfish desires!" Ju pulled him up and said, "If there is fault, correct it; if there is none, be more cautious. As for a balanced approach between extremes, the middle ground between right and wrong, this is valued in the world, but not in matters of emotion." The man bowed deeply to accept her instruction, and they returned to their harmonious state as before.

    362

    娟再迁之志已决，尽弃所有，即日起行。生怪问其故，娟曰：“此皆易得，不足恋也。”生甚疑，且实难舍多珍，行里许，托言腹急，潜回故处视之，但见林树如故，第宅无存，蒿莱间草屋数椽，垣壁颓圯，似久无人居者。四顾茫茫，始大骇异，方徘徊间，翘、楚踵至，呼曰：“郎流连何事？”生曰：“偶忆诗稿未携，欲还取之，不意迷路至此。”楚楚曰：“此去故宅，已百余里矣，庸得归乎？”生曰：“行未一里，那便隔绝若此？”翘笑曰：“偕仙人行，岂同凡俗？郎勿梦梦！”牵引却回。才数武，娟已坐待路侧，见生至，怨曰：“奈何兔脱？再迟一刻，不得相见矣！”生不敢辩，因复进，又数里入林中，夏木千章，荫蔽天日，穿林抵一洞穴，望之黝然。

    Explanation

    362

    Jiao's resolve to move again was firm, and she abandoned everything, starting her journey from that very day. The husband was surprised and asked her the reason. Jiao said, "All these things are easily obtained, not worth longing for." The husband was very suspicious and found it hard to part with his many treasures. After walking a short distance, he pretended to have an urgent need to use the restroom and secretly returned to the old place to look at it. He only saw the trees as before, but the mansion was no longer there. There was a dilapidated thatched cottage among weeds, with crumbling walls, seeming to have been unoccupied for a long time. Looking around in confusion, he finally became greatly alarmed and astonished. As he was hesitating, Qiao and Chu followed closely, calling out, "What are you lingering for, sir?" The husband said, "I accidentally remembered that I had not brought my poetry manuscripts, wanting to go back and retrieve them, but unintentionally got lost here." Chu said, "It is already over a hundred li away from our former home, how can one return?" The husband said, "Having walked only one li, how could it be so isolated?" Qiao smiled and said, "Traveling with immortals, how could it be the same as ordinary people? You should not be in a daze!" She led him back. Just a few steps away, Jiao was already sitting by the roadside, waiting for him. Seeing the husband arrive, she scolded, "Why did you escape like a rabbit? If you had delayed for just a moment, we would not have met!" The husband dared not argue and followed her again. After walking a few more miles into the forest, they came upon a cave, surrounded by tall summer trees that shaded the sky, and the cave looked dark inside.

    363

    娟先入，生却步不敢前，翘、楚自后挤之，失足颠踣，已在穴中。穴旁另辟一门，翘、楚继至，共启门而入，则巨室也，华丽不及故宅，而雅静过之，恍若别有一天。且惊且喜，自念曰：“今夕何夕，入此穴处。”娟笑曰：“穀则异室，寝则同穴。”相与拊掌，呼酒共酌。生问曰：“弃故居如敝屐，散仆婢于四方，其故何也？”娟曰：“天地皆泡幻也，，故居尤幻之幻者，奴婢各有居处，有事聚之，无事各散。郎但取衣食裁足，共图长生。至求盈余，徒自苦耳。此处洞天福地，有离尘出世之妙，无玄冥回禄之虞，虽紫府蓉城，不过如是。尘世幻境，恋恋何为乎？”

    Explanation

    363

    Juan went in first, and the man hesitated and did not dare to follow. Qiao and Chu pushed him from behind, causing him to lose his footing and fall, and he was already inside the cave. Beside the cave, there was another door. Qiao and Chu followed, and together they opened the door and entered a large room. It was not as splendid as the old house, but it was more elegant and quiet, seeming like a different world altogether. He was both surprised and delighted, and thought to himself, "What night is this, that I have entered this cave?" Juan smiled and said, "We live in different rooms, but we sleep in the same cave." They clapped their hands together and called for wine to drink together. The man asked, "Why did we abandon our old home as if it were a worn-out shoe, and scatter our servants and maids in all directions? What is the reason for this?" Juan replied, "The world is all an illusion, and our old home is even more illusory. Our servants and maids each have their own places to stay. We gather them when we need them, and let them go their separate ways when we don't. All you need is enough food and clothing, and we will work together to achieve immortality. If you seek more than what is necessary, you only cause yourself unnecessary suffering. This place is a paradise on earth, with the charm of being detached from the world. There is no danger of disaster or misfortune. Even the celestial palaces of the Purple Palace and the City of Refined Flowers are not better than this. Why cling to the illusions of the mortal world?"

    364

    有顷，楚楚报曰：“莘姨闻娘子偕郎君归，携盒来贺矣。”娟与生曰：“莘妹与儿故相得，郎见之，但呼为姨可矣。”俄而莘至，亦十七八好女子也。相见欢然，犹有羞态，不似娟初会时，载笑载言，有见惯司空之局。裣衽贺娟曰：“久与三姐契阔，靡日不思，讵意去甫两月，遂叶凤占。老母闻之，良喜，先命儿致不腆之仪表意，寻当自来。今观姐夫，如玉山照人，洵称佳偶，非三姐厚福，煞难消受。”娟笑曰：“妹大为溢美，独不虑揄扬过当，惹人笑破唇耶？”呼翘翘耳语，翘诺而去，移时偕一媪至，娟迎拜，以婶呼之，盖莘母也。

    Explanation

    364

    After a while, Chu Chu reported, "Shen Auntie heard that Miss and Master have returned together, and she has come with a gift to congratulate you." Juan said to her husband, "Shen Sister and I have always gotten along well. When you see her, just call her Aunt." Shortly after, Shen arrived, and she was also a beautiful young woman of seventeen or eighteen. They greeted each other happily, but Shen still had a shy demeanor, unlike Juan when they first met, who laughed and talked freely, as if they had seen each other many times before. Shen bowed gracefully and congratulated Juan, saying, "I've been separated from my third sister for so long that I couldn't think of anything else. Who would have thought that after only two months, we would have such a favorable outcome? Our mother is very happy and first sent me to bring a modest token of respect. She will come herself soon. Now that I see your husband-in-law, he is as radiant as a jade mountain, truly a good match. Without your sister's great fortune, it would be hard to bear." Juan laughed and said, "You are being overly flattering. Don't you worry about overpraising and making people laugh until their lips split?" She called Qiaoqiao over to whisper to her, and Qiaoqiao nodded and left. After some time, an old woman arrived with her. Juan greeted her respectfully and called her Aunt, for she was Shen's mother.

    365

    生亦拜，媪且答且相，喜曰：“此即新郎君耶？谁家千里驹因风至此，老身在世六十年，阅人何啻千万，所见英妙者，西城某侯子，某银局祝六官，与郎君鼎足而三焉。然彼二人，如春暮桃花，皆天啬其年，畴不痛惜！郎君独与三姐遇合，愁何不寿？较二子，真天渊也。乌得不贺！”乃命莘女执壶，自把盏，先酌生，次酌娟，最后酌莘，曰：“汝亦当贺一斝者，三姐已得佳婿矣，次当及汝，不一半年，亦有伉债之望也！”莘俯首羞甚，红潮两颊，缩手不取杯，娟接杯强饮之，曰：“娘赐酒，乃敢不饮，老人家语，有一字淫泛耶？”生亦从旁与翘、楚和之，尽欢而后散去。

    Explanation

    365

    The groom also bowed, and the old woman, while answering, also served, and happily said, "Is this the new groom? Which family's thousand-mile horse came here by the wind? I have been alive for sixty years, and I have seen countless people. The most talented and beautiful ones I have seen are the son of a certain marquis from the Western City and the sixth official of a certain silver bureau, and together with the groom, they form a trio. But those two, like peach blossoms at the end of spring, were both short-lived due to fate. Who would not grieve? The groom alone meets and marries the third sister, why shouldn't he live a long life? Compared to the other two, he is truly in a different world. How can we not celebrate?" She then ordered the daughter of Xun to hold the wine pot and held the cup herself, first pouring for the groom, then for Ju, and finally for Xun, saying, "You should also celebrate with a cup, the third sister has found a good husband, next, it will be your turn, not more than half a year, you will also have the hope of a good marriage!" Xun bowed her head, blushing greatly, and pulled her hand back, not taking the cup. Ju took the cup and forced herself to drink it, saying, "Mother has given us wine, how dare we not drink? Is there any word in the old lady's speech that is improper?" The groom also joined in with Qiao and Chu, and they enjoyed themselves fully before dispersing.

    366

    翌日，娟谓生曰：“来而不往，非礼也，请偕郎一候莘姨。”生从之，由穴旁一小门入，行土窟中，约一矢地，更达一土室。莘母女咸在，接待极欢，殷殷留饮。生周视室中，虽俱精洁，唯一榻一几，余无所有，殊形简陋。归问娟曰：“莘家无乃贫甚，何身外别无长物？”娟笑曰：“郎见其仅有几榻乎？不知几榻犹假于儿者！虽苦贫，幸与儿邻，不特免呼庚癸，且多饶裕。”生曰：“此亦足见卿侠矣。”

    Explanation

    366

    The next day, Juan said to the young man, "To come without reciprocating is not in accordance with propriety. Please accompany me to visit Aunt Shen." The young man followed her, entering through a small door beside a hole and walking through an earth cave. After about the length of an arrow's flight, they reached an earthen room. Shen's mother and daughter were both present, and the reception was extremely joyful. They earnestly invited him to stay and drink. The young man looked around the room, noting that although everything was clean and tidy, there was only one bed and one table, and nothing else. The room appeared particularly simple and humble. Upon returning, he asked Juan, "Is Shen's family not very poor? Why does she have no valuables outside of herself?" Juan smiled and said, "This also shows your chivalry."

    367

    居无何，莘持锦笺一幅，乞生书《玉台新咏序》，生为仿《洛神赋》小楷以应之。莘得书，珍同拱璧，谓序与书及书序之人，可称三绝。会娟他出，翘、楚与俱，生独坐，莘又将聚骨小扇一柄来，欲生书汉《杂事秘辛》。生谑曰：“几曾见人家处子，向外人索写秽亵语，不避嫌疑乎？”莘曰：“娟姐不在，翘、楚随行，此事尔我外，畴复知之。”生曰：“信如子言，则女莹之事，何以至今广传？”莘曰：“好事者为之耳，计当日窥见至隐，止吴姁一人，苟秘而不宣，焉能泄漏？”生曰：“然则今日亦幽独矣，子能容我为吴姁否？”莘面发赧，拈带不语。生知其情动，遽前拥之，出示其具，莘掩袖微睇曰：“波俏郎举体皆韵，此物何太不雅观？”生笑曰：“貌虽不韵，而韵事在其中矣。”女心大动，不复抗拒，遂相与绸缪，如胶投漆。

    Explanation

    367

    After a short while, Shen held out a piece of brocade paper and asked the scholar to write the preface to The New Songs from the Jade Terrace. The scholar imitated the small, neat style of The Ode to the Luo River Goddess to respond. Shen treasured the preface as if it were a precious jade disk, saying that the preface, the calligraphy, and the person who wrote it could be called three masterpieces. Just as Jiuan had left, Qiao and Chu were with her, and the scholar was sitting alone. Shen then brought a fan made of bones and wanted the scholar to write Secret Histories of Han. The scholar joked, "Have you ever seen a young woman ask outsiders to write indecent words without avoiding suspicion?" Shen replied, "Jiuan is not here, and Qiao and Chu are with me. Outside of us, who else would know it?" The scholar said, "If I believe your words, then how has the story of Lady Ying spread so widely?" Shen said, "It was done by those who like to meddle. On that day, only Wu Xu saw the most hidden things. If it was kept secret and not announced, how could it leak out?" The scholar said, "Then today is also secluded. Can you allow me to be Wu Xu?" Shen's face turned red with shame, and she fidgeted with her belt and remained silent. The scholar knew her emotions were stirred and hurriedly embraced her, showing her equipment. Shen covered her sleeve and peeked, saying, "The handsome young man is all elegant, but this thing is too not elegant?" The scholar laughed, "Although his appearance may not be elegant, there is elegance within." Shen's heart greatly stirred, and she no longer resisted. Then they were intimate together, like glue and paint.

    368

    既而莘泣谓生曰：“初以郎为儇薄子，久乃知为朴厚人也。儿生不逢辰，死复抱恨。一旦委身君子，亦云奇遇。郎罹祸目前，宁忍坐视？欲明以相告，第虑新不间旧，徒取嫌耳。”生曰：“卿过虑矣。夫以少年孤客，漂泊无依，天假之年，得遇娟姐，何翅裴航之泛鄂渚，阮肇之入天台。今又与卿有契，方自庆多福。罹祸之说，突如其来，诚所不解。”莘闻之废然，良久始叹曰：“儿固知病在膏盲之间者，药石所不能入也。郎并枕於菟，连盘野葛，自谓快心悦口，殊不知通心钻，彻骨锥，虽有燕函，贯七札而犹脱颖矣。

    Explanation

    368

    Afterward, Shen wept and said to the Scholar, "At first, I considered you a superficial and fickle person, but after a long time, I came to know you as a simple and sincere individual. My child was born unlucky and died with regret. Suddenly entrusting himself to a virtuous person, it was indeed a rare encounter. Now, you are facing misfortune, how could I sit idly by and watch? I want to clearly inform you, but I only worry that the new will not interfere with the old, and it would only cause resentment. You overthink, Scholar. As a young man alone and wandering without support, heaven granted me years, and I met Miss Juan, how much more than the legendary adventures of Pei Hang sailing on the waters of E Zhu and Ruan Zhao entering the Peach Blossom Spring. Now, having a bond with you, I celebrate my many blessings. The sudden misfortune, coming without warning, truly leaves me puzzled." Hearing this, Shen was disheartened and, after a long while, sighed, "I knew that the illness lies between the vital organs, beyond the reach of medicine and stone. You lie together with the tiger, eat dishes with wild kudzu, thinking it pleases your heart and satisfies your taste, but you do not know that it penetrates your heart like a drill and pierces your bones like a needle, even with the strong box from Yan, it still stands out through seven layers."

    369

    彼娟姐非人，乃天坛中一老狐也，为其迷媚而死者，指不胜偻。总为采取元精，以恣其欲，岂果有纤毫仁义，与郎作偕老计也？人情固多好色，似不应竭有限之精神，填无穷之沟壑。”生闻之，惊怖股慄，结舌不能语。莘曰：“郎试自维，倘扭情缠爱，虽死不悔，则儿言诚赘矣。若犹有恋世之心，惴死之念，当思早离岌岌之地，遵坦坦之途，儿从中为郎筹划，转祸为福，起死回生，亦易举耳。”生大惧，长跪请计，曰：“听卿言，如梦觉矣。如蒙援手，敢不镂肝！”莘牵使就坐，袖出一符授生曰：“勿惮惶，且将此贴户上，令老魅来，不得入，而后徐计未晚。”

    369

    She is not a human, but an old fox from the Heavenly Altar. Those who died because they were bewitched by her beauty are too numerous to count. In total, she took the primordial essence to satisfy her desires. How could she have the slightest bit of benevolence or intention to live with the young man in old age? Human nature is indeed often fond of beauty, it seems one should not exhaust their limited spirit to fill an endless abyss." Upon hearing this, the man was frightened and trembling with fear, speechless and unable to speak. She said, "Young master, please consider: if you are deeply entangled in love, even if it means death, you would not regret it, then my words are indeed superfluous. If you still have a heart that longs for the world and a fear of death, you should think about leaving this precarious situation as soon as possible and following a straightforward path. I will help you plan, turning misfortune into good fortune, bringing you back from the brink of death, and it is easy to do so." The man was greatly afraid and knelt for a long time to ask for advice, saying, "Hearing your words is like waking up from a dream. If you help me, how dare I not dedicate my heart and soul!" She led him to sit down and took out a talisman from her sleeve to give to him, saying, "Do not be afraid. Stick this on the door, so that the old demon cannot enter, and then it is not too late to plan later."

    370

    生贴符却回，涕泣求救，莘为画策曰：“娟虽淫毒，然通灵有术，能变化。避之不密，彼终能踪迹之。今欲计万全，非求黄道士符录不可。黄道士援太乙秘笈，持五雷正法，住五岳观，郎屈节求之，符即可得。此事不得缓，三日内不得，则大事去矣。彼每出不返者，以仍有人被惑故也。此际必幻化园墅，相与流连。如郎朝死，夕即有他人入室。盖此间为狐之巢穴，虽出千里，迟数年，终须归此耳。彼出已两日矣，再三五日当返，郎欲逃，正其时也。”生且悲且喜，再拜谢曰：“卿起白骨而肉之，何以图报！”莘亦泣曰：“宁生离，无死别，行矣，慎之勿相忘！缘尽于此矣！”生曰：“累卿将奈何！”莘曰：“儿闻贤者急病而让夷，况儿亦有术，自能发付老魅，无虑也！”于是再四促之，生不得已，握手辞行，哭失声，莘急止之。

    Explanation

    370

    The young man returned with a charm, weeping and pleading for help. Xun devised a plan and said, "Although Juan is licentious and poisonous, she is spiritually adept and has magical abilities. If one does not avoid her carefully, she will eventually track them down. To ensure safety, it is necessary to seek the talisman from the Yellow Daoist. The Yellow Daoist consults the secret scriptures of the Supreme One and practices the Five Thunder orthodox methods, residing in the Five Peaks Temple. The young man must humble himself to obtain it. This matter cannot be delayed; if it is not obtained within three days, the major problem will be lost. Why she never returns is because there are still people being deceived by her. At this time, she must be transforming into a garden or villa, lingering together. If the young man dies in the morning, in the evening someone else will enter the room. This place is a den for foxes; even if one goes a thousand miles away, after several years, one must eventually return here. She has been out for two days already and will return in another three to five days. Now is the right time to escape." The young man, both sad and happy, bowed twice and thanked, saying, "You revived my body from bones, how can I repay you?" Xun also wept, saying, "I would rather be separated in life than die apart, go, be careful and don't forget, our缘分 ends here!" The young man said, "What should I do about burdening you?" Xun replied, "I have heard that the wise prioritize urgent matters and yield to righteousness. Besides, I also have skills and can handle the old demon myself, there is no need to worry!" Thus, he urged him repeatedly, and the young man had no choice but to shake hands and bid farewell, crying out loud, and Xun quickly stopped him.

    371

    送至门，忽愕然曰：“为郎几误切己事。”乃复携入室，亟解衣，出一紫罗囊，探囊出一白玉小印，方寸许，上作螭续，其文曰：“异地同符。”赠生曰：“物虽微，即宝之，可以致福也。他日遇购者，究印之所自至，但云‘得诸广渠门外城隍间’可矣。儿所以厚赠，虽聊酬一夕枕席之爱，抑有一事相嘱托，幸垂庇也。”生曰：“一身皆卿之赐，更何事不尽心力者？”莘再拜而谢，乃泣诉曰：“郎勿骇，儿亦非人，实鬼也。生时本河南人，因岁荒流徙入都，随老母佣于崇文门内王氏家。

    Explanation

    371

    送至门，忽愕然曰：“为郎几误切己事。”乃复携入室，亟解衣，出一紫罗囊，探囊出一白玉小印，方寸许，上作螭续，其文曰：“异地同符。”赠生曰：“物虽微，即宝之，可以致福也。他日遇购者，究印之所自至，但云‘得诸广渠门外城隍间’可矣。儿所以厚赠，虽聊酬一夕枕席之爱，抑有一事相嘱托，幸垂庇也。”生曰：“一身皆卿之赐，更何事不尽心力者？”莘再拜而谢，乃泣诉曰：“郎勿骇，儿亦非人，实鬼也。生时本河南人，因岁荒流徙入都，随老母佣于崇文门内王氏家。

    Translation:

    When they reached the door, he suddenly exclaimed in surprise, "I almost made a serious mistake regarding something personal." He then took her back into the room, quickly undressed, and took out a purple silk pouch. From the pouch, he removed a small white jade seal, about an inch in size, with a dragon motif on top. The inscription read, "Different places, the same symbol." He gave the seal to the young man and said, "This object may seem insignificant, but treasure it, as it can bring good fortune. If someone tries to buy it in the future, trace the origin of the seal, but simply say you obtained it from the city god's temple outside the Guangqu Gate. I am giving you this gift not only to repay a night of shared intimacy, but also to entrust you with one thing, hoping for your protection." The young man replied, "Everything I have is thanks to you; what more could I do for you?" The woman bowed twice and thanked him, then wept and said, "Do not be alarmed, I am not human, but a ghost. In life, I was from Henan, and due to a famine, my family moved to the capital. I worked as a servant for the Wang family inside Chongwen Gate."

    372

    王固巨宗，货贩遍天下，所赖以此玉章为符节。玉章者，闽中江皜臣所镌也，人不得借。凡有王氏玉章印记者，无论江楚之远，川广之遥，虽片纸只字，亦能立质千万。一旦失之，十余年来不复响应，王深为恨悒，或诬儿母女盗去，王挞母至死，儿亦投缳。乃藁葬于此，左邻狐穴，右比獾窝，抱恨九幽，愁魂千载。君能买高原一寸土，俾得改瘗，则衔结之报，永矢弗谖。”生曰：“若挟泰山，超北海，予固不能矣。似此琐事，不足萦卿怀抱也。”言讫分手，莘指墓前枯槐树曰：“志此勿忘。”生审视良久，不忍言别，莘引身入穴，始嚎啕而去。

    Explanation

    372

    Wang Gu was a prominent figure, with his goods traded throughout the world, and he relied on this jade seal as a symbol of authority. The jade seal was engraved by Jiang Haochen from Fujian, and no one else was allowed to borrow it. Anyone who bore the Wang family's jade seal, regardless of how distant they were in Jiangnan or how far they were in the southwest, could instantly be worth thousands with even a single piece of paper or a few words. Once lost, there was no response for over ten years, and Wang was deeply resentful and sorrowful. He falsely accused his son and daughter of stealing it, beat his mother to death, and his son also hanged himself. They were then hastily buried here, with a fox den to the left and a badger hole to the right, holding grudges in the underworld and a sorrowful spirit for thousands of years. If you could buy a small piece of high ground, allowing them to be reinterred, then I would pledge gratitude forever not to be forgotten. The living said, "If one could carry Mount Tai across the North Sea, I would not be able to do it. Such a trivial matter is not worth troubling you." After finishing speaking and parting, Xian pointed to a withered locust tree in front of the tomb and said, "Remember this." The living examined it for a long time, unable to bear saying goodbye, and Xian led himself into a cave, sobbing and leaving.

    373

    时辰星映野，斜月入林。蹀躞蹒跚，约十余里，甫望见楼堞，逢人谘诹，始得至五岳观。果有黄道士者，童颜玉色，须眉似金，貌极怪伟，生顿首乞符。黄熟视而咍曰：“妖气濡染未深，何伤弓惊饵之早？子真机警人哉！”书三符付之曰：“终生佩之可也。”生承教而去。

    　　径来所寓寺中，寺僧见而惊曰：“先生一晌在何处？致老僧怀惑至今？”生以诡词绐之，问僮仆焉往，僧曰：“回南月余矣。”行李尚存乎？曰：“携去矣。”生惝怳无措手足处。僧曰：“先生岂无亲故仕于京师者，盍往就之？”生曰：“有亲属为部郎，往岁已左迁外补矣。今也则无。”僧曰：“朋友亦可与谋。

    Explanation

    373

    The stars shone over the fields as the slanting moon entered the forest. Walking slowly and hesitantly, he covered about ten or more miles, just seeing the battlements when he met someone and asked for advice, finally reaching the Wu Yue Temple. Indeed, there was a Yellow Daoist there, with a youthful face and jade-like complexion, eyebrows and beard like gold, and a very strange and majestic appearance. The man bowed deeply and begged for a talisman. The Yellow Daoist looked at him carefully and laughed, saying, "The evil influence has not deeply penetrated; why are you so alarmed so early? You are truly a cautious person!" He wrote three talismans and gave them to him, saying, "You can wear them all your life." The man accepted the advice and left.

    He went directly to the temple where he was staying. The temple monk saw him and was surprised, asking, "Where have you been for a moment? Leaving the old monk confused until now?" The man used deceptive words to deceive him, asking where the servants had gone. The monk replied, "They have been back in the south for more than a month." Is the luggage still there? "They took it away." The man felt lost and did not know what to do. The monk said, "Do you not have relatives or acquaintances who serve in the capital? Why don't you go to them?" The man replied, "I have relatives who are officials, but they were demoted last year and transferred to a remote post. Now I have none." The monk said, "Friends can also be consulted."

    374

    ”生曰：“纵朋友怜而顾我，我何面目见之？况任黎交谊，世有几人；倘觌面云泥，情何以堪！”僧曰：“先生固才貌兼者，怀策以谒时贤，投刺以干当道，必得上贡天子，何衣食之足虑乎？”生潸然曰：“落拓如此，谁复肯斡旋者！自媒不遂，则身辱名裂，宁冻馁以填沟壑，不忍摇尾向人也！”僧乃喟然叹曰：“往者余弗及，来者余弗闻，老僧眼中所见之士，先生一人而已。孓身无依，而不屑干谒；糊口有地，而深耻托钵。爱其品节，重其羔雁。先生尚志，非长贫贱者。请设一榻，而屈先生卖赋长安，以待时至，不亦可乎？”生乃感谢，便栖寺中，为人代书。

    Explanation

    374

    Even if friends pity and look after me, what face can I show them? How many in the world have such a deep friendship? If we meet, it would be like meeting someone from the sky and someone from the earth; how can I bear it emotionally? You are indeed someone who combines talent and appearance, carry your plans to visit contemporary scholars and present your credentials to influential people, you will surely be able to present your talents to the emperor, so why worry about food and clothing? In such a state of poverty and despair, who would be willing to help? If self-promotion fails, you will lose your reputation and dignity, rather than starve to death in a ditch, I would not beg for help. The past I cannot reach, and the future I cannot hear, the only person I have seen in my eyes is you. Alone and without support, yet unwilling to seek favors, having a place to eat, yet deeply ashamed to beg, I admire his moral integrity and value his offerings. You have lofty aspirations and are not destined for poverty and lowly status. Please set up a bed for you, and let you sell your essays in Chang'an, waiting for the right opportunity, is this not possible? He then thanked him and stayed in the temple, writing for others.

    375

    一日，寺僧市五色绢笺，乞生作书，云为檀那作寿轴。书成苦无图章，即取玉章印之，僧更市面桃素食汤饼，易新衣，驾骡车，入城去。生目送而笑曰：“玉章今日出脱矣。守钱虏未必能辨，即以护封为押角，应无不可者，第恐识者见之捧腹耳。”晡时，僧却回，色殊愉快，入门即问曰：“先生写作俱佳，不待言矣，而所用图章，从何得来？”生曰：“偶然得之者，盖吾乡江皜臣之所镌也。”僧曰：“此大异事。城中王翁，敝寺檀施也。见图章，把玩良久，测其意，似喜似惊，再三致诘老僧，具说本末。翁嘱致声，翌日必欲先生入城，并携玉章。此老素浑朴，无废词，其言欲如此，则必如此矣。幸先生勿拘执，明晨同老僧一往，自有代步，不致役役也。”生窃怪莘女之言有征，诺之。

    Explanation

    375

    One day, a monk from the temple bought colorful silk paper and asked the writer to compose a letter, saying it was for a donor's birthday scroll. After finishing the letter, the writer found he had no seal, so he used a jade seal. The monk then bought vegetarian food made from peach tree bark and noodles, changed into new clothes, harnessed a mule cart, and went into the city. The writer watched him leave and smiled, saying, "The jade seal has been disposed of today. A stingy person might not recognize it, but using the protective cover as a corner seal should be fine. Only those who know about it might laugh at it." In the afternoon, the monk returned, looking very happy, and immediately asked, "Master, your writing and calligraphy are both excellent, need no further praise, but where did you get the seal you used?" The writer replied, "I happened to get it. It was carved by Jiang Haochen from my hometown." The monk said, "This is quite an unusual matter. The elderly man Wang in the city is a generous patron of our temple. When he saw the seal, he played with it for a long time, guessing its meaning, seeming both pleased and surprised. He repeatedly questioned the old monk, and I explained everything. He asked me to pass on a message, saying he wants to see you in the city tomorrow and bring the jade seal with you. This old man is honest and straightforward, and if he says he wants you to come, then you must come. Please don't be stubborn, and let's go together with the old monk tomorrow morning. There will be a carriage to take us, and we won't be busy with other tasks." The writer was secretly surprised that the woman's prediction had come true and agreed.

    376

    早起同造王，王接待甚有礼。酒再巡，即索观玉章。生取诸怀，王一见，愕然，审辨色，询曰：“兄此物得自何方？望勿隐。”生曰：“实非故物，晚间偶循城溪闲步，将至广渠门，坐石小歇，见城隍雉缺处玉色莹然，拾得此印。不意见赏于翁，愈当宝贵矣。”王曰：“老夫不言，兄亦不知，盖此印实老夫之故物也，失之十余年矣。今闻得诸城湾，始追忆往日，曾归自郊坰，小遗于城下，恐坠落至损，暂置墙隙中，竟致遗忘，兄所言，询不诬矣。但此物虽微，先世所留遗也，讵可至我而失之。兄忠恕，如肯见还，当以千金奉酬耳。”生曰：“物归本主，理之固然，何敢望酬？”王大喜曰：“老夫有言，驷不及舌，兄勿却。”亟收印入内，一饷方出，奉生千金，更谢僧五十金，尽欢而散。

    Explanation

    376

    Early in the morning, they went together to visit Master Wang, who received them with great courtesy. After the second round of wine, he immediately asked to see the jade seal. The young man took it from his bosom. Wang was startled upon seeing it, examined the color carefully, and asked, "Brother, where did you find this object? Please do not keep it a secret." The young man replied, "It is not an old possession of mine. In the evening, I happened to take a leisurely walk along the city stream, and as I was approaching Guangqu Gate, I sat on a stone to rest briefly. I saw where the city wall had a gap, and the jade color was clear and brilliant. I picked up this seal. Unexpectedly admired by Master Wang, it should be even more precious now." Wang said, "If I had not said anything, you would not know either. This seal is indeed my old possession, which I lost more than ten years ago. Now I hear it was found in the city bay, only now do I recall the past. Once I returned from the outskirts, I left it behind at the city wall, fearing it might fall and get damaged, so I temporarily placed it in a crack in the wall and eventually forgot about it. What you say is not false. But this object, though small, is a legacy from our ancestors. How could it be lost to me? You are loyal and forgiving. If you are willing to return it, I will reward you with a thousand gold pieces." The young man said, "The object returns to its rightful owner. It is only right. How dare I expect a reward?" Wang was greatly pleased and said, "Once an old man speaks, four horses cannot catch his tongue. Do not refuse." He quickly took the seal inside and, after a short while, came out, gave the young man a thousand gold pieces, and also thanked the monk with fifty gold pieces. They parted after enjoying themselves.

    377

    生归寺，亦谢僧百金，始以情告之，并商改葬莘母女之事。僧曰：“先生不肯负恩于鬼，老僧敢绝义于人耶？荼荠不同亩，请早图之。”生遂出资，备双槥，鸠土工，偕僧至枯槐下，掘得骸骨二具。生大恸，沐以香汤，裹以锦襦，纳诸槥中。僧捐柏林净地方二丈以葬之，祭而复归。是夜，梦莘母女来谢，且告彼狐恨儿綦深，誓欲见祸，郎所得三符，祈于墓土焚其二，则无患矣。永诀矣，请从此辞。言讫，哭哀哀而去。生悲而寤，窗月正午，隐隐墙外犹有哭声。反侧不能复寐。次日语僧，僧曰：“莘女有灵，其言胡可不信”。即取符就墓前祝而焚之，纸灰飞起，旋转绕墓三匝，不因微风，直出树杪，知有神气也。

    Explanation

    377

    The man returned to the temple and thanked the monk with a hundred gold coins, then revealed the truth to him and discussed the matter of reburial for the mother and daughter of Xun. The monk said, "If you, sir, are unwilling to repay the ghost's kindness, how dare I, an old monk, sever ties with a human being? Weeds and chrysanthemums should not be planted in the same field. Please act on it early." The man then contributed funds, prepared two coffins, hired earth workers, and accompanied the monk to the withered locust tree, where they dug up two sets of bones. The man was deeply grieved, washed them with fragrant water, wrapped them in brocade robes, and placed them in the coffins. The monk donated two square zhang of clean land in the pine forest to bury them, performed a ceremony, and returned.
