That night, the man dreamed that the mother and daughter of Xun came to thank him and told him that the fox's hatred for the child was very deep, and it swore to bring disaster. They advised him to burn two of the three talismans he had obtained on the tomb soil, and then there would be no harm. "Farewell forever," they said, "please bid us goodbye from here." After speaking, they wept sadly and left. The man woke up in sorrow, and the moon was high in the sky; faint cries could still be heard outside the wall. He tossed and turned, unable to sleep again. The next day, he spoke to the monk, who said, "The daughter of Xun has a spirit; how can her words be doubted?" He immediately took the talismans, chanted at the tomb, and burned two of them. The paper ashes flew up, rotating around the grave three times, without any breeze, straight up to the treetops, indicating a divine presence.

    378

    生肄业成均，次年及第，屡仕清要，年未四十，以病告归，终身不娶，养一侄为螟蛉，教二弟成骐骥，巾栉付之小妾，米盐畀之老姁。日授方略，坐享宴安。冠千与之游，熟悉其事，秋宵剪烛，向予详述之。

    　　闲斋曰：王氏为富不仁，草菅人命，致莘女魂游地府。粉怨香愁，虽不能一控幽冥，为雪恨报冤之举，已足悲矣。虽然，自古钱能役鬼，财可通神，凡受其颠倒者，不知凡几矣。又何有于一莘？莘纵有灵，亦不过于月明雨晦之夕，泣酸风、悲冷露而已，又何能为乎？

    　　兰岩曰：凡人溺于所好，虽土穴几等华屋，村姬视同佳丽，又何必尽人入天台遇仙子哉？邱生闻莘女言，而勇于自新，得千金报不忘酬德，其享富厚、官清要也固宜。

    Explanation

    378

    The subject studied at the Imperial Academy and passed the imperial examinations the following year, securing multiple high-ranking positions in the government before resigning due to illness before the age of forty. He never married and raised his nephew as his own, educating his two younger brothers to become talented individuals. He entrusted the care of his personal grooming to a concubine and the management of daily household chores to an elderly woman. He spent his days devising strategies and enjoyed a life of comfort and ease. A man named Guan Qian, who was acquainted with him, shared a detailed account of his life with me one autumn evening, as we sat together late into the night.

    Xianzhai said: The Wang family, despite their wealth, were cruel and took lives lightly, leading to the soul of Shen's daughter wandering in the underworld. Her complaints and sorrows, though unable to be brought to the underworld for justice, are already pitiable. However, since ancient times, money can enslave ghosts and spirits, and wealth can influence the divine. How many people have been deceived by this? What difference does it make that it was Shen's daughter? Even if she had a spirit, she could only weep in the cold wind and rain during moonlit and rainy nights, unable to do much more.

    Lanyan said: People who are deeply engrossed in their passions, even if they live in a humble cottage, consider it as luxurious as a palace, and view a village girl as beautiful as a celestial being. Why should they all go to the heavens to meet fairy maidens? Upon hearing Shen's daughter's words, Qiu decided to reform himself and did not forget to repay the gratitude of a thousand gold pieces. It is fitting that he enjoyed a wealthy and prosperous life and held a prestigious position in the government.

    379

    陆　水　部周南溪先生，常述其亡友水部主事陆公荣，不谨于言，有罪。戍察哈尔时，单骑至归化城赁驼。有赵姓者，以二驼应，一乘，一载行李。既而曰：“君无仆从，与我三驼价，仆我可乎？”水部如其言，立券授银。将发，其一驼以马代，曰：“驼上下难，马便。”陆知其绐己，盖是时驼价四倍于马，自念彼贪利，吾贪路，驼、马奚择焉，遂行。行一日，赵曰：“一人难兼二役，牧与炊，君请择一。”陆领牧。又数日，称疾，陆牧且炊，赵坐食。适盆有宿餐，冷热半，赵掇热者去，曰：“我不惯冷食。”陆笑曰：“汝北人亦不惯耶？”乃取饭冷者自食之。行两月，食无肉，骂陆，佯不闻；骂甚，辱及所生，陆正色曰：“吾纵不才，曾忝朝籍，况年倍汝，奈何至是！”赵曰：“　！罢职即民耳。老去死来，蝼蚁引领入矣，尚以此傲我乎？”骂益甚。

    Explanation

    379

    Mr. Zhou Nanhxi, a Water Department official, often recounted the story of his deceased friend, Mr. Lu Rong, a Water Department Censor. Mr. Lu was careless in his speech and thus incurred guilt. When he was exiled to Chahar, he traveled alone on horseback to Huihua Cheng to rent camels. A man surnamed Zhao offered two camels, one for riding and one for carrying luggage. Later, Zhao said, "You have no servants. Give me the price for three camels, and I will serve you." Mr. Lu agreed and signed the contract, handing over the silver. Just before they were about to leave, one camel was replaced with a horse, and Zhao said, "Camels are difficult to mount and dismount; horses are more convenient." Mr. Lu realized that Zhao was deceiving him. At the time, camels were four times as expensive as horses. Reflecting on it, Mr. Lu thought that Zhao was greedy for profit, while he was greedy for the journey. What difference did it make between camels and horses? So, they set off. After one day of travel, Zhao said, "One person cannot handle two tasks. Choose between herding and cooking." Mr. Lu took charge of herding. A few days later, Zhao claimed to be ill, so Mr. Lu both herded and cooked while Zhao sat and ate. Just then, there was leftover food in a bowl, half cold and half hot. Zhao picked up the hot part and said, "I am not used to cold food." Mr. Lu laughed and said, "Even you from the north are not used to it?" He then took the cold part and ate it himself. After two months of travel, there was no meat to eat, and Zhao began to curse Mr. Lu, but Mr. Lu pretended not to hear. When Zhao cursed more vehemently, he even insulted Mr. Lu's family. Mr. Lu responded sternly, "Even if I am not talented, I once held an official position in the court. Moreover, I am much older than you. How could you treat me like this?" Zhao retorted, "Ha! Once dismissed, I am just an ordinary citizen. As I grow old and approach death, I am like a mouse or ant, led by the nose. Why should I still be proud of myself in front of you?" Zhao's cursing grew even more intense.

    380

    陆掩耳走至牧所，坐草中，雪纷纷下。追忆昔时，歌《鹿鸣》，登玉陛，在家妻孥相守，出门童仆相随；今破帽敝裘，昼行夜牧，掬蹄涔饮，拾马通炊，肤裂肌消，手龟足皲，又不幸为鼠子所窘辱。不禁涕泗交颐，仰天大恸，曰：“天乎！不意我陆公荣竟至此！”拔佩刀欲自刎，既又自念曰：“吾奉命从军，此非吾死所。”方忖念间，忽见一老翁，年约七旬，方袍古冠，扶筇而至，揖陆而进之曰：“伤哉！贵人失路若此。寒家去此数武，粗粝生刍，足以供给。”陆感之，而阴怪其衣冠不时，意颇逡巡。翁笑曰：“夫以穷发之北，得居停主人如老夫，亦幸甚矣，奈何以物外见疑？”陆释然，问姓名，自称黎公。

    Explanation

    380

    Lu covered his ears and hurried to the pasture, where he sat in the grass as the snow fell heavily. Recalling the past, he sang the "Deer Cry," ascended the jade steps, and remembered the days when his wife and children stayed at home and his servants and attendants followed him outside. Now, however, he wore a tattered hat and a worn fur coat, walked during the day and herded at night, scooped water from hoofprints to drink, and gathered horse feed to cook with. His skin was cracked, his muscles wasted, his hands cracked, and his feet chapped. To make matters worse, he was harassed and humiliated by rats. Unable to hold back his tears, which streamed down his cheeks, he looked up to the sky and wailed loudly, saying, "Heaven! I never thought I, Lord Lu, would come to this!" He drew his sword and attempted to take his own life, but then he thought to himself, "I was ordered to join the army; this is not where I should die." While pondering, he suddenly saw an old man, about seventy years old, wearing a long robe and an ancient-style cap, supported by a walking stick and approached him. The old man greeted Lu and said to him, "Alas! A noble person is lost like this. My humble home is just a few steps away, and we have coarse food and rough fodder that are enough to provide for you." Lu was moved by it, but secretly found his attire out of place and felt a bit hesitant. The old man smiled and said, "To have a shelter in the far north, to find a host like me, is also fortunate. Why doubt me as something otherworldly?" Lu felt relieved and asked his name, and he introduced himself as Lord Li.

    381

    相将行数里，越土山，得巨宅一区，缭粉砌，荫青松，雅洁清幽，迥殊塞外。入门，俊仆十余辈，传呼“太翁邀得陆主事来矣！”即有二少年，华服出迎，执礼恭谨。登堂，陆拜，翁答拜；少年拜，陆亦拜，翁掖之曰：“此吾家豚犬，君不当受其拜耶？”俄而列烛张筵，穷极水陆。酒再巡，陆请辞去，翁曰：“君尚欲听驼人之余骂乎？老夫虽俭陋，犹畜齐马数十匹，足以代君步，此时不必预计。”陆唯唯。翁曰：“老夫家本沈阳，流寓于此，几五十年矣。幸与老妻相守，生四子三女，长子青，入秦探亲未返；少子碧，方在襁褓；次子苍，三子白，所见二子是也。长女阿红，嫁于大同；次女阿黄，嫁于杭城；在阁者，三女阿紫而已。”顾谓二子曰：“入语阿　，可同阿紫出见客也。

    Explanation

    381

    Together they walked for several miles, crossing a hillock, and came upon a grand estate. The walls were whitewashed, and the grounds were shaded by green pines, creating an elegant, clean, and serene atmosphere, quite unlike the frontier beyond. Upon entering, they were greeted by more than a dozen handsome servants who announced, "Grandfather has invited the Magistrate Lu to come!" Soon after, two young men in fine attire emerged to welcome them, performing the ritual with great respect and courtesy. As Lu entered the hall, he bowed to the old man, who returned the bow; the young men also bowed, and Lu bowed back. The old man gently said, "These are my children, you shouldn't receive their bow." Soon, candles were lit, and a grand feast was prepared, featuring dishes from both land and sea. After the second round of drinks, Lu asked to leave. The old man said, "Do you still want to hear the camel driver's remaining insults? Despite my frugality, I still have dozens of horses from Qi that can serve as your carriage, so there's no need to worry about it now." Lu nodded in agreement. The old man continued, "My family originally came from Shenyang, but we have been living here as refugees for almost fifty years. Fortunately, I have been able to live with my old wife, and we have had four sons and three daughters. My eldest son Qing went to Shaanxi to visit relatives and hasn't returned yet; my youngest son Bi is still in his cradle; my second son Cang and third son Bai, these are the two sons I see here. My eldest daughter Ah Hong is married to Datong; my second daughter Ah Huang is married to Hangzhou; the one in the hall is my third daughter Ah Zi." He then said to his two sons, "Go tell Ah Zi, she can come out to see the guest."

    382

    ”陆辞不敢当，翁曰：“固是通家，无回避者。”二子趋入，良久将命而出曰：“母已设肴于室，谓堂上寒，请翁挽客入内，当亲奉杯酒，表意也。”翁笑曰：“有媪周折如此，君当以得贤内助贺我也。”急延入室。室中燃画烛，张锦屏，钩蒜垂帘，氍毹铺地，美婢数十人，拥媪而立，被服鲜花，年与翁埒。陆拜，媪答拜，翁曰：“何不见阿紫？”媪曰：“想羞容，不肯便来耳。”翁笑曰：“儿女态，每每如是，但使出嫁半年，亦便似其二姐，面皮如城堵墙矣。”一室皆笑，媪又使人往促之，移时，一双环婢，启帘报曰：“紫姐来矣。”随以目视陆，含笑而去。既而女至，粉黛云从，麝兰雾霈，年可二九，光艳绝伦，侧立筵前，俯首理袖。翁媪同声曰：“儿勿尔尔，陆君非外人也。”强女再拜，始各就座。酒炙并陈，笙箫聒耳。

    Explanation

    382

    Lu declined, saying he was unworthy, but the old man said, "We are indeed close family, there is no need for avoidance." Two young men hurriedly entered and after a long time, one was sent out to announce, "The mother has already prepared dishes in the room, because the hall is cold, please invite the guest inside, and I will personally serve a cup of wine, to show respect and hospitality." The old man smiled and said, "The old lady has gone through so much trouble, you should congratulate me on having a wise and capable wife." He quickly invited them into the room. The room was lit with painted candles, had brocade screens arranged, garlic hooks hanging from the curtains, and felt carpets laid on the ground. Dozens of beautiful maids gathered around the old lady, wearing fresh flowers and the same age as the old man. Lu bowed, and the old lady returned the bow. The old man asked, "Why don't you see Ah Zi?" The old lady replied, "She is probably blushing and doesn't want to come out immediately." The old man laughed and said, "Children's behavior is often like this, but after half a year of marriage, she will also be like her two elder sisters, her face will be as hard as a wall." Everyone in the room laughed. The old lady then sent someone to urge Ah Zi to come. After a while, a pair of ringed maids opened the curtain and reported, "Ah Zi is here." They looked at Lu with a smile and left. Soon, Ah Zi arrived, accompanied by clouds of rouge and powder, her fragrance filling the air like mist and perfume. She was about nineteen years old, her beauty unparalleled. She stood by the table, lowered her head, and adjusted her sleeves. The old man and old lady said together, "Don't be like that, Lu is not an outsider." They forced the girl to bow twice, and finally, everyone took their seats. Wine and roasted meat were served together, and the sound of flutes and pipes filled the ears.

    383

    夜将半，陆曰：“且休，今宵之会，诚所谓雅集也，盍赋诗以纪，安用此繁弦急管为？”翁曰：“善。”亟命撤乐，侍儿捧砚舒笺，濡毫授陆，陆被酒兴豪，赋七言近体一章，中有“碧血丹心迁客恨，云鬟玉臂故园情”之句，翁览之笑曰：“观君此诗，谓能忘情于小女，老夫不信也。”陆惶恐避席而谢曰：“鄙人讵有异心？聊以自感，故有是鸣，希翁谅之。”翁曰：“此亦数也。小女与君有夙份，遇非偶然，会须蠲吉，与友琴瑟耳。”是夕尽欢，陆□□两目，醉不能语。二子伴陆出宿斋中。

    Explanation

    383

    The night was halfway through, and Lu said, "Let us pause. This evening's gathering is indeed a true example of a refined assembly. Why not compose a poem to commemorate it? What need is there for such complex and excessive music?" The old man replied, "Good." He immediately ordered the music to be stopped, and the young servant girl brought out the inkstone and spread the paper. She wet the brush and handed it to Lu. Under the influence of alcohol, in a state of great enthusiasm, Lu composed a chapter of regulated verse in seven-character lines. Among the lines, there were "with a heart of loyal courage and a spirit of a wanderer's sorrow, and the beauty of her hair and arms evoking feelings for one's hometown." The old man smiled as he read it and said, "Seeing your poem, I cannot believe that you could be indifferent to my daughter. I do not believe it." Lu, filled with fear, stepped back from his seat and apologized, "How could I have any ulterior motives? I merely expressed my own feelings, so I composed this poem. I hope the old man will understand." The old man said, "This is also fate. My daughter has a predestined relationship with you. This meeting was not by chance. You must choose a favorable day to marry her, and become friends through the lute and zither." That night, they all enjoyed themselves fully, and Lu was so drunk that he could not speak. Two of his companions accompanied him to stay in the study.

    384

    翌日，陆请行，二子留鞭截镫以止之，陆无如之何。居数日，有翁之宅相胡秀才者，谒曰：“舅氏慕君名士，欲以少女奉箕帚，幸弗弃也。”陆辞谢曰：“西粤鄙人，身荷重罪，行年五十，落拓穷边，自活未遑，敢累及他人爱女乎？望致语令舅，善为我辞。”胡曰：“不然。吾相君而有死气，远期不过二年，舅氏得道有年，附之足以免祸。况表妹不恶，贞静幽娴。古人斗酒博梁州，君不破一文，成此奇缘，自受多福，否则孤立无偶，窃恐祸至时，欲求一人援手救，不可得也。”陆心动，因出玉蟾蜍一枚以聘，并以交桂二束，奉胡以为谢，曰：“感君进药石之言，故以药为报。”故拜纳而去。

    Explanation

    384

    The next day, Lu requested to depart, but two young men tried to stop him by holding back his reins and cutting his stirrup, yet Lu could not be stopped. After several days, there came a young man named Hu, the son of a prominent family, who paid respects and said, "My uncle admires your reputation as a scholar and wishes to offer his daughter to serve as your wife. Please do not reject him." Lu declined, saying, "I am a rustic from the West, bearing a great sin, and at the age of fifty, I live in poverty at the frontier, too preoccupied with my own survival to trouble another with my presence. I hope you will convey my message to my uncle and kindly decline on my behalf." Hu replied, "That's not true. I see that you have a lifeless appearance, and your lifespan will not exceed two years. My uncle has achieved enlightenment for many years, and by attaching yourself to him, you can avoid calamity. Especially since his niece is not unattractive, virtuous, calm, and refined. As the saying goes, 'Ancient people fought over a cup of wine for the whole province of Liangzhou.' You can achieve this extraordinary match without spending a single coin, and you will receive many blessings. Otherwise, you will remain alone, and I fear that when disaster strikes, you will find no one to help you." Lu was moved by these words and therefore offered a jade toad as a dowry, along with two bundles of cassia wood, to Hu as a token of gratitude, saying, "Grateful for your wise advice, I offer this medicine as a token of appreciation." Thus, he bowed and accepted the proposal before departing.

    385

    花烛之期将届之前二日，胡与黎氏二子，携酒来斋中，与陆小酌。半酣，复话及赘婿事，胡盛称阿紫淑美，陆意得甚，且中酒，乃大言曰：“若人之丰姿，予已于初到时审谛之矣。特笑老翁不学，命名阿紫。夫阿紫者，狐狸之称，淫妇之所化也，奈何取以名女？”言未毕，胡愕然失色，二子颈赤，拂袖而入。胡跣足曰：“君失言矣，予执柯之功，乃至此休矣！可惜，可惜！”陆茫然不解所谓。俄而翁与二子俱至，立帘下，以扶杖指陆曰：“何物书痴，轻薄至此！辜负老夫左顾，何足恨；所可恨者，必害得小女子数日不餐也。君自薄福，于我何尤。行矣！请从此决！”言讫，于袖中采白银一锭，掷地有声，去不复顾。胡亦太息而去。

    Explanation

    385

    Two days before the wedding day, Mr. Hu and his two sons brought wine to the study and had a small drink with Mr. Lu. After they were half-drunk, they began to talk about the matter of a son-in-law. Mr. Hu highly praised Mrs. Li's beauty and virtues. Mr. Lu felt pleased and, perhaps influenced by the wine, spoke grandly: "If the person's appearance is as beautiful as you say, I have already observed it when I first arrived. However, I find it amusing that the old man (likely Mr. Hu's father) is unlearned and named her 'Purple.' The term 'Purple' is the name of a fox, which is said to be a transformation of a seductive woman. How could such a name be given to a daughter-in-law?"

    Before Mr. Lu finished speaking, Mr. Hu was startled and pale, and his two sons turned red in the face. They angrily left the room, brushing their sleeves as they went. Mr. Hu, barefoot, said: "You spoke inappropriately. My efforts as a matchmaker have come to an end! What a pity, what a pity!" Mr. Lu was confused and did not understand what Mr. Hu meant.

    Soon, the old man and the two sons arrived at the door, standing behind the curtain. The old man, holding a walking stick, pointed at Mr. Lu and said: "What kind of bookish fool are you, to speak so lightly! It is a shame that you have disappointed me, but what I truly regret is that you have caused my daughter-in-law to go without food for several days. You lack good fortune, but what does that have to do with me? Leave now! Please leave from here!" Saying this, the old man took a silver ingot from his sleeve, threw it to the ground with a loud sound, and left without looking back. Mr. Hu also sighed and left.

    386

    陆深自愧悔，酒力尽消，隐几而卧。昧爽方觉，则身坐一大石旁，砂碛茫茫，无复第宅。始大惊异，取视白金，固朱提也。徘徊怅悒，泣下数行。赵已不知所之。重至牧所，驼马俱无。茕茕竟日，得遇周南溪先生，适乘一驼两马而来。乃订交于积雪之间，于路备详所遇。南溪决其为狐，且讶曰：“昨遇一人，哭于道周，询之，自称山西赵姓，有一驼一马，为暴客劫去，想即与公为仇者。”陆质其年貌，果赵也。天之报施于人，岂爽哉！相为太息者久之。至军营，陆金尽，以歧黄术，寄食于军中。然性愎而执，口不择言，竟坐讪谤伏法。南溪收其尸，葬之于火，始悟胡生谓其面有死气之说，诚不谬也。

    Explanation

    386

    Lu Shen felt ashamed and regretful, the effects of alcohol completely dissipated, and he leaned against the table to sleep. Just as dawn was breaking, he woke up to find himself sitting beside a large stone in an endless sandy desert, with no trace of his house or estate. He was greatly surprised and astonished. He picked up and examined the white metal, which was indeed pure gold. He wandered aimlessly, feeling melancholy and disheartened, tears streaming down his face. Zhao had disappeared without a trace. Returning to the place where he had been herding, he found that the camels and horses were all gone. He spent the whole day alone and desolate, until he met Mr. Zhou Nanshi, who happened to be riding a camel and two horses. They made friends amidst the snow, and along the way, Lu recounted all that had happened. Mr. Zhou Nanshi concluded that it was a fox and was surprised, saying, "I met someone yesterday, who was crying by the roadside. He introduced himself as Zhao from Shanxi, who had one camel and one horse, which were taken by robbers. I suspect they were the ones who were enemies of you." Lu verified his age and appearance, and it was indeed Zhao. Heaven's retribution for people, how can it be wrong? They sighed deeply for a long time. When he arrived at the military camp, Lu had spent all his money and relied on medical skills to support himself in the military camp. However, he was stubborn and inflexible, and spoke without considering the consequences, and ultimately was punished for slander and defamation. Mr. Zhou Nanshi collected his body and cremated it. He finally understood that the old man's statement about Lu's face having a deathly pallor was indeed correct.

    387

    闲斋曰：轻薄之口，尤见绝于异类，况与斯人为徒，可不凛三缄之戒哉！

    　　兰岩曰：落拓无依，致受辱厮役，斯矣困心衡虑矣。乃稍得意，遽尔轻薄，顿触所忌，见弃于狐；卒之坐谤伏法，身死异域，可哀也夫！

    　　冯勰华亭汪瑾，年五十余，潦倒都门，未离席帽，颇倦游。值秋风起，鲈鲙兴思，买舟南下。候放闸，泊武城故县之西。日薄暮，方苦岑寂，蓦见一小奚奴，汗走而至，投一刺曰：“家主人冯二官奉谒。”阅名纸，称乡眷晚生冯勰，素昧平生。自分老而贫，至亲良友，交臂且不相识，那复有强来亲近者？疑其错误，璧不受，奴曰：“老翁非松江汪姓耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“然则不错也。”遂驰去。

    Explanation

    387

    Translation:

    The Idle Studio said: "A frivolous and superficial mouth is particularly shunned by those who are different or unusual, let alone associating with such people. Should one not be vigilant and cautious, as if sealing one's mouth three times, to avoid such a fate?"

    The Green Rock said: "Wandering aimlessly and without support, leading to the humiliation of menial labor, one's mind is troubled and weighed with careful consideration. Yet, when one begins to feel a little successful, suddenly behaving in a frivolous manner, one provokes what is taboo and is abandoned by the foxes; ultimately, condemned due to slander and punished according to the law, dying in a foreign land—how pitiful!"

    Feng Xie, a man from Huating named Wang Jin, in his fifties, had fallen into poverty in the capital, still wearing his official cap, and was quite tired of wandering. As the autumn wind began to blow, thoughts of the fragrant fish dish arose, and he hired a boat to travel south. Waiting for the sluice gate to open, he anchored to the west of the ancient county of Wucheng. As the sun set and evening approached, he was beginning to feel lonely and desolate when suddenly a young servant appeared, sweating and hurrying up to him, handing over a business card: "My master, Mr. Feng, respectfully pays his respects." Upon reading the card, it was addressed to a fellow townsman and junior, Feng Xie, whom he had never met before. Considering himself old and poor, even close relatives and good friends would pass each other by without recognition, how much more could there be someone who forces themselves to get close? He doubted it was a mistake and refused to accept it. The servant said, "Isn't the old man surnamed Wang from Songjiang?" He replied, "Yes." The servant said, "Then it is correct." And with that, he hurried away.

    388

    俄而冯至，鲜衣新帽，年约三旬，揖让登舟，执礼甚谦谨，以潞癩四端为贺。自称：“山西人，将之扬州就一相识为上官桥巡检者。知兄归松江，愿附便舟，未知肯容纳否？”汪察其人朴厚，许之，冯拜谢。奴乃携襆被，委诸船舱。夜间相叙，汪曰：“兄西人，弟南人，何为称乡眷也？”冯曰：“祖贯松江，鼎革后，入籍汾阳，名纸称乡眷，不忘本也。”汪曰：“胡为不仕，负此壮年？”冯曰：“是有命焉，不可强致。行贿累万矣，终无成就。初甚抑郁，后遂释然。盖转念才如袜线，拆之无寸长。仕必尸位，如曰为贫而仕，则弟固富于资者。于彼于此，一无足取，故甘为布衣耳。

    Explanation

    388

    Soon after, Feng arrived, dressed in fresh clothes and a new hat, about thirty years old, bowing and taking his place on the boat, observing etiquette with great humility and respect, presenting four items of "Lü Lai" as a gift. He introduced himself: "I am from Shanxi, heading to Yangzhou to meet a friend who is the local inspector of Shangguan Bridge. I heard that my brother has returned to Songjiang, so I wish to join your boat. I wonder if you would be willing to accommodate me?" Wang, observing Feng's simple and honest demeanor, agreed, and Feng bowed in gratitude. The servant then carried his bedding and placed it in the boat's cabin. That night, they had a conversation, and Wang said, "You are from the west, and I am from the south. Why do you call yourself a fellow townsman?" Feng replied, "My ancestral home is in Songjiang. After the great change, I registered in Fenyang, and on my name card, I call myself a fellow townsman, not forgetting my roots." Wang asked, "Why do you not seek an official position, given your youth?" Feng answered, "It is fate, and one cannot force it. I have paid bribes amounting to thousands of units of currency, but ultimately achieved nothing. At first, I was very depressed, but later I became resigned. When I thought about my talents, they were like the thread of a sock, insignificant when unraveled. If one enters public service, one will be a mere figurehead. If one seeks an official position for the sake of wealth, then I am indeed wealthy. Whether in that case or in this, there is nothing to gain, so I am content to remain a commoner."

    389

    兄不见江东独步之王文席乎？苟守志不出，则弱冠重名，当终身不隳，何至倒置手板，贻诮后人？”汪叹曰：“兄言是也，贿且不官，况无金行赂如弟者，欲不弃掷，得乎？”冯曰：“贿赂行而无门可入，非世路之难，正皇朝景运之隆，英才用世之秋也。故行贿非难其人，受贿之难其人也；非行贿受贿之难其人，惟不受贿之难其人也。世之衰也，一变而为请托，更变而为贿赂，寒畯之士遗于野矣，厮役之贱升于朝矣。廉介者黜，贪墨者进矣。甚之，臣赂其君，崔烈博司徒矣；君贿甚臣，子明从封禅矣。习俗移人，贤者不免，下焉者又何冀乎？兄际盛世，而不见用，命也，与贫何尤焉？”汪深佩其言，牢愁顿减。自是朝夕晤对，相得益欢。

    Explanation

    389

    Have you not seen Wang Wenxi, the unparalleled figure in Jiangdong? If one remains steadfast in their resolve and does not seek office, then the reputation gained in youth will last a lifetime. How could one invert the handboard, leaving a laughingstock for future generations?" Wang sighed and said, "Brother, your words are true. If one cannot even receive a bribe, how much less so for someone like me who cannot even offer one. How can one avoid being discarded?" Feng said, "Even if bribery is practiced, there is no door to enter. It is not the difficulty of the path of the world, but the prosperous era of the imperial court, the time when talents are utilized in the world. Therefore, it is not difficult to offer a bribe, but it is difficult to find someone to receive it; it is not difficult to find someone to offer or receive a bribe, but it is difficult to find someone who does not accept bribes. As the world declines, it first becomes a matter of seeking favors, and then it becomes a matter of bribery. Talented scholars are left in the wilderness, while lowly servants rise to the court. The honest and incorruptible are demoted, while the corrupt and greedy are promoted. In extreme cases, ministers bribe their sovereigns, as seen in the case of Cui Lie becoming the Grand Tutor; in extreme cases, the sovereign bribes the minister, as seen in the case of Zi Ming participating in the ritual of fengshan. Customs can corrupt even the virtuous, and what hope is there for those of lower character? To live in a prosperous era but not be used, is fate, and what fault is there in being poor?" Wang deeply admired his words, and his worries were greatly reduced. From then on, they met every day, and they benefited from each other's company even more.

    390

    一日，舟次淮安，置仲秋之望，汪市酒邀冯赏月。酣饮间，冯忽把盏叹曰：“华亭鹤唳，可复闻乎？”汪不以为意，因问：“贵友官巡检，官况料必清苦，数千里就之，莫徒劳往返否？”冯不应，良久乃停杯，惨然曰：“旬日来，感兄遇我厚，屡欲以诚告，恐骇听闻，用兹隐忍。今承下询，实难默然。上官桥巡检陈某，虽朋友，实仇雠也。十三年前，弟贩布千捆之苏州，路经茌平，与陈同一逆旅。会大雨，留行，陈与同舍客呼卢一日夜，一败涂地，囊橐尽倾，尚负百余金，无可措置，大为同舍客所窘辱。弟怜之，如数代偿，事乃已。复以二十金赠其行，彼时陈感荷之言，报复之意，一若粉骨縻肌，亦所甚甘者。既而与弟谋，家有老亲，无以养，志欲援例捐一杂职，苦无囊可解，公仗义者，能假五百金任权子母，苟得缺，必不相负。弟方喜其能爱人以德，慨然诺之。彼时亦太鲁莽，竟不立券。

    Explanation

    390

    One day, the boat was moored in Huai'an, on the full moon of mid-autumn, and Wang bought wine to invite Feng to admire the moon. During their merry drinking, Feng suddenly raised his cup and sighed, saying, "Can I hear the cries of cranes in Hua town again?" Wang did not take it seriously and asked, "Your friend is an inspector, and his official life must be very hard. Traveling thousands of miles to see him, isn't it in vain?" Feng did not respond, but after a long pause, he put down his cup and said solemnly, "For the past ten days, I am grateful for your kind treatment of me. I have wanted to tell you the truth, but I was afraid it would shock you, so I held back. Now that you ask, I find it hard to remain silent. Inspector Chen of Shangguan Bridge, though we are friends, in reality, we are enemies. Thirteen years ago, I was trading thousands of bolts of cloth to Suzhou. On the way, I passed through Chiping and stayed at the same inn as Chen. It happened to rain heavily, so we stayed to wait out the rain. Chen and his fellow guests played dice for a whole day and night, completely ruined, all his money was spent, and he still owed over a hundred gold pieces, with no way to handle it. He was greatly humiliated by his fellow guests. I pitied him and paid off the debt on his behalf, settling the matter. I also gave him twenty gold pieces to help him on his journey. At that time, Chen expressed his gratitude, but his intentions of revenge were as if grinding bones and flesh into powder, he would still find it very sweet. Later, he talked to me, saying he had elderly parents who could not be supported and wanted to follow the example of donating a minor official position. He had no means to pay, so if you, a man of righteousness, could lend him five hundred gold pieces on interest, if he gets the position, he will definitely not betray you. I was happy that he could love me with virtue and agreed to it. At that time, he was too rash and didn't even make a contract.

    391

    越五年，予重入都门，闻其得缺扬州，尚未领凭，侨寓宣武门外。急往访之，辞以他出，再四往候，甫得一见。而相见又甚冷淡倨傲。”汪闻至此，不禁裂眦曰：“人心叵测至此乎？”冯曰：“非人心叵测也，乃吾辈心太实，口太真，以君子待小人。未闻有中山狼之事故也？”汪曰：“然，诚如兄言。时亦受此气懑久矣。此种人，弟宜索其所负，绝交而已矣。”冯曰：“弟之转念，讵不若是哉？乃问及欠项，不特不承，且出恶言。

    Explanation

    391

    Five years later, I returned to the capital city and heard that he had obtained a post in Yangzhou but had not yet received the official document. He was residing temporarily outside the Xuanwu Gate. I hurriedly went to visit him, but he excused himself with the reason of being elsewhere. I visited him repeatedly and finally got to see him. However, the meeting was very cold and arrogant. He said, "Can human hearts be so unpredictable?" I replied, "It is not that human hearts are unpredictable, it is because our hearts are too honest and our words too sincere, treating others as if they were gentlemen. I have never heard of the story of the wolf from Zhongshan." I said, "I have also been suffering from this frustration for a long time." He said, "For such people, you should demand what they owe and sever ties." I said, "Wouldn't it be like that?" When I asked about the debt, not only did he not admit it, but he also said bad things.

    392

    弟愤怒时，与之争论。所以然者，不恨失财，恨其人之负心太甚也。岂意其行如鬼蜮，毒甚蜂趸，买嘱坊正，执送官司，无券可伸，官不加察，遂致瘠死他乡，首邱莫正，讼之阴府，已计追偿。幸兄携之入场，得泄愤于彼，必报德于兄，结草衔环，敢忘异日！”汪闻之，悚然曰：“然则兄其鬼耶？”冯曰：“然，试于灯前月下验之，可知矣。”汪验之无影，大惧，对席枯坐，面色如灰。冯慰之曰：“兄勿怖，感戴且无既，岂为兄害者？”

    Explanation

    392

    When the younger brother was angry, he argued with him. The reason for this was not that he hated losing his wealth, but that he hated the person's betrayal too much. How could he have expected the person's actions to be like those of a ghost or demon, as venomous as a scorpion, bribing the local official to have someone arrested and taken to court, with no evidence to support the claim and the official not investigating further, leading to the person dying in poverty in a foreign land, their remains not properly returned to their homeland, and their soul going to the underworld to demand compensation. Fortunately, the ghost was brought into the examination hall, allowing it to vent its anger and promising to repay the favor, repaying the kindness in the future, possibly through acts of gratitude or service. Wang was startled and said, "Then, are you a ghost, brother?" Feng replied, "Yes, try to observe my shadow in the light of a lamp or moon, and you will know." Wang saw no shadow and became very afraid, sitting opposite the ghost, looking pale and scared. Feng comforted him, saying, "Brother, do not be afraid, I am grateful and will not cease, how could I harm you?"

    393

    良久，汪稍定，然兢兢与处，如背有芒。及抵扬，冯悯然曰：“从此别矣。虽然，吾闻为浮图者，必合其奸。知兄与太守有旧，明日希往过之，乘间一白弟冤，无使负心奴，盗清白名，以欺世人也。”言讫，再拜辞行，汪亦恻然送之，指小奚奴谓曰：“此小奚人亦鬼耶？”冯曰：“身且为鬼，安能役人，此亦于冥中以五千钱所买者，亦兄之乡里，南门外市袜人李四之子也。”既去，汪心中始安。汪性谨默，始终未泄于人，故舟人咸不知之。

    Explanation

    393

    After a long while, Wang gradually calmed down, but he remained cautious and careful in dealing with others, as if there were thorns on his back. When they arrived at Yang, Feng spoke in a compassionate manner, saying, "From now on, we must part ways. Although, I have heard that those who practice Buddhism must expose their evil deeds. Knowing that my brother has an old acquaintance with the prefect, I hope you will visit him tomorrow and take the opportunity to clear my brother's name. Do not let a treacherous slave steal my brother's good name and deceive the world." After speaking, he bowed twice and took his leave. Wang also sent him off with a feeling of pity. He asked, "Is this small servant also a ghost?" Feng replied, "If it is already a ghost, how can it serve people? It is also from my hometown, the son of Li Si, who sells socks outside the south gate." After Feng left, Wang's mind finally settled. Wang was a cautious and reserved person, and he never revealed this incident to anyone, so the boatmen all remained unaware of it.

    394

    翌日，谒太守，留饮，正款洽间，忽报上官桥陈巡检，于夜间暴疾死矣。太守愕然曰：“矍铄翁那得便亡？”汪叹曰：“幽冥之理，岂其妄哉！”为述所遇于太守，太守瞠目咋舌者久之。陈死无家可归，太守为具棺衾，瘗诸义冢，计其宦囊约千金，恨其人不良，倾囊赠汪，曰：“吾为冯勰报怨，以报德也。”汪初不受，以太守理直，乃受之，归而小康。询诸乡人，有识李四者，本回民，果有一子，年十五，于二年前，病　死矣。质其形貌，正与冯奴同。第不识冥中为何人所居以货之也。

    　　兰岩曰：负心人卒遭惨报，固无足惜。第冯生索命，托言访友，为官者，慎勿致此等打抽风人来也。

    　　戴　监　生沈阳戴监生懋德，入都乡试，不得志于有司，郁郁归去。道出永平，宿止荒戍古馆之厅。厅西一带，皆及肩土垣。垣外茅屋三间，户常扃锁。秋草满地，落叶堆阶，绕屋三四老槐，六七古冢。屋之西，则连山林，无人迹也。

    Explanation

    394

    The next day, Wang paid a visit to the prefect and stayed for a drink. During their friendly conversation, they suddenly received a report that the official from Shangguan Bridge had died suddenly from a severe illness at night. The prefect was startled and exclaimed, "How could such a vigorous old man die so quickly?" Wang sighed and said, "The principles of the afterlife, could they be false?" He recounted his experiences to the prefect, who stared wide-eyed and gasped in amazement for a long time. Since the deceased had no family to return to, the prefect provided a coffin and shroud and buried him in a public grave. He calculated that the deceased's official salary amounted to around a thousand gold pieces. Disliking the deceased's character, he gave away all his possessions to Wang, saying, "I am repaying the wrong done to Feng Xie, to repay your kindness." Wang initially refused, but since the prefect was acting justly, he accepted the gift and returned home, achieving a modest level of prosperity. He asked the villagers about a man named Li Si, who was a Hui Muslim and indeed had a son who was fifteen years old and had died of illness two years ago. Comparing the deceased's appearance, it matched that of Feng Xie's servant. However, it was not known who in the afterlife would live there and sell it.

    Lan Yan commented, "A heartless person ultimately suffers a cruel retribution, which is not worth pity. But Feng Xie seeking revenge by claiming to visit a friend, officials should be cautious and not allow such erratic people to come."

    Dai, a candidate for the imperial examinations from Shenyang, named Dai Maode, failed to achieve his goals in the exams and returned home dejectedly. On his way, he passed through Yongping and stayed in the hall of an ancient, dilapidated guardhouse. To the west of the hall was a row of earth walls that barely reached shoulder height. Outside the walls were three thatched huts, their doors often locked. The ground was covered with autumn grass, and leaves piled up on the steps. Around the huts were several old Chinese scholar trees and six or seven ancient graves. To the west of the huts lay a continuous stretch of mountains and forests, with no signs of human activity.

    395

    戴牢愁不寐，二更后，犹绕砌闲步。见月色满庭，山林清寂，微闻茅屋中有人语言，倚土垣听了，颇了了。似一老人咳且笑曰：“我岂不明此理者？但余发如此种种，心灰淡久矣。譬如鱼脱于罟，非不悠然深逝也，然不能忘情于饵，必将复上于钩；鸟脱于罗，非不翩然远翥也，然不能慎机于微，必将更罹于弋。子亏功不止一篑，乃不自树立，而复自倾踣之，恐日月蹉跎，臭皮囊不比金刚石也。”一少年声者，笑曰：“我发轫之始，便获美姝，兄弟姊妹，艳羡满室。至今且数年，两腓犹莹，况夫心肾？可见篯铿之术，非无裨补，真精既返黄金室，一颗明珠永不离。翁蹉跎失足，神祗精　，只可窥窃绳枢，绸缪嫫母；绮疏绣榻，非复翁侧足地。譬夫逆旅蜰虫，但夜出偷莈臭脚汉，乃转笑香闺豹脚，亲昵玉肌者为失计。

    Explanation

    395

    Wearing a lock of hair in sorrow, unable to sleep, after the second watch of the night, I still wandered aimlessly around the courtyard. I saw the moonlight fill the courtyard, the mountains and forests were quiet and secluded, and I faintly heard voices from a thatched cottage. Leaning against an earthen wall to listen, I could understand quite clearly. It seemed an old man coughed and laughed, saying, "Am I not one who understands this principle? But my hair is like this, in various ways, and my heart has long been faded and indifferent. Like a fish that has escaped the net, it is not that it does not swim away leisurely, but it cannot forget the bait, and it will inevitably be caught again on the hook; like a bird that has escaped the net, it is not that it does not fly away gracefully, but it cannot be cautious in the smallest details, and it will inevitably be caught again by the hunter. Your failure is not just a single step short, instead of establishing yourself, you have fallen and stumbled again, I fear that time will slip away, your mortal body is not as enduring as diamond." A young man's voice laughed, saying, "At the beginning of my journey, I immediately gained a beautiful woman, my siblings and sisters were envious. Until now, for several years, my calves are still smooth, let alone my heart and kidneys. It is clear that the methods of Peng Keng are not without benefit, the true essence has returned to the golden chamber, a single pearl will never leave. Grandfather, you have stumbled and wasted time, the spirits and essence, you can only peek at the rope hinges, tangle with the ugly woman; silken curtains and embroidered beds, no longer a place for you to stand. Like a worm in a temporary lodging, but it only comes out at night to steal from the smelly-footed man, instead, it laughs at the paws of the fragrant boudoir, those who are close to the jade-like skin are making a mistake."

    396

    非翁眼孔小，直是翁妒心重耳。况寿夭之数，自有分定。即金刚石，能无大小也？”老人揶揄之曰：“老夫年逾五十，讵意今日闻此奇谈，何其诙诡！夫乞丐小儿，宛转于百尺竿头，以为得计，自谓出人头地，初不知地下折臂叟，即是当时竿上儿。方叹天下险稤危途无有甚于此者，乃今子顾以此骄老夫耶？天能与人以寿夭之数，而不能禁人以撙节之方。设有两人于此，得青蚨一千，各分五百，数则同，而用必不同也。其一人一日一钱，或数日一钱，渐至不破一文，则此五百钱，虽终身不尽可也。其一人，初亦一日一钱，或一日四五钱，六七钱，渐至十百文，则此五百钱，其尽也可立而待也。子不明其理，反曰我生不有命在天，是何啻犵鸟獞花，知有炎日，而不知有玄水也，岂不令人绝倒！”久之不闻应答，惟频闻老人嗳声。

    Explanation

    396

    "It's not that the old man has a narrow view, but rather that his jealousy is heavy. Moreover, the predetermined fate of longevity or short life is already decided. Even a diamond, can it not have a fixed size?" The old man mockingly said, "I, an old man over fifty, never expected to hear such an unusual talk today, how absurd! The beggar child, precarious on a hundred-foot pole, thinks it's a good plan, and believes he stands out from others, not knowing that the broken-armed old man on the ground was once the child on the pole. I lament that there is no more dangerous path in the world than this, yet now you dare to boast to me about this? Heaven can give people a predetermined fate of longevity or short life, but cannot prevent people from practicing frugality. Suppose there are two people here, each getting a thousand coins, divided equally into five hundred each. The amount is the same, but the use will differ. One person spends one coin a day, or several days a coin, gradually until not spending even a single coin, then these five hundred coins, even if they last a lifetime, can be used up. Another person, initially spending one coin a day, or four or five coins a day, six or seven coins a day, gradually until spending tens or hundreds of coins, then these five hundred coins, their end can be awaited immediately. You don't understand this principle, yet you say that my life is not determined by heaven, how could it not be utterly ridiculous, like animals and plants that only know heat but not cold?" After a long time, there was no response, only frequent sighs from the old man.

    397

    戴欲归寝，忽又闻老人言曰：“是亦不必多辨，子第思秦州田大郎，亦当淫淫汗下矣。彼非不自命为冥灵也，大椿也，然由今观之，野马而羊角也。朝菌不知有晦朔，蟪蛄不知有春秋。五百钱五日用了，至骷髅，每每惨老夫之目，子宁未之见哉！”少年者哂曰：“翁言非不了了，待童乌预玄自有方也，岂可与小儿强作解事者同日语！予黄庭之榛莽，已粪除久矣，丹田之稂莠，亦除治尽矣。宏于中者肆于外，犹韫明珠于水晶之椟，无事炫露，表里皆莹，非如　腐之石，不任切劘者。

    Explanation

    397

    戴想要回去睡觉，忽然又听到老人说：“这也不必多辩，你只要想想秦州的田大郎，他也应当是汗流浃背了。他不是不自称为冥灵，而是像大椿一样，但从今天看来，却像是野马长着羊角。朝菌不知道有新月和满月，蟪蛄不知道有春秋。五天内用完了五百钱，直到骷髅，常常让老夫感到悲伤，你难道没有见过吗！”年轻人笑着说：“老先生的话并非不清楚，等待童子乌预玄自有方法，怎能与那些假装懂事的小孩同日而语！我心中的荒野已经清理很久了，丹田中的杂草也已彻底清除。内心宽广的人在外表上也会表现出来，就像珍珠藏在水晶盒中，无需炫耀，内外都纯净，不像那腐烂的石头，无法承受磨砺。”

    398

    ”老人曰：“然则遂无　腐时乎？”少年曰：“玉晶何腐之有？”老人叹息曰：“见卵而求时夜，见弹而求鹗炙，痴绝妄绝矣！何异荒祠木居士，不虑风雨，而梁柱蠹生，庇荫我者，即所以摧毁我者也，岂必外来之风雨哉？且斧斤不施，樗散之所以寿也；文采章身，凤凰之所以隐也。子谋隶仙籍，而先窥鬼箓，吾恐子平之愿难毕，而贾谊之　易来，铁铸一个错不成，恓惶甚矣。即如馆中戴监生，本非科甲中人，尚瞆瞆忧思，自加戕贼，正堪与子齐彭殇，未可与老夫较修短也。

    Explanation

    398

    The Elder said, "Then is there no time for decay?" The Young Man replied, "How can jade crystal decay?" The Elder sighed, "To seek the nightingale's song upon seeing an egg, or the roasted owl upon seeing a bow, is utterly foolish and utterly deluded! How different is this from the desolate temple where a wooden scholar is worshipped, not considering the wind and rain, but the beams and columns are infested with worms, what shelters me is also what destroys me, why must it be the wind and rain from outside? Moreover, without the axe being used, the cypress will live long; adorned with fine feathers, the phoenix hides. You plan to join the ranks of immortals, but first look into the records of the dead, I fear your wish for peace will be hard to fulfill, and the early death of Jia Yi will come easily, a mistake cast in iron cannot be undone, you are extremely anxious and distressed. Just like the supervisor named Dai in the hall, who is not a scholar from the imperial examinations, he is still blind with worry and self-destructive, you can equate him with me in terms of life and death, but cannot be compared with me in terms of longevity."

    [见卵而求时夜，见弹而求鹗炙] [痴绝妄绝] [荒祠木居士] [不虑风雨，而梁柱蠹生] [庇荫我者，即所以摧毁我者也] [斧斤不施，樗散之所以寿也] [文采章身，凤凰之所以隐也] [谋隶仙籍，而先窥鬼箓] [贾谊之易来] [铁铸一个错不成] [恓惶甚矣] [齐彭殇] [不可与老夫较修短也]

    399

    ”戴骤聆至此，毛发悚然，惊疑间，又闻少年曰：“休休！我闻太上忘情，最下者不及情。情之所钟，正在我辈。我所以敢锐身任者，诚有所恃而不恐也。翁以钩距来，我但以坦率往，毋啁礰也。”老人遂发怒声曰：“稚子何敢牴牾先辈！汝牂羝不辨，香臭不分，有何尴尬？其恃汝母为护符耶？彼老魅无耻，幻魄媚人，今见弃于同侪。贫类丑鬼，无雷行其诛之，何足附会！汝忘二十年前，跪老夫膝下吮靴鼻、牵衣襟，苦求采药之术，汝母亦跪进履二　、松子一拌，老夫一一授之，奈何饥附饱飏，强项乃尔！”少年便给尤甚，言不少让，寻闻诟谇纷然，渐出户外。

    Explanation

    399

    Wearing hurriedly to the scene, he felt his hair stand on end, filled with shock and doubt. Then he heard the young man say, "Enough! I have heard that the highest level of detachment from emotions is to forget them entirely, while the lowest level of people do not even reach the level of emotion. Where emotion is most concentrated, it is among us. I dare to take on this responsibility boldly because I truly have something to rely on and thus am not afraid. You come with probing techniques, but I will respond with honesty and openness. Do not mock me!" The old man then shouted in anger, "How dare you contradict your elders! You cannot distinguish between right and wrong, good and bad, what is the matter with you? Do you rely on your mother as a protective charm? That old demon is shameless, charming people with false appearances, and now she has been abandoned by her peers. A poor and ugly ghost, there is no need to punish her, why should you align with her! Do you forget twenty years ago, when you knelt before me, sucking the nose of my boots and pulling at my sleeves,苦苦请求采药之术，汝母亦跪进履二、松子一拌，老夫一一授之，奈何饥附饱飏，强项乃尔！”少年便给尤甚，言不少让，寻闻诟谇纷然，渐出户外。

    Translation:

    Wearing hurriedly to the scene, he felt his hair stand on end, filled with shock and doubt. Then he heard the young man say, "Enough! I have heard that the highest level of detachment from emotions is to forget them entirely, while the lowest level of people do not even reach the level of emotion. Where emotion is most concentrated, it is among us. I dare to take on this responsibility boldly because I truly have something to rely on and thus am not afraid. You come with probing techniques, but I will respond with honesty and openness. Do not mock me!" The old man then shouted in anger, "How dare you contradict your elders! You cannot distinguish between right and wrong, good and bad, what is the matter with you? Do you rely on your mother as a protective charm? That old demon is shameless, charming people with false appearances, and now she has been abandoned by her peers. A poor and ugly ghost, there is no need to punish her, why should you align with her! Do you forget twenty years ago, when you knelt before me, sucking the nose of my boots and pulling at my sleeves,苦苦请求采药之术，汝母亦跪进履二、松子一拌，老夫一一授之，奈何饥附饱飏，强项乃尔！”少年便给尤甚，言不少让，寻闻诟谇纷然，渐出户外。

    Translation:

    Wearing hurriedly to the scene, he felt his hair stand on end, filled with shock and doubt. Then he heard the young man say, "Enough! I have heard that the highest level of detachment from emotions is to forget them entirely, while the lowest level of people do not even reach the level of emotion. Where emotion is most concentrated, it is among us. I dare to take on this responsibility boldly because I truly have something to rely on and thus am not afraid. You come with probing techniques, but I will respond with honesty and openness. Do not mock me!" The old man then shouted in anger, "How dare you contradict your elders! You cannot distinguish between right and wrong, good and bad, what is the matter with you? Do you rely on your mother as a protective charm? That old demon is shameless, charming people with false appearances, and now she has been abandoned by her peers. A poor and ugly ghost, there is no need to punish her, why should you align with her! Do you forget twenty years ago, when you knelt before me, sucking the nose of my boots and pulling at my sleeves,苦苦请求采药之术，汝母亦跪进履二、松子一拌，老夫一一授之，奈何饥附饱飏，强项乃尔！”少年便给尤甚，言不少让，寻闻诟谇纷然，渐出户外。

    Translation:

    Wearing hurriedly to the scene, he felt his hair stand on end, filled with shock and doubt. Then he heard the young man say, "Enough! I have heard that the highest level of detachment from emotions is to forget them entirely, while the lowest level of people do not even reach the level of emotion. Where emotion is most concentrated, it is among us. I dare to take on this responsibility boldly because I truly have something to rely on and thus am not afraid. You come with probing techniques, but I will respond with honesty and openness. Do not mock me!" The old man then shouted in anger, "How dare you contradict your elders

    400

    月明如水，见颇分明：一老人伛偻侏儒，扭结一少年，稚齿韶颜，容色如玉，于树下殴甚苦。戴知其非人，扪捉墙头半砖，极力飞击，扑地一声，适中二人交足处，同仆于地，并化为狐，见人，窜入屋后古墓中。戴亦就寝。次日，白诸馆吏，同往发冢，有黑狐十余头，奔逸而出，逐之不及。后戴再试不第，忆狐言，投笔经商，致富十万，遂不复求仕进云。

    Explanation

    400

    The moonlight was as clear as water, and everything was seen quite distinctly: an old man, hunched over and of short stature, was twisting and binding a young man with fresh teeth and a beautiful, jade-like complexion. The young man was being beaten severely under a tree. Dai knew they were not human, so he picked up half a brick from the wall and struck with all his might. There was a loud thud when the brick hit the ground, and it struck the spot where their feet were entangled. Both fell to the ground together and transformed into foxes. Seeing people, they fled into the ancient tomb behind the house. Dai also went to bed. The next day, he reported the incident to the officials in charge of the inn, and they went together to open the tomb. There were more than ten black foxes, which fled out in a rush, and they could not catch them. Later, Dai failed the imperial examinations again. Remembering the fox's words, he put down his pen and went into business, becoming wealthy with ten thousand taels of silver, and thus no longer sought official positions.

    401

    兰岩曰：时运不齐，频居康了。命途多舛，永落孙山。自负文章而遭沦落者如戴生，可胜计哉！

    　　佟　諩　角旗人子傅九者，年二十，以事出正阳门。过一巷，路狭人众，相俟以行。蓦一人迎面来，急走如飞，其势甚猛。傅方仓卒却避，其人已至，两胸相撞，竟与己合而为一，顿觉身如水淋，寒噤不止。急投一缎店下，闭目蹲身歇之，良久，头愈痛，眼愈眩，茫茫然，雇车驰归。夜二更后，忽跃起大言曰：“我因一时赶路不及，正在仓遽，奈何拦我去路？致误大事，我与汝势不两立矣！”于是批颊撞头，自残不顾。家人环守通宵，抢攘不休。

    401

    Lan Yan said: Fortune is uneven, and one often lives in peace. One's destiny is full of twists and turns, always falling short. Those who, like Dai Sheng, have talent in writing but are left in obscurity, how many could be counted?

    Tong Yan, the son of a Jiaoqi person, named Fujiu, was twenty years old. He went out through Zhengyang Gate for some matter. Passing through a narrow alley, the road was crowded with people, and they waited to pass. Suddenly, a man came face-to-face, running as fast as flying, with great momentum. Fujiu was about to hastily avoid him, but the man had already arrived, and their chests collided, merging into one. He suddenly felt as if he were drenched in water, shivering uncontrollably. He rushed into a silk shop, closed his eyes, and crouched down to rest. After a while, his head hurt more, his eyes became more dizzy, and he felt disoriented. He hired a carriage to rush back home. After midnight, he suddenly leaped up and shouted, "I was in a hurry to get somewhere, and just as I was rushing, why did you block my way? You caused a major mistake, and I will not rest until I settle this with you!" He then slapped his cheeks and hit his head, self-harming without regard. His family guarded him all night, and he remained restless and agitated.

    402

    邻人或言某胡同所居，有巫而走无常者，号佟觭角，最能祛除不祥，盍使治之。家人亦夙耳其名，亟往祈请。佟未至，傅已知之，哂且骂曰：“无论铜觭角，铁觭角，又何为哉？”俄而佟至，男女观者如堵墙。佟瞠目视之曰：“何处鬼魅，敢来此处祟人，不实供，即叉汝下油锅矣！”傅瞠目不言，但吱吱切齿不已。佟大怒，命倾油于巨镬中，烧柴煎之。油沸，旋捉一钢叉，向傅面上旋绕，故振响其环以恐吓之。

    Explanation

    402

    A neighbor mentioned that in a certain lane lived a witch who served as a messenger for the netherworld, known as Tong Jijiao, who was most skilled at dispelling misfortune. The family had heard of his name before and hurriedly went to pray and request his help. Tong had not yet arrived, but Fu already knew about it, and he smiled and scolded, saying, "Whether it's copper Jijiao or iron Jijiao, what difference does it make?" Soon, Tong arrived, and men and women watching were like a wall. Tong stared at Fu and said, "Where are the ghosts or evil spirits that dare to come here to harm people? If you don't confess honestly, I'll throw you into the oil pot!" Fu stared back without speaking, but gritted his teeth continuously, making a sound. Tong became greatly angry and ordered the oil to be poured into a large pot, burning wood to heat it. The oil boiled, and he quickly grabbed a steel fork, swinging it around Fu's face, intentionally shaking the ring on the fork to scare him.

    403

    复叱曰：“不速供，则烹矣！”傅哆口长号曰：“嗟乎，冤哉烹也！”佟曰：“无故祟人，罪固当烹，何冤之有？”傅倚壁战栗，计甚恐怖。佟复振叉作欲刺之势，喝令速供。傅肘膝投地求免。于是自供：“本凤阳府人，于某年入京，因迫于饥寒，窃发人之冢，为人所觉，群求擒捉，一时慞惶，用铁锹拒捕，希冀免脱。不意连伤二人，坐法当斩，今日大决，绑赴菜市，已临刑矣。因极力挣扎，得脱身而走，方将逃避他所，讵意为此人拦阻，心实忿恨，故与之较量。既老爷见责，焉敢迟留，第乞秘密，小人他去。”佟曰：“然则速去，勿触我怒。”乃倚叉而坐。观者莫不骇异。

    Explanation

    403

    The authority figure shouted again, "If you don't quickly provide, you will be cooked!" Fang opened his mouth and cried out, "Alas, it's unjust to be cooked!" The authority figure replied, "Without cause to harm others, the crime is indeed deserving of being cooked, why is there any injustice?" Fang leaned against the wall, trembling with fear, and the situation was extremely terrifying. The authority figure again raised the fork, making a threatening gesture to stab, and shouted to quickly provide. Fang fell to the ground on his elbow and knee, begging for mercy. He then confessed, "Originally from Fengyang Prefecture, I entered the capital in a certain year. Due to hunger and cold, I secretly opened a tomb. Discovered by others, many sought to capture me. At once panicked, I used a shovel to resist capture, hoping to escape. Unexpectedly, I injured two people. Convicted by law, I should be executed. Today, the great execution, bound to be taken to the marketplace, already facing the death penalty. Due to vigorous struggle, I managed to escape and run away, just about to escape to another place, unexpectedly blocked by this person. Heart truly filled with anger and resentment, therefore, engaged in a contest with him. Since the master has rebuked, how dare I linger, only ask for secrecy, the humble one will go elsewhere." The authority figure said, "Then quickly leave, do not provoke my anger." He then leaned on the fork and sat down. The observers were all shocked and amazed.

    404

    傅跪坐地，挥涕不止。乃叱曰：“胡不去而泣，必求烹也？”傅哭曰：“小人在狱中时，因天寒，两脚漻 皴瘃，步履甚艰，欲毡袜一双，则感德无量。”佟笑曰：“甫得宽宥，则有所求，一袜所值几何，不吝与汝。”亟命傅之家人，取白纸糊作袜形，每只画一符，书一毡字，焚之，傅即欣然伏地，叩头，即伸足作更易状，观者皆笑。佟因诰其姓名年岁，今去此将安之乎？傅曰：“姓名某某，年若干，今得脱大刑，当奔川滇远省，以避搜捕耳。”佟曰：“汝计左矣。此去川滇数千万里，岂旦夕可至者？倘为逻役所获，重罹网罟，再思侥幸，讵可得乎？不如从吾教，尚可得一啖饭处。”傅曰：“苟得老爷垂怜收纳，必报大德。”佟亦喜，乃探囊出一黄纸小会，焚之，傅遂仆地不动，良久始苏，问之茫然，惟忆致病以先之事耳。家人罗拜，厚赠谢焉。

    Explanation

    404

    Fu knelt and sat on the ground, continuously wiping his tears. Then he shouted, "Why don't you leave and cry? Are you seeking punishment?" Fu wept, "While I was in prison, due to the cold, my feet were chapped and cracked, making walking very difficult. If I could get a pair of felt socks, I would feel immense gratitude." Tong laughed and said, "Just having been pardoned, you have something to ask for. How much is one pair of socks worth? I will not spare for you." He immediately commanded Fu's family to take white paper and paste it into the shape of socks, drawing a talisman and writing the word "felt" on each one, then burning them. Fu then joyfully prostrated himself, bowed, and extended his feet as if to change them, causing all the spectators to laugh. Tong then inquired about his name, age, and background, asking, "Where will you go now?" Fu replied, "My name is [name], I am [age] years old. Now that I have escaped severe punishment, I will flee to the distant provinces of Sichuan and Yunnan to avoid being captured." Tong said, "Your plan is wrong. How can you reach Sichuan and Yunnan in a short time? If you are caught by the patrol officers, you will suffer again from the net. Thinking again about taking a chance, can you achieve it? It is better to follow my advice, and you can still find a place to eat." Fu said, "If you are mercifully accepted, I will certainly repay your great kindness." Tong was also pleased and took out a small yellow paper from his pocket, burned it, and Fu fell to the ground and did not move. After a long time, he regained consciousness, and when asked, he was bewildered, only remembering things before getting sick. His family knelt and bowed, giving generous gifts as thanks.

    405

    是日刑部大决，密访果有其人者，已枭示矣。闻者咸叹异之，愈神佟术。佟年五十余，平日孓然独处，持斋诵佛，寡言喜睡，往往睡三四日不起。至其家者，重门以内，无寸芥纤埃。一切箱匮几案，不见拂试而光洁可鉴，或言其有禁鬼之术，或三年一更代，凡所服役者，悉鬼也。

    Explanation

    405

    On that day, the Ministry of Justice carried out a major execution. After a secret investigation, it was found that there was indeed such a person, and he had already been executed and displayed. Those who heard about it all marvelled at it, and the mystery surrounding Tong's abilities became even more profound. Tong was in his fifties, and in his daily life, he lived alone, maintaining a vegetarian diet and reciting Buddhist scriptures. He spoke little and enjoyed sleeping, often sleeping for three or four days without waking up. Within the inner doors of his home, there was not a speck of dust. All boxes, cabinets, tables, and desks were clean and polished without being wiped. Some said he had the ability to banish ghosts, while others claimed that he changed his servants every three years, and all those who served him were ghosts.

    406

    兰岩曰：观此而哀小民之愚也，饥寒所迫，则相率而为非，朋党所要，每横行而不顾。一旦自罹法网，幸脱无由；既已枭示通衢，犹矜奔避。真乃醉生梦死，谁能唤醒其良心？为鬼为人，尚未辨别其形似者也。为民父母者，尚其念氓蚩之可悯，勿以罔民而可为，思人性之皆良，勿致不教而遂杀，则被泽者，可胜计哉！

    　　谭九京都花户子谭九，奉父母命，探亲于烟郊。策卫出门，日已向夕，道遇一媪，衣悬鹑，而跨白颠马，鞍辔华美，左右相追随，问：“小郎何往？”谭以所之告，媪曰：“此去烟郊尚数十里，路多积潦，颇不易行，小郎不闻乎？风度蒲牢，都城漏下矣。荒野寂寥，保无有暴客相值？茅舍在迩，盍留一宿，翌日早行，得从容也。”谭正恇怯，闻言深荷其谊。媪策马先导。循僻径二里许，隐隐见林际灯光，媪以鞭指示曰：“至矣。”纵辔即之，即矮屋两椽，土垣及肩。媪弃骑启扃，延客入室。

    Explanation

    406

    Translation

    Lan Yan said: Observing this, one grieves for the foolishness of the common people. When driven by hunger and cold, they follow one another into wrongdoing, pressured by factions, and act recklessly without regard. Once they fall into the legal net, they have no way to escape; even when displayed on the main street, they still boast of fleeing. Truly, they are living in a drunken, dreamlike state. Who can awaken their conscience? Whether as ghosts or humans, they have not yet distinguished their true nature. Those who are parents to the people should remember that the common folk are pitiable, do not consider deceiving the people as acceptable, consider that human nature is inherently good, and do not allow them to be killed without proper education. Then those who benefit from this can be counted in the millions.

    Tian Jiu, a son of a household in the capital, was sent by his parents to visit relatives in Yanjiao. He set out with his guard, and by evening, he encountered an old woman on the road. She wore a tattered robe and rode a white mare with a splendid saddle and bridle, followed by others. She asked, "Where are you going, young master?" Tian Jiu told her, and the old woman said, "It is still dozens of miles to Yanjiao, and the road is full of muddy water, making it quite difficult to travel. Haven't you heard? The wind is blowing, and the city gates are already closing. The wilderness is desolate, and there might be bandits lurking. There is a humble cottage nearby; why not stay for the night and leave early tomorrow, to travel more comfortably?" Tian Jiu was already fearful, and upon hearing her words, he deeply appreciated her kindness. The old woman spurred her horse ahead. Following a secluded path for about two miles, they faintly saw lights at the edge of the forest. The old woman pointed with her whip and said, "We have arrived." She let down the reins and approached a low, two-room cottage with a wall barely reaching shoulder height. The old woman dismounted and opened the door, inviting the guest inside.

    407

    室中空无所有，惟篝灯悬壁，一少妇卧炕头哺儿，媪呼曰：“有客来，媳妇可速起。”妇徐徐起，掠鬓，儿呱呱啼，媪探袖出胡饼一枚，付之，啼始止。谭视妇年可二十，泪睫惨黛，殊少欢容，媪曰：“汝起烧茶，老身送马便回。”言讫，出户牵马去。妇折穄引火于灯，着红布短袄，绿布裤，蓝布短袜，趿高底破红鞋，皆敝甚，露一肘一腓并两踵焉。谭年少口讷，不能致诘，但阴怜之。俄而媪还曰：“为还代步，致郎寂坐，渠宅上闻有客至，亦欲延款，老身辞以太晚，嘱为致意。”谭唯唯，媪曰：“奔驰半日，想客亦苦饥矣。媳妇备饭来，老身且出喂驴。”谭曰：“相扰何安，刍豆之费，临行当厚偿。”媪摇手曰：“莫漫作客套语，所值几何哉？”既而饲驴已。

    Explanation

    407

    The room was empty of all things, with only a lamp hanging on the wall. A young woman lay on the kang (heating platform) feeding her child. An elderly woman called out, "There's a guest here; daughter-in-law, please get up quickly." The woman slowly got up, combed her hair, and the baby cried loudly. The elderly woman reached into her sleeve and took out a round flatbread, giving it to the baby, whose cries then ceased. Tan observed that the woman was about twenty years old, her eyelashes wet and her eyebrows sad, lacking a joyful expression. The elderly woman said, "You go and boil some tea; I'll return after I've sent the horse back." After saying this, she went out and led the horse away. The woman broke some millet and used it to light the lamp, then put on a red cotton jacket, green cotton pants, blue cotton socks, and dragged along a pair of high-heeled, red, worn-out shoes. All of her clothes were very worn out, revealing one elbow, one calf, and both heels. Tan, young and tongue-tied, could not ask questions but secretly felt sympathy for her. Soon, the elderly woman returned and said, "I'm returning the horse; it made the guest sit alone. Her house heard that a guest had arrived and wanted to entertain, but I declined due to it being too late and told you to convey greetings." Tan nodded in agreement. The elderly woman said, "You've traveled for half a day; I imagine the guest must be very hungry by now. The daughter-in-law will prepare the meal; I will go out to feed the donkey." Tan said, "How can I trouble you? The cost of hay and beans, I will pay generously when I leave." The elderly woman waved her hand and said, "Don't just speak in formalities; how much is it worth anyway?" After she had finished feeding the donkey, she left.

    408

    妇陈列酒肴，瓦器绝粗，折稊为箸，以盆代壶，而肴皆鱼肉。但冷不中啖。媪移灯劝谭饮，谭辞不能酹，乃进饭，饭又冰冷，勉进一盛。妇敛具去，相与坐话，妇就灯为儿捉虱。谭曰：“听姥言似非京师人，娘子则又旗妆，敢问邦旗？”媪曰：“诚如郎说，身本凤阳侯氏，因岁荒流离入京，为人缝纫补辍，谋衣食。再醮此间村民郝四，近三十年，今成翁矣。生一女一子。女已适人，子为圬者，居城中。翁以衰耄，佣于野肆中，为人提壶涤器，小郎明日当过其处，见鸡皮白髭，耳后有瘤如卵大者，即是也。媳妇余氏，实宅上婢子，其主人为巴参领，久退闲，幼主袭职矣；适借马处也。”谭曰：“视姥家亦甚清苦，何苦盛设待客？”媪笑曰：“仓卒客值茅舍主人，岂能咄嗟办此肴膳？亦缘中元节例，分得宅上余，方愧亵渎，敢云盛设！”

    408

    The woman set out the wine and dishes, using extremely coarse clay vessels, breaking reeds to make chopsticks, and substituting bowls for pots, while the dishes were all fish and meat. But they were cold and not suitable for eating. The old woman moved the lamp to encourage Tan to drink, but Tan refused to pour alcohol as an offering. She then served rice, which was also icy cold, and Tan forced himself to eat a full bowl. The woman gathered up the utensils and left, and they sat and talked together. The woman picked lice from her child by the light of the lamp. Tan said, "From what your mother said, it seems you are not from the capital city, and the lady is dressed in military attire. May I ask which region you come from?" The old woman replied, "Indeed, as you say. I was originally from the noble family of Fengyang, and due to a famine, I wandered into the capital, where I worked as a seamstress, mending and sewing to make a living. I remarried a villager named Hao Si here, and it has been nearly thirty years since then. Now he is an old man. We have one daughter and one son. The daughter is married, and the son is a plasterer, living in the city. The old man, being old and weak, works in a rural tavern, carrying pots and washing dishes. My son will pass by there tomorrow; he has a chicken-skin and white beard, and a lump behind his ear as large as an egg, and that is him. My daughter-in-law, Yu, is actually a servant in the household. Her master was a military leader named Ba, who has retired for a long time, and his young master has taken over his position; that is where the horse was borrowed." Tan said, "Your family seems very poor, why go to such trouble to entertain guests?" The old woman laughed and said, "A guest arrives suddenly at a humble thatched cottage, how could we quickly prepare such a feast? It is also because of the Ghost Festival custom, where we receive leftovers from the household, and we feel ashamed to be so impertinent, how could we say it is a grand feast!"

    409

    谭坐久颇倦，又不便偃息，乃出具就灯吸烟。妇频睃，有欲烟之色，媪察知其意，亟拊掌曰：“媳妇垂诞吃烟矣。小郎肯见赐否？”谭以烟囊付之，媪曰：“近以窘迫，不有此物已半年矣。那得有烟具？”谭乃并具奉之，妇吸之甚适，眉颦顿舒。媪视之，点首曰：“老身在世六十余年，不识此味。诚不解嗜痂者，何故好之如此。”谭曰：“亦自不解，第不会则已，学会则一刻不能离，宁可食无饭，不可吸无烟也。”媪大笑，谭曰：“娘子嗜此，予迟日当市具与烟来，作野人芹敬。”媪颔之。

    Explanation

    409

    The man had been sitting for a long time and felt quite tired, and it was also inconvenient for him to lie down. He then took out a cigarette and lit it by the lamp. The woman kept glancing at him, showing a desire for a cigarette. The grandmother noticed her intention and clapped her hands excitedly, saying, "The daughter-in-law is drooling over smoking. Would the young master be so kind as to give it to her?" The man handed over his cigarette pouch, and the grandmother said, "Recently, I've been in a difficult situation and haven't had this thing for half a year. How can I have a smoking device?" The man then gave her both the device and tobacco. The woman smoked it very comfortably, and her frown suddenly relaxed. The grandmother looked at her and nodded, saying, "I have been in this world for over sixty years and do not know this taste. Truly, I don't understand why those who like scabs enjoy it so much." The man replied, "I also do not understand myself. If one does not understand, it is fine; once learned, one cannot be away for a moment. Rather than eat no rice, one cannot smoke without tobacco." The grandmother laughed, and the man said, "The lady likes this. On a slow day, I will buy the device and tobacco and come, as a humble offering from a rustic." The grandmother nodded in agreement.

    410

    谭出溲，见银河西耿，斜月在林，约略四更。媪扬声于室曰：“客不时欠伸，当使寝息。”谭应曰：“尚可稍坐。”媪曰：“勿太勉强，明日尚有路行，更有所恳，望留意。”谭问何事，媪惘然曰：“明日过市，苟见我家老翁，烦为致声，促其急送数钱来，但言家中吃着都尽矣。”谭曰：“无不尽心。”媪又赧然曰：“以贫故，并无被襆，一夜屈郎甚矣。”谭曰：“假一席地，得一夕安，已承厚贶，敢过望耶？”因各就枕。谭疲极，着枕便熟睡。既而梦回，觉草虫鸣于耳畔，荧火耀于目前，矍然惊起，则身卧松柏间，秋露湿衣，清寒砭骨，系驴树根上，龁草不休。茅舍乌有，媪与妇并失所在，但见古冢颓然，半倾于蒿莱枳棘之中而已，不禁毛发森竖，急捉驴乘之，得得而驱。

    Explanation

    410

    Tang, after relieving himself, saw the Milky Way shining westward, with a slanted moon in the trees, it was roughly the fourth watch of the night. The old woman called out from the room, "The guest is yawning and stretching, you should let him rest." Tang replied, "I can still sit for a while." The old woman said, "Don't push it too hard, there is still a journey ahead tomorrow, and I have another request, please keep it in mind." Tang asked what the matter was, and the old woman hesitantly said, "Tomorrow when you pass through the market, if you see my husband, please tell him to send some money quickly, just say that the food and money in the house are all gone." Tang said, "I will do my best." The old woman then blushed and said, "Because of our poverty, we have no bedding, it was very inconvenient for you to stay with us last night." Tang said, "Borrowing a place to rest, getting a peaceful night's sleep, I have already received a great favor, how could I dare to ask for more?" They then each went to bed. Tang was extremely tired and fell asleep immediately upon lying down. Later, he woke up from a dream, hearing the sound of grasshoppers or crickets near his ear, and seeing the light of fireflies before his eyes, he suddenly woke up in alarm. He found himself lying in a pine or cypress forest, with autumn dew wetting his clothes and the cold penetrating his bones. His donkey was tied to the tree roots, chewing grass continuously. The thatched cottage was nowhere to be found, and the old woman and the young woman were both gone. All he could see was an ancient tomb in ruins, half-tilted among wild grass and thorns. He couldn't help but feel his hair stand on end with fear, and he quickly grabbed the donkey and mounted it, riding away steadily.

    411

    行三五里，天已向曙，稍稍心定，抵烟郊事毕，复遵故道，小歇旗亭。有涤器老人，酷肖老媪所述，询之，果郝四也，愈异之，引至僻处告以前夜所遇。郝泫然曰：“据郎所见，真先妻与亡媳并夭孙也。先妻下世二年，亡媳去岁以难产，母子一夕皆死，讵意尚聚首地下哉！”谭亦恻然，更问巴参领为何如人，郝曰：“某旗某佐领之父也，死亦十余年矣，直北乔木处，即其墓道。亡媳，其家婢也。老朽夫妇，故其守墓人。往岁零雨，屋舍倾圯，佐领无力缮葺，老朽无容身处，故佣工于此，聊以自活。前日中元节，佐领殿墓，犹焚船马数事，第不知亡妻借马，何事何之耳。”谭感叹久之，乃解囊赠以青蚨五百，俾具冥资，勿使魂馁。郝泣谢。谭归后，不欲食言于鬼，亟备纸烟具二枚，烟一封，重至其墓，祝而焚之。更访巴参领墓，果在直北数十武外，松柏森郁，有新碑可扪云。

    Explanation

    411

    After walking three to five miles, the sky began to lighten, signaling dawn. Gradually, Tan's heart calmed down. Upon reaching the outskirts of the town and finishing his business, he returned along the old path and took a short rest at a flag pavilion. There was an elderly man washing dishes who very much resembled what the old woman had described. When Tan asked him, it turned out to be Hao Si. He was even more surprised. Tan led Hao Si to a secluded place and recounted the previous night's encounter. Hao Si, with tears in his eyes, said, "According to what you saw, they were indeed my late wife, my deceased daughter-in-law, and my grandson who died young." Tan's wife had passed away two years ago, and his daughter-in-law had died last year due to difficult childbirth, with both mother and child dying in one night. Who would have thought they were still together in the underworld! Tan was also moved, and he asked what kind of person Ba was. Hao Si replied, "He was the father of a certain assistant commander from a certain banner, who has been dead for over ten years. Directly north, where there are tall trees, is the path to his tomb. My deceased daughter-in-law was a servant in his household. My old wife and I used to be the caretakers of his tomb. Last year during the rainy season, the houses collapsed, and the assistant commander had no power to repair them. We had no place to stay, so we worked here, just to make a living. Last year during the Ghost Festival, the assistant commander performed a ritual at the tomb and still burned paper boats and horses. But he did not know why the deceased wife borrowed a horse and for what purpose." Tan sighed for a long time, then took out his purse and gave him five hundred coins to provide spiritual funds, so that their spirits would not be hungry. Hao Si cried and thanked him. After Tan returned, he did not want to break his promise to the spirits. He quickly prepared two sets of paper and tobacco, and a bundle of tobacco, and went back to the tomb, made a wish, and burned them. He then visited Ba's assistant commander's tomb, which was indeed directly north, about several dozen steps away, with dense and lush pines and cypresses, and a new stele that could be touched.

    412

    兰岩曰：一饭之恩，感而必报，谭诚义矣。独是夜台魂馁，泉下神悲，倍可伤矣。以郝之老迈，贫无容身之地，佣工野肆中，暂谋糊口，斯亦自顾不暇，岂知妻子嗷嗷，犹待哺于地下哉！嗟乎，鬼而贫也，尚有阳世以为不时之需，人而贫也，其将告助于谁氏耶？

    　　陆珪予友仁和陆子瑜，名珪，少游巴蜀，舟泊巫山下。会同载一楚客，病死，其乡人为理棺衾，行李羁滞，计五日方可发。陆固好动，既恶少舲狭隘，又不耐丧事之扰，竟舍舟从陆。行二里，足重茧，不复能越险阻，乃止于乱山孤馆中，欲觅代步，无有也。一日，馆吏来白：“敝处三家村也，往来但有此馆，今夔州参戎莅任，辎重家口将住于此。君幸暂觅居停，官过后，当任君去留，第勿以逐客见猜也！”陆不得已，复移装于馆西三里许，借废兰若居焉。就中唯一僧，年约三十余，形貌奇侅，行复偃蹇。陆作客数千里外，不敢睥睨骄人，惟处之以谦。

    Explanation

    412

    Final Translation

    Lan Yan said: "The kindness of a single meal, if felt, must be repaid; this is true righteousness. But it is even more pitiful that the spirits of the dead are hungry, and the gods in the netherworld are sorrowful. For an old man like Hao, who is poor and has no place to call his own, working in a remote tavern to temporarily make ends meet, he is too preoccupied with his own affairs to know that his children and wife are hungry and crying, still waiting to be fed in the underworld! Alas, the poor spirits still have the world of the living to rely on for occasional needs, but what will the poor people do? To whom will they turn for help?"

    My friend Renhe, Lu Zi Yu, named Gui, traveled to Bashu when he was young and docked his boat at the foot of Wushan. At the same time, he was traveling with a guest from Chu who died of illness. The guest's hometown people arranged for his coffin and shroud, and his luggage was delayed, with plans to leave only after five days. Lu Gui, who was naturally restless, disliked the narrowness of the small boat and could not tolerate the disturbance of the funeral arrangements, so he left the boat and continued on foot. After walking two miles, his feet were so blistered that he could no longer cross difficult terrain, so he stopped at a solitary inn in the mountains. He wanted to find a substitute mode of transportation but found none. One day, a servant came to inform him: "This place is a small village, and there is only this inn for travelers. Now the military governor of Kuizhou has arrived, and his household and baggage will stay here. You are fortunate to find temporary accommodation. After the official has passed through, you will be free to leave or stay as you wish. Just do not be seen as an unwelcome guest!" Lu Gui had no choice but to move his belongings three miles west of the inn and took up residence in an abandoned temple. There was only one monk there, about thirty years old, with an unusual appearance and a slow, awkward manner. Lu Gui, being a guest thousands of miles away, dared not look down on anyone, but treated him with humility.

    413

    无何，月升，值孟秋之半，炎暑未消，梧叶乍飘，蛩声四聚。陆独步荒砌，闻寺门剥啄声，甚杂沓，僧倒屣出应，陆潜伺之，则褐衣三五辈，将主人命，邀僧于山楼，践玩月之约者也。僧诺之，随引扉相与西去。陆私念此僧，踪迹可疑久矣，今盍试从其所之，观其所事？足遣旅怀，倘有所见，亦可以助异日朋友相聚时，花前月下之谈资也。

    Explanation

    413

    Soon after, the moon rose, at the midpoint of the first month of autumn, when the intense summer heat had not yet dissipated, paulownia leaves began to flutter, and the sounds of insects gathered around. Lu walked alone on the desolate stone path, hearing the loud and chaotic knocking on the temple gate. The monk rushed out to answer, even overturning his shoes. Lu secretly observed, and saw three or five people in rough clothing, on behalf of the master, inviting the monk to the mountain pavilion to fulfill the moon-viewing appointment. The monk agreed and followed them as they opened the door and went west together. Lu privately thought that this monk had been suspicious for a long time, and now decided to follow where he went and observe what he did, to dispel the loneliness of travel. If there was anything to see, it could also serve as material for conversation among friends in the future, under the flowers and moon.

    414

    遂潜履其迹，迤逦行数里，山路迍邅。卒至一山楼，半依峭壁，莅半深谭，阶砌倾危，窗寮毁败。陆度不可登，适旁有古松，虬枝夭矫，倚于巨石，因攀松踞石，平跳楼中。色色洞晰。见楼中列双烛，设两筵，长裾高履者三人，貌悉奇伟，靓妆女子一人，绝美丽，闻僧至，咸趋走而迓之曰：“何晏也？”僧笑曰：“早一刻非不佳，奈何俗客在宾榻，故少煞风景。”女子曰：“袁师知之乎？郦三妹不矜细行，竟遭薏苡之谤，乃翁督责过峻，三妹娇养惯，哭泣竟日，目尽肿，今夕不识能赴约与否？如爽约，则师之煞风景，未必如是之甚。”一白衣少年曰：“不然，郦三娘苟知袁师至，目肿即消。

    Explanation

    414

    He stealthily followed her tracks, winding his way through the mountain for several miles, where the path was fraught with difficulties. Eventually, he arrived at a mountain pavilion, half-leaning against a steep cliff, overlooking a deep pool, with steps that were precarious and windows that were in a state of decay. The path by land was impassable, but there happened to be an ancient pine tree nearby, with twisted branches that were graceful and leaning against a large rock. He climbed the pine tree, sat on the rock, and then leaped into the pavilion. Everything inside was clearly visible. He saw that the pavilion was lit by two candles, with two tables set up, and three people dressed in long robes and high shoes, all of whom had remarkable appearances, and one beautifully dressed woman, who was extremely beautiful. When the monk entered, they all hurried to greet him, saying, "Is it He Yan?" The monk smiled and said, "A little earlier would not have been bad, but how can an ordinary guest be on the guest's bed, spoiling the view?" The woman said, "Does Teacher Yuan know about it? Lili's third sister does not care about small details and ended up suffering from the slander of the water chestnut. Her father's supervision is too severe, and the third sister is used to being pampered, crying all day, with her eyes completely swollen. Tonight, I do not know if she can keep the appointment. If she breaks the appointment, then Teacher Yuan spoiling the view is not necessarily as severe." A young man in white said, "Otherwise, if Lili's third sister knew that Teacher Yuan had arrived, her swollen eyes would immediately disappear."

    415

    即不来，卿正好入无双谱也。”女笑且骂曰：“小魅，踣铁未脱，遂敢于阿　前饶舌耶？”众皆大笑。僧曰：“勿多求，一双足矣。”女微笑曰：“今日儿有疾。”黄衣体重者进曰：“卿有疾，予有药，一刀圭足以疗之。”女赧然首垂颈赤，不复应酬。黑衣长髯者，拍女肩而慰之曰：“老子兴复不浅，请与子出陈纳新，以游无穷，彼皆涉恶趣不足与谈，而亦无须芥蒂。子独不意袁师初晤时乎？才一入鹿脯之宴，辄喜惬过望，酒胾并吞，迄今骨鲠尚恐在喉，曾几何时，亦以口给取憎。伊尚如此，况夫斋马，奈何与之口角，独不惜气力耶？月色佳甚，不如谋醉之为得也。”众曰：“熊公平心之论是也。”女亦解颜。纷然入座，举杯欢畅，酒政喧嚣。

    Explanation

    415

    "If he doesn't come, you are just the right person to enter the 'unique album'!" The girl laughed and scolded, "You little demon, you're not yet out of your iron shackles, so how dare you chatter in front of me?" Everyone burst out laughing. The monk said, "Don't ask for too much, just one pair is enough." The girl smiled, "Today I'm feeling unwell." The heavy yellow-robed figure stepped forward and said, "If you're unwell, I have medicine, a small dose is enough to cure it." The girl blushed deeply, her head lowered and her neck turning red, and she didn't respond. The long-bearded black-robed figure patted the girl's shoulder and comforted her, "Old man here has a great idea, let's discard the old and embrace the new, to wander endlessly. Those who engage in evil deeds are not worth discussing with, and there's no need to hold any grudges. Didn't you think of Master Yuan when we first met? As soon as he entered the deer meat banquet, he was so pleased that he ate the wine and meat together, and even now, he might still have a bone stuck in his throat. How long has it been since he started getting annoyed by talkative people? Even the Zen master doesn't want to argue with him, so why bother wasting energy? The moonlight is so beautiful, it would be better to get drunk." Everyone said, "Master Bear's fair judgment is right." The girl also smiled. They all sat down, raised their cups, and enjoyed themselves, with the atmosphere becoming increasingly lively.

    416

    正腾辩间，褐衣奴传语：“郦三娘子来矣。”有顷，一双鬟女郎登楼，貌尤艳，而有忧色，不暇作寒暄语，俇俇然向众言曰：“诸君尚在此高会耶？彼莱夷已税倌人之驾矣。我辈寿则寿矣，虽然，犹未有树也。儿方寸乱矣，敢请诸君早计之。”众骤闻之，大惊挠。黑衣者独咍然曰：“迂奴无胆，必致首鼠两端，大事去矣。吾等已预有间谍，莱夷之旅若林，亦何足畏？忆昔与袁衲子采药西山，遇一瞔妇，方姅，二人未尝回避，而术竟无恙。可见学道既成，虽危无咎也。

    Explanation

    416

    In the midst of a vigorous debate, a servant in a coarse garment relayed a message: "Lady Li San is here." Shortly after, a young woman with braided hair ascended the stairs. She was exceptionally beautiful, yet her expression was one of worry. She had no time for pleasantries and hurriedly addressed the group, saying, "Are you all still here enjoying yourselves? That person has already taken action. We may live, but we still lack a plan. I am at a loss, so I ask you to come up with a plan soon." Upon hearing this, the group was greatly startled and disconcerted. The man in black robes alone laughed mockingly and said, "That foolish servant lacks courage and will surely waver between two choices, causing our plan to fail. We have already prepared spies; the forces of that person are like a forest, but what is there to fear? I recall when I was with Master Yuan, we gathered herbs on the Western Mountains and encountered a startled woman who was in a state of surprise. We did not avoid her, and our skills were unharmed. It is clear that once the path of learning is mastered, even in danger, there is no blame."

    417

    ”女愀然摇首曰：“儿闻福生有基，祸生有胎，吾等近年狂乐极矣，岂能不反。昔胡大师作蜘蛛隐时，再三规戒曰：”行矣，乐不可恃，欲不可纵，三年后，莱夷猖獗，破巢之下，恐无完卵。‘言犹在耳，今非其时乎？惜当日行色匆匆，未及审询，蓦然值此，伎俩辄穷，奈何徒作大言，焉济于事？“僧曰：”无哗。胡师所踞，去此不过五百里，盍共往依之？“众悉首肯，黑衣者独不欲，曰：”胡衲淡泊，惟晓枯禅，吾等避未然之灾，而轻去已成之业，是犹弃苏合之丸，而取蛣蜣之转。袁师素多奇计，今乃出下策耶？“纷议未决。

    　　忽闻林中鸣镝声，陆大惊，窥之，见壮夫百余人，拥一将军，呵殿而至。咸手弓腰矢，嗾犬呼鹰，楼中人辟易星散，壮夫飞马分逐，无不应弦饮羽。陆股栗而颠，猛然惊寤，则身故在兰若阶下卧也。重历旧径，果有山楼松石，悉符所见，徘徊不能自释。

    Explanation

    417

    The woman shook her head sadly and said, "I have heard that blessings have roots and misfortunes have beginnings. We have been excessively joyful for the past few years; how can we not face a reversal? When Master Trisula was in hiding as a spider, he repeatedly warned us: 'Go forth, for joy cannot be relied upon, and desire cannot be indulged. In three years, the Laiyi will become rampant, and under the broken nest, there will be no whole eggs left.' His words are still ringing in my ears. Is this not the time? Alas, we were in such a hurry on that day that we did not have the chance to inquire properly. Suddenly encountering this, our skills are exhausted. How can we merely make grandiose statements without practical benefit?" The monk said, "Be quiet. Master Trisula is not far from here, only five hundred miles away. Why don't we all go and rely on him?" Everyone agreed, except for the man in black, who said, "Master Trisula is simple and detached, understanding only dry meditation. We are avoiding an impending disaster and lightly abandoning what we have already accomplished. This is like discarding precious pills for dung beetles' balls. Teacher Yuan always has ingenious plans. Is this a low strategy now?" The discussion remained undecided.

    Suddenly, they heard the sound of a whistle in the forest. Lu was greatly alarmed and looked secretly. He saw more than a hundred strong men surrounding a general, arriving with shouts and commands. All were holding bows and arrows, whistling to dogs and calling eagles. The people in the tower scattered like stars, and the strong men rode horses and chased separately, hitting the target and shooting down feathers. Lu trembled and staggered, suddenly waking up in a panic. He realized he was actually lying on the steps of the temple. Retracing the old path, there were indeed mountain pavilions and pine stones, all matching what he had seen. He wandered and could not let go.

    418

    归而心悸，不复淹留，乃携装还山馆，馆吏迎笑曰：“子亦巧甚，参戎夜猎大获，今晨甫去，子可以居此矣。”陆问参戎何如人，吏曰：“参戎瞿姓，山东莱州人，新科进士，有勇名，以军功特授绥宁营参戎者也。今夜猎于山中，得熊一、虎一、猿一、狐狸二、兔三五头，不足异也。最可异者，并捕得白马，极神骏，谓是野马，而踣铁宛然，此物奚其至此，子博学能知之乎？”陆虽不能解，而心知昨夜所见者，皆此数兽之妖，黑衣者熊，黄衣者虎，僧称是袁师，即为猿，女称郦三娘子，则二女为狐狸，三五褐衣奴，即为兔，而白衣少年，女嘲其踣铁未脱，其为白马无疑矣。

    　　禽兽精魅，于人何所关系，乃致陆幻化如此，天下事尚可思议哉？此事陆逢人辄述之，予闻之尤熟。

    Explanation

    418

    Returning home, Lu was filled with palpitations, and he did not linger any longer. He then carried his belongings back to the mountain inn, where the innkeeper greeted him with a smile and said, "You are also very clever. The captain had a great night of hunting and just left this morning, so you can stay here now." Lu asked about the captain, and the innkeeper replied, "The captain is named Qu, from Lai Prefecture in Shandong Province. He is a newly graduated scholar-official with a reputation for bravery, and he was specially appointed as a captain of the Suining Camp due to his military merit.
