Last night, he hunted in the mountains and captured one bear, one tiger, one monkey, two foxes, and three to five rabbits. Nothing unusual about it. The most unusual thing is that he also captured a white horse, extremely spirited and swift, which was said to be a wild horse but had iron hooves, just like that. How did this creature come to be here? Can you, with your extensive knowledge, explain it?" Although Lu could not understand, he knew in his heart that what he had seen the previous night were all the spirits of these animals: the one in black clothing was a bear, the one in yellow clothing was a tiger, the monk who called himself Master Yuan was a monkey, the woman who called herself Lady Li San was a fox, and the three or five servants in brown clothing were rabbits. As for the young man in white, the woman had mocked him for not shedding his iron hooves, so there was no doubt that he was the white horse.

    Spiritual entities of birds and beasts, what relation do they have to people, yet caused Lu to transform in this way. Can worldly matters still be believed? Lu recounted this story to everyone he met, and I heard it especially well.

    419

    兰岩曰：深山穷谷，何所不有？而此事尤奇。

    　　白萍林澹人，延平诸生也，貌姣媚如好女子，见者无不啧啧而目送，闽中俗尚龙阳，林独守身如处子，片肌寸体，未尝轻露于人。年十九未娶，以槐黄近，税居城北余氏废园。园多乔木，门枕一溪，地幽僻，少人踪。

    　　时当盛夏，林日墓则辍读，散步溪边，爱其水之清涟，濯足石上，觉水旁嗤嗤有女子笑声。林惊视之，见一女子，齿甚稚，娟妙绝伦，由对岸步水而过，无少沾濡。林叱曰：“何物妖魅，敢近人耶？”女哂曰：“恐世间无此妖魅耳。”林拭足着履，逼女于树下，谛观之。女坐石上笑曰：“我妖魅，子何敢近？不虑噬子耶？”林曰：“苟非妖魅，何能于水上行，衣履悉不沾濡？”女曰：“子不闻，‘圣足行于水，无迹也；众生行于霜，有迹也’？即伏水中一年，亦何不能！”林曰：“踏浪之技，无地无之，不足争论。

    419

    Lan Yan said: In deep mountains and remote valleys, what is not there? But this matter is particularly strange.

    Bai Ping Lin Danren was a student from Yanping, with a beautiful and charming appearance like a lovely woman. Those who saw him could not help but admire and follow him with their eyes. In Fujian, there was a custom of valuing male beauty, but Lin alone kept his body as pure as a virgin, not a single inch of his body was ever lightly shown to others. At the age of nineteen, he had not married, because the time of the imperial examination was approaching, so he rented a house in the abandoned garden of the Yu family in the northern part of the city. The garden was full of tall trees, the gate faced a stream, the place was secluded and rarely visited by people.

    It was the height of summer, and Lin would stop reading after sunset, taking a walk by the stream. He loved the clear and rippling water, washing his feet on the rocks. He heard the sound of a girl's laughter near the water. Lin was startled and looked around, seeing a girl, very young, with an exquisite beauty beyond compare, who walked across the water from the opposite bank, without getting wet. Lin shouted, "What kind of demon dares to approach people?" The girl smiled and said, "I fear there are no such demons in the world." Lin dried his feet and put on his shoes, pushing the girl under a tree, closely observing her. The girl sat on a rock and laughed, "I am a demon, why do you dare to approach me? Don't you fear I might bite you?" Lin said, "If I am not a demon, how can I walk on water, my clothes and shoes are not wet at all?" The girl said, "Haven't you heard, 'the holy foot walks on water, leaving no trace; ordinary beings walk on frost, leaving a trace'? Even if I were to stay underwater for a year, I could still do it!" Lin said, "The skill of walking on waves exists everywhere, it is not worth arguing about."

    420

    所可异者，地僻人稀，旷夫独处，不虞子之涉吾地也，何故？”女应声曰：“年少喜游，所至不暇关白，然滋尔疑抱，虽然不遇明人，儿之大不幸也。譬夫水晶镜片，翳以尘　，宜其暧昧不明。因念如是，不胜凄然。子非善知识，请各事其事，无相问也。”言讫，恻然欲泪，若不胜其感伤者。林怜之，欲邀之入斋，而又畏其非人，颇形踯躅，女复嫣然笑曰：“子真口同百舌，胆如鼷鼠，独不虑贻小儿女以掩口胡卢耶？”林恧然，肩随以行。甫至园门略彴前，即遇馆童逆告曰：“浴汤已寒，郎何往而久不归也？”女匿林后，潜入斋中，格格笑不止。林亦匿笑，谓童曰：“我自洗浴，汝亦不必复来。我倦甚，须早眠也。”童怀惑而去。

    Explanation

    420

    What is surprising is that the place is remote and sparsely populated, with a man living alone in isolation. He did not expect you to enter my territory. Why is that?" The girl responded, "I am young and fond of traveling. Wherever I go, I have no time to report. However, this has caused you to doubt me. Although I have not met a wise person, it is a great misfortune for me. It's like a crystal lens obscured by dust, which should make it unclear. Thinking of this, I cannot help but feel sad." Lin pitied her and wanted to invite her into his study, but he was afraid she might not be a normal person, showing hesitation. The girl smiled sweetly and said, "You truly have a tongue like a hundred birds, and a heart like a tiny mouse. Don't you worry about leaving a bad impression on the children?" Lin blushed and walked shoulder to shoulder with her. Just as they arrived at the garden gate and the small bridge in front, they met the servant boy who reported in advance, "The bath water is already cold. Where have you been, and why have you been gone for so long?" The girl hid behind Lin and secretly entered the study, laughing continuously. Lin also laughed silently and told the boy, "I will bathe myself. You don't need to come back. I am very tired and need to sleep early." The servant boy, puzzled, left.

    421

    林深闭重门，入室，向女而笑曰：“子亦太便捷，必久惯贻香者。”女睨之曰：“含苞花，何漫以缤纷见拟，宁若子美目修眉，丰姿自喜，甘为巾帼之行？倘遇俞大夫，后庭花知添几种，应为子升表于天矣。”林故□□，达于心而濡于言，乍入温柔乡，面□口吃，甚不敌女之便给。女下窗闭户，收书燃灯，与林对席坐，披览诗文，搜索笔砚，不肯少静。见棋枰即取与林弈，一局方布，则以手乱其子曰：“此大费心，非乐事也。纵留几局呕血图，有何关系！”于是促膝谐谑，问林能饮乎？林以量浅对，女以箑轻击其肩曰：“量浅耳，是能饮也。”亟启纱厨出酒一罂，肴一盒，类皆珍美。林怪，问物从何来？“女曰：”预储于此久矣。子第饮食，又何多问！“林知其异，然对此丽人，殊不畏惧，相与浅酌细谈。女自言：”余氏，字白萍，园主人，奴之故主也。主人举族迁城内，儿独留此间，年十七矣。父母兄弟姊妹俱漂泊，踪迹亦各无定。

    Explanation

    421

    Lin deeply closed the heavy door, entered the room, and smiled at the woman, saying, "You are also too convenient, must be accustomed to offering fragrant gifts." The woman looked at him and said, "A bud, why should it be compared to something colorful and varied? How about your beautiful eyes and well-groomed eyebrows, your graceful appearance delights you, willing to act like a woman? If met with Dr. Yu, the flowers in the back garden would know how to add several more varieties, it should be recorded in the heavens." Lin, who was originally awkward, expressed his thoughts and feelings with words, suddenly entering the realm of tenderness, looking awkward and stammering, greatly outmatched by the woman's quick wit. The woman lowered the window and closed the door, collected books and lit lamps, sat opposite Lin, read through poems and prose, searched for writing tools, and would not allow any quiet. When she saw a chessboard, she immediately took it and played with Lin. As soon as a game was set up, she would mess up the pieces and say, "This is very tiring, not a fun thing. Even if we leave a few games that would make one vomit blood, what does it matter!" She then sat close and engaged in playful banter, asking Lin if he could drink. Lin replied that he had a shallow capacity, but she lightly struck his shoulder with a fan and said, "You have a shallow capacity, but you can drink." She quickly opened the silk screen and brought out a jar of wine and a box of delicacies, all of which were precious and beautiful. Lin was surprised and asked where the items came from. "I had stored them here for a long time," the woman said. "Just eat and drink, why ask so many questions!" Lin knew that she was unusual, but in the presence of this beautiful woman, he was not afraid, and they drank lightly and talked in detail. The woman spoke for herself, "I am Yu, styled Bai Ping, the owner of the garden, the former master of the slave. The master moved the whole family to the city, I alone stayed here, now seventeen years old. My parents, brothers, sisters, and I are all wandering, our traces are also uncertain."

    422

    正愁孤孑，幸得与君解逅，见如怜，愿备妾媵。“林喜曰：”予亦未有室，得与卿伉俪，亦何乐而不为？“女粲然，饮酒间，备极欢昵。林原不能饮，少饮则醉，乃同就榻，枕席之事，颠之倒之。林虽弱冠，具甚么麽，女嘲之曰：”子亦幸未娶，即娶，亦不足以清帷薄也。“林大惭，女曰：”无伤也，亟当为子图之。

    Explanation

    422

    Just lamenting loneliness, fortunately meeting you, seeing you as pitiful, I am willing to serve as a concubine. "Lin happily said, "I also have no wife, to marry you, why not do it if it brings joy?" The woman laughed brightly, during the drinking, they were extremely intimate and affectionate. Lin originally could not drink, a little drink makes him drunk, then they went to the bed together, engaging in bedroom affairs, upside down and inside out. Lin, though a young man, possesses what, the woman mocked him and said, "You are also fortunate not to have married, if you marry, it will not be enough to clear the curtains and thin." Lin greatly felt ashamed, the woman said, "No harm done, I will quickly plan for you."

    423

    “因挑灯复起，检荷囊，得末药一撮，和以唾而团之成红丸，使林吞之，仍启衾卧，林觉药入腹中一霎间，势热如火，倦而睡去，四更复寤，怪累累然有物在股际，探之，则势暴长，迥殊平日，大盈握，长咫尺矣。大惊告女，女扪結而笑曰：”以小易大，子何修而得此？“林亦笑曰：”妙则妙矣，无乃太丑观乎？“女曰：”惟其丑观，愈形子之美好，夫何尤焉！“于是尽欢而罢，自此无夕不至，好合无间。然终以馆童之耳目为碍。女商于林曰：”观馆童之为人，颇颖慧，且子之心腹也。盍明告之？“林乃呼童，使拜女，告以故，并诫勿泄，童唯唯而退，遂不复避讳。虽白日亦在斋中。

    Explanation

    423

    Final Translation

    Lit the lamp and got up again, checked the medicine bag, and found a pinch of powdered medicine. Mixing it with saliva, he formed it into a red pill and made Lin swallow it. Then, he went back to sleep under the covers. Lin felt the medicine entering his stomach in an instant, and the sensation was as hot as fire. He was tired and fell asleep, but woke up again during the fourth watch of the night. He was startled to find something piled up between his thighs. Feeling it, he discovered that it had suddenly grown, greatly different from usual, as big as a clenched fist and as long as a foot. Greatly surprised, he told the woman. She touched it and laughed, saying, "You traded small for large. What did you do to get this?" Lin also laughed and said, "It's wonderful, indeed, but isn't it a bit too ugly?" The woman replied, "Just because it's ugly, it makes your beauty stand out even more. What's there to worry about!" They then enjoyed themselves fully and stopped. From then on, there was no night when he didn't come, and they were in perfect harmony without any gap. However, the presence of the servant's eyes and ears was still a concern. The woman suggested to Lin, "Considering the servant's character, he is quite intelligent and trustworthy. Why not tell him openly?" Lin then called the servant, made him bow to the woman, and explained the situation, warning him not to reveal it. The servant nodded and withdrew. So they no longer avoided or concealed anything, even during the day, they were together in the study.

    424

    未几，林赴试入都城，月余始归。女设馔，为作软脚局，相得愈欢。第林出示棘闱，七艺皆不得意，心殊悒悒，女曰：“勿忧，场中固不论文也，子有祖德，必高捷。”及揭晓，林果中第九，名大噪。友人符生，故太守某公之孙，美而少，盖浊世翩翩之佳公子也，夙与林为总角交，今更为同年而同门。性本不羁，得隽而兴愈豪，折柬召林饮，林辞不往。符亲至余园，强之升舆。在座五人，皆新贵而旧识者。饮至午夜，始各散去。林被投辖不获归。符醉，谓林曰：“兄平日守身如玉，每下朋友榻，未尝解衣，今为孝廉，行将在仕矣，岂可复作儿女态，今夜与兄投足谈之，可乎？”林请异榻，符曰：“主人仓卒，不暇备矣。

    Explanation

    424

    Not long after, Lin went to take the examination and entered the capital city. After more than a month, he returned. The woman prepared a meal and made soft-footed cakes, and they got along even better. However, Lin showed his results from the examination, and all seven subjects were unsatisfactory, greatly depressing his heart. The woman said, "Do not worry, in the examination hall, it's not about the writing; you have the virtue of your ancestors, you will surely achieve high success." When the results were announced, Lin indeed ranked ninth, and his name became widely known. Fu Sheng, the grandson of a former governor of a certain official, was handsome and young, and he was a fine young gentleman of the turbulent times. They had been friends since childhood, and now they were also classmates and fellow students. His nature was inherently unrestrained, and after achieving success, his enthusiasm became even greater. He sent an invitation to Lin for a drinking party, but Lin declined to go. Fu personally went to Lin's garden and insisted that Lin get into the carriage. There were five people at the party, all new officials and old acquaintances. They drank until midnight before dispersing. Lin was detained and couldn't return home. Fu was drunk and said to Lin, "Brother, you have always been as pure as jade, never undressing when you get off your friends' beds. Now that you are a scholar-official, you are about to enter the government, how can you still act like a child? Tonight, I will have intimate conversation with you, can you do that?" Lin asked for a different bed, and Fu said, "The host was in a hurry and didn't have time to prepare."

    425

    ”随颐指二童，强为缓衣，林被酒瞑眩，极力支撑，竟不能敌。裤脱，忽露嫪毒之具，符骇谛曰：“此岂兄之故物耶？何便得一第，顿令人刮目如是！”林羞匿不对，符亦觉不雅，给衣着之，退踞胡床，息喘良久。屏二童，闭户，前席致诘。林颈赤面赧，不发一言。符正色曰：“尔我总角之交，岂敢败乃事者，如不以诚告，当飞语诸同年，俾咸以嗷曹目兄也。林大窘，遂以实告，且嘱曰：”幸勿为外人道也。“符愕然曰：”此兄以鳏居致邪，丧无日矣。脱此无他术，惟谋早娶，可以免祸。内人有一女弟，年十八，性贤淑，而色美丽，兄如不弃，弟请执柯斧。“林故无父母伯叔，鲜兄弟，诸事皆得自主，且久闻符内娣之美，族巨而家富，遂许之。符晨起入白于其妻，妻大喜，归告父母，其父素器林，一言而决。

    Explanation

    425

    "Following the instructions of two children, I forced myself to wear loose clothing. Lin was drunk and dizzy, trying hard to support himself, but ultimately could not resist. His pants fell off, and suddenly his private parts were exposed. Fu was startled and said, 'Is this something you had before? How did you manage to pass an examination and suddenly make people look at you differently?' Lin was ashamed and did not respond. Fu also felt it was inappropriate and gave him new clothes to wear. He sat back on a chair and took a long time to catch his breath. He sent away the two children, closed the door, and moved closer to the seat to ask questions. Lin's neck and face were red, and he did not say a word. Fu spoke seriously, 'We have been friends since childhood. How dare I ruin our friendship? If you do not tell the truth, I will spread rumors among our peers and make everyone look at you with disdain.' Lin was extremely embarrassed and finally revealed the truth. Fu advised, 'Please do not tell anyone else.' Fu was surprised and said, 'This is because of your solitary life. It will not be long before you die. If not for this, there is no other way out. Only by planning to get married early can you avoid further trouble. My wife has a younger sister who is eighteen years old, virtuous and gentle, and beautiful. If you do not reject her, I will act as a matchmaker.' Lin originally had no close family members and few siblings, and could make decisions independently. He had heard about Fu's sister's beauty for a long time. Fu's family was large and wealthy, so he agreed to the marriage proposal. Fu got up in the morning and told his wife, who was very happy. She returned and told her parents. Her father always respected Lin and agreed immediately."

    426

    林不复过余园，择日纳采。及合卺，新妇果丽。第林具过伟，定情时大为凿枘。三朝，妇家来瞊，男女亲戚，宴会满堂。忽一女子，瞥然至前，诸眷惊起视之，甚艳，而皆不相识，急呼主人。林入视，则余氏白萍也。惊怛却立，不能出一语。女艴然责林曰：“君诚所谓薄幸人也，儿何负于君，遽以葑菲见遗？”林俯首无以应。扰攘间，符生突至见之，惊为仙人。女忽不见，诸眷骇愕，具聚猜疑，感不知其妖异之由。惟符晓然，神为之夺，叹异不置。

    　　迟半月余，林饮于友人。漏下，归自城北，觉身后有二人从行，疑为逻卒。回顾良久，二人行近前，方辨为二女鬟也，揽林之祛而邀之曰：“小娘嘱招郎君，幸勿见拒！”林却之不可，不得已，从之以行。时际望后，月色皓然，循僻径约三四里，渐近余园。林内疚，止步不前，二鬟强挽之，又数武，见白萍坐溪边石上，黛蛾颦蹙，怨态不支，掩袂而泣。

    Explanation

    426

    Lin no longer passed by Yu Garden, and chose a day for the engagement ceremony. When they performed the union ceremony, the bride was indeed beautiful. However, Lin was overly tall, and at the time of their betrothal, they were quite mismatched. On the third day after the wedding, the bride's family came to visit, and the gathering of relatives and friends filled the hall. Suddenly, a woman appeared before them, and all the guests were startled, standing up to look at her. She was very beautiful, but none of them recognized her, so they quickly called the host. Lin went in to see, and it was Bai Ping from the Yu family. He was shocked and stood back, unable to utter a word. The woman angrily scolded Lin, saying, "You are truly a fickle man! What have I done wrong to you that you would hastily discard me like wild vegetables?" Lin bowed his head, unable to respond. Amid the commotion, Fu Sheng suddenly appeared and saw her, amazed that she was a celestial being. The woman suddenly disappeared, and all the guests were shocked and astonished, gathering together in confusion, unable to understand the mysterious cause of the event. Only Fu Sheng understood clearly, and his spirit was taken away by the wonder, marveling at it endlessly.

    After a delay of more than half a month, Lin drank with a friend. After the strike of the night bell, he returned from the north of the city, feeling two people following him, suspecting they were night watchmen. After looking back for a long time, the two women approached him, and he finally realized they were two maids. They grasped his sleeve and invited him, saying, "The young lady has summoned the gentleman; please do not refuse!" Lin tried to refuse, but he had no choice but to follow them. At this time, it was after the full moon, and the moonlight was bright. They followed a secluded path for about three or four miles, gradually approaching Yu Garden. Lin felt guilty and stopped, unwilling to proceed. The two maids forcibly pulled him, and after a few steps, they saw Bai Ping sitting on a stone by the stream, her brows furrowed in a frown, her face filled with resentment, covering her sleeves and weeping.

    427

    二鬟捺林跪其前曰：“觅得薄情郎来矣！”林顿首引罪曰：“予知过矣，顾卿独不念斋中缱绻之情耶？”女哂曰：“子亦太强记，尚能忆及曩昔，若奴则尽矣。子负心太盛，即王魁、李益，有不逮焉。尤可恨者，子贱玉贵珉，致儿清白之身滥为所玷，思之痛心切骨，衔恨非一朝一夕矣。今子亲身而来，何翅固辙之鲋，纵摇尾乞怜，亦复奚益？应示蒲鞭之辱，以儆狂且。然不致子于死地者，以子有日腾骧，为乃祖隐德之报故也。”乃命二鬟褫林衣，折柳枝鞭之数十，更以溪沙傅其阴，置诸石上，而后舍去。乃林之被辱也，身如梦魇，转侧由人。次日黎明，方可动步。遂踉跄而返，密告符生。符闻之，浃背汗流，终生不过余园。

    Explanation

    427

    Two maidservants named Na Lin knelt before her and said, "We have found the unfeeling husband for you!" Na Lin bowed his head and admitted his guilt, saying, "I know I have made a mistake, but do you not remember the tender feelings we shared in the chamber?" The woman laughed and said, "You are too good at remembering. You can still recall the past, but I have forgotten it all. You have been too unfaithful; even Wang Que and Li Yi could not compare to you. What is even more hateful is that you treat jade as worthless and stones as precious, tarnishing my pure body with your impurities. Thinking about it makes me feel pain deep in my bones, and I have harbored resentment for a long time. Now you have come in person, you are no different from a fish stranded in a dried-up rut. Even if you beg for mercy by wagging your tail, it will be of no use. You should be shown the humiliation of the reed whip to warn the reckless and foolish. But I will not kill you because you will one day rise to prominence, to repay the hidden virtue of your ancestors." She then ordered the two maidservants to strip Na Lin of his clothes, broke a willow branch, and whipped him dozens of times. She then covered his back with sand from the stream and placed him on a stone before leaving him there. When Na Lin was being humiliated, he felt as if he were in a nightmare and could not move on his own. At dawn the next day, he was finally able to move and limped back, secretly telling Fu Sheng about what had happened. Fu heard about it and was drenched in sweat, never going beyond Yu Garden again.

    428

    林自此觉私处冷如垂冰，缩似僵蚕，百治不举，盖已病萎。新妇失所欢，不能无外遇。所赖少年英发，祖德不衰，得捷南宫，使致清要。以符一子为螟蛉。李芰裳为予言，不甚悉，后又得赖冠千详述之。

    　　恩茂先曰：祖有德，而子孙发甲，固天所以报告人，乃又斩厥祀，殊不可解。

    　　闲斋曰：否，否，愈远愈疏，古圣人所以有承祧之义也。林生绝嗣，天所以报林生，非所以报其祖，何则林祖父有发甲之子孙，而林不得为人之祖父也？天何负于吉人哉！“茂先大笑叫绝。

    Explanation

    428

    Lin from then on felt his private part as cold as hanging ice, contracted like a frozen silkworm, and despite numerous treatments, he could not recover, having likely suffered from atrophy. His new wife lost her joy and could not help but seek extramarital affairs. Relying on the youth's vigor and talent, the virtue of the ancestors did not decline, and he achieved success in the imperial examinations, thereby attaining a high and important position. He adopted a child as a son. Li Ji Shang told me, but I was not very familiar with the details, and later I heard a more detailed account from Lai Guan Qian.

    En Maoxian said: "The ancestor had virtue, and the descendants flourished; it is indeed how the heavens reward people, but then the ancestral worship was cut off, which is hard to understand."

    Xian Zhai said: "No, no, the further away, the more distant, the ancient sages had the principle of continuing the lineage. Lin's line ended without an heir; it is a reward for Lin, not for his ancestor. Why should it be a reward for Lin when his ancestor had descendants who flourished, and Lin himself could not become an ancestor? How could the heavens be unfair to the virtuous?" Maoxian laughed heartily and exclaimed in approval.

    429

    刘　大　宾刘大宾者，河州副总戎周公之常随也。署中有大书房，花木丛杂，廨舍宽敞，每见鬼物。刘与辕门官白把总者相友善，偶夜半被酒，觅白闲谈。绕出大堂，黯然无灯光，刘且行且唤曰：“白二哥睡何早也？”忽堂右偏旗纛下有人应曰：“白老爷苦蚊，向大书房宿矣。”刘乃负手高歌，缓步而往。时月色溶溶，万籁俱寂，隐隐见花台畔，一红衣女子倚栏而立。刘默念深夜矣，此女胡为乎来？席其体态服色，必杏花也。盖杏花者，周子妇之媵，颇有姿色，刘平日最为注意者也。于是酒醉兴高，欲就而搂之，去数武，见其面白如粉，眼赤色，舌出唇外三寸许。刘大叫发狂，径挝书室，直宿者惊起环问，咸闻鬼哭之声，渐出院外，无不毛戴。备述颠末，白把总至，闻之讶曰：“我今夜仍宿斜房，且二更时，大堂即静，旗纛下又乌有人，想亦鬼所为也。”

    Explanation

    429

    Duan Da Bin, a constant companion to Zhou Gong, the governor of Hezhou, lived in an office that had a spacious study room, surrounded by dense flowers and trees. The administrative buildings were large and open, and Duan often saw ghostly apparitions. Duan was friendly with Captain Bai, an officer at the gate, and one night, after drinking, he sought out Bai for casual conversation. Walking around the main hall, which was dark and without any lights, Duan called out, "Why did you go to bed so early, Bai?" Suddenly, someone replied from under the flag at the right side of the hall, "Captain Bai is suffering from mosquitoes and has gone to sleep in the large study room." Duan, feeling high-spirited from the wine, walked slowly and sang to himself, heading towards the study. The moonlight was bright, and everything was quiet; he faintly saw a woman in red standing by the flower terrace. Duan thought to himself, it must be late at night, why is this woman here? Her posture and attire matched those of Xinghua, a concubine of Zhou's wife, who was quite beautiful and the one Duan paid the most attention to. Drunk and in high spirits, he wanted to embrace her. After walking several steps, he saw that her face was pale as powder, her eyes were red, and her tongue protruded three inches from her lips. Duan screamed in terror and rushed to the study room, startling the night watchmen who gathered around to ask what happened. All heard the sound of ghostly cries, which gradually moved outside the courtyard, causing everyone to be in a state of shock. Duan recounted the entire incident, and when Captain Bai arrived, he was surprised and said, "I still slept in the side room tonight, and by the second watch, the main hall was already quiet. There was no one under the flag, so this must have been done by a ghost."

    430

    刘由此病癫，日作鬼语，悉不可晓，周以印花制之，不效。惟偻身而行，如负重状，每见杏花，则悲喜追逐，杏花亦不禁凄恻，泣数行下。周公以其怪，令二人复相见。杏花从此失神，或罢绣独语，或停食自伤，几次投缳，皆被同人所觉。一日，周出巡所汛，左右疏防，刘遂得于深夜潜入宅门，直抵寝所，解带缢杏花之颈，比家人觉而救之，气已绝矣。刘病寻愈，茫不知缢杏花之事，恨悒殊甚，又日为杏花父母所窘辱，亦自缢死。

    Explanation

    430

    Liu, thus, fell ill with epilepsy, speaking in ghostly language every day, all of which could not be understood. Zhou attempted to restrain him with a form of punishment (yanhua), but it was ineffective. Liu only bent over and walked, as if carrying a heavy load. Whenever he saw Apricot Flower, he would chase after her with alternating sadness and joy, and Apricot Flower could not help but feel sorrowful and shed tears. Zhou Gong, seeing his strange behavior, ordered the two to meet again. From then on, Apricot Flower lost her spirit, sometimes stopping her embroidery and speaking to herself, or stopping eating and hurting herself. She tried to hang herself several times, but was always discovered by others. One day, Zhou went on an inspection tour of his jurisdiction, and the guards were lax. Liu took the opportunity to sneak into the residence at night, directly reaching the bedroom, and strangled Apricot Flower's neck with his belt. By the time the family discovered and tried to save her, she was already dead. Liu's illness soon healed, but he had no memory of strangling Apricot Flower and was deeply distressed. He was also daily harassed and humiliated by Apricot Flower's parents, and eventually hanged himself.

    431

    兰岩曰：淫心一动，便招如许妖孽，二人俱不得其死，良可哀矣。独不解刘与杏花有何夙冤也，不然，红衣女何敢作祟？

    　　庄　莁　松吉州庄寿年，号鍃松。乾隆初年，贡入国学，僦居城北一废园中。初至时，莘莘茂草，苔茸没阶。荒芜殊甚。庄剪荆辟径，住行李于一轩。居无何，槐黄已届，检点入闱。庄虽名宿，而花样不同，且半世坎　，依然倒绷孩儿，郁愤成疴。延至春，委顿床第，佣奴呆钝，不胜薪水之任。所善同业邱生，亦同伤眊矂者，怜庄老病，携一童并襆被就伴之，朝暮相对，颇慰寂寥。

    Explanation

    431

    Lan Yan said: "When lustful thoughts arise, such calamities are summoned, and both individuals die an unnatural death, which is truly pitiable. I do not understand what old grudge Liu and Xinghua have, otherwise, how dare the red-clad woman cause mischief?"

    As for Zang Shu Song, a man from Jizhou, whose courtesy name was Chun Song, during the early years of the Qianlong era, he was admitted to the Imperial College as a tribute scholar and rented a dwelling in a dilapidated garden in the northern part of the city. Upon his arrival, the place was overgrown with lush grass, and moss covered the steps. The desolation was quite severe. Zang cut through the thorny bushes to clear a path and set up his belongings in a pavilion. Soon after, the time for the imperial examinations approached, and he prepared to enter the examination hall. Despite being a renowned scholar, his approach to the exams was unconventional, and he had faced numerous setbacks throughout his life, much like a child being bound backward. His frustration and anger led to illness. By spring, he was bedridden, and his hired servants were slow-witted, unable to manage even the simplest tasks. His good friend, a fellow scholar named Qiu, who also suffered from poor eyesight, pitied Zang's old age and illness. He brought along a young servant and their bedding to keep him company, and they spent their days together, providing some comfort in his loneliness.

    432

    邱固少年，每拥衾绸，不无瞑想。时际仲春，池草重青，园夜半茁。薄暮，于轩东独步，瞥见一女子，年可破瓜，翠裙红袖，艳莫与京，向邱嫣然一笑，百媚俱生。邱迷惑伫视，形如木鸡。第疑为邻女，罔敢挑达。女寻逾垝垣而逝。邱归轩语庄：“人言京师妇女，装束丑怪，既无旗人大方之度，又无南方袅娜之风。乃弟之所见，实有神仙其人者，装束皆意想不到，今而后知人言之不足信也。”庄曰：“否，否，子有见而佳者，皆非土著也。譬如今科乡试榜下，其魁卷之堪诵一时者甚少，究其实，顺天籍贯者有几人哉！”邱大笑。

    Explanation

    432

    Qiu, a young man, often wrapped in his quilt, would not be without deep thoughts. In the midst of late spring, the grass in the pond turned a deep green, and the garden flourished at midnight. At dusk, he walked alone in the east pavilion and suddenly saw a young woman, about sixteen years old, dressed in a green skirt and red sleeves, whose beauty was unparalleled. She smiled at Qiu, and her beauty came alive. Qiu was so captivated that he stood still, as if turned to stone. He suspected she might be a neighbor's daughter and dared not approach her. The woman quickly climbed over the broken wall and disappeared. Qiu returned to the pavilion and spoke to Zhuang: "People say that the women of the capital are ugly and strange in their attire, lacking the broad grace of the Manchu nobility and the delicate charm of the south. But what I have seen, there are indeed women like immortals, whose attire is beyond imagination. From now on, I know that people's words cannot be trusted." Zhuang said, "No, no, what you see as good is not from the local area. For example, in the current provincial examination, there are very few top candidates whose essays are worth reading, and even fewer are from the local area!" Qiu laughed heartily.

    433

    入夜，邱寤寐反侧，朦胧间，女子倏至，遂瞢腾如醉，相与交媾，猥亵之声四彻。庄惊觉，侧耳听之，不禁精遗满席，次日天晓，而邱起尤晏。朝食顿减，举动不时。庄诘其夜来何事，邱秘而不宣。庄大疑，夜假寐伺之。二更后，声作枕畔，亵语入耳喁喁，精又大泄，邱亦日就疲惫。庄询之，犹不吐实，庄正色曰：“吾二人皆作客异乡，家人系念岂不甚殷，奈何以数千里外之身，致之狐鬼？君少年庶几不患。仆老矣，安能拼将涸之精，为君夜夜遗也？”邱惭谢曰：“君言药石也，敢不拜嘉，如渠再至，当痛绝之。”

    Explanation

    433

    At night, Qiu tossed and turned in bed, half-awake and half-asleep, when a woman suddenly appeared. They were both dazed and drunk, and they engaged in sexual intercourse, with lewd sounds spreading all around. Zhuang was startled awake and strained to hear the sounds, and he could not help but ejaculate all over the place. The next morning, when the sun rose, Qiu woke up even later than usual. His breakfast was suddenly reduced, and his actions were irregular. Zhuang questioned him about what happened the previous night, but Qiu kept it hidden. Zhuang became highly suspicious and pretended to sleep to wait and see what would happen. After the second watch, sounds were made beside the pillow, and lewd whispers were heard. Qiu ejaculated again, and he also became increasingly exhausted. Zhuang inquired about it, but Qiu still did not tell the truth. Zhuang said sternly, "We both are guests in a foreign land, and our families surely miss us deeply. How can you, with a body thousands of miles away, be subject to foxes and ghosts? You are young and may not suffer much. I am old, and how can I sacrifice my dried-up essence for you to ejaculate night after night?" Qiu apologized with shame and said, "Your words are like medicine and stone; how could I not accept them? If she comes again, I will cut her off completely."

    434

    是夕，女不至，邱诩于庄，庄亦心喜。适同监涪州刘生，精歧黄术，偶遇庄，惊曰：“君何病之深也？”诊之，错愕良久，曰：“君年望六，那得有遗精症，岂其为狐鬼所蠹乎？”庄神之，即告以邱生事。俄邱至，刘亦诊之，怃然曰：“此狐祟，非鬼也，无药可医。城南有穆萨嘛者，可延至，使驱之。”庄问穆萨嘛何如人也，刘曰：“厢白旗，蒙古人也，为羽林军。萨嘛者，译言巫觋也。”庄敬诺，挽邱童往邀之，穆迫于官役，约三日后甫得来。

    Explanation

    434

    That night, the woman did not arrive, and Qiu flattered Zang, who was also secretly pleased. A student named Liu from Fuzhou, skilled in traditional Chinese medicine, happened to be present and chanced upon Zang. He exclaimed in surprise, "What illness do you have that is so severe?" After examining him, he was stunned for a long time and said, "You are almost sixty years old; how could you have nocturnal emissions? Could it be that you are being afflicted by a fox spirit or ghost?" Zang was convinced and immediately told him about Qiu's situation. Soon, Qiu arrived, and Liu also examined him, saying thoughtfully, "This is a fox spirit, not a ghost, and there is no medicine to cure it. There is a person named Mu Sama in the south of the city who can be invited to drive it away." Zang asked what kind of person Mu Sama is, and Liu replied, "He is from Xiangbaiqi, a Mongolian, and serves as a guard. 'Sama' translates to shaman." Zang respectfully agreed and took Qiu and a child to invite him. Mu Sama was pressed by official duties and agreed to come three days later.

    435

    庄引领望之。漏三下，女子复至，责邱曰：“君何信刘监生语？”邱曰：“使人召穆，庄为政，己不与焉。”女忿然，以两手捧邱之颊而接吻，曰：“我既死，汝岂能独生耶？”即以舌启唇而吸之，艻艻然气出如绠，心茫茫无所凭。女更加力吸之，邱觉丹田痛如刀割，五内欲裂。庄闻邱帐内撼摇，连呼“邱兄”不应，知其有异，急叫二童起，照邱已昏绝，白身僵卧。喧救一饷始醒，持庄手且告且泣曰：“弟之鬼，将就馁于异乡矣！”庄大恚，向空谩骂，招邱同榻。

    Explanation

    435

    Zhuang raised his head and looked forward. At the third strike of the water clock, the woman returned and scolded Qiu, saying, "Why do you believe what Liu Jiansheng said?" Qiu replied, "I sent someone to summon Mu, and Zhuang was in charge; I was not involved in it." The woman, filled with anger, took Qiu's cheeks in her hands and kissed him, saying, "I have already died; how can you survive alone?" She then used her tongue to open his lips and sucked at him, causing a thin stream of air to escape like a rope, leaving his heart feeling lost and unsupported. The woman increased her force, and Qiu felt a pain in his lower abdomen as if it were being cut by a knife, and his inner organs seemed to be about to burst. Zhuang heard the commotion inside Qiu's tent, repeatedly calling out, "Brother Qiu," but received no response. Realizing something was amiss, he quickly called two servants to check on Qiu, who was found unconscious and lying motionless and naked. After a short while of shouting for help, Qiu regained consciousness, holding Zhuang's hand and weeping as he said, "My spirit will starve in a foreign land!" Zhuang, filled with great anger, cursed into the air and invited Qiu to share the same bed.

    436

    三日，复使延穆，穆果至。邻人观者如堵。穆冠兜鍪，腰金铃，挝鼓咚咚，口诵神咒，绕园而走。至园后废楼前，瞋目仰视。旋弃鼓，捉铁叉趋步登梯，若有所逐。至墙角，极力叉之，闻之声，如犬之被挞然。设鼎镬，提叉烹之。咸见一黑狐，大如獾，脱肠而死。穆炙肉焚皮，取心烧之，研为末，服邱及庄而去。怪遂绝，二人病亦寻瘳。踵门谢赠，穆不受。订交而别。邱丁丑席帽离身，授柘城县尹；庄以教习生满，教职归里。此事庄自言之。

    Explanation

    436

    Three days later, they again sent to invite Yan Mu, who indeed arrived. Neighbors watching were like a wall. Yan Mu wore a helmet, had a gold bell on his waist, beat the drum loudly, recited sacred incantations, and walked around the garden. He reached the back of the garden, in front of the ruined tower, glaring up. Then he discarded the drum, grasped an iron fork, and quickly climbed the ladder, as if chasing something. Reaching the corner of the wall, he struck it with great effort, and the sound was like a dog being whipped. Setting up a cauldron, he lifted the fork to cook it. All saw a black fox, as big as a badger, torn apart and dead. Yan Mu roasted the meat and burned the skin, took the heart, burned it, ground it into powder, and gave it to Qiu and Zhuang before leaving. The strange occurrences ceased, and the two men's illnesses soon recovered. They came to the door to thank and offer gifts, but Yan Mu refused. They formed a friendship and parted ways. Qiu, in the year Ding Chou, left his official position and became the magistrate of Zhecheng County. Zhuang, having completed his term as a teacher, returned to his hometown. This matter was spoken by Zhuang himself.

    437

    兰岩曰：邪不敌正，理固然也。邱数未终，奈何以愤怒而欲置之死地？其自毙也，不亦宜哉？天下如此类者，当自警也。

    　　额 都　司参领德公，世裔也。夫人傅蔡氏，为额都司姊，生一子二女。初居灵椿坊，后徙城南之泡子河。院宇幽深，闾门壮丽，为一方甲第之冠。但多怪异，家人至日暮，非作队不敢行。厩马十数匹，例一夜两惊。公子新娶妇，亦世阀女，年甫二九。未匝月，忽病癫痫，歌哭无恒，或裸跣奔驰，不避臧获。每至漏下，则闭户向隅，垂首衣笥间，两手常忙，不测何务。至午夜，必将一纸裹收衣笥中，封志其秘。婢子偶潜伺之，即诟谇骂詈，滚地娇啼。公子耻之，而亦无如之何。若是者半载余矣。

    Explanation

    437

    Lan Yan said: Evil cannot overcome good, it is the natural order. The days have not yet come to an end; how can one act in anger and seek to destroy them? Their own downfall, is it not fitting? Those in the world who act in such ways should take warning.

    The Righteous Military Commissioner, General-in-Chief De Gong, was of noble lineage. His wife, Fu Cai, was the sister of General-in-Chief De. They had one son and two daughters. Initially, they lived in Lingchun Lane, but later moved to the Baozi River in the southern part of the city. The courtyard was deep and secluded, and the gate was magnificent, making it the most prestigious residence in the area. However, there were many strange occurrences; by sunset, the family would not dare to go out unless in a group. There were ten or more horses in the stable, and they were startled twice each night by some unknown force. The general's son, who had recently married a woman from a prominent family, was just eighteen years old. Not even a month had passed when she suddenly fell ill with epilepsy, singing and crying without regularity, sometimes running naked and barefoot, disregarding the servants. Every night, she would close the door and face a corner, hanging her head in the chest, with her hands always busy, no one knowing what she was doing. By midnight, she would always wrap a piece of paper and place it in the chest, sealing it as a secret. A maid occasionally secretly watched her, and she would scold and curse, rolling on the ground and crying pitifully. The son felt ashamed of her condition but did not know what to do. Such things had been happening for half a year or more.

    438

    会额都司引见入都，下榻于厅之东院，院外即邻马厩，委装日，德公为作软脚局。饮半酣，德曰：“舍下多鬼，夜间独宿，得无惧乎？”额曰：“我辈作武将者，皆亡命徒，死且不避，庸惧鬼哉！”各大笑。夜半酒阑，各归寝所。额劳顿且醉，一夜酣眠，日高方起。德见其无事，心颇安。越三宿矣。第四宿，额方偃息在床，闻顶隔上窸窣有声，额心悸，起身点烛，坐以听之，久乃阒然。遂不复息烛，仍引衾卧。一食顷，声又大作，仰视望板，若有人踏之以行，渐至东北隅，声忽止，屋角一板乱动，随被揭去，有黑物下垂，形如马尾，长尺余。去灯远，恍惚不能辨，而毛发森竖，不克自壮。但瞠目视之而已。

    Explanation

    438

    The governor of the province was summoned to the capital for an audience and lodged in the eastern courtyard of the hall. The courtyard was next to a stable. On the day of unpacking his belongings, Lord De arranged a banquet with soft drinks. After drinking to a moderate degree, Lord De said, "There are many ghosts in my house. Do you not fear sleeping alone at night?" Governor E replied, "We who are warriors are all fugitives. We do not even fear death, how could we fear ghosts?" They both laughed loudly. In the middle of the night, when the wine had run out, each returned to their sleeping place. Governor E was tired and drunk, and he slept soundly all night, waking up late in the morning. Lord De saw that he was fine and felt relieved. After three nights, on the fourth night, Governor E was lying down in bed. He heard a rustling sound above the ceiling. Governor E was startled and got up, lit a candle, and sat to listen. After a while, it became quiet again. He then did not extinguish the candle and continued to lie under the quilt. After a meal's time, the sound became loud again. He looked up at the ceiling and it seemed as if someone was walking on it, gradually moving towards the northeast corner. The sound suddenly stopped, and a board in the corner of the house began to move. It was then lifted away, and a black object hung down, shaped like a horse's tail, about a foot long. Far from the light, it was too dim to see clearly, and his hair stood on end. He could not muster courage and could only stare at it.

    439

    俄而黑物渐长，黑尽继之以白，色如粉，才三四指阔，瞥见二眼，大如榧，方知是一人头颅也。大惧，欲呼人，忽转念人何怕鬼；且畴昔自诩之言，人悉闻之，一旦示弱，将何以见人？于是正心以观之。此时物已出其半面，渐露鼻口，二目绿色，直视灯，灯光敛如豆。头昏昏，如梦魇，四肢不能转移。物遂釶然而下，似旋风透户而出，灯骤明，额飙然如梦醒，但闻院前厩马惊嘶，户牖扃闭如故。谯楼漏三下矣。料其去必复返，亟移灯近榻，抽刀置枕畔，著衣踩靴而卧。辗转不能交睫。至五更，墙外厩马重惊，竹树吼风，而物已入室。灯复黯淡无光，物径扑卧榻，额大叫，捉刀斫之，謣然一声如裂帛，案翻烛灭，随闻屋上逢逢，逾刻方息。额惫极而睡。

    Explanation

    439

    Shortly after, a dark object began to grow, and as the darkness faded, it was followed by a white color, like powder, only a few fingers wide. Suddenly, I saw two eyes, as big as chestnuts, and then I realized it was a human head. Greatly frightened, I wanted to call for help, but suddenly thought that people have no reason to fear ghosts; and since everyone has heard of my past boasts, if I were to show weakness, how would I face people? So, I calmed my heart and observed it. At this time, the object had revealed half of its face, gradually showing its nose and mouth, with two green eyes staring directly at the lamp, causing the light to dim like a bean. My head felt dazed, like being in a nightmare, and my limbs were unable to move. The object suddenly descended, like a whirlwind passing through the door, and the lamp suddenly brightened, my forehead felt as if I had woken up from a dream, but I only heard the horses in the courtyard neighing in fright, the doors and windows were still closed as before. The drum of the watchtower struck three times. I thought it would surely return, so I quickly moved the lamp closer to the bed, drew a knife and placed it beside the pillow, put on clothes and boots, and lay down. I turned restlessly and could not close my eyes. Until the fifth watch of the night, the horses in the stable outside the wall neighed again, bamboo and trees rustling in the wind, and the object had already entered the room. The lamp dimmed again and went out, the object directly pounced on the bed, I shouted loudly, grabbed the knife and slashed at it, a loud sound like tearing silk, the table overturned and the candle went out, then I heard a knocking sound on the roof, after a while, it stopped. I was extremely exhausted and fell asleep.

    440

    次日，拉主人于僻处，备述夜来事，因劝移居以避其厉，且曰：“焉知甥媳之病，非凶宅有以致之？”德曰：“余亦不耐久矣，苦无吉宅耳。”额曰：“何宅不吉于此？予友萨都统之宅，方觅售主，可相也。”德即以三千金易券焉。及迁，新妇大哭不往，公子仗剑恐吓之，白足蓬首而奔，侍儿强为作履袜，纳之车中。处新居，上下贻然，鸡犬不惊，并安群马。新妇病亦骤愈，侍女发其笥，于纸裹内，得五色线绠，长四五尺，大如箭幹，不测何用，问之亦茫然。诸姊妹或述其疯狂之态，羞赧无以自容。额后官至副总戎。德故宅，连更数主，胥不相安，今废为菜圃矣。

    Explanation

    440

    The next day, the narrator took Mr. De to a secluded place and recounted the events of the previous night in detail. He then advised Mr. De to move to avoid the evil influence of the house, saying, "How can we know that the illness of your niece-in-law is not caused by the evil of this house?" Mr. De replied, "I also cannot bear it for long; only that there is no good house available." The narrator said, "What house is not good compared to this one? My friend, Marshal Sa, has a house that is looking for a buyer. It might be suitable." Mr. De immediately exchanged three thousand taels of silver for the deed. Upon moving, the new wife cried bitterly and refused to go. The young master threatened her with a sword, causing her to run away barefoot and with disheveled hair. The maidservant forced her to put on shoes and socks and placed her in the carriage. In the new house, everyone was uneasy, but neither chickens nor dogs were startled, and even the horses were calm. The new wife's illness also suddenly improved. The maidservant opened her chest and found a rope made of multicolored threads, about four to five feet long and as thick as an arrow shaft. It was unclear what it was used for, and when asked, they were also clueless. Various sisters described her insane state, and she felt ashamed and could not bear to face them. Later, the narrator was promoted to the rank of Deputy General. Mr. De's old house, after being passed through several owners, all found it uncomfortable, and now it has been abandoned and turned into a vegetable garden.

    441

    兰岩曰：居宅凶而人遭祟，可不慎欤？

    　　孝女京师崇文门外花院市，居民数千家，皆制蓪草像生花为业。有幼女奉老爷以居者，亦业此。父久病痰喘，按时举发，是年愈甚，吼卧不起，医来弗药。女忘啜废寝，明慰暗忧。

    　　适有邻媪，鸠诸妇女，往丫髻山进香者，女因密询进香何为，媪曰：“或以多病，或以乏嗣，各以心愿求之。山顶娘娘最灵感，应之如响。”女曰：“此间去山，道里几何？”曰：“百余里耳。”女曰：“一里几何？”曰：“三百六十步。”女谨志之。夜俟父安寝，则潜于院中，持香一柱，计其里数，绕院而拜，默祝：“一身孱弱，父病甚，家中更无人，不能朝山进香。谨按里数，一步一拜，有如身到宝山，亲瞻圣像，保佑老父，沉疴速起，百岁康强，自愿绣佛长斋，终身顶礼。”云云。如是得闲则拜，日夕不辍，半月有余。

    Explanation

    441

    Translation:

    Lan Yan said: A house being inauspicious and people being afflicted by misfortune—how can one not be cautious?

    In the flower market outside Chongwen Gate in the capital, there was a filial daughter who lived there. The area was home to thousands of families, all engaged in making artificial flowers from paper grass as their profession. Among them was a young girl who lived with her father, also in this trade. Her father had been suffering from chronic phlegm and wheezing for a long time, and his condition had worsened that year. He lay coughing and motionless, unable to get up, and when doctors came, no medicine was given. The daughter forgot to eat and neglected sleep, showing comfort outwardly but worrying inwardly.

    One day, a neighbor, an elderly woman, assembled the women to go to Yaji Mountain to burn incense. The girl secretly asked the woman what it meant to burn incense, and the woman replied, "Some do it because of illness, others because they lack children. Each prays for their heart's desire. The goddess on the mountain top is most responsive, answering prayers as quickly as an echo." The girl asked, "How far is it from here to the mountain?" The woman replied, "About a hundred li." The girl asked, "How many steps are in one li?" The woman replied, "Three hundred and sixty steps." The girl carefully noted this. At night, when her father was asleep, she would quietly go into the courtyard, hold a column of incense, count the distance, and circle the courtyard while kneeling, silently praying, "I am weak, my father is very ill, and there is no one else at home. I cannot go to the mountain to burn incense. I will follow the distance, kneeling down once every step, as if I have reached the treasure mountain, personally seeing the holy image. I pray for my old father to quickly recover from his serious illness and live a hundred years in good health. I voluntarily embroider Buddhas and practice long-term vegetarianism, worshiping devoutly for life." She continued like this, kneeling whenever she had a moment, day and night without ceasing, for more than half a month.

    442

    旧说丫髻山上祀奉山顶碧霞元君之神，灵应昭于畿辅。上自大内后妃、中使及王公缙绅，下迄庶民，每际四月，则进香赛会者，车马络绎，不绝于道。而五更鸡鸣时，即上殿拈香者，谓之上头香，头香必待宫使巨珰，他人罔敢僭越。

    　　时有中涓魏公者，奉皇太后旨，往降头香。甫辟殿门，即有香在炉中，光焰甚盛。魏怒责庙主曰：“老佛爷香，犹未降，何得令他人预焚于此！”庙主惶恐曰：“爷不来，殿不敢开，诚不解此香何由至。”魏默念：“初至时，殿始振管，香灰未寸，良可骇异。明日更早来，看如何。”亟嘱庙主，曰：“既往不咎矣。汝其敬慎，翌日当早来上头香也。”遂去。

    Explanation

    442

    Traditionally, it is said that on Mount Yajiji, the Queen of Heaven (Bixia Yuanjun) is worshipped at the summit, and her spiritual influence is widely recognized in the vicinity of the imperial capital. From the empresses, eunuchs, and high-ranking officials of the imperial court to common people, every April, those who come to offer incense and participate in religious festivals travel in a continuous stream, never ceasing along the road. At the early morning hour when roosters crow, those who ascend to the temple hall to offer incense are said to be offering the first incense, which is reserved for high-ranking imperial officials; others dare not overstep their bounds.

    At that time, there was a high-ranking eunuch named Wei, who, at the command of the Empress Dowager, went to offer the first incense. As soon as the temple doors were opened, he found incense already burning in the censer, with a very bright flame. Wei angrily scolded the temple master, saying, "The Empress Dowager's incense has not yet been offered, how could someone else have lit it here beforehand?" The temple master, terrified, replied, "If you hadn't come, the temple wouldn't have been opened. I truly don't understand how this incense came to be here." Wei silently thought, "When I first arrived, the temple doors were just being opened, and the incense ash was less than an inch long. This is truly startling. I will come even earlier tomorrow to see what happens." He hastily instructed the temple master, saying, "There will be no punishment for past actions. You must be respectful and cautious. You should come early the next day to offer the first incense." Then he left.

    443

    庙主畏罪，与其徒终夜巡视。才四更，魏已至，至则炉中香火宛然，一女子方礼拜于地，咸大惊。女闻人声惊起，倏忽不见。众以为鬼，魏公曰：“岂有神圣之前，鬼敢公然出现者乎？必有因，吾有以处之矣。”遂上二香于山门下，踞胡床，聚众香客而告之，并详其年岁、貌容、肥色，众客愕然，但以为奇。卒一媪闻之，沉思曰：“据爷所见，无乃吾之邻女乎？何其色色相符也。”魏问：“是何邻女，幻化若此。”媪曰：“居花院市，固孝女也。”魏拊髀曰：“无疑矣。”亟驰归，复命讫，密访得女。就见之，果殿中所见者。致诘其女，悉以诚告，言虽未出门，恍惚身历其境。父病获痊，亦神之灵也。魏叹曰：“至诚感神，真纯孝也。”认为义女，不啻所出。

    Explanation

    443

    The temple master, fearing punishment, patrolled all night with his disciples. Just before the fourth watch, Wei had arrived, and upon his arrival, the incense and fire in the stove were still burning, and a woman was found worshipping on the ground, which greatly surprised everyone. The woman, startled by the sound of people, suddenly disappeared. The crowd thought it was a ghost, but Wei said, "How could a ghost dare to appear openly before a sacred being? There must be a reason, and I have a way to deal with it." He then burned two incense sticks at the temple gate, sat on a trapeze-like chair, gathered the crowd of incense burners, and told them, detailing the woman's age, appearance, and demeanor. The crowd was startled but thought it was a strange occurrence. An old woman, upon hearing this, thoughtfully said, "According to what your lord saw, isn't it my neighbor's daughter? How closely does her appearance match!" Wei asked, "What neighbor's daughter, and how could she be so magically transformed?" The old woman replied, "She lives in the flower market, and she is a filial daughter." Wei slapped his thigh and said, "There's no doubt about it." He quickly returned home, reported back, and secretly found the girl. When he saw her, it was indeed the girl he had seen in the temple. He questioned her, and she answered truthfully, saying that although she had not left her house, she had experienced it as if she were there. Her father's illness was cured, which was also a sign of the deity's benevolence. Wei sighed and said, "Sincere devotion moves the gods; she is truly a filial daughter." He then adopted her as his daughter, treating her as if she were his own.

    444

    其父得享三十年温饱，百岁令终。女嫁于大兴张氏子，妆奁之盛，不下数千金，皆魏独任。婿家缘此，累世为富商云。

    　　兰岩曰：真诚所至，神灵可通，骄若寺人，那不令其佩服。虽然，人苟无良，遇如不遇矣。

    　　请仙予闲览《太平广记》及志异诸书，其所载怪异之事，不胜枚举。又每闻人所言，亦皆凿凿可据，心窃信而又疑之。疑其无，则古人无论矣，即今人之言，亦多朴城谨悫之士，岂肯以欺人语？信为有，而予生四十年矣，曷曾未一目睹也？

    Explanation

    444

    The father enjoyed thirty years of comfort and peace, passing away at the age of a hundred. The daughter married into the Zhang family of Daxing, and her dowry was not less than thousands of gold pieces, all arranged by Wei alone. The husband's family, due to this, became wealthy merchants for generations.

    Lan Yan said: Sincerity can reach the divine, even if one is as arrogant as a eunuch, how could he not be convinced? However, if a person lacks virtue, even if they encounter something, it is as if they did not. Asking the divine to browse leisurely through Taiping Guangji and other books of strange tales, the strange and unusual events recorded in these books are too numerous to list. Additionally, every time I hear people speak of them, they are all solid and verifiable. I secretly believe in them but also doubt them. If I doubt their existence, then we won't even mention the ancients. Even the accounts of modern people are mostly those of honest and cautious individuals, how could they lie? Believing they exist, yet I have lived for forty years, how often have I personally seen them?

    445

    惟忆从先君子随宦于宜君时，先大父摄篆乌兰，先父母奉祖母留居宜君置中，适县君张公荐一戏术人来。观其术，平平耳，先君子以张公故，与白金二两遣之。术人不受，曰：“今日作戏法，环观者数十百人，而未有一啧啧称叹者，是所学不足以惊人目可知也，何敢妄邀厚赏？虽然，小人少遇异人，得隆仙之术，今夜请献之，或博太太一粲也。”先君许之，赐之酒食。日薄暮，术人择园中废轩三楹，洒扫至洁，窗破者补之，壁剥者垩之，凡有穴隙如针孔大，亦必弥缝完密，更张帐幔，以蔽户牖。灯后，于西壁画一门，如圭窦状。门前设一矮脚几，几上置一炉，焚紫降，不令烟绝。余无他物。选秀慧童子十五岁者二人，丫其髻而赤其脚，名之曰：“清风明月，”使背立几前。先祖母率姑母、先母及诸姊妹，垂筠帘，坐于东楹，先君子率予兄弟翼坐帘外。僮仆辈有令观者，有不令观者，一惟术人之命是听。

    Explanation

    445

    I recall the time when my late father served as an official in Yijun, and my late grandfather temporarily held the seal of power in Wulan. My parents, accompanied by my grandmother, remained in Yijun. At that time, County Official Zhang recommended a person skilled in magic to come. When we observed his performance, it was quite ordinary. However, due to the recommendation of County Official Zhang, my father gave him two taels of silver to send him away. The magician declined the reward, saying, "Today's performance was watched by dozens or even hundreds of people, yet none expressed any admiration or praise. This shows that my skills are not impressive enough to astonish anyone, so how dare I rashly seek a generous reward? Nevertheless, I have once met a mysterious figure who taught me advanced magical techniques. Tonight, I will present them to you, perhaps to bring a smile to your face." My father agreed and provided him with wine and food. As dusk approached, the magician chose an abandoned pavilion in the garden, cleaned it thoroughly, repaired broken windows, plastered peeling walls, and sealed even the smallest gaps as tightly as possible. He also hung curtains to block the doors and windows. After lighting the lamps, he drew a door on the western wall, shaped like a small opening. He placed a low table in front of the door and set a brazier on it, burning incense without letting the smoke stop. There were no other objects. He then selected two intelligent boys, aged fifteen, with their hair tied in buns and their feet bare, and named them "Clear Wind" and "Bright Moon." He made them stand with their backs to the table. My late grandmother, aunt, mother, and sisters sat behind a bamboo curtain in the eastern pavilion, while my father and I, along with my brothers, sat on either side of the curtain. Some servants were allowed to watch, while others were not, and all followed the magician's instructions.

    446

    漏三下，术人炷香于炉，焚符于烛，教二童俯身从胯下反视几下圭窦，问曰：“何所见？”曰：“开门矣。”术人即噙水向壁上噀之，复亟问：“何如？”童曰：“梳头矣。匀面矣。”既而曰：“易履矣，着衣矣。”术人曰：“然则可以出矣。”噙水三噀之，瞥见一女子立几后，约长五尺许，衣大红衫，拖素裙，眉目娟好，微笑作羞耻态。术人嘱曰：“太太在此，可为礼。”女则裣衽再拜。术人曰：“太太最尊贵，胡为不行全礼，乃以一福了事耶？”女子以袖掩口，微笑不动。术人亦笑曰：“是见人多怕羞矣。”令童向前，把其袖牵出几外。童子力牵，女子力却，相持良久。术人作急状，止之曰：“彼修仙人，性村野，且放手，我自有处。”童子乃放手，女子仍还故处。

    Explanation

    446

    At the third strike of the night clock, the magician lit incense in the brazier and burned talismans in the candle. He instructed two children to bend over and look back through the small opening under the table, asking, "What do you see?" The children replied, "The door is open." The magician then held water in his mouth and spat it onto the wall, and quickly asked again, "What now?" The children said, "She is combing her hair. She is applying makeup." Shortly after, they said, "She is changing her shoes. She is putting on clothes." The magician said, "Then she can come out." He spat water three times onto the wall, and suddenly a woman appeared standing behind the table. She was about five feet tall, wearing a red blouse and a white skirt, with beautiful eyebrows and eyes. She smiled with a bashful expression. The magician instructed, "The lady is here, you should pay respects." The woman then performed a deep bow. The magician said, "The lady is the most respected, why don't you perform the full ritual, and just do a single bow?" The woman covered her mouth with her sleeve and smiled, not moving. The magician also smiled and said, "She is shy because she sees so many people." He ordered the children to move forward and pull her sleeve, bringing her out from behind the table. The children pulled strongly, and the woman resisted strongly, and they held each other for a long time. The magician looked alarmed and stopped them, saying, "She is a practitioner of Daoism, with a rustic nature, let go, I will handle it." The children then let go, and the woman returned to her original place.

    447

    术人复噀水壁上，倏又一女子出，双鬟垂髫，齿灵于前，貌犹娟妙，衣浅碧衫，腰下衣树叶一围，长尺余，赤双足，而手足指爪，皆长四五寸，并立几后，左顾红衣而笑。术人曰：“汝姊幽居久，习于村野，见太太不复知有礼数。汝最知礼者，可率伊行礼，无失仪，致重我罪累也。”女子乃推前女，绕出几外，捺其头令跪，举止柔媚，观者神痴，拜讫，还故处，术人复以水噀之，随隐。

    　　于是神其术，厚赐而遣之，细询二童子，当牵袖之时，是人耶，非人耶？童子曰：“不知是人与否，但把其臂，如握棉絮，力又微弱，才四五牵扯，已汗出淫淫，娇喘不胜矣。如非术人令放手，再两三扯，即可扯至太太前矣。”予时年十四，至今记之了了，每举以告人，无有能测之者，或谓即障眼法，不足为异。然障眼法，不过能障眼耳，未能有握之有质者，是不可解。

    　　恩茂先曰：此记如善奏口技者，无不逼真。

    Explanation

    447

    The magician spits water on the wall, and suddenly another girl appears. She has two hanging buns, sharp teeth, and a delicate appearance. She wears a light green robe, a leaf skirt around her waist, and is barefoot. Her fingers and toes are all about four to five inches long. They stand together behind a table, looking to the left at the red-clad figure and laughing. The magician says, "Your sister has been living in seclusion for a long time, has become accustomed to rural life, and does not know how to behave properly when meeting Lady. You are the most polite one, can lead her in performing the ritual, do not make any mistakes in etiquette, and do not bring additional blame upon me." The girl then pushes the previous girl, steps out from behind the table, presses her head to make her kneel. Her movements are gentle and charming, and the spectators are enchanted. After the bowing is completed, they return to their original place. The magician spits water again, and they disappear immediately.

    Thus, the magician's skill is revered, and he is given generous rewards and sent away. He asks the two children in detail, when holding hands, is it a person or not? The child says, "I don't know if it's a person, but holding her arm feels like holding cotton, the strength is weak, after a few pulls, sweat starts to flow profusely, and she breathes heavily and weakly. If the magician hadn't ordered to let go, a few more pulls would have brought her to Lady's presence." I was fourteen at the time, and I still remember it clearly, every time I tell others, no one can understand it. Some say it's just an illusion, not worth being surprised about. However, the illusion only deceives the eyes, and it's unexplainable that it feels tangible when holding it.

    En Maoxian said, "This record is like a skilled performer of oral arts, everything is very realistic."

    448

    某　太　医太医某，大兴人，失其姓名。轻裘肥马，日奔走于九门，以是致富。延者日积于门，非日晡不到病家，不顾病者之望眼穿也。每视一病，写一方，不论效不效，例奉千钱，否则不至也。日暮归，从人马后，囊橐尽满，人或怪其来迟，则色然曰：“甫从某王、某公主、某大老爷府宅中来。”盖非一时势位炫赫者，不肯流诸齿颊也。人无如之何，任之而已。

    　　一日，看病归，独宿斋中，梦见一人，若甚相熟，而不记姓名，持片纸付之曰：“时日至，所负当见还矣。”医取纸反复检视，空无一字，怀惑间，已失其人所在。惊而寤，听漏声三下，家人叩户报孺人生子矣。医毛发森竖，心知子为索债者，特未审所负几何耳。子既长，忤逆异常，视父母如寇仇，看钱财如粪土，日向母索钱百文，顷刻即尽。积十余年，家渐落，母或稍吝，则裂眦相向，势将用武。母惧而复给之，不敢面斥，阴诉医，医闭目摇首曰：“勿再言，此子使我心胆坠地。

    Explanation

    448

    A court physician, a native of Daxing, whose name has been lost, lived a life of luxury, clad in light fur coats and riding fat horses. He frequented the imperial court daily, amassing great wealth. Patients waited for him, but he rarely visited them, showing no concern for their longing gazes. For each visit, he wrote a prescription and demanded a fixed payment of 1,000 coins, regardless of the outcome. At dusk, he returned home, his bags filled with money. When people wondered why he was late, he boasted, "I just came from the home of a certain prince, princess, or high-ranking official." He would not mention anyone unless they were of high status. People could do nothing about it, so they let it be.

    One day, after visiting a patient, he stayed alone in his study. He dreamt of a person who seemed familiar, though he couldn't recall their name. The person handed him a piece of paper and said, "The time has come, and what you owe will be returned." The physician took the paper and examined it, but it was blank. Confused, he looked around, but the person was gone. He woke up, startled, and heard the clock strike three. His servant knocked on the door to announce that his wife had given birth to a son. The physician's hair stood on end, realizing that the child was the one to whom he owed money. He did not know how much he owed, however.

    As the child grew, he became increasingly disobedient, treating his parents like enemies and money like dirt. Every day, he demanded 100 coins from his mother, which he spent immediately. After more than ten years, the family gradually fell into poverty. When his mother was slightly reluctant to give him money, he would glare at her, ready to use violence. Fearing him, she continued to give him money, never daring to scold him openly. She secretly complained to the physician, who closed his eyes and shook his head, saying, "Do not speak of it again. This child has made my heart and courage drop to the ground."

    449

    ”因以所梦告妻，妻惊曰：“有字之券，或可量力取偿；若无字之券，知负彼几何。宁有穷斯耶”老奴以药杀人，不知几许，新鬼繁冤旧鬼哭，此子必其酋也。彼奉冥檄，挟恨而来，敢与之较耶？“言次大恸曰：”老奴草菅人命，毒心应手，致获此报。牵率老娘，以致于此，老奴已矣，抑念老娘何辜乎？“妾从旁慰谢曰：”大郎虽不肖，小郎行当成立，何必反目？“妻唾其面曰：”呸，汝痴心，尚过望耶？天之报施老奴者，如此不爽，纵有百子，亦必沆瀣一气，岂复有以德报怨者？“医默然无以应，条釺而已。

    Explanation

    449

    He then told his wife about the dream, and she was shocked, saying, "With a written contract, one might be able to repay according to one's ability; but with an unwritten agreement, how can one know how much is owed? How can there be an end to this?" The old servant had killed people with medicine, not knowing how many, and the cries of new ghosts added to those of old ghosts. This one must be the leader. He came with a summons from the underworld, carrying grudges, so how dare one argue with him? As he spoke, he cried out in great sorrow, "The old servant treated human lives as straw, his evil intentions manifesting easily, leading to this retribution. Dragging my mother into this, leading to this, the old servant is finished, but think of my mother, what has she done wrong?" The wife comforted and apologized from the side, saying, "The eldest son is unworthy, but the younger son will eventually succeed, why should we turn against each other?" The wife spat in his face, saying, "Pah, your foolish heart still hopes? The heavens' retribution against the old servant is so precise, even if he had a hundred children, they would all be corrupt, how could there be anyone who repays grievances with kindness?" The doctor remained silent, unable to respond, only tightening the bandage.

    450

    又十余年，一夕，复梦其人至，言：“债负已清，可还汝券，然尚欠一命，会须同见冥王。”医醒而大病，自知不起，乃以其语告妻，嘱托后事。阅二日，其子暴死。医泣曰：“时至矣。”夜分果亡。少子亦不肖，遂落魄，啼饥号寒，迄今不止云。

    　　闲斋曰：庸医杀人，当获此报。特一人之债易偿，多人之命难抵，轮回堕落，尚有穷期耶？医之不能有活人手，而影响脉理以渔利者，睹此惨报，未识亦肯稍袖毒手否？

    　　兰岩曰：庸医杀人，罪不容死，况趋势贪利，虽不以病者为事，潦草匆忙，以药人者乎？病家之心如焚，而医人之视若戏，死者虽属天数，庸不冤乎？耗其财，索其命，报亦惨哉！

    Explanation

    450

    After more than ten years, one night, he dreamed of the person again, who said, "Your debts have been cleared, and you can retrieve your bond. However, there is still one life owed, and you will need to meet the King of Hell together." The doctor woke up with a serious illness, knowing he would not recover. He told his wife about the dream and arranged for his affairs after death. Two days later, his son died suddenly. The doctor cried, "The time has come." He died at midnight. His younger son was also unfilial, becoming destitute and suffering from hunger and cold, a state that has not stopped to this day.

    The mediocre doctor who kills people should receive this retribution. Especially, a single person's debt is easy to repay, but many lives are hard to repay. The cycle of rebirth and fall, is there still an end to it? The doctor cannot have the power to save lives, and those who affect pulse diagnosis for profit, seeing this terrible retribution, would they be willing to slightly restrain their harmful actions? The crime does not deserve death, especially following trends and seeking profit, even if they do not take the patient's illness seriously, being careless and hasty in treating patients with medicine. The patient's heart is burning with anxiety, while the doctor treats it as a game. The dead are determined by fate, is it not also a pity? They spend their money and take their lives, and the retribution is also terrible.

    451

    地震老人相传，雍正庚戌岁，京师地震之前一日，西域一人，抱三四岁小儿入茶肆，甫及门，小儿辄抱其颈，啼不肯入。其人怪之曰：“畏此地人多耶？”乃之他肆。至则复啼，易地皆然。其人以为异，问：“汝平日极喜入茶社食蜜果，今日胡为乎尔？”儿曰：“今日各肆卖茶人，及吃茶人，皆各颈带铁锁，故不欲入。且今日往来街市之人，何带锁者之多耶？”其人笑其妄，路遇一相识问所之，白其故，大笑而去，儿哂曰：“彼亦被锁，尚笑人耶！”其人归，逢所知则告之，或言小儿眼净，所见必有因，伺之可也。小儿有堂兄二人，儿亦惊其有锁。次日地大震，人居倾毁无数，凡小儿不入之肆，无不摧折，竟无一人得免。二兄亦为墙所压。访所遇相识，已履屋下矣。劫数之不可逃也，类如此。

    Explanation

    451

    Translation

    The story of the Earthquake Elder is passed down: In the year of Gengxu during the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, one day before the earthquake in the capital city of Beijing, a man from the western regions was carrying a child of about three or four years old into a teahouse. As soon as they reached the door, the child clung to his neck, crying and refusing to enter. The man was surprised and asked, "Are you afraid of the crowd here?" He then took the child to another teahouse. When they arrived, the child cried again, and this happened in every place they went. The man found this strange and asked, "You usually love entering teahouses to eat honey-coated fruits, why are you acting like this today?" The child replied, "Today, in every teahouse, the tea sellers and tea drinkers all had iron locks around their necks, so I didn't want to go in. And why are there so many people with locks today?" The man laughed at the child's nonsense. On the road, he met an acquaintance and explained the reason, and the acquaintance laughed heartily and left. The child said mockingly, "He was also locked up, still laughing at others!" The man returned home and told those he knew. Some said the child's eyes were clean, what he saw must have a reason, and it would be good to wait and see. The child had two cousins, and he was also surprised to see them with locks. The next day, there was a great earthquake, and countless homes collapsed. All the teahouses where the child had not entered were destroyed, and no one escaped. The two cousins were also crushed by the walls. When he visited the acquaintances he had met, they were already lying under the houses. Destiny cannot be escaped, just like this.

    452

    兰岩曰：事之所有，未必非理之所无。

    　　朱　佩　砓宜君塘汛兵朱佩茞，有甥女，为农家妇，居焦家坪。嫁半年，方姅，梦见一人，耏然青面，欣然赤帻，来与妇交。凡三夜，妇遂有娠。腹中时时蠕动，动则痛楚欲绝，大声呼号。其姑以少年妇，比邻密迩，禁令隐忍。妇不能将顺，致相勃谿.邻媪闻之来劝，见妇惊曰：“媳妇有妖胎，会将摩厉以须，毋复聒之，使不安静也。”姑始大惧，使其夫告母家，母躬往守视之。及临蓐，转侧叫号，四邻皆塞耳走避。久之，产一物，人首蛇身，发赤色，长三尺余，面目如粉，首尾及寻，见人则笑，众皆惊奔，无敢入房者，每就乳哺，妇遽惊绝。

    Explanation

    452

    Lan Yan said: What exists in the world may not necessarily contradict reason.

    　　Zhu Pei, a military officer stationed at Yijunta, had a niece who was a farmer's wife and lived in Jiajiaping. After she had been married for half a year, she was pregnant and dreamed of a man with a green face and a red headdress, who came to have intercourse with her. Over three nights, the woman became pregnant. Her abdomen would often move, and when it did, she would be in excruciating pain, crying out loudly. Her mother-in-law, thinking she was a young wife with nearby neighbors, forbade her from complaining. The woman could not comply and this led to a quarrel. A neighbor heard about it and came to persuade her. Seeing the woman, the neighbor was shocked and said, "Your daughter-in-law has a demon pregnancy; the demon will be sharpening its tools, so do not disturb it, or it will not be at peace." The mother-in-law was greatly frightened and sent her son to inform her family. The woman's mother went to guard and watch over her. When it was time for childbirth, the woman turned and called out, and the neighbors all plugged their ears and ran away. After a long time, she gave birth to a creature with a human head and a snake body, red hair, over three feet long, with a face like powder, from head to tail. When it saw people, it would laugh, and everyone was shocked and ran away, no one dared to enter the room. Whenever it came to nurse, the woman would be suddenly terrified and die.

    453

    适朱来探甥女，甫入门，遇其妹摇手止之，拉入草堂，告其所怪。朱曰：“既以为妖，盍杀之？”妹曰：“终日蟠踞儿侧，投鼠亦当忌器也。”朱试往观，物方蟠屈，闭目如睡，朱潜解佩刀突前，握物之发，拖之出房。物惊寤，瞠目张口，声磕磕如击石，蜿蜒缠朱左股。众遥立喧呼。朱刀已落，血蓝色，淋漓满衣，腥气入脑。朱复剥其皮而卷之，曰：“吾正需此以鞔三弦也。”两家深感之，妇亦至今无恙。

    Explanation

    453

    Zhu came to visit his niece and had just entered the door when he encountered his sister, who waved her hand to stop him and dragged him into a simple dwelling. She told him what she found strange. Zhu said, "Since it is considered a demon, why not kill it?" His sister replied, "It spends all day coiled by the child's side. We should fear collateral damage when throwing at a mouse." Zhu went to take a look and saw that the thing was coiling and bending, with closed eyes as if sleeping. Zhu secretly unsheathed his sword and stepped forward, grasping the thing's hair and dragging it out of the room. The thing startled awake, staring wide-eyed and opening its mouth, making a clattering sound like striking stone, and winding around Zhu's left thigh. The crowd stood at a distance, shouting. Zhu's sword had fallen, and the blue blood dripped all over his clothes, the stench entering his brain. Zhu again peeled its skin and rolled it up, saying, "I just need this to stretch the strings of a three-stringed instrument." The two families were deeply grateful, and the woman is still unharmed to this day.

    454

    纸钱友人护军景君禄，居近城北，一夕，同其友富海归家，路经灵官庙，漏已三下。倏见二粉蝶，翩翩飞绕，去地二尺余。时际隆冬，且深夜，乌得有蝶？就视之，则二纸钱也。并无风，相去咫尺，旋转对舞不已，大以为怪。适一人骑马自西来，马耳耸鼻鸣，连鞭不进，其人厉声问：“二人胡为者？”景指纸钱令观之。击拆老军过而诫之曰：“各走路，何管闲事。即此一席地，已倒毙二人矣。”骑者惧，疾驰而去。景、富皆少年好事，直追随纸钱，至人家矮墙下，旋入狗窦中，始散。是年富死，又二年景亦亡。

    Explanation

    454

    One evening, my friend Jing Junlu, who lived near the northern part of the city, was returning home with his friend Fu Hai. They passed by the Lingguan Temple, and it was already past three o'clock. Suddenly, they saw two white butterflies, fluttering gracefully around them, about two feet above the ground. It was the middle of a severe winter, and it was late at night, so how could there be butterflies? They looked at it closely, and it turned out to be two pieces of paper money. There was no wind, yet they were within a foot's distance, spinning and dancing continuously. They greatly found it strange. Just then, a man riding a horse came from the west. The horse's ears stood up, and it snorted, but the rider whipped the horse repeatedly, and it wouldn't move forward. The rider shouted, "What are you two doing?" Jing pointed to the paper money and asked him to look. An old soldier passed by and warned them, "Each go your own way, why meddle in others' affairs. Right here on this spot, two people have already died." The rider was afraid and galloped away quickly. Jing and Fu were both young and curious, so they followed the paper money directly, to the base of a low wall around a house, where they turned and entered a dog hole, and finally dispersed. That year, Fu died, and two years later, Jing also died.

    455

    兰岩曰：岂以二殍之故，而示其怪耶？抑二纸钱作祟以毙人耶？是不可解。

    　　三　李　明光山李明，家素贫，佣舂糊口。邑有监生钟秀者，他出值雨，避檐下，明延入，具酌与语。秀大悦，遂订交于杵臼间。自此时相往还，迭为宾主。一日，邻家失火，殃及秀家，明奔至，冒烟突火以救，须眉皆尽。秀获不死，于是交愈深。

    　　会秀欲赴南昌总戎幕，拉明同往。买舟南下，于道上忽遇风飓，舟覆，人尽溺，秀为一山西客所拯。客亦贾于南昌者，遂与同载。秀深感之，叩客姓名，则亦李明也。前李明尸，竟不获，秀哭之哀恸，如丧考妣。至湖口，客遇其乡人，得母讣，恸绝，亟返棹。语秀曰：“方寸乱矣，不暇为君谋，谨馈八金为赆，请从此别。”秀力叩问，客舟已扬帆远去矣。秀伫立以泣。疾且作，僦居一兰若中，优枕匝月。僧厌之，终日絮聒。

    Explanation

    455

    Final Translation

    Lan Yan said: Is it because of the two dead bodies that this strange occurrence is shown? Or is it that the two pieces of paper money are causing harm to kill people? This is incomprehensible.

    Li Ming, a poor man from Guangshan, worked as a hired miller to make a living. In the county, there was a scholar named Zhong Xiu. One day, while out in the rain, Zhong sought shelter under a roof, and Li invited him in, prepared wine, and talked with him. Zhong was very pleased and they established a friendship in the midst of their daily activities. From then on, they visited each other, alternating as host and guest. One day, a fire broke out in a neighboring house and spread to Zhong's home. Li rushed to help, rushing through smoke and fire to save them, and lost his eyebrows and mustache in the process. Zhong was saved, and their friendship deepened.

    Zhong then wanted to go to the military headquarters in Nanchang and invited Li to join him. They bought a boat and headed south, but on the way, they suddenly encountered a storm. The boat capsized, and everyone drowned, except for Zhong, who was rescued by a guest from Shanxi. The guest was also a merchant in Nanchang and allowed Zhong to ride with him. Zhong was deeply grateful and asked the guest for his name, only to find that it was also Li Ming. The previous Li Ming's body was never found, and Zhong was deeply grieved, as if he had lost his parents. When they reached Lake Kou, the guest met someone from his hometown and learned that his mother had died. He was overwhelmed with grief and quickly returned home. He told Zhong, "My heart is in chaos, I have no time to think about you, I will give you eight pieces of gold as a gift, please part ways here." Zhong tried to ask more, but the guest's boat had already set sail. Zhong stood there weeping. His illness worsened, and he rented a place to stay at a Buddhist temple, resting comfortably for a month. The monk was annoyed and nagged at him all day.

    456

    邻有老人闻之，恶僧之忍，忿忿入刹，谓秀曰：“君安用污此清静地，欲望谁发慈悲心耶？”乃率其僮仆，打其行李，并舁秀，悉置其家。为延医进药，旬日大瘥。秀乃顿首而谢曰：“老丈之于鄙人，所谓肉白骨而重生之也。敢请姓名，铭五中以图涓埃之报。”老人正色曰：“我怜君失路，故一援手，岂望报者哉？若夫贱名，则不妨相闻，我李明也，年七十二矣。”秀大异之。老人询其所适，教之曰：“胡不诣夫塘汛？”秀以为然，即诣汛告之。汛兵闻为总戎幕友，奔告所司，因得乘传入南昌。述其所遇于镇公，镇公拊髀而叹，以为奇事焉。秀后称素封。

    　　闲斋曰：三李明不奇，奇在皆于钟有再生恩，皆有恩于钟不奇，奇在秀皆不闻有以报之，而安心素封也。

    　　兰岩曰：是盖夙缘，方能屡遇此义人，扶危济弱耳。前后三人姓名相同，犹为奇异。

    Explanation

    456

    An elderly neighbor heard of the matter, detesting the monk's cruelty, he angrily entered the temple and addressed Xiu, saying, "Why are you defiling this pure place, and who do you wish will show compassion?" Then, he led his servants, attacked Xiu's belongings, and carried Xiu to his own home. He called in a doctor and gave medicine, and after ten days, Xiu greatly improved. Xiu then bowed and thanked, saying, "The elder gentleman to me, is like fleshing out bones and bringing back to life. Dare I ask for your name? I will engrave your name in my heart to repay even the smallest favor." The elderly man replied sternly, "I pitied your misfortune and thus extended a helping hand. I did not expect any repayment! As for my humble name, it is not necessary to mention, I am Li Ming, seventy-two years old." Xiu was greatly surprised. The elderly man inquired about Xiu's destination and advised, "Why not go to the military outpost?" Xiu thought it reasonable and went to the outpost to report. The soldiers there heard that he was a military advisor, and they rushed to inform the relevant authorities, allowing Xiu to travel by official carriage to Nanchang. Xiu recounted his encounter to the governor, who sighed and patted his thigh, considering it a strange and remarkable event. Xiu later lived a simple life.

    Xianzai said, "The three Li Mings are not strange, but the strangeness lies in all having shown the grace of rebirth to Zhong. It is not strange that they all had kindness to Zhong, but the strangeness lies in Xiu not hearing of any way to repay them, and remaining content with a simple life."

    Lanyan said, "This is due to a karmic connection, allowing Xiu to repeatedly meet such righteous people, who help those in danger and the weak. The fact that the names of the three people before and after were the same is still quite unusual."

    457

    霍筠大兴霍管，霍筠，霍筤，皆疡医之子，独筠秀逸姣好，颖慧不凡。不屑屑于本业，年弱冠即喜读书。其父以其梗家教，怒而缚于庭之槐，将痛惩之。

    　　有邻翁姚学究者，适至，惊问：“作何过犯，异常示辱？”其父告以故。姚遽前解释曰：“吾以为面忤腹诽，乖戾子职，乃为读书！所谓狐裘并无羊袖，亟当鼓之舞之，奈何扑作教刑，阻其迈往？君真立意不为贤父兄者？”其父曰：“隳祖宗成业，废家教，岂克肖之子！”姚曰：“彼将相岂有种哉！君幼而逃塾，老犹坑儒耶？”其父不禁失笑。姚问筠曰：“子喜读何书？”筠曰：“时艺耳。”“能详乎？”曰：“能。”“能为之乎？”曰：“能。”“既能为之，必有窗搞，盍出之，一惊老眼？”筠呈一帙，姚且阅且讶曰：“作手也，非时下拾渖者所能辨矣！持此以往，取青紫如拾地芥耳。幸勿施羁勒，俾成其志。”其父本市井，闻姚赞扬，私心窃喜，不复禁止。

    457

    Ho Yun, Ho Guan, and Ho Lang were all sons of a physician of sores. Ho Yun was especially exquisite, elegant, and handsome, with exceptional intelligence and uncommon qualities. Disdainful of his own profession, he began to enjoy reading books at the age of twenty. His father, angered by his defiance of family education, tied him to a locust tree in the courtyard, intending to severely punish him.

    At this moment, an elderly neighbor named Yao Xuezhou happened to arrive and was shocked to see the unusual punishment. He asked, "What wrongdoing has he committed to deserve such disgrace?" Ho Yun's father explained the situation. Yao Xuezhou then stepped forward and said, "I thought he was disobedient and seditious, rebellious as a son, but it turns out he is reading books! The fox coat has no sheep sleeves, and one should encourage and inspire him. Why beat him as a form of punishment and hinder his progress? Are you truly not a good father or elder brother?" Ho Yun's father replied, "He is ruining the ancestors' achievements and neglecting family education. How can he be a worthy son?" Yao Xuezhou countered, "How can generals and ministers be born of a certain kind of family? You ran away from school when young, and still persecute scholars in old age?" Ho Yun's father couldn't help but laugh. Yao Xuezhou then asked Ho Yun, "What kind of books do you like to read?" Ho Yun replied, "Just current literary works." "Can you explain them in detail?" Yao asked. Ho Yun said, "Yes, I can." "Can you write them?" Yao asked. Ho Yun answered, "Yes, I can." "Since you can write them, you must have some notes or drafts. Why not show them to surprise my old eyes?" Ho Yun presented a scroll, and as Yao read it, he was astonished and said, "This is the work of a good writer, not something that can be recognized by those who just copy others! With this, you can achieve high office as easily as picking up mustard seeds from the ground. Please do not restrict him, let him achieve his ambitions." Ho Yun's father, a businessman, secretly rejoiced upon hearing Yao's praise and no longer prohibited him from reading.

    458

    筠自此益加精进，遂成书癖，日把一编，行立不辍，然而赴童子试不中。年十六，其父欲为之娶室，筠自矢曰：“不得功名，终身不娶也。且书中称美女，有螓首蛾眉，倾国倾城，予未见其人也。如世间苟不遇其人，宁鳏居以没世耳。”父母无之如何，渐生厌恶，因悔恨曰：“此皆向日为姚老儒一言所误，致聪颖儿，一朝迂腐致此。吾老矣，岂可使管、筤二子，坐受其累哉！”乃析田分产，使三子各立门户。

    Explanation

    458

    Since then, Jun furthered his efforts and became a bookworm, holding a book in hand every day, never ceasing to read even while walking or standing, yet he failed to pass the examination for young scholars. At the age of sixteen, his father intended to arrange a marriage for him, but Jun swore to himself, saying, "Without achieving fame and success, I will remain unmarried for life. Moreover, the beautiful women described in books have beautiful foreheads and eyebrows, and are so captivating that they could topple a nation, but I have not met such a person. If I do not meet such a person in this world, I would rather live as a bachelor until the end of my life." His parents could do nothing about it and gradually developed disgust, regretting and saying, "All this was due to the one word of the old scholar Yao in the past, leading to the clever son becoming so rigid and impractical in one day. I am old now, how can I let my two sons Guan and Lang suffer from this?" He then divided the land and property, making each of his three sons establish their own households.

    459

    既而父母相继死，管、筤日出行道，颇能自赡，唯筠谋生计拙，日就狼狈。所隶老仆，谏之曰：“二郎勿复读此死书矣，试看大郎三郎，逐日轻裘肥马，不费一毫心力，钱如流水入门。郎不如重理旧业，时向大郎三郎讨论，不过数月，亦可出马矣。何必日夜占毕，徒自苦为？”筠曰：“彼岂有真才实学，能起死回生耶？徒以人命为孤注耳，良心安在？乃欲我效之！且云与彼讨论，即与讨论，亦不过求田问舍，有何可采！汝姑待之，当为汝觅金鱼也。”仆叹曰：“老仆岂不作如是想，第恐行将就木，不克见此荣幸耳。”怏怏而退。筠自讼曰：“予信及豚鱼，而见嗤于妇仆辈，岂其格物易而化人难哉！”

    Explanation

    459

    After the parents died one after another, Guan and Lang traveled daily on the road and were able to support themselves. Only Jun was inept at making a living, and his situation worsened day by day. An old servant who was part of the household advised him, saying, "Second Brother, do not continue reading this useless book. Look at the eldest and third brothers—they have fine clothes and well-fed horses every day, without expending any effort, and money flows into the house like water. You should resume the old business and occasionally discuss with the eldest and third brothers. In no more than a few months, you can also go out and work. Why bother studying day and night, only to make yourself suffer?" Jun replied, "Do they have real talent and knowledge, can they bring the dead back to life? They only gamble their lives, where is their conscience? So why should I follow their example! And talking to them, even if I do, it would only be about buying land and houses, what is there to learn? You just wait and see, I will find a golden fish for you." The servant sighed and said, "I have thought of this, but I fear I will be on my deathbed soon and will not be able to witness this honor." He left in a sullen mood. Jun said to himself, "I have faith even in the smallest creatures, and I am ridiculed by the women and servants. Is it easier to understand things than to transform people?"

    460

    无何，又值试期，治任之通州，一车一僮，老仆为御。辕下驹复蹇劣，首途太晏，甫行二十余里，辄曛暮难进。无止宿所。僮仆方怨咨，忽见林际灯光，自远而近，渐至面前，则一翁一媪，奔走气促。老仆遮问曰：“此间有人家可供宿否？”翁曰：“方有急事，何暇攀谈？”僮曰：“是何要务，败坏至此？”媪且走且应曰：“家有病人，去觅外科耳。”筠于车中闻之，则曰：“我是外科医国手也，何必他求？”媪回首，驻足曰：“莫见诳否？”筠曰：“失路仓卒，岂敢诳言！”媪曰：“然则年岁几何矣？若已老，则又不巧。”仆曰：“郎甫二十，尚未有室，那得便老？”翁媪乃喜跃就车前，举灯笼照之，啧啧曰：“不特不老，且大是波俏郎，此事当谐矣！”即左右超辕坐，指挥令进。仆曰：“郎虽世代疡医，然自来业儒，恐不胜任。”翁曰：“郎君自言能之，汝何赘辞？”媪曰：“巧合如此，必非偶然，硍谦奉璧可也。”

    Explanation

    460

    Shortly thereafter, during the examination period, they prepared to travel to Tongzhou, with a single carriage and a boy servant, while the old servant drove. The horse tied under the carriage was weak and inferior, and they started their journey too late. After traveling just over twenty miles, it became dusk, and they found it difficult to proceed. There was no place to stop and stay. The boy servant was just complaining and lamenting, when suddenly he saw lights in the distance, moving closer until they were in front of them. There was an old man and an old woman, both rushing and breathless. The old servant stopped them and asked, "Is there a place to stay here?" The old man replied, "I am in a hurry with an urgent matter, how can I spare time for conversation?" The boy said, "What urgent matter is it that has you in such a state?" The old woman, while still walking, replied, "There is a sick person at home, and we are looking for an expert in external medicine."
