Jun, hearing this from inside the carriage, said, "I am an expert in external medicine, why seek someone else?" The old woman turned around and stopped, saying, "Are you not lying?" Jun replied, "We have lost our way in haste, how could I dare to lie?" The old woman said, "Then how old are you? If you are old, then it is not a good match." The old servant said, "The young master is just twenty, and has not yet married, how could he be old?" The old man and woman were delighted and jumped up to the front of the carriage, holding up a lantern to look at him, exclaiming, "Not only is he not old, but he is also a very handsome young man, this matter will surely work out!" They then sat on either side of the carriage and directed it to proceed. The old servant said, "The young master, though from a family of external medicine practitioners, has always studied Confucianism, and may not be competent." The old man said, "The young master says he can do it, why are you saying unnecessary things?" The old woman said, "Such a coincidence must not be by chance, we will humbly offer our jade (a metaphor for offering one's skills)."

    461

    俄至一庄院前，林木森郁，门庭壮丽，俨然巨家。翁媪下车，嘱曰：“稍候于此，容入白太太。”遂启阖而入。老仆执辔低语：“郎本业荒疏，何便负荷？此事脱有不妙，何以解免？”筠曰：“我岂冒昧作事者？汝勿多虑。”言次，翁媪率僮婢数人，趋走而出曰：“郎君请即入，太太立候矣。”于是簇者导者，寻达一广厅。见所谓太太者待于檐下，年约三十六七，奢华艳异，都冶颇极。筠罕见如许富丽，势不得不拜。太太急命掖起，以常礼相见，分宾主坐，亟问邦族、姓字、年岁，及曾议婚否，筠悉以实对。太太凝睇久之，颜色甚怡，屏去侍婢，谓筠曰：“身姓梅氏，本河南人，流寓于此，近百年矣。孀居无子，赖有一女，名宜春才十八，待字于家。

    Explanation

    461

    Soon they arrived at a rural estate, where the forest was dense and lush, and the courtyard was grand and magnificent, clearly belonging to a wealthy family. The elderly couple got out of the carriage and instructed, "Wait here for a moment while we go inform Mrs. White." They then entered the estate. An old servant held the reins and whispered, "The young master has neglected his original profession; how can he bear the responsibility? If something goes wrong, how will he resolve it?" The young man replied, "Am I someone who acts rashly? You need not worry excessively." As they spoke, the elderly couple returned with several servants, saying, "Young master, please come in; Mrs. White is waiting for you." Surrounded and guided by the servants, they eventually reached a large hall. There, Mrs. White, the lady in question, was waiting under the eaves. She was around thirty-six or thirty-seven years old, dressed in luxurious and exotic attire, her beauty reaching its peak. The young man, having never seen such opulence before, felt compelled to bow. Mrs. White quickly had him rise, greeted him with customary courtesy, and they sat down, with her taking the position of host and him the guest. She immediately asked about his clan, surname, age, and whether he had ever been proposed to. The young man answered all her questions truthfully. Mrs. White gazed at him for a long time, her expression very pleased. She then dismissed her servants and said to the young man, "My surname is Mei. Originally from Henan, we have been residing here for nearly a century. I am a widow with no children, but I have a daughter named Yichun, who is eighteen years old and awaiting marriage at home."

    462

    不意忽构疮疾，日甚一日，心甚忧之，故命其阿保往聘疡医，何幸路遇郎君，自称国手，曷胜欣幸。但小女以患处幽隐，不肯令人医治，闲尝与之商酌，谓当密为访求，得有医人少年未娶者，俾治之，倘得病愈，即以为配。今得郎君，温文韶秀，适副私愿，应是天缘，非人力所及。”筠初念不过一时失路，漫为权变，以图一宿，诚不料被迫至此，不胜遑遽，又不敢易辞，但鞠躬曰：“医治痈疽，敢不竭力。若夫婚姻之事，曾向先人设誓，必待成名而后议之。”太太曰：“郎君迂腐矣，不从此议，岂可治病耶？果有誓词，不妨聘定，待大登科后再小登科，亦何不可？”筠固懦于言，及闻太太快论，语塞不能对。太太命唤蕊儿传语姑娘：“一小太医至矣，亟打点，好入看病。”群婢哄应而入。

    Explanation

    462

    Unexpectedly, I fell ill with a wound that worsened day by day, causing me great worry. Therefore, I ordered my nanny to hire a surgeon. How fortunate to meet the young master on the road, who claims to be a master. How can I express my joy and happiness! However, my daughter has a wound in a hidden place and is unwilling to let anyone treat it. I have often discussed this with her, suggesting that we secretly seek out a young, unmarried healer to treat her. If the illness is cured, then we will marry him. Now, the young master is gentle, elegant, and handsome, exactly matching my private wish. It must be a matter of fate, beyond human control. Initially, Jin thought it was just a temporary loss of direction and made a temporary adjustment to arrange for a night's stay. He did not expect to be forced into this situation and was unable to cope with the suddenness. He also dared not change his words, but bowed and said, "To treat abscesses, how dare I not do my best. As for matters of marriage, I had sworn to my ancestors that I must wait until I have achieved fame before discussing it." The lady said, "The young master is too rigid. If he doesn't agree to this, how can he treat the illness? If there is a vow, we can arrange the engagement. After passing the major examination, then the minor one, why not?" Jin is naturally timid in speech, and upon hearing the lady's quick remarks, he was unable to respond. The lady ordered Rui'er to convey a message to the young lady: "A young physician has arrived. Quickly prepare, and come in for a visit." The maids responded in a hurry and entered.

    463

    良久，一美婢出，极娟丽，立太太侧，耳数语四。太太笑曰：“待太医入内，自审谤之，去取任伊为政，我不相强。”婢诺诺，频目筠，笑而去。又久之，乃请太医入室。太太亲握筠腕而行，历回廊曲室数重，始至闺闼。一婢启帘，太太扬声曰：“儿坐耶？卧耶？太医来矣！”寻入室，至榻前，女衣红绣，拥锦衾，倚鸳枕而坐，鬓发黛眉，明眸皓齿，面色如朝霞和雪，光采夺目，艳绝人寰。筠一见，目眩意迷，不能正视。太太曰：“此郎君，即太医也，汝阿保遇之途中者，可否令视汝疾？”女窃睇流盼，俯首默然，两颊红晕。太太曰：“可否？密对娘言，无羞出口。”女徐徐低语曰：“娘视为可则可耳。”太太笑曰：“无赐郎君至此，为儿消灾，娘何不可之有！娘且暂去，但留蕊儿一人扶侍可矣。

    Explanation

    463

    After a long while, a beautiful maid appeared, extremely graceful and lovely, standing by the lady's side. She whispered something to the lady. The lady smiled and said, "Wait for the doctor to enter, and you may examine yourself privately. Leave it to him to decide, and I will not force you." The maid nodded and frequently looked at Jun before leaving with a smile. After a longer wait, the lady invited the doctor into the room. She personally led the doctor through several winding corridors and rooms, finally arriving at the private chamber. A maid opened the curtain, and the lady called out, "Are you sitting or lying down? The doctor has arrived!" Soon, they entered the room. The girl was dressed in red brocade, wrapped in a luxurious quilt, and sitting on a pair of embroidered pillows. Her hair, eyebrows, bright eyes, and white teeth were all perfect. Her complexion was like the beauty of dawn and snow, her radiance dazzling and her beauty unparalleled in the world. Jun, upon seeing her, was dazzled and confused, unable to look straight. The lady said, "This young master is the doctor, the one who met you on the road. Can he see your illness?" The girl glanced furtively, bowed her head silently, and blushed. The lady said, "Speak to mother privately, without shame." The girl slowly and quietly said, "Mother, if it is granted that the young master comes here to dispel your calamity, what can mother not agree to? Just leave Rui'er alone to attend to you."

    464

    ”向筠曰：“郎君须尽心，无草草。看病已，当出用饭也。”遂率同群婢径出。女命蕊儿请太医坐，蕊儿曰：“既来看病，盍早看之，省却忍受痛楚。”女羞涩之态，几不能支，蕊儿屡促之，女不得已，嘤然一呻，斜卧向内，以袖障面，任其所为。蕊儿乃含笑登床，以手招筠。筠半坐床侧，蕊儿款款启衾，则下体赤露，粉臀雪股，致致生光，温香馥馥，惟私处以红帕覆之。疮大如茶瓯，正当股际。筠见以奇艳，鹿撞心头，如梦如醉，勉强视疮已。蕊儿覆衾下床，呼他婢导见太太。太太令坐，问看疮何如矣，筠曰：“不当要害，无虞也，灵药一敷即愈耳。”太太喜，加笾布筵，即僮仆亦极丰美。太太曰：“郎君食已，可即赐药，此女已是郎君人，幸将视为肺腑。”筠曰：“敢不尽心，但须假一净室，以便和药。”太太曰：“已扫除书轩，为郎君设榻矣。”筠乃告退。

    Explanation

    464

    Xiang Yun said, "Young master must be diligent and not rush. After seeing the illness, we should go out to eat." Then she led the group of maids out directly. The girl ordered Rui'er to invite the doctor to sit. Rui'er said, "Since you have come to see the illness, why not look at it early, to avoid enduring pain?" The girl's shy and embarrassed state could hardly bear it. Rui'er repeatedly urged her. The girl, unable to help it, let out a soft moan, lay sideways facing inward, covered her face with her sleeve, and let the doctor do as he pleased. Rui'er then smiled and climbed onto the bed, waving her hand to Yun. Yun sat half on the side of the bed, and Rui'er slowly lifted the quilt, revealing the girl's lower body, with pink buttocks and snow-white thighs, emitting a warm fragrance, only the private parts were covered with a red cloth. The wound was as large as a tea bowl, right between the thighs. Yun saw it as strange and beautiful, feeling a deer colliding in his heart, as if in a dream and drunk. Struggling to look at the wound, he finished. Rui'er covered the quilt and got off the bed, calling another maid to guide them to see the lady. The lady ordered them to sit and asked how the wound looked. Yun replied, "It is not in a critical area, there is no worry, a magical medicine will heal it immediately." The lady was pleased, adding more dishes and setting up a feast, even the servants enjoyed it immensely. The lady said, "Young master, after eating, you can immediately give the medicine, this girl is already yours, please treat her as your innermost friend." Yun said, "I dare not be less than my best, but I need a clean room to mix the medicine." The lady said, "I have already cleaned the study and set up a bed for you." Yun then took his leave.

    465

    入轩果雅洁，轩中位置器玩，乃笔砚等事，靡不精良。几上烧红烛，大如臂，二美婢服役其中，筠曰：“得小僮一人为伴足矣，何劳卿等？”婢曰：“家中唯老圃公，更无男子，何处得有小童？”筠曰：“患疮姑娘，果未字乎？”婢曰：“太太无子，惟生姑娘一人，欲得一才貌兼者，方许为赘，寻常岂许委禽。”筠曰：“然则许配医人之说，恐未必确。”婢曰：“果似郎君，亦何不确之有？第恐不能逾其疾耳。”筠喜动眉宇，笑曰：“愈此疾，予操之若券耳。卿等姑退，予合药最忌阴人，但呼我小价来，祗候可矣。”婢笑而去。

    Explanation

    465

    The pavilion was indeed elegant and clean. Within the pavilion, the arrangement of objects of play and decoration, including writing implements such as brushes and inkstones, was nothing but excellent. On the table, a large red candle, as big as an arm, burned brightly, and two beautiful maidservants served there. The speaker said, "A young boy as a companion would be enough, why bother you?" The maid replied, "In our household, there is only the old gardener, and no other men. Where could we find a young boy?" The speaker asked, "Is the girl with the disease really unmarried?" The maid answered, "The lady has no sons, only one daughter. She wants a man who is both talented and good-looking to marry her, and would only allow such a man to become a son-in-law. Ordinarily, she wouldn't allow a man to bring a bird (a symbolic gesture of marriage proposal)." The speaker said, "Then the idea of marrying a doctor might not be certain." The maid reassured him, "If you are like the doctor, why wouldn't it be certain? Just be careful, you might not be able to cure her disease." The speaker's face lit up with joy, and he smiled, "Curing this disease, I can do it with certainty. You may go, I am preparing medicine, and it is most taboo to have women present. Just call my young boy to wait for me." The maidservants laughed and left.

    466

    有顷，僮至，筠令先闭院门，低语曰：“予有一山水画扇，携来否？”僮曰：“在枕函中。”筠大喜，曰：“吾事济矣！”亟开函取扇，扇上固有紫金锭扇坠，碎而末之，调以茶脚，调未匀，一婢出问曰：“太太致问郎君，药合得否？”筠曰：“已合得矣。”即携入见太太，曰：“此药忌阴人犯手，须亲敷乃可。”太太曰：“但得病愈，任郎为之。”命一婢引之入。蕊儿见药，欣然曰：“人固有美好如郎君者，而无良药，可乎？”复上床启衾，筠左手持药，右手挥鸡翎敷之，乃故以手揩摩其私处，红帕忽被触落，女急缩玉足，足指拂筠口而过，阴沟已见。蕊儿红潮满面，掩袖而笑，筠不觉精流满盉.女向蕊儿小语曰：“药敷完，可请郎君出矣。”筠怅怅而出。太太复殷勤臻至，亲送归寝。

    Explanation

    466

    After a while, the servant arrived, and Jun ordered him to close the courtyard gate first, then whispered, "Do I have a landscape painting fan with me?" The servant replied, "It's in the pillow case." Jun was overjoyed and said, "My plan is successful!" He quickly opened the case and took out the fan, which had a Zijin Ding fan pendant on it. He crushed it into powder and mixed it with tea residue, but the mixture was not yet uniform. A maid came out and asked, "Madam has sent a message to Young Master, is the medicine prepared?" Jun replied, "It is already prepared." He then carried it in to see Madam and said, "This medicine is taboo for women to handle, it must be applied personally." Madam said, "As long as the illness is cured, let Young Master do it." She ordered a maid to lead him in. Rui'er saw the medicine and happily said, "There are certainly people as good-looking as Young Master who don't have good medicine, can there be such a thing?" She climbed back onto the bed and uncovered the quilt. Jun held the medicine in his left hand and waved a chicken feather to apply it with his right hand. He deliberately rubbed her private parts, and the red handkerchief was accidentally knocked off. The girl quickly pulled back her beautiful feet, and her toes brushed past Jun's mouth, revealing her private parts. Rui'er's face turned red, and she covered her sleeve and laughed. Jun couldn't help but ejaculate into the container. The girl whispered to Rui'er, "The medicine is applied, it's time for Young Master to leave." Jun reluctantly left. Madam was again very attentive and personally escorted him back to his bedroom.

    467

    筠就枕冥索宜春艳质，独得亲其下体，何修得此？即蕊儿之姝丽，亦复非凡。辗转反侧，欲心之炽，五更始睡去。翌日鸡鸣，筠尚酣梦，即有二婢剥啄而入，直至榻前，褰帐而启曰：“姑娘敷药，一夜安眠，已消肿矣。第须膏药，以封固疮口，故太太命白郎君。”筠惊喜，披衣起曰：“即刻奉上矣。”二婢去，筠沉思无得膏药处，殊徬徨。既而思得一策，急蹑履下床，嘱僮速去，密解车上毂　来，僮曰：“何所用之？”筠曰：“非尔所知，第速取来，切勿泄于人！”僮哂而去，须臾提　至，筠取其陈油积垢，和以棂尘，并所剩紫金锭末，剪书包布，摊为膏药，亲往贴之。

    Explanation

    467

    Bai lay down and pondered deeply on the beautiful spring-like appearance of Rui'er, wondering how he had earned the privilege to be so close to her. He thought to himself, "What merit or effort led to this?" Indeed, Rui'er's beauty was extraordinary. Tossing and turning in bed, his desire burned intensely, and it was not until the fifth watch of the night that he finally fell asleep. The next morning, as the rooster crowed, Bai was still in a deep sleep when two maids knocked gently on the door and entered, approaching his bedside and pulling back the curtains. They said, "Madam has applied medicine to the wound, and after a night of rest, the swelling has subsided. However, she needs a medicinal ointment to seal and protect the wound, so she has commanded Mr. Bai to prepare it." Bai was surprised and immediately got up, saying, "I will present it right away."

    The maids left, and Bai sat in deep thought, unsure where to find the medicinal ointment. He felt at a loss. After a moment, he came up with a plan and quickly put on his shoes, instructing his servant to hurry and bring something from the carriage. The servant asked, "What do you need?" Bai replied, "It's not for you to know. Just bring it quickly, and do not reveal it to anyone!" The servant laughed and left. Soon, he returned with the wheel hub from the carriage. Bai took the old, accumulated grease and dirt, mixed it with fine dust from the window frames, and combined it with the remaining powder of the purple gold pill. He cut and wrapped it in cloth, spread it into a medicinal ointment, and personally applied it to the wound.

    468

    数日疮大愈，可以行立，太太乃举酒属筠曰：“郎君之于小女，再生之恩也，请择吉合卺，可乎？”筠终不通权，谢曰：“筠非能生死人也，此自当生者，筠能使之起耳。且姑娘之疮虽愈，亦须调摄百日，筠亦功名未就，不敢渝誓。”太太首肯曰：“若然，姑留聘以俟后图。”筠出白玉带　一枚奉之，太太遂设祖席，以百金为赆，筠三让而后受。

    Explanation

    468

    A few days later, the wound had greatly healed, and she could walk and stand. The high-ranking lady then raised a cup of wine and addressed Jyun, saying, "Young gentleman, you have bestowed upon my daughter the grace of being reborn. May we choose a favorable day for the wedding ceremony? Would that be acceptable?" Jyun finally did not make a decision and replied, "It is not that I can bring someone back to life; this person would have naturally recovered, and I was only able to help her stand up. Moreover, although my daughter's wound has healed, she still needs to rest and recuperate for a hundred days. I also have not yet achieved my goals and cannot break my promise." The high-ranking lady nodded in agreement and said, "If that is the case, let us temporarily hold the betrothal and plan for the future." Jyun then presented a white jade belt as a gift. The high-ranking lady then prepared a farewell banquet and gave him a hundred gold coins as a parting gift. Jyun politely refused three times before finally accepting.

    469

    乃抵通，一战冠军，即驰书报捷于梅氏，，议娶宜春。老仆曰：“无大郎之命，媒妁之言，无乃不可乎？”筠曰：“虞舜，圣人也，且不告而娶英皇；况我无可以告，即大郎何能为乎？”遂赘于梅氏，花烛之盛，人世罕俦，鱼水之欢，人世罕匹。女复使筠纳蕊儿为妾。既满月，筠请于太太，欲暂归筹画，徙男妇老幼同来居此。太太曰：“此间荒野，不可久居，京师右安门外，有旧宅一区，曷若同往居之？”筠大悦。择日并发辎重近百两，络绎于道，道旁观者，以为公侯眷属，莫不骇瞩。及至旧宅前，闾门极萧条，入大门，破屋欹垣，亦殊荒废。至二门以内，则崭然一新，峻宇雕墙，焕如天上矣。筠既获美姝，又享厚富，心满意足，无复书癖，于是尽移家口，同入新居。

    Explanation

    469

    He then arrived at Tong and won a decisive victory in one battle. Immediately, he sent a letter to report the victory to the Mei family and discussed marrying a woman from Yichun. An old servant said, "Without the permission of the eldest son and the words of the matchmaker, is this not inappropriate?" Jun replied, "Emperor Shun was a sage, and he married the princess without informing anyone;况且我没有什么可以报告的，那么大郎又能怎么样呢？" So, he went to live as a son-in-law with the Mei family. The grandeur of the wedding ceremony was rarely seen in the world, and the harmonious and joyful relationship was hardly comparable in the world. The woman then made Jun take Rui'er as a concubine. After the full moon period, Jun asked the grandmother and wanted to temporarily return to plan, moving the men, women, and children to live here together. The grandmother said, "This place is a wilderness and cannot be lived in for long. Outside the Right Peace Gate in the capital, there is an old residence. Why not go there together to live?" Jun was very pleased. He chose a day and sent out nearly a hundred loads of baggage, which continuously filled the road. People on the side of the road thought they were the family of a prince or marquis, and no one was not amazed. When they arrived at the old residence, the gate was extremely desolate. Entering the main gate, the broken houses and leaning walls were also very dilapidated. Inside the second gate, it was completely new, with tall buildings and carved walls, shining like the sky. Jun had obtained a beautiful woman and enjoyed great wealth, feeling completely satisfied and no longer had a passion for books. Then, he moved all the family members together into the new residence.

    470

    往省兄管弟筤，衣服仆从之盛，色色动人。管惊曰：“闻汝入泮，几番使人下通，皆云已归，询之家中，又道未返。日深疑抱，卜筮胥无征验。今从何来？发迹若此！”筠备述已入梅氏甥馆，甫定新迁，即来祗谒。筤曰：“不意二哥成家，实愧缺礼。今既获宁宇，当登堂一拜新嫂。”管曰：“予虽叨一日之长，然弟之岳母，亦我之母执也，讵可不一往起居，会当与三弟并发耳。”因同车而往。及门见其荒凉，管笑曰：“吾弟避嚣喜僻，得此佳境，暇时辟为蔬圃，开畦畛，滋灌溉，足够一年酸齑之用，第恐异日得第，则阀阅棨戟，不无稍费调停耳。”筤和之以笑。俄入重门，蓦然改观，二子咋舌，相视不敢加嘲笑。太太者出，二子拜见，谦谨不觉太过，俯仰唯唯。太太曰：“二位远来不易，今为至戚，合令小女出拜伯叔。”须臾女出，娇逐步来，羞从面起，苗条婉媚，目所未经。二子眩惑痴迷，如作游仙之梦。女拜讫即退，筠胹炮羔，为华萼之宴。

    Explanation

    470

    Upon visiting my elder brother Guan and younger brother Lang, I was struck by the splendor of their clothes and attendants, every detail captivating. Guan exclaimed in surprise, "Hearing that you have entered the school, I sent messengers several times, and they all said you had returned. Inquiring at home, they said you had not. The longer I waited, the more I suspected, and divination offered no proof. Where have you come from? Your rise is so sudden!" Jun explained that he had just settled into his new home at the Mei family's son-in-law's residence and came to pay respects immediately. Lang remarked, "I did not expect my elder brother to have married, and I feel ashamed for not having prepared properly. Now that we have a peaceful home, we should go upstairs to pay respects to the new sister-in-law." Guan said, "Although I am older by a day, my brother's mother-in-law is also my mother-in-law. How could I not go to greet her, and I will go with my younger brother." They went together in a carriage. Upon arriving, they saw the place was desolate. Guan smiled and said, "My brother prefers seclusion and avoids noise, finding this ideal setting. In his spare time, he can turn it into a vegetable garden, cultivate the fields, and irrigate them, enough for a year's worth of pickles. But I fear that if he passes the imperial examinations in the future, the family will have to make some adjustments." Lang agreed with a laugh. Soon they entered the main gate, and suddenly the scene changed. The two brothers gasped in amazement, looking at each other and not daring to mock. The mother-in-law came out, and the two brothers bowed respectfully, being modest and cautious, not aware of being overly so, bowing and nodding. She said, "It was not easy for you two to come from afar, now that we are close relatives, we should have my daughter come out to greet her uncles." Soon, the daughter came out, stepping gracefully, blushing. She was slender and charming, something I had never seen before. The two brothers were dazzled and lost in admiration, as if in a dream of a fairy. She bowed and then withdrew. Jun prepared roasted lamb, for a banquet of flowers and fruits.

    471

    二子神往丽人，食不知味，逡巡辞去，评论于轩中。筤曰：“述先人之业，出入王公巨卿及士庶之家，阅人闺秀，何啻千万，几曾见有如新嫂者！从此富贵浮云，功名粪土矣！”管曰：“何物书痴，享此大福，岂梦想所能到！”筤曰：“焉得与之一夕绸缪，死亦无憾！”管曰：“但有目者，皆当作是想。奈名分所关，徒思何益？”筤曰：“大哥亦拘执矣。夫唐文皇，英主也，犹纳弟妇；陈曲逆，良相也。尚盗其嫂，我辈凡人，又何泥焉！”各归与妇谋，管妻贾，筤妻王，亦妒而不明理者，共往见宜春，归无人色，亦百计欲其夫乱之，以畅其妒心。

    Explanation

    471

    Two men were deeply infatuated with a beautiful woman, so much so that they lost their appetite and left reluctantly to discuss her in a pavilion. Láng said, "I have been involved in our family's business, moving in and out of the homes of high-ranking officials and common people, and I have seen countless refined women, but how many have been as captivating as the new sister-in-law! From now on, wealth and nobility are like floating clouds, and achievements and honors are like manure!" Guǎn said, "What kind of bookworm enjoys such great fortune, which is beyond imagination!" Láng replied, "How could one spend a night together intimately with her, even if it means dying, there would be no regret!" Guǎn said, "Anyone with eyes would think the same. But social norms and conventions are at stake, and mere contemplation is of no use." Láng retorted, "Brother, you are too rigid. Even Emperor Taizong of Tang, a brilliant ruler, took his brother's wife; Chen Ping, an excellent minister, married his brother's widow. We, mere mortals, should not be bound by such conventions!" Each returned home and discussed the matter with his wife. Guǎn's wife, Jia, and Láng's wife, Wang, were also jealous and did not understand the situation. They went together to see the new sister-in-law, Yi Chun, and returned pale-faced, plotting various ways to ruin their husbands' reputations and relationships to satisfy their jealousy.

    472

    会元夜，相与筹画，布盛宴邀宜春及蕊儿入城踏灯，王亲往迎之，强而后可。宜春翠被红绚，蕊儿锦裙绣袄而至。管、筤掬之于门。既而入席，命梨园演《肉蒲团》，极其秽亵。宜春谈笑自如，殊无愠色。贾、王以为可动，复相间试以浮语，宜春曰：“曷请大伯、三叔偕来奉一殇乎？”贾、王大喜，亟遣婢趋告管、筤，管、筤闻之，若掘得藏金，踉跄而入，宜春命酒跪春管，蕊儿跪奉筤，管、筤亦跪领之，筤曰：“嫂何多礼！”宜春曰：“酒以合欢，礼以缀淫，既奉酒，可不为礼乎？”众皆笑。席散，贾邀入房中更衣。宜春娭光眇视，醉态不支，低语向王曰：“婶知之乎？今夕醉甚，当宿此，不能踏灯矣。

    Explanation

    472

    On the Lantern Festival, they deliberated together and planned a grand feast to invite Yichun and Rui'er into the city to view the lanterns. Wang personally went to welcome them, and only after being forced could they agree. Yichun arrived with a green quilt and red decorations, while Rui'er came in a brocade skirt and embroidered jacket. Guan and Lang greeted them at the door. After entering the hall, they ordered the theater troupe to perform The Flesh Pillow, which was extremely indecent and vulgar. Yichun talked and laughed naturally, showing no signs of anger. Jia and Wang thought they could be moved and tested them with flattery, saying, "Why not invite Uncle and Uncle to come and take a drink together?" Jia and Wang were very pleased and hurriedly sent a maid to inform Guan and Lang. Upon hearing this, Guan and Lang rushed in excitedly, as if they had found hidden treasure. Yichun commanded wine to be served and knelt before Guan, while Rui'er knelt to serve Lang. Guan and Lang also knelt to receive it. Lang said, "Why are you being so polite!" Yichun replied, "Wine to unite joy, etiquette to complement lust. Having served the wine, can we not show respect?" Everyone laughed. After the feast, Jia invited them into the room to change clothes. Yichun looked around with a distracted gaze, drunk to the point of being unable to support herself, and whispered to Wang, "Do you know? I am very drunk tonight; I will stay here, I cannot go out to see the lanterns."

    473

    ”贾曰：“娣姒谈心甚快，踏灯何乐哉！”贾、王私议曰：“看其桃李之艳，必当有松竹之操，不谓瞈糟亦醉，直一淫奔之女耳！”乃密嘱管、筤，隐身户外，倘有隙可乘，即下手拏云也。言讫而入，极力挑逗之，宜春莈袖微笑曰：“古人易内而饮酒，初不解其何乐，今乃颇悟其趣。大伯、三叔，自家人也，何不入室一谈，以尽清兴？”管、筤即户外应声，争奔而入。蕊儿遽灭烛，房中骤暗如漆，窗上虽有月光，竟一物不见。然二子潜听已久，某在斯，某在斯，早知之稔熟，一时同扑宜春，管挤筤曰：“兄先弟后，序不可紊也。”筤不得已，遂拥蕊儿，各接吻扪私，无所不至。二女极力抵拒，呼叫声嘶，竟不能脱，不觉暗中摸索，渐入佳境矣。二子情动已久，稍纵即泄。方图再举，小婢忽秉烛至，二子惊起视之，则王为管乱，贾为筤乱，宜春、蕊儿查无踪影，不知所之。彼此惊惭悔恨，夺门而散。

    Explanation

    473

    Jia said, "Sisters-in-law chatting happily, what joy is there in stepping on the lamp!" Jia and Wang privately discussed, "Seeing her beauty like peach and plum blossoms, one must also have the integrity of pine and bamboo, but to say she is blind drunk and still drunk, she is just a wanton woman!" They then secretly instructed Guan and Liang to hide outside the door, ready to pounce if there was an opportunity. After finishing their conversation, they entered and tried to seduce Yichun. Yichun smiled and said, "Ancient people exchanged wives and drank wine, initially not understanding what the fun was, now I somewhat understand its charm. Uncle, third uncle, we are family, why not enter the room for a conversation to enjoy a clear and pleasant mood?" Guan and Liang responded from outside and rushed in. Rui'er quickly extinguished the candle, and the room suddenly became dark like ink, even though there was moonlight on the window, nothing could be seen. However, the two men had been listening secretly for a long time, "Here I am, here I am," they had known it very well, and at once pounced on Yichun together. Guan pushed Liang and said, "The elder goes first, the younger follows, the order cannot be confused." Liang, unwillingly, then embraced Rui'er, and they kissed and touched each other intimately, going to all lengths. The two women struggled fiercely to resist, crying out until hoarse, and could not escape, unconsciously feeling around in the dark, gradually entering a good state. The two men had been emotionally aroused for a long time, if slightly relaxed, it would be released. Just as they were about to try again, a young servant suddenly brought a candle, and the two men were startled and looked around, only to find that Wang had been confused by Guan, and Jia had been confused by Liang, and Yichun and Rui'er were nowhere to be found, their whereabouts unknown. Both were filled with shock, shame, regret, and anger, and fled through the door.

    474

    先是贾、王来邀宜春时，并不及筠，筠已疑之，不许往。太太独以为可，筠不能阻拗，惟嘱早归。迨二更不返，大忿恨恨，背烛而坐。既而车声辘辘，传呼姑娘及蕊姐归来矣。筠既喜见，俄而入室诘其故，宜春笑曰：“君之兄弟，大非良善，故作淫剧惑人儿。已小施戏术，俾通室以颠倒之矣。”因备述其事，筠跌足曰：“此太毒狠，令我不安。”蕊儿曰：“蔑伦之人，不足为衅钟之豭砪，虐惩之亦不为过。”筠曰：“既往不咎矣。第夜已深，相隔重城，何以得出？”宜春曰：“江湖之深，岱华之高，不能阻儿飞越。卑卑重城，庸足限乎？”筠终不释然，自此兄弟，无颜相见，声息不通。

    Explanation

    474

    Previously, when Jia and Wang came to invite Lady Yichun, they did not even mention Jin. Jin was already suspicious and refused to go. The lady-in-waiting alone thought it was acceptable, and Jin could not stop her, so he only instructed her to return early. By the second watch, she did not return, and Jin was greatly furious and resentful, sitting with his back to the candle. Soon after, the sound of wheels rolling was heard, and it was announced that Lady Guanyin and Lady Ruie had returned. Jin was glad to see them and soon entered the room to question the reason. Lady Yichun smiled and said, "Your brothers are not good people; they intentionally created obscene performances to bewitch people. I have already used a small trick to make the entire room upside down." She then detailed the matter, and Jin exclaimed in dismay, "This is too cruel and ruthless, making me uncomfortable." Lady Ruie said, "People who despise moral norms are not worthy of being punished as a sacrificial pig; punishing them harshly is not excessive." Jin said, "Past mistakes are forgiven. But it is already late at night, and we are separated by a heavy city wall. How can we get out?" Lady Yichun replied, "The depth of the rivers and lakes, the height of Mount Tai and Mount Hua, cannot prevent me from flying over. A mere heavy city wall, how can it limit me?" Jin finally did not feel relieved, and from this point on, the brothers no longer had the face to see each other, and there was no communication.

    475

    宜春学尤淹博，筠所为诗文，多改政之。筠爱之如珍，敬之如宾。逾年生一子。筠举孝廉，身厌绮罗，口穷甘软，人称为小石崇。一日，宜春忽泣谓筠曰：“儿初罹疮疾，得君疗之而瘥，不惭自荐以酬大德。讵意中道乖离，痛心孰甚！”筠大骇曰：“何为出此？”宜春曰：“夙缘已尽，夫复可言！幸留此子，以承君嗣。今夜即当永别以去耳。”筠不胜悲苦，哽咽不能成语，蕊儿亦从旁而泣，尤助酸辛。

    Explanation

    475

    宜春学尤淹博，筠所为诗文，多改政之。筠爱之如珍，敬之如宾。逾年生一子。筠举孝廉，身厌绮罗，口穷甘软，人称为小石崇。一日，宜春忽泣谓筠曰：“儿初罹疮疾，得君疗之而瘥，不惭自荐以酬大德。讵意中道乖离，痛心孰甚！”筠大骇曰：“何为出此？”宜春曰：“夙缘已尽，夫复可言！幸留此子，以承君嗣。今夜即当永别以去耳。”筠不胜悲苦，哽咽不能成语，蕊儿亦从旁而泣，尤助酸辛。

    Translation:

    Yi Chun was particularly learned, and he often revised or corrected the poetry and prose that Jun wrote. Jun loved him/her as a treasure and respected him/her as a guest. After a year, they had a son. Jun was recommended as a filial and upright official, clothed in fine silk, and enjoyed delicacies, earning him the nickname "Little Shi Chong." One day, Yi Chun suddenly wept and said to Jun, "When I was first afflicted with an illness, you cured me and I recovered. I am not ashamed to recommend myself to repay your great kindness. Who could have thought that we would part ways, causing such profound sorrow!" Jun was greatly startled and asked, "Why are you saying such things?" Yi Chun replied, "Our past karmic connection has ended, what more can be said! Fortunately, I leave this child behind to carry on your lineage. Tonight, I will depart forever." Jun could not bear the sorrow and grief, choked with sobs and unable to form coherent words. Rui'er also cried beside him, adding to the bitterness and sorrow.

    476

    顷之，太太出，挽宜春径行，嘱筠曰：“郎君无徒悲，好自爱，四十年后，当复相聚耳。”旋出门，门前已驻一犊车，犊黄色，甚小，角才茧栗，车亦不广，而美泽可鉴。一家十数人，悉乘之，人不觉挤，小车亦不觉隘。老翁执策为御，车行甚捷，瞬息而逝，而宜春与蕊儿泣声，犹恍然在耳也。筠伫立滂沱，家人劝不能止，强掖入门，则第宅化为乌有，仅存破屋数椽，荆杞满目而已。举家惊骇，始知遇怪，然所遗黄白甚多，筠得别购广居，仍不失为豪富。后筠授某县君，颇有政声，迁刺史。子亦克肖，不绝书香，究不详四十年后复聚之说，果何如耳。

    Explanation

    476

    In a short while, the respected elder emerged, taking Yichun by the hand and proceeding directly. She instructed Jun, saying, "Young master, do not grieve in vain; take good care of yourself. We will meet again in the future." She then turned to leave the door. Outside the door, a small calf-drawn carriage had already stopped. The calf was yellow, very small, with horns just like those of a young calf. The carriage was not wide, yet it was beautiful and lustrous, almost reflective. A family of more than ten people all fit into it, and no one felt crowded, nor did the small carriage feel cramped. An old man held the reins and drove the carriage, which moved very quickly, disappearing in an instant. The cries of Yichun and Rui'er still echoed in Jun's ears. Jun stood still in the downpour, and his family tried to persuade him to stop, but they could not. They forced him to enter the door, and then the mansion turned into nothingness, leaving only a few dilapidated houses, with thorny bushes filling the eyes. The whole family was shocked, finally realizing it was a strange encounter. However, the amount of gold and silver left behind was considerable, and Jun was able to buy a spacious residence elsewhere, still remaining wealthy. Later, Jun was appointed as a county-level official and had a good reputation for governance, eventually being promoted to the position of governor. His son also resembled him, continuing the scholarly tradition, but the details of the meeting after forty years remained unclear.

    477

    恩茂先曰：虽不测其何妖，即其艳冶异常处，写来纸上，自是尤物移人。予尝闻此事于锐别山，继见斋园此记，又小异而大同，终不知孰确，要其事则真实不虚。

    　　兰岩曰：美丽富贵，往往于无意中得之，彼营营者，何处觅得？亦徒劳奔走耳。筠立志不业疡医，而终以此得佳妇，为富人，享受多年，亦可谓非本志乎？此女莫知所自来，莫知所自往，飘然无累，岂神龙作用耶？

    Explanation

    477

    Master En Moxian said: Although it cannot be discerned what kind of demon it is, even its extraordinary beauty, when written down on paper, naturally moves people. I once heard this story from Rui Bieshan, and later saw this record in Zhaiyuan's writings, which have minor differences but major similarities, ultimately it is not known which is more accurate, but the essence of the matter is truly not false.

    Master Lan Yan said: Beauty and wealth are often obtained unintentionally. Those who are busy and anxious, where can they find such things? They only labor in vain. Jun set his mind not to practice as a surgeon, but ultimately gained a good wife through this, became a wealthy person, and enjoyed it for many years. Can it also be said that it was not his original intention? This woman's origin is unknown, her destination is also unknown, floating freely without burden, is this not the work of a divine dragon?

    478

    赵　媒　婆彰德赵媒居积取盈，家称小康。郡有恶豪，欲娶吴秀才女，以重金啖媒。媒贪其利，巧言胹合，致女失所配。吴忿甚，送官痛惩之，媒愧悔改业，誓不复为人作伐，避居羡河铺。

    　　一日，自城中探女归，跨卫独行，日已向暮，忽见一青衣自歧路来逆，问：“莫是执柯赵姥否？”曰：“然。”曰：“然则请转辔，随儿行，家主母有事相浼也。”言讫，却步为导。媒阴念自蒙辱后，久不作蹇修，今观此青衣举止，故是大家婢子，从之必作多金，不妨一作冯妇。冥想颇得意，乘代步，捉衔以从。下路斜行，约数里，渐见青石甃路，绿树成行，奄至一巨宅，闬闳高峻，阀阅焕然。青衣曰：“主翁行役未归，僮仆强半随去，家少男子，姥径入可矣。”接驴代系庭树上。转出一厅，有婢媪数辈，坐立其间，见媒群喜曰：“唤得冰上人来矣！”即有争趋入告者，俄传语：“可速入，夫人立候矣。”

    Explanation

    478

    The Matchmaker of Zhao

    Zhao, a matchmaker from Zhangde, had accumulated wealth through her business dealings, and her household was moderately prosperous. In the prefecture, there was a powerful and wicked man who wished to marry the daughter of Scholar Wu. He bribed Zhao with a large sum of money. Greedy for the profit, Zhao used clever and persuasive speech to bring about an agreement, causing the woman to lose her intended match. Scholar Wu was very angry and sent the man to the authorities for severe punishment. Zhao felt ashamed and regretful, changing her profession, and swore never to act as a matchmaker again, retreating to live in Xianhepu.

    One day, after visiting her daughter in the city, Zhao returned alone on horseback. As the sun was setting, she suddenly saw a young servant approaching from a side road. The servant asked, "Are you not the matchmaker Zhao?" Zhao replied, "Yes." The servant then said, "If so, please turn your horse around and follow me. The lady of the house has something important to ask of you." After speaking, the servant stepped back and led the way. Zhao secretly thought that since being disgraced, she had not acted as a matchmaker for a long time. Now, seeing the young servant's demeanor, she realized that the servant was from a noble household. Following her would surely bring her a large sum of money, so she might as well act as a matchmaker once more. Deeply pleased with this thought, Zhao mounted her horse and followed the servant. They took a winding path, traveling about three miles, and gradually saw a road paved with blue stones, lined with green trees, leading to a grand mansion with high and majestic gates, and a splendid entrance. The young servant said, "The master is still on a journey and has not returned. Most of the servants have gone with him, so there are few men at home. You may enter directly." Zhao dismounted and tied her donkey to a tree in the courtyard. Turning into a hall, she saw several maids and elderly women sitting and standing around. When they saw Zhao, they all greeted her happily, saying, "We have finally brought the icebreaker here!" One of them rushed to report inside, and soon the message was passed, "Please come in quickly; the lady is waiting for you."

    479

    复相与历院宇数重，始达正寝。夫人者，倚隐囊而坐，年约四十许，媒再拜，夫人命曳起赐坐。自展邦族，云：“是大名郑氏，流寓于此，夫为卢侍郎，奉祠有年矣。所以命婢奉邀者，三豚儿已冠，未有佳妇，媪能为我攀一门楣，必当重赏也。”因命呼三郎至，则亭亭玉立，英妙绝伦。媒极口赞扬：“无论公子内慧如何，即此外秀，便足削尽天下公侯之色。遮莫老身减齿三十年，亦必拼死充作姬媵。阿谁有闺秀，肯不急设东床？”左右闻者皆笑，夫人亦解颜曰：“无怪婆子起家，谈锋煞是犀利。第老身谋聘者，为东偏薛参政女，亦系世家。参政已捐馆，夫人牛氏，择婿甚苛，且多疑，往往垂成而悔，婆子自料能令此事必谐否？”媒曰：“老身平生，不惯作模棱语，凭三寸舌往说之，必有佳报。”夫人喜，饮食之，便促其行。媒请诘朝往，夫人曰：“事不宜迟，迟则中变矣。”媒不敢方命，夫人仍使为伴，向东二里许，即至其处。

    479

    They continued together, passing through several courtyards and buildings, finally reaching the main hall. The lady was reclining on a cushion, around forty years old. The matchmaker bowed twice, and the lady commanded her to be pulled up and given a seat. The matchmaker introduced her family background, saying, "I am from the prestigious Zheng clan, temporarily residing here. My husband is a junior official named Lu, who has been serving in the temple for many years. I have sent a maid to invite you because my three sons are already adults and have not yet found suitable wives. Can you help me find a good match for them? There will be a generous reward." She then commanded the third son to be brought in, and he stood tall and graceful, exceptionally talented and beautiful. The matchmaker praised him to the utmost, saying, "No matter how intelligent and wise the young master is, even his outer appearance is enough to outshine the beauty of all princes and dukes. Even if I were thirty years younger, I would still be willing to die to serve as a concubine. Who has a suitable young lady who wouldn't rush to set up the eastern bed?" Those around them all laughed, and the lady smiled and said, "It's no wonder the old woman has risen in status; her conversation is extremely sharp. But I am seeking a match for my daughter, for the daughter of the Eastern Secretary of State, who is from a noble family. The Secretary of State has passed away, and his widow, Lady Niu, chooses a husband very strictly and is very suspicious. Often, just as it is about to succeed, she regrets it. Can the old woman ensure this will definitely succeed?" The matchmaker said, "Throughout my life, I am not used to speaking ambiguously. Relying on my eloquent tongue to persuade her, there will definitely be good news." The lady was pleased and treated her to food and drink, urging her to leave. The matchmaker requested to go the next morning, and the lady said, "The matter should not be delayed, as it might change." The matchmaker did not dare to disobey, and the lady still made her a companion, heading east about two miles, and they reached the place.

    480

    亦巨宅一区，沤钉兽环，壮丽埒卢氏。司阍只一媪，为通之。谒牛于房，备陈来意，牛曰：“老身亦夙闻卢家三郎，非龌龊纨绔子，特未亲见耳。”媒曰：“自是台阁品。姥阅人多矣，几曾见有如卢家三公子之才貌兼者？将来若不大富贵，老妇请自抉两眸子，誓不复相天下士矣。”牛曰：“汝莫揄扬过当，老身已心许之矣。小女往省其舅妗，三日后甫得还。为致声卢家亲母，打点纳采，约清明节后，嫁奁可完，即归鱼轩。儿女皆各长成，无事耽阁，终非了局。汝亦不必奔波往返，年老路迂，劳劳碌碌，心所不忍。且汝辛苦不辞者，徒以欲得酬谢礼物耳，老身即便相付，再来亦素手矣。”乃取廿金赠之，媒且喜且拜，以谀之曰：“端底大家不比小户，见理真，故作事快。”牛大悦，命具酒，并款之。果盘中有杖杜，甚甘美，媒伺隙，以帕裹纳袖中。饮数巡，谢酒辞行。牛复叮咛，速就婚礼。媒敬诺。

    480

    Also a large mansion, adorned with rusty nails and animal-shaped rings, its magnificence equal to that of the Lu family. The gatekeeper was only an old woman, who introduced the visitor. The visitor went to see Niu in her room and fully explained their intentions. Niu said, "I have also heard of Lu's third son for a long time; he is not a vulgar or spoiled son, but I have not personally met him." The matchmaker said, "He is a person of high social status. Grandmother has seen many people, how many times have I seen someone as talented and handsome as Lu's third son? If he does not become very wealthy and powerful in the future, I will pluck out my own eyes and swear I will never look at any other scholars again." Niu replied, "Do not exaggerate too much; I have already agreed in my heart. My daughter went to visit her aunt and uncle and will only return after three days. I will convey my greetings to Lu's mother, prepare the dowry, and arrange for the wedding around the Qingming Festival. The dowry can be completed, and then we will return to the fish carriage. The children are all grown up, there is no reason to delay, it is not a final solution. You also do not need to travel back and forth, old age and remote roads are tiring, it is heart-wrenching. And you do not hesitate to work hard, only to receive rewards and gifts, I will give it to you immediately, next time you will come empty-handed." She then took out twenty gold coins to give to her. The matchmaker was both happy and bowed, flattering her by saying, "The great families are not like small households, understanding the truth, therefore, actions are quick." Niu was very pleased and ordered to prepare wine, entertaining her. There was a type of fruit called 'zhangdu' in the fruit platter, very sweet and delicious. The matchmaker took advantage of the opportunity, wrapped it in a handkerchief and put it in her sleeve. They drank several rounds, thanked for the wine and left. Niu repeatedly reminded, to quickly proceed with the wedding. The matchmaker respectfully agreed.

    481

    既归，摇箑摆裙，得色满面，指谓夫人曰：“夫人但询之，老身应得格外赏否？夜漏三时，往返五六里，委婉一两言，致牛夫人降心允肯，但待聘甚急，只在清明后，便赋于归也。”夫人笑曰：“老魅亦太孟浪，那见以女嫁人，而如此汲汲者！婆子得渠廿金，亦不为薄，老身倍之可也。”于是媒人又得四十金，并红绫一端。俄闻远寺钟声，夫人曰：“夜阑矣，婆子当归。”仍令送之，遭三郎于屏门下，媒戏索谢礼，三郎笑指其具，媒人大笑而出。

    Explanation

    481

    After returning home, she fanned herself and twirled her skirt, her face beaming with pride. Pointing to the lady, she said, "Lady, just ask me, should I receive extra reward? I traveled back and forth for five or six li during the third watch of the night, with a few tactful words, and persuaded Lady Niu to lower her heart and agree. But the engagement is very urgent, only after the Qingming Festival, then she will be married off." The lady laughed and said, "The old witch is also too rash. How often do you see a woman being married off in such a hurry? The old woman got twenty gold coins from her, which is not too little, I will double it." Thus, the matchmaker received another forty gold coins and a piece of red silk. Soon after, they heard the distant temple bell ringing. The lady said, "It's late at night, the old woman should return." She still had her sent back, and they encountered Third Master at the screen door. The matchmaker playfully asked for a gratuity, and Third Master laughed and pointed to his own body. The matchmaker laughed loudly and left.

    482

    至歧路前，以驴授之，仓皇却回。媒策蹇且行且念：“夫人亦殊吝啬，何难赏一壶酒，两盛饭，一张床，俾老婢醉饱而睡？乃中夜逐客，岂其但卜其夜不卜其昼耶？”迨至家，旭日始旦，子妇尚未兴，以鞭挝门。子白足出应，讶其太早，媒曰：“且捉驴去，待徐徐告汝。”既而妇亦出迓，媒入室坐，吸烟啜茶，炫其夜来事，子妇倾听，眉宇间，喜色发越。小孙闻人语惊寤，呼母索乳，妇鸣之不睡，媒曰：“儿勿啼，为汝携得佳果来矣。”急取帕裹掷炕头，但见清渖滴沥。媒曰：“鲜果不耐时，可惜揉坏矣。”嘱妇解帕，则见蝌蚪数十枚，半如墨汁，犹有一二蠕蠕者。咸大惊异，急取两家赠金视之，已俱化为冥镪，红绫亦折纸所为。媒木立如偶人，良久，喉中作逆，呕出浊水升余，树叶无数，始悟遇鬼。病半月，颜色始复。

    Explanation

    482

    At the fork in the road, he gave the donkey to him and hurriedly turned back. The matchmaker, riding a slow donkey, thought while walking: "The lady is unusually stingy. Why is it hard to offer a pot of wine, two bowls of rice, and a bed to make the old maid drunk and full and then sleep? Yet in the middle of the night, she drove the guest away. Is she only concerned with the night but not the day?" By the time he arrived home, the sun had just risen, and the son's wife had not yet woken up. He struck the door with a whip. The son, barefoot, came out to answer, surprised that it was so early. The matchmaker said, "First, let's go get the donkey, and then I'll tell you slowly." Soon after, the wife also came out to greet. The matchmaker entered the room and sat down, smoking and drinking tea, boasting about the events of the night. The son's wife listened attentively, and her face showed joy. The little grandson, hearing the voices, woke up in surprise and called out to his mother for milk. The mother called out to him but he did not sleep. The matchmaker said, "Child, don't cry, I've brought you some good fruit." He quickly took a handkerchief, wrapped it, and threw it on the kang (heating platform). Only to see clear liquid dripping. The matchmaker said, "Fresh fruit doesn't last long, it's a pity it got crushed." He told the wife to unwrap the handkerchief, and they saw dozens of tadpoles, half of which were like ink, with a few still moving. They were all greatly surprised. They quickly took the gifts given by both families to see, and they had already turned into paper money, the red silk was also made of paper. The matchmaker stood motionless like a doll. After a long time, he felt nauseous and vomited out a cupful of murky water and countless leaves. Finally, he realized he had met a ghost. He was ill for half a month, and his complexion only returned.

    483

    兰岩曰：改业已久，仍复为利动，宜鬼物戏弄之也。每见世人，当痛遭窘辱时，未始不立志变计，悔心惭炽。一旦有重金以啖之者，遂致故态复萌，舍身不顾，名行堕丧，不可收拾。其不为鬼所侮弄，几希矣。吁，可不见利思义哉！赵姥为媒多年，岂于日夕来往之地，有此大族，未之前闻耶？乃毫无疑虑，徒事跋涉，想亦利令智昏耳。

    Explanation

    483

    Lan Yan said: Having changed careers for a long time, yet still moved by profit, it is appropriate for ghostly things to mock him. Every time I see people who suffer severe humiliation, they never fail to resolve to change their plans, with intense feelings of regret and shame. If there is a substantial amount of money to tempt them, they then revert to their old ways, disregarding their own safety, and their reputation and conduct are ruined, beyond repair. It is very rare for them not to be mocked by ghosts. Alas, can they not consider righteousness in the face of profit? Aunt Zhao has been a matchmaker for many years. How could there be such a large family in a place she visits daily, which she has never heard of before? Yet she had no doubts, only going through the trouble of traveling, it must be that profit has clouded her judgment.

    484

    三　官　保友人景君禄为予言：其表弟三官保，满洲某旗人也。年十七八岁时，皓齿明眸，雪肤华发，言笑妩媚，俨然好女子，且善自修饰，见者靡不流瞩；外秀如此，宜其温文蕴藉，蔼然可亲矣。乃负气凌人，好勇逞力，往往于喧衢闹市间，与人一言牴牾，或因睚眦小怨，必致狠斗凶殴，虽破脑裂肤，终不出一软款语。有北宫黝之风，不知者亲而近之，知者避而远之。邻里畏惮，号为花豹子，以其美而暴戾也。

    　　更有佟某，号佟韦驮，亦城北之市虎也。与保素不相识，尝与茶社中，片言龃龉，辄相殴击，其朋极力解纷。佟大言曰：“汝既称好汉，敢于明日清晨，在地坛后见我否？”保以手抚膺，双足并踊，自指其鼻曰：“我三官保，岂是畏人者？无论何处，倘不如期往，永不为为于北京城矣！”于是彼此不复言，各自散归。

    Explanation

    484

    San Guanbao's Friend Jing Junlu told me: His cousin San Guanbao is a Manchu from a certain banner. When he was in his late teens, he had bright teeth, clear eyes, snow-white skin, and lustrous hair. He spoke and smiled in a charming and flirtatious manner, appearing like a beautiful woman, and was good at self-adornment. Everyone who saw him could not help but be attracted; so beautiful on the outside, one would expect him to be gentle, cultured, and warm and approachable. However, he was arrogant and domineering, loving to show off his strength, and often engaged in fierce fighting in busy, noisy public places, even causing himself to be injured, yet he never uttered a single soft or conciliatory word. He had the style of Beigong You, and those who did not know him were drawn to him, while those who knew him avoided him. Neighbors feared him and called him the Flower Leopard, due to his beauty and ferocity.

    There was also a man named Tong, known as Tong Weituo, who was also a feared and dangerous person in the northern part of the city. He and San Guanbao had never met before, but once in a teahouse, they had a brief disagreement and immediately started fighting. Their friends tried their best to stop the fight. Tong loudly said, "You claim to be a hero, dare you to meet me tomorrow morning behind the Earth Altar?" San Guanbao put his hand on his chest, both feet jumping together, pointing to his own nose and said, "I am San Guanbao, am I someone who fears others? No matter where, if I do not go as scheduled, I will never be seen again in Beijing!" Thus, they did not speak any further and each went their own way.

    485

    翌日黎明，保单身径至地坛后，坐俟良久，始见佟率其党十五六人，悉恶少年，汹汹而至。保迎叱曰：“汝鸠众来，欲打我耶？”佟曰：“然。”保大笑曰：“我苟惧打，岂敢复来？任汝鼠辈所为，但一皱眉一呼痛，非好汉也！”言次自去其衣，赤身卧地上，曰：“勿污我衣，速打！速打！”佟众蜂拥其前，木棒铁尺乱下如雨，一霎体无完肤，四肢不能转侧，犹哂笑怒骂。佟益怒，取棘针一掬，刺入保两足指甲缝中；又用猪鬃，探其尿管，深入二寸许，仍骂不绝口。佟知其终不可伏，急投杖跪而抱持之曰：“君神人也，吾等甘拜下风矣！请破产调摄贵体，愿终身伏事作一鹰犬，肯收录否？”保惫甚，不能作了然语，但首肯而已。佟覆之以衣，畀归家，医治两月始愈，疮痂渐脱，美好如故。遂与佟约为兄弟，逐日与俱。乡邻窃叹，以为保得佟，虎角而翼矣。

    Explanation

    485

    The next day at dawn, Baoyu, alone, went straight to the rear of the Earth Altar and sat waiting for a long time. Eventually, he saw Tong leading about fifteen or sixteen wicked young men, who arrived in a fierce manner. Baoyu met them and shouted, "Have you gathered so many to beat me?" Tong replied, "Yes." Baoyu laughed loudly, "If I were afraid of being beaten, would I dare to come again? Let you rat-like fellows do as you please, but if I were to frown or cry out in pain, I am not a true hero!" As he spoke, he took off his clothes, lay naked on the ground, and said, "Do not dirty my clothes, hit me quickly! Hit me quickly!" Tong and his gang rushed forward, wielding wooden sticks and iron rulers like rain, and in an instant, Baoyu's body was completely covered in wounds, his limbs unable to move. Yet, he still sneered and cursed. Tong became even angrier, took a handful of thorny needles, and stuck them into the gaps between Baoyu's toenails; then, using pig bristles, he probed his urethra, inserting them about two inches deep, while Baoyu continued to curse without stopping. Realizing that Baoyu could not be subdued, Tong quickly threw down his weapon, knelt, and embraced Baoyu, saying, "Sir, you are a divine being! We are willing to yield to your superiority! Please use all your wealth to care for your body, and I wish to serve you for life as a dog and a hawk. Will you accept me?" Baoyu was exhausted beyond measure, unable to speak clearly, but he nodded his head in agreement. Tong covered him with a garment and sent him back home. After being treated for two months, Baoyu recovered, his scabs gradually falling off, and he was as beautiful as before. He then made a pact of brotherhood with Tong, accompanying him every day. The neighbors secretly sighed, believing that Baoyu had gained Tong, like a tiger with horns and wings.

    486

    保居近安定门，门外旧营房之东，故有关帝庙，保与佟暨其党十余人，常聚集于其中。或掇石较力；或悬空架横木，为翻筋斗竖晴蜓诸戏；或在巨竹长数丈，张布为帆，仿白虎幢之制，腾掷身首以示技巧，名曰中幡。入夜，则聚谈开饮，评论某也强，某也弱。所言强者必寻衅，以折辱之，是以睥睨一方，称为土霸。虽屡为官司惩劝，不少悛也。

    　　一日，方与众掷坛为乐，忽一人贸然直前曰：“汝亦闻城南有张阎王乎？”保曰：“亦或闻之。”其人哂曰：“即我是也。”保曰：“来此欲何为？”张于膝裙中出一匕首，长七八寸，甚铦利，举足踏石按匕首于膝，须髯尽张，目眥欲裂，叱保曰：“鼎铛犹有耳，岂不闻张阎王是好汉乎？观汝形貌，不过一女子加弁耳，乃亦盗虚名，称豹子，得不令好汉扫地？今来与汝一较，苟不苛，当留汝命。”“不苛”者，其类创语，犹言“不输”也。

    486

    Bao resided near An Ding Gate, east of the old barracks outside the gate, where there was an old Guan Di Temple. Bao, along with Tong and about a dozen of his associates, often gathered there. Sometimes they would pick up stones to test their strength; other times they would hang a horizontal beam in mid-air and perform acrobatic tricks like somersaults and dragonflies. Occasionally, they would use a tall bamboo pole several meters long, with a cloth stretched as a sail, imitating the design of a white tiger banner, tossing their bodies and heads to display their skills, a performance known as Zhong Fan. At night, they would gather to talk and drink, commenting on who was strong and who was weak. Those they considered strong would seek trouble to humiliate the weaker ones, thus looking down on the area and earning the nickname of local bullies. Despite being punished and warned by officials many times, they did not change their ways at all.

    One day, while playing with others, throwing tiles for fun, suddenly one person boldly stepped forward and said, "Have you heard that there is a Zhang Yama in the south of the city?" Bao replied, "I may have heard of it." The person sneered, "It is me." Bao asked, "What do you want to do here?" Zhang took out a small knife from his skirt, about 7-8 inches long, very sharp and sharp. He stepped on the stone with his foot and placed the knife on his knee, his beard fully spread, his eyes about to burst. He shouted at Bao, "Even a pot has ears, how can you not hear that Zhang Yama is a good man? Looking at your appearance, you are no more than a woman with a hat. You also steal a false name, calling yourself a leopard, isn't it making good men ashamed? Now I come to fight you, if you don't lose, I will let you live." "Not lose" is a colloquial term used in the text, meaning "not to be defeated."

    487

    保睨之而笑，回首视佟曰：“常言太岁头上动土，今果有其人矣，试言何以较量？”张曰：“将此匕首自刺肌肤，不形隐忍之色，汝自审能否？”保拊掌曰：“吾谓挟泰山，超北海，或有不逮，若仅此区区，何云不能！”亟接匕首，退坐石上，裸其右股示张曰：“即刺此可乎？”张曰：“可。”保曰：“但平平一刺，何足道哉！吾试一新汝目！”乃于股上刻划至骨，吱吱有声，劙成“天下太平”四字，皮翻肉突，血流被踵，肌肤白嫩映面，色如胭脂染雪。旁观者无不蹙眉啮齿，代为不耐，而保谈笑自若，似不毫痛楚者。然张大惊，自投于地，曰：“名下故无虚士，小人瞻仰无由，故假此以相试耳，望海涵以恕唐突！”保掖之起曰：“君是吾辈中人，如不弃，请兄事君。”张大喜过望。保得佟、张为左右手，愈纵横无所忌惮。

    Explanation

    487

    Bo glanced at him and smiled, then turned to look at Tong and said, "It is often said that one who dares to challenge the Tai Sui must have a person like you. Let me ask, how do we compare?" Zhang replied, "Take this dagger and pierce your own flesh, showing no sign of restraint, and then you will know if you can." Bo clapped his hands and said, "I thought lifting Mount Tai and leaping over the Bohai Sea might be beyond my reach, but this trivial task, how can it be impossible!" He quickly took the dagger, stepped back and sat on a stone, bared his right thigh for Zhang, and asked, "Can I be pierced here?" Zhang said, "Yes." Bo said, "Just a simple stab, what is there to talk about! Let me give you something new to see!" He then carved the characters "天下太平" into his thigh, the sound of the carving echoing, the skin peeling back, the flesh bulging, blood flowing down his legs, the pale, tender skin reflecting the color of rouge on snow. The onlookers all furrowed their brows and gnashed their teeth, unable to bear it, but Bo talked and laughed as if he felt no pain at all. Zhang was greatly surprised, fell to the ground, and said, "Your reputation is well-deserved, I had no reason to admire you, so I tested you with this. I hope you can forgive my boldness!" Bo helped him up and said, "You are one of us, if you do not reject me, I will treat you as a brother." Zhang was overjoyed. Bo gained Tong and Zhang as his left and right hands, and became even more reckless and fearless.

    488

    上元夜，三人踏灯于四牌楼，漏三下，饮于酒家楼。见一人貌帽狐裘，肥胖长大，年约三旬；又一少年，约二十许，冠紫貂冠，袭黑羔裘。从八九健仆，对席而坐，频目视保，耳语而笑，笑讫复视之。保益作婿态，眼波频溜，二人心醉已久，况加酒醉，少年乃出席向保曰：“元夜相逢，缘却前定，曷不同席一饮，快谈衷曲乎？”佟、张怒，勃然欲动，保肘张而蹑佟之足，即趋对曰：“即蒙垂爱，何幸如之！”二人喜极，拥之入席，狎亵百端，忽少年以所饮余酒斝保曰：“小哥能尽此杯，洵可人也。

    Explanation

    488

    On the Lantern Festival night, three men were enjoying the lanterns at Four Pillar Square. At the third strike of the ancient water clock, around 3 AM, they drank at a restaurant on the upper floor. They saw a man wearing a fur hat and a thick fur coat, who was fat, tall, and about thirty years old. Another young man, around twenty, wore a sable fur hat and a black fur coat. They were accompanied by eight or nine strong servants, and they sat across from each other. The young man frequently looked at Bao with his eyes, whispered and laughed, and then looked at him again. Bao acted more like a potential groom, his eyes constantly flickering, and the two men had been deeply infatuated with him for a long time, especially after getting drunk. The young man then stood up and said to Bao, "Meeting on the Lantern Festival night is destiny. Why not sit together and drink, and have a lively conversation about our true feelings?" Tong and Zhang became suddenly angry and ready to act, but Bao pushed Zhang with his elbow and stepped on Tong's foot, then quickly responded, "If you favor me, how fortunate I am!" The two men were overjoyed and embraced him into their seats, engaging in various forms of intimate behavior. Suddenly, the young man offered Bao a cup of leftover wine and said, "Brother, if you can finish this cup, you truly are delightful."

    489

    ”保一手接杯，一手握其臂，极力扭之，少年大声呼叫，蹲身凳下。中年者，以为戏，方鼓掌而笑，保回肘撞其胸，仰踣于地，佟、张复来相助蹴踢，二人滚地甚苦。众仆乌合抢攘，三人大挥老拳，势不可当。四俯纷纷走散，颠扑狼藉。三人一无所伤，径下楼去。比金吾步军来捕，三人已去远不可踪迹矣。次日处处相传，某宗室在某酒楼，为匪类所窘辱，亦平日恣横恃势之报也。保闻之，意得甚。

    Explanation

    489

    "Ba caught the cup with one hand and grabbed the arm with the other, twisting with great force. The young man shouted loudly and crouched under the bench. The middle-aged man, thinking it was a joke, clapped and laughed. Ba then struck him in the chest with his elbow, causing him to fall backward onto the ground. Tong and Zhang joined in, helping to kick and stomp. The two rolled on the ground in pain. The servants rushed together in a chaotic scene, and the three swung their fists with great force, making them unstoppable. The four scattered in all directions, falling and creating a mess. The three left the scene without any injuries. By the time the imperial guards arrived, the three had already fled far away and could not be found. The next day, the story spread everywhere, describing a noble family member being humiliated at a certain tavern by some ruffians, which was seen as a form of retribution for their usual arrogance and abuse of power. Ba heard about it and felt very pleased."

    490

    会夏日，保偕佟、张游行郊外，小歇一墓门下，论及刚勇，保叹生平不逢敌手。佟曰：“一人善射，百夫决拾。虽然，京师之大如海，岂无杰出之士，惜我辈未遇。”随戟手指门内一冢曰：“弟知之乎？此余斑龙之墓也。余斑龙者，山东临清之回人也，号余大汉，在生时卖大刀丸于庙市，起家数千金。有李存孝之勇。尝与勇士马猛较力，马挥铁锏劈其首，余奋臂一格，铁锏飞坠二十步外，折为三段。又尝生拔鹿角，故号斑龙。吾侪生晚，不获同时，今日对墓景仰，犹令人徘徊不能去。贤弟勿轻量无下士，恐斑龙有知，摄揄于地下也。”保艴然不悦，曰：“斑龙之事，传闻太过。予若遇李存孝，当北面事之；若遇斑龙，正未知鹿死谁手耳。”言次，大雨暴至，抵暮不休。三人四顾，蓦见百步外有鸱吻露树间，冒雨就之，则废寺一区，无有主者。佟、张喜曰：“即此可以宿矣。”携有酒肴，除地坐饮。保终不乐，佟深悔失言，多方引咎。

    Explanation

    490

    On a summer day, Bao accompanied Tong and Zhang on a tour outside the city. They took a brief rest under the gate of a tomb and began discussing bravery. Bao sighed that he had never met an equal in his life. Tong said, "One good shot can make a hundred others miss. Although the capital is vast like the sea, how could there not be outstanding individuals? It is a pity that we have not met them." Pointing with his spear to a tomb inside the gate, he said, "Do you know about it? This is the tomb of Yu Banlong. Yu Banlong, a Hui person from Linqing in Shandong, was nicknamed Yu Dahans. In his lifetime, he sold large knife pills at temple markets and amassed thousands of gold. He was as brave as Li Cunxiao. Once he competed in strength with the brave warrior Ma Meng, who swung an iron mace at his head, but Yu raised his arm and deflected it, causing the mace to fly twenty steps away and break into three pieces. He also once pulled antlers from a living deer, hence the nickname Banlong. We were born too late to meet him, but today we pay respects at his tomb, and it still makes us linger and not want to leave. Brother, do not underestimate ordinary people, for if Yu Banlong knew, he would surely mock us from below." Bao became angry and dissatisfied, saying, "The stories about Yu Banlong are exaggerated. If I met Li Cunxiao, I would serve him with respect; but if I met Yu Banlong, it is uncertain who would win." As they spoke, a sudden heavy rain began, and it did not stop until evening. The three looked around, suddenly seeing a roof finial exposed among the trees a hundred steps away, and they went there in the rain, finding an abandoned temple with no one in charge. Tong and Zhang said happily, "We can stay here tonight." They brought food and wine, cleared the ground, and sat to drink. Bao remained unhappy, and Tong deeply regretted his words, trying to take responsibility in various ways.

    491

    已而雨霁月来，夜近三鼓。保见门外有人窥伺，躯休仿佛甚伟，保叱问：“为谁，宁不知花豹子与佟韦驮、张阎王在此耶？”言未已，其人履阀而入，指保大笑曰：“今来与汝较，果鹿死谁手！”保大怒，右足飞起，其人以手格之，足痛甚，不觉踣地。其人提保之臂，却步出门，保匍匐随之，肘膝并行，直至阶下，蓦然抛掷之，保身起半空，飘飘然如风卷落叶，坠落墙外。其人倏不见。佟、张大呼追救，杳不可得。大索半夜，至天明始得，保于余斑龙墓侧，瞠目僵卧，形如梦魇；呼叫移时，始苏，不能动履。佟、张迭负以归。右足五指俱折，胫跗青肿。

    Explanation

    491

    After the rain cleared and the moon appeared, it was nearly the third watch of the night. Bao saw someone lurking outside the door, whose physique was somewhat impressive. Bao shouted and asked, "Who are you? Surely you know that Hua Baozi, Tong Weituo, and Zhang Yanwang are here!" Before he had finished speaking, the person entered with a certain demeanor and pointed at Bao, laughing loudly, "Now I come to compete with you—indeed, who will be the victor?" Bao became very angry and flew his right foot up. The person blocked it with his hand, and Bao's foot was very painful; he fell to the ground without realizing it. The person grabbed Bao's arm, stepped back and went out, and Bao crawled after him, crawling on his elbows and knees until he reached the steps. Suddenly, he threw Bao, and Bao's body rose into the air, lightly like a leaf being blown by the wind, and fell outside the wall. The person suddenly disappeared. Tong and Zhang shouted and tried to rescue Bao, but could not find him. They sought for a long time during the night and finally found him at dawn near the tomb of Yu Banlong. Bao was staring wide-eyed, lying stiff, looking like he was in a trance. After calling out for a while, he finally regained consciousness but could not move. Tong and Zhang took turns carrying him back home. His right foot's five toes were all broken, and his lower leg and ankle were bruised and swollen.

    492

    保自此爽然若失，幡然而悔，遂折节读书，不复语力。见人谦抑巽顺，犯而不较，卒为善士。或遭素日党类于途，辄逡巡走避，若将浼焉。人有述其向日行径者，即赧然如不自容。佟、张劝其振作，但含笑不语，佯以怒激之，唯敬谢而已。二人无如之何，索然而去，终身誓不相见。后入籍为羽林军，从征缅甸，阵殁，年甫二十有零。

    Explanation

    492

    From this point, he felt as if something had been suddenly lost, and abruptly repented. He then humbled himself to study, no longer speaking of strength. When he encountered others, he was modest, humble, and compliant, forgiving offenses without resentment, and ultimately became a virtuous man. If he encountered former associates on the road, he would hesitate and retreat, as if he would be sullied. When people mentioned his past actions, he would be deeply embarrassed, as if unable to face himself. Tong and Zhang urged him to rise up, but he merely smiled and remained silent, pretending to be angry to provoke a reaction, and only respectfully thanked them. The two could do nothing about it, left disappointedly, and swore never to meet again for the rest of their lives. Later, he enlisted in the imperial guard, joined a campaign to Myanmar, and died in battle, having just turned twenty-something.

    493

    恩茂先曰：一趺辄悟，改过如决，若三官保，真勇者也。

    　　兰岩曰：刚勇自恃，渐至朋党多，而所为不善，祸不旋踵至矣。屡困以极苦事，不稍挫，卒为斑龙一销其戾气，改过迁善，以获安全。余之有施于保者厚矣。虽然，苟非保有从善之机，斑龙有灵，亦将奈何？

    　　倩儿潮州富人江翁，世居南安。一子名澄，小字蛮秀。潮州谓至极曰蛮，以澄韶秀，故字之。年十七，入郡庠。母家姓萧氏，有舅为部郎，殁已数年。妗母王氏，孀居，有一子一女。子六岁，女字倩儿，与澄同庚，艳丽区匹，缙绅之家，竟思委禽。王溺爱其女，择配甚苛，不能既就。澄龆龀时，与女同儿戏。及长，澄务举业，女事针黹，形迹遂相间隔。然每一谋面，澄一心向女，笑靥当迎；女一意注澄，星眸频掷。或王不在前，澄必百计与言，女亦罔拂其意，不吝应答。

    Explanation

    493

    En Maoxian said: With a single step, he realized, and corrected his mistakes decisively, just like San Guan Bao, truly a brave one.

    Lan Yan said: Self-reliant with courage and bravery, he gradually led to many factions, and his actions were not good, disaster would come quickly. Repeatedly faced with extremely difficult situations, he was not slightly discouraged, and finally, the spotted dragon completely dissipated his fierce spirit, correcting his mistakes and turning to goodness, to achieve safety. My contributions to Bao are great. Although, if Bao did not have the opportunity to turn to goodness, and the spotted dragon were to have a spirit, what could it do?

    Qian'er, the son of a wealthy man from Chaozhou, lived in Nan'an for generations. He has a son named Cheng, with the nickname Manxiu. In Chaozhou, 'màn' means 'extreme' or 'ultimate', because Cheng is graceful and elegant, so he was given this nickname. At seventeen, he entered the county school. His mother's family name is Xiao, and he has an uncle who was a departmental official, deceased for several years. His aunt Wang is widowed, and has one son and one daughter. The son is six years old, and the daughter is named Qian'er, born in the same year as Cheng, beautiful beyond compare. Families of officials and scholars competed to propose marriage. Wang deeply loves her daughter and chooses a match very strictly, could not agree to any proposals. When Cheng was young, he played with the girl. When they grew up, Cheng focused on his studies, and the girl engaged in sewing, their paths became separated. However, every time they met, Cheng's heart was directed towards the girl, her smile greeted him; the girl focused her attention on Cheng, her eyes frequently looked at him. When Aunt Wang was not present, Cheng would try every means to talk to her, the girl did not oppose his intentions, did not hesitate to respond.

    494

    一日，同在亲戚家赴汤饼会，女眷满房，饭后有入内更衣者，有匀面理鬓者，有行食院中探花者，扑蝶者，如厕者，惟女独立廊下。适澄自外来，向女索槟榔，女对以无有，澄不信，搜其两袖。方嬉笑间，王猝至，女急欲引避，王呼而止之曰：“儿与尔四哥幼小即在一处，且至亲，莫作小家相，无事回避也。”女含笑应之，澄曰：“妹索槟榔，甥误以豆蔻奉之。妹取之伤廉，故甥笑之。”王亦笑曰：“汝妹素喜食之，尔四哥药肆中，宁无此物？异日勒索百斤，不为多也。”女与澄皆笑，自此稍得亲近。澄或乘间入以游语，亦不甚愠，但作不解，渐至狎昵。

    Explanation

    494

    One day, they were all gathered at a relative's house for a noodle soup banquet. The women filled the room. After the meal, some went inside to change their clothes, others applied makeup and groomed their hair, some went to the garden to look for flowers, others chased butterflies, and still others went to the restroom. Only the girl stood alone under the eaves. At this moment, "澄" arrived from outside and asked the girl for betel nut. The girl replied that she did not have any. "澄" did not believe her and searched her sleeves. While they were laughing, "王" suddenly arrived. The girl tried to avoid the situation, but "王" called her back and said, "You and my fourth brother have been close since childhood, and we are very close. Do not act like strangers; there is no need to avoid each other." The girl smiled and nodded. "澄" said, "My sister asked for betel nut, and I mistakenly gave her a spice called '豆蔻.' She took it, which is inappropriate, so I laughed." "王" also laughed and said, "Your sister usually likes to eat it, and my fourth brother has a medicine shop; he certainly has this spice. If she ever demands a hundred pounds of it, it won't be too much." The girl and "澄" both laughed, and from then on, they became slightly closer. "澄" sometimes entered to chat casually, and the girl did not get angry but pretended not to understand, gradually becoming more intimate.

    495

    值王寿，澄随萧往祝，雨阻不得归。萧、王话旧，夜饮于室。澄与女坐明间，抹牙牌，赌拍臂为戏。女连负，索臂拍之，匿不肯，澄握其腕，揎其袖，用强出之，白如雪，滑如脂，润如藕，澄怜惜之，曰：“如此嫩且白者，忍拍之乎？”戏啮以齿。萧、王闻其嬉笑，呼问之，女绐曰：“四哥赌牌屡负，令其叩头，赖不肯跪耳。”萧、王咸笑曰：“十六七大儿女，尚作此小儿戏耶？”澄与女各笑而退。于是益无忌惮，狎亵无所不至，但无隙及乱耳。

    Explanation

    495

    During the time of Wang Shou, Cheng followed Xiao to offer prayers, but heavy rain prevented them from returning. Xiao and Wang reminisced about the past, drinking in the room. Cheng and the woman sat in the well-lit part of the room, playing a card game and gambling on slapping each other's arms as a game. The woman lost repeatedly, demanded to slap his arm, but he refused to show it. Cheng grasped her wrist, rolled up her sleeve, and forced it out. Her skin was white like snow, smooth like fat, and moist like lotus root. Cheng pitied her, saying, "How can you bear to slap something so soft and white?" He playfully bit her skin. Xiao and Wang heard their laughter and called out to ask what was happening. The woman lied, saying, "Older brother lost repeatedly in the card game, ordered him to kowtow, but he refused to kneel." Xiao and Wang both laughed, saying, "At sixteen or seventeen, even young men and women still play such childish games?" Cheng and the woman each laughed and then withdrew. They became even more brazen, engaging in flirtatious and intimate behavior to any extent, but there was no opportunity to go too far.

    496

    女有婢名春兰者，娇媚慧黠，稍逊于女，女虑其惑澄，防闲甚密。兰怀怨，日伺其衅。会澄以事早见王，王尚未起，女方乱头立栏畔，吸烟看花，澄觑便求哺，女他顾不理，澄突前捧颈，强接其吻，不意为春兰所见，潜告王。王怒呼女至榻前，诘之，女不承，曰：“谁其见之？”王曰：“春兰亲见！无耻婢尚口辩耶？”女颈赤面　，转背欲泪，骂春兰何故妄传飞语，兰含笑而跪曰：“无事，奴敢妄言耶？姑扶栏吃烟，四郎至，求哺良久，姑乃三哺之。无事，奴敢妄言耶？”女羞忿至极，掩面大恸。王召澄，澄已逸去矣。王虽爱女，而事关闺阃，殊深痛恨，不遽假以辞色。萧闻之，亦怒告江翁，挞澄数十，不许复至舅家。女恚甚，哭一日，不食。王气平，爱女之心复炽，密令他婢，私往劝慰，女皆不应，是夜竟投缳。王恸绝数四，悔恨无及，惟痛骂春兰多事而已。

    Explanation

    496

    A Lady Named Chunlan

    A lady had a maid named Chunlan, who was cute, charming, clever, and mischievous, slightly inferior to the lady in status or character. The lady feared that Chunlan might bewitch Chéng, so she guarded her very closely. Chunlan harbored resentment and daily waited for an opportunity. Coincidentally, Chéng happened to see the king early in the morning, and the king had not yet arisen. The lady was casually standing by the railing with disheveled hair, smoking and admiring flowers. Chéng took the opportunity to seek a kiss, but the lady ignored him, looking elsewhere. Chéng suddenly rushed forward, grabbed her neck, and forced a kiss. Unaware that Chunlan had seen them, she secretly reported to the king. The king, furious, summoned the lady to his bed, questioned her, and she denied it, saying, "Who saw it?" The king replied, "Chunlan saw it herself! A shameless maid still tries to argue?" The lady’s neck and face turned red; she turned away, on the verge of tears, and scolded Chunlan for spreading false rumors. Chunlan, smiling and kneeling, said, "If there’s nothing, how dare I speak falsely? The lady was leaning on the railing, smoking, when Fourth Brother came and sought a kiss for a long time, and she kissed him three times. If there’s nothing, how dare I speak falsely?" Embarrassed and furious to the extreme, the lady covered her face and wept bitterly. The king summoned Chéng, but he had already fled. Although the king loved the lady, the matter concerned the inner chambers, and he deeply hated it, not immediately showing leniency. Xiao heard about it and angrily reported it to Uncle Jiang, who lashed Chéng dozens of times and prohibited him from returning to the uncle’s house. The lady was very angry, cried for a whole day without eating. The king’s anger subsided, and his love for the lady flared up again. He secretly ordered another maid to go privately to comfort her, but the lady did not respond. That night, she hanged herself. The king was overcome with grief several times, regretted it too late, and only cursed Chunlan for being meddlesome.

    497

    既葬，澄旦夕追思，神昏形瘠，恒书空作“咄咄怪事”字。屡欲一往哭其墓，无由也。然澄之祖茔，与舅家茔相去仅里许。值中元节，父母皆以疾不往，命澄独往祭扫，因得至女墓，抚冢一尽其哀。是夕归宿其庐。约二更，群动尽息，风木悲鸣，明月满天，四顾清寂；虫声唧唧，絮绕荒阶；萤火星星，乱沾秋草。忆美人黄土，再见无期，欹枕睡床，泪下如雨。俄而星移汉转，竹影筛窗，恍惚间，闻门外弹指声，止而复作。披衣启扉，见一人当户立。视之，女也。惊喜出于非望，携之入室，并坐而泣。此言别恨，彼述离愁，哝哝者久之，始得相与绸缪。女欲澄假托读书，留居于此，澄曰：“此计不谐矣。双堂寝疾，且家有严师，居此无名，请别图之。

    Explanation

    497

    After the burial, Cheng mourned day and night, his mind clouded and body emaciated, always writing in the air the words "Dude, what a strange thing." Many times he wanted to go to her grave to mourn, but could not. However, Cheng's ancestral grave was only about a mile away from his mother-in-law's family grave. On the Zhongyuan Festival, his parents were both ill and could not go, so they ordered Cheng to go alone to pay respects and clean the graves. Thus, he could reach her grave and mourn fully. That night he returned to stay in her house. Around the second watch, all movements ceased; the wind through the trees mournfully howled, the moon was full in the sky, and everything around was quiet and desolate. The sound of insects chirped, cicadas fluttered around the desolate steps, and fireflies twinkled, scattered over the autumn grass. Remembering the beautiful lady now turned to dust, never to see her again, leaning on the pillow, tears fell like rain. Soon the stars moved and the bamboo shadows filtered through the window. In a daze, he heard a finger snapping sound outside, stopping and then starting again. Putting on clothes and opening the door, he saw one person standing by the door. Looking at her, it was her. Joy and surprise came unexpectedly. Taking her into the room, they sat together and wept. She spoke of the pain of separation, he described his own sorrow. They talked for a long time, eventually becoming intimate. She wanted Cheng to pretend to study, stay here. Cheng said, "This plan won't work. My father is sick, and I have a strict teacher at home. Staying here without a reason, please think of another plan."

    498

    ”女颔焉，少间，女曰：“欲暂归家，一省老母，子能导我归乎？”澄曰：“其不可者有三：此去家四十余里，尽属山蹊，卿力弱足纤，断不能至，况乎夜行？此不可者一也；比至家，天且曙，日值中矣。卿生长闺中，足迹不出户庭，出则乘舆，今徒步而返，邻里所惊，此不可者二也；与卿偕行，嫌疑莫避，老父问罪，何以措辞？此不可者三也。有此三不可，卿其鉴之。”女曰：“用志不纷，乃凝于神。

    Explanation

    498

    The woman nodded, and after a short while, she said, "I wish to return home temporarily to visit my elderly mother. Can you guide me there?" The man replied, "There are three reasons why it cannot be done: This place is more than forty miles from home, all of which are mountain paths. You are weak in strength and slender in feet, so you cannot possibly reach it, especially at night. This is the first reason. By the time we arrive at your home, dawn will be approaching, and the sun will be high in the sky. You have never left the house, and when you go out, you ride in a carriage. Now returning on foot would surprise the neighbors. This is the second reason. If I accompany you, there would be no way to avoid suspicion, and my father would ask for punishment. How would I explain it? This is the third reason. With these three reasons, you should consider them carefully." The woman said, "If one's mind is not distracted, then it becomes focused in the spirit."

    499

    儿居此学步久，且思亲甚挚，君第携我行，三不可应不一犯。”澄不忍拂其意，乃扶之以行。甫出门，觉身体轻忽，飘飘然如落叶，因风不克自主，食顷即至舅家。径抵寝室，见王流涕而叹，方嘱家人：“明日可先将酒果香楮往，予后日当亲到倩姐坟头一奠也。”女停足户外不敢入，但掩泣而退，澄曰：“来何草草，去何匆匆？”女曰：“百八蒲牢将动矣。且归休！”遂复同出，遭春兰于厅，女挟旧恨，直前批颊，兰惊扑于地，噤不能语。女不释，命澄褫其裤淫之。淫讫，又取泥土实阴中，始舍去。

    Explanation

    499

    The child had been learning to walk for a long time and was deeply missing his parents. The gentleman could not bear to go against his wish, so he helped him along. As soon as they stepped out the door, he felt his body become light and unsteady, as if floating like a leaf, unable to control himself due to the wind. In a short time, they arrived at the uncle's house. They went directly to the bedroom, where they saw Wang crying and sighing, then instructing the family: "Tomorrow, you may first take alcohol, fruits, and fragrant paper, and on the day after, I will personally go to Qian Sister's grave to offer a memorial." The woman stopped at the doorway, not daring to enter, but covered her face in tears and left. The gentleman said, "Why did you come so hastily and leave so quickly?" The woman replied, "The drum is about to sound. Let's return and rest!" They then went back out together and encountered Chunlan in the hall. The woman, carrying old grudges, slapped her face directly. Chunlan was shocked and fell to the ground, unable to speak. The woman did not let up, and ordered the gentleman to remove her pants and commit a sexual act. Afterward, she took mud and filled it into her private parts before finally letting her go.

    500

    至巷口，有施食者，女与澄亦就食焉。倏忽至山间，月已西沉，明星在东，景甚凄凉。澄曰：“归矣。”女曰：“盍一过我家乎？”澄曰：“方得还，又欲往耶？”曰：“否。谓儿之潜闼也。”穿松林不数十武，至一土穴前，穴大如盏，女拖澄入之，身觉缩小，自视才数寸。既入，四壁皆木，仅可容膝，女与促膝坐，因泣嘱曰：“儿阳数未尽，冥司悉不收录，神魂守此不去，故尸尚完好。苟君不遗，可归告寡母，往祈南关行乞病疥僧，儿可复活也。”澄此时方悟女已死。坐之室，乃其殡宫也。且惊且喜，诺之。顷之，澄欲女仍返其庐，女亦诺之。乃复出穴，步月徐行。

    Explanation

    500

    To the alley entrance, there were people distributing food, and the woman and Cheng also partook of the meal. Suddenly, they arrived at a mountainous area, where the moon had already set and the stars were visible in the east, creating a very desolate scene. Cheng said, "Let's return." The woman asked, "Why not stop by my house?" Cheng replied, "I just managed to return; do you want to go again?" She answered, "No. I mean the hidden door of my child." Passing through the pine forest for not more than several dozen steps, they reached a small earth cave. The cave was as large as a cup, and the woman dragged Cheng inside. Cheng felt himself shrink, and when he looked at himself, he saw that he was only a few inches tall. Once inside, the four walls were made of wood, and the space was barely large enough to accommodate their knees. The woman and Cheng sat close together, and she wept as she instructed, "My child's time in the human world is not yet complete. The underworld does not accept him, so his spirit remains here, and his body is still intact. If you do not forget, you can return home and tell my mother to go to the beggar monk suffering from leprosy at the South Gate, and my child can be revived." At this moment, Cheng realized that the woman had died. The room they were in was her burial chamber. He was both surprised and joyful, and he agreed. Soon after, Cheng wanted the woman to return to her hut, and she also agreed. They then exited the cave and walked slowly under the moonlight.

    501

    既至，澄复见自身僵卧榻上，父母抚之哭于侧，大骇，女推之曰：“几坏尔事，勿逡巡，可急入也。”澄犹延伫，女惶遽，极力挤之，澄觉举身火发，飙然而起，父母惊却数步，注视啜泣曰：“儿苏矣！”澄怅怅者久之，心神始定，问父母何为在此，萧曰：“儿尚梦梦耶？一睡不醒，已一夜一日又半夜矣，谓儿必无生理，胡复不死，且愈之速也！吾一人以儿故，病亦惊失矣。”澄始悟神结之奇。不敢发，但漫应之。

    Explanation

    501

    Having arrived, Cheng saw himself lying stiff on the bed, with his parents comforting him and crying by his side, greatly frightened. The woman pushed him and said, "You almost ruined your matter, do not hesitate, you can quickly enter." Cheng still hesitated, the woman was panicked, and she pushed him with all her might. Cheng felt his whole body burning and suddenly rose up. His parents were startled and stepped back several steps, looking at him and weeping, "The child has revived!" Cheng remained in a state of melancholy for a long time, his mind and spirit finally settling. He asked his parents why they were there, and Xiao said, "Are you still in a daze? Having slept without waking, it has been one night, one day, and half a night, thinking that the child would surely die, why didn't you die, and you recovered so quickly! One person, because of the child, was also sick with shock." Cheng then realized the strange nature of the spiritual binding. He dared not speak, but replied vaguely.

    502

    诘朝，父母与同归，遇王于途，述春兰为鬼所虐状，正符夜来事，澄阴异之。既过王巷口，果有施食三日者，益怪之。因访行乞僧，得诸废寺中。澄膝行蒲伏，以诚恳诉，僧欠伸曰：“呵呵，无知小儿女，草草作事，致老僧多此色相。”遂同诣王，告以能活女之故，王疑信参半，第念事出于创，或有非理之效，姑听之，以觇其术。亟至墓所，掘冢出棺，剖而见尸，颜色不变。僧自顶至踵，以手拿之曰：“已死二寸矣。枯鱼衔索，几何不蠹？再七日，庸得生乎？”探皮囊，取朱色药一粒，大如粟，纳女口中，接其吻以气运之。逾时，闻呻吟者，举体温软，王心喜，如获异珍，以软榻舁入庐。一宿复活，尚不能言，唯握王手涕泣而已。王稽颡谢僧，额为之肿。僧笑而去，其行甚速，追之不及，瞬息失所在。咸知其为异人也。

    Explanation

    502

    The next day, the parents returned together, and on the road, they met Wang. They described the state of Chunlan being tormented by a ghost, which exactly matched what had happened the previous night. Cheng was surprised by it. After passing Wang's lane, they indeed found someone offering food for three days, which made them even more curious. They then visited a begging monk, who was found in a ruined temple. Cheng crawled on his knees and pleaded earnestly. The monk yawned and said, "Haha, ignorant little children, doing things carelessly, causing this old monk to have so many appearances." Then, they went together to Wang, telling him about the reason for being able to revive the girl. Wang was half-believing and half-doubting, but thought that the matter came from a novel approach, and perhaps there would be some irrational effects. So, he decided to listen to it, to test the monk's skills. They hurriedly went to the tomb, dug up the grave, and removed the coffin, cutting it open and seeing the body, which appeared unchanged. The monk took hold of it from head to toe and said, "She has been dead for two inches. A dried fish hanging on a rope, how long before it rots? Another seven days, how could it live?" He searched in the bag, took out a red pill as big as a grain of millet, put it into the girl's mouth, and applied his breath to her lips. After a while, they heard moaning, and the body became warm and soft. Wang was delighted, as if he had gained a rare treasure, and carried her into the hut on a soft stretcher. After one night, she revived, but still could not speak, only holding Wang's hand and weeping. Wang bowed his head to thank the monk, and his forehead swelled. The monk laughed and left, his departure very fast, and they could not catch up to him. He disappeared in an instant. Everyone knew he was a strange person.

    503

    女还家，卧病月余，形始复初，唯两足至踝，常冷如冰，僧所云已死二寸之说，亦信。王感澄义，即以女妻之，琴瑟甚敦。上官老人周与江翁善，知之颇稔，尝为予述之。

    　　兰岩曰：天下好事，本可顺理而成，往往多生魔障，致令美人黄土，佳士伤心，终成恨事。然必系不省事妇女，拘执腐见，率意为之。幸天不忍令此情种，卒为情死，生一异僧以全之，使人心一大快。噫！天下亦安得常有此僧，以活此可人哉！

    　　褦襶有官沈阳者，署中传有鬼物，往日被惊悸而死者，男女接踵。官留心伺之，夜间果见一物，通体乌黑，无头无面无手足，唯二目雪白，一嘴尖长如鸟啄，乍见亦甚可惧。后无夜不至，遂亦习之，渐至狎匿。物亦娴熟，麾之不去，招之即来，间尝戏以手捺其顶，随手消灭；捺至地，灭亦尽，浑如烟雾，软如棉絮；甫招手，寻复充仞如故。甚异之。因其块然一物，名之曰褦襶，呼之辄前。

    Explanation

    503

    The woman returned home and lay ill for over a month, her form beginning to return to its original state. Only her feet up to the ankles were always cold like ice, and the monk's claim that she had died two inches was also believed.
