Moved by Chen's righteousness, the king immediately married the woman to him, and their marital relationship was very harmonious. The elderly Mr. Shangguan Zhou, who was friends with Mr. Jiang, knew about it very well and once told me about it.

    Lanyan said: Good things in the world can be accomplished smoothly, but they often encounter obstacles, causing beautiful women to become dust and talented men to grieve, ultimately becoming regrettable events. However, it is usually due to women who do not understand the situation and hold outdated views, acting on impulse. Fortunately, heaven did not allow this kind of person to die for love, but instead produced a strange monk to preserve them, making people feel greatly relieved. Alas! How can the world always have such a monk to save such a person?

    There was an official named Shen Yang, who was called 'Naidai.' There was a rumor of a ghost in the office, and in the past, many men and women died from being frightened. The official carefully observed it. At night, he indeed saw a thing: it was completely black, with no head, face, hands, or feet, only its two eyes were snow white, and its beak was sharp and long like a bird's beak. At first sight, it was very frightening. Afterward, it came every night, and he gradually got used to it, becoming intimate and close. The thing also became familiar, not leaving when he waved it away but coming when he called it. Sometimes he would playfully press his hand on its head, and it would disappear at his touch. When pressed to the ground, it would completely vanish, like smoke and fog, soft like cotton. As soon as he waved his hand, it quickly returned to its original form. He found it very strange. Because it was a solid thing, he named it 'Naidai,' and calling it would make it come forward.

    504

    一夕寒夜思酒，家人皆睡，无人行沽，褦襶适在侧，戏之曰：“汝能为沽酒乎？”声呦呦，似应诺然。官乃以青蚨数十并一瓶，置其顶上。褦襶去，俄顷已在面前，顶上有瓶无钱矣，取之白酒满中，大喜。自是零星细物，无不遣之。市物之家，但失物得钱，传以为怪，唯官心明其故，特秘而不宣。数年，未尝须臾离。会考满，得闽中一郡，既束装，褦襶依依，似不忍舍，官亦怅悒。

    　　抵闽逾岁，靡日不思。偶独立，褦襶忽至，大惊喜，呼之入室，眷属惊怔。官白其故，家人亦素闻其事，遂各相安。及见惯，无不怜其驯者。亲友亦多见之。又岁余，失褦襶所在，举家怀思，后竟不复至。

    Explanation

    504

    One cold night, craving wine, the official's family were all asleep, and no one was available to fetch it. The dog, named Nai Tai, happened to be nearby, and the official teased it by saying, "Can you go fetch some wine?" The dog responded with a soft, affirmative sound, as if agreeing. The official then placed several pieces of ancient currency and a bottle on its head. The dog returned quickly with the bottle, but the money was gone. The bottle was full of white wine, which delighted the official. From then on, no small or trivial items were sent without the dog. Merchants found it strange that they lost items but received money, and only the official knew the reason, keeping it secret. For many years, the dog was always present. When the official was reassigned to a region in Fujian Province, he packed his belongings, and the dog, reluctant to leave, seemed unwilling to part. The official also felt sad. After arriving in Fujian, the official missed the dog every day. One day, the dog suddenly appeared, causing great surprise. The official called it into the room, and the family was stunned. The whole family missed the dog, but it never returned.

    505

    白　衣　怪御史洋公海巡视南城，一夜大雨，驱车过梁家园，从三骑，冒雨行。远远见二人白衣白冠，杖策，循人家屋檐，伛偻自北来。辕下驹鼻鸣耳耸，惊骇不前，仆夫连鞭之，马负痛而奔，相去约丈余，二人以袖蔽面，蹀蹀徐行，所至之地，雨水随步划然开数尺，哭哀哀而过，折入小巷中去。从人悉见之。唯洋及仆夫独见其面白如粉，巨口至耳，吻若涂朱云。

    　　兰岩曰：鬼多哀哭，岂自悲其死耶？抑悲人之生不知死耶？悲人之生亦等于死耶？

    　　某　领　催内务府领催某甲，家在阜城门外某庄，去城七八里。逐日公事毕，则乘一健骡归去，往往至夜。路旁故有井，骡过饮水而后行，率以为常。去井数十武，有歧径，较官道近里许，然极荒僻，骡行贯，至此必嘶奔而就之，虽极力鞭勒，终舍大路而弗由也。

    Explanation

    505

    Translation

    The white-clothed strange censor Yáng Gōng Hǎi was inspecting the southern city when, one night during heavy rain, he drove his carriage through Liáng Garden, accompanied by three riders, braving the rain. From afar, he saw two figures in white robes and white caps, holding staffs, walking along the eaves of people's houses, stooped over and coming from the north. The horse under the carriage snorted and had its ears pricked up, frightened and refusing to move forward. The servant lashed the horse repeatedly, but the horse, in pain, ran away, leaving a distance of about ten feet. The two figures covered their faces with their sleeves, walked slowly and cautiously, and wherever they stepped, the rainwater split open several feet. They passed by crying sadly and turned into a small alley and disappeared. Everyone else saw them, but only Yáng Gōng Hǎi and the servant saw their faces as white as powder, with huge mouths reaching to their ears, and lips like painted red.

    Lán Yán said: Ghosts often cry, is it because they are mourning their own deaths? Or are they grieving that people do not know they will die? Are they grieving that people's lives are also equal to death?

    A low-ranking official of the Imperial Household Department, named Mǎ Jiǎ, lived in a village several miles outside the city walls of Fùchéngmen. Every day after completing his official duties, he would ride a strong mule back home, often returning at night. There was an old well by the side of the road, and the mule would drink water from it before continuing. This became a regular habit. About fifty paces from the well, there was a fork in the road that was a little closer than the main road, but it was very desolate. Whenever the mule reached this point, it would neigh and run towards the well, no matter how hard the servant tried to control it, the mule would always choose the less-traveled path instead.

    506

    一日，归去既晚，又于关中遇一相识，拉入酒肆中，盘桓一饷，始得脱身。比至井旁饮骡讫，已二鼓余矣。时际初秋，树木荫浓，黍稷夹道，虽有微月，为轻云所蔽，亦不甚明朗。即入歧径，纵辔而前。乱蛰唧唧，四顾无人。蓦见一灯光自远而来，其行甚速，隐隐有声如报马。默念夜将半亦，是何事件，急如星火。俄而声渐近，相去约一矢地，骡耳耸鼻鸣，窜入黍稷中，执勒不住。灯光顺路而至，甲侧目审顾，非报马也，第见一无首妇人，裸身浴血，双手自奉其头，口眼向天，颈血作碧光，如萤火，如小镜，瞬息已远。甲大骇，急驰而归，面无人色，备述所见于其父。其父亦凿凿究理者，戒之曰：“深夜荒郊，何所不有，况汝所遇者，刑天之流亚也，保不受其殃乎？嗣后但早归，苟太晏，城中亲故处，何妨一宿。今既经此异，再不知慎，非老人之所安也。”甲唯唯受教。

    Explanation

    506

    One day, after returning home very late, Jia met a familiar acquaintance in the Guanzhong region, who pulled him into a tavern. They lingered for a while before Jia finally managed to escape. By the time he reached the well to give the mule water, it was already past two drums. It was the beginning of autumn, with dense and shady trees lining the road, and grain crops growing alongside. Even though there was a faint moon, it was obscured by thin clouds, making it not very bright. Jia immediately entered a forked path and let the reins go, riding forward. Insects buzzed in confusion, and looking around, there was no one. Suddenly, he saw a light coming from afar, moving very fast, with a faint sound like that of a horseman. Silently, he thought that it was nearly midnight, wondering what this urgent event could be. Soon the sound grew closer, and it was about the distance of an arrow shot away. The mule's ears went up, and it whinnied, darting into the grain crops, which Jia could not control. The light followed the road, and Jia glanced sideways and looked carefully. It was not a horseman; he only saw a woman without a head, naked and covered in blood, holding her head with both hands, mouth and eyes facing upwards, with the blood from her neck glowing green, like fireflies, like small mirrors. In an instant, it was far away. Jia was greatly frightened and rushed back home, his face pale. He carefully described what he had seen to his father. His father, who was also someone who carefully investigated things, warned him, saying, "Deep into the night in a desolate countryside, anything can happen, especially since what you encountered is similar to Xingtian. How can you be sure you won't suffer from it? Afterwards, just return home early. If it's too late, it's not a problem to stay overnight at the places of relatives and friends in the city. Now that you have experienced this strange thing, you must be more cautious. This is not something I can be at ease with." Jia nodded and accepted his father's advice.

    507

    阅数月，甲复晚散，忆家中小儿出痘，不可不归，且阴计怪异之事偶或遭之，讵必常有？骡导其故道仍如曩时，复往其处，方回溯当日主况，未已，远远灯光，随声又来，不一而足，益而三焉。甲屡选怯，不待骡惊，鞭入田中。此时黍稷已获，一望旷朗。须臾三物，鱼贯而至，形状犹昔，唯增一男。骡一见惊嘶，三物截然而止，并立向甲啾啾作声，如小儿吹葱然。甲不觉褫魄，昏坠骡下。其父见骡之独逸以归也，知其子有变，即鸠合家人，操兵执炬，觅至所说僻径，遍索田中，良久始获，抢攘舁归，呼救半夜始苏，更述其怪，闻者罔不错愕。其父延缁羽为禳，不复有效，越数日竟死。

    Explanation

    507

    After several months, Jia returned late from a gathering, remembering that his young child had smallpox and could not avoid returning home. Moreover, he secretly calculated that strange events might occasionally occur, but they are not always present. The mule led the old path as before, returning to that place. Jia was just recalling the situation of that day when far-off lights, accompanied by sounds, came one after another, increasing to three in number. Jia repeatedly chose to be timid, without waiting for the mule to be startled, he lashed it into the field. At this time, the millet and grain had been harvested, and the landscape was vast and clear. In a moment, three beings appeared in a line, their shapes the same as before, but with an additional male. The mule, upon seeing them, neighed in fright, and the three beings stopped abruptly and stood together, making sounds like children blowing through chives. Jia was so frightened that he lost his soul and fainted from the mule.

    His father, seeing the mule return alone, knew something was wrong with his son, and immediately gathered the family, armed with weapons and torches, to search the described secluded path. They searched the fields for a long time before finding Jia, who was carried back in a rush. He was called for help and woke up in the middle of the night, recounting the strange events. Those who heard him were all surprised and shocked. His father invited monks and Taoists to perform rituals, but they were no longer effective. After several days, Jia died.

    508

    兰岩曰：岂其有宿冤耶？抑阳衰阴盛，死期将至耶？不然非其所害，辄两遇之，而卒以亡也，职何故哉？

    　　宋　秀　才鄂渚宋秀才，迍踬名场，感世情淡泊。少时游江陵，晚过城隍，遇一道士，　面重颐，须长四尺许，白如雪。宋奇其貌，邀至寓所进酒食，皆不辞。及对酒纵谈，语多玄妙，宋知为异人，叩及荣悴。道士曰：“吾闻神人无功，圣人无名，君将为名乎？名者，实之宾也，君将为宾乎？”宋大惭，因问长生之术，道士曰：“人世乌得有长生，君能去宾务实，即长生之道也。君不闻刘纲之言乎？‘大凡人寿皆可至百年，而以七情六欲，伐根竭源，颠倒方寸，顷刻万变，神倦思怠，难全天和。譬彼淡泉，汩于五味，欲不财坏，弗可得矣。’君未尝知此，何处得长生！”宋拜谢。

    Explanation

    508

    Lan Yan said, "Could it be that there is an ancient grudge? Or is it that yang (positive energy) is waning and yin (negative energy) is flourishing, signaling the approach of death? If it is not due to harm from a specific cause, why did he encounter it twice, and ultimately perish? What is the reason for this?"

    The Scholar Song, from Ezhu, had repeatedly failed in his pursuit of fame and felt disillusioned with the world. In his youth, he traveled to Jiangling. One evening, as he passed by the city隍, he encountered a Taoist priest. The priest had a prominent jawline, a long beard about four feet in length, and hair as white as snow. Song was struck by his appearance and invited him to his residence to offer wine and food, which the priest accepted without hesitation. As they drank and conversed freely, the priest's words were filled with profound and mysterious wisdom. Song realized that he was an extraordinary person and asked about the causes of prosperity and decline. The priest said, "I have heard that a god-like person does not seek fame, and a sage does not seek recognition. Are you seeking fame? Fame is but a guest to reality, are you seeking to be a guest?" Song was greatly ashamed and then asked about the secret of longevity. The priest replied, "In this world, how can there be true immortality? If you can discard the pursuit of fame and focus on substance, that is the path to longevity. Have you not heard the words of Liu Gang? 'Generally, human life can reach a hundred years, but the seven emotions and six desires cut at the roots and exhaust the sources, confusing the mind and undergoing countless changes in an instant. The spirit becomes weary and the mind becomes lazy, making it difficult to maintain the natural harmony of life. Like clear water, it becomes turbid when mixed with the five flavors. If one does not guard against this, it is impossible to avoid ruin.' If you do not understand this, how can you achieve immortality?" Song bowed in gratitude.

    509

    是夜月如昼，道士曰：“能从我游乎？”宋曰：“固所愿也。”道士乃于怀袖间，出纸鹤二，以水噀之，暴长如生者。与宋各跨其一，嘱勿回顾，以掌拍鹤背，祝曰：“起！”鹤即鼓翼长鸣，飞翔云表，鹤背安稳如北地冰床。俯瞰下土，历历如掌上之纹。道士一手捉宋臂，指点江山，谓：某处烟一点，某府某州某县也；某处培砄，或如覆杯，如连冢，某山某岳也；又指一缕水，光如银线然，曰：“长江也。”宋问洞庭安在，道士指一点光小如镜者，曰：“彼是也。”宋阴念一身蜩寄世间，真如恒河一沙，沧海一粟，吾生亦何有涯？所不能痛处一刀者，妻子之情耳。念未息，道士喟然撒手，宋飘然而坠，如因风秋叶，寸肤不伤。有闻声出视者，则其妻与子女也，相见各惊异。宋具言其事，且嘱曰：“不足为外人道也。”自是神仙之事，汲汲求之，不复仕进。长沙郭昆甫解元俊，与其长子同年，曾述其说如此。

    Explanation

    509

    That night, the moon was as bright as day. The Taoist priest said, "Would you like to join me on a journey?" C responded, "It is indeed what I wish for." The Taoist priest then took two paper cranes from his sleeves, sprinkled water on them, and they suddenly grew to look alive. He and C each mounted one, instructing them not to look back, and they clapped their hands on the cranes' backs, chanting, "Up!" The cranes immediately flapped their wings and let out long calls, soaring into the clouds. The cranes' backs felt as stable as an ice bed in the north. Looking down on the earth, everything was clearly visible as if it were the lines on one's palm. The Taoist priest held C's arm with one hand, pointing out the landscape, saying, "That point of smoke is a certain prefecture, county, and state; that mound of earth, sometimes like an overturned cup or a series of graves, is a certain mountain or peak; and that thread of light, shining like a silver thread, is the Yangtze River." C asked where Lake Dongting was, and the Taoist priest pointed to a small spot of light like a mirror and said, "That is it." C silently reflected that he was but a tiny grain of sand in the vast Ganges River, a speck of rice in the ocean, and that his life had no bounds. The only thing that prevented him from taking a decisive step was his affection for his wife and children. As he was still pondering, the Taoist priest sighed and let go, and C fell gracefully, like a leaf in the autumn wind, without suffering any harm. Those who heard the sound and came out to see were his wife and children, and they were all amazed to see him. C recounted the entire event and instructed them, "Do not tell outsiders about this." From then on, he eagerly sought after matters of immortals, no longer pursuing official positions. Changsha's Guo Kunfu, who was also a scholar, and his eldest son, both of whom were in the same year, once recounted the story in this way.

    510

    闲斋曰：予少游湟中，临青海，水之清如潇湘，深如彭泽，遥望波心烟一点，番人曰：“龙驹岛也，周回约千里，其大倍于洞庭。”其后游闽，登夏门，观冥海，则青海犹盆池也。吁！水大亦何常之有？所见大，则所过皆小；所见小，则所过皆大。覆杯水于堂坳之上，群蚁过之，如洪水之怀山襄陵也固宜。

    Explanation

    510

    Idle Studio says: When I was young, I traveled to the region of湟 and approached Qinghai Lake. The water was as clear as the潇湘 rivers and as deep as彭泽. Looking far into the heart of the waves, a wisp of smoke appeared. The locals said, "It is the Dragon Horse Island, circling about a thousand li, its size double that of Dongting Lake." Later, I traveled to Fujian, climbed Xia Gate, and observed the冥海. Then Qinghai Lake seemed like a small pond. How can the size of water be constant? What one sees as large makes everything else seem small; what one sees as small makes everything else seem large. Pouring a cup of water into a low place in the hall, the ants passing over it would seem like a flood surrounding mountains and hills, which is only natural.

    511

    护　军　女某护军女，有殊色，十九未嫁。邻家一少年，甫二十，亦为护军，素以丰姿自诩。窥女，艳之，时乘间以言色相挑，女辄引避。

    　　偶值其父从军征南，母亦归宁，唯女在室，及一老妪。少年侦知之，故拍板壁，借用烟具，女不应。少年以刀挖板一孔如钱大，以目就之，向女笑曰：“借一烟袋，何便靳惜？”女见之，勃然怒，寻即色定，冁然曰：“素不相识，那便以物相假？”少年得其应答，惊喜欲狂，挑之曰：“子勿作态，今既能钻穴相窥，莫谓不能逾墙相搂也。”女曰：“既此一孔，已足盘桓，何必涉险。”话间眄睐其目，愈增妩媚。少年心动，伸一指入孔，女遽握之。少年心大动，谓其可诱，乃低语以睻之曰：“我有一物，子识之乎？”女曰：“是何希罕物？”少年曰：“子姑视之。”亟解盉出势，纳入孔中。女即捉之，佯为摩弄，潜扳鬓钗横贯之，脱颖而出。少年僵立痛甚，号叫声嘶。女出房扃其户，置若罔闻。

    Explanation

    511

    The Young Woman of the Guard

    A young woman from a family of military officers, she possessed exceptional beauty and was nineteen years old but had not yet married. A young man from a neighboring household, just twenty years old and also a military officer, always boasted of his handsome appearance. He secretly admired the girl and often tried to flirt with her using words and expressions, but she always avoided him.

    Coincidentally, her father went to war in the south, and her mother also returned home to visit her parents. Only the girl and an old woman were at home. The young man discovered this and began knocking on the wall to communicate. He asked to borrow tobacco tools, but the girl did not respond. The young man then used a knife to dig a hole in the wall as big as a coin and looked through it. He smiled at the girl and said, "Why be so stingy? Lend me a pipe." The girl saw him and became angry, but soon calmed down and smiled. "We don't know each other, so why should I lend you anything?" she asked. The young man was delighted by her response and said, "Don't pretend. Now that we can peek at each other through this hole, why say we can't climb over the wall and embrace each other?" The girl replied, "This one hole is enough for us to linger. Why take risks?" As she spoke, she glanced at him, becoming even more charming. The young man was moved and extended a finger into the hole. The girl quickly grabbed it. The young man was greatly moved and thought he could tempt her. He whispered to her, "I have something. Do you recognize it?" The girl asked, "What kind of rare thing is it?" The young man said, "Just look at it." He quickly undid something and revealed it, inserting it into the hole. The girl immediately grabbed it, pretending to rub and play with it. She secretly grabbed a hairpin and pierced it through, causing the object to come out sharply. The young man stood stiffly in great pain, shouting with a hoarse voice. The girl left the room and locked the door, treating it as if she had not heard him.

    512

    少年有妹，闻之往瞰，骇极，奔告其母。母趋至，百计不能救，乃过女家，长跪求免。女曰：“待娘回，当释汝儿。”母大窘，奔其母家求之。母与其弟偕归，女见母大哭，觅死；慰藉再四方止。舅启户，见少年势，怒且笑曰：“此亦足以小创而大惩矣！”骂而拔钗，少年昏绝仆地。扛之入室，医治月余方愈，遂徙去。

    　　闲斋曰：此固一大快事，然不足为训也。夫女子不能正色闲邪，故作媚态以导淫，是罔人也。焉有处子守礼，罔人而可为也？昔山左李氏，因逆旅主人拖其臂，则断臂以自洁。女虽自贞，而纤手已污，终属杂霸，岂曰行权。

    Explanation

    512

    A young man had a sister. Hearing of the situation, she went to see and was terrified. She ran to tell her mother. The mother rushed to the scene, tried every means but could not save her, so she visited the girl's home and kneeled down, begging for mercy. The girl said, "Wait until I return, then I will release your son." The mother was in great distress and rushed to her mother's house to seek help. The mother and her brother returned, and the girl saw her mother, crying bitterly and trying to die. Her mother consoled her, and after four attempts, she finally stopped. The uncle opened the door, saw the young man's situation, and scolded and pulled out his hairpin, causing the young man to faint and fall to the ground. They carried him into the room and treated him for over a month before he recovered, then they moved away.

    The narrator said: "This is indeed a great joy, but it is not a good example. A woman who cannot maintain a proper demeanor to avoid evil, therefore making seductive gestures to lead to lust, is deceiving people. How can a maiden who upholds propriety deceive people and be considered virtuous? In ancient times, a woman from the eastern part of the state of Qi, because the innkeeper grabbed her arm, she cut off her own arm to maintain her purity. Although the woman maintained her chastity, her delicate hands were already tainted, ultimately making her a mixed ruler, not a true authority."

    513

    兰岩曰：少年男女，一壁之隔，其窥探情动，亦所必有。乃遗女归宁，其母亦失于检点，致令出此丑态。乃舅乃母，不闻一惩其女之摩弄非理，而但笑少年之足创，其家教亦概可知矣。轻薄至死，夫谁惜此少年哉？

    　　卷四秀姑太原布客田辚，美姿容，喜吟啸，少失怙恃，兄弟皆故，一身仅存。年二十，茕茕落魄，亲戚多不齿数，颇无聊赖。乃尽鬻田宅，获百金，入都营运。半年，子母几相等，因思归娶。携装策蹇，将出广宁门，适过菜市口，值秋决，刑人于市，阻不得进。田故少年好事，挨挤稠人中，延颈歧足，以看杀人。良久，觉腰间顿轻，用手扪結，则腰缠尽失，盖也为劙囊者携去矣。瞠目结舌，手足无所措，幸余一驴，牵之入市，并鞍辔售得五金。归娶之念顿息，独坐逆旅中，辗转无策，唯忆其姑母嫁卫辉，盍往就之？

    Explanation

    513

    Lan Yan said: Young men and women, a wall apart, their curiosity and emotions stirred, are also inevitable. To send the daughter back to her parents' home, her mother also failed to be vigilant, causing her to display such shameful behavior. Her maternal uncle and mother, they did not hear of any punishment for her improper touching, but only laughed at the youth's foot injury. The family's discipline can be inferred from their actions. To be so frivolous as to die, who would care about this youth?

    Chapter Four: Xiu Gu, the Cloth Merchant from Taiyuan, Tian Lin was a handsome man who enjoyed poetry and song. He lost his parents at a young age, and his brothers had all passed away, leaving him as the sole survivor. At twenty years old, he was destitute and friendless, with few relatives who cared for him. He felt utterly aimless. So, he sold all his land and property, earning a hundred gold pieces, and went to the capital to conduct business. After six months, his profits nearly equaled his initial investment, and he began to think of returning home to marry. He packed his belongings and set out on a donkey, intending to leave through Guangning Gate. Just as he passed by the vegetable market, it was the time of autumn executions, and the city was crowded with people watching the executions. Tian, being a curious young man, pushed through the crowd, stretching his neck and straining to see the executions. After a long time, he felt a sudden lightness in his waist. He reached down to feel and discovered that his money belt had been stolen. He was left speechless and at a loss, but fortunately, he still had one donkey left. He led the donkey into the market and sold it along with its saddle and bridle for five gold pieces. His thoughts of returning home to marry were suddenly extinguished. He sat alone in a roadside inn, turning over the situation in his mind with no solution. All he could think of was his aunt who had married in Weihui. Perhaps he should go there to seek her out.

    514

    于是负囊就道，将至顺德，日已曛暮，四顾旷野，渺无人烟。方追程前进，瞥见林间，灯火闪烁，自北而南。心稍定，急趋赴之。则一垂髫婢，提白葵花灯，导一女郎，绿衣红裙，盖十八九，绝代姝也。田踵之以行，相去有咫尺。女回顾见之，促婢速行，田不少却。女且行且顾，若甚慌怯者。因循里许，女挥汗且喘，止步谓婢曰：“且稍停，让渠捷足者先行，无事追随，成何光景。”其声呖呖，如微风振箫。田聆之，神出于舍，趋向路侧，以揖之曰：“小人失路，茫茫无所之，欲从小娘子觅一宿，未卜可肯假一席地否？”女以袖障面，侧身低笑，向婢小语曰：“孟浪人有如此者！”婢亦吃吃不已。良久，女始忍笑应曰：“家有母氏为政，儿凡百不与闻，姑至舍，试为汝告白，去留听再决也。”田诺诺，复从之。

    Explanation

    514

    Then, carrying his bag and setting out on the road, he was nearing Shunde when the sun had already set, and all around him was a vast, desolate wilderness with no sign of human habitation. Just as he was following the path forward, he caught a glimpse of lights flickering in the forest, moving from north to south. His heart slightly calmed, and he hastened to approach them. There was a young girl, perhaps around eighteen or nineteen years old, dressed in a green robe and red skirt, accompanied by a young maid with hanging hair who carried a white calyx flower lamp. Tian followed behind them, keeping a few steps' distance. The young girl looked back and saw him, urging the maid to hurry, but Tian did not back away. The young girl walked while glancing back, appearing very fearful and hesitant. After walking for a few li, the young girl was sweating and panting, and she stopped to say to the maid, "Let's stop for a moment. Let the swift-footed one go ahead. What kind of scene is this, following without purpose?" Her voice was clear and melodious, like a gentle breeze vibrating a flute. Tian listened to her, feeling entranced, and moved to the side of the road, bowing and saying, "A lowly person lost in the road, vastly without a destination, wants to seek shelter for the night from the young lady. Uncertain if you would allow a place to rest." The young girl covered her face with her sleeve, leaned aside, and laughed softly, whispering to the maid, "There are such reckless people!" The maid chuckled continuously. After a long time, the young girl finally restrained her laughter and replied, "The mother rules the household, and I am not involved in anything. Let's go to the house, try to inform her. Whether to leave or stay will be decided again." Tian nodded affirmatively and followed her again.

    515

    行又里许，始至，门户整洁，居然富家。婢扣门，一媪出启扉，絮絮怨女何归之晚，女曰：“为阿婻所纠缠，不容摆脱。若非婢子矫娘命，几不得归。路上又遇一失路人，再三求住，聒聒不休。不晓今日出门，向着其底凶煞，令人薅恼竟日！”媪曰：“何物失路人，擅与人家闺秀借宿？若使遇着老身，当挤却渠两睾丸，问渠尚敢佻达向人否？”女莈袖而笑，回眸睇田曰：“闻之否？设想已左，不知及早之他，勿得诟谇。”田逡巡欲去，媪止之，举烛审照曰：“颈以山而瘦，齿以晋而黄，水土使之然也。视小郎面白发浓，脚大腿长，大类山西人。郎岂山西人耶？”田曰：“然。”媪曰：“然则乡里也。何难下蜗居一草榻，暂屈一宵，乃可峻拒乎？”

    Explanation

    515

    After walking another li or so, they finally arrived at a place. The door was clean and tidy, and it turned out to be a wealthy household. The maid knocked on the door, and an elderly woman came out to open it. She scolded the girl for coming back late, saying, "You were纠缠 by Ah Nan, couldn't escape, and if not for the maid pretending to be the mistress, you almost couldn't return. On the way, you also met another lost traveler who repeatedly asked to stay, kept talking incessantly." The girl smiled while holding up her sleeves and turned her eyes to look at Tian, saying, "Have you heard this? The idea is already wrong. Didn't know to leave early, shouldn't be scolded." Tian hesitated and wanted to leave, but the old woman stopped him, lit a candle to examine him, and said, "Her neck is thin like a mountain, her teeth are yellow like those of someone from Jin, it's due to the water and soil. Look at the young master with pale skin and thick hair, long legs, looks a lot like a person from Shanxi. Are you from Shanxi?" Tian said, "Yes." The old woman said, "Then he is from the same village. Why not stay in my humble home for one night, stay for one night, why should I refuse so harshly?"

    516

    亟引入，设酒相款，问何姓，曰：“田。”媪曰：“老身母家亦姓田。亦太原籍乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“十八都田布商同谱乎？”田欠身曰：“小人之祖也。”媪愕然，曰：“老身之父也。汝父何名？”曰：“终亩。”媪大骇，起握田手，熟视其面，曰：“汝真田十二之子耶？老身去家时，十二弟才十三岁，犹未议婚。音问梗塞，近四十年矣，不谓阿咸如此成立。老身为汝父胞妹，汝之姑也。汝虽后生，岂不闻汝有三姑母，嫁为卫辉杨家妇者乎？”田骤闻之，悲喜交并，趋拜膝下曰：“侄实将往卫辉，投托姑母，不意邂逅于此！”媪曳之起，且泣曰：“老身移此十二年矣，非天假之缘，焉能相遇之巧。汝父母无恙乎？”田亦泣之曰：“侄七八岁时，皆已下世矣。二兄一弟，亦相继病殁。生业凋谢，孤孑至今。

    Explanation

    516

    The host quickly invited him in, served wine to entertain him, and asked what his surname was. He replied, "Tan." The old woman said, "My maternal family also bears the surname Tan. Are you also from Taiyuan?" He answered, "Yes." She asked, "Are you from the same clan as the eighteen-town textile merchants?" The man inclined his body slightly and said, "My ancestor." The old woman was startled and said, "He was my father. What was your father's name?" He replied, "Zongmu." The old woman was greatly shocked, stood up, took his hand, and looked at his face closely. She said, "Are you truly the son of Tan Shier? When I left home, Tan Shier was only thirteen years old and had not yet arranged his marriage. We lost contact for nearly forty years, and I did not expect that Ah Xian would have grown up so well. I am your father's younger sister, your aunt. Although you are younger, have you not heard of your three aunts, who married into the Yang family of Weihui?" The man was overwhelmed with a mix of sorrow and joy, hurriedly knelt before her and said, "I was actually planning to go to Weihui to seek help from my aunt. I never expected to meet her here!" The old woman pulled him up and wept, saying, "I have been here for twelve years. Without the help of heaven, how could we meet so serendipitously? Are your parents well?" The man also wept and said, "When I was seven or eight years old, they had already passed away. My two brothers and one younger brother also died in succession. Our family business has declined, and I have been alone until now."

    517

    ”媪太息感伤者久之，又问曰：“儿年岁何矣？”曰：“二十。”媪谓女曰：“汝表兄也。”女拜，田答拜。媪曰：“姑无儿，只生汝妹一人，取字秀姑，娇养惯，一事不关心，年十八，尚尔憨跳。汝姑父殁后，家中更无男子。幸儿来，足以把持门户，留心为汝妹觅一人家，则老身之事毕矣。”田曰：“表妹秀慧如此，无虑不归世族。”言讫，以目睃女，女羞晕两颊，默然俯鬟拈带而已。媪曰：“儿娶乎？”曰：“未聘。”曰：“有姑在，儿不忧无好媳妇。儿向日作么生？”田曰：“向在京作小经纪，颇获利息，不意失盗，一身之外无长物。窃意姑为骨肉至亲，必不以侄为外人，是以千里相就。”媪叹曰：“咱家世代贸易，从无坐食者。至儿不幸，罹此闵凶，至先人之业中断，殊惭继绍。迟日会当摒挡蓄资，儿仍作布客，争似游惰过日。儿细思维，谅不以老身之言为河汉。”田敬诺。

    Explanation

    517

    The old woman sighed and felt sorrowful for a long time. Then she asked, "How old are you, young man?" He replied, "Twenty." The old woman told the young woman, "He is your cousin." The young woman bowed, and the young man reciprocated the bow. The old woman said, "My daughter has no sons, only one daughter named Xiu Gu, who was spoiled and accustomed to being pampered, not concerned with anything, and still acting like a child at eighteen. After my husband's death, there are no more men in the family. Fortunately, you have come, and you are able to manage the household. Please take care to find a good match for your sister, and then my affairs will be complete." The young man said, "Your cousin is so beautiful and intelligent; there is no worry that she won't marry into a noble family." After speaking, he looked at the young woman with his eyes, and she blushed on both cheeks, silently lowering her head and playing with her hair and belt. The old woman asked, "Have you married yet?" He replied, "Not yet engaged." She said, "With me here, you won't worry about not having a good wife. What did you do before?" The young man said, "I used to work as a small trader in the capital, making some profit, but unfortunately, I lost everything to theft, and I have nothing but myself. I believe you are my closest relative, and you won't consider me an outsider, which is why I traveled a thousand miles to come here." The old woman sighed, "Our family has been engaged in trade for generations, and we have never had anyone who does nothing. Unfortunately, you have suffered this misfortune, and the family business has been interrupted, which makes me very ashamed to continue the family legacy. In the future, I will save money and prepare, and you will continue to be a traveling merchant, better than idling away your days. You think carefully, and you won't consider my words as nonsense." The young man respectfully agreed.

    518

    至三更，辞不能酌。姑始呼婢敛具，即于厅之东厅下榻。伺候者即前提灯婢，年十六七。极慧黠，问其名，曰：“秋罗。”乃以秋姐呼之。因诘之曰：“向于路上挑灯者，非子也耶？”曰：“是也。”曰：“何所之？夜深犹犯草露。”秋罗曰：“亲戚往来，郎君何心知之。”既而设衾绸，下帘剪烛，趋事颇殷勤。良久犹倚几不去，田曰：“秋姐劳碌，此间无事，可以入内矣。”秋曰：“七房尚有春罗姐，儿奉主母命，专侍东厢。”田曰：“虽然，夜深矣，吾亦欲寝，秋姐亦合少歇。”秋始含笑举步，将启帘，复停步回眸曰：“苟有所需，幸相闻也。”言讫，再瞬而去，意颇欣属，田心为之荡。

    Explanation

    518

    By the third watch, he could no longer drink. The middle-aged woman then called for a maid to gather the items and settled down in the east hall. The attendant was the same maid who had held the lantern earlier, a girl around sixteen or seventeen years old. She was extremely clever and quick-witted. When asked for her name, she replied, "Qiu Luo." He then called her "Qiu Sister." He questioned her, "Wasn't it you who was holding the lantern on the road earlier?" She said, "Yes." He asked, "Where are you going? It's so late, and you're still exposed to the dew." Qiu Luo replied, "Visiting relatives, how could you know about it?" Afterward, she arranged the bedding, lowered the curtains, and trimmed the candle, attending to matters with great diligence. For a long time, she still leaned on the table, not leaving. He said, "Qiu Sister, you've been working hard. There's nothing more to do here; you can go inside now." Qiu Luo said, "There is still Chun Luo Sister in the seventh room. I am following the orders of the mistress to serve specifically in the east hall." He said, "Even so, it's late now, and I also want to sleep. You should also rest a bit." Qiu Luo then smiled and took a step forward, about to open the curtain, but stopped and turned her head back, saying, "If there is anything needed, please let me know." After she finished speaking, she quickly left, seeming very pleased. His heart was stirred by her.

    519

    翌日，媪以管钥付田曰：“老身有未了事，久欲之彰德。恐去后，一门细弱，受侮强暴，故迟迟至今。今可以往矣。儿诸事可任，勿庸多嘱。但耐心半月余，老身却回也。”田曰：“姑年高，彰德路远，恐独往不易。”媪曰：“儿莫为老身虑，速多备糗腊，明日早发也。”田以目视女，女虽无言，而颜色甚适。因思姑去，可以浸润矣，遂亦不复谏阻。诘曰，媪展辖就道，惟一仆媪从。女送母去，呼春罗、秋罗亟阖扉，谓田曰：“娘远去，家中更无人，阃以内，儿主之；阃以外，兄司之。勿致不谨事，负老人嘱托。”田曰：“第恐韩寿在室，自防不密耳。”女佯若不闻，敛笑入内。

    Explanation

    519

    The next day, the old woman handed the key to the lock to her son, saying, "I have some unfinished business that I have long wanted to attend to in Zhangde. I fear that after I leave, the weak and vulnerable in our family may be mistreated by the powerful, so I have delayed until now. Now it is time to go. You can handle all matters; there is no need for further instructions. Just be patient for a little over a month, and I will return." The son replied, "Mother is advanced in age, and the journey to Zhangde is long. I fear it is difficult for her to travel alone." The old woman said, "Do not worry about me, my son. Quickly prepare plenty of dry food and preserved meat, and set out early tomorrow." The son looked at his daughter, who said nothing but had a very pleased expression. Thinking that with his grandmother gone, he could take advantage of the situation, he did not object further. The next day, the old woman set out with only one servant. The daughter saw her mother off, calling out to Chun Luo and Qiu Luo to quickly close the door, and told her brother, "Mother is far away, and there is no one else at home. Inside the inner chambers, I will take charge; outside the inner chambers, you will be responsible. Do not cause any careless mistakes, as that would disappoint the old woman's trust." The son said, "I only fear that Han Shou is in the house, and we must be cautious and not careless." The daughter feigned not hearing and smiled before entering the inner chambers.

    520

    田知其可动，及归房，神魂丧失。冥想间，适秋罗送茶至，田启小簏，出绉纱红帕送之，秋罗辞不纳，田捉其臂，强纳袖中，秋笑曰：“郎君莫作恶剧，强以贿赂啖人，豚蹄祝满篝，蚯蚓饵连鳌，何其所持者狭，而所欲者奢！”田笑曰：“物虽微，意则良厚，子非不知濡猛者，奈何故作颟顸，令人蹐跼？”言次，遽拥之。秋嘤咛作欲泣声曰：“从未见恁底一冉弱郎，腼腆不翅女子，何作事必尔，蠢蠢然，杂露若此！”田曰：“霸者以力服人，子可请盟矣。”捺之床而谑之，秋故含苞，大为凿枘。兴未阑，倏见一人启帘入，惊视之，春罗也。却立阈外，点头斜视，笑向秋罗，以指划颊，口唧唧作羞之之状。田错愕愧悔，无地自容。顷之，春罗始入室，笑曰：“秋妹，娘子唤汝矣。”秋徐徐整衣理鬓，与春俱去。田痴坐，不敢出声，但侧耳以察动静。

    Explanation

    520

    Tian knew it could move, and upon returning to his room, he lost his composure. In deep thought, just as Qiu Luo was bringing tea, Tian opened a small box and took out a red silk handkerchief to give it to her. Qiu Luo refused to accept it, but Tian grabbed her arm and forced it into her sleeve. Qiu Luo laughed and said, "Young master, don't play tricks. Forcing bribes to tempt people, small offerings filling the firewood, small baits catching large fish—how narrow what you hold, yet how extravagant what you desire!" Tian laughed and said, "The object is small, but the intention is sincere. You are not unaware of the gentle and fierce, why deliberately act foolishly, making one feel uncomfortable?" As he spoke, he suddenly embraced her. Qiu Luo made a sound as if about to cry and said, "I have never seen such a timid young master, as shy as a woman, why must you always act like this, so clumsy and revealing?" Tian said, "A conqueror subdues people by force, you can now swear an alliance." He pushed her onto the bed and teased her. Qiu Luo remained closed like a bud, and there was a lot of friction. The excitement was not yet over when suddenly, someone opened the curtain and entered. Tian was startled to see it was Chun Luo. She stood back outside the threshold, nodded and looked sideways, smiled at Qiu Luo, touched her cheek with a finger, and made a sound as if blushing. Tian was stunned, ashamed, and regretful, feeling nowhere to hide. After a while, Chun Luo entered the room and said, "Qiu sister, the mistress is calling you." Qiu Luo slowly adjusted her clothes and combed her hair, and she left with Chun. Tian sat in a daze, not daring to speak, but listened intently to the sounds around him.

    521

    一饷时，闻裙履声，不觉心头鹿撞。至则秋罗也，而故作嗔态，曰：“几害死人！儿死，汝岂安心独生耶？际此时吓得面白如纸，两眼似败，霸者之民，欢虞如也，恐未必如是。”田曰：“勿复相嘲，请问春罗泄之否？”秋袖出一纸裹掷几上，曰：“不泄漏，此物奚其至哉！速阅视，娘子俟回话矣。”田不测何物，心殊摇摇，颤手折之，则锦笺一幅，上书小楷数行，字体秀媚，如美女簪花，诵之，得诗一绝，曰：“春云一朵趁风来，有意无心罨碧苔。既有闲情能作雨，何如舒卷上阳台。

    Explanation

    521

    At one moment, I heard the sound of skirts and shoes, and my heart raced involuntarily. When I looked, it was Autumn Luo, who feigned anger and said, "Almost killed me! If the child had died, would you be at peace living alone? At that moment, you were so frightened that your face turned pale as paper, your eyes defeated, like the people of a powerful ruler, joyful yet uncertain." Tian replied, "Please don't tease me anymore. Did Spring Luo reveal anything?" Autumn Luo pulled out a wrapped piece of paper and threw it onto the table, saying, "It was not leaked. How did this thing come to be here! Read it quickly. The lady is waiting for your response." Tian was unsure what the object was, and his heart was greatly agitated. He trembled as he unfolded it, revealing a piece of fine silk paper with a few lines of small, neat handwriting. The characters were graceful and beautiful, like a flower in a hairpin. He read it aloud and found a short poem, which said, "A spring cloud comes with the wind, seemingly intentional yet unintentional, covering the green moss. Having leisure time can bring unexpected rain. How would it be to spread out on a high platform?"

    522

    ”田玩索再四，惊喜若狂，谓秋曰：“的是娘子示我者否？”秋曰：“言语愈出愈奇矣，非娘子畴能为此？”田曰：“然则子稍待，便携和章去。”乃吮毫濡濡，磨墨隆隆，搜索枯肠，勉成即就，以次其韵，曰：“春云一朵趁风来，故意氤氲罨碧苔。白日有情先作雨，夜间打点上阳台。”诗付秋罗，并以实告，浼其从中调剂，当有厚报，秋白：“自己一身赤贫，脱布衫黑如皂罗袍，尚不能一易，乃妄口许人。事至急处，不过仗胯间物，作丑态向人耳！”田方欲戏之，已笑而脱去矣。去则不复更来，茶饭皆停。田疑念复萌，起坐不定，渐至漏下，秋罗始出，仍送一诗笺，秉烛展阅，获次韵也，曰：“坐待秋风出岫来，东墙月已上莓苔。娘家兄妹休回避，例有媪峤玉镜台。”秋罗且告曰：“娘子致声郎君，可即入矣。”田喜惬过望，澡颈漱齿，整肃以随。

    Explanation

    522

    After pondering repeatedly, Tian Wan was amazed and excited beyond measure, and said to Qiu, "Is this indeed what the lady showed me?" Qiu replied, "The speech becomes increasingly strange and unusual, who but the lady could do this?" Tian said, "Then you should wait a moment, and I will take the harmonious composition with me." He then licked the brush, wet it, ground the ink, and made a loud sound, struggling to think of something and finally managing to complete it immediately, composing a poem in response: "A cloud drifts with the wind, intentionally creating a misty atmosphere over green moss. Even the sun has feelings and will bring rain first, and at night it will prepare to go to the platform." He handed the poem to Qiu and told her the truth, asking her to mediate, promising a generous reward. Qiu replied, "I am myself poor, my cotton robe is black like a dark silk robe, and I cannot even afford to change it, yet I rashly promised someone. In times of urgency, I can only rely on my body and make a disgraceful show to others!" Just as Tian was about to tease her, she laughed and left. She did not return, and he stopped eating and drinking. Tian's doubts resurfaced, and he sat restlessly, gradually until the night deepened. Qiu finally appeared, still holding a poem, and lit a candle to read it, receiving the next verse: "Waiting for the autumn wind to blow out of the mountains, the moon has already risen on the east wall covered with moss. Even the family members should not hide, as there is always a mirror in the family." Qiu also informed him, "The lady sends her message to the gentleman, you may enter immediately." Tian was overjoyed, washed his neck, brushed his teeth, and prepared to follow.

    523

    甫入院门，即见女倚栏而待，把握极欢。布筵对酌，各述倾慕。从此依倚闺中，不离跬步。女性好动，喜吟诗，多幽怨，田劝其节制，恐致不详，女虽是之，而吟咏不辍。一夕方对谈，忽春罗扬声户外也：“主母归来矣。”二人惊怔，未下床，媪已入室，见之，大怒曰：“男女受授不亲，促膝可乎？”田惶恐投地，愿甘责罚。媪瞋目视女，女泪萦两颊，愧而不惧。媪哂曰：“留亲下榻，竟成揖盗入门！为是自家侄子，且似谨愿，非嬛薄者，故坦然付托，出外不疑。不意亲骨肉，才半月之久，何意草创便尔，禽处兽爱？今之所谓少年老成者，尚可信哉？第事已舛谬，侄之肉亦不足食。今与侄约，领老身资本二千金，往山东贩货，须志如翁伯，勿为康乐。苟能获利三倍，即当以秀姑妻汝，否则无相见也！”田崩角稽首，额为之坟。

    Explanation

    523

    As soon as he entered the courtyard, he saw the woman leaning on the railing, waiting, and they were extremely happy. They set up a table for drinking together and each recounted their admiration for the other. From that point on, they relied on each other in the inner chamber, never leaving a step apart. The woman was active, enjoyed reciting poetry, and often filled with melancholy. Tian advised her to be moderate, fearing it might lead to misfortune, but the woman agreed and continued to recite poetry without stopping. One evening, as they were talking, Spring Luo suddenly shouted from outside the door, "The mistress has returned." The two were startled and had not yet gotten out of bed when the old woman had already entered the room. Seeing them, the old woman became very angry and said, "Men and women should not be too intimate; can they sit close together?" Tian was terrified and fell to the ground, willing to accept punishment. The old woman glared at the woman, whose tears welled up in her eyes, but she was ashamed but not afraid. The old woman sneered, "Inviting a relative to stay turned out to be like inviting a thief into the house! Because he is a family member, and seems to be diligent and sincere, not frivolous or shallow, so I entrusted him without hesitation, not doubting him. Unfortunately, even family members, only for a month, how could they have acted so hastily, like animals in their behavior? What we call mature young people today, can they still be trusted? But the matter has already gone wrong; even my nephew's flesh is not worth eating. Now I make a deal with my nephew: take my capital of two thousand gold pieces, go to Shandong to trade goods, must have the same determination as my father-in-law, do not be lazy. If you can make three times the profit, I will marry my daughter Xiu Gu to you; otherwise, we will never see each other again!" Tian fell to the ground and bowed deeply, his forehead becoming a mound.

    524

    迟数日，姑出金斗一只，玉瓶一枚，付田曰：“持此去，售之，善价可得二千金，明日当去。途中如遇相识，但云先世所遗，无吐实也。”田唯唯受教，归室束装，而拳拳怀思，如蘖之苦。夜漏二下，秋罗导女潜出，相持呜咽，各有涕　。秋在旁亦啜泣，助二人悲哀。女脱臂上紫金条脱为赠，更送别以诗曰：“愁对空庭月影斜，涔涔别泪恨无涯。他时相访应如梦，认取棠梨一树花。”田卷而怀之，报以白玉指环，并和其韵，以留别，曰：“话别匆匆月已斜，无端分手向天涯。痴情不比浮梁客，珍重东风撼落花。”女见诗，泪零如雨，未及再言，春罗仓卒来告曰：“主母已起盥栉，将送田郎上路。”女悲不自胜，拜而送之曰：“行矣，勉之，强饭自爱！苟富贵，勿相忘！”言次大恸，二婢扶掖而去。

    Explanation

    524

    After several days, the aunt took out one golden ladle and one jade bottle, and gave them to Tian, saying, "Take these and sell them; you can get a good price of two thousand gold coins. I will leave tomorrow. If you meet someone you know on the way, just say they were left by our ancestors and don't reveal the truth." Tian repeatedly acknowledged the instruction, returned to the room to pack, and deeply thought about it, with the bitterness of a bitter plant. At the second hour of the night, Qiu Luo led the girl out secretly. They held each other and sobbed, each had tears, and Qiu was also crying beside them, helping the two to be sad. The girl took off a purple gold bracelet from her arm as a gift, and sent her off with a poem saying: "Sadness in the empty courtyard with the moonlight slanting, tears flow continuously, hatred endless. When we meet again, it should be like a dream, recognize the pear tree with its flowers." Tian rolled it up and kept it in his heart, he replied with a white jade ring, and composed a poem in response to keep as a farewell: "Farewell hastily, the moon is already slanting, parting without reason towards the horizon. Foolish feelings are not like those of a traveler from Fuliang, cherish the east wind that shakes the fallen flowers." The girl saw the poem and her tears fell like rain. Before she could speak again, Chun Luo rushed in to say: "The master's wife has already risen, washed, and combed her hair, and will send Tian off on his journey." The girl was so sad she couldn't bear it, she bowed and sent him off saying: "Go now, encourage yourself, eat well and take care of yourself! If you become rich and powerful, do not forget us." As she spoke, she wept bitterly, and the two maids supported her away.

    525

    鸡再鸣，媪出祖于庭，戒田曰：“姑钟漏俱歇，惟此一女，汝既污之，理无他适，勉为之。俗云：”三卵两成，‘汝兄弟四人，惟汝在矣，讵可复毈乎？姑举眼无亲，今倾囊付汝，一以免盗贼窥视，一以俾汝克绍先业。他日归来，倘失于记忆，但于近村咨询卫辉杨氏宅，应无不知之者。“田谨志之，强进数觥，再拜泣别。媪掩面而哭呜呜，女隐身屏后，相对汛澜。田不敢请见，负囊出门，心忽忽不知所从，步步回顾。约半里许，残月如雾，高树如山，烟草迷离，门庭已不可复见矣。

    Explanation

    525

    The rooster crows again, and the elderly woman performs the ancestral rites in the courtyard, instructing the young man, "Grandmother, the clocks and water clocks have both stopped, and this is our only daughter. You have defiled her, and there is no other option. Do your best. The common saying goes, 'Three eggs hatch two chicks.' Among your four brothers, only you remain. How could you fail again? I have no relatives left. I am entrusting everything to you now, to prevent thieves from spying and to enable you to inherit the family business. One day when you return, if you forget, just inquire in the nearby village at the Yang family residence in Weihui; no one there will not know. The young man takes this seriously and drinks several cups, then bows twice and weeps as he bids farewell. The elderly woman covers her face and sobs, while the young woman hides behind the screen, both shedding tears. The young man dares not ask to see her, carries his bag, and goes out the door, his heart restless and unsure of what to do, looking back step by step. After about half a li, the crescent moon is like mist, the tall trees like mountains, the tobacco plants hazy and indistinct, and the gate and courtyard are no longer visible."

    526

    宿昔至齐鲁间，易金市瓶，置货行贾，自夏至秋，获利三倍，窃喜有以报命，好合可期。乃尽以其资，易黄金，轻装简载，乘健骡，星夜驰归。比至故处，但见春林草茂，风景依稀，第宅门庭，杳不可得。忆姑临别所嘱，急往村中问之，咸曰：“此间但有卫辉杨氏坟，葬已二十余年矣。不闻有卫辉杨氏宅也。”田大惊，重至其处，果有二冢，冢前各树短碣，半没土中，拭拂读之，一题“河南卫辉府杨门田氏之墓，”一题“卫辉府杨氏女秀姑之墓。

    Explanation

    526

    In the past, Tian traveled to the region of Qi and Lu, where he exchanged gold for bottles and placed them in a shop for sale. From summer to autumn, he gained triple profits, secretly pleased to have something to report back, and expected a good match. He then used all his capital to exchange for gold, traveled lightly with minimal baggage, rode a strong mule, and rode back at night as if by starlight. When he arrived at the old place, he only saw a spring forest with lush grass, and the scenery was vaguely familiar, but the main house and its courtyard were completely gone or untraceable. Remembering what his aunt had instructed him before parting, he hurriedly went to the village to ask. All said, "Here there is only the grave of the Yang family from Weihui, buried for over twenty years. No one has heard of the residence of the Yang family from Weihui." Tian was greatly surprised and returned to that place. Indeed, there were two tombs, each with a short stone tablet in front, half buried in the ground. He wiped it clean and read it. One read "The Tomb of the Yang Family of Weihui, Henan," and the other read "The Tomb of Miss Xiugui of the Yang Family of Weihui."

    527

    ”冢宅有棠梨树，花已半卸，树后数武，又有小冢四五，知为秋罗等瘗处也。田痴立良久，拊膺大恸，始悟所遇，即其姑及表妹之鬼也。不肯负姑之恩、妹之情，遂僦居村中，鸠工百人营建墓道，植松柏，筑垣墉；复想象旧宅，如式建宅一区，买僮蓄婢即居焉，为墓道之主，终身誓不娶妇，但纳妾生子，以继田氏。每逢节序，必厚奠恸哭而祭之。恩茂先有田数顷，隶顺德，时往征租，与田氏子相交，诚恂恂儒雅之美少年，而为隐君子者也。茂先下榻其家，因得吊女之墓焉。其唱和之作，皆录归以示所亲，予因得寓目。茂先有诗赠田，极温厚，得风人之旨。具稿中，兹不载。

    Explanation

    527

    The main residence had a loquat tree, and the flowers had already fallen half, indicating the late spring or early summer. Behind the tree, several paces away, there were several small burial mounds, which were recognized as the burial places of Qiu Luo and others. Tian Chizhi stood there for a long time, embracing his chest and weeping bitterly, and then realized what he had encountered — the spirits of his aunt and cousin. Determined not to betray the kindness of his aunt and the affection for his cousin, he rented a house in the village, hired a hundred workers to construct the tomb path, planted pines and cypresses, and built walls and fences. He then reconstructed the old house according to its original design, bought servants, and lived there, becoming the guardian of the tomb path. He vowed never to marry a wife but only to take concubines to have children to continue the lineage of the Tian family. Every time during festivals or seasonal changes, he would offer rich sacrifices and weep bitterly during the rituals. En Maoxian had several hectares of land belonging to Shunde and often went to collect rent, interacting with the sons of the Tian family. He was a truly modest and scholarly young man, a hidden gentleman. En Maoxian stayed at his home and therefore got to mourn the graves of the women. The collection of poems and responses that were all recorded and shown to relatives, I therefore got to see them. En Maoxian had a poem to Tian, which was very warm and thick, capturing the spirit of the Shijing (Book of Songs). The draft is available, but it is not included here.

    528

    兰岩曰：尝读《西厢记》而叹夫人之俗也，以家无白衣婿，促张生就道，且誓以必获荣贵，何其不近情理也！乃杨氏妇疏放其女，以致偷情；卒复不能暂留，责令货殖三倍，始许好合，其为利之心，与为名等。何天下妇人，同出一辙哉！是可笑而可慨也。

    　　季斋鱼曰：晋人以钱为命，田之姑已纵其女，而犹欲田作贾三倍，而后以女妻之。其贪利之心，更甚于爱女。无怪碌碌者，白首行贾，不以妻女为念也。

    528

    Lan Yan said: After reading The Western Chamber, I lament the vulgarity of Lady Lady. Because there is no suitable match in the family, she urged Zhang Sheng to depart and swore that he would certainly attain honor and wealth. How unreasonably impractical it is! Lady Yang, being lax, allowed her daughter to behave freely, leading to secret love affairs. Finally, she could not even stay for a short time, and ordered him to make three times the profit before they would be allowed to marry. Her desire for profit is equal to her desire for fame. How all women in the world are alike! This is both laughable and regrettable.

    Ji Zhai Yu said: The people of Jin regard money as their life. Tian's aunt had already allowed her daughter to act freely, yet still wanted Tian to make three times the profit, and then marry her daughter to him. Her greed for profit is even greater than her love for her daughter. It's no wonder that those who are mediocre live to old age as traders, without caring about their wives and daughters.

    529

    玉　公　子津门郁公子，显宦之裔，家累数十万金，食饩于庠，矫矫有声。年甫二十，丰姿韶秀姣媚，人以玉公子称之。妻章氏，亦世家女，美而贤，甚敦好逑。第宇延袤半里许，一巷之中，无他族偪处。宅之东，新获李总兵园，虽甚荒废，而极宏敞。公子每思修葺，以事未果。

    　　一日，阍人通一刺云：“蔚州韦秀才过访。”公子好客成癖，则倒屣迎之。客人，则十八九美少年也，眉目娟秀，飘然若仙，公子一见倾慕。韦登堂展拜曰：“久冀瞻韩，无缘御李，兹获披睹，实慰夙心。知公子得李氏废园，虚置弗居，意将岁奉百千，暂居家中，未识肯见诺否？”公子答拜曰：“君若惠然肯来，是玉树俯倚蒹葭也。石上因缘，何敢方命。”韦喜动颜色，再拜申谢，话谈良久，然后辞去，订以即日与族俱来。公子唯唯，送之门外，一揖而去。

    Explanation

    529

    Translation:

    In Jinmen, there was a young man named Yu Gongzi, the descendant of a prominent official, whose family wealth amounted to tens of thousands of gold. He received stipends at the school and was distinguished and renowned. At the age of twenty, he was graceful and charming, and people called him "Yu Gongzi." His wife, Lady Zhang, was also from a noble family, beautiful and virtuous, and they were deeply devoted to each other. His mansion extended for half a li, and in that lane, there were no other families nearby. To the east of his mansion, he had recently acquired the garden of General Li, which, though very dilapidated, was extremely spacious. Yu Gongzi often thought of renovating it, but he was unable to do so.

    One day, a servant announced, "A scholar from Yuzhou, Wei Xiucai, has come to visit." Yu Gongzi, who was fond of entertaining guests, rushed out to greet him. The guest was a handsome young man in his late teens or early twenties, with beautiful eyes and an ethereal appearance. Yu Gongzi was immediately captivated. Wei Xiucai ascended the hall and bowed, saying, "I have long admired you, but had no opportunity to meet you. Now that I have finally had the chance to see you, it truly fulfills my long-held desire. I know that you have acquired the abandoned garden of General Li, which you have left vacant and unoccupied. I intend to offer you a hundred thousand in annual rent and would like to temporarily reside there. Would you be willing to agree?" Yu Gongzi replied, "If you are gracious enough to come, it will be like a jade tree leaning on reeds. Such a chance encounter, how could I refuse?" Wei Xiucai was overjoyed, bowed again to express his gratitude, and they talked for a long time before parting. He agreed to come with his family the next day. Yu Gongzi nodded in agreement, saw him off at the door, and bowed farewell.

    530

    公子入告章，章曰：“岁百千税一废园与人，计亦非左，第恐其言之未必践耳。”公子曰：“岂有温文尔雅如韦生，肯食言者乎？吾同学之友多矣，未有能及之者。倘移居来此，不特得一芳邻，且得一腻友也。”晡时，韦复率二童来，先奉百千，公子立却，韦强委之而去。公子追问：“宝眷何时移来？”韦曰：“行当入新居耳。”公子授钱于章，伫立门外俟之。先见多人，扛抬箱笼几榻等物，陆续不绝，最后香车十余辆，辘辘而至。时日已曛暮，望之不甚了了，但闻诸女眷笑语声，轻脱如群燕，相将入园去。其气象之豪华，有非百万之富不能仿佛者。怀惑而入，与章共测之，章曰：“翌日汝不往拜乎？相见详询之，无不得者，底事妄猜疑？”公子以为然。

    Explanation

    530

    The young nobleman entered and reported to his father. His father said, "Every hundred thousand coins, one tax payment is made to abandon the garden to someone; the plan is not unreasonable, but I only fear that his words may not be kept." The young nobleman replied, "How can there be a gentle and refined person like Wei Sheng who would break a promise? Among my classmates, I have many friends, but none can match him. If he moves here, not only will we have a fragrant neighbor, but also a close friend." In the late afternoon, Wei Sheng came again with two boys, first presenting a hundred thousand coins. The young nobleman stepped back, and Wei Sheng forcefully left the coins behind and left. The young nobleman asked, "When will your beloved family move here?" Wei Sheng replied, "We will soon enter the new residence." The young nobleman gave the money to his father and stood outside the door waiting for him. First, he saw many people continuously carrying boxes, chests, tables, and chairs, and finally, more than ten fragrant carriages rumbling and arrived. It was already dusk, and it was not very clear, but he only heard the laughter and talking of the various female relatives, who were light and carefree like a flock of swallows, going into the garden together. The luxurious atmosphere could not be imagined without a fortune of a million. With doubts, he entered and examined it with his father. His father said, "Will you not go to pay respects the next day? When you meet, ask in detail; nothing will be unknown, why make wild guesses?" The young nobleman agreed with the advice.

    531

    早起肃衣冠，踵门请谒。刺甫投，韦即趋出，把握甚欢。公子环顾厅内，铺陈华丽，即栋宇榱桷，亦若新构，殊形疑讶。韦笑曰：“君谓旧宅不应遽生新色耶？知君必即辱临，恐茅茨不剪，有亵贵人，故于夜间督率僮仆，稍加粉饰耳。”公子疑遂释，而愈信其富，更请拜其尊人。韦曰：“二亲与诸昆弟，寄居关中，并一姑适商南殷氏，已二年矣。此间相依者，仅有新妇与弱妹三人也。”

    Explanation

    531

    Early in the morning, the gentleman straightened his attire and stepped up to the door to request an audience. As soon as the card was presented, Wei hastened out to greet, with a very joyful handshake. The gentleman looked around the hall and noticed the luxurious furnishings, and even the beams and rafters seemed newly constructed, which made him feel puzzled. Wei smiled and said, "Do you think that an old house should not suddenly have new colors? Knowing that you would surely visit, I was afraid that the thatched roof would be untidy and disrespectful to a noble person, so I supervised the servants at night to make some minor decorations." The gentleman's doubts were thus dispelled, and he became even more convinced of Wei's wealth. He then asked to pay respects to Wei's parents. Wei replied, "My parents and brothers are staying in Guanzhong, and one aunt has married into the Yin family in Shangnan. They have been there for two years. Those who depend on each other here are only three: my wife and two younger sisters."

    532

    公子志之，归与章议：“韦生有妻妹，当为具米面鱼肉薪水，聊尽东道谊。”章诺之，亲往馈遗。韦妻秦氏，年十八，娇艳无可比伦，与三妹美相埒。章故姝丽，郡中无出其右者，兹与诸美相对，殊惭形秽。秦少章二岁，与三妹皆以嫂子呼章，殷勤留饮，极相契厚。迟数日，章亦厚设，招秦与三妹饮，尽欢而罢，由是两家往来如至戚焉。

    　　章一子，方在襁褓。秦亦有娠，尝谓章曰：“生男则已，苟生女，当为嫂家妇。”章曰：“恐绨戏言耳。如果然，实副奢愿。”三妹复从旁怂恿之。阅数日，秦果生女，章闻之，举室欢腾，粥米馈赠，旁午于两宅之间。及弥月，韦折简召公子曰：“翌日作汤饼会，诸亲毕集，所需尊客，唯君一人耳。”公子许诺，预瞂珍品。至期盛服以往。亲暱久，不介而入，见婢媪棒拌操器者，来去纷纭。堂上寂无人语，惟闻吸面啜汁，咀嚼之声，杂沓甚伙。公子启帘，坐中一少年瞥见之，投箸而起，呼韦曰：“舅亟来，有客至矣。

    Explanation

    532

    The young lord resolved to remember and returned to discuss with Zhang, saying, "Wei has a sister-in-law; we should prepare rice, noodles, fish, meat, and firewood to show our hospitality as the host." Zhang agreed and personally went to send gifts. Wei's wife, Qin, was eighteen years old, her beauty unmatched, and she was equally beautiful as Wei's third sister. Zhang was naturally graceful and beautiful, unmatched in the prefecture; now, facing these beauties, she felt inferior. Qin was two years younger than Zhang, and both she and her third sister called Zhang "sister-in-law," warmly inviting her to drink, deeply cherishing each other. After a few days, Zhang also prepared a lavish feast, inviting Qin and her third sister to drink, enjoying themselves fully before parting, and thus the two families visited each other as if they were close relatives.

    Zhang had a son who was still in swaddling clothes. Qin was also pregnant and once told Zhang, "If it's a boy, that's fine; but if it's a girl, she should become your sister-in-law." Zhang said, "I fear this is just a playful remark. If it comes true, it would truly fulfill my wish." The third sister also encouraged her from the side. After several days, Qin indeed gave birth to a daughter. Zhang heard the news and the whole household was filled with joy, sending rice and other foodstuffs as gifts, going back and forth between the two households. By the time the child was a month old, Wei wrote a letter inviting the young lord, saying, "Tomorrow, we will hold a noodle feast, where all relatives will gather. The honored guests we need are only you." The young lord agreed, preparing precious items in advance. On the appointed day, he dressed in his finest attire and went. Being familiar for a long time, he entered without being introduced. He saw maids and servants bustling about, coming and going in a flurry. The hall was silent with no one speaking, only the sounds of slurping noodles, drinking broth, and chewing filled the air. The young lord opened the curtain, and a young man in the room caught a glimpse of him, put down his chopsticks and stood up, calling Wei, "Uncle, come quickly, a guest has arrived!"

    533

    ”诸女眷仓惶走避，咸退入屏门后。韦出见公子，拊掌曰：“方怪是何疏远恶客，孟浪入人内寝，乃东道主人耶？”复促诸女眷出，曰：“此西宅郁公子，通家谊也，奚避焉。”诸女眷悉含羞俯首，裣衽而拜。公子答拜。窃睨之，皆不世姝也，而秦氏尤光艳动人，神为之夺，勉强成礼。诸男客亦各通姓字，要皆少年而富豪者。内有韦之小姨夫白生者，与公子一见如故，恨相见之晚。相与入席饮啖，至晚而散。归而念秦不置，微露其情于章，章笑曰：“焉有长厚君子，而垂涎于亲家母者乎？”公子曰：“名分虽定，亦无大碍，况名分未定乎？卿其为我筹策，不敢忘报。”章笑而许之。

    Explanation

    533

    The female guests hurriedly fled and all retreated behind the folding doors. Wei stepped out to greet the young man, clapping his hands and saying, "I was just wondering what kind of distant and rude guest this could be, to rashly enter someone's private quarters. Is it the host of the eastern house?" He then urged the female guests to come out, saying, "This is the young master from the western house, a close friend, why should you avoid him?" The female guests all lowered their heads in shame and bowed formally. The young man returned the bow. He glanced at them and found them all extraordinary beauties, especially Qin, who was especially radiant and captivating, so much so that he was momentarily taken aback and went through the motions of the formal greeting. The male guests also introduced themselves, and they were all young and wealthy. Among them was Wei's brother-in-law, Bai Sheng, who felt an immediate connection with the young man, regretting not meeting him sooner. They sat down together to eat and drink, and the gathering lasted until late before dispersing. Upon returning, the young man could not stop thinking about Qin and subtly revealed his feelings to Zhang, who laughed and said, "How can there be a noble and upright gentleman who would be tempted by his mother-in-law?" The young man replied, "Even if the marital relationship is established, there is no great harm, especially since it is not yet established. You should help me plan a strategy, and I will not forget to repay you." Zhang laughed and agreed.

    534

    迟数日，章设具招秦及三妹饮，而密置媚药于酒中，独以醉秦，秦瞑眩不能支，倩婢扶入章室，着床辄熟寐。章笑曰：“秦妹今日颇不济，能饮几杯酒，便尔至此，必是诈耳。”三妹曰：“量素浅，醉应不妄，稍息当自起。”章乃命婢反闭衴子，戒勿复入惊扰。乃入座，劝三妹酒。室内故有圭窦，隐床后，内通曲室，章预伏公子其中。公子窥伺极审，见衴子已闭，乃款款启帘，鹤行鹭伏而出。秦已黑甜，摇之不觉，而冶容妖态，中酒益媚，先接其吻，柔香入脑，欲情火炽，因徐徐退其亵衣，见雪股粉臀，腻如暖玉，锦衾绣幕，掩映生辉，心旌摇摇，抚摩备至。

    Explanation

    534

    After several days, Zhang prepared a feast and invited Qin and her sister to drink, while secretly placing a love potion in the wine, specifically to intoxicate Qin. Qin became dazed and could not bear it, asking a maid to help carry her into Zhang's room, where she lay down on the bed and immediately fell into a deep sleep. Zhang laughed and said, "Qin's sister, you are not doing well today. After drinking just a few cups of wine, you have fallen into this state; it must be an act." Qin's sister replied, "My tolerance for alcohol is naturally low, and I should not be drunk. After resting, I will get up by myself." Zhang then ordered the maid to shut the door again and not to disturb them. He sat down and poured wine for Qin's sister. The room had a hidden passage behind the bed, leading to a secret chamber where the young man had been waiting. The young man carefully observed and, seeing that the door was shut, gently opened the curtain and moved out gracefully and silently. Qin was in a deep sleep, and even when shaken, she did not wake up. Her beautiful appearance and seductive posture were even more charming when she was drunk. The young man kissed her first, and the soft fragrance entered his mind, igniting intense desire. He slowly removed her undergarments, revealing her white thighs and rosy buttocks, smooth and warm like jade. The embroidered quilt and tapestry curtains shone through the shadows, and his heart fluttered with excitement. He caressed her thoroughly.

    535

    方欲狎之，忽幡然而悔，因念：“吾与韦生至交也，今见色心荡，欲淫朋友之妻，何殊禽兽！苟不忍此须臾，则一生阴德丧尽矣。”念及此，情欲冰释，急为掩覆下体，蹑足而返。既而三妹入室，促秦起，曰：“漏下矣，可归休！”秦徐起掠鬓理裳，面有赧色，但呼茶啜数口，则起身欲归。章留曰：“娣尚未进餐，何可枵腹去，岂其夜晚到家，更复起炊，不惹伊韦叔笑我太吝耶？”秦氏哂曰：“尔非好人，不足与较皂白，明日自有人来，讨回话耳。”言讫遂去，章面赧颈赤，不敢酬酢。送客回，觅见公子，潜何以败露之由，公子惊曰：“彼始终熟寐，何云败露？”因以实告。章不信，公子指灯自矢，章乃笑曰：“小狐媚亦太弄乖，妖言隐谜，几愧悔杀人！明日恐有他说，君须预为检审。”公子不言，而心殊忐忑。

    Explanation

    535

    Just as he was about to make advances, he suddenly had a change of heart and thought, "I am a close friend of Wei Sheng. Now, seeing her beauty, my heart is stirred, and I desire to commit adultery with my friend's wife. What difference does that make from beasts! If I cannot resist this for a moment, then my lifetime of hidden virtue will be completely lost." Upon this thought, his desire melted away like ice, and he hastily covered himself, tiptoeing back. Soon after, the younger sister entered the room and urged Qin to get up, saying, "It is late, you can go home and rest!" Qin slowly got up, smoothing his hair and fixing his clothes, with a blush on his face, and only took a few sips of tea before standing up to leave. Zhang said, "Your sister hasn't eaten yet, how can you leave with an empty stomach? Wouldn't that provoke Mr. Wei to laugh at me for being too stingy?" Qin laughed and said, "You are not a good person, it's not worth arguing with you, tomorrow someone will come to ask for an explanation." After saying this, she left, and Zhang had a blush on his face and neck, unable to respond. After seeing the guests off, he met the young nobleman and asked him how the affair had been exposed. The young nobleman was surprised and said, "She was asleep all along, how could it have been exposed?" He then told the truth. Zhang did not believe him, and the young nobleman swore by the lamp. Zhang then laughed and said, "That little fox spirit is too tricky, with her deceptive words and hidden riddles, it almost made me regret it! Tomorrow there might be more issues, you must prepare for an inspection." The young nobleman did not speak, but his heart was very uneasy.

    536

    次日，韦果至，必欲诘见，公子不得已，趑趄而出。韦一见笑曰：“兄连日不晤，在家作底事？闻兄居恒喜读《毛诗》，必有所得，盍诵一二，请以开茅塞。”公子窥其色不愠，中心少安，乃笑曰：“是语奚其至哉，予焉足以讲《毛诗》！”韦曰：“兄不读诗，何以能好色而不淫也！”公子闻此说，打入心坎，羞愧不复能对。

    Explanation

    536

    The next day, Wei arrived and insisted on questioning the young man, who, unable to avoid it, hesitantly stepped out. Wei, upon seeing him, smiled and said, "Brother, I haven't seen you for several days. What have you been doing at home? I've heard that you often enjoy reading the Mao Shi. Surely you've gained something from it. Why not recite a couple of verses? Please share them to enlighten my ignorance." The young man noticed that Wei's expression was not angry and felt a little relieved, then smiled and said, "How could it be so? How could I possibly be qualified to discuss the Mao Shi?" Wei retorted, "Brother, if you don't read poetry, how can you appreciate beauty without indulging in lust?" The young man was struck to the core, ashamed and embarrassed, unable to respond.

    537

    韦大笑曰：“无伤也，今而后，愈信兄之为人矣！昨夕所为，几希禽兽，而一念之转，大祸去身，兄真吉人哉！昨有妙手空空，伏兄卧室，窥伺久矣，苟非兄猛勇迁善，则虽绕以于阗，化为蟭螟，无所逃遁也。床下有物，可入验之。”公子惊怔，不甚解其所谓。韦去，急索床下，果见一物，光白如雪，大骇，取视之，则利匕首一具也。不觉毛发森竖，汗出如渖，章亦股栗。乃相与踵门负荆，匍匐请罪。秦扶章入室，毫不介意，嫣然向章曰：“嫂何必尔，儿与公子，本有一宿之缘，昨晚已勾却一半矣。嫂为公子运筹，不无罪过，今既能改，是无过矣，儿何芥蒂之有？但此事仍须秘密，倘泄漏于人，儿将羞死矣！不久尚有祈请，容缓言之。”章感其情，转增羞赧，从此仍相往来，和好无间。

    Explanation

    537

    Final Translation:

    Wei laughed and said, "There is no harm done. From now on, I believe even more in your character! What you did last night was almost like that of a beast, but with a single thought of repentance, a great calamity has been averted from me. You are truly a fortunate person! Last night, a pickpocket lurked in your bedroom, watching for a long time. If it were not for your bravery and repentance, even if surrounded by the finest guards from Yutian, he would have turned into a tiny insect and there would have been no escape. There is something under the bed; you can check it." The young man was stunned and did not fully understand what he meant. Wei left, and the young man hurriedly looked under the bed, and indeed found something shining white like snow. He was greatly startled, picked it up to look at it, and it turned out to be a sharp dagger. He could not help but shiver, his sweat pouring out like water, and Zhang also trembled with fear. They then went together to the door, carrying a bundle of thorns on their backs, and crawled to ask for forgiveness. Qin helped Zhang into the room, showing no resentment, and smiled at Zhang and said, "Why bother, sister-in-law? My son and your brother had a previous agreement, and last night half of it was fulfilled. If you were planning for your brother, there is no fault. Now that you have repented, there is no fault, so why harbor any resentment? But this matter still needs to be kept secret. If it is revealed to others, I will be so ashamed! There will be a request soon; I will explain it later." Zhang was moved by her feelings and became even more embarrassed. From then on, they continued to visit each other, remaining in harmony without any conflict.

    538

    居无何，白生忽衣冠而至，执礼恭谨。公子愕然曰：“忘形已久，胡复拘拘？”白曰：“平日无冠婚丧祭之事，不过诗酒招携，今有大庆，安敢失度？”公子问有何庆，白曰：“韦家姨夫，有弱妹三人，久贞不字。以兄锦心绣口，玉质金心，欲尽归于兄，以充妾媵。谅兄不见却也。”公子乍闻而惊，继而喜，惊喜稍定，乃疑而笑曰：“兄勿妄言，世间宁有此事耶？”白曰：“此事不奇，何故天下无之？且诸事或可妄言，此何事，可妄耶？”公子曰：“韦君至交，其妹犹吾妹也，何敢出此！”白曰：“惟其至交，胡萌此意，否则万金为聘，不能求其一诺也，况乎三哉？”公子入商于章，章惊喜尤甚，极力赞成，公子出拜白曰：“苟能如愿，当以身酬。”白笑诺而去。

    Explanation

    538

    Not long after, Bai Sheng suddenly arrived dressed in formal attire, behaving with utmost respect. The young nobleman was startled and said, "I have long forgotten formality, why are you being so formal again?" Bai replied, "There have been no formal occasions such as weddings, funerals, or sacrifices in recent days, just poetry and wine gatherings. But now there is a great celebration, how dare I be out of line?" The young nobleman asked what the celebration was about, and Bai said, "The uncle-in-law of the Wei family has three chaste younger sisters who have not been married. He wishes to give all three to you, to serve as concubines, knowing you would not refuse." The young nobleman was initially shocked, then delighted, but finally doubted and laughed, saying, "Brother, don't speak nonsense, how can such a thing exist in the world?" Bai said, "This is not unusual, why should it not exist? And while other matters might be spoken of lightly, how could this be?" The young nobleman said, "The Wei family is a close friend, their sisters are like my own sisters, how dare I say this!" Bai said, "Because he is a close friend, how could he have such an idea? Otherwise, he would not ask for a huge dowry, let alone three." The young nobleman consulted with Zhang, who was even more surprised and strongly supported the idea. The young nobleman then bowed to Bai and said, "If this wish is granted, I will repay you with my life." Bai smiled and agreed, then left.

    539

    越数日，韦先送妆奁至，大小百余抬，靡不穷极华美，约值万金。公子向韦道谢，韦致敬曰：“以兄勇于改过，洵中流砥柱也。三妹得所托付，无任庆幸。”公子硍谦特甚，及定情和好，如鼓瑟琴。三妾妖艳，各善所长，与章亦极莫逆。公子自讶何修得此，喜出望外。

    Explanation

    539

    After several days, Wei first delivered the dowry. There were over a hundred items of various sizes, none of which were not extremely luxurious and beautiful, with an approximate value of ten thousand gold pieces. The young man thanked Wei, who respectfully said, "Brother, your courage in reforming yourself is truly a pillar in the middle of the stream. The third sister has found a suitable match, and I am overjoyed." The young man was particularly humble. After establishing a harmonious relationship, they were like playing a lute and zither in harmony. The three concubines were beautiful and each had their own talents, and they were extremely close to Zhang. The young man was surprised at his good fortune and overjoyed beyond expectation.

    540

    一日，秦谓章曰：“女可离乳，自是郁家妇，当留汝家，与三妹共掬育之，行将远别。”章骤闻之，不胜骇愕。问将何往，秦曰：“归关中依舅姑耳。”章以告公子，公子废然，即往见韦，韦适过访，相遭于门，韦曰：“归心迫切，急于戒途。离别自今，趋承无日，不胜惆怅。”公子凄然曰：“相得正欢，遽忍言别。兄虽脱口，弟不忍闻。”韦曰：“三妹一女，幸托乔松。东游之愿不虚，西归之念遂挚。言瞻屺岵，眷念椿萱，归思顿兴，刻不容缓，十年后当复相聚，无戚戚也。”公子潸然，不克自乐。韦慰之而去。公子与章谋，欲盛宴而饯，三妾止之曰：“无庸，恐弗及。”公子不听，部署已定，亲往邀之，至则门馆空虚，一无所有，不知何时举族皆行矣。涕泗而返，章亦啜泣。三妾毫不介意。

    Explanation

    540

    One day, Qin told Zhang, "Your daughter is old enough to wean, and from now on, she will be a wife of the Yu family. You should stay at your home and raise her together with your third sister, as we are about to part ways for a long time." Upon hearing this suddenly, Zhang could not help but be shocked and astonished. She asked where Qin was going, and Qin replied, "Returning to Guanzhong to depend on my in-laws." Zhang informed the young master, who was greatly disheartened. He immediately went to see Wei, who just happened to be visiting, and they met at the gate. Wei said, "With a strong desire to return, eager to start the journey. Parting from now on, no more opportunities to serve, unable to bear the sadness." The young master said sorrowfully, "Just when we were getting along well, suddenly having to say goodbye. Though the elder brother may speak freely, the younger brother cannot bear to hear." Wei said, "The third sister's daughter is fortunate to be entrusted to the care of the elders. The wish to travel east has not been in vain, and the desire to return west has become firm. Speaking of looking up at the hills and mountains, thinking of one's parents, the desire to return arises suddenly, and there is no time to waste. We will meet again in ten years, so there is no need to be sad." The young master was filled with tears, unable to find joy. Wei consoled him and left. The young master and Zhang planned to have a grand farewell feast, but the three concubines stopped them, saying, "There is no need, we are afraid it won't be in time." The young master did not listen, and after the arrangements were made, he personally went to invite them. When he arrived, the mansion was empty, with nothing left, and he did not know when the entire family had already left. Returning with tears streaming down, Zhang also wept quietly. The three concubines were not at all bothered.

    541

    又三年，三妾忽仓惶谓公子曰：“知君家有《贝叶梵宇金刚经》，尚存否？”公子曰：“此镇家之宝也，尚什袭供奉于佛堂，焉得不存？”三妾喜跃曰：“然则儿辈得生矣。”公子惊询何故，三妾乃赧然实告曰：“儿辈非人，实狐也，以大劫在迩，故父母令兄嫂携来东游以避之。知君家供奉此经，遂托宇下。继见君改过如决芜，祥和满室，灾害不侵，故以儿辈见托。今大劫已届，午后雷雨大作时，祈君念一夕之情，匿儿辈与侄女于佛座下，君开经虔心跪诵佛经，则此劫可逃。然后共究性命之原，讲修持之道，仙籍可登也。”公子始大惊异，谨志之。

    Explanation

    541

    Three years later, the three concubines suddenly spoke to the young master in a state of panic: "Do you still have the Diamond Sutra written on Běiyè and housed in the Fànyǔ?" The young master replied, "It is a family treasure, still carefully preserved and revered in the Buddhist hall. How could it not still be there?" The concubines were overjoyed and said, "Then our children can be saved!" The young master was astonished and asked why. The concubines then shyly admitted the truth: "Our children are not human; they are foxes. Because a great calamity is imminent, our parents sent our older siblings eastward to escape it. Knowing that your family reveres this sutra, we sought refuge under your roof. Later, we saw that you have reformed like a field cleared of weeds, bringing peace and safety to the household. That is why we entrusted our children to you. Now that the great calamity has arrived, during the afternoon thunderstorm, we beg you to remember your kindness and hide our children and their niece under the Buddha statue. If you open the sutra and recite it with sincere devotion, this disaster can be avoided. Then we can together explore the origins of life, discuss the path of cultivation, and potentially achieve immortality." The young master was greatly surprised and shocked, and he carefully noted down the concubines' words.

    542

    午后，果见西北方奔云如墨，隐隐雷鸣，三妾慞惶伏佛座下，立化为狐。公子恻然，急纳小女于案下，以佛幡覆敝之。与章虔心开经，向佛跪诵不辍。顷之，雷电大作，天地震摇，公子与章，俯伏战兢，而诵经愈急。良久，忽闻人语曰：“何如？”又一人应曰：“止止，已奉佛旨免之矣。”俄而寂然，雷声渐远，三妾已抱侄女鹄立于前，喜溢眉宇，叩谢公子与章，各相庆幸。公子自此，世念顿灰，日与三妾讲求至道，章亦究心玄学，十年不懈，后竟徙家关中，不知所终。想与韦会矣。章有侍女青苹者，嫁为鹾商范氏侄妇。玉公子事，苹每向其亲戚，凿凿言之。

    Explanation

    542

    In the afternoon, dark clouds raced like ink from the northwest, accompanied by distant thunder. The three concubines, in panic, crouched before the Buddha's altar, instantly transforming into foxes. Moved with pity, the young master quickly hid his niece under the table and covered her with a Buddhist banner. Together with Zhang, he opened the scriptures with sincere devotion, reciting them continuously while kneeling before the Buddha. Soon, thunder and lightning raged, causing the earth and sky to tremble. The young master and Zhang crouched and trembled in fear, yet recited the scriptures more urgently. After a while, they suddenly heard someone say, "How is it?" Another voice replied, "Stop, stop. The Buddha has decreed forgiveness." Soon everything became silent, and the thunder gradually moved away. The three foxes stood upright and dignified in front, their joy overflowing from their faces. They bowed and thanked the young master and Zhang, each expressing their relief. From then on, the young master suddenly lost all worldly desires, pursuing the highest truth daily with the three foxes. Zhang also studied esoteric learning, diligently for ten years. Later, they moved to Guanzhong, but their ultimate fate remained unknown. Perhaps they reunited with Wei. There was a servant named Qingping who married into the family of a salt merchant named Fan. The story of the Jade Prince, Qingping spoke of with certainty to her relatives.

    543

    闲斋曰：淫心一炽，已伏祸机；正念一生，遂登仙箓。甚矣，人之贵能改过也！克己复礼，天下归仁，一念之善，可不扩充哉？

    　　萤火恩茂先秋夜见过，把酒持螯，相与谈鬼。茂先因言其伯祖达公，为永州太守时，一僮名淘气者，年十七，颇颖慧，貌亦韶秀。公命掌书记。夏夜苦热，僮独宿书斋，移榻当轩，白身高卧。见檐前流萤一点，光大如鸡卵，异之。转瞬间，增五六点，绕轩而飞。默念此地萤火，乃如此之大，足见土地异，气候别，而物亦殊也。寻亦睡去，朦胧之际，觉私处有物蠕动，惊起视之，一萤集焉。亟捉之，已飞去矣。笑曰：“么麽小虫，亦思此道耶？”因引被覆下体，仍觅黑甜。甫交睫，似有人嗤嗤然，启其被角。鼾寝中，懒于究竟。少间，渐伸一手入被中，竟扪其势，遂　然，如梦魇，不能转侧。继又似女人就而交接者，良久方去。乃蓦然而寤，精已遗矣。次日颇倦惫，然冥想其趣，正欲其再至，故不以告人。

    Explanation

    543

    The Idle Studio said: When lustful desire flares up, it already harbors the seeds of disaster; when righteous thoughts arise, one ascends to the divine register. How precious it is for people to be able to correct their mistakes! By restraining oneself and restoring propriety, the whole world returns to benevolence; a single thought of goodness, should it not be expanded?

    Feng Huo visited on an autumn night, and they drank wine and held crabs, discussing ghosts together. Feng Huo then recounted his grandfather, Tai Gong, who served as the governor of Yongzhou. There was a young servant named Taoqi, seventeen years old, quite intelligent and wise, with a beautiful and refined appearance. Tai Gong appointed him to manage the records. On a scorching summer night, the young servant slept alone in the study, moving his bed to face the hall and lying down with his body exposed. He saw a single firefly before the eaves, its light as large as a chicken egg, finding it strange. In an instant, there were five or six more, flying around the hall. He silently thought that the fireflies here were so large, clearly indicating different land, climate, and creatures. Soon, he fell asleep. In a drowsy state, he felt something moving in his private parts, and he woke up to see a firefly there. He quickly tried to catch it, but it had already flown away. He laughed and said, "Such a tiny insect, also thinks about this path?" He then pulled the quilt over his lower body and sought sweet darkness. Just as his eyelids met, he felt someone lifting the corner of the quilt. In his snoring sleep, he was lazy to investigate thoroughly. After a while, he gradually extended one hand into the quilt, finally touching his body, and he suddenly felt paralyzed, unable to turn or move. Then, it was as if a woman approached and engaged in intercourse, lasting for a long time before leaving. He suddenly woke up, and his essence had already been lost. The next day, he was quite tired and exhausted, but he contemplated the interest of the experience, desiring for it to happen again, so he did not tell others.

    544

    日暮，澡体修容，躺卧故处。移时，萤火渐繁，假寐以俟之。漏三下，果有一女子来，启被角，微窥之，绰约如仙子。僮喜极，急起抱持之，女愧缩殊甚，摆脱欲逃，僮低语曰：“既自来就人，何须尔尔？”女因默然不动，俯首羞颜，任僮所为。僮遂狎之，绸缪备至。自此无夜不来，五更便去。两月如一日也。僮叩其姓字，女曰：“妾以诚告君，君其勿怖。妾姚氏，父为明季太守，曾居此署，颇事科敛。妾时少，年十八，以婚姻非时，憔悴而死。生时酷爱梨花，故属纩时，嘱托老母，即瘗此园中梨树下，因见君年雏貌美，不避草露之嫌，辄尔搴裳相就，幸勿以葑菲见弃也。”

    544

    At dusk, he bathed and tidied himself, lying down in his usual spot. After a while, the fireflies became more numerous, and he took a brief nap in anticipation. When the water clock struck three times, indeed, a woman came. She unfolded the corner of the quilt and peered at him, her appearance graceful like a fairy. The boy was extremely happy and quickly got up to embrace her. The girl was very embarrassed and tried to escape, but the boy whispered softly, "Since you came to me, why do you need to act like this?" The girl then remained silent and motionless, lowering her head with a blushing face, allowing the boy to do as he pleased. The boy then played with her, with great affection. From then on, she came every night and left at the fifth watch. Two months passed like a single day. The boy asked her name, and she said, "I will tell you honestly, please do not be afraid. My surname is Yao. My father was a magistrate during the late Ming period and once lived in this office, involved in collecting taxes. I was young, only eighteen years old, and died due to the inopportune timing of my marriage. In life, I loved peaches very much, so when I was on my deathbed, I entrusted my mother to bury me under the peach tree in this garden. I saw that you were young and handsome, so I did not avoid the embarrassment of meeting you in the grass and dew, and here I am, hoping you will not reject me as you would wild vegetables."

    545

    僮款昵方深，忽闻其为鬼，惊悸欲狂，举枕击之，应手而灭，遂裸跣而走，直叩宅门。宅中已寝，闻叩声非时，疑为火、盗，亟振管辟扉。僮猝然挤入，举宅无不惊避。公自出，叱而止之，僮投地战兢，良久始定，备述所见，崩角求宽。公命服以丹砂，便为着裤。翌日，果于梨树下发得一朱棺，剖视女尸，容华不变，急命人舁之郊外焚而葬之。僮卧病月余，寻卒。其父母至今存焉。

    　　兰岩曰：一时科敛，异代犹败露于鬼物，深堪警惕。乃僮始艳美，而不加察之；无端又痛绝之；继闻其言，而不少怜惜，又枕击之：其病月余而就木也，宜哉！

    　　柴四固原柴四，贩羊磁州，生计潦倒。值秋风起，归思迫切，策蹇就道。偶失路，误入丛薄中，迍如邅如，饥且疲，乃舍骑而徒。是时驴龁枯苇，人啖乾瞐，且林树在望，可谋小歇。正行间，蓦然蹴起一兔，窜出草间，驴惊闪；适道旁一眢井，驴失足而坠，韁在柴手，猝不及脱，亦随坠焉。

    Explanation

    545
