    A servant, deeply attached and close, suddenly heard that the one he was with was a ghost, and was terrified and nearly mad. He picked up the pillow and hit it, and it vanished instantly. He then ran naked and barefoot, knocking directly on the door of the house. The people in the house were already asleep and heard the knocking sound at an unusual time, suspecting it to be fire or thieves, and quickly roused and opened the door. The servant suddenly rushed in, and the entire household was startled and avoided him. The master came out and shouted to stop him; the servant fell to the ground trembling, and after a long time, he calmed down. He detailed his observations and bowed his head, begging for leniency. The master ordered him to wear vermilion and put on pants. The next day, they indeed found a red coffin under the pear tree, and examined the female body, which remained unchanged in beauty. They quickly ordered someone to carry it to the outskirts and burn and bury it. The servant fell ill for over a month and soon died. His parents are still alive today.

    Lan Yan said: A momentary collection of taxes, even in a different era, can still be exposed by ghostly phenomena, deeply worthy of caution. The servant was initially beautiful but was not carefully observed; he ended up dying without any mercy. He was hit with a pillow after hearing that the one he was with was a ghost, and he fell ill for over a month and died, which was appropriate!

    Chai Si, originally from Guan Yuan, was a sheep trader in Cizhou, and his livelihood was in disarray. When the autumn wind arose, his longing to return home became urgent, and he set out on the road. He lost his way and mistakenly entered a dense thicket, feeling weary and hungry, so he abandoned his horse and walked. At that time, the donkey was nibbling on dry reeds, and he was eating dry food. With trees in sight, he could plan a short rest. While walking, he suddenly kicked up a rabbit, which darted out of the grass, causing the donkey to be startled and jump. Just then, there was an abandoned well beside the road, and the donkey lost its footing and fell in. The reins were in Chai's hand, and he couldn't untangle them in time, so he also fell in.

    546

    井中黑暗如夜，泥深没踝，暗中摸索，无计可出，自拼必死。悲悼逾时。已而有隙光透入，望如一线；即之，得一石门，力撼之，豁然开朗。门外细草葺葺，万花如绣，远山横黛，近水拖蓝，天朗气清，一目千里。柴惊喜出意外，即牵驴而入。度花丛才半里许，便得一径。夹径奇葩异卉，悉平生所未睹。桃花千叶，皆大如碗。时际残秋，而其地风景，则似暮春。怀惑殊甚，乃骑驴得得行去。卒至一村落，清流环绕，绿树荫浓，板屋竹墙，俨如画里。就中黄童白叟，各有怡颜之色。蓦见柴，无不惊怪，而尤怪其驴，虽聚观纷议，而莫敢近者。柴不测何意，但下气柔声，告以饥苦。一老人指示之曰：“向西石桥畔，有荀孺子宅，富而好礼，盍往见之？”

    Explanation

    546

    The well was as dark as night, the mud deep up to the ankles. Feeling around in the dark, he found no way to get out, ready to die. He grieved for a long time. Suddenly, a ray of light pierced through, like a thread; he followed it and found a stone gate. With great effort, he pushed it open, and suddenly the surroundings were bright and open. Outside the door, the grass was thick and lush, thousands of flowers like embroidery, distant mountains like horizontal dark green, near water like dragging blue, clear sky and fresh air, a vast view. Chai was surprised and delighted beyond expectation, immediately led the donkey in. Crossing through the flower garden for about half a li, he then found a path. Strange and rare flowers lined the path, all of which he had never seen before. Thousand-petaled peach blossoms, all as large as bowls. At the time of late autumn, but the scenery there was like late spring. Feeling very puzzled, he then rode the donkey slowly on its way. Finally arrived at a village, clear streams surrounding, green trees with dense shade, wooden houses with bamboo walls, as if in a painting. Among them, young children and old men, each with a happy face. Suddenly seeing Chai, all were surprised and puzzled, especially finding his donkey strange. Although they gathered to watch and discuss, no one dared to approach it. Chai was unsure of their intentions, but with a gentle voice, he told them of his hunger and hardship. An old man pointed and said, "At the stone bridge to the west, there is the house of Xu Ruzi, rich and well-mannered, why not go and see him?"

    547

    柴如其教，至则一高门，面桥，极焕赫。剥啄之，一苍头出应门，讯而入。又久之，荀孺子出，白皙美髭髯，年约四十许，岸帻方袍，制度甚古。荀见驴，讶曰：“此何兽也？”柴以驴对。荀细玩，审谛，笑其形怪，曰：“‘驴”字多见于诗书，今始识之矣。“延客入堂，系驴庭树，未暇叙谈，亟呼家人共来看驴。中杂一女郎，甚冶，频目柴，似甚欣属者，柴神为之夺。已而驴鸣，众为惊散。荀大笑曰：”度甚形状，马之流亚耳，必非噬人者，又何惧乎？适审其音声，则在宫羽之间矣。洵尤物也。“遂留柴馆谷，意颇殷勤，以二僮服役。

    Explanation

    547

    Chai followed his instructions and arrived at a tall gate facing a bridge, which was extremely splendid. He knocked on the door, and an old servant came to answer it, inquired, and then entered. After a while, Xun Ruizi came out. He was fair-skinned with a beautiful mustache and beard, around forty years old, wearing a square robe with a headband, and the attire was very ancient. Xun saw the donkey and was surprised, asking, "What kind of beast is this?" Chai answered with the word "donkey." Xun carefully examined it, observed it closely, and laughed at its strange appearance, saying, "The character 'donkey' is often seen in poetry and books, but I only recognize it now." He invited Chai into the hall, tied the donkey to a tree in the courtyard, had no time for conversation, and quickly called the family to come and see the donkey. Among them was a beautiful young lady who frequently looked at Chai, seeming very interested, and Chai was captivated by her. After a while, the donkey brayed, and everyone was startled and scattered. Xun laughed and said, "Judging from its appearance, it is a descendant of the horse, must not be a beast that bites people, so what is there to fear? Just now, I heard its sound, which falls between the musical tones of Gong and Yu. Indeed, it is a rare thing." Then he kept Chai as a guest and provided him with food and lodging, being very hospitable, and assigned two servants to serve him.

    548

    居数日，柴乘间以女郎为问，童子不答，笑而去。顷之，荀出，谓曰：“闻君询及小女，必非无心也。”柴惭汗而谢曰：“偶一失口，实无他意。幸宥之耳。”荀曰：“君亦尝闻韦娭光之事否？”柴曰：“少小贾贩，胸无墨渖，焉知故事。”荀曰：“彼娭光者，精神汹涌，渣滓销铄，餐六气而饮沆瀣，漱正阳而含朝霞，非不能乘风云而上下也。乃一见仲鉴，遂成伉俪。今日之事，夙契也。苟不弃村野，愿结丝萝。”柴闻之，不胜狂喜，虽辞而不力。荀即索聘，柴解囊出紫金条脱二枚，奉之。荀曰：“即此为已足矣。

    Explanation

    548

    After several days, Chai took the opportunity to ask about the young lady, but the young boy did not answer, smiled, and left. Soon after, Xun came out and said, "Hearing that you inquired about my daughter, it must not be without purpose." Chai, blushing and sweating, apologized and said, "It was just a slip of the tongue, I truly had no other intention. Please forgive me." Xun asked, "Have you also heard of the story of Wei Xi Guang?" Chai replied, "I have been a small-time merchant since childhood, with no knowledge of literature, how could I know such stories?" Xun explained, "That Wei Xi Guang, with surging spirit, refined and purified, feasting on the six qi and drinking the mist, rinsing with the pure yang and containing the morning glow, was not one who could not ride the winds and clouds up and down. But upon meeting Zhong Jian, they became a couple. Today's matter is a predestined match. If you do not disdain our humble village, I wish to tie the knot with you." Upon hearing this, Chai was extremely overjoyed, though he declined, he did not strongly resist. Xun immediately asked for the betrothal gifts, and Chai took out two purple gold bracelets from his bag and presented them. Xun said, "This is sufficient."

    549

    ”因问柴平日作么生，对曰：“贩羊。”荀愕然曰：“业几年矣？”对曰：“父作之，子述之，盖两世矣。固云不富，亦可小康。”荀惨然不怿，曰：“非仁人也，讵可妻吾女？”柴曰：“贩而不杀，疑若无罪。”荀曰：“汝虽不杀多羊，多羊因汝而死，乌得无罪！”柴请改业，荀曰：“两世贩羊，死羊若干矣，罪不可逭也，改业亦晚矣。”反其聘，留其驴，赠金一锭，而遣之。柴大悔恨，而不敢争辩，怏怏负囊而出，僦荀之左邻以居。欲谋归去，问途于人，而无知者，心殊郁结，幸主人不索房值，且日供两餐，无所缺乏。柴喜其地之风土秀美，人情敦朴，故亦安之。

    Explanation

    549

    Xun asked Chai about his usual occupation, and Chai replied, "I trade in sheep." Xun was startled and said, "How long have you been in this business?" Chai replied, "My father did it, and I continue it. It has been two generations. Although it is not rich, it is still a modest living." Xun looked displeased and said, "You are not a benevolent person; how could I marry my daughter to you?" Chai said, "I trade without killing; it seems I am not guilty." Xun said, "Although you do not kill the sheep, many sheep die because of you; how can you not be guilty?" Chai requested to change his profession, but Xun said, "Two generations of trading sheep have resulted in the death of many sheep; the sin cannot be avoided, and changing your profession is too late." Xun returned the dowry, kept the donkey, gave Chai a piece of gold, and sent him away. Chai was greatly regretful and dared not argue, leaving with a heavy heart and carrying his bag. He rented a house next to Xun's. He planned to return home and asked people for directions, but no one knew. His heart was deeply troubled, but fortunately, the owner did not ask for rent, and he was provided with two meals a day, having nothing lacking. Chai was pleased with the beautiful and fertile land and the sincere and simple people, so he also felt at ease.

    550

    一日，闻邻人共相传说：“荀孺子嫁女于鲍处士家，今日迎亲矣，盍往观乎？”于是合村之男妇老幼，观者如堵墙。柴挤稠人中，见彩旌前导，华毂后随，鲜衣花帽，簇拥鱼轩左右者甚盛。又盛饰其驴，有簪花美少年乘之，咸曰：“乘异兽者，鲍家郎，荀家婿也。”柴见之，妒心火炽，突前遮道，问何故夺我驴。众乍见而惊，既而怒，群集以马箠挝之。柴冒首捉衔，不肯稍却。荀闻变奔至，见柴怒曰：“牧羊儿，乃敢扰我大礼耶？”遽命缚之，柴滚地大呼曰：“今日断脰陷胸，岂惧一缚乎？”众不能决，乃送之官，官颇袒荀，坐以刁诈梗化，鞭三百，流五百里，发遣戍尘界关。

    Explanation

    550

    One day, he heard neighbors passing on rumors that Xun Ruzi was marrying off his daughter to the family of Bao Chushi, and today they were welcoming the bride. Why not go and see? Thus, all the men, women, and children of the village gathered, and the spectators were as dense as a wall. Chai pushed through the dense crowd and saw colorful flags leading the way, and splendid carriages following. Brightly colored clothes and flowered hats crowded around the fish-shaped carriage on both sides. He also adorned his donkey, and a handsome young man wearing flowers rode it. They all said, "The one riding the strange beast is Bao's son-in-law and Xun's son-in-law." Chai saw this and his jealousy burned like fire. He rushed forward to block the path and asked why they were taking his donkey. The crowd was initially surprised, then became angry, and gathered to beat him with horsewhips. Chai held onto the reins despite being beaten and would not back down slightly. Xun heard the commotion and rushed over, saw Chai, and angrily said, "You shepherd boy, how dare you disrupt my grand ceremony?" He immediately ordered him to be bound. Chai rolled on the ground and shouted, "Today I will cut off my neck and pierce my chest, am I afraid of just being bound?" The crowd could not decide, so they sent him to the authorities. The official favored Xun and charged him with fraud and obstruction of social order. He was whipped three hundred times, exiled five hundred miles away, and sent to guard the Chenshijie Pass.

    551

    吏关命司启闭。柴在关匝月，无一人出入关门者，殊觉寂寞。值关吏以事他往，嘱柴谨守锁钥，勿轻窥伺关外。吏即去，柴得间，启关速逃。甫出关，风景顿殊，且寒甚。奔走至暮，得到一村市，闻诸行者曰：“湖南某县某村也。”问何时，曰：“某年十一月某日。”柴大惊，盖去所坠之井已千余里；计坠井之期，已十余年矣。星夜归家，家已易主。访求亲友，迁流殆尽。唯一季弟尚存，贫为酒家佣，须髯似戟矣。展先人之墓，庐舍无存，松柏为樵矣。拊膺长恸，尽以贩羊余资与弟，遂弃家为黄冠，云游不知所终。

    Explanation

    551

    The official at the gate was responsible for managing its opening and closing. For a full month, the protagonist stayed at the gate, with no one entering or leaving, feeling profoundly lonely. When the gatekeeper left for some other business, he entrusted the protagonist to guard the key and not to peek out of the gate. As soon as the gatekeeper left, the protagonist found an opportunity to open the gate and escape quickly. Just as he stepped out of the gate, the scenery suddenly changed, and it was much colder. He ran around until dusk and arrived at a village market. He heard from passing travelers, "This is a certain village in a certain county of Hunan." When he asked what time it was, they replied, "It is the tenth day of the eleventh month of a certain year." The protagonist was greatly shocked, realizing that he had traveled over a thousand miles from the well where he fell. Calculating the time of his fall, it had been more than ten years. Under the stars, he returned home, only to find that his family property had been sold or transferred. He sought out relatives and friends, but almost all had moved away. Only his younger brother remained, poor and working as a servant in a wine shop, with a beard like a halberd. Visiting the ancestral tomb, he found the house no longer existed, and the pine and cypress trees had been cut down for firewood. Clasping his chest, he wept bitterly, giving all his remaining funds from sheep trading to his younger brother. Then, he abandoned his family, became a Taoist priest, and wandered without knowing where he ended up.

    552

    闲斋曰：落眢井，入洞天，柴之分合为仙矣。乃以贩羊之故，即时脱仙籍而还尘障，贩羊者可以鉴诸。夫子谓“始作俑者无后，为其象人而用之？，非仁人心也。况贩羊两世，不仁孰甚？古人慎于择业，世之谋生业者，门路甚多，奈之何必欲为渔、为猎、为屠刽也哉？观于此，宁为驴，不为柴矣。

    　　兰岩曰：择术不仁，仙缘无分。一跌十余年，始得再蹈人世，可不慎欤？可不戒欤？

    　　吴哲宜兴吴哲，少年尚气，胆勇过人，以罪长流五　，为乡绅张氏记室。张三世皆为总戎，世胄巨族。城南即别墅，吴夏月常往避暑，地极幽邃，亭轩台榭，曲折连绵。池塘广数亩，塘西跨一板桥，对桥一轩，绕以曲廊。轩后高楼五楹，树木映蔽。楼为张次女所居，女年甫及笄，有容色，许字邑绅周方伯少子。未嫁而夫死，重字　镇马总戎之孙。马世系回纥，秉夷教，甚乖女愿，郁郁成疾，渐发狂语，哭笑不恒，巫医不能救。张无如之何，唯严其防守而已。

    Explanation

    552

    Master Xianzhai said: Falling into a dry well and entering a heavenly realm, the division and combination of the 'Chai' make one a god. Yet, for the sake of selling sheep, one immediately loses divine status and returns to the mundane world. Those who sell sheep can take this as a warning. The Master said, "The first to start a new practice will have no descendants, because it resembles a human and is used, it is not the heart of a benevolent person. Especially since selling sheep spans two generations, what is more unkind? The ancients were cautious in choosing their careers. There are many ways to make a living, why must one choose to be a fisherman, hunter, or butcher? Considering this, rather be a donkey than 'Chai.'"

    Master Lanyan said: If the method chosen is unkind, there is no chance of becoming a god. A fall after many years, one can only return to the human world after a long time, can one not be careful? Can one not be cautious?

    Wu Zhe of Yixing, Wu Zhe, was a young man with high spirits and courage exceeding others, exiled for five years due to a crime, as the secretary of the gentry Zhang. The Zhang family has been generals for three generations, a powerful and influential family. The southern part of the city is a villa, in summer, he often goes there to escape the heat, the place is extremely secluded and deep, with pavilions, halls, terraces, and pavilions, winding and continuous. There is a pond covering several acres, a wooden bridge spans the west side of the pond, a pavilion faces the bridge, surrounded by a winding corridor. Behind the pavilion is a five-bay high building, trees obscure the view. The building is where Zhang's second daughter lives, the girl is just coming of age, she is beautiful, engaged to the son of the county magistrate Zhou. She died before she was married, re-engaged to the grandson of the general Ma. The Ma family is of Huihe descent, adheres to the Huihe religion, very much against her wishes, she became depressed and fell ill, gradually started speaking nonsense, crying and laughing uncontrollably, neither witch doctors nor physicians could save her. Zhang had no way to deal with it, only strict guarding was possible.

    553

    一日薄暮，吴独坐藤花下。东偏有屋数椽，隔以粉垣，久荒废，忽闻其中有人絮语，谛听之，隐不可辨，大疑。乃蹑足属耳于垣，既而逾垣，属目于窗。见二少年对坐地下，貌极都美，方巾阔服，不类时装。一衣紫，一衣绿。吴知其非人，亦不惊骇，第屏息以察其所事。紫衣者手弄一玉指环，且玩且叹曰：“非物之为美，美人之贻。忆昔游酒泉，入云中，客晋阳，又居抱罕三年，由临洮、皋兰一路，仍归于此。奇遇之多，指不胜偻。若今日所昵，未数数觏也。方我在临洮道上，与令叔謢霞公邂逅时，对坐河干，款言移晷，便云阿咸在　，不无所遇。所惜道术浅薄，恐至磋跎，深萦怀抱。彼时我慰之曰：”季锢闭已久，内照晶莹。

    553

    One evening, Wu sat alone under a wisteria tree. To the east, there was a small house with a whitewashed wall separating it from him. The house had been long abandoned, and suddenly, he heard murmured conversation coming from inside. He listened closely but could not make out the words, which greatly puzzled him. He tiptoed and pressed his ear against the wall, then climbed over it and focused his eyes on the window. He saw two young men sitting on the ground, both extremely handsome, wearing square caps and loose clothing that was not like modern fashion. One wore purple, and the other wore green. Wu knew they were not ordinary people, but he did not feel alarmed. Instead, he held his breath to observe what they were doing. The one in purple played with a jade finger ring, and as he did, he sighed, saying, "Not the object itself is beautiful, but it is a gift from a beautiful person. I recall my past travels to Jiuquan, entering Yunzhong, visiting Jinyang, and staying in Baohan for three years, traveling along the route through Lin洮 and Gao Lan, and returning here. The number of strange encounters was so great that one could not count them all. If today's encounter were to be cherished, it would not be a frequent occurrence. When I was on the road to Lin洮 and met my uncle Huo Xia Gong, we sat by the river, speaking cordially for a long time. He said that Ah Xian was present, and there were no empty encounters. What is regrettable is the shallow knowledge, fearing it will lead to wasted time, deeply troubling my heart. At that time, I comforted him, saying, 'The younger brother has been confined for a long time, and his inner light is clear and bright.'

    554

    尝以火酒试之，运用合度，其声啑啑然，能尽五升，习则术精，恃此可无恐。‘此三年前事，今与子相聚，殊乖所望。岂其讹以传讹，本属子虚乌有之事乎？“绿衣者笑曰：”日与子偕，莫知我褄，子真为其所昏瞀矣。子昔日遇柳姑时，其自守綦严，无隙可乘，啖之以重金，眩之以美色，胥不可动。故示之以术，始获相从，然犹百计千万，一年斯得其元精，以其自操者坚，而所禀者厚也。今张家女见我盫裆，即不自禁，虽有奇术，何所用之？夫干将补履，不及两钱之锥，诚以戋戋者，不足以俣俣者当之矣。岂若子前夕之窘，三战三北，大贻所笑，出胯下以甘辱，一指环庸足贵乎？“紫衣少年大惭，强笑曰：”方欲从学，底事见侮？“绿衣少年曰：”愿学亦易事，但宜秘密，勿使墙外措大闻之也。“

    Explanation

    554

    Tried with fire spirits, the effects were used appropriately, the sound was soft and gentle, and it could consume five liters. With practice, the skill becomes refined, and relying on this, one can be fearless. "This was a matter three years ago; now that I am with you, it greatly contravenes my expectations. Could it be that the falsehoods have been passed down, making it just a matter of empty talk or a fabrication?" The green-clad youth smiled and said, "Day by day with you, no one knows my secret. You are truly confused by it. When you met the Willow Maiden before, she guarded herself so strictly that there was no opportunity to take advantage of her. Bribed with heavy gold and dazzled with beauty, she could not be moved. Therefore, I showed her the skill, and only then did she agree to follow me. Yet, it still took countless strategies and efforts over a year to gain her essence, because what she controlled was firm, and what she inherited was substantial. Now, the Zhang family's daughter, upon seeing my open drawers, immediately cannot control herself. Even with a strange skill, what use is it? It's like using Gan Jiang's sword to mend shoes—it cannot match the value of a two-qian cone. Indeed, because of its smallness, it is not enough to match the strength of something else. How could it compare to your recent plight, where you suffered three defeats in three battles, greatly laughed at, bowing down to the waist to accept humiliation, and a single finger ring is truly not valuable?" The purple-clad youth was greatly ashamed and forced a smile, saying, "I was just about to learn from you, what is this insult?" The green-clad youth said, "Wishing to learn is also an easy matter, but it should be kept secret, do not let the scholars outside the wall know about it."

    555

    吴乃悟二人即祟张女也。大怒，亟返其室，取腰刀，并弹弩。潜从窗隙弹之，中绿衣者之目，岳岳绕地而叫。紫衣者惊惶欲遁，弹又发，中鼻。随弃弩抽刀入室，已失二狐所在，惟衣服履袜，委地上如蜕，及玉指环一枚。持示主人，洵女物也。主人深惭恨。俟之数日，不复再至，患遂绝。女病亦渐瘥，后归马氏。马氏子以荫官参戎。女尚在，年四十余，予居　时每见之，吴有《逸狐歌》，周南溪先生尝和之。

    Explanation

    555

    Wu finally realized that the two people were the ghostly entity known as the "Sui Zhang Nü." Enraged, he quickly returned to his room, took his waist knife and crossbow, and stealthily shot at them from the window crevice. He hit the eye of the person in green, who cried out furiously and ran around the ground. The person in purple, frightened, tried to escape, but Wu shot again, hitting their nose. Wu then discarded the crossbow, drew his knife, and entered the room. By then, the two fox spirits had disappeared, leaving only their clothes and shoes on the ground, as if they had shed their skins, along with a single jade finger ring. He held these items to show the host, who confirmed they belonged to the girl. The host felt deeply ashamed and resentful. After waiting several days, the entity did not return, and the trouble ended. The girl's illness also gradually improved, and she later returned to the Ma family. The son of the Ma family became a military officer through family influence. The girl was still alive, over forty years old, and I often saw her at that time. Wu had a song called "The Wandering Fox," and Mr. Zhou Nanxi once composed a response to it.

    556

    兰岩曰：张氏女以乖所愿，遂致邪物凭虚而入，颠倒数年，卒归马氏，不已徒受窘辱哉？世之乖所愿者不少，幸无多狐凭之为祟耳！

    　　周琰岑溪诸生周琰，字昆玉，富而乡居，能饮酒。琰特暴戾多力，往往因小忿，辄挥老拳，家人既不相安，邻里亦不敢犯。同社有廖生者，喜其才而恶其横，目为周处。琰闻之，怒曰：“奈何隐刺朋友？”廖曰：“周处初年，因似周琰，然卒为善士，是琰未必如处也。”琰欲行殴，廖走免，琰逐之，得众劝乃解。

    Explanation

    556

    Lan Yan said: "The woman from the Zhang family, due to her unfulfilled desires, was possessed by evil spirits, leading to years of confusion, and finally ended up with the Ma family, suffering only humiliation and hardship. There are not a few people in the world whose wishes are unfulfilled; fortunately, there are not many who are troubled by fox spirits."

    Zhou Yan, a wealthy scholar from Cenxi, lived in the countryside and enjoyed drinking. Zhou Yan was particularly violent and strong, often getting into physical altercations over small disputes, making both his family and neighbors uncomfortable. A fellow scholar named Liao admired Zhou Yan's talent but disliked his violent behavior, comparing him to Zhou Chou.

    When Zhou Yan heard this, he became angry and asked, "Why would you secretly criticize a friend?" Liao replied, "Zhou Chou was initially similar to you, but he eventually became a good man. Therefore, you might not be as virtuous as he is." Zhou Yan tried to attack Liao, but Liao escaped. Zhou Yan chased him, but the crowd intervened and calmed him down.

    557

    一日，有道士出门，施以钱米，悉不受。琰自出，问：“道士欲何为？”道士曰：“贫道善搏虎，欲为公效力。”琰嗤曰：“即有虎，我且自搏之，何需汝？况此间近方廓，焉得有虎！”道士指琰曰：“即子是虎。”琰怒曰：“何物道士，敢指人为虎！”攘臂而前，揕其胸，道士以袖拂之，颠仆丈余，伏地不能便起。中心怛怯，壮气顿消。道士笑曰：“如此软弱，乃亦与人较力耶？贫道之来，宁有恶举？以公将沦于异类，故相援手，夫何冥顽不灵，以至于此！”琰曰：“何谓也？”道士曰：“公前生本虎也，幸而为人，亦一念之善所致。不谓公肆行无忌，迷昧殊深，不过今秋，将复化为虎矣。”琰惊曰：“然则奈何？”道士曰：“无他术，静气平心，勉为善事，可以挽之。更赠公良药一刀圭，服之必效，勿蔑视也。”留药而去。

    557
    Original Chinese Text Translation
    一日，有道士出门，施以钱米，悉不受。 One day, a Taoist priest came out, offering money and rice, which were all refused.
    琰自出，问：“道士欲何为？” Yan stepped forward and asked, "What do you want, Taoist priest?"
    道士曰：“贫道善搏虎，欲为公效力。” The Taoist priest said, "I am skilled at fighting tigers and wish to serve you."
    琰嗤曰：“即有虎，我且自搏之，何需汝？况此间近方廓，焉得有虎！” Yan sneered, "If there is a tiger, I will fight it myself. Why do you need you? Besides, this area is close to a vast open space, how could there be tigers!"
    道士指琰曰：“即子是虎。” The Taoist priest pointed at Yan and said, "You yourself are a tiger."
    琰怒曰：“何物道士，敢指人为虎！” Yan was furious and said, "What kind of Taoist priest dares to call someone a tiger!"
    攘臂而前，揕其胸，道士以袖拂之，颠仆丈余，伏地不能便起。 He advanced with raised arms, stabbing his chest. The Taoist priest brushed him away with his sleeve, causing Yan to fall over several feet and lie on the ground unable to rise immediately.
    中心怛怯，壮气顿消。 Yan felt deep fear in his heart and lost his courage suddenly.
    道士笑曰：“如此软弱，乃亦与人较力耶？贫道之来，宁有恶举？以公将沦于异类，故相援手，夫何冥顽不灵，以至于此！” The Taoist priest laughed and said, "So weak, are you also to compete with others? I came here with no evil intentions. It is because you are about to fall into a different category that I am helping you. Why are you so stubborn and unresponsive, leading to this situation!"
    琰惊曰：“然则奈何？” Yan was shocked and asked, "What then?"
    道士曰：“公前生本虎也，幸而为人，亦一念之善所致。不谓公肆行无忌，迷昧殊深，不过今秋，将复化为虎矣。” The Taoist priest said, "You were originally a tiger in your past life, fortunately becoming human due to a single good thought. I did not expect you to act without restraint, deeply confused, and by the end of this autumn, you will revert to being a tiger again."
    琰惊曰：“然则奈何？” Yan was shocked and asked, "What then?"
    道士曰：“无他术，静气平心，勉为善事，可以挽之。更赠公良药一刀圭，服之必效，勿蔑视也。”留药而去。 The Taoist priest said, "There is no other method. Calm your mind and spirit, strive to do good deeds, and you can change your fate. I will also give you a good medicine in a small measure. Taking it will definitely work; do not look down on it." He left the medicine and departed.
    558

    琰杜门数日，玩忽旋生。同社友闻之，踵接来贺，琰曰：“公等为道士所惑耶？吾思天命为性，率性为道。吾性暴故行亦暴，是吾能率性而修道也。天之所赋，岂能戕贼哉！”于是暴戾如故。倏忽西风卷叶，序属三秋。琰纵饮酒家，方醉归，鼾卧榻上，梦中觉遍身卷曲，筋骨悉毕暴作声，惊寤而起，见两手背隐隐起虎皮纹，大骇，急解衣视之，举体皆然，失声大叫。家人环视，无不错愕。琰忽忆道士所留药，急取服之，一食顷，皮肤即复其旧，始知道士为异人也。由是改过自新，平心静气，勉为善事，铭八字于座右曰：“放情诗酒，绝想功名。”自号为虎变居士云。贵筑刘昱东说。

    Explanation

    558

    For several days, Yan secluded himself, but his indulgence and carelessness quickly returned. His fellow members of the society heard of it and came in succession to congratulate him. Yan said, "Are you all deceived by the Taoist? I thought the mandate of Heaven is nature, and following nature is the Way. My nature is violent, so my actions are also violent. This is how I can follow nature and cultivate the Way. What Heaven has given, how can it be destroyed?" Thus, he remained as violent as before. Suddenly, the west wind rolled up the leaves, and it was late autumn. Yan drank excessively in a wine house, and upon returning drunk, he lay snoring on his bed. In his dream, he felt his whole body twisting, and his bones and muscles all made loud sounds. He woke up in a panic and saw that his hands had tiger skin patterns. He was greatly terrified and quickly undressed to look at it. The whole body was like that. He screamed out loud. His family members looked around, all startled and confused. Yan suddenly remembered the medicine left by the Taoist. He quickly took it and drank it. After a meal's time, his skin immediately returned to its original state. He then realized that the Taoist was an extraordinary person. From then on, he reformed and became a new person, calm and composed, striving to do good deeds. He inscribed eight characters on the right side of his seat: "Indulge in poetry and wine, abandon thoughts of fame and glory." He called himself the Tiger Transformation Hermit. This was said by Liu Yudong of Gui Zhu.

    559

    兰岩曰：一念之善，虎可为人；玩忽旋生，人而为虎。此圣狂之间，在于几希也。虽然，虚亦非寻常兽也。琰慷慨豪爽，故得变为虎，若世之险邪庸碌辈，恐欲变犬而亦不可得为守夜者矣，敢望成虎耶？

    　　傻白太监白某，面白，人称傻白。年四十余矣。间尝为余言，其十六时时，值上元节，金吾不禁，灯月交辉，从其叔之西城外祖母家，与诸姊妹兄弟呼卢半夜。四更后，始告归。至半途，忽忆表妹所赠升官图一纸，骰子六枚，忘未携得，欲返取之。叔不耐往还，约在西安门妳茶铺中坐候。白独返外家，取得二事，更为留连一饷，然后行。

    Explanation

    559

    Lan Yan said: A single moment of goodness can transform a tiger into a human; negligence quickly arises, and a human can become a tiger. This boundary between sainthood and madness lies in the subtlest of distinctions. Although, the tiger is not an ordinary beast. Yan was generous and bold, so he could transform into a tiger; but as for those in the world who are wicked, mediocre, and庸碌, they might even desire to become dogs but cannot be guards, how can they hope to become tigers?

    As for the eunuch Bai, who is pale and foolish, known as Bai. He is in his forties. Once he told me that when he was sixteen, it was the Lantern Festival, and the guards were not enforcing restrictions, the lanterns and moonlight shone together. He went to his maternal grandmother's house in the west city with his uncle, and played hu lu with his sisters and brothers until late at night. After the fourth watch, he finally returned home. On the way, he suddenly remembered the promotion map given by his cousin and the six dice, which he had forgotten to take. He wanted to go back to get them. His uncle was not patient with the back-and-forth and agreed to meet at a teahouse in Xi'an Gate. Bai returned alone to his maternal home, obtained the two things, and spent some more time lingering before continuing his journey.

    560

    时已五更，街市人迹已稀，路出白塔寺后回廊下，见一人，隔车轨并行，不禁寒栗满身。视其人，高不过三尺，块然一物，淡黑色，别无头面耳目手足，如一簇浓烟，且月下无影。大怖，奋步急行，而物行尤驶。相随里许，蓦一人迎面来，正与物对，物且却且跃，倏左倏右，状颇仓皇。来人浑如未睹，直前无恐，物窘迫一闪，化为旋风羊角而起，高丈余，投东去。司栅老军瞥见之，弃柝惊喊，曰：“何人？”白答以归家者，老军曰：“非问汝也，适有一人到栅前，何一旋则不复见？”白心知为鬼，漫应之。

    　　比至西安门，心旌未定，见其叔坐茶铺中，神色沮丧，方将以所见告之，叔急摇手止之，似有所讳。乃相与茫茫然归。又于途间频嘱，即有所遇，归家慎勿宣泄。白口应而心疑焉。越数夕，其叔病死。

    Explanation

    560

    It was already the fifth watch of the night, and the streets were nearly deserted. Walking along the rear corridor of the White Pagoda Temple, I saw a figure walking alongside the tracks of a cart, and I was struck with a shiver that ran through my body. Looking at the figure, it was no taller than three feet, a mere object, a pale black color, without a head, face, eyes, ears, hands, or feet, like a thick cluster of smoke, and it cast no shadow under the moon. I was filled with great fear and ran quickly, but the thing moved even faster. It followed me for a distance of about a mile, when suddenly a person came face-to-face, confronting the thing directly. The thing retreated and leaped, suddenly to the left, then to the right, looking quite panicked. The newcomer, as if he hadn't seen it, moved forward without fear. The thing, in distress, suddenly darted away, transforming into a swirling wind like a ram's horn and rising up, about three feet high, and flew eastward. The old gatekeeper caught a glimpse of it, dropped his staff, and shouted in fright, "Who is it?" White replied that he was going home. The gatekeeper said, "I'm not asking you. Just now, a person came to the gate, why, after a moment, did it disappear?" White knew in his heart that it was a ghost and replied carelessly.

    By the time he reached the Xi'an Gate, his mind was still unsettled. He saw his uncle sitting in a teahouse, with a dejected expression, and was about to tell him what he had seen, when his uncle quickly shook his head to stop him, as if hiding something. They returned home in a daze. Along the way, the uncle repeatedly warned him, "If you meet anything, be careful not to reveal it at home." White agreed with his mouth but doubted in his heart. After several nights, his uncle died of illness.

    561

    兰岩曰：白之所遇，其叔之鬼耶？令人不解。

    　　孪生同州有兄弟孪生者，年各二十，貌皆姣好，声音笑言，虽家人往往误识，唯于衣履取别焉。少孤，同从学其季父于解库中，并知名于乡里，然性皆多疑。既授室，各防闲其妻，甚于缧绁。伯得子，见之讶曰：“何酷似其叔也！得毋汝已作陈平嫂耶？”妻大恚，嗤曰：“汝与叔有何分别，何怪怀抱中物？”伯终不释，然故疏其防，留心以伺其隙。

    　　仲妻为郡中巨族女，容色埒其嫂，而针黹过之，尤工绘事。一日，仲嘱曰：“汝既善画，盍写吾二人小照？”妻问作何装束，布何景物，仲曰：“俗式须避之，今当作梧下花间，赏春坐月。汝衣短衫，支颐倚湖山；画我出浴，着单裙，不衫不履，把卷栏前。”妻曰：“似太鄙媟，将何以举示人？”仲固强之。数日写成，神情逼肖，集古句以题之，曰：“但传消息不传情，一半梨花一半莺。珍重从今常倚壁，卿须怜我我怜卿。

    Explanation

    561

    Lan Yan said: The white one encountered, could it be the spirit of his uncle? This leaves one unable to understand.

    There were twins born in the same place, both brothers, each twenty years old, both very handsome. Their voices and laughter were so similar that even family members often mistook one for the other, distinguishing them only by their clothing and shoes. Orphaned at a young age, they studied together under their uncle in a pawnshop, both gaining fame in their hometown, yet both had a tendency to be suspicious. After they married, they both closely monitored their wives, even more so than if they were prisoners. The elder brother saw his son and was surprised, saying, "How much he resembles his uncle! Are you already acting like Teng Ping's wife?" The wife became very angry and sarcastically said, "What difference is there between you and your uncle, why are you surprised by the baby?" The elder brother remained unconvinced but lessened his vigilance, watching for any signs of wrongdoing.

    The younger brother's wife was a daughter of a prominent family in the region. Her appearance was comparable to her sister-in-law's, but she excelled in needlework, especially in painting. One day, the younger brother instructed her, "Since you are skilled in painting, why don't you paint a small portrait of the two of us?" She asked what attire and scenery to depict, and he said, "Avoid the conventional style; let's do it under a wutong tree in a flower-filled setting, enjoying spring and sitting under the moon. You wear a short shirt, leaning against a lake and mountains; paint me taking a bath, wearing a single skirt, without a shirt or shoes, holding a book before the railing." She said, "It seems too vulgar, how can we show it to others?" The younger brother insisted on it. After several days, the painting was completed, capturing their expressions very accurately. The painting was inscribed with ancient phrases, which read: "Only messages are passed, not emotions; half is like pear blossoms, half like orioles. Cherish each other as you lean against the wall; you must love me, and I must love you."

    562

    ”仲玩不去手，既而谤视，忽大疑曰：“汝为谁写真乎？”妻不测其由，还应曰：“我亦弗知为谁写矣。”仲曰：“嘱汝写我，几曾着汝写兄。”妻闻之，两颊晕生，强笑曰：“汝兄弟面貌，原不相远，但我第知写汝，不知写伯。”仲见其面赧变色，曰：“不予汝证据，汝肯甘耶？兄左腋下黑痣，惟我知之，汝未见其裸裎，何处见此？”妻无以对，取图视之，始莞尔曰：“几为汝所窘，此蝇矢所污，非笔点者，汝自目力不济耳。”仲不顾，则握拳捽发痛殴，欲出之。

    Explanation

    562

    Zhong was so preoccupied with the portrait that he kept it in his hand. Suddenly, he scrutinized it with suspicion and said, "Who did you draw a portrait of?" His wife, unable to guess his reasoning, replied, "I also don't know who it was for." Zhong said, "I asked you to draw me, how could you have drawn my brother instead?" Hearing this, her cheeks turned red, and she forced a smile, saying, "Your brothers' appearances are originally not very different, but I only knew to draw you, not your elder brother." Seeing her face turn red, Zhong said, "If I don't give you evidence, will you admit it? My elder brother has a black mole on his left armpit, which only I know. You haven't seen him naked, how could you have noticed this?" The wife had no answer, so she took the portrait and looked at it, then chuckled and said, "You almost had me cornered. This is just a spot of fly excrement, not a brushstroke. It's just that your eyesight isn't good enough." Without hesitation, Zhong grabbed her by the hair and beat her, intending to expel her.

    563

    妻之父母，闻之大哄，具牒鸣于太守。太守验其兄，果有痣，狱不能决。会邑宰入白事，守告之，宰曰：“职初任沔县时，亦有孪生姊妹，为夫家所出者，母家来诉，询之，盖其妹夫佻达，恒绐其姊夫曰：”素与大姨交好，苟不信，乳间有朱瘢，可证也。‘其姐夫归验其妻，果有赤斑大如钱，遂信而出之。职诘其娅，力言实出戏言，缘己妻乳间有瘢，故聊以为戏，初不料其亦然也。复验其妹不妄。讼始息。

    Explanation

    563

    The in-laws of the husband raised a great commotion and prepared a formal document to lodge a complaint with the prefect. The prefect examined the husband's brother and found that he had a mole, but the case could not be decided. Just as the county magistrate entered to report, the prefect informed him, and the magistrate said, "When I was first appointed as the magistrate of Mianxian County, there were also twin sisters who were expelled by their husband's family. The mother's family came to complain, and upon inquiry, it turned out that her sister-in-law's husband was frivolous and always deceived her brother-in-law by saying, 'I have always been on good terms with the elder aunt. If you don't believe me, there is a red mark between my breasts, which can prove it.' Her brother-in-law returned home to check his wife and indeed found a red spot as big as a coin, so he believed her and expelled her. I questioned her sister-in-law, and she strongly stated that it was just a joke because her own wife had a mark between her breasts, so she just used it as a joke, not expecting it to be the same. Upon re-examining her sister, it was found to be true, and the lawsuit finally ended."

    564

    今毋仍亦若是乎？“守因裸仲观之，左腋下亦有黑痣，与伯无异，始屈服。守不之罪，判而释之。好事者录其辞曰：”审得某氏子，双生并育，一乳同胞。合浦明珠，剖胎得二；昆山白玉，琢璧成双。即各缔其丝　，恒不调其琴瑟。姒防夫弟，记生儿敢羡参军；娣避夫兄，轻写照逢嗔太尉。反夫妻之目，生疑在两靥红潮；传伉俪之神，聚讼为弹丸黑子。谊关手足，看来俱玷微瑕；痛切肌肤，归去仍完太璞。从此纵窥青帐，嫂不妨为阿叔解围；时或出易新装，婶岂至将伯兄错唤。无更寻瘢索绽，还须笃爱敦伦。“

    　　兰岩曰：愚庸中笑，类多如是。独是太守判词，如此该博秀雅，而决讼不能明断，苟非邑令引证，几至淹留案牍，岂读书人徒工词章，而不留心政事耶？

    Explanation

    564

    Why is it still like this today? "The official observed the person and saw that there was also a black mole on his left armpit, no different from the elder brother, and he finally submitted. The official did not punish him but judged and released him. A well-intentioned person recorded his words as follows: 'It was found that the son of a certain family, twins born together, nursed from the same breast. Like the two pearls found in the belly of a turtle from Hépǔ, which are rare and valuable; like two jade disks carved from the white jade of Kuānshān, which are rare and valuable. They each have their own thread or destiny, but they never harmonize their lutes and zithers. The younger sister-in-law avoids her husband's younger brother, remembering that he was envious of her being a military officer; the elder sister-in-law avoids her husband's elder brother, lightly sketching him and causing him to become angry. They are not recognized as husband and wife, and they suspect each other due to the red flush on their cheeks. They are considered a model of marital harmony, but they argue over a small black spot on a ball. Their relationship is like that of siblings, and they both have minor flaws. It hurts deeply, but they return and remain whole and unblemished. From now on, even if they look into the green tent, the sister-in-law will not hesitate to help her brother-in-law out of trouble; sometimes they change into new clothes, and the aunt will not mistakenly call her elder brother. Do not search for scars or stitches, but still need to show deep love and maintain proper relationships.'

    Lan Yan said: Among the foolish and mediocre, laughter often arises in this manner. However, the magistrate's judgment, so comprehensive and elegant, could not make a clear decision. If it were not for the county magistrate providing evidence, the case might have lingered in the archives. Is it true that scholars are only skilled in writing and do not pay attention to governance?"

    565

    某　王　子相传明朝某王子，出侧室，性残忍。居恒无所事事，雅与阉奴媚子，纵肆淫暴。媵侍小有过，辄烧铁褫衣烙之，或将未烬烟灰置其掌中，灰烬皮焦而后已；不容转侧，苟不隐忍，则非刑复更矣。猫犬稍不惬意，猫则缚四足于四犬，鞭之四走，以分其体；犬则用四驴或四马，盖仿古车裂刑也。尝设巨镬于殿中，沸油满之，捕燕雀蝙蝠生煎之，俾焦黑，蘸椒盐以佐酒，逐一下箸，数十枚不厌也。未袭封，病痨瘵而死。

    　　死已二年矣，其府中长史某，忽一夜梦见之：被发裸身，颜色悲惨。惊询所自，王子泣诉曰：“予生时不仁至极，死后备尝地狱之苦，今阴谴已定，当托生为驴，公明日可至某大街某坊某市前，系有牝白草驴一头，瘦而秃尾者，即予之生母也；驴腹中怀朐，即予也。公幸念夙昔，赎我母子归，不致毙命屠刀，则恩同再造矣。”言讫，悲声悔切。长史惊而寤，阴异之，叹息不能复寐，反侧达旦。

    Explanation

    565

    A Certain Prince of the Ming Dynasty

    It is said that a certain prince of the Ming Dynasty, born of a concubine, was cruel and merciless by nature. He spent his days idly, often associating with eunuchs and favored servants, indulging in wanton and brutal behavior. If a concubine or attendant made even a minor mistake, he would strip them and brand them with a hot iron, or place unburned ashes on their palms, scorching their skin until it was charred black. He would not allow them to turn away, and if they could not endure the pain in silence, he would subject them to further tortures. If a cat or dog displeased him, he would bind the cat's four legs to four dogs and whip them, causing the cat to be torn apart; for a dog, he would use four donkeys or four horses, imitating the ancient method of dismemberment. He once set up a large cauldron in the palace, filled with boiling oil, and caught sparrows and bats, frying them alive. He would dip them in pepper and salt to serve with wine, consuming them one by one, never tiring of the number, even when dozens were eaten. He died of tuberculosis before inheriting his title.

    Two years after his death, a certain grand secretary in his household suddenly had a dream of him: the prince appeared with disheveled hair and a naked body, his expression sad and pitiful. Startled, the grand secretary asked him why he had come. The prince wept and said, "In life, I was utterly unkind, and after death, I have suffered all the torments of hell. Now my karmic retribution is determined, and I am to be reborn as a donkey. Tomorrow, you may go to a certain street, alley, and market, where you will find a female white donkey, thin and with a bald tail. That is my mother. The donkey's belly is pregnant, and that is me. If you remember our past, please redeem my mother and me, so that we do not die at the butcher's knife. Your grace will be equivalent to giving us a second life." After speaking, he wept bitterly and regretfully. The grand secretary woke up in shock, finding the dream strange and sighing deeply, unable to sleep again, tossing and turning until dawn.

    566

    翌日驰车入市，往观之，果有怀驹牝驴，系肆前，形色如所梦。甫下车，驴向之长鸣，两目泪下如渖，长史亦为之潸然，呼肆主询曰：“此驴鬻乎？”对曰：“此昨日用钱五千买得者，今将杀以卖肉，不生鬻也。”长史曰：“不然。杀以卖肉不过欲多得钱耳，汝但言杀此驴得利几何，吾当倍赎之。”肆主人曰：“大人具恻隐心，必欲赎之，小人何敢过索？并本利得钱六千可矣。”长史如数给之，牵驴以归。

    　　是夜复梦王子及母来谢。长史弗敢隐，乘间白诸王。王乍聆之，不胜错愕，继而叹惋良久，复恨恨曰：“暴戾子，固应服此冥报。即其母之阴贼悍妒，亦当如是。虽然，父子之情，未可绝也。城外园寝，地广草盛，可纵之其中，俾樗散以终其天年可也。”长史唯唯从命。纵之日，即生驹。王一日过之，二驴见王，伏地流泪。王试呼其名，则摇尾而嘶，似呼似答，王亦恻然者久之，忧悒而返。及王薨，二驴不知存否。

    Explanation

    566

    The next day, the Prefect drove his carriage to the market to look for the donkey. Indeed, there was a pregnant female donkey tied in front of a shop, and its appearance was as in his dream. As soon as he got off the carriage, the donkey neighed loudly at him, and tears flowed from her eyes like a stream. The Prefect also shed tears and called the shop owner, asking, "Is this donkey for sale?" The shop owner replied, "This was bought for five thousand coins yesterday and is now going to be slaughtered to sell the meat; it will not be sold alive." The Prefect said, "Not so. Slaughtering it to sell the meat is just to get more money. Just tell me how much profit you would make by slaughtering this donkey, and I will double the amount to redeem it." The shop owner said, "You have a compassionate heart. If you definitely want to redeem it, how dare I ask for too much? Six thousand coins including the original price and profit would be sufficient." The Prefect gave him the amount as requested and took the donkey home.

    That night, he had another dream where the prince and his mother came to thank him. The Prefect did not dare to hide it and took the opportunity to report to the king. The king was initially shocked and surprised, then sighed and lamented for a long time, and said with deep resentment, "A cruel and violent son should indeed suffer this karmic retribution. Even his mother's jealousy and cruelty should also be punished in the same way. Although, the relationship between father and son cannot be completely severed. The royal cemetery outside the city is vast and overgrown with grass. It can be released there to live out his natural life in peace." The Prefect obediently followed the order. On the day it was released, a foal was born. One day, the king passed by, and the two donkeys saw him, laying on the ground and weeping. The king tried calling their names, and they wagged their tails and neighed, as if responding. The king also felt deeply moved for a long time and returned feeling sad and depressed. When the king died, the fate of the two donkeys is unknown.

    567

    闲斋曰：甚矣夫！福善祸淫之理，毫发不容假贷也。以王子之贵，不悛于恶，降而为驴，天岂有私于人哉！人往往不信因果之说，而此事则又一时所共传，尚何因果之不足信哉？此事可信，则相传白起、李林甫、秦桧托来生为猪之说，亦必不诬矣。汉昭烈曰：“无以恶小而为之，无以善小而不为。”后世王公，有守此言，为子孙义方之训，日耳提而面命之，庶几乎世德相承，箕裘之克绍也。

    　　兰岩曰：生前凶暴残忍备至，死后为驴，几不免毕命屠刀，亦云惨极矣。世之暴戾狠毒阴险辈，幸早回头，免至系颈市前时，望人赎救而不可得也。

    Explanation

    567

    闲斋曰：How great it is indeed! The principle of rewarding virtue and punishing vice allows for no leniency, not even a single hair. Even a prince, with his high status, who does not repent of his evil, descends to become a donkey; does Heaven show any favoritism towards humans? People often do not believe in the doctrine of cause and effect, yet this matter is widely accepted and spread at the time, so how can the doctrine of cause and effect be doubted? If this matter is credible, then the belief that Bai Qi, Li Linfu, and Qin Hui would be reborn as pigs in their next life is also likely to be true. Han Zhao Lie said, "Do not do evil because it is small, and do not neglect good because it is small." Future rulers who adhere to this saying, use it as a moral education for their descendants, instruct them personally and face-to-face, may perhaps ensure the transmission of virtue through generations and the continuation of their ancestors' legacy.

    　　兰岩曰：Before death, extremely cruel and violent, after death becomes a donkey, almost inevitably meeting a gruesome fate at the butcher's knife, it is truly a tragic end. Those in the world who are cruel, vicious, and treacherous, should happily turn back early, avoiding being tied around the neck in front of the market, hoping for redemption by others but finding it impossible.

    568

    再生永平某村，有翁媪业豆腐者，性皆好善。遇有桥梁道途朽敝泥淖者，则出所蓄资，极力修补，数十年如一日也。

    　　会村有石桥，为大水所坏，行旅不通，翁复鸠工缮理，身亦操作其间。日午倦惫，倚坐桥柱少憩。瞥见二青衣人，蓦然来前，类县中差役，呼翁曰：“可亟往。”翁问何之，曰：“至则自知耳。”翁不敢违拂，乃起身从之行。约十余里，入一村，见巨宅甚壮，翁识为某村大富家某人宅也。青衣促翁入，历数重门，直到寝室，室中妇女甚伙，其环绕一少年妇，方临蓐。翁愕然惊却，青衣拼力推挤，不觉跌入少妇腹中，骤觉通身如渥沸汤，辗转挣斗，旋复寒甚，恍卧霜雪，耳中闻人语曰：“恭喜娘子，生得一儿郎矣！”翁大惊，开眼四顾，悉如所见，自视其拳，仅如胡桃，始悟身死，已降生于此地矣。悲从中来，方呱呱而哭。

    Explanation

    568

    In the village of Yongping, there lived an elderly couple who made tofu for a living. Both of them were inclined to do good. Whenever they encountered broken or dilapidated bridges and muddy roads, they would spend their savings to repair them with great effort, consistently over many years without interruption.

    One day, there was a stone bridge in the village that had been destroyed by a flood, making it impassable for travelers. The old man once again gathered workers to repair it, and he also worked there himself. At noon, he was tired and exhausted, so he leaned against a bridge pillar to rest briefly. Suddenly, he saw two men in blue robes appear before him, resembling county officials or servants. They called out to the old man, saying, "You must go quickly." The old man asked where he should go, and they replied, "You will know when you get there." The old man dared not disobey, so he got up and followed them. After walking about ten or so miles, they entered a village and saw a large and impressive house. The old man recognized it as the house of a wealthy man in a certain village.

    The men in blue robes urged the old man to enter, and he passed through several gates until they reached the bedroom. There were many women in the room, surrounding a young woman who was in labor. The old man was startled and stepped back. The men in blue robes pushed him forcefully, and without realizing it, he fell into the young woman's womb. Suddenly, he felt his whole body as if soaked in boiling water. He struggled and fought, but soon felt extremely cold again, as if lying in frost and snow. He heard someone say in his ear, "Congratulations, Madam, you have given birth to a son!" The old man was greatly shocked. He opened his eyes and looked around, and everything was exactly as he had seen. He looked at his own fist, which was only as big as a walnut. He finally realized that he had died and been reborn in this place. Sadness welled up from within him, and he began to cry loudly like a newborn.

    569

    忽一半老妇，持剪刀剪其脐，痛入心髓，不禁失声曰：“老乞婆莫恶作剧！”举室猝闻儿语，咸大惊扰，翁曰：“汝等勿恐，我某村某翁也。今观此局，是托生汝家矣；既至汝家，即为汝家儿，夫复何言。但我有老妻贫且病，我死，彼将何依？可招之来此，分得屋两间，使居之，日给粗粝三餐，冬给一棉衣蔽寒，以终其余年，斯可矣。无过分，恐其福薄不胜也。

    Explanation

    569

    Suddenly, an elderly woman appeared, holding scissors to cut the child's navel. The pain was so intense that it penetrated to the bone, and the child couldn't help but cry out, "Old beggar woman, don't play tricks!" The entire household suddenly heard the child's voice and was greatly startled. The old man said, "Do not be afraid, I am an old man from a certain village. Now, seeing this situation, I have been reborn into your family; once I have come to your family, I will be your family's child, what more can I say? But I have an old wife who is poor and ill. When I die, who will she rely on? You can bring her here, give her two rooms, let her live there, provide her with three coarse meals a day, and give her a cotton coat for winter to keep warm, to see out the rest of her years. That will suffice. Do not go too far, fear that her blessings are thin and she cannot bear it."

    570

    我尸在桥柱下，可使人急往，殓以布衣布衾，一柏木棺，即瘗之桥侧，无过费，则吾始得安心处此。”其家不信，翁躁怒，大声促之，家人欲往，翁曰：“汝等去或行诈，须抱我亲往料理。”家人不得已，以绣被裹翁而行。至其处，果一一悉如所言。翁与媪絮絮回答，宛然结发。媪大恸，翁止之曰：“有我在，无忧孤寡。”既而至桥下，翁尸亦官验将殓矣。翁叹息再四，命易以柏棺，亲视安葬，遂与媪俱归，豢之别室。其家只翁一子承嗣，拥资百万。阅年，其父死。母二十而寡，爱翁如掌珠。翁行善好施，由于天性，逾于前生，人以为善人之报云。

    Explanation

    570

    My corpse is under the bridge pillar, you can send someone to hurry there, shroud it in simple clothing and quilt, place it in a single cypress wood coffin, and bury it beside the bridge without excessive expense, then I will finally be able to rest in peace here." The family did not believe him, and the old man became angry and impatient, shouting urgently to them. The family members wanted to go, but the old man said, "If you go, you might cheat; you must carry me personally to handle it." The family members had no choice but to wrap the old man in a brocade quilt and take him along. When they arrived, everything was exactly as he had said. The old man and the old woman conversed warmly, as if they were newly married. The old woman wept bitterly, and the old man comforted her, saying, "With me here, there is no worry about being alone or widowed." Soon they reached the bridge, and the old man's body was also being examined by officials and prepared for burial. The old man sighed repeatedly, ordered the use of a cypress coffin, personally supervised the burial, and then returned with the old woman to a separate room where they were cared for. The family had only the old man's son as the heir, who possessed a million in assets. After a year, the father died. The mother, twenty years old, became a widow and loved the old man as if he were her most precious possession. The old man was kind and generous, due to his nature, surpassing his previous life, and people believed it was a reward for his goodness.

    571

    王侃王侃行三，房山农家子。耘于田，大风倏起，沙石飞走，方欲引避，瞥见一画衣女子，被发跣足，冒风而至，连呼：“三郎救我命！”王仓卒不暇致详，则问曰：“何以救子？”女曰：“但匿我于庐棚下，少时有旋风来，即追我者，第云已西去矣。”言讫，钻入棚。俄而果有旋风来自东北，大如浮屠，急如奔马，绕田数匝，木叶尽脱。王如女所教，向风西指以绐之，风即雷鸣而西，似解人语。王大错愕。

    Explanation

    571

    Wang Kan, the third son of a peasant family from Fangshan, was working in the fields when a sudden, violent wind arose, carrying sand and stones. Just as he was about to take shelter, he caught a glimpse of a woman in a painted robe, her hair loose and her feet bare, coming against the wind. She called out repeatedly, "Third Young Master, save my life!" Wang, in a state of panic, did not have time to ask for details, so he asked, "How can I save you?" The woman replied, "Just hide me under the shed. In a short while, a whirlwind will come to chase me. Just say I have already gone west." After saying this, she darted into the shed. Soon enough, a whirlwind indeed came from the northeast, as large as a pagoda, swift as a galloping horse, circling the field several times, stripping all the leaves from the trees. Wang followed the woman's instructions, pointing westward to the wind to deceive it. The whirlwind then roared and moved westward, as if it understood human speech. Wang was greatly surprised.

    572

    风既过，启芦棚，女子已危坐其中，裂裙缚足，含笑绾髻，香汗尚濡，喘息未定。娥眉曼□，嫭目腾光。薄而观之，妖艳无匹。王年当戒色，且喜且惊，款言慰藉，曰：“追者已杳，子可无患，第不自信，亦有施于予否？”女起拜曰：“深恩大德，永志弗谖。”王曰：“然则何以报我？”女曰：“金帛珠玉，惟郎所欲。”王笑曰：“吾何欲于是，将以求吾所大欲也。”女曰：“郎之所大欲，可得闻乎？”王笑而不言。女怒之以目，且笑曰：“郎大不良善，儿不得不作负心人矣。”言讫欲去，王张臂遮之，女从腋底钻出，殊轻迅，牵挽不及，釶无踪影，王大失所望，颇怀怨恨。

    Explanation

    572

    The wind had passed, and he opened the reed hut to find the woman already sitting upright inside. She had torn her skirt and bound her feet, smiling as she tied her hair, her sweat still damp, and her breath not yet steady. Her beautiful eyebrows were graceful, and her eyes sparkled with light. Upon closer inspection, she was gorgeously beautiful without equal. The man, who should have been abstaining from lust, was both relieved and surprised, and he spoke gently to comfort her. "The pursuers have vanished, and you can be free of worry. But I don't believe you, did you also help me?" The woman stood and bowed, saying, "Your great kindness and virtue will always be remembered by me." The man replied, "Then how shall I repay you?" The woman said, "Gold, silk, pearls, and jade—whatever the gentleman desires." The man laughed and said, "I have no desire for these things; I will seek what I greatly desire." The woman asked, "What is the gentleman's great desire, can it be heard?" The man smiled but did not speak. The woman glared at him with her eyes and then smiled, saying, "The gentleman is not very good, and I must become a heartless person." After finishing speaking, she wanted to leave, but the man stretched out his arms to block her. The woman slipped out from under his armpit, moving very light and swift, and he was unable to catch up. The sound of her footsteps disappeared, and the man was greatly disappointed, holding a lot of resentment.

    573

    日且暮，悒悒荷锄返。将度略彴，女子已预坐溪畔石上，笑谓王曰：“得无以中山狼见目耶？”王骤见之，化忧为喜，故作愠色曰：“子已脱祸，不自觅乐地，留此何为？”女遽前把握曰：“聊相戏，何便怨怼！若竟以儿为负心人。是知石而不知韫玉也，请偕归，幸勿以葑菲见弃！”王不胜狂喜，携之至家。

    Explanation

    573

    As the day was nearly over, the farmer, dejected and carrying his hoe, returned home. About to cross a narrow bridge, he saw a woman already seated on a stone by the stream. She smiled and said to him, "Surely you do not regard me as the 'wolf of Zhongshan'?" The farmer, seeing her suddenly, changed from worry to joy and feigned anger, saying, "You have escaped disaster, but you are not seeking your own place of happiness. Why are you staying here?" The woman quickly stepped forward and grasped his hand, saying, "I was just playing a joke. Why be resentful so soon? If you think of me as a heartless person, this is like knowing the stone but not the jade hidden within. Please come with me, and do not reject me as you would weeds!" The farmer was overwhelmed with joy and took her home.

    574

    王年甫二十有一，父母皆下世，唯一妹操作甚劬。见王携玉人至，惊问所自。王具告之，妹熟视而笑曰：“我见犹怜，何况三哥？”王曰：“多言可畏，请划一策。”妹曰：“不足虑也，所可虑者，东邻钟八耳。平日□□，好瑕疵乡里，飞短流长，殊堪厌恶。今已远避，去如黄鹤矣。观三嫂媚曼婉妙，秀于外，必慧于中，正好相依过日。第恐三哥福薄，不能消受耳。”女郎裣衽谢曰：“三郎有大恩于儿，委身事之，情理宜然，所虑姑不容耳。苟姑能见悯，诸事包荒，则和气致祥，安如磐石，人言不遑恤也。”妹得谀词愈喜，杀鸡为黍，俾二人合卺焉。

    Explanation

    574

    The young man was just turning twenty-one, and both his parents had passed away, leaving only his younger sister, who worked very hard. When he saw the young man bringing a beautiful woman, she was surprised and asked where she came from. The young man explained everything, and the sister looked at him carefully and laughed, saying, "I find her even more pitiable, how much more so for your brother?" The young man replied, "Much talk is to be feared, please suggest a plan." The sister said, "There's no need to worry, the only thing to worry about is East Neighbor Bell Eight. He usually likes to find faults in the village, spreading rumors and gossip, which is very annoying. He has already fled far away, like the Yellow Crane. Seeing how charming and graceful your sister-in-law is, beautiful on the outside, she must be wise on the inside. It's perfect to rely on each other for daily life. But I'm afraid your brother's fortune is thin, he might not be able to handle it." The young lady bowed and said, "Third Brother has done me a great favor, I will dedicate myself to him, which is reasonable and proper. The only concern is that the mother-in-law might not accept it. If the mother-in-law can show mercy, all matters will be covered, and harmony will bring good fortune, stable as a rock, and we won't have to worry about what others say." The sister, pleased by the flattering words, killed a chicken and prepared millet, allowing the two to consummate their marriage.

    575

    嗣此好逑甚敦，与妹亦相得无间。询其邦族，云是良乡白氏，年十九矣；幼失怙恃，孑然一身。昨偶出春游，不意为妖风所薄；微三郎，定为阎摩罗什天尊唤去矣。王曰：“夙昔只身寄托何所？”女曰：“无枝可栖，逐日漂泊如萍梗，幸藏身之固，不遭强暴。”王曰：“然则何以为生？”曰：“针耨而已。”妹曰：“心苟无瑕，何恤乎无家？从此三哥耕，嫂炊，儿瞔，无忧不作个好人家。三哥明日且办数匹布，为嫂作衣裙，几曾见农家妇女着此艳服者？”王以乏资对，女曰：“无作难，积得十匹布，收贮溪畔土地祠内香案下，劳往取之。”王初之不信，再四促之，王试往，果得十匹布，归以告妹，妹曰：“古庙荒凉，嫂何时置此物？”女漫应之。女性极慧巧，女红针黹，无不能，且无不精。妹凡百不逮，益爱敬之。

    Explanation

    575

    Since then, the couple have been very close, and she also gets along with her sister without any gap or conflict. When they inquired about her family background, she said she is from the Bai family of Liangxiang, and she is nineteen years old. She lost her parents at a young age and is alone and without family. Yesterday, she went out for a spring outing, unfortunately, she was caught in a sudden strong wind; without the help of Sanlang, she would have been called away by the Lord Yama. The king asked, "Where has she been staying since she became an orphan?" The woman replied, "She has no place to rest, wandering like a floating duckweed or a wandering weed. Fortunately, she found a safe place to hide and avoided being taken advantage of." The king asked, "Then how does she make a living?" She said, "She only knows how to sew and farm." The sister said, "If her heart is pure, why worry about not having a home? From now on, Sanlang will farm, I will cook, and the child will play. We will have no worries and live a good life. Sanlang should buy some cloth tomorrow to make clothes for me. Have you ever seen a farmer's wife wearing such beautiful clothes?" The king replied that he lacks the funds. The woman said, "Don't worry, there are ten bolts of cloth stored under the incense table in the earth god shrine by the stream. Please go and get them." Initially, the king did not believe her and urged her repeatedly. The king went to check and indeed found ten bolts of cloth. He returned and told his sister. The sister asked, "The ancient temple is desolate, when did you put these things there?" The woman replied vaguely. The woman is extremely intelligent and skilled in embroidery and sewing, she can do everything and does everything well. The sister is not as capable in everything, and thus loves and respects her more.

    576

    会旱蝗，田数十亩，仅获十之二三。兄妹日夕焦愁，谓冻馁不暇计，所虑无以输官。女独怡然，不以为意。王与妹计，往贷于同村牛大户，女止之曰：“汝二人设想左，计遂左矣。彼守钱虏，别有肺腑。苟无势力以压之，虽其至亲好友，少有所求，尚睫毛一寸长，棱棱若不相识；况陈远一贫人，年少面薄，徒取其辱，庸有济乎？不如任天顺命，事到至急至危，自有救解，郎姑待之。”王不听，整肃而往，果为牛所不礼，不胜忧悒。比反，催租吏已在门矣。

    Explanation

    576

    During a time of drought and locusts, the family's several tens of acres of fields yielded only two or three tenths of their usual harvest. The brothers and sisters were in distress day and night, thinking that they could not even afford to worry about freezing and starving, but what they were concerned about was not being able to pay the taxes. The daughter alone remained cheerful and did not take it to heart. Wang and his sister planned to borrow from the wealthy farmer in their village, but the daughter stopped them and said, "Your two assumptions are wrong, so your plans are also wrong. He is a money-grabbing person, with his own intentions. If you do not have power to pressure him, even if he is a close friend or relative, if he has a small request, he will still look at you with cold eyes as if he does not know you; how much more so for a distant poor person, a young and thin face, only to humiliate yourself, how could it help? It is better to leave it to fate and follow destiny, when things reach the most urgent and dangerous situation, there will naturally be a solution, please wait." Wang did not listen and went there in a formal manner, indeed, he was not treated with courtesy by the wealthy farmer, and he could not bear the distress. When he returned, the tax collector was already at the door.

    577

    见王大作威势，扭结不释手。王极力腾辩，延吏暂坐草堂。逡巡入室，议所以款之，女问应输几何，王曰：“并旧欠七两余矣。”女嗤曰：“儿以为盈千累万，须费数日踌躇，仅如此，有何不了！土地祠内，西北隅地砖下，有白金一坛，取偿讫，尚多余金，足为薪水之费。”王初闻之殊喜，既而疑为戏言，妹促之曰：“据前十匹布，此应不妄。速去，勿濡滞！”王乃越屋后短垣，急往掘之，果得黑磁坛，启视白镪满中，狂喜如寒儒乍第，急脱衣裸负以归，如数纳官，吏不能扰，仅取醉饱去。

    Explanation

    577

    Seeing Wang display his authority and being tightly bound, Wang vigorously argued and invited the officials to temporarily sit in a thatched cottage. He cautiously entered the room and discussed how to treat the officials. The woman asked how much should be paid, and Wang replied, "Including the old debt, it is seven taels and some more." The woman derisively remarked, "You thought it would be a large amount requiring several days of deliberation, but it is not so much!" In the land god temple, in the northwest corner under a tile, there is a jar of silver. After settling the debt, there is still some extra money left, which is enough for living expenses. Initially, Wang was very happy, but then he doubted it was a joke. His sister urged him, "Based on the previous ten bolts of cloth, this should not be a lie. Hurry and do not delay!" Wang jumped over the short wall behind the house, quickly went to dig it up, and indeed found a black ceramic jar. Upon opening it, he saw it was full of silver and was ecstatic, like a poor scholar who suddenly became wealthy. He quickly took off his clothes and carried the silver back, paying the official the exact amount owed. The officials could not interfere and only took enough to get drunk and full before leaving.

    578

    王权金适五百两，买田置宅，日渐饶裕，凡有营运，但听女言，无不获利数倍。二年，富甲一乡。王或以无嗣为念，女艴然曰：“郎甫得温饱，便思纳妾，何薄情至此！”王曰：“非云负义，恐先人之祀自我斩耳。”女曰：“然则勿絮聒，行当为郎举子。”王笑其谑。是夕同坐房中，女戒王且勿便睡，独登榻下帷，轧轧不知何作，约食顷，忽闻呱呱之声，女易衣而出，曰：“盍去看儿。”王大骇，启帷已绷一儿于床，眉目如画。王惊喜，便告于妹，妹来省视，靡不欢然，就室布筵为庆，女言笑饮啖，无殊平日。王兄妹窃疑之，因名为异生。

    Explanation

    578

    The king's wealth amounted to five hundred taels of gold, which he used to buy land and build a house, gradually becoming increasingly prosperous. In all business ventures, if he followed the woman's advice, there was no profit without doubling or tripling. After two years, he became the wealthiest in the village. The king sometimes worried about not having heirs, and the woman angrily said, "You have just achieved comfort and warmth, and already you think of taking a concubine, how unfeeling are you!" The king replied, "I am not saying you are unfaithful, but I fear that the ancestral sacrifices will be cut off by me." The woman said, "Then do not nag, I will soon give you a child." The king laughed at her jest. That night, they sat together in the room, and the woman warned the king not to fall asleep, then she alone climbed onto the bed and lowered the curtain, making clattering sounds as she did something unknown. After about an hour, suddenly they heard the sound of a baby crying. The woman changed her clothes and came out, saying, "Why don't you go see the child?" The king was greatly startled. When he lifted the curtain, there was a newborn child on the bed, with eyes and eyebrows like a painting. The king was both surprised and delighted, and immediately told his sister. She came to see the child, and all were joyful. They set up a feast in the room to celebrate. The woman talked, laughed, ate, and drank, no different from her usual self. The king and his sister secretly doubted her, and thus named the child "Yisheng."

    579

    同邑有大户刘翁，家资巨万，有子名璇，为国学生，二十未娶。闻王之妹美且艳，其家遣媒来议婚，王欲许之，女独力阻，以为不可。王曰：“刘家富而好礼，璇亦少年诚恳，以之归妹，得所天矣。卿奈何作梗？”遂不听女言，竟许之，女叹曰：“姻缘的是天定，违天不祥，第儿与刘家子有仇隙，虽为亲患，仍当避之，郎至时，切勿使彼与儿相见。苟相强，则祸作矣。幸志之勿忘！”王漫应之。及于归，琴瑟甚和。然璇熟闻女美，甚思见之，亟请于王，王弗许。

    Explanation

    579

    In the same county, there was a wealthy man named Liu, who had millions of assets and a son named Xuan, who was studying at the national academy and was twenty years old but not yet married. Hearing that Wang's sister was beautiful and charming, Liu's family sent a matchmaker to discuss marriage. Wang wanted to agree to it, but his daughter strongly opposed it, believing it was not right. Wang said, "Liu's family is wealthy and well-mannered, and Xuan is a sincere young man. Marrying her to Xuan would be a perfect match. Why are you obstructing it?" He did not listen to his daughter's words and finally agreed. The daughter sighed, "Marriage is determined by fate, and going against fate is unlucky. But my daughter has a grudge with Liu's son, even though it is a family matter, it should still be avoided. When Liu arrives, do not let him see my daughter. If they insist, disaster will follow. Please remember this!" Wang nodded vaguely. When she returned home, the relationship was very harmonious. However, Xuan, having heard about the daughter's beauty, greatly wanted to see her and urgently asked Wang, but Wang did not agree.

    580

    璇乃与妇谋，设酒招王饮，因乘便潜至王家。适值女哺儿于庭，璇突前揖之，女仓卒不及回避，但以袖蔽面，伫立不敢少动。璇审视大惊，踉跄奔归。比抵家，色犹灰败，王兄妹惊问何故。璇宁息良久，始转问王曰：“尊嫂谁氏女，伉俪几年矣？其中大有异，幸明示，勿少隐。”王初支吾，不以实告，璇正色曰：“至亲骨肉，无所用伪。吾所以谆谆致诘者，自有深意，兄何见外之甚也？”妹怀惑已久，闻璇言有因，亦从旁和之。王不得已，悉为吐实，璇骇曰：“兄遇妖矣！”王曰：“何以见得？”璇曰：“不敢相欺，弟久慕嫂贤淑，深以不获一面为憾。顷者留兄饮，特引身造府一拜，相遭于庭，弟甚惊其艳丽，熟视之，非他，即祸弟者也。弟三年前，适野展墓，遇此女于中途，倾慕綦殷，既归，女已在室，云是白氏女，与弟有夙因，彼时神魂丧失，无所顾瞻，遂相欢好。两月余，日渐尪羸，父母知为邪祟，百计驱逐之不去。

    Explanation

    580

    Xuan then conspired with his wife to invite Wang for a drink and secretly visited Wang's house. Just as Wang's wife was feeding their child in the courtyard, Xuan suddenly stepped forward and bowed to her. She was so surprised that she didn't have time to avoid him; she only covered her face with her sleeve and stood still without moving much. Xuan examined her closely and was greatly shocked, stumbling back home. By the time he reached home, his face was still pale and disheartened. Wang's brother and sister were surprised and asked him what had happened. After calming down for a long time, Xuan finally turned to Wang and said, "Who is your wife, and how many years have you been married? There is something very strange about her, please be frank with me and don't hide anything." Wang initially stammered and did not tell the truth. Xuan said seriously, "As close relatives, there is no need to be deceitful. I repeatedly questioned you because there is a deeper meaning to it; why are you so distant, brother?" The sister had been puzzled for a long time and, hearing Xuan's words with reason, also agreed from the side. Wang, unable to help but, told everything honestly. Xuan was shocked and said, "Brother, you have met an evil spirit!" Wang asked, "How can you see that?" Xuan said, "I dare not deceive you. I have long admired your wife's virtue and deeply regret not having met her. Recently, I invited you for a drink and especially came to your house to pay respects. We met in the courtyard, and I was greatly surprised by her beauty. I looked at her closely, and she is not someone else, but the one who has caused me trouble. Three years ago, when I went to the countryside to visit graves, I met this woman on the way and was deeply infatuated. After returning, she was already in the room, saying she was a woman from the Bai family and had a predestined connection with me. At that time, I lost my senses, without any consideration, and we became intimate. More than two months later, I gradually became weak and emaciated. My parents knew it was an evil spirit and tried every method to drive it away but failed."

    581

    会有姜道士者，以神术闻于山东，父母以礼致之，求其作法，姜但朱书一符，命焚其一于中堂，其一令什袭藏之，言数年后尚有用处。父母遵其数，即日焚之。弟亲见一神人，状类庙中所塑灵官然，入房来捉女。女仓皇被跣，御风而奔。神人逐之，遂不复返。弟病渐痊。今闻兄得嫂之日，正神人逐妖之日也。兄溺爱枕席，必不以弟言为是。朱符虽在，不足为凭，然倘为妖女，体有异香，又尝深护其尻骨，不令人扪結.倘尊嫂亦然，确为妖矣。

    Explanation

    581

    There was a Daoist named Jiang, famous for his supernatural abilities in Shandong. The parents invited him with courtesy, seeking his help. Jiang merely wrote a red talisman and instructed them to burn one in the main hall and carefully store the other in ten layers, saying it would still be useful in several years. The parents followed his instructions and burned it on the same day. The younger brother personally saw a divine being, resembling the deity depicted in the temple, enter the room to catch the woman. The woman, in a state of panic, was stripped and flew through the wind. The divine being chased her, and she did not return. The younger brother's illness gradually healed. Now, hearing that the elder brother married his wife on the same day the demon was chased away, he certainly did not believe the younger brother's words. Although the red talisman is still there, it is not a reliable proof. However, if the woman is indeed a female demon, she would have a strange fragrance and her coccyx bone would be carefully protected, making it impossible for anyone to touch. If your respected sister-in-law is the same, she is certainly a demon.

    582

    第未识尊嫂，果有此可取证否？”王闻之，哆口张目，欲言不能。妹曰：“尻骨吾弗知，体香良不妄，三哥宜早为计，勿贻后悔。”王徐徐喟然叹曰：“据妹丈言，其为妖女无疑，但好合以来，家赖之以富，子赖之以育，妹赖之以适君子，其有造于我王氏者，亦大矣。尝闻以德报怨，不闻以怨报德，况内人贤淑，必非酖毒，虽云异类，何忍弃之？休矣，愚兄不忍复闻。”璇曰：“蜂虿有毒，矧妖魅乎？脱拂良言，行当索兄于枯鱼之肆耳。”相与不欢而罢。

    　　王去后，其妹终不释然，乃潜以符至家，焚于寝门，顿觉狂风大作，女自房奔出，未数武，则踣地化为黑狐，冲门而去。有旋风随其后，急如飞电，顷刻不知所向。王惊定大恸，不食，数日而死。女亦不复至，惟异生仅存，萧然一室云。

    Explanation

    582

    "Have you not recognized my respected sister-in-law, and can this truly be proven?" Wang heard this and opened his mouth wide, unable to speak. His sister said, "I do not know about the coccyx, but the body odor is indeed genuine. Brother Three, you should make plans early and not regret it later." Wang slowly sighed and said, "According to my brother-in-law, she is undoubtedly a demon, but since we have been together, our family has prospered, our children have been raised, and my sister has been able to marry a gentleman. Her contributions to our Wang family are also great. I have heard that one should repay resentment with virtue, not vice versa. Moreover, my wife is virtuous and gentle, and she certainly did not poison anyone. Even if she is of a different kind, how can I bear to abandon her? Enough, my foolish elder brother cannot bear to hear more." His sister said, "Even stinging insects and scorpions have venom, how much more so for demons? If you disregard good advice, you will soon find me in the dried fish market." They parted ways without being happy.

    After Wang left, his sister remained troubled and secretly used a charm to go home. She burned it at the bedroom door, and suddenly a strong wind blew. The woman rushed out of the room, but after a few steps, she fell to the ground and transformed into a black fox, rushing out through the door. A whirlwind followed her, moving as fast as lightning, and in an instant, she was gone. Wang was shocked and deeply grieved, stopped eating, and died after several days. The woman no longer came, and only the surviving child remained, leaving the room desolate and empty.

    583

    兰岩曰：受恩图报，人且不能多得，况异类耶？王饮食子女，都赖此女，以死继之，亦不为过。

    　　台　方　伯故方伯合公讳布，罢官居家。夜起如厕，挂烛笼于壁。少间闻窗外窸窣有声，忽见一红袖出户下，广尺余，徐徐就壁，掩烛无光。叱之，亟缩去。既而又来，叱之复去。凡数四，台心悸，急起烛之，无所见。告诸夫人，夫人素有胆，乃率婢秉烛往视。甫及门，婢恐怖不敢入，夫人唾而诟之曰：“汝命独尊贵，怕吓死耶！”夺烛入照，觉有人隐身屋角。逼视之，则一红衣女子也。面然近尺，白如粉，掀唇蹙额，尸立如僵。夫人厉声曰：“汝鬼耶？现形欲何为？”以手批之，倏不见。台踵至，扶夫人归寝，灯下视夫人，面无人色。未几台病卒，越两日，夫人暴亡。

    583

    Lan Yan said: To repay kindness, even humans cannot always do so, how much less so for beings of a different kind? The King's food, drink, and children all depended on this woman; to follow through even unto death, it would not be excessive.

    The former Governor Fang Bo, whose courtesy name was He Gong, retired from his official position and lived at home. One night, he got up to go to the toilet and hung a candle lantern on the wall. After a short while, he heard a rustling sound from outside the window. Suddenly, he saw a red sleeve emerge from under the door, about a foot wide, slowly approaching the wall, covering the candle so it had no light. He scolded it, and it quickly retreated. It came again, and he scolded it again, and it retreated again. This happened several times, causing the official to feel heart palpitations. He quickly lit the candle and saw nothing. He told his wife, who was usually brave, and she led a maid with a candle to inspect. Just as they reached the door, the maid was terrified and dared not enter. The wife spat and scolded her, saying, "Your life is uniquely noble, are you afraid to be scared to death?" She grabbed the candle and entered to illuminate, feeling that someone was hiding in the corner of the room. She looked closely at it, and it was a red-clothed woman, her face about a foot away, white as powder, with lips parted and brows furrowed, standing stiff like a corpse. The wife shouted sternly, "Are you a ghost? What do you intend by appearing?" She slapped her with her hand, and the woman disappeared suddenly. The official followed closely, helped his wife back to bed, and by the lamp, he saw that his wife was pale-faced. Shortly after, the official died of illness, and two days later, his wife suddenly died.

    584

    兰岩曰：方伯显宦，鬼物何敢相近？或亦有冤抑郁？现形不避，亦方伯夫妇数当尽耳。

    　　瓦器京江陈扶胥先生，有佃户垦田，牛忽蹶，鞭之不起。察之，则牛蹄陷入泥中，已没至膝，拔而出之，得瓦器一窑，色唯黄白二种，共十二件，质绝粗，似盆而小，形类腰鼓，缘口缀磁珠如鸡头大，联属亦若鼓钉。佃户触落十余枚，越宿完好如故。先生试之，果然，深以为怪，复命葬之。或有言：“凿而复完，必聚宝之物。”再命发之，不可复得。

    Explanation

    584

    Lan Yan said: "A high-ranking official, how dare the spirits approach him? Perhaps they have grievances and are depressed? They appear without fear, merely because it is the time for the official's couple to come to an end."

    As for Mr. Chen Fusu from Jingjiang, who was a scholar, he had a tenant farmer tilling the land when the ox suddenly stumbled and could not be made to move. Upon inspection, it was found that the ox's hoof had sunk into the mud, up to the knee. When pulled out, a cache of pottery was discovered, consisting of twelve pieces in only yellow and white colors. The pottery was very coarse, similar in size to a small basin, shaped like a waist drum, with ceramic beads attached around the rim, as large as chicken heads, and linked together like drum nails. The tenant farmer accidentally knocked over several pieces, but the next day they were intact as before. Mr. Chen tested them and found this to be true, finding it very strange. He ordered the pottery to be buried again. Someone suggested, "If it can be dug out and remain intact, it must be a treasure." Mr. Chen ordered it to be dug up again, but it could not be found.

    585

    兰岩曰：既掘之而后葬之，先生究属何心？乃物已炫于人寰，卒隐而不可复得，岂预知其非人世应有之物，而故化去耶？

    　　梁　氏　女陕西白水县村民，其妻死，遗一子一女，皆六七岁，民复娶同村梁氏女为继室。梁少艾，民为所惑，于是日虐子女，击刺熨烙，体无完肤，民不能庇。民力食者，每戴星入市趁墟，梁早起炊饭。际夏月，窗牖不闭，觉窗外有人，凭窗向内而叹。梁仰视，见一妇人，蹙眉黄颡，满面流泪。梁惊悸发狂，自批其颊，邻人环救，梁大骂：“淫婢，奈何毒如蛇蝎，残我儿女？”众始悟为前妇之鬼所附。急灌以朱砂，愈时始定。遂自此病癫，往往自褪其衣，令儿女极力挞之，方以为快；或引锥自刺，遍身流血，尚不满意。一日，乃烧火箸，自烙其阴，深入八寸，大叫“快活”而死。白水令邱公理此案，尝为先君述之。

    Explanation

    585

    Lan Yan said: Having dug it up and then buried it again, what was the true intention of the master? The object had already been displayed in the world, yet it was finally hidden and could not be recovered again. How could they have known it was not something that should exist in the world, and intentionally transformed it away?

    A woman from the Liang family in Bai Shui County, Shaanxi Province, was a villager whose wife died, leaving behind one son and one daughter, both six or seven years old. The man remarried a woman from the same village as his second wife. She was young and beautiful, and the man was deceived by her. As a result, he abused his children daily, beating, stabbing, ironing, and branding them until they had no skin left intact. He could not protect them. The people who worked for him often went out very early to the market. The woman got up early to cook rice. During the summer month, the windows and doors were not closed, and she sensed someone outside the window. Leaning on the window and sighing inwardly, she looked up and saw a woman with a frowning expression and a yellow forehead, tears streaming down her face. The woman was shocked and went mad, slapping her own cheek. Neighbors gathered to help, and she shouted loudly, "Promiscuous maid, how can you be as poisonous as a snake or scorpion, harming my children?" Everyone realized that the woman was possessed by the ghost of her previous wife. They quickly gave her a dose of cinnabar, and she recovered after some time. She then became mentally ill, often taking off her own clothes and making her children beat her fiercely, finding it pleasurable; sometimes she would use a needle to stab herself, causing blood to flow all over her body, still not satisfied. One day, she burned a fire poker and burned her own private parts, penetrating eight inches deep, shouting loudly that it was enjoyable, and died. The magistrate of Bai Shui County, Mr. Qiu, handled this case and once told my father about it.

    586

    兰岩曰：荼毒子女，终罹惨报，天心岂或爽哉！

    　　铁　公　鸡济南某富翁，拥资数十万，性极悭吝。居积取赢，持筹会计，日不暇给，而敝衣破帽，向亲故作贫窭状。老小数十口，日市肉半斤、菜数斤，饭脱粟，皆取给于一灶，早餐恒午饭，晚餐恒夜食。不设茶酒，终年不宴客，虽骨肉至亲，未尝见其匕箸是何形状。翁亦不知款客作何周旋。然往往见招于人，歌筵舞席，颇极欢洽，又似毫不知生人之乐者。乡人号之为铁公鸡，谓一毛不拔也。

    Explanation

    586

    Lan Yan said: To torment one's own children, one will ultimately suffer a terrible retribution; could the will of heaven possibly be mistaken?

    The Iron Rooster, a certain wealthy man from Jinan, possessed assets worth tens of thousands, and had an extremely miserly nature. He hoarded goods to make a profit, manipulated counting rods for accounting, and was too busy to handle everything, yet he appeared very poor in front of relatives and acquaintances. His family of several dozen people, including both adults and children, bought half a pound of meat and several pounds of vegetables daily, ate coarse millet, and all got their food from one stove. They had breakfast for lunch and dinner for supper. He did not serve tea or alcohol, never hosted banquets throughout the year, and even close family members never saw what his chopsticks looked like. The old man also didn't know how to entertain guests. Yet he was often invited by others to banquets with singing and dancing, which were very enjoyable, and he seemed unaware of the joy of socializing. The villagers called him an iron rooster, meaning he wouldn't part with even a single hair.

    587

    近五旬无子，议纳妾，价欲极廉，而人欲至美。媒笑曰：“翁所谓又要马儿好，又要马儿不吃草也。是当求之于牡牝骊黄之外，讵可骤得？”翁嘱其速觅。居无何，有陕西客携一女来，不索值，但取衣食，不致冻馁以死足矣。女年十八，丽如舜华，翁喜惬过望，留为侧室，赠客钱一缗，不争而去。翁得女，嬖幸殊甚，曲意悦之，而鄙吝犹昔。女戒之曰：“昔乌氏倮鄙人牧长，寡妇清穷乡嫠妇，而名显天下，礼伉王侯，徒以富之一事耳？君之富堪敌国矣，不特不能知名当时，且将泯焉漠焉，几不得与中人伍，窃为君羞之苦之。”翁讶曰：“尔胡为出此言？独虑不造次间有人属耳耶？且尔言过矣，钱之为物，难聚而易散。我自龆龀时节，多市扑满。日积数钱，积十二年，共得二百二十余扑满，扑而计之，得钱三十余千。

    Explanation

    587

    A man nearing fifty years old without a son, he began to discuss taking a concubine, desiring the price to be extremely low while the person to be extremely beautiful. The matchmaker laughed and said, "The old man wants both the horse to be good and the horse to eat no grass. Such a match should be sought outside the usual standards, and how can it be obtained suddenly?" The old man instructed him to find one quickly. Not long after, an innkeeper from Shaanxi brought a young woman, asking for no payment, only seeking food and clothing, as long as she did not starve or freeze to death. The girl was eighteen years old, beautiful like the flower of Shun, and the old man was very pleased beyond his expectations, keeping her as a concubine and giving the innkeeper one string of cash, leaving without argument. After obtaining the girl, the old man greatly favored and delighted her, pleasing her in every way, yet still remained stingy and mean. The girl advised him, saying, "In the past, Wu Shi Luo, a common herdsman, and the poor widow Qing, a widow from a remote village, both gained fame and were respected by nobles, simply because of their wealth? Your wealth is enough to rival a nation, yet you cannot even gain recognition in your time, and will likely be forgotten, hardly able to compare with the average person. I feel ashamed for you." The old man was surprised and said, "Why do you say such a thing? Are you worried that someone might overhear in a moment of haste? And your words are wrong. Money is difficult to gather but easy to disperse. Since my childhood, I often bought piggy banks. Daily saving a few coins, over twelve years, I accumulated more than two hundred piggy banks, and when I broke them open, I counted over thirty thousand coins."

    588
