    贯之以索，贷之于人，权其子母，又三十年，计之，甫能盈兆。中间又设赌局，如一切呼卢、压宝、樗蒱及琐琐罗丹拍格诸戏，取其头，迄今又十余年矣。凡经营五十余年，仅有今日，则积财之辛苦，予备尝之矣。平生所见所闻，诸晋绅世家，或竭资营第宅，或倾囊助亲友，更有老悖不念子孙者，辄以白似雪、圆如月之宝物，沽酒市肉，日与宾客欢宴，一似与银钱二物有深仇大恨者，必欲尽力消耗之而已。予每以之自惩，犹恐久而不逮，尔乃欲我蹈此窠臼，其未知物力艰难，故漫作是语耶？小儿女福大几许，而自捐折如是，幸勿更举是念，罪过不小！”女笑曰：“聊以相试，何遽惊讶？儿岂不知君之志，牢不可破，将厚积余藏，欲以遗所不知何人者哉？”然翁自闻此说，终不能释，虽爱之如珍错，而防之如盗贼矣。

    Explanation

    588

    Tying it with a rope, borrowing it from others, calculating the principal and interest, and doing so for another thirty years, just barely able to reach a million. In between, setting up gambling games, such as all sorts of dice games, betting games, and other small-scale gambling games, taking the top prize, and doing so for another ten years. All in all, managing for over fifty years, and just having what I have today, then the hardships of accumulating wealth, I have experienced it all. Throughout my life, seeing and hearing about the wealthy and influential families, some exhausting their resources to build mansions, some emptying their purses to help friends and relatives, and even those old fools who don't think of their descendants, using valuable items as white as snow and as round as the moon, buying wine and meat, daily feasting with guests, as if they have a deep-seated grudge or hatred against silver and money, doing their best to spend it all. I always use this as a warning to myself, still fearing that over time I might not keep up, and now you want me to follow this path, not knowing the difficulty of accumulating wealth, so you casually say this? How much good fortune do my children have, and yet they waste it like this, please don't even consider this again, it's a great sin!" The daughter laughed and said, "Just to test you, why are you so surprised? Do I not know your resolve, which is as firm as a rock, planning to accumulate wealth for the future, to leave it for someone unknown?" However, the old man, after hearing this, still couldn't let go, loving it as if it were precious treasure, yet guarding it as if it were a thief.

    589

    其密室中，旧有贮银铁柜十数，封志甚固，例一月一开检视。居无何，又值检视之期，婢媪僮仆尽摽诸大门之外，独与女闭户下窗，柜既发，则藏镪尽空。大惊，如失左右手，瞠目视女，诘其故。女笑而不答，翁大怒，即抽刀逼之，女笑曰：“君以儿为人乎？”翁怒曰：“尔非人，鬼耶？”女曰：“亦非鬼，实狐也。以尔鄙陋，故盗而之他人耳。”翁大怒曰：“平生血资，盗归何所？”女曰：“流通物也，盗去，何处不足以济人；岂必深藏固守于一老秃翁之手乎？”言讫，径入内室，觅之杳无踪迹。翁始信果为狐祟，大恸而绝。家人草草殡殓，所遗财物，劫夺一空，其宅亦随废为蔬圃云。

    Explanation

    589

    In his secret room, there were several iron chests used for storing silver, which had been tightly sealed and secured. It was customary to open and inspect them once a month. Soon after, it was time for another inspection. The servants, both male and female, were sent outside the main gate, leaving the father alone with his daughter, who closed the door and lowered the window. When the chests were opened, they found that the hidden silver had vanished entirely. The father was greatly astonished, as if he had lost his hands, and stared at his daughter, asking her why. She laughed but did not answer. The father became very angry and immediately drew a knife, threatening her. She laughed and said, "Do you consider me human?" The father shouted, "Are you not human? Are you a ghost?" She replied, "I am not a ghost, but a fox spirit. Because you are coarse and vulgar, I stole it and gave it to someone else." The father, now even more furious, asked, "Where did you steal my lifetime's hard-earned wealth?" She responded, "It is a circulating thing. If stolen, where would it not be sufficient to help people? Why must it be kept hidden and guarded by an old bald man?" After saying this, she directly entered the inner room, and upon searching, she disappeared without a trace. The father finally believed it was truly a fox spirit and died of grief. His family hastily buried him, and all his remaining property was looted. His house was subsequently converted into a vegetable garden.

    590

    先是，翁宅后有楼七楹，为狐所据，已近百年。其祖父相延，于每月初二、十六日，具鸡子白酒祝而祀之，罔敢驰懈。及翁承家后，以多费罢之，又以楼房出租于人。狐遂大扰，妖异迭兴。其妻力劝，翁愤恨出入谩骂。一日，见群狐来辞曰：“翁全福之人，吾辈何能为，请徙去不敢复居此矣。”遂不再至。翁以为得计，初不意为其所愚弄至此。

    　　兰岩曰：守钱虏深可憎恶，安得如此快狐，行此快事哉！辛苦五十年，未得一文享用，一旦尽空，大恸而绝，翁亦可怜矣。每读一过，令人叫快者三。

    　　多　前　锋前锋多某，行二。未得前锋时，与所亲同往东直门外城门下，习骑射，坠马昏绝。所亲扶掖以归，归家即苏，一无所损，但神痴不复解言笑，与食则食，不与亦不食也；与饮则饮，不与亦不饮也。越半月弗瘥，家人莫不闷闷。

    Explanation

    590

    Previously, there was a seven-story building behind the old man's house that had been occupied by a fox for nearly a century. The old man's ancestors continued the tradition, offering eggs and rice wine on the second and sixteenth days of each month, and they dared not slack off or be negligent. After the old man took over the household, he abolished the ritual due to excessive costs and rented out the building to others. The foxes then caused great disturbances, and strange phenomena occurred repeatedly. The old man's wife strongly advised him, but he became furious and went out to curse them. One day, he saw a group of foxes come to bid farewell, saying, "You are a fully fortunate person, what can we do? We will move away and will not return here again." They did not come back after that. The old man thought he had won, but he did not expect to be deceived by them to this extent.

    Lanyan said: The miser is deeply detestable. How could there be such clever foxes to do such amusing things! After struggling for fifty years, he did not get to enjoy a single coin, and one day all was emptied. He cried out in great sorrow and died. The old man is also pitiable. Each time I read this, it makes me shout with joy three times.

    Many Frontlines, also known as "前锋" with the prefix "多," was second in line. Before becoming a front-line soldier, he went with a close friend to practice riding and shooting outside the Dongzhimen (East Direct Gate) of the city. He fell off the horse and fainted. His friend supported and helped him home, and he woke up upon returning home, suffering no damage. However, he became mentally confused and no longer understood speech or laughter. He ate when given food and did not eat when not given food. He drank when given drink and did not drink when not given drink. After fifteen days, he did not recover, and all family members were depressed.

    591

    会有服役老妪，出外市菜归，忽瞠目视。其主母问之，不答，良久，乃大言曰：“半月前，汝家多二爷，因坠马不能行动，汝等则弃之去，令多二爷踽踽城下，盼望家中人，两眼欲穿，屡次浼我寄信，未得其便，今日始得到此，可即令人去接，幸勿更缓。”家人闻之，大骇，同声唯诺。或问：“我家二爷，今在城下乎？”曰：“现在东直门外角楼下。”曰：“然则子为谁也？”曰：“我旧营房南门口开小铺之王老四也。缘去年与掌柜者算帐不平，呕气自缢死，冥中怜我冤，命协同溺死鬼那三，管理角楼下城湾河沿一带地方。前生亦曾蒙多二爷下交者。”家人闻之，愈错愕，应之曰：“知之矣，深劳尊驾，第请回，即刻使人去接也。”遂取冥镪焚之。老妪踣地，逾时方醒，叩之，悉不自知耳。

    Explanation

    591

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, an old woman who served as a laborer went out to buy vegetables and returned. Suddenly, she stared blankly. Her mistress asked her, but she did not reply. After a long time, she then loudly said, "Half a month ago, your family had another young master who fell off a horse and could not move. You all abandoned him and left, leaving the other young master alone and wandering below the city wall. He yearned for family members, his eyes nearly bursting from longing. He repeatedly asked me to send a message, but I did not find an opportunity. Today I finally arrived here. You should immediately send someone to fetch him, please do not delay further." The family heard this and were greatly alarmed, all responding affirmatively. Someone asked, "Is my young master now below the city wall?" She replied, "He is now at the corner of the city wall outside the East Gate." Another asked, "Then who are you?" She said, "I am Wang Laosi, who used to run a small shop at the south gate of the old barracks. Last year, due to an argument over accounts with the shopkeeper, I hanged myself out of frustration. In the afterlife, my suffering was pitied, and I was ordered to work together with the drowned ghost Na San to manage the area around the corner of the city wall, the river bend, and the riverbank. In a past life, I also had the honor of befriending the other young master." The family heard this and were even more bewildered, replying, "We understand, deeply grateful for your trouble, please return, and we will send someone immediately." They then took some paper money and burned it. The old woman fell to the ground and only woke up after a while. When questioned, she had no memory of what had happened.

    592

    家人不敢视为荒诞也，群扶多至坠马处，呼其名而招之，往返三四。多忽发一寒噤，即时清白，向家人涕泣而道之曰：“汝辈一何忍心弃我于此，半月之久，不来一顾。苟非王二哥寄信，再十余日，我不复见汝辈矣！”家人环而谢之，无不先悲而后喜。多有少弟，亦童心而选事者，乘间访王老四及那三事，果不诬也。多今年已三十，为前锋且十年矣。每逢令节，必具香楮鸡酒于城湾，呼王二哥、那三哥而祭之，谓报其施，期于终身不哀云。

    Explanation

    592

    The family dared not regard it as absurd, and they all helped Du to the place where he had fallen off the horse, calling his name to summon him. They went back and forth several times. Suddenly, Du shivered, and instantly he became lucid. He wept and told the family, saying, "How could you be so heartless as to abandon me here? For half a month, not coming to visit once. If it were not for 'Wang Er' sending a letter, in another ten days, I would no longer see you." The family surrounded him and thanked him, none being first sad and then happy. Du has a younger brother who also has a childlike heart and chooses things. Taking advantage of the opportunity, he visited 'Wang Laosi' and 'Na San' to verify the story, and indeed it was true. Du is now thirty years old and has been a vanguard for ten years already. Every time on a festival day, he always prepared incense, paper money, chicken, and wine at the city bend, calling 'Wang Er' and 'Na San' to worship them, to repay their kindness, with the hope of not mourning for life.

    593

    兰岩曰：受恩必报，不欺于鬼，多亦厚德人也。

    　　骷髅某甲好打生。一日，归自朝阳门外吕祖阁，时已曛暮，见土城下一草屋中（土城，元时旧城），灯火荧荧，一扉半掩，探身窥之。见美妇人独坐炕头，笑容可掬，以手相招。甲喜而入，甫跨一足，即仆。次日为人救活，则一足陷古冢矣。问之，泣曰：“初以为奇遇，才入门，即见骷髅也。”

    　　兰岩曰：世间纷纷，尽肉骷髅也，前人曾言之矣。然非心动，必不为所诱。噫！天下奇遇，尽属骷髅耳。甲当从此悟道，涕泣何为？

    　　姚　植　之姚壮行，字植之，祖门名士，应聘入甘州提督李公幕府。府中园亭极胜，楼台池沼，广大幽深；绿树数百章，多百年物。往往有鬼物现形，日暮，人不敢过。相传康熙间，某为提督时，每杀人填园东夹壁中。迄今白骨髑髅，犹有存者。植之悉未之知。

    Explanation

    593

    Final Translation

    兰岩曰：受恩必报，不欺于鬼，多亦厚德人也。

    Lan Yan said: "One must repay kindness, not be deceived by ghosts; he is also a person of great virtue."

    骷髅某甲好打生。一日，归自朝阳门外吕祖阁，时已曛暮，见土城下一草屋中（土城，元时旧城），灯火荧荧，一扉半掩，探身窥之。见美妇人独坐炕头，笑容可掬，以手相招。甲喜而入，甫跨一足，即仆。次日为人救活，则一足陷古冢矣。问之，泣曰：“初以为奇遇，才入门，即见骷髅也。”

    A man named Skull Jia enjoyed hunting living beings. One day, returning from the Lüzu Pavilion outside the Chongyang Gate, as dusk was falling, he saw a small thatched cottage near the old city wall of the Yuan Dynasty. The lights inside were flickering, and one door was half-open. He leaned forward to peek inside. He saw a beautiful woman sitting alone on a kang, smiling broadly, and beckoning with her hand. Jia was delighted and entered, but as soon as he took one step inside, he fell. The next day, he was rescued and found that one of his feet was stuck in an ancient tomb. When asked about it, he wept and said, "At first, I thought it was a strange encounter, but as soon as I entered, I saw a skull."

    兰岩曰：世间纷纷，尽肉骷髅也，前人曾言之矣。然非心动，必不为所诱。噫！天下奇遇，尽属骷髅耳。甲当从此悟道，涕泣何为？

    Lan Yan said: "The world is full of people who are like walking corpses, driven by material desires, as previous people have said. However, if one's heart is not moved, one will not be lured. Alas! All strange encounters in the world ultimately lead to meeting a skull. Jia should awaken to this truth from this; why cry?"

    姚　植　之姚壮行，字植之，祖门名士，应聘入甘州提督李公幕府。府中园亭极胜，楼台池沼，广大幽深；绿树数百章，多百年物。往往有鬼物现形，日暮，人不敢过。相传康熙间，某为提督时，每杀人填园东夹壁中。迄今白骨髑髅，犹有存者。植之悉未之知。

    Yao Zhi, also known as Yao Zhuangxing, was a scholar from a distinguished family and was hired to join the staff of General Li in Ganzhou. The governor's garden was extremely beautiful, with grand pavilions, pools, and courtyards, vast and deep. There were hundreds of ancient trees, many over a hundred years old. Ghosts often appeared there, and at dusk, no one dared to pass by. It is said that during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, when someone was the governor, they would kill people and bury them in the eastern wall of the garden. To this day, white bones and skulls still exist there. Yao Zhi was completely unaware of this.

    594

    向夕，植之独步园中，使馆僮行沽，将赏秋月。主人李公兴亦豪，适携酒盒来觅，遂相与坐亭畔，羏湖山下。并邀同幕二友，共举觞政。漏三下，二友皆醉，呕吐狼藉，各舁归寝所，主人亦扶醉入内。

    　　姚量宏，仅半酣，兀立回廊，搔首看月，瞥见三人立池畔树荫中，姚问为谁，再三不应，移影向东去。姚疑为署中职役相戏，怒诃之。二人仍立不行，似嗔其以恶声相加者。姚欲就问之，乃绕出回廊，相去数武，二人倏不见。姚始悟其为鬼，连声呼童，而童不在侧。姚大惧，促步出园，惶遽中误走歧径。花深树密，秋草纵横，此际风鹤皆兵，一履脱落泥中，不遑拾取，　□袜而奔。蓦至一废轩，前有三人坐栏干上，姚急呼“救我！”三人不应，而起悲声。惊视之，二男一女，男无首，女浴血满身，皆裸身而坐。姚狂叫返走，颠踣无算，幸馆童提灯来觅，掖之归室。病忡梦悸，两月始瘥。

    Explanation

    594

    In the evening, Zhizhi walked alone in the garden, sending the servant to buy wine in preparation for enjoying the autumn moon. The host, Li Gongxing, was also in a jovial mood and just happened to carry a wine box and come looking for them. They sat together by the pavilion under the hills and lakes, and invited two friends from the same office, toasting and drinking together. By the third stroke of the night clock, the two friends were both drunk, vomiting messily, and each was carried back to their sleeping quarters, while the host also staggered drunk into the inner chamber.

    Yao was only half drunk, standing alone in the corridor, scratching his head and looking at the moon. He caught a glimpse of three people standing in the shade of a tree by the pond. Yao asked who they were, but they repeatedly did not respond, moving their shadows to the east. Yao suspected they were officials from the office playing a prank and scolded them angrily. The two men still stood there without moving, seeming to resent being addressed with harsh words. Yao wanted to go and ask them, so he circled around the corridor, and they were several steps away when the two men suddenly disappeared. Yao finally realized they were ghosts and called out for the servant repeatedly, but the servant was not nearby. Yao became greatly afraid and hurried out of the garden, in a state of panic, mistakenly taking a wrong path. Deep in flowers and dense in trees, with autumn grasses sprawling everywhere, even the wind and cranes seemed like soldiers. One shoe fell off into the mud, and he had no time to pick it up, running barefoot. Suddenly, he came to an abandoned pavilion, and in front were three people sitting on the railing. Yao shouted desperately, "Save me!" The three people did not respond but raised a mournful cry. Looking in shock, he saw two men and one woman, the men with no heads, the woman covered in blood, all sitting naked. Yao shouted wildly and ran back, stumbling and falling countless times. Fortunately, the servant came with a lantern to look for him and helped him back to his room. He suffered from illness and nightmares, taking two months to recover.

    595

    兰岩曰：断首残躯，其形何惨；想黑暗地狱，不知几许矣！世之掌兵权者，幸勿草菅人命，徒嗜杀戮也。

    　　新安富人新安有富人某，为葛商于江西。性贪淫残忍，力结官府，人多畏之。其在洪都时，尝同数客游松门，见一浣衣濑女，婉妙殊绝，命僮仆捉入密林深境处，欲污之，女滚地哭骂，抵死不从。富人意兴索然，将纵之去矣，而客有附庸为虐刘姓者，教其缚女手足，裸而仰绷于石上，主客童仆递淫之。自午至晡，更番一十六人，女不能任，竟死林下。遂委之而去。女家得尸讼官，严捕凶徒不获，事亦寝。

    Explanation

    595

    Lan Yan said: Severed heads and mutilated bodies, how pitiful their forms; imagine the dark hell, who knows how many! Those in power who control military forces, please do not treat human lives as grass, merely enjoying slaughter.

    In Xianan, there was a wealthy man who engaged in trade in Jiangxi. He was greedy, lustful, and cruel, strongly connected to the government, and feared by many. While he was in Hongdu, he once traveled with several guests to Songmen and saw a woman washing clothes at a stream. She was beautiful and extraordinary, so he ordered his servants to catch her and take her to a secluded forest, intending to defile her. The woman rolled on the ground, crying and cursing, resisting to the death. The rich man lost all interest and was about to let her go, but one of his followers, a cruel man named Liu, taught him to bind her hands and feet, naked and tied to a stone. The master, guests, and servants took turns raping her. From noon to dusk, they took turns sixteen times. The woman could not bear it and died in the forest. They left her there and went away. Her family found her body and reported to the authorities, who strictly searched for the criminals but failed to capture them. The case was dropped.

    596

    富人家有一子，为太学生；一女年十八，尚未字人。新安风俗勤俭，虽富家眷属，不废操作。值采茶时节，结诸女伴入山，暴雨骤至，各觅歇处。富人女独立于岩下，徘徊间，闻有唤其小名者，张皇四顾，而声在石内。女大惊，痴立，石曰：“汝勿惧，我山神也。汝父在客中恣横，淫死人女，女控诸阴司，阴谴甚重，将报之于汝身。大士以汝母日诵经咒，绣佛长斋，发大慈悲，令解汝难。汝父作恶不悛，惨祸即将至矣，汝其速归，勿集于此，此非善地也。”女怯惧泣拜，踉跄冒雨而走，山径滑溜，起跌数四，始见诸女伴聚集山亭下，群讶曰：“许时在何处？令人悬拟。”女绐以失路。言次有四五恶少踵至，咸指女笑曰：“不在岩下，何故狂奔至此？”饱眼而去，女始悟岩下非善地之说，微神告，几遭强暴，阴诵佛号不绝。

    Explanation

    596

    A wealthy family had a son who was a student at a prestigious academy, and a daughter who was eighteen years old and had not yet been married. The customs of the Xin'an region were frugal and industrious, and even the wealthy household did not neglect manual labor. During the tea-picking season, the daughter gathered with her female companions to go into the mountains. A sudden heavy rainstorm came, and everyone sought shelter. The wealthy family's daughter stood alone under a rock, hesitating, when she heard someone calling her pet name. She looked around in panic, and the voice seemed to come from within the rock. The girl was greatly startled and stood there in a daze. The rock spoke: "Do not be afraid, I am a mountain deity. Your father, when away, is arrogant and domineering, and he has seduced and killed a woman. The woman has complained to the underworld, and the punishment from the underworld is severe, and it will be inflicted upon you. The Bodhisattva, because your mother daily recites sutras and mantras, and embroiders Buddha, and observes a vegetarian diet, shows great compassion and allows you to escape from your troubles. Your father continues to do evil without repentance, and a terrible disaster is about to come. You should return quickly, do not gather here, this is not a good place." The girl, terrified, cried and bowed, then stumbled and ran in the rain. The mountain path was slippery, and she fell several times. Finally, she saw her companions gathered under a mountain pavilion. They were surprised and asked, "Where were you for so long? It made us anxious." The girl lied and said she had gotten lost. As she was speaking, four or five ruffians arrived and all pointed at her and laughed, saying, "If you weren't under the rock, why were you running so wildly here?" They looked their fill and left. The girl then realized the truth of the mountain deity's warning, thanks to the deity's warning, she had nearly suffered violence, and continued to silently recite Buddhist mantras.

    597

    既归，以白其母，母叹且泣曰：“以汝父素行，又何事不屑为，神佛岂欺人哉？”嗣此戒律愈严，女亦信心奉佛焉。

    　　其子年虽少，颇有父风，乡人称其克肖。屡梗母教，母甚忧之。一日，有亲戚归自京师者，其子往候之，话及京师人物众矣，究竟何等人为最乐？亲戚曰：“乐者甚伙，要皆高不可下耳。尔我今生断不能及。唯一等人，极可歆羡，盖太监也。”子曰：“刑余之人，有何可乐？”亲戚曰：“汝但知其人道已绝，必乏乐趣，而不知其可乐之处甚多，试为子偻指注之。夫王公至贵者也，然望天子之居，不翅天上；彼以阉故，得出入不禁，一乐也。

    Explanation

    597

    After returning, he informed his mother, who sighed and wept, saying, "Considering your father's usual behavior, what kind of thing would he disdain to do? Could the gods and Buddhas deceive people?" From then on, the rules became even stricter, and the daughter also sincerely devoted herself to Buddhism.

    His son, though young, had much of his father's style, and people in the village praised him for being very like his father. He often resisted his mother's teachings, causing her great concern. One day, a relative returned from the capital, and the son went to visit him. They talked about the many people in the capital, and the son asked, "Ultimately, who is the happiest?" The relative replied, "There are many who are happy, but they are all so high that they cannot be compared to us. You and I in this life can never match them. There is one type of person, extremely enviable—they are eunuchs." The son said, "People who have been castrated, what can they be happy about?" The relative said, "You only know that their human nature is gone, so they must lack pleasure, but you don't know that there are many things they find enjoyable. Let me list them for you. Nobles and high-ranking officials are the most prestigious, but when they look at the emperor's residence, it seems no different from heaven. Because of their eunuch status, they can come and go freely, which is one joy."

    598

    不耕不织，而一生吃着不尽，二乐也。父母不敢以为子，兄弟姊妹尊而奉之，三乐也。靡不素封，人不见之物，彼能见之；人不得食之物，彼得食之，四乐也。无妻子之累，有福独享，不必为后人计，五乐也。有此五乐，何乐如之？”其子倾听，神为之移，问：“吾辈亦可作太监否？”曰：“谁不可为，但多此胯下一物耳。”一笑而罢。其子归，一路冥想，决意自宫，尚恐见阻于其母，潜袖剔刀入厕，自割其势，大叫晕绝，家人觉而救之，已殒矣。

    Explanation

    598

    A Life of Ease and Pleasure

    Not farming or weaving, yet enjoying a lifetime of abundance, this is the second pleasure. Parents dare not regard him as their son, and brothers and sisters respect and serve him, this is the third pleasure. Nothing is lacking in his wealth, things unseen by others, he can see; things others cannot eat, he can eat, this is the fourth pleasure. Without the burden of a wife and children, enjoying blessings alone, not needing to consider future generations, this is the fifth pleasure. With these five pleasures, what could be more enjoyable than this?” His son listened intently, his spirit moved by the words, and asked, “Can we also become eunuchs?” He replied, “Who cannot do it, but just remove this extra part.” They laughed and ended the conversation. His son returned home, deep in contemplation, and decided to castrate himself. Still fearing opposition from his mother, he secretly carried a knife into the toilet, self-castrated, cried out and fainted, and when family members discovered and tried to save him, he had already died.

    599

    无何，富人归省，其妻以女之所以生，并子之所以死，悉告之，意在讽谏，富人伸首向天，呵呵作怒笑声曰：“妇人女子，畏信鬼神，古人或遭腐刑，或置面首三十，岂皆宜报与其祖、父耶？总地狱之说，荒唐耳。如果有之，吾将向冥王乞请，必遍历所谓刀山剑树者，以广见闻，又何惧之有！”其妻哂曰：“虽十八层地狱，尽当奉屈一游，所虑流连忘返，不得再见天日，为妻子忧耳。”富人怒而大闹，遂析宅另居，不复结谈。仅月余，即为二竖所困，日见前所淫濑女立榻前，或与青衣数人杂坐于室，若有所俟。凡数夕，女又领两青衣械一人至，囚首垢面，向富人泣诉曰：“松门事发矣。”视之，即前日附庸为虐之刘姓客也。富人亦惨凄不胜，呼其妻女至前，哭告所见，并详述前事，乞为忏悔。言未终，忽声喘如牛，大叫：“我去！我去！”而死。

    Explanation

    599

    Soon after, the wealthy man returned home for a visit. His wife informed him of the circumstances surrounding their daughter's birth and their son's death, intending to advise him indirectly. The wealthy man, raising his head to the sky, laughed in a mix of anger and amusement, saying, "Women, out of fear and belief in ghosts and spirits, may be subject to cruel punishments like castration or keeping thirty male concubines. Are all these things meant to be passed down to our ancestors and fathers? The idea of hell is just absurd. If there is such a place, I will ask the king of the underworld to let me experience the so-called mountains of knives and trees of swords, to broaden my knowledge and experience. What have I to fear!" His wife replied with a smile, "Even if there are eighteen levels of hell, I will accompany you on a tour of all of them. The concern is that you might become so engrossed that you forget to return, and we will be left without seeing the light of day again, worrying for you." The wealthy man became angry and caused a commotion, leading to the separation of their household. They no longer talked to each other. Only a month later, he fell ill, and every day he saw the woman who had previously seduced him standing by his bed, or sitting with several attendants in the room, as if waiting for something. Over several nights, the woman led two attendants to bring a man who was bound and dirty, weeping and telling the wealthy man, "The scandal at the pine gate has been exposed." Looking at him, it was the Liu family guest who had been cruel the day before. The wealthy man was also filled with sorrow and grief, calling his wife and daughter to him, crying and recounting what he had seen, detailing the previous events, and begging for forgiveness. Before he finished speaking, he suddenly began to breathe heavily like a bull, shouting, "I'm going! I'm going!" and died.

    600

    妻女悲其罪孽之深且重也，同向佛为诵经，以求超度。女终身不嫁，奉母终焉。后有人自江西来，传言刘客于某月日自残死矣。计之，正当富人死之前一日也。祁门尹吴金泉尝述以勉人。予及诸外弟，皆熟闻之。

    Explanation

    600

    The wife and daughter, sorrowing over the depth and gravity of their sins, prayed to the Buddha by reciting scriptures, seeking deliverance. The daughter remained unmarried for life, serving her mother until her death. Later, a messenger came from Jiangxi, reporting that Liu had committed suicide on a certain day. Upon calculation, it was the day before the rich man's death. The magistrate of Qimen, Wu Jinquan, often recounted this story to encourage people. I and my brothers-in-law are all well-acquainted with it.

    601

    兰岩曰：为恶不悛，终遭显报，冥冥中岂或爽哉！

    　　维　扬　生江都某诸生之宿迁，同二友谒西楚霸王庙。因话及巨鹿之战，及垓下之败，感叹移晷。生独以为不然，曰：“千古无才无识，庸而且碌者，项王一人而已。昔虬髯客志在天下，一旦见文皇，自惭不逮，甘心逊避，远帝扶余；吴越王负盖世之雄，奄有东南，而观衅中原，终守臣节。此二人者，非不欲创业垂统，为一朝烈祖，施后世而传无穷也；特度德量力，见机而起者，亦见机而止，故不愧为豪杰，不失为英雄。岂若项王矜扛鼎之雄，逞拔山之力，以沛公之豁达大度，不识其为真人；以张良韩信之才，不识为国士。亚父以反间死，韩生以直谏烹。徒具盖世之资，虚负重瞳之表，乃太史公犹列入本纪，江淮人祀以崇祠，此天下大不平事，而诸君尚津津然置诸齿颊，且有景行之慕，独不虑贻识者笑乎？”

    Explanation

    601

    兰岩曰：为恶不悛，终遭显报，冥冥中岂或爽哉！

    In the unseen realm, how could there be any deviation? Those who persist in doing evil without repentance will ultimately suffer a clear and severe retribution.

    维　扬　生江都某诸生之宿迁，同二友谒西楚霸王庙。因话及巨鹿之战，及垓下之败，感叹移晷。生独以为不然，曰：“千古无才无识，庸而且碌者，项王一人而已。昔虬髯客志在天下，一旦见文皇，自惭不逮，甘心逊避，远帝扶余；吴越王负盖世之雄，奄有东南，而观衅中原，终守臣节。此二人者，非不欲创业垂统，为一朝烈祖，施后世而传无穷也；特度德量力，见机而起者，亦见机而止，故不愧为豪杰，不失为英雄。岂若项王矜扛鼎之雄，逞拔山之力，以沛公之豁达大度，不识其为真人；以张良韩信之才，不识为国士。亚父以反间死，韩生以直谏烹。徒具盖世之资，虚负重瞳之表，乃太史公犹列入本纪，江淮人祀以崇祠，此天下大不平事，而诸君尚津津然置诸齿颊，且有景行之慕，独不虑贻识者笑乎？”

    A student from Jiangdu, residing in Suqian, along with two friends, visited the temple of the Overlord of Western Chu. They discussed the Battle of Julu and the defeat at Gaixia, expressing their feelings for a long time. The student alone believed otherwise, saying: "Throughout history, there has been only one person who was mediocre and lazy, lacking both talent and insight—Xiang Yu alone. In the past, the Dragon Beard Guest aspired to rule the world, but once he saw Emperor Taizong, he felt ashamed of his own inadequacies, willingly yielded, and retreated to the remote kingdom of Buyeo; King Qian Liu of Wuyue, with his extraordinary strength, dominated the southeast region, yet he looked for opportunities in the central plains and ultimately maintained the loyalty of a subject. These two men were not unwilling to establish a legacy for future generations, to be revered as founding ancestors of a dynasty, to benefit posterity and leave an enduring legacy. Instead, they assessed their virtue and strength, acted when the opportunity arose, and knew when to stop, so they did not disgrace themselves as heroes and did not lose their status as heroes. How could it be like Xiang Yu, who boasted of his strength in lifting a cauldron and his legendary power, failing to recognize Liu Bang's broad-mindedness and generosity as a true ruler; failing to recognize Zhang Liang and Han Xin's talents as statesmen. Fan Zeng was killed by a counterintelligence plot, and Han Xin was boiled alive for his frank advice. He only possessed extraordinary talents, but falsely boasted of having two pupils in each eye, a sign of great wisdom. Yet, Sima Qian still included him in his annals, and people in the Jianghuai region worship him in grand temples. This is a great injustice in the world, and yet you all still speak of him with admiration and even have a desire to emulate him. Do you not fear that this will bring ridicule from those with discernment?"

    602

    二友曰：“不然。项王以暴，人故小之，要亦劫数使然。究其人亦有足多者，如烧秦宫室，毅然不袭秦弊，封六国后，义也；会鸿门，释沛公，信也；七十余战，未尝败北，勇也；不杀太公，仁也，恕也；一败涂地，不忍复生，果也。君书生之见，妄诋英雄，毋乃不自量乎？”

    　　生艴然曰：“君辈不足论古人。我与我周旋久，自为酬酢可也。”因呼僮索笔题句壁上，曰：“炎刘受命顺皇天，天使重瞳作獭鹯.千古中原群盗贼，让君马首一鞭先。”题毕，掷笔大笑。二友默然，遂分路而去。是夜，生梦中为人缚至一广殿下，见项王按剑而坐，盛怒叱之，声如巨雷，栋宇震撼。生震慴仆阶下，伤折一股。王命拔舌，即有数壮士同声而应，蜂拥至前，一人抠抉其舌，极力拔之，生大叫而寤。舌遂卷曲，不复能作了然语，右股亦病瘫痪，终生不瘥云。

    Explanation

    602

    The two friends said, "Not so. Xiang Yu was tyrannical, and people naturally belittled him, but it was also due to fate. Upon closer examination, there are many admirable qualities about him. For example, he burned the Qin palaces, firmly refusing to follow the corrupt practices of the Qin, and bestowed titles on the descendants of the Six States, which was righteous; he met at Hongmen and released Xiang Yu, which was trustworthy; he fought over seventy battles without ever retreating, which was brave; he did not kill Tai Gong, which was both benevolent and forgiving; after one defeat, unable to bear living on, which was resolute. Your narrow-minded view of a scholar, falsely criticizing heroes, is it not a sign of overestimating yourself?"

    The scholar angrily said, "You are not qualified to discuss ancient people. I have been dealing with myself for a long time, and can answer for myself." He then called for a servant to fetch a pen and wrote a verse on the wall, saying, "The Han dynasty received its mandate from Heaven, and the gods sent the double-eyed Xiang Yu to act as a hawk. Among the bandits of Central China for a thousand years, let you lead the way with your horse's head." After finishing, he threw down his pen and laughed. The two friends remained silent and left by different paths. That night, the scholar had a dream in which he was bound by someone and brought to a grand hall. He saw Xiang Yu sitting with a sword drawn, roaring in great anger, his voice like a thunderclap, shaking the beams and pillars. The scholar was so terrified that he fell to the ground, injuring one of his legs. Xiang Yu ordered his tongue to be pulled out, and several strong men responded in unison, rushing forward. One of them pulled out his tongue with great force, and the scholar screamed and woke up. His tongue curled up, and he could no longer speak clearly, and his right leg also became paralyzed, never recovering.

    603

    兰岩曰：项王事已隔几千年矣，何来狂生，畅一时无稽，致终生残废，悔何及哉！甚矣，人不可不慎言也！

    　　市　煤　人癸巳仲夏，过访宗室双丰将军，立谈廊下。见一人裸身荷担，入庖厨供煤炭者，胸前背后各有伤痕，长咫尺，阔寸余，怪而询诸将军。将军曰：“此奇闻也，会须细谈。”乃煮酒设馔，为予详述之。因言其人王姓，雄县人，市煤十余年矣。方其少年时，村居贫甚，肩挑以食力，逐日担瓜茄之属赴菜市。而所居去市遥远，鸡鸣而起，犹恐后人，例于五更辄往趁墟。一日，行至半途，遇迅雷洪雨，行不能前。

    Explanation

    603

    Lan Yan said: The matter of Xiang Wang has been separated by several thousand years. Where does this reckless youth come from, who enjoys a moment of baseless talk, causing lifelong disability, and how can one regret it now? Indeed, one cannot be careful enough with words!

    In the second month of the lunar calendar in the year of the Water Snake, I visited General Shuangfeng from the imperial family and stood talking under the veranda. I saw a man carrying a heavy load, naked, entering the kitchen to supply coal. He had scars on his chest and back, each about a foot long and an inch wide. I found this strange and asked the general about it. The general said, "This is a strange story, and we must discuss it in detail." So, we brewed wine and prepared a meal, and he told me the story in detail. He said that the man was surnamed Wang, from Xiong County, and had been selling coal for over ten years. When he was young, he lived in a village and was very poor, supporting himself by carrying heavy loads. Every day, he would carry vegetables to the market. His home was far from the market, and he would rise at the crow of the rooster, still fearing to be late, and would set out at the fifth watch to take advantage of the early market. One day, on his way to the market, he encountered a sudden heavy rain and thunderstorm, and could not proceed.

    604

    于电光中，见路旁矮屋数椽，葭蓠绕之。王入篱窥户，则门环系以麻索，虚无人焉。王解索启扉，息肩其内，复闭门，蹲踞炕头。一食顷，忽闻橐橐之声，窃讶之。久之声渐繁，于烨烨电光中见一人绕地而踊。王大骇惧，屏气不敢移动，惟瞠目直视。瞬息间，其人倏至面前，遂能辨其面目：被发蹙眉，吐舌唇外，长数寸。王骇极，手足失措，正张皇，其舌忽触于额。王狂叫惊走，奋力扑窗，纵身而出，昏然仆地。黎明后，始为行人救苏。备详其事，众咸集错愕。

    Explanation

    604

    In the lightning, he saw a low, thatched hut by the roadside, surrounded by reeds and rushes. The king entered the fence and peered into the house, where the door ring was tied with hemp rope, and there was no one inside. He untied the rope and opened the door, resting his burden inside, then closed the door again and sat cross-legged on the kang (heating platform). After the time it takes to eat a meal, he suddenly heard a tapping sound and wondered at it. After a while, the sound gradually became more frequent, and in the bright lightning, he saw a person leaping around the ground. The king was greatly terrified, held his breath and dared not move, only staring straight ahead. In an instant, the person suddenly appeared before him, and he could then distinguish his face: disheveled hair, furrowed brows, with his tongue protruding from his lips, several inches long. The king was so terrified that he was at a loss, just as he was panic-stricken, when the tongue suddenly touched his forehead. He screamed and ran in terror, struggled to reach the window, leaped out, and fainted and fell to the ground. After dawn, he was rescued and revived by passersby. He recounted the details of the event, and everyone gathered in astonishment.

    605

    既而村邻渐至，共云前一日有妇人缢死梁间，已报官，尚未检验，不意竟作怪如此。同入视尸，已僵卧炕下矣。王惊定思痛，觉胸前背后似刀割不可忍，解衣视之，皮肉狼藉，众共测其故，乃悟突出时，因撞折窗棂，是以上下两受其伤也，不割腹拖肠，亦云幸矣。迄今逾二十年，将终其身患疤痕焉。予初闻，甚异之，既而相与捧腹。

    　　兰岩曰：负气自缢，又作怪异，诚不可解。岂不得其死者，果皆为厉哉？王不幸遭此惊痛耳。

    　　鼠狼某佐领好酒喜啖。一夕夜归，市羊蹄六七枚，火酒一瓶，拥炉独酌，弃蹄骨于地。蓦闻墙角下窸窣有声，挑灯谛视，见小人十余，各高五六寸，或男或女，装束悉类时人；皆背一竹筐弯腰拾取蹄骨，置筐中，移时而尽。某心悸，取火箸掷而击之，一人仆，余惊走，悉入壁洞，仆者滚地唧唧，随化为鼠狼而逝。

    Explanation

    605

    After a while, the villagers gradually arrived, all saying that the day before, a woman had hanged herself between the beams, and the authorities had been informed, but the body had not yet been examined. Unexpectedly, strange events occurred. They entered together to see the body, which was already stiff and lying on the kang. The narrator calmed down and began to feel the pain, sensing as if a knife was cutting through his chest and back, unbearable. He undressed to see the injuries, finding his skin and flesh in a mess. The villagers guessed the cause, and he then realized that when he was thrown out, he had broken the window frame, thus receiving injuries both above and below. Not being cut in the abdomen and having his intestines dragged out was also fortunate. Up to now, more than twenty years have passed, and he will bear scars for the rest of his life. I initially found this very strange, but then laughed together with others.

    Lan Yan said: "Hanging oneself out of anger and then becoming strange—truly incomprehensible. How could those who did not die, would all become spirits of unrest? The narrator unfortunately suffered this shock and pain."

    A certain officer who enjoyed drinking and eating well, on one night returning home, bought six or seven sheep trotters and a bottle of fiery liquor. He sat by the stove alone, drinking, and discarded the trotter bones on the ground. Suddenly, he heard a rustling sound from the corner of the wall. He lit a lamp and looked closely, seeing ten or so small people, each about five or six inches tall, some male and some female, dressed like people of their time. They all carried bamboo baskets and bent over to pick up the trotter bones, placing them in the baskets, and soon they were all gone. The officer was startled and took a fire poker, throwing and hitting them. One fell down, and the rest fled in shock, all entering a wall hole. The fallen one rolled on the ground, making a squeaking sound, and then transformed into a mouse or rat and disappeared.

    Example 6

    Source: 既而村邻渐至，共云前一日有妇人缢死梁间，已报官，尚未检验，不意竟作怪如此。同入视尸，已僵卧炕下矣。王惊定思痛，觉胸前背后似刀割不可忍，解衣视之，皮肉狼藉，众共测其故，乃悟突出时，因撞折窗棂，是以上下两受其伤也，不割腹拖肠，亦云幸矣。迄今逾二十年，将终其身患疤痕焉。予初闻，甚异之，既而相与捧腹。

    　　兰岩曰：负气自缢，又作怪异，诚不可解。岂不得其死者，果皆为厉哉？王不幸遭此惊痛耳。

    　　鼠狼某佐领好酒喜啖。一夕夜归，市羊蹄六七枚，火酒一瓶，拥炉独酌，弃蹄骨于地。蓦闻墙角下窸窣有声，挑灯谛视，见小人十余，各高五六寸，或男或女，装束悉类时人；皆背一竹筐弯腰拾取蹄骨，置筐中，移时而尽。某心悸，取火箸掷而击之，一人仆，余惊走，悉入壁洞，仆者滚地唧唧，随化为鼠狼而逝。

    Translation: After a while, the villagers gradually arrived, all saying that the day before, a woman had hanged herself between the beams, and the authorities had been informed, but the body had not yet been examined. Unexpectedly, strange events occurred. They entered together to see the body, which was already stiff and lying on the kang. The narrator calmed down and began to feel the pain, sensing as if a knife was cutting through his chest and back, unbearable. He undressed to see the injuries, finding his skin and flesh in a mess. The villagers guessed the cause, and he then realized that when he was thrown out, he had broken the window frame, thus receiving injuries both above and below. Not being cut in the abdomen and having his intestines dragged out was also fortunate. Up to now, more than twenty years have passed, and he will bear scars for the rest of his life. I initially found this very strange, but then laughed together with others.

    Lan Yan said: "Hanging oneself out of anger and then becoming strange—truly incomprehensible. How could those who did not die, would all become spirits of unrest? The narrator unfortunately suffered this shock and pain."

    A certain officer who enjoyed drinking and eating well, on one night returning home, bought six or seven sheep trotters and a bottle of fiery

    606

    兰岩曰：为拾余骨，至遭掷击，怪亦贪矣。人之贪财物而任意攫取者，须于取时防为人之所击也。

    　　巨人应城王家口，有村氓十余辈，以秋稼将登，同于田间作芦棚守之。一夕，聚饮月下，倏有旋风自北来，势如山岳，群以为怪。既而渐近，约去二矢地，忽停吹不动，形如浮图。但闻声震如雷，化为巨人，高二丈许，白衣白冠，手持白幡，向众一挥，仍为旋风向南去，急如奔马。众悉惊绝，良久，始陆续复苏，哄传乡井。伙中有三人，一持观音咒已三年，一不食牛肉，一大醉熟睡，未尝与睹，尚以为妄，然亦不敢复往守田矣。迟数日，十余人接踵暴死，唯三人无恙。

    Explanation

    606

    The Scholar Lan Yan said: To gather the remaining bones, even to the point of being thrown and struck, the creature is also greedy. People who greedily take possessions without restraint must be cautious when taking things to avoid being attacked by others.

    A giant appeared at Wangjia Kou in Yingcheng. There were more than ten villagers who, as the autumn harvest was about to be reaped, built a reed hut in the fields to guard their crops. One night, they gathered to drink under the moonlight. Suddenly, a whirlwind came from the north, its power like that of a mountain. The villagers were all startled. As the whirlwind approached, it stopped about two arrow shots away, and suddenly ceased to blow, taking on the shape of a pagoda. They heard a sound as loud as thunder, and the whirlwind transformed into a giant, about two zhang tall, wearing white clothes and a white hat, holding a white banner. The giant waved the banner at the crowd and then turned back into a whirlwind, rushing south like a galloping horse. The villagers were all shocked and fell unconscious. After a long time, they gradually regained consciousness and spread the news in the village. Among them were three people: one had been reciting the Guanyin mantra for three years, another did not eat beef, and the third was heavily drunk and asleep, having not seen the giant. They still considered it a lie, but they也不敢再回去守田了. A few days later, more than ten people died one after another, but only the three people remained unharmed.

    607

    兰岩曰：诵咒戒牛，得免于难，固矣。至于酒能误事，人尽知之，而此人独以大醉免死，是酒又能救命也，岂巨人亦惧其酒狂耶，抑醉亦为冥间所弃耶？

    　　白　莲　教京山富人许翁，世居皂市阳桑湖畔。为其子娶妇，亦乡宦而富豪者，妆奁丰厚，一乡之所艳羡。有偷儿杨三，觊觎半年，以许防范严，无从措手。会其子拔贡，许亲送入都，将肄业成均，以图进取。杨俟进行，而夜入内室，伏暗处俟之。时新妇方娠，不耐久坐，二更即寝。相伴唯二婢，就灯作灯黹。良久，始闭户亦各谋睡，移灯至几上，光明如昼。杨闻鼾声，知已睡熟，方欲窃发，蓦见房门自开，一人启帘入，深目耸鼻，黑须绕颊，背负黄布囊，狞恶殊可怖。杨阴念吾道中未见此人，必有诡异，姑屏息蝟缩，以觇其所为。

    Explanation

    607

    Lan Yan said: Reciting incantations to warn the ox, one can avoid difficulty, which is true. As for wine causing trouble, everyone knows this, but this person alone was spared from death by being drunk. Could even a giant fear his wine-induced madness? Or is drunkenness also abandoned by the underworld?

    The White Lotus Society had a wealthy man named Xu from Jing Shan, who lived for generations by the Yang Sang Lake in Zao Shi. He arranged a marriage for his son to a local official and wealthy woman, whose dowry was rich and abundant, envied by the entire village. There was a thief named Yang San who coveted this for half a year, but since Xu was cautious, Yang could not find a way to act. When Xu's son was selected as a scholar-official, Xu personally accompanied him to the capital to study at the Imperial Academy in order to advance his career. Yang waited for the journey to begin and entered the inner room at night, hiding in the dark and waiting for him. At the time, the new wife was pregnant and could not sit for long, so she went to bed at the second watch of the night. She was accompanied only by two maids who worked by the lamp on needlework. After a long time, they finally closed the door and each went to sleep, moving the lamp to the table, making it as bright as day. Yang heard snoring and knew he was asleep, just about to steal something when he suddenly saw the door open by itself. A person entered by opening the curtain, with deep-set eyes and a prominent nose, a black beard around the cheeks, carrying a yellow cloth bag on his back, fierce and ugly, very frightening. Yang thought secretly that he had never seen this person before, so there must be something strange. He temporarily held his breath and crouched down to observe what he was doing.

    608

    其人鹗顾房中，探袖出香一枝，燃之于灯，插二婢枕畔，乃立新妇榻前，挂罗帐于金钩，启绣衾以秃指。妇面内而卧，花睡正浓。其人戟指闭目，口中喃喃似有所诅，随以手指妇背者三，妇忽蹶然而起，向其人赤身长跪。其人开布囊，出一小刀，剖腹取胎，破胎取子，复剖子腹取其心肝，贮小磁罐内，纳裹囊中，背负之径出房去。妇尸随仆床下。

    Explanation

    608

    The man cast a suspicious glance around the room, reached into his sleeve to retrieve a branch of incense, lit it on the lamp, and placed it near the pillows of the two maids. He then stood in front of the newlywed's bed, hung a silk curtain on a golden hook, and opened an embroidered quilt with a blunt finger. The woman lay with her face inward, her sleep deep as a blooming flower. The man pointed with a threatening gesture, closed his eyes, and muttered something in a curse-like manner. He then pointed at the woman's back three times. Suddenly, the woman jumped up, knelt naked before him. The man opened a cloth bag, took out a small knife, cut open the woman's abdomen to remove her fetus, then cut open the fetus to remove its heart and liver. He placed them in a small porcelain jar, carried them in a cloth bag, and left the room without a word. The woman's body followed, falling to the floor.

    609

    杨睹之，惊怕忿恨，盗念顿灰。出户尾之，密观其所经，历门数重，皆见其人以手拂之，悉洞开无阻。卒至村口一旅店，尚掩半扉，其人侧身入，扉乃阖，且闻落锁声，知为妖人寄迹之所。因念彼既伪作行客，岂能出不由户，聊憩檐下，坐以待旦。

    　　鸡初唱，店门复启，其人负囊而出，杨急起捉其臂曰：“客请少停，有密事举白。”言次，拖入店中，抱持之大呼曰：“主人速来，为汝擒得妖人矣！”其人大惊，极力摆挣，杨抱持益坚。俄而群客惊起，主人亦至，环问其故，其人曰：“我四川蜡客，欲赴江南，今日早行趁路，不知此兄何故突来纠缠。”杨曰：“勿听其饰说，但检其布囊，便有证据矣。

    Explanation

    609

    Yang witnessed him and was filled with fear, anger, and resentment, and his thoughts of theft suddenly turned to ashes. He stepped out the door and followed him, secretly observing where he went. He passed through several doors, seeing the man wave his hand at each one, and they all opened without obstruction. Finally, he arrived at a village entrance inn, where the door was still half-closed. The man sidled in, and the door closed, accompanied by the sound of the lock being engaged. Yang realized this was a place where the sorcerer was temporarily staying. Thinking that the man was pretending to be a traveler, Yang reasoned that he would not leave without going through the door. So, he took a brief rest under the eaves and waited for dawn.

    When the rooster first crowed, the inn door opened again, and the man came out carrying a cloth bag. Yang quickly sprang up and grabbed his arm, saying, "Please stay a moment, there is a secret matter to report." As he spoke, he dragged the man into the inn, holding him tightly and shouting, "Owner, come quickly! I have caught the sorcerer for you!" The man was greatly alarmed and tried to struggle, but Yang held him even more firmly. Soon, the other guests were startled awake, and the owner arrived, asking what was happening. The man said, "I am a wax merchant from Sichuan, heading to Jiangnan. I left early this morning to catch up with the road, but I don't know why this brother suddenly came to纠缠 me." Yang said, "Do not listen to his embellished words. Just check his cloth bag, and you will find the evidence."

    610

    ”众是之，开囊聚观，则累累然磁罐数枚，复欲开罐，其人惶遽抱罐而呼曰：“罐中黄白为一生衣食之本，奈何扰攘！欲劫我财耶？”佥怒曰：“青天白日之下，众目共睹之时，谁劫汝财？无事出言伤众，显有情弊！”主人挺身奋出，曰：“有事无事，予一人任之，第开看，无多言！”即夺一罐开之，见鲜血满中，腥气触鼻。取器倾视，尽小儿心肝，数之得七罐，尚空三罐。众莫不骇异，致诘那得此物，杨曰：“彼必不承，请以代白。”因述夜间之事。众人大惊，曰：“纣以天下之尊，刳剖孕妇，尚为不可；汝何等人，破卵伤胎，不一而足。苟非上天好生，假手宵人，则吾乡之孕妇小儿，无噍类矣。”于是大动公忿，竞挥老拳。

    Explanation

    610

    Everyone agreed, and they opened the bag to gather around and look. Inside were several jars, stacked up like a pile. They wanted to open one of the jars, and the man, in a state of panic, clutched the jars and cried out, "The gold and silver inside these jars are the foundation of my entire life's sustenance! Why disturb them? Are you trying to rob me?" Everyone was angry and said, "Under the clear sky and bright daylight, with everyone watching, who would dare to rob you? To speak without cause and harm others, clearly shows some ulterior motive!" The owner stood up boldly and said, "Whether there is something or not, I will take responsibility for it. Just open one and see, no more words!" He immediately took one jar and opened it, and he saw blood all over it, with a strong smell of blood. He took a container and poured it out to look, and it was all the hearts and livers of children. Counting them, there were seven jars full, and three jars were still empty. Everyone was shocked and amazed, and they asked how he could have obtained such things. Yang said, "He will surely not admit. Please let me speak on his behalf." He then recounted the events of the night. Everyone was greatly alarmed and said, "King Zhou, with his supreme status, still could not bear to disembowel pregnant women; what kind of person are you, breaking eggs and harming fetuses, doing so without end? If it were not for the benevolence of heaven, using the hands of night people, then all the pregnant women and children in our village would have no survivors." Thus, they were greatly stirred by public anger and started hitting him with their fists.

    611

    主人恐其致毙，方欲止之。其人忽瞑目大叱，众拳到处，如触木石，指节损破。主人大惊，仓卒间急提一罐，自其人头上倾之。其人连作恨声，曰：“罢了！罢了！莫非数也。”众复殴之，主人曰：“小不忍则乱大谋，倘打坏，谁任其咎？不如执之送县，自有国法在，听官断可也。”送其之县，许家男妇已在，杨更述之。许妪大哭曰：“凶犯已获，吾不忍复至公庭，致宦家闺秀，暴露尸骸也。”妇母家感其言，亦皆罢讼，相与驱车而返。县宰细讯得实，方知为白莲教妖人之党，取小儿心肝者，亦行持邪术必需之物也。时湘汉一带胎妇被剖者甚多，至此始得其故，并得其党名姓面貌数十人，陆续捕获。狱成，寸磔其人于市。杨杖二十，给银五十两，责其为盗而赏其捉奸也。

    Explanation

    611
    The host feared that the situation would lead to death and was about to stop it. The person suddenly closed his eyes and roared loudly; the crowd punched him, as if hitting wood or stone, damaging his fingertips. The host was greatly alarmed and, in a hurry, quickly grabbed a jar and poured it over the person's head. The person continuously made sounds of regret, saying, "It's over! It's over! Is it fate?" The crowd beat him again, and the host said, "A small act of impatience can ruin a great plan. If we hurt him, who will be responsible?" It would be better to take him to the county, there are national laws to follow, let the official judge it. They took him to the county, where the men and women of the Xu family were already there, and Yang recounted the events. The elderly woman of the Xu family wept, saying, "The culprit has been caught. I cannot bear to go to the public court again, to expose the body of a noblewoman." The relatives of the woman were moved by her words and also stopped the lawsuit, driving away together. The county magistrate carefully interrogated and found the truth, realizing they were part of the White Lotus Sect's heretical group. Those who took the hearts and livers of children were also necessary for practicing heretical magic. At the time, many pregnant women in the Xiang and Han regions were being cut open, and only now did they understand the reason, finding the names, faces, and appearances of dozens of their accomplices, capturing them one by one. The case was resolved, and the person was dissected in the market square. Yang received twenty strokes of the cane and fifty taels of silver, punished for being a thief but rewarded for catching a wrongdoer.
    612

    兰岩曰：妖术杀人，惨酷已极，固天人之所共愤者。卒乃假手宵人，以败其事，抑亦巧矣。不然，兴讼结仇，多人牵累，何能一旦痛雪新妇之冤哉！

    　　鬼哭贵阳太守某公之母，病濒危。亲戚邻里来候问者，皆设酒馔于厅上款之，二更始散去。　余尚多，有子侄四五人，复聚饮于斋中。三更后，忽有哭声，越北窗外，类少妇而音甚惨切。举室惊默相向。有二三胆勇者，出户视之，于月下见一白衣妇人，循墙而西，径入角门去，无不毛戴，咸知其为鬼也。一食顷，闻宅内悲声群动，家人奔走来告，太夫人已气绝矣。俗谚有丧门吊客之说，理或不诬也。

    612

    Lan Yan said: The use of witchcraft to kill people is extremely cruel and brutal, something that both heaven and people together detest. In the end, they used the hands of a lowly person to thwart the matter, which was also clever. Otherwise, initiating lawsuits and creating enmity, implicating many people, how could they suddenly clear the new wife's冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤冤

    613

    兰岩曰：其事有之，其理不解。

    　　袁翁长山袁翁，少极贫，居城外一破屋中，几于行乞。一日窘甚，饥虚已数日矣，无如何，检点破衣襦数事，至典肆欲质钱若干。肆主曰：“此等物不值一文，可持去。”翁太息曰：“我非滥为者，特以饥不得食，称贷无路，乞食不能，故万不获已，以此为质，不过聊以为信，得钱则取赎耳。幸念素识之情，用质数十百文，以延残喘也。”肆主以为笑谈，置不理，翁愤然曰：“恨我一时在困苦中耳。苟有日发迹，誓亦开一解库，彼时虽有人将死孩儿来质，亦必质之矣！”店肆最忌质死孩儿之说，闻之颇不甘，第以其贫窭至极，不足与较，故为隐忍。

    Explanation

    613

    Lan Yan said: Such a matter exists, but the principle behind it is not understood.

    Mr. Yuan from Longshan, Mr. Yuan, was extremely poor when he was young, living in a dilapidated house outside the city walls, almost begging for food. One day, he was in dire straits, having gone hungry for several days. With no other recourse, he gathered several pieces of old, tattered clothing and went to a pawnshop to pawn them for some money. The shop owner said, "These items are not worth a single coin; you can take them back." Mr. Yuan sighed and said, "I am not doing this frivolously. I am simply starving and have no way to borrow money or beg for food. Therefore, out of sheer necessity, I am pawning these items, only to use them as collateral, and I will redeem them once I get the money. Please consider our long-standing acquaintance and accept these items as collateral for a few dozen or hundred coins, to prolong my life." The shop owner treated it as a joke and ignored him. Mr. Yuan angrily said, "I regret being in such hardship at the moment. If I ever succeed in the future, I swear to open a pawnshop myself. At that time, even if someone brings a dying child to pawn, I will still accept it!" Pawnshops most detest the idea of pawning a dying child, and hearing this, he was quite displeased. However, since Mr. Yuan was extremely poor, it was not worth arguing about, so he endured it silently.

    614

    翁归去，一路冥想，毫无生趣，乃止步向天号泣曰：“呜呼！袁某自问于心，所行之事，无不可告人者，胡为而竟至于此耶？”良久辍泣，复行。忽破衣为棘刺所牵，猝难摆脱，屈身摘之。觉棘下土甚松，试抄以手，土中有物累累然，白光灿铄，取视二枚，则朱提也。大惊喜，即以破衣裹数锭，仍以土密掩其余者以归。次晚，复往取之，多不胜取。数旬方尽，约略二万金，不敢彰露，先作些小生意，逐渐张大。一年之后，遂为巨贾。问舍求田，买僮蓄婢，故于宅旁开一典肆。

    Explanation

    614

    Yuan returned home, meditating along the way, feeling no interest in life. He then stopped, turned to heaven, and wept aloud, saying, "Alas! Yuan awoke himself to the heart, the things I have done, none of which are shameful to confess, why have I come to this state?" After a long time, he stopped crying and resumed walking. Suddenly, his tattered clothes were caught by thorns, and he found it suddenly difficult to escape. He bent down to pick them up. He felt that the soil under the thorns was very loose, so he tried to scoop it with his hand. There were objects piled up in the soil, and white light shining brightly. He picked up and saw two pieces, and it turned out to be Zhu Ti. Great surprise and joy filled him, and he immediately wrapped several ingots in his tattered clothes, still covering the rest with soil and returned home. The next evening, he went back to take it, too many to take, after several weeks, it was all taken, approximately two million coins, did not dare to show it, started with some small business, gradually expanded. After one year, became a wealthy merchant, asked for houses and fields, bought servants and maids, therefore, opened a pawnshop next to his house.

    615

    前肆主闻之，讶曰：“袁饿鬼果有今日耶？昔者受其恶言恶声，每一念及，心实不甘。今趁其发市之始，盍一往，故犯其忌，聊申夙忿乎？”乃觅二死孩，裹以襁褓，挟至其市，求质银十两。主柜者大怒，势将用武，翁适在侧，急止之，而拱手向肆主曰：“老兄欲证成我为信人耶？此孩之死，正值小肆开市之日，不为无缘，请如数质之。”因使人贾一小棺，殓孩于内：“此孩不必远送，即我所立地砖下瘗之可也。”急呼僮仆执锸，就脚下掘一穴，才尺余，忽得一石板，发之，板下列巨瓮十数，瓮中白镪皆满，一肆大惊。肆主见之感叹，始知翁长者，天固有以默启之也。再拜谢罪而去。

    Explanation

    615

    The previous shop owner heard about it and was surprised, saying, "Has the starving ghost Yuan really come to this? In the past, he suffered from my harsh words and criticism, and every time I think of it, my heart truly resents it. Now, taking advantage of the beginning of his market, why not go and do it, intentionally provoke his displeasure, and just vent my long-held resentment?" He then found two dead children, wrapped them in swaddling clothes, and carried them to the market, asking to pawn them for ten taels of silver. The person in charge of the counter became very angry and was about to resort to violence. The old man happened to be nearby and quickly stopped him, bowing and saying to the shop owner, "Old brother, are you trying to prove that I am a trustworthy person? The death of this child coincided with the day my small shop opened, not without reason. Please accept it as collateral in the same amount." Then, he sent someone to buy a small coffin and placed the child inside. "This child does not need to be sent far away; it can be buried right under the bricks I have placed." He quickly called the servants to bring shovels and dug a hole at their feet. Just a foot deep, they suddenly found a stone slab, which they removed. Under the slab were several large jars, all filled with silver. One of the shops was greatly surprised. The shop owner saw it and sighed, then realized that the old man was a virtuous person. Heaven must have silently enlightened him. He bowed twice, apologized, and left.

    616

    翁自此富甲一县，已而生子，子生孙，皆能读书上达，有仕至尚书者、督抚者、卿贰者，科甲连绵，迄今正当鼎盛也。

    　　堪舆护军参领某，少壮时，从征青海，为贼所掳，械送某喇嘛处。至则入一大刹，喇嘛据床坐，年届期颐，两睫垂皮寸余，尽掩其目。闻某至，呼至床下。侍者进牙箸一枝，喇嘛以箸拨启其睫，束以哈达（帕也），露两瞳如碧琉璃，明彻似蜻蜓眼。某异之，再拜顶礼，祈为解脱。喇嘛曰：“半年后，当返中国。此亦定数，未可幸脱也。吾视汝无大根柢，只可授一术以终身耳。”遂留之，朝夕悉有秘授。凡六越月，大将军底定青海，喇嘛致书将军，言某终守苏卿之节，将军取之以归。某累官至护军参将，遂以精青鸟之术，知名辇下。

    Explanation

    616

    The man thus became the wealthiest in the county, and later gave birth to sons, who in turn gave birth to grandsons. All were able to study and advance, with some rising to the rank of Shangshu (Minister), Dufu (Governor), or Qing'er (Imperial Court Official). Their success in the imperial examinations continued, and they are now at the height of their prosperity.

    As for the Kanyu (Geomancy) Huojun (Military Commander) of a certain rank, during his youth and prime, he participated in the campaign against Qinghai and was captured by the enemy, being sent in chains to a certain lama. Upon arrival, he entered a large Buddhist temple, where the lama was seated on a bed, aged one hundred years old, with his eyelids drooping and skin hanging down about an inch, completely covering his eyes. Upon hearing of the man's arrival, the lama called him to the foot of the bed. A servant brought a pair of ivory chopsticks, and the lama used the chopsticks to lift his eyelids, tying them up with a khata (sash). His eyes, clear and blue like green jade, were bright and round like a dragonfly's eyes. The man was amazed by this and bowed twice, touching the ground with his forehead, praying to be freed. The lama said, "Half a year later, you will return to China. This is also fate, and you cannot escape by chance. I see that you have no great foundation, and I can only teach you one skill for your lifetime." He then kept him, teaching him in secret every day. For six months, the great general pacified Qinghai, and the lama wrote a letter to the general, stating that the man would ultimately uphold the integrity of Su Qin. The general took him back, and the man was gradually promoted to the rank of military commander. He became famous in the imperial capital for his skill in the art of divination.

    617

    时有山西布客死京邸，乡人瘗之丛葬处老槐之下。后十余年，其子经商颇利，累资巨万，故乡已获牛眠地，议发槥归正首邱，祈某一往勘之。某至墓所周视，即曰：“此穴得木之气甚旺，不可更迁也。且发土更见肢体，与君大不利。”子欲中止，其乡人皆不欲，曰：“富而不荣葬其亲，至掩骼异地，非孝也。”子不得已，佣工发掘，未及咫尺，已见槐根萦绊，抽而断之，清香扑鼻。及棺，则尽为桑根蟠络，不露寸木。竟半日之力，始取棺出。棺已朽，一臂在外，工纳之，臂折。子大哭，观者靡不惋惜慨叹。子扶柩归，于路坠马，折一臂，遂成废疾，卒于逆旅。棺厝一古田中，无马鬣封也。

    Explanation

    617

    A Merchant from Shanxi Dies in the Capital

    Once upon a time, a merchant from Shanxi died in a lodging in the capital. His fellow villagers buried him in a mass grave under an old槐 tree. After more than ten years, his son, who had become a successful businessman, amassed a vast fortune. Upon learning that his hometown had acquired a favorable burial site, he decided to exhume his father's remains and return them to their rightful place in their hometown. He sought the advice of an expert and asked him to inspect the grave.

    The expert visited the grave and examined it thoroughly. He then said, "This grave site has a very strong presence of the wood element, and it is not suitable to move it again. Moreover, if you disturb the earth, you will uncover more of the body, which will be very unfavorable for you."

    The son wanted to stop the plan, but his fellow villagers were against it, saying, "To be wealthy but not honorably bury one's parents, to bury bones in a foreign land, is not filial piety."

    Unable to persuade his fellow villagers, the son hired workers to exhume the grave. Before they had dug even a short distance, they encountered the roots of the槐 tree entwining around the coffin. They pulled the roots away, and the air was filled with a fragrant aroma. When they reached the coffin, they found that it was completely wrapped in桑 roots, with not an inch of wood visible. It took half a day of effort to finally remove the coffin.

    The coffin had rotted, and one of the father's arms was sticking out. When the workers tried to push the arm back in, it broke. The son wept bitterly, and the onlookers all expressed their regret and sorrow.

    The son carried the coffin back home, but on the way, he fell off his horse and broke his arm. He became disabled and eventually died in a temporary lodging place. The coffin was placed in an ancient field, without the traditional mound of earth in the shape of a horse's mane.

    618

    又，护军统领某公为其先人营葬，会葬者接轸。灵辇甫至穴前，某趋至公前，启曰：“职家贫，资钱四十万，所不能矣，谨具生刍之吊。今观佳城郁郁，而土色纯殷，恐至不祥，请一观朱寿之器。”公素耳其名，亟命启幕示之。某惊曰：“穴已定乎？”公曰：“定矣。”某曰：“且勿葬此穴。”盖是穴为张某所点，张亦素有盛名，师心自用者，闻之，大恚曰：“君勿喋喋！舍此岂复有正穴哉？”众多附和之，遂下棺而崇封焉。某顿足曰：“此大缪矣！”急取锸向墓之南，掘地为沟，深尺余，长二丈，阔一尺，曰：“得此，其庶几免乎。”既而辞去。以煤炭大书一“火”字于碑阴。张见之，诮姗不已。俄见数骑自城中飞奔来报，宅中失火，廪厩俱焚，公大惊，始信其术之神。自此，名愈噪。

    Explanation

    618

    Another time, a certain commander of the guards, whose name is not specified, prepared for the burial of his ancestors. The attendees were numerous, their carriages lined up in succession. As the palanquin carrying the deceased just arrived at the burial site, the speaker hurried to the commander's side and said, "My family is poor, and we cannot afford forty million coins; we have only offered a modest funeral gift of fresh grass. Now, seeing the tomb lush and flourishing, with the soil color pure and dark red, I fear it may be an omen of misfortune. Please allow me to inspect the vessel for storing wine or other liquids." The commander had long heard of his name and immediately ordered the tent to be opened and shown to him. The speaker was surprised and asked, "Is the burial site already determined?" The commander replied, "Yes, it is." The speaker then said, "Please do not bury in this site." It turned out that the burial site had been chosen by a person named Zhang, who was also well-known for his expertise. Zhang, who acted according to his own ideas and not following others, upon hearing this, became greatly angry and said, "Do not chatter on! Is there any other proper burial site besides this one?" Many others agreed with him, and they proceeded to lower the coffin and cover it with earth. The speaker stamped his foot and said, "This is a great mistake!" He quickly grabbed a spade and dug a trench to the south of the tomb, about a foot deep, two zhang long, and one foot wide, saying, "With this, perhaps we can avoid disaster." After that, he took his leave. He inscribed a large "fire" character on the back of the tombstone with coal. When Zhang saw this, he continued to mock him. Soon, several riders flew in from the city to report that the house had caught fire, and the granary and stables were all burned. The commander was greatly surprised and finally believed in the power of the speaker's method. From then on, the speaker's name became even more famous.

    619

    所居邻历代帝王庙，院东悉属红楼，或谓：“大不雅观，盍去诸？”某曰：“吾今老矣，平生信天株守，不善夤缘，所赖此数仞红墙，冬来可博一外任，以饯余年耳。”至冬，果以卓异授江南一参将，五年后乞休归里。宦囊颇裕，但不敢复为人相地，相则两目赤瘇，每数日不瘥。

    　　闲斋曰：参戎公今下世矣，伊君其婿也，尝为予言其异绩甚多，悉堪纪述。方其为护军校时，偶偕三四友人，携酒郊游，小歇一墓门下，墓前松楸荫翳，咸啧啧以为佳城，公曰：“此绝地也，何足称羡！”友问其故，公曰：“此松柏皆百年物也，苟有子孙，则斩伐而货为栋梁也久矣，焉能至今无恙乎？”友群笑以为恶谑。既而坐旗亭，询及墓主，酒家佣曰：“此汉军张氏之茔也。张故百万富，而今已矣，绝嗣数十年矣。”众大骇，益神之。夫公之术固神矣，乃为所谑，亦穷理至乎其极者也。

    Explanation

    619

    The place where he lived was next to the Temple of Emperors of All Dynasties, and the eastern part of the courtyard was all red buildings. Someone said, "It is very unseemly, why don't you get rid of it?" He replied, "I am old now, I have always trusted fate and stayed in one place, not good at making connections. I rely on this several-foot-high red wall, in winter it can help me secure an external appointment, to spend my remaining years." By winter, he was indeed appointed as a general in Jiangnan for his outstanding performance. Five years later, he requested retirement and returned to his hometown. His official income was quite substantial, but he dared not do it again for others. If he did it, his eyes would turn red and ulcerate, not healing for several days.

    Xianzhai said: General Gong has passed away now. His son-in-law is the one who said this. He once told me about his many unusual achievements, all of which are worth recording. When he was a guard officer, he occasionally went on a trip with three or four friends, carrying wine to the suburbs, and took a short rest under the gate of a tomb. The cypress trees in front of the tomb were lush and shady, everyone murmured that it was a fine tomb. He said, "This is a place of utmost importance, what is there to be envious of?" A friend asked him why, he said, "These cypress trees have been here for a hundred years, if there were descendants, the trees would have been cut down and sold as pillars long ago, how could they still be intact today?" Everyone laughed, thinking it was a joke. Later, they sat in a pavilion and asked about the tomb owner, a servant from the inn said, "This is the grave of Zhang, a Han general. Zhang was once a millionaire, but now he is gone, and his descendants have died out for several decades." Everyone was greatly shocked, and he became even more revered. General Gong's skill is indeed divine, but he was jokingly teased, which also shows his understanding of the ultimate truth.

    620

    兰岩曰：一术之精，便能言之如响，趋吉避凶，未始非道也。神乎技而进乎道，信乎！

    　　尤　大　鼻咸宁尤大鼻，贩皮货于天津。与布客董九，亲戚也，而相友善。董有子名韶，年十七，丰神隽逸，资质慧秀，不类贾人子，尤深爱之。

    　　值年午节，尤携韶出游河上，过闹市，车马阗凑，遂相失不能复聚。韶觅尤不得，独坐河干树下暂歇，见肩挑白酒卖者，呼而沽饮之，白酒甘冽，殊适渴喉，一举数碗，炎暑顿消。韶固稚弱，未尝饮酒，白酒虽薄，亦不能任，眩晕颇甚，就卧树下，无复知觉。

    Explanation

    620

    Lan Yan said: Mastery of a skill allows one to speak as if echoing, to seek good fortune and avoid misfortune, which is not far from the path. The skill transcends mere technique and approaches the level of a moral principle; indeed!

    Especially in the case of You Dabi, who traded fur goods in Tianjin. He was related to and friendly with a cloth merchant named Dong Jiu. Dong had a son named Shao, who was seventeen years old, with a graceful and elegant appearance and a sharp and refined intellect, unlike the typical merchant's son. You deeply loved him.

    On the Dragon Boat Festival, You took Shao out for a boat ride on the river. They passed through a bustling market, where the streets were crowded with carriages and horses, and they got separated and could not reunite. Shao searched for You but could not find him, so he sat alone under a tree by the riverbank for a rest. He saw a vendor selling white wine and called out to buy some. The white wine was sweet and refreshing, quenching his thirst, and he drank several bowls in one go, cooling down from the heat. Shao was young and inexperienced with alcohol, and although the wine was mild, he could not handle it, becoming very dizzy. He lay down under the tree and lost consciousness.

    621

    良久醒来，则在一纱帐中，衾枕悉具，惊起欲遁。忽一人振管辟扉，秉烛而入，则一十八九女郎也。修眉素面，含笑嫣然，置烛于几，低鬟敛衽曰：“日间归自外家，见子醉卧茵草，恐犯风露，故设榻相款，子其勿疑。”韶始恍然，感荷无尽，即欲辞去。女止之曰：“时已入夜，路且隔城，去将安之？宿此为便。”韶曰：“与子向无交涉，保敢便住？”女曰：“饮啜前定，即邂逅亦非偶然，幸勿作客态向人。”韶谢曰：“惠然肯留，深惬素望，第惭少子不学，出言市井，谈锋不敌，徒聒听闻。”女笑曰：“儿闻丹漆不文，白璧不雕，质既无亏，何必受饰？且子硍词谦语，婉而多风，齿颊芬芳，须堪取则。得承一夕色笑，死不为夭。”因问何字，韶曰：“童子无字。问名，则是董韶耳。

    Explanation

    621

    After a long while, he woke up and found himself in a silk canopy, with bedding fully prepared. Startled, he wanted to escape. Suddenly, a person waved a pipe and opened the door, entering with a candle. It was a young woman in her late teens or early twenties. She had well-groomed eyebrows and a plain face, smiling gracefully. She placed the candle on the table and bowed respectfully, saying, "I returned from my family home during the day and saw you drunk and lying on the grass. I was afraid you might catch a chill from the wind and dew, so I set up a bed to entertain you. Please do not doubt me." At this, Shao became aware and felt immense gratitude, immediately wanting to leave. The woman stopped him, saying, "It is already nighttime, and the road is far away, across the city. Where will you go? Staying here is convenient." Shao replied, "I have never had any interaction with you before. How can I stay here so casually?" The woman said, "Our drinking and eating were predestined. Even if it was a chance encounter, it was not accidental. Please do not act like a guest." Shao thanked her, saying, "Your kindness in allowing me to stay is deeply satisfying. However, I feel ashamed as a young boy who has not studied. My words are crude, and my conversation is no match. I am only causing noise." The woman smiled and said, "I have heard that fine lacquer does not need to be carved, and a white jade does not need to be polished. If the essence is already complete, why should it be decorated? Moreover, your words are humble and graceful, with a gentle and charming tone. The fragrance of your words is delightful and worthy of emulation. To receive even a single night of your smile and conversation would be enough to ensure I do not die young." She then asked what his name was. Shao replied, "I am a child without a given name. If you ask for my name, it is Dong Shao."

    622

    ”于是絮语间杂，妖言隐谜，女或如不闻，或偶一应答，尖颖刺人。韶神魂俱荡，如在醉中。壁上悬乐器，制甚古雅，不识其名，女曰：“参差也，一名洞箫。”韶曰：“然则卿必知音者。”女曰：“有孔则吹之，有弦则拨之，顺其自然，自能合调。若夫胶柱鼓瑟，虽有元音，从何发泄？是以知音之难也。”“能歌乎？”女曰：“《懊”》之曲，《子夜》之声，但堪礗　于一时，讵足喤聒于大雅？乖音错节，不足以征。深夜矣，与其隔锦屏于鄂渚，何如觅佳梦于巫山。“韶腼腆从命，遂相与就寝，低帐昵枕，极尽欢爱。留连数日，不思旋返。更得逐渐尽交其同类。有名小兰者、小惠者，有名小寿者、秋红者，要皆姝丽，各具所长。女名春翠，色艺独为群姬之冠，诸姬亦自知不逮，凡百将顺。

    Explanation

    622

    "Thus, the conversation was a mix of casual talk and cryptic remarks. The woman sometimes seemed not to hear, or would occasionally respond with sharp and piercing words. Shaohua's spirit and soul were moved, as if he were in a drunken state. Instruments hung on the wall, ancient and elegant in design, though he did not recognize their names. The woman said, 'They are called "参差" (cēncī), also known as "洞箫" (dòngxiāo).' Shaohua replied, 'Then you must be a connoisseur of music.' She said, 'Blow into the holes and pluck the strings, follow the natural flow, and the music will harmonize on its own. If one is rigid and inflexible, like tightening the strings of a zither, even if there is a fundamental tone, how can it be expressed? That is why it is difficult to truly understand music.' 'Can you sing?' Shaohua asked. She replied, 'The "懊" (ào) melody and the "子夜" (ziye) sound are only suitable for a moment, but they are not worthy of the refined elegance of high art. Out of tune and out of rhythm, they are not enough to convey meaning. It is late at night. Would it not be better to seek a beautiful dream in Wushan than to be separated by a screen in Ezhu?' Shaohua, blushing with shyness, followed her command, and they lay down together, under a low canopy and close to the pillows, indulging in their passion. They lingered for several days, not thinking of returning. Shaohua gradually became acquainted with others of the same kind. There were women named Xiaolan and Xiaohui, Xiaoshou and Qiuhong, all of whom were beautiful and talented in their own ways. The woman named Chun Cui was the most beautiful and skilled among them, and the other women also knew that they could not compare, so they all submitted to her."

    623

    时盛夏暑热，四姬邀韶，共浴莲沼中。狎戏方殷，春翠忽纵目远瞩，大惊曰：“妖道亦太狠毒，直寻逞至此耶！”不暇着衣，白身回走，四姬失措，提裙挈裤，纷纷狂走。俄一人飞马至，绣衣青巾，貌极雄伟，问韶曰：“彼众女子安往？”韶战悚水中，口不能言，但以手乱指。其人随手所向，绕沼而驰，卒无所得，意殊忿躁，连鞭其马。马拌鬣一嘶，御风腾起，急如飞电，瞬息不知所逝。韶翘首向天，痴立瞠目，旋闻人声喧嚣，似有人呼其名者。惊视之，则其父及尤并相识数辈，毕集沼畔，扶之出水，衣而守之。

    Explanation

    623

    During the intense summer heat, the four concubines invited Shao to bathe together in a lotus pond. As they were just beginning to play, Chun Cui suddenly looked far away with a start, exclaiming, "The demon path is also too cruel, how could it be allowed to reach this extent?" Without time to dress, she ran back naked. The other four concubines were panicked, pulling up their skirts and pants, running away in a frenzy. Soon, a person on horseback arrived, dressed in embroidered clothing and a blue headband, with a very majestic appearance. He asked Shao, "Where have those women gone?" Shao was trembling in the water, unable to speak, but only pointing frantically. The person followed the direction he pointed, circling the pond, but found nothing. He was extremely angry and frustrated, lashing his horse repeatedly. The horse neighed and suddenly flew up like a flash of lightning, disappearing in an instant. Shao looked up at the sky, standing in a daze with wide eyes. Soon, he heard a loud commotion, as if someone was calling his name. Startled, he looked around and saw his father and some acquaintances gathered by the pond, helping him out of the water, dressing him, and guarding him.

    624

    一饷时神色甫定，四顾园亭乌有，莲沼无存，但见几树高槐，数抔荒冢，冢前积雨成潦，葭荻丛生，复爽然若有所失。回念诸女，不觉潸然。以车载归，众亦散去。唯其父与尤在旁。因诘如许时宿食何处，乃一旦独浴积潦，甚不可解。韶不能隐，一一吐实，二人不胜骇愕。尤叹曰：“自午日相失，在在谘诹，令尊为汝忘啜废寝，憭慄自伤。原其咎在予之不谨，讵能自安。亦曾拟议，或遭狐鬼，愈益忧惶。夙闻某庙李道士有奇术，往祈之，彼授一符，令去郊外焚之，焚讫，必有狂风，但从风而往，必有究竟，不意果能获汝，李道士真神仙矣！第可恨此女，不测是鬼是狐，则作如许狡狯，必报之以泄吾恨！”董曰：“得人为幸，遑计其他。且彼既能幻化惑人，岂无术自卫？苟一选事，为祸不浅。”韶亦劝慰，谓奈何与异类较短长，尤终不释然。

    Explanation

    624

    After a brief moment, his expression calmed down, but upon looking around, the garden and pavilion were nowhere to be found, and the lotus pond had vanished. All that remained were a few tall willows, several abandoned tombs, and rainwater pooling in front of the tombs, with reeds and rushes growing wild. He felt empty as if something were missing. Thinking back on the girls, he couldn't help but shed tears. He returned in a carriage, and the others dispersed. Only his father and You remained by his side. His father asked where he had spent the time and where he had eaten, as it was impossible to understand how he could have bathed in the accumulated rainwater alone. Shao could not hide the truth and revealed everything. The two were greatly astonished. You sighed and said, "Since the fifth day, we have been searching everywhere for you. Your father has forgotten to eat and sleep, lamenting his own misfortune. The fault lies with me for not being careful, and I cannot rest easy. I have also considered the possibility that you might have encountered fox spirits or ghosts, which only increased my anxiety. I had long heard that the Taoist priest at a certain temple possesses mysterious skills. I went to ask him, and he gave me a talisman, instructing me to burn it in the outskirts. After burning it, there would certainly be a strong wind. Following the wind would lead me to the truth. I didn't expect that I would find you, and the Taoist priest is truly a god! However, I am still angry with this girl, who is unknown whether she is a ghost or a fox spirit, and yet she played such tricks. I must take revenge to vent my anger!" Dong said, "Finding you is a blessing; there's no time to think about anything else. Besides, if she can transform and deceive people, she must have some way to protect herself. If we provoke her, it could lead to more trouble." Shao also tried to comfort him, saying, "Why bother to compare lengths with supernatural beings? You still couldn't let go."

    625

    翌日，城门甫启，即提一短梃，奔至积潦前大索，无所获，卒至古冢旁，见茂草中一穴，大如碗，黝然而深，莫测底止。尤笑曰：“得其巢穴矣！”然无可用武，踌躇得一策，乃多取朽木槁枝，填塞穴口，燃火薰之。一食顷，釶然一物，冲烟突火而遁。视之，一黑狐也。迅走如风，追之不及。方却回，又连出四头，一白三黑，仓皇四散，皆不能逐。后遂无继者，尤笑曰：“此即所谓诸女郎也，与族俱行，此举徒劳矣。”欣欣然归，述之于董。董大惊曰：“胡不见商，则独冒此险？彼皆甘人者，既皆逸去，必图报复，兄不可疏防。为兄计，不如暂归，以避其祟。”尤曰：“予正望其来，岂可反为葸避？”董知其不可谏，阳称其勇，而阴为护卫。

    Explanation

    625

    The next day, when the city gate was just opened, he carried a short cudgel and rushed to the area of accumulated water, searching thoroughly but finding nothing. Finally, he arrived at the side of an ancient tomb, where he saw a hole in the dense grass, as large as a bowl, dark and deep, with no bottom in sight. He laughed and said, "I've found its nest!" However, there was nothing to fight with, so he thought of a plan. He collected many rotten wood and dry branches, blocked the entrance of the hole, and burned them to smoke it out. After one meal's time, something suddenly emerged, rushing through the smoke and fire. Looking at it, he saw a black fox. It ran swiftly like the wind, and he could not catch up. Just as he turned back, four more emerged, one white and three black, fleeing in panic. He could not chase any of them. After that, no more came, and he laughed, saying, "This is what is called the 'many daughters,' coming together with their clan, so this effort was futile." He returned happily and recounted the story to Dong. Dong was greatly surprised and said, "Why didn't you see the merchant, yet you alone took this risk? They are all willing to harm people, and since they have all escaped, they will surely seek revenge. You must not be careless. For your sake, it would be better to return temporarily to avoid their curses." The hunter replied, "I am just waiting for them to come, how can I turn back and be timid?" Knowing that he could not dissuade him, Dong pretended to praise his courage while secretly guarding him.

    626

    一日，尤将出城责逋。董父子请偕，尤许之。及出关，尤内逼，往登溷，董父子伫俟檐下。少焉，闻尤在厕中骂人，董方猜疑，忽謣然一声，骂声顿止。董父子趋入，尤已倒置溷中，两足伸缩，厕中更无一人。拼力扶救，粪蛆无处不有，幸不致死。董父子亦不能无染。遂相与至河上浣濯，逾时始各就绪。董因诘尤：“与谁口角，致坠溷中？”尤笑且叹曰：“不听药石言，便有腌脏气。予始登厕，即见一黑狐人立壁角，向予切齿，予方骂数声，彼突至面前，极力排挤，不觉仰面颠坠。平日英雄，扫地尽矣。”董父子亦为之捧腹。

    Explanation

    626

    One day, You was about to leave the city to demand repayment of a debt. The father and son of Dong requested to accompany him, and You agreed. Upon exiting the gate, You felt compelled inwardly and went to the toilet, while the father and son of Dong stood waiting under the eaves. Soon after, they heard You cursing someone in the toilet. Dong began to suspect, when suddenly a loud noise erupted, and the cursing stopped abruptly. The father and son of Dong rushed inside to find You already turned upside down in the toilet, his feet moving in and out, and there was no one else in the toilet. They struggled to rescue him, and fly larvae were everywhere, but fortunately, he did not die. The father and son of Dong were also affected by the contamination. They then went together to the river to wash and cleanse themselves, and after some time, they were finally done. Dong then asked You, "Who did you argue with that caused you to fall into the toilet?" You laughed and sighed, saying, "If I don't heed advice like medicine and stone, I start to develop a foul smell. I just entered the toilet, and I saw a black fox standing at the corner of the wall, gnashing its teeth at me. I cursed a few times, and it suddenly appeared before me, shoving me forcefully. I didn't realize I was falling over backward. My usual heroism has all been washed away." The father and son of Dong also laughed heartily.

    627

    亟归店，议作归计。董虑韶召邪，亦令同归就婚。择日趋装就道，暮宿逆旅。夜半，春翠忽至，与韶同寝，尤闻韶喃喃絮语，谛听之，如与人交媾者然。悟其又为狐祟，大声恐吓，韶惊觉，已失女之所在，然遗精濡席矣。尤诘得其故，复大骂。

    　　已而就寝，忽失尤。韶起，秉烛遍觅，闻鼾声出自一米瓮中，瓮上覆一瓦盆，泥封甚固。急呼主人，俾开之。主人曰：“此腌菜未熟，开之何为？”韶曰：“有人在内，焉得不开？”主人骇异，听之果然。急开之，则尤蹲踞其中，周匝皆菜，仅露头面，撼之始觉。问所以入瓮之由，荡然不知。众猜想移时，莫得其解。久之，尤忽自悟曰：“此必彼狐，请我入瓮耳。”主人求得其故，亦笑而咋舌，曰：“更无可疑矣。”尤一路为狐所弄，愈出愈奇，直入河南界，始获安宁，其后亦无他云。

    Explanation

    627

    Dong, fearing that Yao would be summoned by a demon, also ordered her to return home to get married. They hurried back to the inn and discussed plans to return home. They packed up and set off on the road, staying overnight at an inn. At midnight, Chun Cui suddenly appeared and slept with Yao. Ery heard Yao murmuring and babbling, listening carefully, it sounded like Yao was having intercourse with someone. Ery realized it was another fox haunting and shouted loudly to scare Yao. Yao woke up, but Chun Cui was already gone, though semen had wet the mat. Ery questioned and found out the reason, then cursed loudly.

    After that, she went to bed and suddenly lost Ery. Holding a candle, she searched everywhere and heard snoring coming from a rice jar. The jar was sealed with a clay pot and mud, very tightly. She called the owner to open it. The owner said, "These pickled vegetables are not yet ready, why open it?" Yao said, "There is someone inside, how can we not open it?" The owner was startled and listened, indeed there was snoring. They quickly opened it, and found Ery squatting inside, surrounded by vegetables, only her head and face visible, she only then realized. When asked why she was in the jar, she had no idea. Everyone speculated for a while, but could not figure it out. After a long time, Ery suddenly realized, "This must be that fox, it put me in the jar." The owner understood the reason and also laughed and gasped, saying, "There is no doubt about it." Ery was continuously tricked by the fox all along, the more it went, the more extraordinary it became, until they entered Henan territory, they finally found peace, and after that, there was nothing else.

    628

    兰岩曰：一夕欢爱，天缘已早定之。狐虽摄入洞中，并无伤害意。既已去之，何必仇之，致遭戏弄，尤亦选事人哉！

    　　董　如　彪嵩阳董恒，字建威，以参将褫职家居。年四十余，称雄一乡。性好武勇，所交游悉射皮饮胄、飞苍走黄之人。艳妾六七人，争妍斗媚，以悦一人。第宅复闳壮，园亭之胜甲一邑。园中有池，可容刀。缘池绿围千章，就中构轩五楹，颜曰“万绿”，极宏敞。值夏日，与其俦类讲武其中。其父禁之，弗悛也。父殁，愈不自戢。

    Explanation

    628

    Translation

    Lanyan said: A single night of joy and affection, fate had already determined it. The fox, although it entered the cave, had no intention to harm. Since it has already left, why should one harbor resentment towards it, leading to mockery? Is this not also a person who chooses matters unwisely?

    Dong Heng, courtesy name Jianwei, was a military officer who was stripped of his rank and lived at home. He was over forty years old and was a dominant figure in his village.
