He loved martial prowess and associated with people who engaged in hunting and military activities. He had six to seven beautiful concubines who competed for his favor. His mansion was grand and spacious, and his garden and pavilions were the finest in the county. There was a pool in the garden that could hold a sword. Around the pool, there were thousands of lush green trees, and within this, a five-bay pavilion was built, named "Ten Thousand Greens," which was extremely spacious and magnificent. During the summer, he and his companions practiced martial arts in the pavilion. His father tried to stop him, but he did not change his ways. After his father's death, he became even more unrestrained.

    629

    生二子，长如彪，年十八，次如虎，年十六，皆出侧室。而如彪禀赋与父殊，秀外慧中，尤喜篇什，驰马试剑，非其所好，以故失父爱，鸡肋常遭老拳。家有老仆葛封者，质朴憨直，好强谏，董稍惮之。封有子印儿，年亦十八，为彪、虎馆僮，韶秀慧黠，一家之所钟爱。

    　　适秋高马壮，董率二子及僮仆三十余人，负弩肩枪，呼鹰嗷犬，往猎于山。自辰至申，获禽甚少。兴尽将返。釶一大黑狐窜出草中，董逐射之，连发不中，狐突至如彪马前，逡巡欲遁，董急呼如彪射之，如彪但束手笑，狐遽逸去。董叱曰：“懦弱子何颜甲至此，不畏若辈笑耶？”如彪曰：“家中羊豕甚多，岂必猎食？”董大怒，曰：“小子生为男儿，毫无丈夫气，岂复董建威子耶？汝欲食羊豕，吾偏以汝伺虎狼。”遽喝下马，夺其弧矢，但与一火枪曰：“留汝于此，不得狐勿相见也！”言讫回马。

    Explanation

    629

    Dong Jianwei had two sons, the elder named Roubiao, aged eighteen, and the younger named Ruhu, aged sixteen, both born to concubines. Roubiao was outwardly beautiful and inwardly wise, especially fond of poems and verses, but he did not enjoy riding horses or trying swords. Because of this, he was often subjected to harsh punishment, much like a chicken's rib, which is of little value. There was an old servant named Ge Feng, who was honest and straightforward, good at giving strong advice, and Dong was somewhat afraid of him. Ge Feng had a son named Yiner, aged eighteen, who served as a servant to Roubiao and Ruhu. Yiner was graceful, intelligent, and clever, and was loved by the whole family.

    It was the autumn, and the horses were strong. Dong led his two sons and over thirty servants to hunt in the mountains, carrying crossbows and spears, calling out to eagles and dogs. From morning till late afternoon, they caught very few animals. As their spirits began to wane, they prepared to return. Suddenly, a large black fox darted out from the grass. Dong chased and shot at it, but missed several times. The fox suddenly appeared in front of Roubiao's horse, hesitating before fleeing. Dong quickly called out to Roubiao to shoot it, but Roubiao simply stood by smiling. The fox then fled quickly. Dong scolded, "What kind of weakling are you to be here looking so pitiful? Don't you fear what others will say?" Roubiao replied, "There are plenty of sheep and pigs at home; why must we hunt for food?" Dong became very angry and said, "You were born a man, yet you show no manly spirit. Are you still my son, Dong Jianwei? If you want to eat sheep and pigs, I will make you guard against tigers and wolves!" He immediately ordered Roubiao to dismount, took his bow and arrows, and gave him only a fire gun, saying, "Stay here, and do not see me again unless you catch the fox!" With that, he turned his horse and left.

    630

    葛封弃枪投鞭，涕泣叩马而谏曰：“大郎所言非无理，主人奈何逞一时之怒，则弃之万山之中而不顾乎？且为人之父者，教子于义方，弗导于邪。凡邪嬖之事，无足为子孙效法者，主人自为之则已矣，何必戕贼大郎，欲其济恶，而不欲其斡蛊也哉？”董怒曰：“汝病狂耶？胡为悖逆至此！”对曰：“老奴不悖，主不自知其非耳。夫人之所以修身齐家者，仁也，孝也，慈也，悌也。

    Explanation

    630

    Ge Feng threw away his weapons and knelt before the horse, weeping and pleading, "What my younger brother says is not unreasonable. How can the master indulge in a momentary fit of anger and abandon him in the mountains without a second thought? As a father, one should guide one's son on the path of righteousness and not lead him astray. All immoral and corrupt actions are unworthy of being emulated by future generations. The master should do as he pleases, but why harm my younger brother to further his own evil ends, rather than protecting him from corruption?" Dong angrily replied, "Are you mad? Why have you gone so far as to defy me?" Ge Feng answered, "I am not defying you, master. It is you who do not realize your own faults. The reason why people cultivate themselves and manage their families is through benevolence, filial piety, kindness, and fraternal respect."

    631

    今主日以杀兽获禽为乐，不体上天好生之心，可谓仁乎？父死未葬，爱及田游，可谓孝乎？弃弱子于荒山，以餍麋鹿，可谓慈乎？二郎旁观，不发一言劝止，岂教之以悌之义乎？使大郎有罪，主人且当分谤；矧其无罪，弃之何名？”董怒发如雷，马箠乱下如雨，封头面皆破，流血满衣，释手而退。董遂纵辔出山，众人毕从。封大骂众人，助纣为虐，一何丧心。乃呼印儿而嘱之曰：“汝其追随大郎，生死与共，吾耄矣，无能为役。俾大郎得狐而返，不致他变，则汝亦当如汉帝列侯得功狗矣。不然，即此永诀耳！”唏嘘上马，连促令去。

    631

    Translation:

    Today, the master finds joy in killing animals and capturing birds, not embodying the benevolent heart of heaven; can this be called benevolence? The father has not yet been buried, yet he loves hunting in the fields; can this be called filial piety? Abandoning the weak child in the wilderness to satisfy the deer; can this be called kindness? Second Brother watches in silence, not uttering a word to stop him; is this teaching the virtue of fraternal duty? If Great Brother has committed a crime, the master should share the blame; especially if he is innocent, what name can be given to abandoning him? Dong's anger was like thunder, and he beat the whip down like rain; Feng's head and face were all broken, and his clothes were stained with blood; he let go and stepped back. Dong then loosened the reins and left the mountain, everyone followed. Feng loudly cursed everyone, accusing them of aiding the wicked, how utterly heartless! He then called Yin'er and instructed him, saying: "You must follow Great Brother, sharing life and death with him. I am old now, unable to serve. If Great Brother can catch the fox and return, there will be no other changes; then you will also be like the Han Emperor and the Marquises who gained a loyal dog as a reward. Otherwise, this will be our final farewell!" He sighed, mounted his horse, and urged them to go on.

    632

    印儿踊跃而去，见如彪于岩下，方倚枪而泣，印儿慰藉之。如彪得伴，殊慰，相与觅狐，杳不可得。既而苍然暮色，自远而近，渐无所见。四山清寂，繁星满天。树响水鸣，狼奔鸱叫。二人蹲伏石畔，恇怯殊甚。

    　　久之，月出峰巅，烟笼涧壑，依稀有数人循岸径来，相去一矢地，谛之，非人，夜叉也。敦脄血拇，齿稫稫如锯，鹊行鹗顾，目光睒闪，气息咻咻。如彪战栗俯伏，屏息不敢动。印儿低语曰：“怪物非一，此间非藏身所，不如升彼高树，庶几免患。”如彪曰：“素未娴习，焉能升树？汝速自为计，明日收吾骨焉。稍迟回，即成两毙，转非汝父付托之意。”印儿不得已，潜登一松，自浓密处，垂首下观，历历皆辨。一夜叉行至石畔，蓦见如彪，遽滚地风旋，良久始定，拊膺而踊，若甚惊怪，作声呜呜，余者闻声毕集。一夜叉蹲地上，耸其背，一夜叉提如彪腰胯，置其上，负之而去。

    Explanation

    632

    Yin'er eagerly went away and met Rou Biao at the foot of the rock. Rou Biao was leaning on his spear and weeping, and Yin'er consoled him. Rou Biao, finding a companion, was greatly comforted, and together they searched for a fox, but could not find it. Soon, the gloomy evening twilight came from afar and approached, gradually obscuring everything. The four mountains were quiet and desolate, with stars filling the sky. The trees rustled, the water murmured, and wolves ran while owls hooted. The two crouched by the side of a rock, extremely fearful.

    After a while, the moon rose over the peak of the mountain, and the valleys and ravines were enveloped by smoke. Vaguely, several people were faintly coming along the riverbank path, a bow's length away. Upon closer inspection, they were not human but demons. They had thick shoulders and bloody fingers, sharp teeth like a saw, walked like a magpie, and looked like an eagle. Their eyes flashed, and they breathed noisily. Rou Biao shivered and crouched, holding his breath and not moving. Yin'er whispered, "There are not just one monster here, and this place is not a place to hide. It would be better to climb that tall tree to avoid danger." Rou Biao replied, "I am not skilled at climbing trees, how can I climb one? You should quickly make your own plan. Tomorrow, my bones will be gathered. If we delay even a little, we will both die, which would not be in line with my father's intention." Yin'er, unwillingly, secretly climbed a pine tree, choosing a dense place, lowered his head, and looked down, seeing everything clearly. One demon came to the side of the rock, suddenly saw Rou Biao, and rolled on the ground, spinning like the wind, for a long time before finally settling. The demon clutched his chest and jumped, appearing very surprised and strange, making a moaning sound. The others heard the sound and gathered. One demon crouched on the ground, hunched his back, while another demon lifted Rou Biao's waist and placed him on his back, carrying him away.

    633

    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Explanation

    633

    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Translation:
    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Final Translation:
    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Final Translation:
    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Final Translation:
    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体甚伟岸。趋跄其下者，又不下数十人，皆不作夜叉形。又见诸野兽，如虎豹、如熊罴、如豺狼獐鹿狐免者，纷纷庙外，何止千百头。夜叉置如彪于阶，蒲伏而出，似极震慑。右坐者曰：“董恒恣虐不仁，冥报在迩，今乃忍弃其子，亟当先杀之，以抑众怒。

    Final Translation:
    印儿心胆坠地，忽下树密觇向往。历数稥稤，卒至一破庙前，有夜叉甚伙，皆拱立庙侧。后数大树皆参天，印儿复缘其上。隐隐见庙中有二人，一左一右，正面坐；又有数人列坐，衣冠奇古，身体

    634

    ”列坐一人曰：“不可！董恒虽恶，其子无罪，且一言梗父，有止杀之心。罪人不孥，不肖子犹将宥之，况如彪贤子乎？”右坐者曰：“然则将何以处之？”列坐者曰：“不如释之，上以体上帝好生之仁，下以行明公恤刑之惠。至于报德报怨，自有主者，非吾曹事也。”右坐者曰：“参军之言是也。”命夜叉仍负之去，置故处。夜叉方举趾，即有一老人跪阶下启曰：“适承明谕，报德报怨，自有主者，董如彪与臣有恩，请主之。”右坐者曰：“可。”老人叩谢负如彪而出，蹒跚东去。

    Explanation

    634

    One seated in a row said, "It cannot be done! Although Dong Heng was wicked, his son is innocent, and moreover, he has the intention to stop the killing with a single word that obstructs his father. Punish the guilty, not the innocent; even a wayward son should still be pardoned, how much more so a virtuous son like Biǎo?" The one seated to the right said, "Then what shall we do with him?" The one seated in a row replied, "It would be better to release him, to uphold the divine benevolence of cherishing life and to carry out the wise public official's mercy in punishment. As for repaying kindness and resentment, there are those who are responsible; it is not our business." The one seated to the right said, "The words of the military officer are correct." He ordered the night demon to carry him away again and place him in his former place. As the night demon was about to lift his foot, an old man knelt at the steps and said, "Just received your clear instruction, repaying kindness and resentment, there are those who are responsible, Dong Rúbiāo has shown kindness to me, please be responsible for it." The one seated to the right said, "Agreed." The old man knelt in gratitude and carried Dong Rúbiāo out, slowly and unsteadily going east.

    635

    印儿下树尾之，越险履稫崎数里，抵一洞口，老人欲入，忽回首见印儿，讶曰：“尔何为者？”印儿曰：“偶迷路，欲觅一宿耳。”老人曰：“此间非子所宜至，宿愈不可。”印儿曰：“主人被负至此，予将安归乎？”老人熟视曰：“得无见诳？”印儿曰：“如其不然，予纵好事，亦不当深山暮夜，涉险绐人。”老人点首曰：“此说大有理，不复汝疑，但从我行，保汝主仆得啖饭处。”因同入洞，洞中黝暗，颇不易行。凡数折，忽大开朗，平衍广阔，虽戴石履土，而回廊曲室，无所不备。

    Explanation

    635

    The child followed the old man down the tree, crossing dangerous and rugged terrain for several miles, and arrived at a cave entrance. The old man wanted to enter, but suddenly turned around and saw the child. He was surprised and asked, "Who are you?" The child replied, "I got lost and am looking for a place to stay for the night." The old man said, "This place is not suitable for you, and staying overnight is even more inappropriate." The child said, "My master was brought here against his will. Where shall I go?" The old man looked carefully and asked, "Are you not deceiving me?" The child said, "If it is not so, even if I am fond of exploring, I should not take risks and deceive others in the deep mountains at night." The old man nodded and said, "This makes sense. I will no longer doubt you. Just follow me, and I will ensure that you and your master find a place to eat." They then entered the cave together. The cave was dark and gloomy, making it quite difficult to move around. After several turns, the cave suddenly became very open and bright, flat and spacious. Even with stones on the head and earth on the feet, the area had circular corridors and winding rooms, indicating a well-structured space.

    636

    男女数十人，聚候于庭，见负如彪至，莫不欣慰，争来扶掖，安顿榻上。饮以朱砂汤，如彪神气始复，双眼微开，印儿遽遂拥之泣曰：“大郎苏矣，勿惊。”如彪见印儿，矍然起坐，问此何地，岂其梦中耶？印儿哽咽告之，老人曰：“此洞天也，隔绝人世，不知其几由旬，欲归不得矣。汝止此，无徒悲。”如彪拜问出处。老人自称胡叟，“儿女顽劣，不计利害，非子仁者开一面之罗，则此时肝脑涂地矣。”如彪故颖悟，便知即日间所纵之狐也。自念既有施于彼，住亦无患，密语印儿，印儿亦恍然，遂相安，不以为异。

    Explanation

    636

    A group of men and women, numbering in the dozens, gathered in the courtyard, awaiting the arrival of someone being carried like a tiger. Upon seeing him, they were all relieved and rushed to support him, settling him on a couch. They gave him a drink of cinnabar soup, and his spirit began to return; his eyes slowly opened. Yin'er quickly embraced him and wept, saying, "Big Brother has woken up, do not be alarmed." Ru Bi saw Yin'er and suddenly sat up, asking where they were, wondering if this was a dream. Yin'er choked back tears and told him. The old man said, "This is a fairyland, isolated from the world, unknown how many miles away, and you cannot return anymore. Stay here, do not just grieve." Ru Bi bowed and asked about the origin. The old man introduced himself as Hu Shou, saying, "My children are mischievous and do not consider the consequences; it was not for your kindness that I opened a door, otherwise, they would have been in a dire situation." Ru Bi, naturally intelligent, understood that it was the fox released that day. Thinking that he had already done something for them, he realized that staying here would be without worry. He whispered to Yin'er, and Yin'er also understood. They then settled down, not finding it strange.

    637

    日渐惯熟，虽闺人亦不相避忌。叟二女，长曰阿笋，身小而洁白如玉，媚曼双绝，为九姻所重；次曰阿嫩，修眉细目而微麻，婉妙殊甚。叟议以一女妻如彪，而莫决谁可，胡媪曰：“盍效法古人，以红丝系女腕，而棼其头绪，令董郎随意牵其一，为宝窗之选。”叟曰：“是或一道也。”阿笋止之曰：“董郎有大恩于妹，以妹家之，情理兼尽，谁曰不宜？”叟拊髀曰：“此不易之论也，夫复何疑。第如汝之能让，亦有足多者。”笋含羞而退，于是以嫩归如彪，举室艳羡，以为玉蕊璚英，天然佳偶也。

    Explanation

    637

    Gradually becoming accustomed, even the women of the household no longer avoided or were cautious of each other. The old man had two daughters: the elder was named Ah Sun, small in stature but pure and white like jade, graceful and charming, highly valued by all the family members; the younger was named Ah Nen, with long, slender eyebrows, fine eyes, and a slight mottling, extremely gentle and beautiful. The old man discussed marrying one of his daughters to Ru Biao but could not decide which one was suitable. Grandma Hu suggested, "Why not follow the example of ancient people, tying a red thread around the wrists of the girls, tangling the threads, and letting Dong Lang pull any one of them at will, as the chosen one for the precious window?" The old man said, "That might be a way." Ah Sun stopped him and said, "Dong Lang has done a great favor for my sister. Marrying her off to him fulfills both emotional and rational considerations. Who would say it is inappropriate?" The old man patted his thigh and said, "This is an unchangeable opinion; what more is there to doubt? But your willingness to yield is also commendable." Ah Sun withdrew with a blush. Thus, Ah Nen was married to Ru Biao, and the whole household admired and envied, considering her a naturally good match, like a precious gem.

    638

    笋酷好咏吟，时时如彪夫妇，相与谈诗，或分笺拈韵，共相唱和。如彪尝盗小婢，为嫩所执，戏令长跪，而批其颊，诸婢传以为笑。笋谑之以诗曰：“鹣鹣比翼鸟，一夕忽分单。夜静更深后，鹤行鹭伏前。雪肤依草荐，玉掌示蒲鞭。俯首天生气，郎当犊鼻边。”如彪见诗笑曰：“阿姨可谓揣摩到家矣，然而尚有未尽处，试为足之。”乃和而返之曰：“垂成事忽败，肘膝赴床前。方寸痴如醉，双腮热似燃。夜深孤鸟动，春老一蚕眠。不杀刑犹酷，飞凫压两肩。”笋展诵一遍，衔袖而笑。

    Explanation

    638

    Snack deeply loves to recite poetry and often discusses poetry with Like Tiger and Young. They sometimes divide paper and choose rhymes, exchanging poems back and forth. Like Tiger once stole a young servant girl, and Young caught him, playfully ordering him to kneel for a long time and slapping his cheek. The other maids passed it around as a joke. Snack responded with a poem: "The鹣鹣比翼鸟, one night suddenly separated. In the deep night, a lone bird moves, with elbows and knees rushing to the bed. The white skin lies on grass, the delicate hands show a reed whip. Bowing down with a natural spirit, a man with a loose shirt and a calf's nose." Like Tiger read the poem once and laughed, saying, "Auntie can be said to have mastered the art of understanding, but there is still something not fully captured, let me add to it." He then responded with a poem: "A nearly completed matter suddenly fails, elbows and knees rush to the bed. The heart is as crazy as drunk, the cheeks are hot like burning. In the deep night, a lone bird moves, in the late spring, a silkworm sleeps. Without killing, the punishment is still cruel, a flying duck presses on both shoulders." Snack read the poem once and held his sleeve, laughing.

    639

    嫩怒之以目，曰：“子无伎俩偷香，奈何以我解嘲？”如彪曰：“句句实，字字真，岂有虚假？”嫩曰：“字经三写，乌焉成马。况事已隔日，汝等诗人更多附会，往往诬妄好人，那足为凭！心正何怕眼斜，一任汝曹喋喋！”笋曰：“妹以阃威自鸣得意，妹夫又口有雌黄，皆非儿所当究。但借此作一诗题，聊以破闷耳。”嫩戏拍其肩曰：“姊姊作奇想，便强使人削趾适履，独不念隔膜之词，传之悠久，徒为乱真之赝乎？亟当自忏，勿泄于人！”笋笑曰：“妮子包羞矣。既出软语，姑置之。”遂裂诗于烛上焚之，欢而散。自此与如彪相狎，无所不至，但不及乱耳。

    Explanation

    639

    嫩 gave her a stern look and said, "You have no skill in seduction, how dare you use me to mock?" As Biǎo replied, "Every word is true, every character genuine, how could there be any falsehood?" Nèn retorted, "Words change with repeated copying, even 'Wu' becomes 'Ma.' Moreover, the incident has already occurred a day ago. You poets tend to embellish more, often falsely accusing good people. How can that be relied upon! If one's heart is upright, what do they fear from a wandering gaze? Just let you chatter on!" Sǔn said, "My sister boasts of her authority, and my sister-in-law speaks recklessly; neither is something I should investigate. I merely borrowed this as a poetic theme to relieve my boredom." Nèn playfully patted her shoulder and said, "Sister, you have such a strange idea, forcing someone to fit awkwardly, don't you think that irrelevant words, passed down over time, will only become false imitations? You should repent immediately and not reveal it to others!" Sǔn laughed and said, "You bear the shame, my dear. Since you've spoken gently, I'll let it go." She then tore the poem and burned it on the candle, and they parted in good spirits. From then on, she and As Biǎo became intimate, going everywhere together, but they did not go too far.

    640

    一日，姊妹同往舅家，翁央印儿为御。笋于碧纱中，见其韶秀，归制《如梦令》辞曰：“掷果潘郎风味，傅粉何郎风致。底事不同车，忍作执鞭之士？留意，留意，留意询伊名字。”既而出户，疏于防检。适嫩携如彪来，得辞竞观。嫩笑曰：“儿今日又得诗题矣。”遂擘笺和之曰：“渐识石榴滋味，蓦见莲花标致。有女正怀春，谁是诱之之士？留意，留意，留意印儿名字。”如彪方欲捉笔，笋已归室，过窗下，闻窗内折纸戢戢，磨墨隆隆，猛忆诗笺未收，急入视，嫩已睨之而笑矣。笋羞涩无以自容，嫩曰：“知姊又得一诗题，故来相贺。”因以和词示之。笋大惭，二人戏语间杂，良久始去。叟闻风笑曰：“婢子下流，乃悦及舆夫耶？吾不可效王郑之所为，致儿女子憔悴以死。”即择吉以印儿赘笋。

    Explanation

    640

    One day, the sisters went together to their uncle's house, and the uncle entrusted Print to serve as a driver. Sprout, from behind the green silk screen, saw her beauty and grace, and upon returning, composed a poem titled "Like a Dream" which went as follows: "The flavor of Pan Yue, who was showered with pears; the elegance of He Zhi, who used white powder. Why not ride in the same carriage? How can one be content to be a mere driver? Pay attention, pay attention, and ask for her name." Afterward, she went out the door, being careless in guarding against something. Happened Nen carrying Ru Biao came, and they looked at the poem together. Nen laughed and said, "Today I gained another poetic subject." Then she took out a sheet of paper and responded with a poem: "Gradually recognizing the taste of pomegranates, suddenly seeing the beauty of a lotus flower. There is a girl in the throes of love, who is the one to seduce her? Pay attention, pay attention, and ask for Print's name." As Ru Biao was about to take out a pen, Sprout had already returned to her room. Passing by the window, she heard the sound of folding paper and grinding ink inside. Suddenly remembering that the poem paper was not collected, she hurriedly went in to see, and Nen had already looked at her sidelong and laughed. Sprout, bashful and unable to bear to show herself, Nen said, "Knowing the elder sister gained another poetic subject, so came to congratulate her." Therefore, she showed the response poem to her. Sprout greatly ashamed, the two of them jokingly conversed, after a long time, they finally left. The elder heard the news and laughed, saying, "A maidservant of lowly status, and likes a carriage driver? I cannot imitate the actions of Wang and Zheng, leading to children and women wasting away and dying." Immediately chose an auspicious day to marry Print to Sprout.

    641

    居久之，叟谓如彪曰：“子二人可以归省矣。”如彪虑父不容，叟曰：“虽欲不容，岂可得乎？二女任携之去，第无所赠，实为可愧耳。”是日置酒为饯，唯一小驷驾巾车，命四人乘之，行甚驶，转瞬已失洞之所在。并无执辔者，而小驷不须鞭策，循路委折，直抵家门，宛若熟路。四人下车，小驷自返，入门，一家惊以为鬼物，又见二女之艳，弥各诧异。印儿备述颠末，家人始定，争为泣告曰：“大郎在外二载余，岂知家中一败涂地。

    Explanation

    641

    After a long time, the old man said to Ru Biao, "You two can go home to visit now." Ru Biao was worried that his father would not allow it, but the old man replied, "Even if he wants not to, how can he prevent it? The two girls can take them away. But we have nothing to give, which is truly embarrassing." On that day, they set up a farewell banquet and had only a small carriage drawn by a single horse. They ordered the four people to ride it. The journey was very fast, and in an instant, the cave was lost. There was no one holding the reins, and the small horse did not need to be whipped or spurred. It followed the winding road and arrived directly at the family gate, as if it were a familiar path. The four people got off the carriage, and the small horse returned on its own. Upon entering the house, the family was shocked and thought it was a ghost. They also saw the beauty of the two girls. Yin'er recounted the entire story, and the family finally calmed down. They all cried and told him, "Big Brother has been away for more than two years. How could he know that the family was in ruins?"

    642

    主人自弃大郎，归来三日，即捐馆矣。二郎病癫痫，接踵而殁。唯葛封于一月前，自云上帝命为某山之神，是夜无疾而逝。房中诸姨，均已改醮。奴婢之所以不致星散者，徒以有大郎生母在耳。”如彪大恸，登堂拜母，引罪自伤。母曰：“儿见弃于父，罪不独归也。今得妇而返，殊慰老身。”又念葛封之忠，印儿之义，养为己子，更名如麟。二女事姑极孝，家资十倍于昔。各生一子一女。亲故知为狐育，无肯结婚者。男娶女嫁，皆求之于远方。

    　　后十余年，母死。殡葬之礼，哀祭皆尽。既服阕，如彪悉以田宅分属二子，同如麟复从二女入山，遂不复返。其亲多言狐女别无异人处，唯衣不更新，亦不旧敝，面貌常如十八九岁人，善食鸡肉，嗜火酒，为可异耳。又言其姣媚处，见之者无不狂惑失志。所遗子女虽美，然较其母，百不能逮也。

    Explanation

    642

    The master abandoned his eldest son, returned home after three days, and then passed away. The second son fell ill with epilepsy and followed in death shortly after. Only Ge Feng, a servant, claimed a month before that he was appointed by God as a god of a certain mountain, and that night he died without any illness. The various aunts in the household had all remarried. The reason the servants did not disperse was merely because the eldest son's biological mother was still alive. Ruo Biao mourned deeply, went to his mother's room, and took responsibility for his faults. His mother said, "You were abandoned by your father, but the blame is not solely on one person. Now that you have returned with a wife, I am greatly comforted." She also remembered Ge Feng's loyalty and Yin Er's righteousness, adopting them as her own sons and renaming them Ruo Lin. The two daughters served their mother-in-law with great filial piety, and the family's wealth increased tenfold. Each of them had a son and a daughter. Relatives and acquaintances knew they were raised by fox spirits and were unwilling to marry them. The men sought wives and the women sought husbands from distant places.

    After more than ten years, the mother passed away. The funeral rites were performed with great sorrow and mourning. After the mourning period, Ruo Biao divided all the land and property among his two sons and accompanied Ruo Lin to rejoin the two daughters in the mountains, never to return. Many of his relatives said that the fox-raised women were not different from ordinary people in any other way, except that their clothes did not need to be replaced and did not wear out, their appearance always looked like that of a person in their late teens, they enjoyed eating chicken and drinking fire wine, which was quite strange. They also said that their beauty was captivating, and those who saw them were all crazed and lost their way. The children they left behind were beautiful, but compared to their mothers, they could not match them in a hundred ways.

    643

    兰岩曰：董恃财自恣，弃子拒谏，可谓不慈矣。身死家败，立见销亡，非冥报乎？印儿从如彪于万山中，历涉危险，虽死不避，忠义可嘉，其获佳丽于意外，不亦宜哉？

    　　某　别　驾某别驾之任岭南，值大雨，借馆于山左许氏家。许故大户，宅第深广。书舍后朱楼五楹，别驾欲下榻其上，许有难色。别驾固请，许踌躇久之，始曰：“下榻固无防，但楼中所有什物，幸勿移置也。”别驾敬诺。许置酒相款，至二更，乃命烛导别驾登楼，郑重而去。别驾环视楼中，一切箱柜几案，琴书妆奁，床帐等物，无不整洁。别驾默念：“此必主人闺秀所居，乃是曲房宴私之处，以我力请下榻，故尔曲意腾那，其谊亦良厚矣。事出冒昧，心中不安。翌日会须厚馈，以酬其情也。”筹计更余，始就寝，启帐视之，见翠被绣衾，麝三芬馥，心愈　然，然无如之何，姑就寝。

    Explanation

    643

    Lan Yan said: Dong relied on his wealth to act recklessly, abandoned his child, and rejected advice; he can be called uncaring. His death and the destruction of his family were immediate, is this not a retribution from the afterlife? Yin'er followed Ruobiao through the mountains, enduring dangers, and though death was not avoided, his loyalty and righteousness are commendable. That he unexpectedly gained a beautiful wife is fitting.

    A certain local official was appointed to a position in Lingnan. During heavy rain, he stayed at Xu's house in the eastern mountains. Xu was a wealthy family with a deep and extensive residence. Behind the study, there was a five-bay red pavilion. The local official wanted to stay there, but Xu hesitated. The local official insisted, and after much deliberation, Xu finally said, "Staying here is certainly fine, but please do not move any of the items in the pavilion." The local official respectfully agreed. Xu hosted a banquet and, at midnight, lit candles to guide the local official to the pavilion, then left with great care. The local official looked around the pavilion and saw that all the cabinets, desks, books, makeup cases, beds, and curtains were neatly arranged. He silently thought, "This must be the room of the owner's daughter, a place for private gatherings. I insisted on staying here, so he went to such lengths to accommodate me; his kindness is truly great. I acted rashly, and I feel uneasy. Tomorrow, I will certainly give him a generous gift to repay his hospitality." After pondering for a while, he finally went to bed, opened the curtain, and saw a green quilt and embroidered coverlet, filled with the fragrance of musk. He felt a mix of emotions but could do nothing about it, so he went to sleep.

    644

    辗转间，恍闻履声藉藉，心异之。伏枕潜窥，见一女子，丽甚，年约十六七，衣裳槁素，就几上剪烛，开镜匣，作晚妆，盥漱讫，徐徐尽缓结束，置诸椸枷；独留亵衣数事，置诸薰笼；焚香易履，即移烛启帐，上床。一足甫入衾，别驾神荡已久，遽以手握之。女骤惊，戛然一声，破窗而去。急起索衣，杳无所见。窗纸如故，衣饰亦亡。别驾始悟非人，大声急呼，僮仆毕至，亟起主人而告之，并诘其故。

    Explanation

    644

    In the midst of turning, he vaguely heard the muffled sound of footsteps, which puzzled his mind. Crouching in bed and secretly peeping, he saw a woman, extremely beautiful, around sixteen or seventeen years old, with simple, plain clothing. She went to the desk to trim the candle, opened the mirror case, applied evening makeup, washed and rinsed, then slowly and carefully finished dressing, placing her clothes on the clothes rack; she left behind only a few undergarments, placing them in the incense box. She burned incense and changed shoes, then immediately moved the candle and lifted the curtain, getting into bed. Just as one foot entered the quilt, the governor's assistant had been entranced for a long time and suddenly grabbed her hand. The woman was suddenly startled, making a sharp, sudden sound, and broke the window to leave. He quickly got up to look for clothes, but nowhere could he see her. The window paper was as before, and the clothes and ornaments were also gone. The governor's assistant finally realized it was not a human, and shouted loudly and urgently. All the servants arrived, and he quickly woke the master and told him, questioning the reason.

    645

    许始而愕然，继而愀然，既而泣然，曰：“客长者矣，诚以实告。小人有妹，色艺俱不下人。许字同里吴孝廉之少子江，未嫁而江短命。妹誓不更嫁，屏居此楼，日唯事书画自遣。前岁季秋，年甫十八，病不起，遗嘱母氏曰：”儿死亦不下此楼矣，望母勿忘珍爱，勿撤床第，凡夙昔玩习之物，妆奁之具，悉位置如生前。‘嘱讫即瞑。母不忍拂其意，悉如所嘱，迄今阅二年矣。昨公欲下榻于此，小人所以犹豫者，职此故也。后思人亡已久，似无事涉嫌，故不敢方公命。讵意贞魂未灭，亵渎贵人，骤聆所言，并详容色，的是亡妹。惊定悲生，老母闻之，尤虞痛绝耳。“别驾拊案而叹，心惋鼻酸，吊之以诗，奠之以酒，详志里居，并书姓氏，诘朝辞去。至于任所，下车伊始，即为请旌于抚军。抚军亦为感动，第未审其后果能旌表否也。逢书农能为悉述之。

    Explanation

    645

    The man was initially startled, then saddened, and finally wept, saying: "Sir, you are a gentleman indeed; I will tell you the truth. I have a younger sister, whose appearance and talent are no less than others. She was engaged to the younger son of Mr. Wu, a scholar from our village, named Jiang. Before she could marry, Jiang died young. My sister swore never to remarry and lived in seclusion in this building, spending her days engaged in painting and calligraphy to pass the time. Last autumn, at the age of eighteen, she fell ill and did not recover. On her deathbed, she left instructions to her mother: 'Even after my death, I will not leave this building. I hope that you will not forget to cherish it, do not remove the bed and bedding, and all the items I used to play with and the makeup and dowry items should all be placed as they were in my lifetime.' After giving these instructions, she passed away. Her mother could not go against her wishes and followed all her instructions. To this day, it has been two years. Yesterday, you wanted to stay here, and the reason I hesitated was due to this matter. Later, thinking that she had been dead for a long time, it seemed there was nothing to suspect, so I dared not refuse your command. Who would have thought that her pure spirit was still alive, offending a noble person. Suddenly hearing what she said and observing her appearance, it is indeed my deceased sister. After the initial shock, sorrow arose. The old mother heard about it and especially feared she would be devastated." The deputy governor tapped the table and sighed, feeling sorrow in his heart and tears in his nose. He paid tribute with a poem and offered wine as a libation, detailed the place of residence, and wrote the surname. He departed the next morning. Upon arriving at his post, he immediately requested commendation from the governor. The governor was also moved, but it is uncertain whether it will be officially recognized. Feng Shunan was able to recount it in detail.

    646

    闲斋曰：未嫁而能守志，不奇；奇在身死而鬼犹守志也。第贞烈之性，生得全归，而一行作鬼，乃为人窥素体，捉纤足，鬼而有知，吾恐自伤有污，必将投环而复作贞洁之鄕耳。

    Explanation

    646

    The Idle Study says: A woman who can uphold her resolve before marriage is not remarkable; what is remarkable is that after her death, her spirit still upholds her resolve. The virtue of chastity and loyalty, when achieved in life, is a full and honorable return to her family. Yet, once she becomes a ghost, she is seen by others in her pure body, with her delicate feet touched. If the ghost is aware, I fear she will feel ashamed of being defiled and will surely throw herself into the ring again to return to the place of purity.

    647

    兰岩曰：贞烈之魂，金石并永，询不诬也。嗟乎！香奁粉匣，犹存昔日之精神；冷雨凄风，独受今兹之悲楚。空楼阒寂，独往独来；尘境萧条，自嗟自感。详其姓氏，志厥里居，请而旌之，庶可以勉贞魂也夫！

    　　双髻道人酆都市上有道人，面黑而髯，身而瘦。不详其姓氏里居，亦不详其年岁。或曰：“听其语音，似湖湘人。”或曰：“似河南。”“似成都。”悉不可必。以形求之，常绾双髻，咸以双髻道人呼之。县有富人吕氏，生七子二女，同居各爨，有贾者、客者、从军者、游而惰者，无足纪述。惟六子骅，纳粟为太学生，少年任侠，尤癖好符咒之事。平居购求秘书，盈囊累笈，终日闭门检阅，硃笔黄纸，与香烛错列，夜间戟指禹步。一家莫测所为，唯二妹附和之，而卒无一成，殊为郁结。

    Explanation

    647

    兰岩说：贞烈之魂，金石并永，确实不虚。唉！香奁粉匣，仍存昔日之精神；冷雨凄风，独受今兹之悲楚。空楼阒寂，独往独来；尘境萧条，自嗟自感。详其姓氏，志厥里居，请而旌之，庶可以勉贞魂也夫！

    　　酆都城上有一位双髻道人，面黑而髯，身瘦。不详其姓氏里居，亦不详其年岁。有人说：“听其语音，似湖湘人。”有人说：“似河南人。”“似成都人。”皆不可定。以形求之，常绾双髻，皆以双髻道人呼之。县有富人吕氏，生七子二女，同居各爨，有贾者、客者、从军者、游而惰者，无足纪述。惟六子骅，纳粟为太学生，少年任侠，尤癖好符咒之事。平居购求秘书，盈囊累笈，终日闭门检阅，硃笔黄纸，与香烛错列，夜间戟指禹步。一家莫测所为，唯二妹附和之，而卒无一成，殊为郁结。

    648

    一日，游平都山，偕徐、邵二友过市，见道人立坊下，遮道谓骅曰：“诸郎雅游，能携我一行否？”骅难之曰：“马止于三，先生岂可独步，与厮仆伍？”道人曰：“郎第行，勿为我虑。”骅及徐、邵并辔往，既至，道人已先在。骅问来何速，道人曰：“由捷径耳。”骅颇疑之。酒半，邵言其先人官九江时，每游庐山，熟闻其名胜，恨远不能至。道人曰：“诸郎有庐山在念耶，盍即此一往游之？”徐、邵咸笑其诞，骅独欣然愿往。

    Explanation

    648

    One day, Hua toured Pingdu Mountain and went with his friends Xu and Shao through the market. They saw a Taoist standing at the gate, blocking the path and saying to Hua, "Gentlemen of refined tastes, can you carry me along?" Hua found it difficult to answer, saying, "The horses are limited to three; Sir, how can you walk alone, mingling with servants?" The Taoist said, "Just go ahead, do not worry about me." Hua and his friends rode on, and the Taoist was already there when they arrived. Hua asked why he came so quickly, and the Taoist said, "I took a shortcut." Hua was somewhat doubtful about this.

    Halfway through the wine, Shao spoke of his ancestor when he served in Jiujiang, every time he toured Mount Lu, he heard of its famous scenic spots, but regretted that it was too far to reach. The Taoist said, "Why not take this opportunity to go and tour it?" Xu and Shao all laughed at his absurdity, but Hua was delighted and willing to go.

    649

    道人令闭目，去其履襪，以指蘸唾书符于两跖，喝曰：“起！”便觉两耳风涛汹涌之声，一食顷，足已践地，开眼见白云满衣，罡风砭骨，盖已立五峰绝顶。道人曳之，并坐石上，以袖拂之，风定云开。俯瞰下方，一目千里，诸山扑地如培砄，湖光一片。康郎、大姑，似螺嵌冰盘；万点风帆，若蝇矢集镜；绕山诸郡县，尽作碧烟数点，历历可指。道人曰：“子知之乎？此庐山极巅也。值此亦有出尘之想否？则生斯世，凡百可为，若能登最上乘，斯不负精力。况神仙一道，又子昌歜、羊枣之嗜，诚所谓一求便得者。子其留意，时哉弗可失也。”骅不觉自投于地，涕泗交流，千万首肯。既而道人曰：“可以归矣。”仍前摄以归。

    Explanation

    649

    The Taoist priest commanded him to close his eyes, removed his shoes and socks, and dipped his finger in saliva to draw a talisman on the soles of his feet. He shouted, "Rise!" and Hua immediately felt a rushing wind in his ears. After a meal's worth of time, his feet had already touched the ground. He opened his eyes to see white clouds covering his clothes, and a strong, piercing wind that felt like it cut through his bones. It turned out they were standing at the summit of the Five Peaks. The Taoist priest pulled him, and they sat down on the stone together. He wiped the wind away with his sleeve, and the wind stopped and the clouds cleared. Looking down below, Hua saw a thousand miles in one glance, the mountains lying flat like mounds of earth, and a vast expanse of lake water. The islands of Kanglang and Dagū resembled snails embedded in an ice plate, and thousands of sails looked like flies gathered on a mirror. The counties and cities around the mountains were all seen as a few points of green smoke, clearly visible. The Taoist priest said, "Do you know this? This is the extreme summit of Mount Lu. In such a place, do you have any thoughts of transcending the mundane world? To live in this world, anything can be done, but if you can ascend to the highest level, you will not waste your energy. Especially the path to becoming a god or immortal, it is like your deep-seated passion for something, truly what is meant by something that is easily obtained when sought. You should pay attention, the time is now, do not miss it." Hua could not help but fall to the ground, tears and snot flowing, nodding thousands of times. The Taoist priest then said, "It is time to return." As before, he took him back.

    650

    徐、邵但见其闭目久坐耳。骅至家，延道士入厅，跪而拂席，膝行再拜曰：“始吾以先生为一邑之狂人也，乃今而后知先生为当世之仙人也。愿委贽为弟子，肯收录否？”道人曰：“小郎之志则大矣，心则诚矣，然而时未至也。”骅曰：“传数奇术异法，先为入道之门，庶不虚此良缘也。”道人筮之，吉，乃许之。骅大喜，呼二妹出拜。净后园精舍三楹，以居道人，与二妹受法，日夜练习，妻妾亦不得面。道人又淫其妹曰：“吾将使二仙姬怀仙胎也。”半年后，道人或去或来，骅与二妹，亦时夜出，达旦始返。骅面色日渐青白，二目瞠然，能登云作雾、唤雨呼风、召神役鬼等术。其妻屡诫，勿炫于人。

    Explanation

    650

    Xu and Shao only saw him closing his eyes and sitting for a long time. When Hua returned home, he invited the Taoist into the hall, knelt and swept the mat, then knelt and bowed twice, saying, "At first, I thought you were a madman of this town, but now I know you are a true immortal of our time. I wish to present myself as your disciple, will you accept me?" The Taoist replied, "Young master, your ambition is great and your heart is sincere, but the time is not yet right." Hua said, "Teach me some extraordinary skills and methods, to open the door to the path of Dao. This way, I will not waste this good opportunity." The Taoist divined and it was auspicious, so he agreed. Hua was greatly delighted and called out his two sisters to bow. He cleaned up three rooms in the back garden to house the Taoist, and with his two sisters, he received the teachings. They practiced day and night, and his wives and concubines were not allowed to see him. The Taoist also promised, "I will make the two fairy maidens conceive divine children." Six months later, the Taoist would come and go, and Hua and his two sisters would sometimes go out at night, returning only at dawn. Hua's face gradually turned pale, his eyes wide, and he could perform feats such as ascending clouds, creating fog, summoning rain, calling the wind, and summoning spirits and ghosts. His wife repeatedly warned him not to show off in front of others.

    651

    骅曰：“吾有此术，可横行天下，人其奈我何？”于是不自秘密，衴邑莫不知之。其妻告其妾曰：“良人出，则必尽夜而后返，其踪迹甚诡秘也。汝盍瞰之？”妾诺焉。是夕，施从良人之所之，卒至西门外密林中。已先有六七人环坐，其次有似秀才者，军卒者，卖菜佣者，又有一僧一尼，貌极狞恶，而双髻道人亦在焉。见骅至，群起迓曰：“皇帝来矣。”骅中坐，诸人列坐，相与计议。其妾隐身于黍稷中，谛视之。咸称僧、尼、道人为国师，秀才为军师，军卒为元帅。所论无非先取某州、据某县、杀某官，大抵皆叛逆之事。尼问曰：“二仙姑胡不至？”骅曰：“彼追魂之法，尚不精练，来时令其演之，今夜不至矣。”日曛暮，遂各起身向西去，不测所往。

    Explanation

    651

    Hua said, "I have this skill, which allows me to act with impunity across the land; what can people do to me?" Thus, he did not keep it secret, and all the towns and villages knew about it. His wife told his concubine, "When the master leaves, he must return at night, and his tracks are very mysterious. Why don't you observe him?" The concubine agreed. That night, she followed the master to where he went, finally arriving at the dense forest outside the west gate. Already there were six or seven people sitting in a circle, including a scholar, a soldier, a vegetable vendor, and a monk and a nun, whose appearance was extremely fierce and ugly, and a Daoist with two braids was also there. When they saw Hua arrive, they all rose to greet him, saying, "The emperor has arrived." Hua sat in the center, and the others sat around him, discussing together. His concubine hid herself among the millet and barley, watching closely. They all called the monk, nun, and Daoist "national teachers," the scholar "military advisor," and the soldier "general." What they discussed was nothing but capturing certain states, occupying certain counties, and killing certain officials, mostly rebellious acts. The nun asked, "Why haven't the two fairy aunts come?" Hua said, "Their method of pursuing souls is not yet refined; they will perform it when they come, so they won't be here tonight." As dusk fell, they all rose and went westward, their destination unknown.

    652

    其妾惊怛，奔告其妻。妻大惧，潜至后园，从后门隙窥之。见树下有土台，高尺余，上设一几，几上烧双烛，大如臂。烛光下，有骷髅七八枚。台四角皆燃灯一盏，二妹被发跣足，仗木剑、步罡风于其上。觉阴惨怖人。却回，相与曰：“良人者，所仰望于终身也，今若此，不我能慉矣！”乃相泣而讪于庭中。邻妇过而怪之，殷殷至诘。其妻忿甚，以实告。

    Explanation

    652

    The concubine was shocked and terrified, and rushed to inform the wife. The wife, greatly alarmed, secretly made her way to the back garden and peered through the gap in the back door. She saw a small earthen platform, about a foot high, with a table set upon it. On the table, two large candles burned, as thick as arms. In the candlelight, there were seven or eight skulls. At each corner of the platform, a lamp was lit, and two sisters stood with their hair loose and barefoot, wielding wooden swords and performing a ritual dance. The atmosphere was dark and terrifying. She returned and, speaking to each other, they said, "Our good husband, whom we had hoped for all our lives, is no longer loving us!" They wept and scolded each other in the courtyard. A neighbor woman passed by and found their behavior strange, persistently questioning them. The wife, filled with anger, told her the truth.

    653

    邻人恐为所累也，鸣于官。官虑其不轨也，密白总戎。总戎阳寝其事，而遣其子及标将密迹之。得一洞于万山中，妖人出没其间。飞骑报闻，总戎乃亲率轻骑一千，衔枚电赴，夤夜抵其处，以枯柴裹秽物，杂以硝磺，堆积洞口如山，举火焚之，烟焰蔽天，次日未刻始熄。使壮夫入洞拽之，得薰毙僵尸二百有奇。揭榜月余，无敢认尸者，遂瘗为巨冢焉。一僧一尼，人皆不识，唯一道士，一黄衣少年，咸识为双髻道士及骅也。总戎令裨将，率众就吕家，掩执二女。二女用邪法咒脱，严捕未获。迟数日，有人于酉阳山中，见雷殛死二女尸于岩下，告官验之，背有朱书曰：“左道惑众，妖人吕氏”云云，方知二女，虽幸脱国法，终难免天诛也。

    　　兰岩曰：今试有执途人而告之曰：“汝为皇帝”，未有不骇然而走，以为能罹灭族之祸者矣。骅固蓄有逆根，故道人得阿其所好而欺之。左道惑人，愚人往往迷而弗悟，卒之身首异处，悔之何及。吁！可哀也夫！

    Explanation

    653

    A neighbor, fearing being implicated, reported the matter to the authorities. The officials, suspecting foul play, secretly informed the general. The general pretended to ignore the case but sent his son and a subordinate commander to discreetly investigate. They discovered a cave hidden among the mountains, where mysterious figures were seen appearing and disappearing. A swift rider was dispatched to report the findings, and the general then personally led a thousand light cavalry, moving silently and swiftly, arriving at the site under the cover of night. He ordered the entrance of the cave to be blocked with dry wood and refuse, mixed with saltpeter and sulfur, piling it up like a mountain. The fire was set, and the smoke and flames obscured the sky, lasting until late afternoon the next day. Men were then sent into the cave to drag out the charred remains of over two hundred corpses. A notice was posted for over a month, but no one dared to claim the bodies, so they were buried in a large mound. One monk and one nun were unrecognizable, but a single Taoist priest and a young man in yellow robes were identified as the double-braided monk and Hua. The general ordered a subordinate commander to lead a force to the Lu family and arrest the two women. The women used magic to escape, and despite a strict search, they were not captured. After several days, someone reported that the two women had been struck dead by lightning at the foot of a rock in Youyang Mountain. The authorities examined the scene and found red writing on their backs, which read, "Using false doctrines to deceive the masses, the evil women of the Lu family." It was then realized that although the women had escaped legal punishment, they could not avoid divine retribution.

    Lan Yan commented: If one were to approach a passerby and tell them, "You are the emperor," no one would not be startled and flee, fearing the disaster of being executed along with their entire family. Hua indeed harbored rebellious intentions, which is why the Taoist priest was able to flatter her and deceive her. False doctrines can deceive people, and fools often become entranced and fail to see the truth, ultimately meeting a fate where their bodies and heads are separated, and it is too late to regret it. Alas, how pitiable!

    654

    阮　龙　光新建阮龙光，公车入都。将抵繁昌曹县，遇风，亟舣舟入僻港，泊荒塘之下。二更后风息，明月满天，十数邻船，尽楚巫巴客，神箫夜火，杂沓纷嚣。阮不耐其哗，独登岸谋静，同载者咸不知也。

    　　信步得一巨石，倚大树一株，即坐踞其上。食顷，隐隐闻斥堠下，有人絮语，察之，见八九人团坐沙际，相去不过十数武。阮以为汛兵值宿，故憩于此，初不为意。夜静，江山清寂，语言了了可辨。闻一老人带晋音者言曰：“一眨眼又一年矣。黄六爷父子尚未来时，咱与耿先生、薛三哥、金大嫂、宋姑娘，每夜共坐此地，亦时聚饮，彼时薛三哥尚捕鱼，必系船于渡头枫树下，金嫂戏窥其篮筐中，窃取小鱼；耿先生独守腐局，始终不肯下箸。我等群咻之。及薛三哥同李七侄入伙后，耿先生被伊终夜啁礰，犹征酒逋，亦何可笑。今黄六爷……”云云，语遂轻，殆不可辨。俄一操吴音者曰：“莫污蔑人！”

    Explanation

    654

    Mr. Yuan Longguang, newly built, traveled to the capital by public vehicle. As he approached Fanchang County, he encountered a strong wind and hastily anchored his boat in a secluded harbor, mooring at a desolate pond. After the second watch, the wind subsided, and the moon was full in the sky. Around a dozen neighboring boats were filled with people from Chu and Ba, their mysterious flutes and night fires creating a clamorous and chaotic atmosphere. Mr. Yuan could not bear the noise and went ashore alone to seek quiet; the others on the boat were unaware of this.

    Leisurely walking, he found a large stone, leaned against a large tree, and immediately sat on top of it. After a short while, he faintly heard chatter from the watchtower. Observing, he saw eight or nine people sitting in a circle near the sand, no more than a dozen steps away. Mr. Yuan thought they were soldiers on patrol duty, resting here, and initially paid no mind. The night was quiet, and the landscape was clear and tranquil. The words were clear and distinguishable. He heard an elderly man with a Jin accent say, "In the blink of an eye, another year has passed. Before the arrival of Mr. Huang and his son, we would sit together here every night with Mr. Geng, Mr. Xue, Mrs. Jin, and Miss Song. We would sometimes gather for a drink. At that time, Mr. Xue was still fishing, and he would tie his boat to a maple tree at the ferry landing. Mrs. Jin would playfully peek into his basket and secretly take small fish; Mr. Geng alone kept to a decaying situation, always refusing to eat. We would collectively criticize him. When Mr. Xue joined the group with his nephew Li, Mr. Geng was mocked all night long, still collecting overdue wine debts, how laughable. Now Mr. Huang..." and so on, the words became lighter, almost indistinguishable. Soon, a man with a Wu accent said, "Don't slander people!"

    655

    寻闻一少年哀泣声。又一人曰：“一人向隅，满座不乐。忆昔泊此，被伧楚窘迫时，金家姑嫂，亦不克兔脱。是时耿先生茕独无依，实大可悯。”一人嗤之曰：“彼受赵抚台托办贡物，尽出何楼。李总戎嘱作碑文，悉由摭拾。诗不解蜂腰鹤膝，字不能虿尾蝇头，卯酉参商，随笔凑合。岁縻脩金百两，日市瞁肉二斤，然犹唆讼投词，危于累卵，忧忿怨贱，窘若拘囚。今冤处九幽，幸全四体。不闻‘楚语’，但作‘吴吟’。薛三哥蹇滞一生，漂泊半世，得鱼换酒，出险入夷。先生酒冲愁阵，固然矣，而抑念奇兵之所自来乎？读书人漫作颟顸，已不足为训矣。顾又礥然哀鸣，妄夙债而念夙隙，是先生犹有蓬之心也。所谓不矜细行者，乃至此乎？”

    Explanation

    655

    Soon, I heard the sound of a young man weeping. Another person said, "When one person stands in a corner, the whole room is unhappy. Remembering when we were anchored here and were in distress due to the rough and uncultured people, the sisters-in-law of the Jin family could not escape either. At that time, Mr. Geng, alone and without support, was truly pitiable." Another person scoffed and said, "He accepted the task of preparing tribute items from Governor Zhao, all of which came from He Lou. General Li commissioned him to write an epitaph, which was all collected from various sources. He does not understand the intricacies of poetry and cannot write elegant calligraphy. His writing is disjointed and pieced together. He spent a hundred taels of silver annually and bought two pounds of meat daily. Yet he still incited lawsuits and submitted complaints, as precarious as stacking eggs, full of worries, anger, and resentment, and as confined as a prisoner. Now he is in deep trouble, but fortunately, he is still alive. He no longer hears 'Chu language' and only sings 'Wu songs.' Brother Xue, who has been hindered all his life and wandered half his life, trades fish for wine and moves from danger to safety. Mr. Geng drinks to dispel his sorrows, which is natural. But do you consider where the unexpected help comes from? Scholars who act carelessly and recklessly are not worth emulating. Yet he still mourns in a loud voice, recklessly thinking of old debts and grudges, showing that he still has a heart of grass. Is this what is meant by not being meticulous about small matters?"

    656

    既而少年啼愈哀，入耳极凄楚。移时有秦音老人慰之曰：“吾辈亦已无生趣矣，乃对酒当歌，希图破闷，奈何复事野哭，令人不忍复闻！纵李兄言太刻毒，适足破泣成笑，何须芥蒂？即如老朽三五少年时，视取科第真不啻摘髭，祸福罔知，一味骄满，形骸放浪，思与晋人分道扬镳，未遇严师，不亲诤友，性由习改，心为境移，以致乔梓相乖，藁砧多舛。不意　鸠伎俩，决飞祗枪榆枋；白发青衫，竟作道旁苦李。迄今髑髅载士，念鱼腹而心酸；魂魄思乡，望鸡头而气苦。不幸之幸，邂逅多君；不言之言，乌乎吾子。”无何，少年哭渐止。

    Explanation

    656

    Soon after, the young person's cries grew even more sorrowful, reaching the ears with extreme poignancy. After some time, an elderly person with a Qin accent came to comfort him, saying, "We too have lost interest in life. We drink wine and sing to try to alleviate our boredom, but how can we continue to hear such wild weeping, which is unbearable to hear again! Even if Li's words are too harsh, they are just enough to turn tears into laughter, so why hold a grudge? Just like when I was in my thirties and fifties, seeing the imperial examination as no different than plucking one's beard, ignorant of good and bad fortune, entirely arrogant and full of oneself, body and spirit wandering freely, thinking of parting ways with the people of Jin, not having met a strict teacher, not being close to诤友, nature is changed by habit, heart is moved by circumstances, resulting in father and son being at odds, husband often suffering misfortune. Unintentionally, the tricks of a jay, flying only as far as the elm and mulberry trees; white hair and blue robe, end up as a bitter plum by the roadside. Until now, the skull-bearing scholar, thinking of the fish's belly and feeling heartache; soul and spirit yearn for home, looking at the chicken's head and feeling bitter. Unfortunate good fortune, to meet you by chance; unsaid words, alas, my child." Soon, the young person's crying gradually ceased.

    657

    继有作歌声，声如曳缕。歌未竟，群作嗟叹声。阮始知遇鬼。恇怯间，瞥见一灯莹莹自远而近，所坐树根石下，哗剥有声，青磷如豆，转瞬遍地皆是。阮大惧，毛发蝟张，仓皇归去，步步迍邅。觉月色不明，两眼皆障，奔走半夜，筋力俱疲。迨东方既白，始如梦觉，依然在树下石畔，跬步未移。色变神痴，颠踣于地。舟子晨兴，失阮所在，同来踪迹，掖之以登舟。阮述夜来所见，或曰：“此鬼打墙也，无足怪。所可怪者，前月有凤翔黄监生父子，贩法帖于苏州，覆舟于此。鬼所称黄六爷，及所闻秦音老人，必其人也。其余既分先后，必有新旧，盖相继溺死于江中者。”阮入都，为咸安宫教习。予尝闻其自述如此。

    Explanation

    657

    In the aftermath, a song began to be sung, its sound thin and drawn out, like a thread being pulled. The song had not yet finished when the crowd started to emit sighs and lamentations. It was then that Ruan realized he had encountered a ghost. In his fear, he caught a glimpse of a faint, distant light approaching, coming from the tree roots and stones where he was sitting. There were sounds of crackling, and green phosphorescence resembling beans appeared, spreading rapidly across the ground. Ruan was greatly frightened, his hair standing on end like a porcupine's quills. In a panic, he fled, each step slow and difficult. He felt the moonlight was dim, and his vision was obscured. He ran for half the night, his strength and energy completely exhausted. By the time the eastern sky began to lighten, he finally awoke from his dream-like state, still at the base of the tree, not having moved even a step. His face pale and his mind blank, he fell to the ground. The boatman woke up in the morning, realizing Ruan was missing, and searched for him, eventually helping him back onto the boat. Ruan recounted what he had seen the previous night, and someone said, "This is what is known as 'ghosts building walls'; it is not surprising. What is surprising is that last month, there were the father and son from Fengxiang, Huang Jiansheng, who were trading calligraphy works in Suzhou and capsized here. The ghost mentioned Huang Six and the elderly man with a Qin accent, who must have been them. The others, being of different statuses, must have drowned in the river at different times." Later, Ruan entered the capital and became a tutor at the Xian'an Palace. I have heard this account from him.

    658

    兰岩曰：阮冢间遇鬼迷惑，亦常事也。未闻若是之言语，历历如晤生平者。

    　　某　太　守某大僚，位首揆，甲第连云，富拟卓、郑，门庭若市。干谒者，恒旬月不得一见，名纸堆积。某太守，失其名，夙与其家奴某季相友善。每入都，则馆其家。季巨富，拥资百万，喜交仕宦为光宠，往来无白丁。太守呼季之父为叔。其父出入，太守每为执鞭捉衔，修子侄礼，以是为众人所羡，亦以是为君子所轻，鄙不齿数，而太守自以为得计，处之怡然。

    Explanation

    658

    Lan Yan said: Encountering ghosts and being confused in the tomb of Ruan is also a common occurrence. It is unusual to hear such detailed and vivid descriptions of past events.

    A certain governor and a certain high official held the highest position, had mansions that reached the clouds, and were as wealthy as Zuo and Zheng. Their homes were bustling with visitors, and those who sought an audience with them often had to wait for months, with their names written on paper that piled up. The governor, whose name is not mentioned, had been friendly with a certain servant named Ji since childhood. Whenever the governor came to the capital, he stayed at Ji's home. Ji was extremely wealthy, with assets worth millions, and enjoyed befriending officials to gain honor and prestige. His social circle consisted only of educated and refined people. The governor called Ji's father "uncle." Whenever Ji's father went out, the governor would hold the whip and bridle, showing the respect due to a younger relative. While others admired the governor's behavior, it was also seen as beneath the dignity of a gentleman and was thus looked down upon. However, the governor felt he had done the right thing and was content with his actions.

    659

    适相国寿辰，季父子皆入府供役，太守独坐斋中。夜分有叩门环声，启户视之，则一秾纤合度、位置得宜、皓齿明眸、雪肤花貌二八佳丽人也。太守惊异，询所由来，女称家人之女，怜公岑寂，聊过一谈。太守神思惚惚，弗克定情，乃相与缱绻，无夜不然。每至则醇醪膳馐，满前列罗，不审从何处得来。女无所不能，能无不妙。而尤精李虚中秘传之术。太守问功名胡底，女推之曰：“八字入格，自是二品贵人。所可惜者，官品高而人品低，人爵进而天爵退耳。”太守曰：“敢闻其说。”女曰：“人生富贵贫贱，皆有命焉，非人力所可迁就也。世人不安其命，不明此理，以为人力可以致之，奴颜婢膝，倚靠冰山，百计经营，以达夤缘之路。即如今日相君之门庭奔竞者是矣。然相君之势位日崇，则门下之趋承日盛。

    Explanation

    659

    During the Chancellor's birthday, both the uncle and his son went to the Chancellor's office to serve, while the prefect sat alone in his study. At midnight, there was a knock on the door. The prefect opened the door and looked out, and saw a beautiful woman of sixteen, well-proportioned, properly positioned, with white teeth, bright eyes, snow-white skin, and beautiful features. The prefect was surprised and amazed, and asked where she came from. The woman said she was from her family, pitying the prefect for being lonely, and just came to talk. The prefect's mind was confused, and he could not settle on his feelings. They then became intimate, and every night was like that. Every time she came, there was fine wine and delicious food arranged in full display, and the prefect did not know where she obtained these things. The woman could do everything, and everything she did was wonderful. She was especially skilled in the secret techniques passed down by Li Xuzhong. The prefect asked how far her success and fame would go, and the woman said, "Her eight characters fit into a pattern, making her naturally a nobleman of the second rank. What is regrettable is that her official rank is high but her moral character is low, and her human titles advance while her heavenly titles decline." The prefect dared to ask for the explanation, and the woman said, "Life's wealth and honor or poverty and disgrace are all determined by fate, and they cannot be adjusted by human effort. People do not accept their fate, and they do not understand this principle. They think human effort can achieve it, and they are servile and rely on a powerful patron. They use all kinds of ways to manage and reach the path of favoritism. Just like those who rush to compete at the Chancellor's court today. But the Chancellor's power and position daily increase, then those who rush to serve under his jurisdiction daily increase."

    660

    此而千金为寿，彼则蓓蓰以进之；彼而万金为赂，此则什伯以形之。相君纵欲市恩，而即此两端，已不得不高下其手。况趋炎附势者，如蝇之逐臭，蚁之慕膻，不堪屈指，讵止此两端而已。公欲叨淑世之荣，而先蹈失身之辱，且又等而下之，媚及臧获，此巾帼尚以为羞，宁须眉反不为愧？异日莫云二品，虽位极人臣，夫何功名之足称述乎？”太守闻之，惭汗如雨，改容谢曰：“敬闻命矣，会当他徙。”女曰：“徙之似矣。白圭之玷，尚可磨也。还须痛改前非，勿蹈故辙为得。”太守曰：“虽然，舍卿而去，何以为情？”女曰：“儿亦从此永诀。”太守愕然曰：“何遽出此？”女曰：“儿非人，实日坛中一老狐也。与公稍有夙缘，故来了之。了却夙缘，虽欲一夕聚首，不可得也。前程远大，慎之重之！”言讫遽去，不复至。太守不胜感喟。翌日，托故他徙。

    Explanation

    660

    In giving a thousand gold as a gift, the other presents something modest in return; when offered ten thousand gold as a bribe, one shapes a more substantial offering in response. The minister indulges in desires to buy favors, and even in these two cases, he must adjust his actions accordingly. Moreover, those who follow the powerful, like flies attracted to filth and ants drawn to strong odors, are countless, not limited to just these two aspects. You seek the honor of being praised for doing good in the world, but first, you fall into the shame of losing your integrity, and even further, you flatter even the lowest servants. Even women would be ashamed, let alone men who should feel no shame. In the future, do not claim a high rank, for even if you reach the highest position, without true merit, what achievements are worthy of praise? The magistrate, upon hearing this, was so ashamed that he sweated profusely, changed his expression, and apologized, saying, "I respectfully accept your advice and will move away." The woman said, "Moving away seems right. Even a small flaw on a white jade can still be polished. You must deeply reform past wrongs and not repeat old mistakes." The magistrate said, "Even so, leaving you, what will become of our relationship?" The woman said, "I will part from you forever." The magistrate was startled and said, "Why such a sudden statement?" The woman said, "I am not a human, but an old fox from the sun temple. I have a slight predestined connection with you, so I came to see you. After fulfilling our predestined connection, even if we want to meet again, we cannot. Your future is bright, be cautious and serious about it!" With that, she quickly left and did not return. The magistrate could not help but be moved. The next day, he moved away under the pretext of an excuse.

    661

    未一年，相国以罪免，季亦罹法。太守深自悔过，磨琢自新，后果仕至某省巡抚，晋兵部侍郎，一如狐女所云。

    Explanation

    661

    Within a year, the chancellor was dismissed due to a crime, and the younger brother also fell under the law. The governor deeply repented, polished and reformed himself, and later served as the governor of a certain province, was promoted to the position of Vice Minister of War, just as the fox girl had said.

    662

    闲斋曰：人设喻借人之势，以恣威福者，曰假虎，曰凭城。是天下胁肩谄笑，最工媚人者，莫狐若也。今观此狐之所以规正太守者，人而狐，狐而人矣。如此狐，固为仅见，而世之如太守其人者，胡何多也！

    　　邓　县　尹衡水某村，有妇人与豪右私通而谋杀本夫者，为尸侄所首。奸夫以多金赂仵作行人，俾其袒己。相尸无伤，官不能理，转斥其告诬妄，痛惩之。

    　　复诉诸府，太守委定兴令邓公往按之。邓至，反复相验，不得证据，夜宿馆舍，思维不置，披衣起坐。时约三更向尽，从人熟寝，地上鼾声相和。已而有寒风起户下，帘幕动响，烛光昏暗，隐隐见壁角现一人，乍前乍却，倏跪于地下。邓不禁毛发森竖，凝神省谛，则形质服色，仿佛日间所相尸也，微作啼泣声，右耳畔垂一白物。邓忽悟，乃大言：“被害之冤，吾必为尔雪之！尔其敛迹，吾知之矣。”其人叩头而隐，烛亦骤明。邓遂就寝。

    Explanation

    662

    Idle Studio said: Those who set up analogies by borrowing others' power to indulge in their own authority are called "false tigers" or "leaning on a city." These are the people who fawn and flatter, most skilled at pleasing others, and none is as good as the fox. Now, observing how this fox corrects the magistrate, it becomes a human, and a human becomes a fox. Such a fox is indeed rare, but how many people in the world are like the magistrate?

    In a certain village in Heshui, Magistrate Deng of the county, a woman was found to have been having an affair with a wealthy and powerful man and plotted to kill her husband, reported by the deceased's nephew. The adulterer bribed the coroner and officials with a large sum of money to cover up his involvement. The examination of the body showed no injuries, and the official could not handle the case, instead accusing the accuser of false reporting and severely punishing him.

    He then appealed to the prefecture, and the prefect sent Magistrate Deng of Dingxing to investigate. Magistrate Deng arrived, repeatedly examined the case, but could not find evidence. He stayed at an inn at night, pondered without rest, and got up and sat down. It was around the end of the third watch, and the attendants were sound asleep, with the snoring sounds blending together on the ground. Suddenly, a cold wind arose from beneath the door, the curtains and screens moved with a rustling sound, and the candlelight was dim. Faintly, he saw a person appear in the corner of the wall, moving forward and backward, and suddenly knelt on the ground. Magistrate Deng could not help but stand on end with fear, focused his mind and examined carefully. The person's form, appearance, and clothing were similar to the body examined during the day, making a faint crying sound, with a white object hanging from the right ear. Magistrate Deng suddenly realized and loudly declared, "The injustice suffered by the victim, I will certainly clear for you! You should disappear, I know it now." The person knelt and disappeared, and the candle suddenly brightened. Magistrate Deng then went to bed.

    663

    翌日，折柬召衡水尹曰：“氓之嗤嗤，诡辞兴讼，苟不立铁案以杜其口，将何以肃公令而靖刁风？请与公督责相人，同至尸所，使死者无遗憾，生者无遁辞。庶上可以复府尊，下可以服观者。”衡水尹见书笑曰：“人谓邓公书痴，良不妄矣。作县十年，贫如寒士，其才可想矣。似此公案，岂拙官所能办耶？”于是复往相之，邓叱令检视右耳，仵作失色，乃于耳中取出水湿棉絮，须臾堆积，约略半斤。邓指示衡水尹曰：“此奸夫淫妇之所以得志也。”尹大惊骇，再揖谢曰：“似此奸谋，不特目所未睹，亦且耳所未闻，实《洗冤录》中所未载。微夤兄，其孰能知之？”邓曰：“此冤魂之灵，非弟之能。”即尸前提奸夫淫妇，严刑拷掠，尽得其状。奸夫坐斩，淫妇坐凌迟。案结，一邑再称神明。

    Explanation

    663

    The next day, Mr. Deng invited the magistrate of Hengshui with a folded letter, saying, "These common people, with their foolish and contemptible behavior, start lawsuits with false accusations. If we do not establish a solid case to stop them from speaking, how can we enforce public order and pacify unruly behavior? Please join me in examining the case and go together to the place of the corpse. Let the dead have no regrets and the living have no excuses to hide. So that it can please the superior and satisfy the observers." Upon seeing the letter, the magistrate of Hengshui laughed and said, "People say that Mr. Deng is a bookish fool, and it is not without reason. Having served as a county magistrate for ten years, he is as poor as a scholar. His talent can be imagined. How could a clumsy official handle such a case?" Thus, he went again to examine the case. Mr. Deng ordered an inspection of the right ear, and the coroner turned pale. Wet cotton was extracted from the ear, and in a short while, it piled up to about half a catty. Mr. Deng pointed to the magistrate of Hengshui and said, "This is how the adulterer and the adulteress managed to succeed." The magistrate was greatly shocked and bowed again, saying, "Such a scheme, not only has it never been seen, but it has also never been heard. It is not recorded in the Washing Away of Wrongs. Without your help, who else could know it?" Mr. Deng replied, "This is the spirit of the wronged soul, not my ability." Immediately, he brought the adulterer and the adulteress forward, subjected them to severe torture, and got all the details. The adulterer was sentenced to be beheaded, and the adulteress was sentenced to be slowly dismembered. The case was closed, and the entire county once again praised him as a god-like judge.

    664

    兰岩曰：真心为民，细心办事，不辞辛苦，不惮繁冗，魑魅情弊，焉能逃秦鉴哉？倘草草了事，以为明决不究，其不为奸吏，欺诳也几希。为民父母者，尚其加意哉！

    　　靳　总　兵鱼河堡在无定河畔。河流移徙无定，往往不遵故道，辄有时去堡三四十里，居民取汲甚艰。会夏月，零雨浃旬，所在沙漠洼窊处多渟潦，居民赖之。

    Explanation

    664

    Lan Yan said: With genuine concern for the people, handle affairs with care and attention to detail, spare no effort or hardship, and fear no complexity or tedium. Hidden evils or corruption, how could they escape the scrutiny of the Qin dynasty? If one were to rush through matters carelessly, thinking that the investigation will not be thorough, how could they not be corrupt officials, deceiving others? Those who serve as parents to the people, they should pay more attention!

    Jin, the General, was stationed at Yuhe Fortress by the Wuding River. The river's course is unpredictable, often deviating from its original path, sometimes moving as far as thirty to forty li away from the fortress, making it extremely difficult for the residents to fetch water. During the summer, after ten days of light rain, the low-lying and marshy areas in the surrounding desert retained pools of water, which the residents relied on for their daily needs.

    665

    有一潦，甚深阔，历年不涸，遂有妖物据之，窃食村中羊豕，渐及小儿。村人通宵逻守，比户戒严。或有见其形者，则一大黑人，高丈余，乌衣长鬣，猛鸷惊人。村人患之。适一道人，年近八旬，与二徒自湖南来，自言能祛邪怪。众公醵四十千，浼其用法，道士以老辞。其徒请行，道士曰：“汝术无能为也。”徒曰：“昔在川中，何以成功？”道士曰：“此非其比矣。彼川中之水，分沙漏石，易为措置。顾此浊流，何以设施？”徒曰：“一符一箓，犹致一流金，谅此么麽，何足齿数！”遂不听师言，步至水滨，禹步焚符，以召妖物。久之不至，乃亟解衣仗剑，泅入水中，即刻波涛汹涌，众以为道士捉得妖物矣，喧呼以助其威。一食顷，水尽赤，见一臂浮水面，俄又一头浮出，就视之，则道士已肢解矣。众大惊，四解而奔。

    Explanation

    665

    There was a marsh, deep and wide, that never dried up, and thus a demon dwelled within it, stealing livestock from the village and gradually targeting children. The villagers guarded it all night, with strict vigilance in every household. Some who saw its form described it as a tall, black figure, over ten feet tall, wearing black clothes and having a long beard, with a fierce and alarming presence. The villagers were troubled by it. An elderly Taoist, nearly eighty years old, arrived with two disciples from Hunan, claiming to be able to expel evil spirits. The villagers pooled forty thousand coins to ask him to use his magic, but the Taoist declined due to his age. His disciple volunteered, but the Taoist said, "Your skills are no match for this." The disciple replied, "In Sichuan, how did you succeed?" The Taoist answered, "This is not the same as before. The water there was clear and easy to manage. But this turbid flow, how can it be managed?" The disciple said, "One talisman and one scroll can still summon a stream of gold. Surely this small thing is not worth mentioning!" So, he ignored his master's advice, walked to the water's edge, performed a ritual dance, and burned a talisman to summon the demon. After a long time, the demon did not come, so he quickly undressed, took up his sword, and swam into the water. Immediately, the waves became turbulent, and the villagers thought the Taoist had captured the demon, shouting to aid his efforts. After a meal's time, the water turned red, and a limb floated to the surface, followed by a head. Upon seeing this, it was revealed that the Taoist had been dismembered. The villagers were greatly shocked and scattered in panic.

    666

    会榆林总戎靳公（桂）行部过其处，见奔民而讶之。询知其故，急遣兵三百人，凿渠运戽，尽彻其水，得一黑鱼，长二丈许，巨口无鳞，拨刺泥淖中。杀而烹之，味劣甚，自是怪绝。

    　　闲斋曰：予闻北海有冰鼠焉，常伏层冰下，啮一穴，岁久大如象，啮愈甚，穴愈阔，水愈薄，暑或泮焉。泮则失所天，失所天而见其真天，则死。人取其肉为餐，骨为器。因叹世间傍门户求利达者，人皆名为趋炎，而张彖独有冰山之喻。尝疑拟非其伦，且冰既山矣，庸有消时乎？观于此而后知彖之善喻也。此黑鱼亦大类是。

    Explanation

    666

    Coincidentally, General Jin Gui of Yulin, during his inspection tour, passed by the area and saw fleeing civilians, which surprised him. Upon inquiring about the cause, he hastily dispatched 300 soldiers to dig a channel and use buckets to drain the water. They found a black fish, about two zhang long, with a large mouth and no scales, moving in the mud. They killed and cooked it, but the taste was very poor, and from then on, the strange occurrence ceased.

    As for this, I have heard that in the northern sea, there is an ice mouse that often lies beneath layers of ice, biting a hole. Over time, the hole becomes as big as an elephant, and the mouse bites more intensely, making the hole wider and the water thinner. Sometimes, in the summer, the ice may melt. If it melts, it loses its natural habitat, and losing its natural habitat reveals its true nature, leading to its death. People take its meat as food and its bones as tools. Therefore, I sigh at those in the world who cling to power and influence to seek profit and success. They are all called "following power," but Zhang Tan alone used the metaphor of an ice mountain. I once doubted that this metaphor was not fitting, and if ice is already a mountain, how can it melt? Seeing this, one understands that Zhang Tan's metaphor is well-chosen. This black fish is also similar to this.

    667

    恩茂先曰：和霁园言其祖诚斋公（明）镇武威时，秋稼将登，忽为李左车所虐。公怒，选壮夫百人，向云头施火攻迎击之。云雷辄退，冰雹顿止。盖其地近阴山，雹有大于石硙者。自公行此法，数年无雹患。奇人奇举，何异钱塘之弩！又，公忧岁旱，数祈雨不应，乃至城隍庙与神约，三日内不雨，必毁像焚庙。是日向午，黄沙蔽天，闾阎间挑灯为市，日暮遂雨。初如毛，渐如丝，继而大雨如注，尽夜方止。四野沾足，一郡欢声雷动。绅衿父老，齐集辕门，焚香拜祝多福。二事皆载武威东门外功德碑。

    　　兰岩曰：至诚感神，昭然不爽，韩文公驱　鱼，同一理也。

    　　藕花商丘宋文学，客禹航，僦居湖干。薜荔衣墙，苔茸毯砌，地极幽僻。柴门面湖。夏秋之间，莲花最盛。宋性故爱莲，有诗百首咏之。

    Explanation

    667

    En Maoxian said: He Jiyuan recounted that his ancestor, Chengzhai Gong (of the Ming Dynasty), was stationed in Wuwei when the autumn crops were about to be harvested, but suddenly suffered from the ravages of Li Zuoche. Gong was furious and selected a hundred strong men to launch a fire attack towards the clouds to counterattack. The clouds and thunder retreated immediately, and the hail stopped suddenly. Because the place is near Yanshan, some hailstones were as large as millstones. Since the public official implemented this method, there has been no hail disaster for several years. A remarkable person and a remarkable deed, no different from the crossbows of Qiantang! Also, the public official was worried about a drought. He prayed for rain several times but it did not come. He even went to the City God Temple and made an agreement with the gods: if it does not rain within three days, I will destroy the statue and burn the temple. That day at noon, yellow sand covered the sky. People lit lamps in the lanes to form markets. It started raining at dusk. At first it was like fine hair, then like threads, and finally it rained heavily. It stopped only at the end of the night. The fields were thoroughly soaked, and the entire prefecture was filled with thunderous cheers. Scholars, officials, and elders gathered at the gate, burned incense, and prayed for blessings. Both events are recorded on the Merit and Virtue Stele outside the eastern gate of Wuwei.

    Lanyan said: Sincere devotion moves the gods, and this is clearly without error. Han Wengong driving away fish is the same principle.

    Ouhua Shangqiu Song Wenxue, traveling to Yuhang, rented a house by the lake. The walls were covered with薜荔 (a type of climbing plant), and the steps were covered with苔藓 (moss). The place was extremely secluded. A thatched door faced the lake. During summer and autumn, the lotus flowers were in full bloom. Song Wenxue loved lotus flowers by nature and composed a hundred poems to praise them.

    668

    会夏日，倚门纵目；见二女郎，操艇子来采莲。一衣红，一衣紫，姿态甚美，而衣红者尤艳绝。次日复至。大约申来酉去，比日皆然。宋初不敢问，后以其频，渐相熟识。因诘之曰：“荡舟亦属险举，采莲不为急务，何不惮烦？”女笑而不答。宋复以言挑之曰：“蜗居在望，何不一过吃茶？”女复不应，但促回棹。紫衣女转舣船近岸。曰：“彼既强来作东道主，即一往过临，看其将何以逆客。”宋大喜，踊跃为异。

    　　宋固独处，唯一佣奴服役，见之疑讶，问：“那得致此丽人？”宋绐之曰：“家中姊妹也，来此见访，万勿泄言外人，致增酬酢。”奴唯唯而去，但司庖厨，无暇旁及。二女相顾而笑。紫衣女曰：“谁谓书痴诚悫，矢口虚妄，尚须思索耶？”宋亦笑，于是狎昵殊甚。询及姓氏里居，红衣女曰：“儿名藕花，小婢名菱花，家在湖上不远，土著也。”是夕遂留与乱。

    Explanation

    668

    On a summer day, leaning against the door and gazing out, he saw two young ladies rowing a boat to pick lotus flowers. One wore red, the other wore purple, both very beautiful, but the one in red was exceptionally stunning. The next day they came again. They usually arrived around the afternoon and left by evening, and this was the case every day. Chen was initially hesitant to ask, but later, due to their frequent visits, he became more familiar with them. He then asked them, "Rowing is also a risky endeavor, and picking lotus flowers is not an urgent task, why do you bother?" The girls smiled but did not answer. Chen then tried to tease them by saying, "My humble home is just ahead, why don't you come in for a cup of tea?" The girls again did not respond, but urged the boat to turn back. The girl in purple turned the boat toward the shore. "He has insisted on being the host, so let's go and see what he will do to welcome us," she said. Chen was delighted and eagerly agreed.

    Chen lived alone, with only a hired servant at his service. The servant was surprised to see such beautiful women and asked, "How did you manage to attract such lovely ladies?" Chen lied to him, saying, "They are my sisters from home, visiting me here. Do not tell anyone else, or it might cause unnecessary trouble." The servant nodded and went away, leaving only to manage the kitchen, with no time to pay attention to anything else. The two girls looked at each other and laughed. The girl in purple said, "Who would say that a bookish person is sincere and honest, yet speaks in vain, still requiring thought?" Chen also laughed, and they became very intimate. He asked about their names and hometowns. The girl in red replied, "My name is Lotus Flower, and my maid is named Water Lily Flower. We live not far from here on the lake, and are native to this place." That night, they stayed and made love.

    669

    鸡鸣则欲言别，宋因挽之。女愀然良久，乃谓宋曰：“荷君雅爱，讵忍一刻睽隔，特势有所不能耳。知君达者，必不为怪，请以实告。儿辈非人，实花妖也。君苟不弃，祈至湖上，见芙渠中有一茎红鲜异常者，即其下有菱花一簇，可并移归。勿伤其寸根片叶，置诸盆中，养以湖水，勿畜犬扰，勿接恶客，则儿与菱花当得朝夕相对矣。”宋且惊且喜，谨志之，遂纵之去。

    　　旭日始旦，即荡小舟，遍阅花中，果有一茎，红俪朝霞，香逾冰麝，大亦倍于凡品。更验其下，有菱花迥异。即出重赏募渔人，并泥移归，培植巨瓮中。闭门谢客，终日卧坐其侧。三日不见女来，颇深疑抱，默搜冥想，万虑纷然。

    Explanation

    669

    At the crow of the rooster, the girl wanted to say goodbye, and Cao tried to stop her. She remained sad for a long time before speaking to Cao: "Bearing the gracious love of you, how could I bear to be separated for even a moment? But circumstances prevent me from doing so. Knowing that you are a wise person, you will not find this strange. Please allow me to tell you the truth. We are not human, but actually flower spirits. If you do not abandon us, please come to the lake, see in the lotus pond a stem that is exceptionally red and fresh, and beneath it there is a cluster of water caltrop flowers, you can transplant them together. Do not damage even a tiny root or leaf, place them in a pot and nourish them with lake water, do not allow dogs to disturb them, do not entertain bad guests, then I and the water caltrop flowers will be able to see each other every day." Cao was both surprised and delighted, carefully noted it, and then let her go.

    As the sun begins to rise, he rowed a small boat and thoroughly examined the flowers. Indeed found one stem, red like the morning glow, fragrant beyond ice and musk, and its size was twice that of ordinary flowers. Upon closer inspection, there was a cluster of water caltrop flowers that stood out. Immediately offered a heavy reward to fishermen and had them transplant the plants together, cultivating them in a large pot. Closed the door to visitors, spending the whole day lying or sitting by its side. After three days without seeing the girl, he began to feel deeply worried, quietly searching and pondering, with myriad thoughts swirling in his mind.

    670

    至第四日，闷而午睡，觉耳畔有拖裙声，视之，则二女已至榻前矣。相见惊喜。藕花曰：“蒙君滋养，感深五内。第资质脆弱，不任劳瘁，故数日苏息，不能动履。至君寂寞，诚不自安。”宋曰：“但得长聚首，何妨暂违颜？鲰生年来，如穷波斯，落落不称意，今得与二卿为偶，虽死不恨。”女曰：“君此心真堪心感，但能终守不渝，则怀与安虽败名，诚非无益于性命也。且名者，实之宾也。轻鸥泛水，起灭须臾，苟不行乐及时，纵活百年，如蜉蝣朝菌耳。即如儿辈，去千顷之广，而就一勺之多，辞镜湖之深，而居瓦缶之浅，非不知犹鱼游釜中，燕巢幕上，其安危无寿，天壤之悬殊也，亦以孑生不如偶死耳。”因贻宋诗曰：“弹指韶光易老，瞥眼初阳又曛。从此朝朝暮暮，不隔秋水思君。”自此，三人如形影之随，不离跬步。二女极相恤，衣服履舄易着，不分尔我。

    Explanation

    670

    On the fourth day, feeling drowsy and taking a nap, she heard the sound of dragging skirts by the ear. Looking up, she saw that two women had already come to the bed. They met with surprise and joy. Lotus said, "We have been nourished by you, and our gratitude is deep within us. However, our bodies are weak and cannot bear hard work. Therefore, we have been resting for several days and cannot move around. Your loneliness is indeed unbearable for us." Cóng said, "As long as we can stay together, it is not a problem to temporarily part ways. I have been like a poor Persian these years, feeling lonely and unsatisfied. Now that I can be with you two, even death would not be regrettable." The woman said, "Your heart is truly worthy of admiration. If you can remain steadfast, then even if it means losing your reputation, it is not without benefit to life. Moreover, a name is just an accessory to reality. Like a light gull on the water, rising and falling in an instant, if one does not enjoy life in time, even living a hundred years is like a morning insect or a day mushroom. For example, like us, leaving the vastness of a thousand acres to live in a small pond, leaving the depth of the Mirror Lake to live in a shallow earthen pot. We are not unaware that living like a fish in a pot or a swallow on a curtain is precarious and short-lived, with a vast difference from the natural world. But we prefer to die together rather than live alone." Therefore, she gave Cóng a poem saying, "Fingers snap and youthful days pass quickly, a glance at the morning sun turns dim. From now on, day and night, I will not be separated from you by the autumn waters." From then on, the three of them were inseparable, always together. The two women cared for each other deeply, sharing clothes and shoes, without distinguishing between us and them.

    671

    一日，宋他出，二友过访，不值。见盆中菱花秀异，采之而去。日暮宋归，藕共泣诉菱花被创之由：“君不怜而救之，儿岂忍独生？”宋大恸，问何术以救之，女曰：“但培其根，每清晨为诵‘观音咒’九九遍，明年此际，可以再生矣。”宋如所教，至心持咒，时以湖泥培养，日夜不辍。次年复出。菱花忽至，虽觉瘦生，而姿态愈艳，相见悲喜交集，各叙间阔，刺刺不休。宋自得二芳，精神发越，形气清爽，读书一过，辄能默诵。

    Explanation

    671

    One day, Song Tā went out, and two friends came to visit but did not find him at home. They saw the lotus flower in the pot, which was exceptionally beautiful, and picked it and left. When the sun set and Song Tā returned, the lotus flower, along with the lotus root, wept and told him the reason it was injured: "You did not pity us and save us. How could we bear to live alone?" Song Tā was deeply moved and asked what method could save the lotus flower. The lotus flower said, "Just nurture its roots, and every morning recite the 'Guanyin Mantra' ninety-nine times. By this time next year, it can be reborn." Song Tā followed the instructions, reciting the mantra with sincere devotion, and regularly nurturing the lotus flower with lake mud, day and night without interruption. The next year, the lotus flower came out again. It suddenly appeared, although it seemed thinner, its beauty and grace were even more splendid. They met, and their emotions were a mix of sorrow and joy. They talked incessantly, recounting the time they had been apart. Song Tā, having gained two beauties, became more spirited and physically and mentally refreshed. He could read a text once and recite it by heart.

    672

    又一年隆冬，大雪盆冰，一夜寒冱，二芳不至，宋独居萧然，不测何故，夜夜不寐，涕泣沾衾，日对瓦盆，潜祈默祷。倏忽春尽夏来，藕花独至，形容憔悴，悉苦不胜。宋拥置膝上，为之拭泪整鬓，问：“何为孱弱至此？菱花安在，不与偕来？”女泣曰：“尚忆菱妹耶？已作冻鬼隔年矣。儿亦不耐严寒，虽苦不死，而奄奄一息，不久亦将辞人世，与君永诀矣。”宋一恸几绝，思之不置。赖藕花相伴，不至哀死。但藕花日渐瘠羸，宋又忧之，延医调治。医一见失志，诊其脉又甚异人，漫留药一刀圭，志其门径而去。虽去而日伺于门，冀其一面。

    Explanation

    672

    Another year of harsh winter, heavy snow and frozen ice, a night of intense cold, the two fragrant ladies did not arrive, Song lived alone in a desolate state, the reason unknown, sleepless every night, tears wetting the quilt, facing a clay pot daily, secretly praying and silently petitioning. Swiftly, spring ended and summer came, Lotus Flower arrived alone, looking emaciated and exhausted, completely worn out. Song embraced and placed her on his knees, wiped away her tears and arranged her hair, asking, "Why have you become so weak? Where is the lotus flower? Why didn't she come with you?" The girl wept and said, "Do you still remember the lotus sister? She has been frozen to death the previous year. I also cannot withstand the severe cold, though suffering but not dying, I am on the verge of death, soon leaving the world, forever parting from you." Song was deeply sorrowed, nearly leading to death, constantly thinking about it. Relying on Lotus Flower for company, not dying of grief. But Lotus Flower gradually became emaciated, Song was concerned and invited a doctor for treatment. The doctor, upon seeing her condition, lost heart, diagnosed her pulse as very unusual, left a small amount of medicine, left the address and departed. Though gone, he waited daily at the door, hoping to see her again.

    673

    适宋又他出，是日薄暮，医偷见藕花独步湖上，丰姿绰约，与湖莲争妍。医不复能耐，突前抱持之，藕花骇而逸，纵身湖中。医慌持其足，足拍然而折。视之，藕一段耳，始知其妖幻。急告宋，宋大痛恨，趋湖上哭之，深恨医之选事，欲鸣诸官，佣奴劝阻曰：“明明妖异，虽之官，庸得理乎？”宋乃止。翌日，仍至湖上哭之。见一莲花浮水面，断藕犹存，痛哭抱归，种于盆，越宿即萎。乃具棺衾，葬之湖上，作《芙蓉诔》以吊之。遂髡缁为比邱，云游不知所终。

    Explanation

    673

    He went to Song and then went out elsewhere. On that day, as dusk approached, the doctor secretly saw the lotus flower walking alone by the lake, her graceful and elegant appearance competing in beauty with the lotus flowers in the lake. The doctor could no longer restrain himself and rushed forward to embrace her. The lotus flower was startled and fled, leaping into the lake. The doctor panicked and held onto her foot, but it snapped suddenly. Upon closer inspection, it was only a piece of lotus root, and he then realized it was a supernatural being. He hurriedly informed Song, who was deeply grieved and ran to the lake to mourn her. He deeply resented the doctor's actions and wanted to report the matter to the authorities. His servants dissuaded him, saying, "It is clearly a supernatural being. Even if you go to the authorities, how could it be resolved?" Song then stopped. The next day, he still went to the lake to mourn her. He saw a lotus flower floating on the water, with a broken piece of lotus root still attached.
