He cried bitterly and carried it back, planting it in a pot. The next day, it wilted. He then prepared a coffin and shroud, buried it on the lake, and wrote the Fu Hua Lai to mourn her. He then shaved his head and became a monk, wandering as a monk and his final destination is unknown.

    674

    兰岩曰：花是美人前影，美人是花后身，原无分别耳。弱体柔肢，珍惜之且恐不胜，那当此庸医恶客，叠加损折哉？彩云易散琉璃脆，信不诬也。

    　　王　塾　师宗室某王子向问亭，方其未袭爵时，家有塾师王姓者，教授有年矣。往往作戏术，颇奇幻；偶一炫露，渐为家人所知。一日，与白之亲故夜饮，客曰：“此时安得鲜鱼汤啜之？”王曰：“易易耳。”乃觅一篮子，命馆僮提之，闭目绕地而走，僮且走且作摸鱼状形。有顷，王曰：“止！得之矣。”果得一鱼，长尺许，拨刺篮内。烹食之，味极鲜美，众诘馆僮何来此鱼，则云：“在水中摸得耳。”或又思市卖肴馔，王即取钱如价，置篮中，仍命僮闭目行。随见多品在篮，烹饪之美，如初出镬者，热烁唇齿。诸如此类，不一而足，愚者惊其神，智者但谓其有搬运法耳。

    Explanation

    674

    Lan Yan said: Flowers are the precursors of beautiful women, and beautiful women are the successors of flowers, originally there is no distinction. Delicate bodies and tender limbs, even if cherished, are still feared to be insufficient, how much more so when faced with incompetent doctors and bad guests who cause additional harm? Colorful clouds are easily dispersed, and glass is fragile, indeed it is not false.

    Wang, the Tutor: Prince Xiang Wenting, when he had not yet inherited his title, had a tutor named Wang who had been teaching for many years. He often performed magical tricks, which were quite wondrous; occasionally, he would showcase them, gradually making them known to the household. One day, he told his relatives and friends about his abilities during a night drinking session. A guest said, "At this time, wouldn't it be nice to have some fresh fish soup to drink?" Wang replied, "It's easy." He then found a basket, ordered the student to carry it, and walked around with closed eyes. The student walked while pretending to catch fish. After a while, Wang said, "Stop! I've got it." Indeed, he caught a fish about a foot long and placed it in the basket. They cooked and ate the fish, which tasted extremely fresh and delicious. The guests asked the student where the fish came from, and the student said, "I caught it in the water." Someone else then thought of buying prepared dishes, and Wang immediately took out money equivalent to the price, placed it in the basket, and again ordered the student to walk with closed eyes. Soon, they saw many dishes in the basket, which were cooked beautifully, as if just taken from the pot, hot and scorching the lips. There are many more examples like this, too numerous to mention. Fools are amazed by his magic, while wise people merely say he has a method of moving things.

    675

    居久之，王子忽患痨疾，日渐尪羸。易医数十，药石罔效。亲串中来探候者，进则相慰，退则共议，以为断无痊理。其母某福晋，只生王子一人，日夜焦愁，眠食皆废。或言王子之病，非俗医所能瘳，馆中王先生，法术玄纱，福晋倘能降心求之，彼必有以授手。福晋以为然，即使内监延王入，涕泣而道之，王正色力辞，以为不能。福晋跪请，声泪俱下。王请福晋起，俯首沉思，移时未决。福晋又再四拜恳，良久，王始许诺，曰：“明日当有以报命耳。”趋出，而嘱其馆僮曰：“无扰我睡，俟吾自寤。”遂引衾而卧，状若死人。

    Explanation

    675

    After a long time, the Crown Prince suddenly fell ill with tuberculosis, growing increasingly emaciated day by day. Dozens of doctors were consulted, but their medicines and treatments proved ineffective. Relatives and close associates who came to visit him comforted him when they entered, but privately discussed among themselves, believing that there was no hope of recovery. His mother, a noblewoman, had only one son, the Crown Prince, and she was constantly anxious and distressed, neglecting her own sleep and meals. Some suggested that the Crown Prince's illness could not be cured by ordinary doctors, and that Mr. Wang, a scholar with special techniques, might be able to help. If the noblewoman could humble herself and seek his assistance, Mr. Wang would certainly have a way to treat him. The noblewoman agreed with this suggestion and sent a servant to invite Mr. Wang. She explained the situation to him while crying, but Mr. Wang firmly refused, saying that he could not help. The noblewoman knelt and begged him, her tears and sobs falling freely. Mr. Wang asked her to stand up, bowed his head, and thought deeply for a while. Still undecided, he hesitated. The noblewoman knelt and begged him again and again, for a long time, until Mr. Wang finally agreed. He said, "I will report back tomorrow." He hurriedly left and instructed his student, "Do not disturb me while I sleep; wait until I wake up on my own." He then lay down and pulled the blanket over himself, looking like a dead person.

    676

    王子有山陵在某处，祖茔也。是夜二更后，守陵人有直宿者，瞥见一人，由甬道径入宫门，审谛之，则王先生也，大骇愕。隐念先生在城内，夜深来此何为？方冥想间，旋见殿上有人出迓，衣四团龙褂，拱王入殿，分宾主坐，执礼恭谨，似有所恳。王亦有言，相隔远，悉不能辨，但潜身屏息，于窗隙中窥伺之。俄闻门外呵殿声甚严，见侍卫多人，拥一王者入，像貌瑰丽，气度尊崇，冠履衣裳，皆非时制。王与殿上人，疾趋迎拜，同入殿中坐。

    Explanation

    676

    During the reign of the prince, there was a tomb located in a certain place, which was the ancestral burial ground. On that night, after the second watch, one of the guards on duty caught a quick glimpse of a person entering the tomb through the passageway. Upon closer examination, it turned out to be Mr. Wang, and the guard was greatly startled. Secretly pondering why Mr. Wang, who was in the city, had come here at such a late hour, the guard was deep in thought when he suddenly saw someone emerge from the hall to greet the newcomer. The person was dressed in a four-pleated dragon robe, and they escorted the newcomer into the hall, where they sat down with the host and guest. The greeting was conducted with great courtesy, as if there was some urgent matter to discuss. The newcomer also spoke, but the guard, being far away, could not fully understand the conversation. He remained hidden and silent, peering through a crack in the window to observe the scene. Suddenly, he heard a loud and solemn retinue outside the door, and he saw many attendants escorting a king into the tomb. The king had a beautiful and majestic appearance, exuding a dignified and revered presence, and his attire, including his hat, shoes, clothes, and garments, did not conform to the current era or fashion. The newcomer and the person from the hall rushed forward to greet and bow to the king, and they all entered the hall together to sit down.

    677

    王者居中，王居左，殿上人居右。王起坐再三，似代为殿上人请托者然，王者无言。少焉，忽闻一片喧嚣，见一人裸形，手将一人发辫，且打且行，同跪阶下，细视被将者，即王子也。殿上人趋步下阶，向其人哀恳求宽，复拜求良久，其人终不许。殿上人泣而入殿，王随趋下，向其人耳语数四，亦不允。王嗒然却回。既而王者出殿，当陛而立，开谕再三。其人不得已，始释手，痛哭而去，其声甚惨。殿上人拜谢王者及王，殊形感荷。已而王者去，王亦继去，殿上人送之出门，返入殿上，遂寂然无所见。

    Explanation

    677

    The king sits in the center, the prince to his left, and the person on the dais to his right. The prince stands up and sits down multiple times, as if pleading on behalf of the person on the dais, though the king remains silent. After a short while, suddenly heard a loud noise, saw a person naked, holding another person's hair, while hitting and walking, kneeling together at the steps. Upon closer inspection, the person being held is the prince. The person on the dais quickly descends the steps, pleading with the person to be lenient, bowing repeatedly for a long time, but the person finally does not agree. The prince cries and enters the hall, sadly retreating. Afterwards, the king comes out of the hall and stands on the steps, repeatedly explaining or advising. The person, unable to help it, finally releases the hold, crying bitterly and leaving, the sound was very sad. The person on the dais bows to thank the king and the prince, feeling deeply grateful. Afterwards, the king leaves, and the prince follows, the person on the dais sees them off at the door, returns to the hall, and everything becomes quiet and empty.

    678

    翌日入城，备述夜来事以报福晋，曰：“小爷病当愈矣。”福晋未遽信。无何王睡起，入告福晋曰：“昨为王子事，大费调停。盖王子之祖，在生时曾枉杀一渔人，渔人诉于冥司，冥谴先王当斩嗣，至王子即绝，以偿渔人之怨。吾感福晋之诚，竭力关说，始得暂免王子之厄，然夙冤未解，尚需建醮超度，方克解脱，幸福晋勿忘也！”福晋感谢，一如其教。王子病遂痊，自是，合府之人，敬王如神明。

    Explanation

    678

    The next day, upon entering the city, he reported in detail the events of the previous night to the Lady, saying, "The young master's illness should be cured." The Lady did not immediately believe him. Shortly after, the King woke up and entered to report to the Lady, saying, "Yesterday, I made great efforts to mediate on behalf of the Prince. It turns out that the Prince's ancestor, while alive, unjustly killed a fisherman. The fisherman appealed to the underworld, and the underworld condemned the deceased king to behead his successor, leading to the extinction of the lineage to settle the fisherman's grudge. I was moved by the Lady's sincerity and exerted great effort to intercede, and only managed to temporarily exempt the Prince from this calamity. However, the ancient grudge remains unresolved, and further rituals are still needed to liberate and free him from suffering. Please do not forget this, Lady!" The Lady thanked him and followed his advice. The Prince's illness finally recovered, and from then on, everyone in the household revered the King as a deity.

    679

    一日，王子约王游西山，夜宿山中清话，蓦见一黑物，大如牛，蠕蠕而至。王见之大惊，急嘱曰：“知之矣。可先去，于某处某潭下待我，行将至矣。”物遽去。王子骇甚，问此胡为者，王叹曰：“吾以不自检束，每自炫露，今此物欲与吾较量，吾之厄也。此物法术至精，吾非其敌。然与之较必死，不较亦死，不能不与之较。请王子备棺衾，明日于潭侧收吾骨焉！”王子大惊，力止其行。王曰：“是无所逃避也，即当往矣。”言讫，唏嘘而往。

    　　王子心不释，潜率家人十数，踵至潭边察之，不见踪迹，惟闻芦荻中奔腾迅跞，或见白光乱斗，横若掣电，旋若釶火，如数百金戈铁马之声。听之胆寒，见之股栗，直至鸡鸣始静。向晨入视，则箭攒黑物，遍身皆满，伏地不动；而王亦赤身僵卧潭边，须眉毛发皆尽。舁之以归，越宿始苏。细诘其故，乃知杀物之剑，悉须眉毛发之所化也。

    　　王子每举以质人，博识者多以为剑仙之流亚者也。

    Explanation

    679

    One day, the Prince invited the King to travel to the Western Mountain, and they spent the night there, talking leisurely. Suddenly, they saw a black creature, as large as a cow, slowly and wriggling its way toward them. The King was greatly surprised and hastily instructed, "I know what this is. You may go first and wait for me at a certain place and a certain pond. I will soon arrive." The creature left hastily. The Prince was extremely frightened and asked, "What kind of thing is this?" The King sighed and said, "I have not been self-restrained and have often shown myself off. Now this creature wants to compete with me, and this is my misfortune. This creature's mastery of magic is extremely refined, and I am not his match. However, if I compete, I will die; if I do not compete, I will also die. I cannot avoid competing with it. Please, Prince, prepare a coffin and burial shroud. Tomorrow, collect my bones by the side of the pond!" The Prince was greatly surprised and tried to stop him. The King said, "There is no way to escape this. I must go." After saying this, he sighed and left.

    The Prince could not let go of his concern and secretly led a dozen family members to the edge of the pond to investigate. They saw no traces, but they heard the sound of rapid and fierce movement in the reeds, or saw white light fighting wildly, like lightning streaking across the sky and spinning like a whirlwind, like the sound of hundreds of spears and horses. It was so frightening to hear and so terrifying to see that it did not stop until the rooster crowed at dawn. In the morning, they went inside to look, and they saw arrows piercing the black creature, covering its entire body, lying motionless on the ground; and the King was also naked and stiffly lying by the side of the pond, with all his hair, beard, and eyebrows gone. They carried him back home, and he did not wake up until the next day. After asking in detail about the reason, they found out that the sword that killed the creature was transformed from his hair, beard, and eyebrows.

    The Prince often brought up this incident to consult with others, and those with broad knowledge mostly believed that it was similar to the Sword Sage.
